notes: RUNTIME-LOCALIZED 2026-07-18: freeze reproduces on pure save_redirect BOOT (no reload fired). Boot mms_step progression 1(MSB LOAD)->2(MSB LOAD WAIT)->STUCK at 3(WORLD RES WAIT); product-core-autoload 'waiting for core readiness state=6/6'; draw_group never enables because WorldResWait never resolves (dest block-res never created, blk_ls=0). This IS the known central S9 blocker. Prior RE (bd step3-reactive-processmsbloadlists-crashes-init-point-fix-needed-2026-07-17): reactive ProcessMsbLoadLists AVs; CORRECT FIX = run world-res rebuild at STEP_MoveMap_Init(0x140aec210)/_Common_Initialize(0x140aed910) with the DEST map loadlistFileCap. title: Picker-boot crash on load: stale fullread slot request detonates at world arrival (gaitem AV 0x67141a)
description:
SYMPTOM (user-reported 2026-07-07): every launch via ~/Elden/launch.sh (quicksave.me3 -> target/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/release/er_effects_rs.dll) crashes ~22s in, exactly as the character finishes loading into the world. 6/6 crashes: 19:01 run + all five 22:45-22:58 runs. Signature: access-violation rva=0x67141a (CSGaitemImp::Deserialize, gaitemInsTable[-1] OOB dispatch, fault_addr=0xffffffffffffffff), NtTerminateProcess 0xc0000005. Evidence: GAME_DIR/er-effects-crash-log.txt lines 81-98; er-effects-autoload-debug.log runs 112,114-118.

ROOT CAUSE (verified from logs + source): PR #40 (feat/game-native-file-picker, merged to main at 562b96f; key commit a61c793 "Load the picked save via the native full-read chain") routes the missing-save picker boot into native_fullread_tick (now called UNGATED from title_tick_cover.rs:918/1212). Its SUBMIT phase arms the native load request (GameMan+0xb78=slot, set_save_slot ac0=slot) BEFORE calling submit 0x67b1a0. In these runs submit returned 0 and the deserialize 0x67b290 returned 0 (read the default/empty container: c30 stayed 0xa010000, level=9) so the step-6 GUARD FAILed and the tick went PHASE_DONE "save-safe" -- but NOTHING DISARMS b78/ac0/req_slot. gm-snap shows req_slot=2 armed from ~12.8s onward. The own_stepper fallback then mounts the real character (world loads fine, LOAD-CORRECTNESS level=90), and on the first in-world frames the game's save-manager services the stale request (gm-snap save_state 1->2, req_slot 2->-1 at ~21.85s) -> SECOND full deserialize into the live world via native call site 0xafbc3e -> gaitem free-queue exhaustion (table already populated by the boot mount) -> the known gaitemInsTable[-1] AV (bd system-quit-postswitch-crash-gaitem-freequeue-exhaustion-2026-07-02, same class). Correlation across the retained 118-run log: every run entering fullread SUBMIT crashed at world arrival; no run without it crashed on load.

WHY THE USER GOT THIS BUILD: agent session 106491a3 (ended 22:59) built main@562b96f in .worktrees/game-native-file-picker at 22:43:03 and cp -f'd it over target/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/release/er_effects_rs.dll (the user's quicksave.me3 natives path). The 18:xx build (earlier iteration, session 6456e3ba) had the same routing -> 19:01 crash. NOTE: that DLL is STILL at the natives path; the user's next launch crashes again until it is replaced.

STATE MATRIX: pre-PR#40 evening builds soft-lock after pick (runs 107/109/111 never reach world load -- the bug PR#40 was fixing). Post-PR#40 loads then dies at world arrival. Morning builds (runs <=111 flavor) played but crash at System Quit/profile switch (separate ASSERT-family crashes at +33..168s, a0_rva=0x29c7aa0/0x2bb1aa8 -- distinct bug, not this issue).

FIX DIRECTION: (1) native_fullread_tick MUST disarm what SUBMIT armed on every non-commit exit (GUARD FAIL, DRAIN timeout, submit ret=0): restore GameMan+0xb78/+0xac0/request lane to pre-SUBMIT values before storing PHASE_DONE. With the stale request gone, guard-fail boots fall back to the own_stepper mount which demonstrably works (runs 112+ mounted level-90 char fine) -- no world-arrival deserialize, no crash. (2) Root-cause DESER ret=0: the fullread deserialize read the default container despite the redirect stage being active and b80 RESIDENT (run 118: 60-Slots stage read diag_hits growing, yet c30=0xa010000/level=9 after deser). Possibly slot-state/ProfileSummary not populated for the picked slot at deser time even after MarkProfileIndexAsUsed. (3) Runtime smoke required before merge (runtime-affecting, main is PR-only).

SAVE SAFETY: user's real progress is in save-files/60-Slots/ER0000.sl2 + its er-effects-save-redirect-stage tree (redirect worked; writes went there). The APPDATA ER0000.sl2 "ZERO readable character slots" empty container predates today (first seen run 86) -- no new save loss in these crashes.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: FIX IMPLEMENTED + LIVE-VALIDATED (2026-07-08). Branch fix/fullread-stale-request-disarm, draft PR #42 (off main@562b96f). native_fullread_tick now clears GameMan+0xb78 (requested_save_slot_load_index) back to -1 on every NON-commit terminal exit (drain-timeout, guard-fail, verify-only, both commit-aborts) via new fullread_disarm_slot_request(). Commit path untouched. Runtime smoke PASS run 119 (user-driven pick, offline vanilla): DISARM req_slot 2->-1 at +13252ms (guard-fail), Vagabond L90 loaded (c30=0x220c0000) at +15455ms, save-mgr servicing window at +21.7s saw req_slot=-1 (no 2nd deserialize), survived 30s past load, crash-log 0x67141a AV count unchanged at 18. oracle_fullread_req_disarm_count=1 last_prev_slot=2. vs crash run 118: req_slot stayed 2 at same window -> 2nd deser -> AV. check.sh green. Leave open until PR #42 merges to main; user undrafts.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: SOFTLOCK FOLLOW-UP FIXED + LIVE-VALIDATED (2026-07-08, same branch/PR #42, commit 4fc862b). SECOND bug in the picker boot: LATE pick (wait for loading bar to reach save-check, then pick) softlocked -- char never mounted, boot idled forever on native-fullread DONE-observe (c30=0xa010000 level 9). ROOT CAUSE (two live runs): native title auto-opens its menu ~25-38s into a parked boot (online->offline sign-in timeout chains the menu-open check steps: NetworkCheckJob+save-data ShowProgressJob). If menu opens BEFORE pick, Continue/Load rows build vs empty ProfileSummary -> disabled; own_stepper's accept-byte menu-open path only runs at a40==0, so post-pick (a40==1 already) it's skipped and own_stepper never reaches S2 mount. Early pick works because menu still closed at pick -> accept-byte opens it fresh with save present. FIX: title_open_menu_suppress_hook (MinHook on TitleTopDialog::open_menu 0x1409b24e0) no-ops while missing_save_selection_pending() -> menu only built post-pick (rows enabled) -> normal accept-byte flow -> own_stepper mounts. Self-gates (early pick / non-picker = pass-through). oracle_title_open_menu_suppressed_count. VISUAL: bar now stalls at TITLE creep-top 458 (MENU milestone can't latch while held); moved SAVE CHECK marker 570->458. SMOKE PASS (late pick): 18 suppressions, accept-byte post-pick at (Loop,a40==0), own_stepper mount, LOAD-CORRECTNESS Vagabond L90, native handoff world=true, clean quit, no AV. SEMAPHORE NOTE: char oracles (oracle_char_level/name/c30/player_present) are LIVE and reset to default on quit-to-title, so a final snapshot reads empty even on success -- load proof is the latched LOAD-CORRECTNESS line. Follow-up worth doing: add a latched peak-load oracle + split msgbox oracle load-time(bad) vs in-world/quit(benign). Both telemetry-only.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: SEMAPHORE FOLLOW-UP added (2026-07-08, commit c706430, same branch/PR #42). Telemetry-only, no runtime behavior change, check.sh green. (1) LATCHED PEAK-LOAD: live oracle_char_* reset on quit-to-title so a final snapshot reads empty even on success; dump_load_correctness now latches highest-level real char (level>=1 + non-empty name) into process-lifetime statics -> oracle_load_correctness_seen (>0 proves a real char reached world this run), oracle_loaded_peak_level/_c30/_name/_name_len. Quit/switch can't falsify. (2) LOAD-TIME MSGBOX SPLIT: oracle_msgbox_total_builds conflated the load-time msgbox (crash trigger) with benign in-world quit-confirm dialogs; MSGBOX_POSTLOAD_BUILDS already tracked the in-world subset, so exposed derived oracle_msgbox_loadtime_builds (=total-postload) + oracle_msgbox_loadtime_seen -- crash-trigger count now reads directly (last run: total=1 postload=1 loadtime=0, i.e. the 1 msgbox was the benign quit dialog). PR #42 now carries 3 commits: disarm crash fix + menu-open-hold softlock fix + marker + these semaphores. Removed throwaway test monitor scripts (tripped the no-sleep-sync lint; never committed to the branch).

title: Release DLL soft-locks when product autoload is only env/config armed
description: User hit a release/default ME3 soft lock after building with plain cargo xwin build --release. Static source showed title/native-menu visual suppression is default-on for real runs, but PRODUCT_AUTOLOAD_ARMED only armed when SaveLoadMethod::DirectMenuLoad plus the experimental direct-menu gate were selected. Agent smokes had been adding env/config gates behind the scenes, masking the plain-release failure.
close_reason: Release default arming, game-dir TOML, package audit, and ME3 profile pitfall documented/fixed

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Implemented static fix for the release/default soft lock.

Root cause from source inspection:
- `title_native_menu_visual_suppression_enabled()` was default-on for real non-telemetry runs.
- `PRODUCT_AUTOLOAD_ARMED` was only set when `SaveLoadMethod::DirectMenuLoad` plus the experimental direct-menu gate was selected.
- Agent smokes had been using env/config gates, so a plain release DLL could hide the native menu/cover while never starting `product_core_autoload_tick`.

Fix:
- `arm_product_autoload_from_request()` now arms `PRODUCT_AUTOLOAD_ARMED` for every real release/default attach unless explicitly disabled (`no-autoload`), telemetry-only, or native-profile-capture.
- Added repo instruction: product behavior must pass from a normal release DLL under ME3 with no hidden `RUNTIME_*`, `ER_EFFECTS_*`, or smoke-driver env vars.

Validation:
- `bash scripts/check.sh` PASS
- `cargo xwin build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc` PASS
- Release DLL rebuilt at `/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/release/er_effects_rs.dll`, size 1967104, mtime_ns 1783307170124518225.

Runtime proof status:
- Tried ME3 from the agent with no DLL env vars and only `--steam-dir /home/banon/.local/share/Steam`; ME3 resolved the profile and wrote an attach config for the release DLL, but did not reach an `eldenring.exe` process within 90s in this agent environment. No DLL telemetry was produced. Artifact log: `/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/release-default-me3-no-env-long-20260705-200900.log`; ME3 log: `/home/banon/.local/share/me3/logs/quicksave/2026-07-05_20-09-00.log`.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Follow-up root cause from user manual ME3 soft lock:
- Live module list proved `er_effects_rs.dll` was loaded from `/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/release/er_effects_rs.dll`.
- DLL output was in the game directory, not DLL-adjacent: `/home/banon/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/ELDEN RING/Game/er-effects-telemetry.json` and `er-effects-autoload-debug.log`.
- Telemetry showed `product_autoload_armed=true`, so the prior arming fix was active.
- But `product_core_callsite_slot_ok_ticks=0`, `profile_slot_active_bytes_qword=0x0`, `oracle_corrupted_save_seen_count=1`, `oracle_corrupted_save_seen_id=401107`, `game_save_slot=-1`.
- ME3 log showed attach_config `savefile: Some("")`, caused by `/home/banon/Elden/quicksave.me3` containing `savefile = ""`.
- Removed that empty `savefile = ""` line from `/home/banon/Elden/quicksave.me3` so ME3 stops overriding default save handling with an empty alternative savefile.

title: Decision: use c0000-rigged armor/body route for DS1 mushroom playable proof
description: Decision record for the DS1 mushroom playable-character investigation. The first implementation should rig/import a DS1 mushroom mesh (prefer c2280 Mushroom Child) onto Elden Ring's c0000/player armor-body pipeline rather than first trying to run DS1 NPC skeleton/animations directly as the player.
design:
Decision: Use Route A as the first and only correct initial implementation path: DS1/DSR mushroom mesh -> Elden Ring c0000/player armor-body rig.

Confidence statement:
- I now have very high confidence that doing anything else first is the wrong path for the stated goal of a playable mushroom-person character.
- The only condition that would change this is a concrete offline asset finding that c2270/c2280 FLVER geometry cannot be imported/exported or cannot be acceptably weighted to an ER c0000-compatible skeleton at all. That is unlikely because public tooling directly supports FLVER import/export/FBX-to-FLVER and animated FLVER vertex groups.

Evidence behind the decision:
1. The user goal is player-controlled playability, not first preserving DS1 NPC behavior fidelity.
   - Elden Ring player input is mediated by c0000 player behavior/HKS. EldenRingHKS documents c0000.hks as the interface between player inputs and the player character.
   - Therefore any route that keeps a DS1 mushroom as its own enemy/NPC skeleton/behavior must either reimplement player control over a non-player chr or deeply integrate that skeleton into player behavior. That is larger than the actual first playable requirement.
2. Standard ER custom-animation path is behavior-heavy.
   - SoulsModding adding-moveset docs state a new TAE ID alone is not enough; new TAE IDs must be added to c0000.behbnd.dcx.
   - ERClipGeneratorTool docs say new clip generators must be added for custom ANIBND entries and recommends modified c0000.hks to reduce HKS work.
   - ERClipGeneratorTool troubleshooting says animations may fail to trigger when HKS logic depends on expected TAE sections and Common_define/player tables do not contain the new section.
   - SoulsModding Havok behavior reference says CustomManualSelectorGenerator selects child hkbClipGenerators based on active TAE section/offsetType. That means a DS1 animation-first path is not just file conversion; it is behavior graph + HKS integration.
3. Route A keeps all normal player semantics by construction.
   - If the mushroom mesh is weighted to ER c0000/player bones and delivered as an armor/body replacement, ER's existing c0000.hks, c0000.behbnd, c0000.anibnd, combat, locomotion, input, stamina, lock-on, hitboxes, weapon state, camera, and equipment code remain the control path.
   - This directly satisfies “using buttons on their controller like a normal character control would function” without needing host input simulation or custom runtime control.
4. Route A is supported by known model tooling.
   - Soulstruct-Blender documents FLVER meshes as Blender Mesh children of an Armature for animated models, controlled through weighted vertex groups; animated FLVER vertices must have 1-4 bone weights and illegal weights are reported.
   - FbxImporter supports importing FBX meshes into FLVER for Dark Souls 3, Sekiro, and Elden Ring and preserves correct vertex buffer layouts/material UV needs.
   - FLVER Editor/Soulstruct/SoulsFormats provide established read/write paths for FromSoft model formats.
5. Route B remains viable but should only follow Route A.
   - soulstruct-havok and Soulstruct-Blender suggest DS1<->ER animation data can be inspected/imported and that FromSoft animation format has not changed much from DS1 to ER at the Blender/keyframe level.
   - However the integration burden for controller-triggered ER gameplay is still c0000.anibnd + c0000.behbnd + CMSG/clip-generator + HKS semantics. That is substantially more work and has more failure states than mesh rigging.
6. Offline DSR GZF probe supports source RE readiness but does not change the asset-route decision.
   - Offline Ghidra 12.1 import/probe of C:\Users\choza\DarkSoulsRemastered_1.3.1.0_dump.exe.gzf found asset and animation manager symbols/strings: ChrbndFileCap, AnibndFileCap, FlverFileCap, MovtaeFileCap, TAE processing, hkaSkeleton/hkaSkeletonMapper, chr:/c%04d.chrbnd, chranibnd:/c0000.anibnd and c0000_c%04d.anibnd patterns.
   - That helps later static/runtime semantic lookup, but playable asset authoring still starts with the mushroom chr binders.

Rejected-first alternatives:
A. “Play as DS1 mushroom NPC skeleton using DS1 animations directly.”
   - Rejected as first path. It bypasses the ER c0000 player pipeline and requires solving non-player skeleton control/behavior ownership before the player can simply move/attack normally.
B. “Retarget DS1 mushroom animations before mesh proof.”
   - Rejected as first path. Even successful HKX conversion does not make ER input trigger those animations; behavior/HKS registration is required.
C. “Runtime DLL forces player pose/control as mushroom.”
   - Rejected as first path. It fights the native player controller and would need a custom gameplay controller/oracle. It also does not solve meshes/materials/equipment presentation.
D. “Replace an ER enemy with mushroom and possess/spawn it.”
   - Rejected as first path. It proves enemy replacement, not player-character playability with normal controller/equipment semantics.

Committed path:
- Route A: c2280/c2270 source mesh inventory -> choose c2280 first -> import mesh -> transfer to ER c0000 skeleton/armor-body armature -> export ER-compatible FLVER/partsbnd loose mod -> offline validate -> later runtime smoke only when explicitly appropriate.

Decision gate:
- Proceed with Route A unless c2280 and c2270 both fail offline FLVER import/export or cannot be weighted to c0000 with sane deformation. If that happens, revisit Route B/C with the concrete failed asset evidence attached.
acceptance_criteria: Route A is the committed first implementation path unless offline asset inventory proves c2280/c2270 cannot be rigged to c0000 at all; any Route B work is explicitly secondary after mesh proof.
close_reason: Decision recorded with evidence; Route A is the committed first path.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Decision confidence strengthened by offline tool and asset checks:
- The DSR c0000.anibnd sample (from available overhaul files) unpacks into c0000 player TAE/HKX structures and 30 delayload enemy descriptors, but no c2270/c2280 delayload descriptors. So even the DS1 player animation binder sample does not already expose mushroom animations as a ready player path.
- That makes the animation-first/possess-NPC route even less attractive as the initial implementation: it would require asset extraction plus adding mushroom animation delayload/TAE/behavior glue, not just reusing an existing player bridge.
- Route A remains the only sensible first path: import the mushroom mesh and weight it to the already-working ER c0000/player armor-body path.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Route A confidence increased again after ER reference unpacking:
- ER c0000.flver has no visible mesh/material payload in the unpacked c0000.chrbnd (0 materials/meshes), so direct visual replacement of c0000.flver is not the right first visual proof target.
- A real ER body armor donor (BD_M_1010) has normal mesh/material payload and parses cleanly: 16 materials, 18 meshes, 17,349 vertices, 24,446 triangles.
- This makes the first concrete implementation even more specific: c2280 mushroom mesh -> ER body armor donor FLVER/partsbnd -> native c0000 player controller/equipment pipeline.

title: Second angrE load (System->Quit->Load Profile) must reach world readiness, not stall at WORLD RES WAIT 3/20
description: Goal 2026-07-17: replay user's recorded inputs to drive a second in-process load of angrE (area 0x1c legacy) after the first autoload, and reach world readiness with zero user involvement. Blocker: switch stalls at mms_step 3 WORLD RES WAIT because block-res entry for 0x1c000000 is never created (blk_ls=0x0, blk_found=1) -- the fast switch skips STEP_MoveMap_Init's world-res rebuild. Reactive ProcessMsbLoadLists AVs mid-stream (disabled c43879c). Fix must run at STEP_MoveMap_Init/_Common_Initialize time OR route the 2nd load through the proven autoload mechanism. Also: harness must force game foreground at first world-readiness (user clicks taskbar before pressing START -> flips DLUID input-accept).
notes:
POST-FINISH REVERT IS NATIVE (2026-07-19, DLL 8868ee72). Added a decoupled per-epoch stable-proof (SYSTEM_QUIT_STABLE_PROOF_EPOCH, not FRESH_DESER_DONE which own_load consumes) that fires on finalize-done (SYSTEM_QUIT_RELOAD_FINALIZE_DONE_EPOCH set when finalize>=8) OR can_move OR sf>=30, going phase->IDLE + clearing b78. It FIRED correctly ("post-finish stable proof OK ... phase IDLE ... holds the reloaded world" +86276). But the world STILL reverted: SetState(owner,2) committed_was=6 at +88042 (~1.8s AFTER the hold). So phase->IDLE does NOT prevent the revert -> the post-finish revert is a genuine NATIVE InGameStep decision (committed=6 in-world -> SetState(2)), NOT driven by our AUTOLOAD_HANDOFF-gated return-title logic (hypothesis disproved). can_move never latches for the reload epoch (player torn down before the 60-frame probe; can_move=True in load2 rows is epoch-0 carryover, move_epoch=1 stays can_move=false).

STATE OF THE RELOAD (huge progress this session): FD4-IO residency (world loads resident, real char) -> case-7 finalize unblock (drain b80 + clear save_requested/b73 -> finalize completes 5->9, requestCode=2, MoveMap FINISH) -> world momentarily entered (player present). LAST BLOCKER: native post-finish SetState(6->2->10) revert to title ~1.8s after finish, tearing down the entered world before the move probe can prove control. This is the bd 2026-07-17 "return-to-title after finish" issue, now reached with a genuinely-loaded world.

CANDIDATE HYPOTHESIS to test next: the forced b73/save_requested clears (needed for case-7) may leave the native InGameStep in a state it rejects -> reverts. Prior direction (No-Compromises native-ownership) says route the reload through the native load recipe rather than force-clearing flags. NEXT: RE what native code drives SetState(owner,2) from committed=6 after the reload MoveMap finish (find the caller / the InGameStep step-7 exit that chooses revert-to-title vs stay), to fix the ROOT rather than force-hold.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
2026-07-19 (DLL af27ec75) analysis correction + loading-bar N+1 granularity.

(1) LOADING-BAR GRANULARITY (user directive 2026-07-19 'Nth step is really N+1'). RE of the InGameStep step table (dump 0x143d70190) shows STEP_MoveMap_Init -> STEP_MoveMap_Update -> STEP_MoveMap_Finish, and the MoveMapStep child (steps 0..20) runs ENTIRELY INSIDE STEP_MoveMap_Update. So the child reaching FINISH (20) is NOT genuine readiness: the InGameStep must still advance MoveMap_Update -> MoveMap_Finish (requestCode InGameStep+0xd8 draining 1->2) then to the resident in-world step. Added synthetic main load steps 21 MAP FINISH HANDOFF and 22 IN WORLD (return_title.rs boot_load_step_name / BOOT_LOAD_STEP_MAX), wired into boot_progress.rs switch override (furthest-advanced of child-step vs requestCode-derived step, monotonic) + submilestone (step21 = IG D8 REQUEST 1 / STABLE 2; step22 = PLAYER RESIDENT / MOVE PROOF). Bar now shows the true post-FINISH handoff and freezes on the exact handoff substep instead of resting at FINISH 20/20.

(2) SetState(owner,2) NATIVE-vs-OURS reopened. Grep proof: OUR code NEVER calls the SetState setter (0x140b0d960) with state=2 -- we write TitleStep committed/requested fields directly (raw ptr) or submit the game's OWN return-title builder 0x79d700. So the trace-logged SetState(2) is executed by native code; the only question is whether our earlier return-title submit triggered it or the engine did it spontaneously. My prior 'genuine native' verdict conflated the own_load switch-reload phase (which I set to IDLE) with SYSTEM_QUIT_QUICKLOAD_PHASE (the re-drive arm) -- different variables. Added attribution to the SetState trace log (req_code, quickload_phase, rt_submit count, own_phase) so the next run decides it directly.

(3) DEADLOCK HYPOTHESIS (task_registration.rs 464-489): in the strict-repro path (er-effects-prove-movement.txt present) the menu_free_reload_ready completion latch requires movement_proven_for_epoch (can_move for the reload epoch). can_move can't latch because the world is torn down before the 60-frame move probe -> phase stays >= RETURN_TITLE_REQUESTED -> product_core_autoload_tick keeps running. This couples 'stop re-driving' to 'movement proven', which the re-drive itself prevents. Candidate fix (pending attribution): disarm the re-drive the instant native in-world is reached (requestCode==2 / mms child done) INDEPENDENT of movement proof; collect movement proof AFTER phase IDLE. Aligns with bd warpfix-broke-freeze-remaining-is-teardown-after-finalize-2026-07-18 (disarm on requestCode==2).

Run af27ec75 launched (markers reload-fd4io + reload-drainb80 set, PROBE_FOREGROUND=1) to capture the attribution.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
DECISIVE load1-vs-load2 divergence (run samechar-3x-20260719-141726, DLL af27ec75, loadcmp-diff). The goal's core comparison, pinned:

LOAD1 (boot autoload, SUCCESS): MoveMapStep 3 WORLD RES WAIT -> 18 MOVE MAP (skips 13-17). finalize 5->7->8->9 POST-FINALIZE, then child 18->19 CLEANUP->20 FINISH cleanly (player STAYS present through CLEANUP+FINISH, can_move=True), THEN InGameStep advances ig_parent 7->8 / d8 1->2. i.e. the MoveMapStep child fully completes its own 18->19->20 BEFORE the InGameStep declares MoveMap done. (Load1 then reverts to title only because the sq-repro autopilot intentionally tears it down to start load2.)

LOAD2 (same-char reload, FAILURE): MoveMapStep 3 WORLD RES WAIT -> 13/14/15 HIT STABILIZE -> 16 TEX STABILIZE -> 17 HORSE WAIT -> 18 MOVE MAP (TRAVERSES 13-17 that load1 SKIPS = the warm-reload/character-resident stabilize path). finalize reaches only 8 WARP/SERVER FINALIZE, NEVER 9 POST-FINALIZE. Child STAYS at mms=18 (never 19 CLEANUP / 20 FINISH). YET ig_parent jumps 7->8 / d8 1->2 WHILE the child is still at 18 -> player torn down (player True->False, wcm->0x0) -> native SetState(owner,2) revert to title.

ROOT (revised, supersedes 'post-finish revert'): the InGameStep advances STEP_MoveMap_Update -> STEP_MoveMap_Finish (d8 1->2) PREMATURELY in load2 -- before the MoveMapStep child's finalize genuinely completes (8->9 then child 19/20). The premature advance abandons the still-running child, tears down the player, and native reverts. This is NOT a post-FINISH revert (the child never FINISHed); it is a premature-InGameStep-advance that STRANDS the child at finalize=8/mms=18.

PRIME SUSPECT: my case-7 unblock force-drains b80 (save_state) 3->0 (reload-drain-b80 fired repeatedly at mms18 finalize=5). If the InGameStep MoveMap_Update->Finish transition (d8->2) is gated on b80==0 (load-done), forcing b80->0 to pass the child's 7->8 gate ALSO makes the InGameStep read the load as done and advance prematurely, before the child reaches finalize 9 / cleanup. Tension: draining b80 unblocks the child's 7->8 gate but prematurely signals the parent InGameStep. Per No-Compromises/native-ownership the real fix is to let the load genuinely complete so b80 clears naturally, OR scope the b80 drain so it does not signal the InGameStep. RE subagent dispatched to decompile the child's 8->9 gate + the InGameStep Update->Finish d8->2 predicate.

Attribution confirmed: the load2 teardown SetState(owner,2) fires at quickload_phase=0 rt_submit=0 own_phase=0 = NATIVE, no DLL re-drive (deadlock hypothesis refuted). New loading bar rendered 'MAP FINISH HANDOFF 21/22 (IG D8 STABLE 2 3/3)' correctly.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED (native RE + runtime timing correlation). The load2 player-teardown-at-Cleanup is caused by OUR OWN post-finish stable-proof clearing GameMan+0xb78 (requestedSaveSlotLoad) to -1 at finalize>=8, which removes the MoveMap WARP TARGET mid-warp.

NATIVE MECHANISM (Ghidra, dump VAs): InGameStep::STEP_MoveMap_Update @0x140aec810 waits for the child (FUN_140eb5550) then sets requestCode+0xd8=2, fetches the destination BlockId (protocolState==WaitReload -> GameMan->loadTargetMapId; else GameMan->moveMapStepBlockId set by orchestrator FUN_140afb970 from GameMan->requestedSaveSlotLoad=b78), and GATES: if (BlockId==0xffffffff) { SetCurrentStatsToMax(); skip FUN_140aebeb0 (the block/map load); goto finish }. Orchestrator FUN_140afb970 dispatches the profile load ONLY when b78 != -1 AND BOOL_143d856a0==false. The player null at Cleanup itself is EXPECTED (MoveMap advancer FUN_140afa7c0 case 8 frees WorldChrMan/FieldArea tasks to unload the source world); the BUG is that no valid destination is set so nothing reloads -> InGameStep finishes empty -> TitleStep reverts (committed 6 -> SetState 2). Retention-critical native fields at the Cleanup/Finish boundary: b78(requestedSaveSlotLoad)!=-1, moveMapStepBlockId/loadTargetMapId!=0xffffffff, bc4(+0xbc4)==0, BOOL_143d856a0==false.

OUR BUG: title_tick_cover.rs post-finish stable-proof (commit 40df100, line ~1709) clears *((gm+b78))=OWN_STEPPER_SLOT_NONE(-1) when finalize_done_for_reload (finalize>=8). Runtime af27ec75: 'post-finish stable proof OK ... cleared GameMan+0xb78' fired at +84315 (finalize WARP/SERVER FINALIZE 8), and player went true->false at +84746 (~430ms later). loaders.rs:281-288 ALREADY documents that b78 MUST stay armed through finalize because 'it is the warp target that MoveMapStep finalize case 8 consumes to warp the character and autoclear warpRequested; clearing it early leaves the load with no warp target'. The stable-proof (added to 'hold the world') does the OPPOSITE -- it clears the warp target during the warp.

FIX: remove the b78 clear from the post-finish stable-proof (keep phase->IDLE + save_requested clear which do not remove the destination). Let the native warp (finalize case 8) consume+autoclear b78. Also verify the b78 world_up guard (line ~1887) does not clear b78 during the mms18 finalize 1..9 warp window. Then rerun the samechar-3x probe: expect the warp to complete, mms 18->19->20, finalize->9, player retained, no revert.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: FIX APPLIED (DLL pending build): removed the b78 clear from the post-finish stable-proof (title_tick_cover.rs ~1709). Kept phase->IDLE + save_requested clear (neither removes the warp destination). b78 now stays armed through the finalize/warp so InGameStep::STEP_MoveMap_Update reads a valid destination BlockId and issues FUN_140aebeb0 (the block load) instead of the 0xffffffff skip -> world retained. Case 8 reads NO server/network/session condition (only WRITES server-log fields) so it is fully offline-satisfiable; case 8 does NOT write bc4 or trigger eventNetworkErrorReturnTitleStep.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
POST-WARP REVERT ROOT CAUSE = GameMan+0xb73 (RE decisive, subagent). The revert is the quit-save-RETURN-TITLE LATCH, confirming the user's 'quit without saving' intuition (NOT the network path, though both funnel through the same setter). Our System->Quit runs FUN_14067a490 (deobf 0x14067a3a0) which sets saveRequested(+0xb72)=1, b73(+0xb73)=1, bc4(+0xbc4)=1. b73 persists from the quit until the return-title completes (cleared only by FUN_140678800 via STEP_ReturnTitle, or FUN_14067b660). The MoveMapStep per-frame ending evaluator FUN_140afa7c0 (deobf 0x140afa6d0) polls FUN_140679460 (deobf 0x140679370 = b73 && !FUN_14080d660() && bc4!=3); when b73=1 it ORs the return-title condition into CSMenuMan.menuData+0x5e, ending the session -> propagates to STEP_EndFlow (0x140b0ccf0) -> SetState requestedState++ (0x140b0d940, deobf 0x140b0d870) -> committed 6->2 revert. Fresh boot load1 never ran System->Quit so b73=0 -> evaluator returns early (menuData+0x5e stays 0) -> stays in-world.

bc4==0 at the revert does NOT exonerate the quit path: bc4 is a transient job counter (1->2->3->0) that already completed; the persistent driver is b73. saveRequested oscillation is a BENIGN side-effect: GameMan::_Update auto-save (0x14067f6c0) re-asserts saveRequested when saveState==0 && interval elapsed && ...; our per-frame clear stops the save so field137_0xb88 never updates and it re-asserts. Not the revert cause.

SINGLE-FLAG FIX: hold GameMan+0xb73==0 through the reload finalize/post-warp window. My case-7 drain ALREADY clears b73 but only at finalize (mms18) -- TOO LATE: the ending evaluator runs from MoveMap entry and latches menuData+0x5e before the finalize-time clear (and clearing b73 after does not un-latch +0x5e). FIX: clear b73 (and bc4) continuously from reload commit (FRESH_DESER_DONE=1), before mms18, so the evaluator never sees b73=1. This is semantically correct: once load2 is streaming (post SetState5) the quit-return-title intent is already fulfilled; nothing legitimately re-sets b73 during the stream (auto-save touches saveRequested not b73), so an early continuous hold keeps it 0. Also consider clearing menuData+0x5e if it can latch before our clear. Verified offsets: b72=0xb72 b73=0xb73 bc4=0xbc4. Implementing the early b73 hold now.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
b73 REVERT MECHANISM FULLY PINNED (run 1643). Our system_quit_submit_direct_return_title_chain fires the native return-title REQUEST FUN_14067a490 (0x67a3a0) EXACTLY ONCE (+63492, source=menu-pump-run-hook) for the load1->title->load2 transition, setting saveRequested/bc4/b73, then forces bc4->READY(3). That b73 is then CLEARED when the native return-title completes and load2 begins streaming (my FRESH_DESER_DONE-gated b73-hold finds b73==0 all through load2 mms 1-17). b73 is RE-SET at mms18 -- and NOT by our request (fired once, not at mms18), so it is the NATIVE auto-save / RequestSave path firing at load2 world-entry (RE Q3: GameMan::_Update auto-save 0x14067f6c0 sets saveRequested when saveState==0 && interval elapsed && menu-ok && saveSlot!=-1; b73 is its return-title companion). The ending evaluator FUN_140afa7c0 (engine thread) reads b73 the SAME frame -> latches menuData+0x5e -> STEP_EndFlow -> SetState 6->2. My b73/ending-latch clears run on the game-task thread and LOSE THE RACE to the engine-thread evaluator -> the timing-variant revert.

This IS a product bug (the native auto-save fires for a user load2 too, not just the sq-repro harness -- the request fired once for the transition, correctly). CLEAN FIX (well-defined, next step): suppress the native b73 set at load2 world-entry -- hook the auto-save/RequestSave setter (find the exact fn that writes GameMan+0xb73 at world-entry; candidates GameMan::_Update auto-save 0x14067f6c0 or RequestSave) and no-op the b73 write while a reload epoch is active (FRESH_DESER_DONE=1, not yet movement-proven). That wins the race deterministically (b73 never becomes 1) instead of chasing it on the wrong thread. Alternatively hook FUN_140afa7c0 (the evaluator) to force its b73 input to 0 for the reload. The forced-clear approach (b73-hold/ending-latch on the game task) is abandoned -- wrong thread, loses the race.

My 'b73 is the revert trigger' conclusion is NOT established: we FORCE bc4->READY(3) after the return-title request (system_quit_force_return_title_bc4_ready), and the ending predicate FUN_140679460 = b73 && !FUN_14080d660() && bc4!=3 is NEUTRALIZED while bc4==3. comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
REVERT TRIGGER NAMED (run 1650, ENDCOND snapshot). SetState(6->2) revert at +81714: ENDCOND[warp=0 b73=0 bc4=0 md5d=0 md5e=1]. The ONLY set condition is menuData+0x5e. CRITICAL REFRAME (the-fix-remove-warp-clear memory): md5e = cVar10 written by FUN_140afa7c0 (MoveMapStep::CheckReturnToTitle) EACH FRAME; cVar10=1 is what DRIVES the finalize (walk field25_0x12a 0..8, case 8 = advance 18->19 + consume warpRequested). So md5e=1 DURING finalize is CORRECT (offline: warpRequested drives cVar10=1). The bug: after case 8 consumes the warp (warpRequested 1->0), menuData+0x5e stays 1 RESIDUAL, and the next step reads that residual as a return-to-title -> SetState 6->2 revert (at the revert warp=0 = warp already consumed, md5e=1 = residual). 

MY reload-ending-latch-hold IS COUNTERPRODUCTIVE: it clears md5e=1 DURING the finalize (log: reload-ending-latch-hold #1-8 at mms18 finalize=5, warp still 1) -> it fights the finalize driver (the advancer must re-set md5e faster than I clear, causing the MMS-CLEANUP re-drive bursts + timing variance). FIX: only clear the md5e RESIDUAL AFTER the warp is consumed (gate the clear on warpRequested(GM+0x10)==0), never while warp==1 (finalize in progress). During a reload (FRESH_DESER_DONE=1) + warp==0 + md5e==1 = the post-warp residual to clear so the world is not returned to title. This is the precise, corrected fix -- b73/bc4/md5d are all 0 (not triggers); md5e residual is the sole trigger; my during-finalize clear was sabotage.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: REVERT ROOT NAMED via STEP_GameStepWait decompile (dump 0x140b0ced0 = TitleStep::STEP_GameStepWait, deobf 0x140b0cde0). The revert is NOT any menuData/b73/warp condition -- it is the GameStepWait EXIT: when InGameStep->field24_0xd8 (requestCode) == 0, STEP_GameStepWait runs: sets CSMenuMan+0x6b0=1; if FUN_140679520()!=0 -> SetState 7 (EndFlow); elif FUN_140679530()!=0 -> SetState 9; ELSE (both 0) -> GameMan::SetMapId(0xffffffff) + SetState 2 (BeginLogo/TITLE) via FUN_140b0da50(this,2). So the load2 revert (SetState 6->2, all ENDCOND=0) is exactly this DEFAULT branch: requestCode drained to 0 with FUN_140679520 and FUN_140679530 both 0 -> STEP_GameStepWait invalidates the map and returns to title. While requestCode != 0, GameStepWait KEEPS WAITING (stays in-world). So the TRUE discriminator is: requestCode (InGameStep+0xd8) must STAY 2 (in-world) -- if it DRAINS to 0, GameStepWait's default exit is TITLE. On a successful stay-in-world load requestCode holds 2; load2 drains it to 0. NEXT: RE what SETS requestCode=2 and holds it (STEP_MoveMap_Update sets it when child finished) vs what DRAINS 2->0 on load2 prematurely; and what FUN_140679520 / FUN_140679530 are (the two flags that would divert to EndFlow(7)/state9 instead of the title default). The whole b73/menuData/warp/defer line was the wrong mechanism -- the revert is the requestCode==0 GameStepWait exit.

comments.author: Banon

title: Native Windows consecutive profile-switch: deterministic run_post + autonomous harness
description: PAB detour now owns 0x7ad1c0 and calls system_quit_menu_window_run_post (fixes ghosting + non-interactive ProfileSelect install race). Build autonomous harness to load 2nd/3rd character consecutively from the 169-Invades multi-char save, quit via Return-to-Desktop each iteration. Prove via RAM oracles (distinct char identity per switch, 0 softlock, 0 msgbox).
notes: HARNESS FEASIBILITY (empirical, 2026-07-15): built switch_harness_discovery_tick (lifecycle.rs, marker-gated ER_EFFECTS_SWITCH_HARNESS_DISCOVERY) that blocks the keyboard + pulses DIK_ESCAPE in-world and logs every MenuWindowJob::Run filename via run_post. RESULT: after ESC, only HUD/boot filenames appeared (01_000_FE, 01_100_Clock, 01_080_EmergencyNotice, 01_090_SummonMessage, 01_060_Caption, 05_000_Title, 01_900_Black, 02_903_NowLoading2) -- NO 02_000_IngameTop, NO menu opened. So the DInput keyboard block/inject vehicle (the Proton path in input_blocker.rs) does NOT reach the game's menu input on NATIVE WINDOWS (game likely uses raw input / a different device). HARNESS BLOCKER: need a native-Windows input vehicle -- verify with a movement-key (DIK_W) test whether DInput reaches the game AT ALL, else use PostMessageW(WM_KEYDOWN) (hooks.rs:285) or a native menu-open call. Also still need the user's actual menu-open keybind. Discovery code is env/marker-gated OFF for product; harness driver NOT built beyond discovery.

description: User chose No-Compromises path (2026-07-15): reverse-engineer a genuinely safe animated portrait drive on native Windows. The drive crashes at FUN_141e90290 rcx=0x20 -- a half-seeded GX offscreen resource (null wrapper+0x40) dereferenced by the GX resource-state classifier while the engine's OWN ResMan offscreen render runs mid-build (+0x754/+0x755 in flight). Existing guard profile_offscreen_gx_resources_ready (lookat_bone_hooks.rs:286) checks wrapper+inner readiness but was insufficient (native still crashed) so the whole drive was gated off. Goal: Ghidra-decompile FUN_141e90290/FUN_141a02de0/FUN_141a853a0 + the engine offscreen build lifecycle, find the serialization primitive that lets us drive ONLY when the engine is provably not rendering, gate strictly on it, native-validate. Refs er-effects-rs-n4x.

title: Native-Windows save picker: render list on overlay + keyboard input
description: On native Windows the loading experience is owned by the separate-window overlay (own D3D12 device). When no autoload save is configured the game shows a save picker; we must render the save-file list ON the overlay and wire keyboard navigation/pick. The existing WH_KEYBOARD_LL path (ensure_save_picker_keyboard_hook / save_picker_overlay_input_tick) was designed for the Wine Present-hook thread, which never installs on native Windows. Need the overlay's own message-pump thread to source keyboard input for the picker. User will test interactively (launch without a configured save, drive with keyboard).
close_reason: Proven live end-to-end on native Windows (no-save boot): picker armed + boot held; WH_KEYBOARD_LL hook drove full keyboard nav (src_hook=true) across AppData->C:\->drives D/M/O/P/T/V/X/Y-> A:\...\169-Invades\ER0000.sl2; file pick->character sub-picker->slot 0->direct-file stage + SHGetFolderPathW/CreateFileW redirect->save-check hold released->character loaded in-world; clean exit no AV. Commit 6fa84c9.

title: Native-Windows loading overlay: separate window + own D3D12 device
description: ARCHITECTURE PIVOT (user 2026-07-15, 'own what the user sees across the loading experience'). Root cause established over 17 native-Windows runs (er-effects-rs-n4x): compositing on the GAME's shared D3D12 device crashes the strict native AMD driver at every phase (shared-device contention with the game's async rendering). vkd3d/Proton isolates it; native Windows does not. FIX: don't composite on the game's device at all. Instead OWN the screen with a SEPARATE always-on-top borderless overlay window + our OWN D3D12 device + swapchain, covering the game. Render the full loading experience (loading bar, static portrait, save picker) on our independent device -- no shared-device race, cannot crash the game. Game boots+loads underneath headless-to-screen; hand off (hide/destroy our window) when player_present (world loaded). Input: reuse the WH_KEYBOARD_LL low-level hook (focus-independent) for the picker. Requires BORDERLESS mode (overlay windows can't cover exclusive fullscreen). Reuse: HDR pack helper (pack_rgba8_to_r10g10b10a2), the CPU bar/text rasterizer, the save-picker model. Retire on native Windows: the game-swapchain composite path (kept for Wine). Plan: (1) minimal proof -- window+device+swapchain, clear-to-color over the game, hide on world load, verify no crash + visible; (2) add bar; (3) add picker+input; (4) add static portrait; (5) handoff polish.
notes: PROGRESS 2026-07-15: overlay working on native Windows. Validated: (1) separate window + own D3D12 device/swapchain, stable 60s, reaches gameplay (run18, stage=10, 3662 frames); (2) ownership cycle -- SHOW while player absent, HIDE in gameplay (run19, show=0 in-world); (3) loading BAR driven by real progress (run20, USER CONFIRMED visible); (4) caption TEXT via CPU-rasterize(boot_draw_text_rgb, exposed pub(crate)) + upload buffer + CopyTextureRegion on OUR device (run21). Render path: ClearRTV bg+bar rects, then RT->COPY_DEST, upload+copy a text strip, ->PRESENT. Swapchain is 8-bit R8G8B8A8 (no HDR pack needed on our own swapchain). Progress source: LOADING_SCREEN_BAR_PROGRESS_PERMILLE (native loading bar) + boot creep. Commits on branch fix/windows-gx-resource-null-crash. NEXT: (a) save PICKER -- render the file list (reuse save_picker_overlay model + the text-region blit) + wire WH_KEYBOARD_LL nav into it; the picker shows during the boot cover when no save provided (user wants to test w/ keyboard); (b) static portrait + stats. The upload/CopyTextureRegion text path is the reusable primitive for both.

title: y22i: RE-grounded FUN_141e90290 crash fix (needs Ghidra/deobf host)
description: The native-Windows loading-screen AV FUN_141e90290 (deobf 0x141e90290, rcx=0x20 reads [rcx+0x10]->+0x30) is a game-side advance-before-seed race in the CSMenuProfModelRend offscreen GX pipeline: our +0x754/+0x755 kick drives the engine to build the profile model+offscreen mid-load, and the engine's own ResMan-scheduled offscreen render reaches the GX resource-state classifier with a half-seeded resource. Session 2026-07-15 shipped a MITIGATION (suppress our Present composite while +0x754/+0x755 build latches are set, present_overlay.rs profile_model_build_in_flight) because the composite's concurrent GPU device work was the timing trigger vs 6 clean runs. Two deeper fixes need the Ghidra dump + eldenring-deobf.bin (both live under /home/banon, ABSENT on the /home/choza validation host): (1) correct profile_offscreen_gx_resources_ready in lookat_bone_hooks.rs:286-302 -- it misreads off+0x58 (a scene-registered BYTE flag per portrait_worker.rs:449-450) as a 4th wrapper pointer, so it perma-returns false on ALL platforms (block_off_resource fired but never let the drive through); re-RE the true off+0x40..+0x58 layout and the resource-state field. (2) Add a null-check DETOUR on FUN_141e90290 mirroring the proven Scaleform descriptor guard (scaleform_descriptor_guard.rs) -- needs the function's return contract + prologue from the dump. Do this work on the RE host (banon) with scripts/ghidra-query.sh + scripts/dump-deobf-shift.py.
notes: DECISIVE ISOLATION 2026-07-15 (run14, drive-off-on-native + composite gate). Result: 60s cap, ZERO crashes, player_seen=true, char='Experiment Bean' -- user confirmed the game reaches the loading screen + in-game and 'that all works'. CONCLUSION (proven, not inferred): the character-profile RENDER-DRIVE (force_profile_render kick + portrait pump) is THE native-Windows crash cause; it makes the game rasterize its own profile offscreen mid-load which the strict AMD D3D12 driver faults on (every crash site chased -- 0x1e90290/0x1e862fd/0xd6510d -- was downstream of this drive). vkd3d/Proton tolerates it => Linux fine. The DISPLAY/composite path is NOT the cause. Committed c9a9e5a: is_native_windows() gates force_profile_render_enabled + portrait_render_drive_enabled OFF on native Windows (env ER_EFFECTS_ALLOW_NATIVE_PROFILE_DRIVE=1 override); composite early-boot gate. DLL now STABLE on native Windows. CONSEQUENCE: the ANIMATED portrait cannot work on native Windows (needs the drive) -- architectural. REMAINING OPPORTUNITY: the loading BAR + a STATIC portrait do NOT need the drive; wire the composite to a DRIVE-INDEPENDENT display gate (game's own CS::LoadingScreen active / oracle_now_loading, not PROFILE_LOADSCREEN_TABLE_BUILDS which only fires with the drive) and test if they display visible pixels safely. Ghidra dump at /home/choza/ghidra_y22i if needed.

title: y22i Windows validation: A/B guard vs control for 0xec95d1 Scaleform crash
description: Execute docs/y22i-windows-validation-handoff.md on the native Windows dual-boot (real RX 6900 XT). Build guarded DLL (59b025b) and control DLL (de53e76) natively with MSVC, run me3 offline A/B probes, confirm control reproduces access-violation rva=0xec95d1 and guard build shows oracle_scaleform_desc_guard_installed=1, oracle_scaleform_desc_provider_null_hits>0, no AV. Record evidence + bd memory.
notes: DECISIVE FINDINGS 2026-07-15 (native Windows RX6900XT + Special K + HDR). All committed on branch fix/windows-gx-resource-null-crash. FIXED+PROVEN: (1) swapchain discovery (present_hook_hits 0->196/41; QI-fallback accepts SpecialK-wrapped swapchain, vtable in SpecialK64.dll, accept_path=2). (2) HDR composite (backbuffer=R10G10B10A2 fmt24; pack_rgba8_to_r10g10b10a2 + BackbufferEncoding; boot_view_draw_hits 0->3). (3) device-removed crash 0x1e8ad57 (boot-pump self-present used a wrong-state PRESENT<->COPY_DEST barrier at arbitrary time -> hr=0x887a0005; FIX: on wrapped swapchain accept_path==2, skip self-present, hand off to Present detour which draws only inside Present when backbuffer is guaranteed PRESENT state). (4) GX classifier consumer guard on FUN_141e90290 (RVA 0x1e90290, NOT 0xe90290 - watch the digit): null_hits=71, that crash GONE. KEY DIAGNOSTIC: Present hook installed + composite DISABLED = 60s CLEAN (no crash); composite drawing = crash. So our concurrent GPU composite submit races the game's own profile-offscreen render (advance-before-seed), producing a null GX resource (rcx=0x20) dispatched to MULTIPLE consumers. REMAINING CRASH (needs Ghidra/deobf host, absent on /home/choza): a SECOND consumer at deobf 0x141e862fd (fault +0x38, rcx=0x20), reached via a DIFFERENT chain than FUN_141a853a0->FUN_141a02de0->0x141e90290. 0x1e862fd crash stack (deobf): 0x141e862fd<-0x141e87eac<-0x141e86db3<-0x141e97b2a<-0x141e883b7 (all 0x141e8/e9 GX cluster). My FUN_141a853a0 @ 0x1a853a0 source-guard did NOT fire (skip_hits=0) = wrong chokepoint; REMOVED it. NEXT (RE host): decompile the 0x141e8xxxx GX cluster to find the true dispatch chokepoint that loads wrapper+0x40 and passes to BOTH 0x141e90290 and 0x141e862fd; guard THAT (or all consumer entries). The consumer-guard PATTERN is proven. Alt lead: test WITHOUT Special K (reduces GPU contention + swapchain wrapping).

title: Windows no-input crash in GX offscreen resource classifier (FUN_141e90290 rcx=0x20)
description:
User-provided Windows crash report for md5 32083d17: repeated access violation after no input at game+0x1e90290 / deobf 0x141e90290. Static RE: crash function is `mov eax,[rcx+0x10]; and eax,0x3f`; rcx=0x20 means caller passed null resource + 0x20. Stack maps to GX/offscreen command path `FUN_141a853a0 -> FUN_141a02de0 -> FUN_1419e3080 -> FUN_1419e35c0 -> FUN_141ac63d0 -> FUN_141e90290`; native `FUN_141a853a0` checks four offscreen wrapper pointers but not wrapper+0x40 before `FUN_141a02de0` blindly loads it. Agent fix adds a guard before our extra profile draw: all four `off+0x40/48/50/58` wrappers and each `wrapper+0x40` must be non-null, plus telemetry `oracle_portrait_pump_block_off_resource`.

Local smoke build md5 2135746709c14309b1c86333dd120598: `/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/windows-null-gx-resource-guard-20260714`. No access violation in crash log; guard fired (`oracle_portrait_pump_block_off_resource=215`, `off_resource_bad=215`, `oracle_portrait_pump_draws=0`) and watcher timed out before world-stable, so Windows/user-method retest remains needed before calling it fully proven.

title: Fix main-commit guard when branch signal is missing
description: Guard gap observed 2026-07-13 in er-effects-rs: agent ran git add ... && git commit while current branch was main. .cupcake/policies/claude/git_block_main_commit.rego only denies when input.signals.current_branch resolves to main; live Pi Bash invocation did not provide that signal, so current_branch fell back to empty string and the guard allowed the commit. The commit was preserved on fix/quickload-wwise-mute and local main was reset to origin/main before pushing the feature branch. Fix should make main-commit enforcement fail closed when branch signal is absent or add live hook signal coverage plus regression test for missing current_branch.
close_reason: Fixed: project hook now loads .cupcake/rulebook.yml current_branch signal, git_block_main_commit declares required_signals, and OPA tests cover missing/empty branch signal.
