title: [bug] Return-to-title crash: 05_000_Title MenuWindowJob dtor UAF on owningMenuWindow (rva 0x7ada87)
description:
SYMPTOM (crash log 2026-07-08, fresh main@4da4084 DLL built 12:37): access-violation rva=0x7ada87 (game+0x7ada87), NtTerminateProcess 0xc0000005. Reproduced 2/5 runs in er-effects-autoload-debug.log, both ~1ms after the SECOND '05_000_Title' MenuWindowJob build in the run.
  - Run 4 (no manual save-swap): autoload -> in-world (Maddened Bean lvl125, SetState5 AUTOSAVES @17.5s) -> in-world MessageBoxDialog @33.5s -> 2nd 05_000_Title build @37.845s (job 0x17c3d15c0) -> CRASH.
  - Run 5 (manual save-swap): system-quit -> ProfileSelect confirm slot6 -> return-title -> SetState(owner,2) committed_was=6 @134.232s -> 2nd 05_000_Title build @134.313s (job 0x2da5fbc0) -> CRASH @134.314s.

ROOT CAUSE (static RE via Ghidra dump, confirmed): the crash is in NATIVE game code, in CS::MenuWindowJob::~MenuWindowJob (dump FUN_1407ac810 / deobf 0x1407ac810). The dtor sets the vtable, then calls the finalize helper FUN_1407adb30 (deobf 0x1407ada40). Finalize reads this->owningMenuWindow (non-null), calls its vfptr[3]() to get an input-event descriptor, passes it to the event-table getter (deobf 0x140749960: rax = table_base 0x143d6a860 + (*(i32*)descriptor)*0x60), then does 'movups (%rax),%xmm2' at 0x7ada87. The descriptor's first i32 index is GARBAGE (fault_addr ~0x314256a860 => index ~137M), so the table pointer is wildly OOB => AV read.

MECHANISM: use-after-free. owningMenuWindow is a dangling/freed pointer at destruct time (its vfptr[3] dispatches through freed memory and returns a readable-but-garbage descriptor). The job being destroyed is the FIRST-boot 05_000_Title job that our title-cover masquerade preserves+pumps (part-a BeginTitle wrapper hook 0x14081f9f0 'native 05_000_Title MenuWindowJob will be replaced by 05_010_ProfileSelect'; part-b MenuWindowJob::Run pump 0x1407ad1c0; FadeIn helper 0x140744dd0). On the SECOND BeginTitle (return-to-title rebuild) the native machinery destroys the stale first job whose owningMenuWindow we let dangle -> crash. Common trigger across both runs: returning to the title screen after being in-world while the masquerade is active.

NOTE: the game's OWN sibling CS::MenuWindowJob::Run (0x1407ad2b0) guards the identical event-table index with 'if (index < 0x47)' before touching CSMenuMan->field106_0x90[index]; the dtor finalize FUN_1407adb30's FIRST getter deref has NO such guard.

FIX OPTIONS:
  A. Prevent the dangling: release our part-a latch/ref and stop part-b pumping the first title job before the 2nd BeginTitle, or null owningMenuWindow when we detect it is freed, so the native 'if (pMVar1 != 0)' finalize block is skipped.
  B. Targeted guard: hook FUN_1407adb30 (or the getter usage) and skip the event-table deregister when owningMenuWindow's event index is out of range (>= 0x47), mirroring the bounds check the game already applies in Run. Existing 'scaleform-handler-guard live-set double-free guard' (0x1411a8870/0x1411a8900) is analogous but does NOT cover this MenuWindowJob dtor.

VALIDATION CONSTRAINT: user directive 2026-07-07 -- no auto-teardown runtime probes; manual/no-teardown (RUNTIME_NO_TEARDOWN=1) only. Evidence: GAME_DIR/er-effects-crash-log.txt (2 AV entries), er-effects-autoload-debug.log runs 4-5.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: FIX IMPLEMENTED (branch fix/return-to-title-menuwindowjob-dtor-uaf): chose option B (targeted guard). Hooked CS::MenuWindowJob::~MenuWindowJob (deobf 0x1407ac720) with menu_window_job_dtor_hook. At dtor entry it reads owningMenuWindow (job+0x130) and its cached menu_id (window+0x180); if the id is unreadable OR out of the game's own valid range (>= 0x47 and != 0xffff unmapped sentinel), the window is freed/garbage, so it nulls job+0x130 before forwarding. That makes the native finalize (0x1407ada40) skip the event-table deref that AVs at 0x7ada87 AND makes the dtor's later unref (0x1407ac750) skip the freed window. All 4 arg registers (rcx/rdx/r8/r9) forwarded verbatim -- verified by disasm that the dtor passes rdx/r8/r9 to finalize untouched. Never false-positives on a live teardown (no live menu has menu_id >= 0x47; field106_0x90 has only 0x47 entries). Telemetered: MENU_WINDOW_JOB_DTOR_FREED_WINDOW_GUARDS counter + a crash-log line 'menu-window-job-guard: FREED owningMenuWindow ...' written to er-effects-crash-log.txt (the same file the user checks). Files: constants/autoload_state.rs (consts+statics), startup_hooks/system_quit_ownership_repro.rs (hook+install), startup_hooks/layout_global_hooks.rs (install call). Residual risk: a freed+reused window whose +0x180 coincidentally holds a value < 0x47 would slip the guard, but that's no worse than today and would also pass the game's own bounds check. Release build + scripts/check.sh: pending confirmation. Needs manual RUNTIME_NO_TEARDOWN validation (return-to-title flow) per 2026-07-07 directive.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
RUNTIME RUN #1 (guard v1) INCONCLUSIVE + iterated to v2. User ran the return-to-title repro (picker-boot, no configured save; loaded 2nd char 'angrE' via system-quit ProfileSelect slot0 -> return-title) and crashed at the SAME point: debug log ends at the 2nd '05_000_Title' build (+47576ms), identical to the pre-fix runs 4/5. Guard v1 INSTALLED (+252ms) but stayed INERT (0 FREED lines), and NO crash log was written.

ROOT of the missing rva: crash_logger_enabled() was opt-in (env ER_EFFECTS_CRASH_LOG / ER_EFFECTS_CRASH_LOG_PATH, or the er-effects-crash-log.txt sentinel file existing). I had rotated that sentinel to .bak before the run, which DISABLED the VEH crash logger -- so the crash produced no rva. My mistake, not a boot-path difference.

GUARD v2 (this build, DLL md5 6077c85d263a1faaf160b80ba6c6aeb9):
1. crash_logger_enabled() -> ALWAYS true (user directive 2026-07-08). Non-fatal diagnostic VEH (EXCEPTION_CONTINUE_SEARCH); does not change game behavior. deliberate_fail_fast stays separate opt-in.
2. Strengthened the dtor guard predicate: freed = (menu_id unreadable | menu_id>=0x47 & !=0xffff) OR (refcount unreadable | refcount<=0 | refcount>0x100000). refcount is DLReferenceCountObject.referenceCount (int at window+0x8, Ghidra-verified; the job holds a ref so a live window is >=1). This catches a freed window whose +0x180 coincidentally looks like a valid menu_id -- the residual false-negative in v1.
3. Added a capped (<=3000) per-dtor diagnostic debug line capturing job/window/menu_id/refcount/vtable/freed for EVERY non-null owningMenuWindow at ~MenuWindowJob, so the crash-time dtor's window state is on record even if the guard forwards.

Next run is now decisive: (a) FREED line + survives = fixed; (b) no FREED + access-violation rva=0x7ada87 = guard still false-negatived (need identity live-set) -- and the per-dtor line shows why; (c) different rva = different crash. release build + check.sh: build OK, check pending.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
RUN #3 (guard v2) result + PIVOT to root-cause prevention. Run #3 (crash logger now always-on): SAME access-violation rva=0x7ada87. The v2 per-dtor diagnostic captured the crashing window's exact state: job=0x11b47700 owningMenuWindow=0x21067080 menu_id=0xffff refcount=1 vtable=0x1429ce1d8 -- and it is the LIVE title dialog (== title owner+0xe0 in the SetState(2) trace). It is NOT freed. Every SURVIVING menu dtor this run also had refcount=1 / menu_id=0xffff; the only distinguishing factor was the class vtable. So no dtor liveness check can identify the crashing window, and (user directive) recovering from the fault after it happens is 'escaping', not preventing.

ROOT CAUSE (static RE): the shared leaf getter FUN_140749960 computes EVENT_TABLE(0x143d6a860)+idx*0x60 with NO bounds check. Its callers deref the result. The sibling CS::MenuWindowJob::Run guards the idx with '< 0x47'; the ~MenuWindowJob finalize (0x1407ada40) does NOT. During return-to-title the finalize runs on the title window whose vfptr[3] leaves the descriptor's idx UNINITIALISED (stack garbage ~0x51EBA95), so the getter returns a wild pointer and the caller's 'movups (%rax)' AVs at 0x7ada87. Both the first getter and the menu_id==0xffff second getter go through the same leaf.

FIX (PREVENTION, replaces the dtor guard which is fully removed): hook FUN_140749960; read the same i32 the getter reads; for a valid idx (0..0x1000) forward byte-identically; for an OOB idx return a private always-mapped 0x60 sentinel entry (all 0xFF) instead of the wild pointer. Every caller's own sign/'< 0x47' guard then sees the negative/huge sentinel and skips the invalid op -- the bad deref NEVER executes, and the finalize still unref's the window on its normal path (no leak). Universal (covers Run + both finalize getters). crash_logger_enabled() stays always-on. DLL md5 2f4bf72fd1de58263ee0cf7863f4a127 (built 13:46). release build + check.sh: PASS. Awaiting manual re-run of the load-second-character/return-to-title repro.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
RUN #4 (getter-sanitizer) result + added complementary dtor guard. Crash MOVED to rva=0x7adb28 (the finalize's SECOND vfptr[3] virtual call at 0x7adb28: 'call *0x18(%rax)'). Crash log: window=0x1f2c7080 vtable=0x1f2c7e80 -- vtable is a HEAP address BELOW the game base, i.e. the window is FREED AND REUSED (a different mode than run #3, whose window had a valid game vtable 0x1429ce1d8). The getter-sanitizer's OOB path fired 0 times (the crash is the virtual CALL, before the getter), confirming this mode is structurally uncatchable at the getter.

Two complementary crash points in the SAME finalize, both 'finalize run on an invalid owningMenuWindow during return-to-title':
 - mode A (run #3, rva 0x7ada87): game vtable + garbage descriptor index -> OOB event-table DEREF. Prevented by EVENT_TABLE_GETTER sanitizer.
 - mode B (run #4, rva 0x7adb28): heap/garbage vtable (freed-reused) -> virtual CALL faults. Prevented by the new ~MenuWindowJob dtor guard: if owningMenuWindow's vtable is not in the game module, null job+0x130 so the finalize skips its window block (and does NOT unref reused memory). Live windows always have an in-module vtable -> zero false positive; a freed-but-not-reused window (stale game vtable) passes here and is covered by mode A instead.

DLL md5 75fe34a795f5921550cf6bdbfc2659d1 (built 13:56). Both guards installed via install_event_table_index_getter_guard + install_menu_window_job_dtor_guard. release build + check.sh PASS. Awaiting manual re-run. NOTE: if the crash next morphs to the finalize's window UNREF (AtomicDecrement window+0x8) that would be a freed-not-reused mode C -> would need a MenuWindow live-set for definitive freed-detection; watch for that rva.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
RUN #5 (getter-sanitizer + dtor vtable-guard) result + PIVOT to root-level whole-block skip. Getter sanitizer FIRED (event-table-guard: OOB index Some(2147221504)=0x7FFF0000 from descriptor=0x3bc0200 -> returned sentinel) so rva 0x7ada87 was prevented -- but crash MOVED to rva=0x7adb28 (second vfptr[3] virtual call). Crash log this run: window=0x26a6ee80 vtable=0x0 (NULL) fault_addr=0x18. The dtor vtable-guard did NOT fire (window vtable was valid/in-module at dtor entry -- the first vfptr[3] at 0x7ada7c succeeded -- and only appeared null by 0x7adb28). Also discovered the getter-sentinel approach is fundamentally unsafe: the finalize CONSUMES the event-table entry as a refcounted CSScaleformLoadResult (later AddRef on entry[0]), so any sentinel value is either AddRef'd (crash) or enters the CSMenuMan block; there is NO safe sentinel. Confirmed the user's 'whack-a-mole' warning: patching individual derefs just moves the crash.

ROOT-LEVEL FIX (replaces BOTH prior guards, which are removed): at ~MenuWindowJob (dtor 0x1407ac720), for an unmapped window (menu_id==0xffff, the state all crashes were in), REPRODUCE the finalize's own call owningMenuWindow->vfptr[3](window,&scratch) and read the descriptor's first i32 (the event index). If the vtable/ vfptr[3] is not in the game module (freed+reused) OR the index is out of [0,0x1000) (doomed), null job+0x130 so the finalize's ENTIRE 'if(owningMenuWindow!=0)' block is skipped -- both virtual calls, both getters, the entry-consume, and the window unref. No 'next deref' to hop to. Gated to 0xffff so healthy mapped windows are byte-identical (no extra vf3 call). New behavior/risk: one extra vf3 getter call per unmapped-window dtor (same method the game calls a beat later; expected side-effect-free). DLL md5 229a71609e638fd824068b38aa9b772f (built 14:08). release build + check.sh PASS. Awaiting manual re-run.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
RUN #6 result + ROOT CAUSE FOUND (static RE). Run #6: my doomed-guard FIRED correctly (nulled owningMenuWindow, same garbage index 0x7FFF0000) so finalize skipped its block -- but crash MOVED to rva=0x733f80 with rcx=0x255eee80[vt=0x0] = the SAME window my guard flagged. 0x733f80 = FUN_140734020 (called by CS::TitleStep::STEP_MenuJobWait) iterating the title-step's active-window DLFixedVector and calling vtable[2] on each entry; the freed window is still IN that vector.

ROOT CAUSE: the finalize's OWN cleanup removes the window from its push-target vector via FUN_140733e70(job->field2_0x50, window) at deobf 0x1407adbf5 -- but that call sits AFTER the crashing getter (0x7ada87). So the getter crash (or my null skipping the whole block) means the removal never runs, and the window dangles in the vector STEP_MenuJobWait walks -> AV. Verified statically: MenuWindowJob::Run pushes owningMenuWindow into *(job+0x50) (FUN_140733ff0), the finalize removes it from *(job+0x50) (FUN_140733e70), and STEP_MenuJobWait walks that same vector -- so *(job+0x50) == the crashing vector. FUN_140733e70 is a pure pointer-remove+compact (reads count at vector+0x48, never derefs the window vtable) -> safe on a doomed window.

FIX (producer-side, replaces the whack-a-mole): in the ~MenuWindowJob doomed branch, DO the removal ourselves -- FUN_140733e70(*(job+0x50), window) (deobf 0x140733d70), guarded by a readable+sane count (0<count<=64) check -- THEN null owningMenuWindow. This removes the dangling entry (fixes STEP_MenuJobWait 0x733f80) AND skips the getter crashes (0x7ada87/0x7adb28). Timing verified: dtor (+34596ms) runs before STEP_MenuJobWait (+34611ms), so the vector is clean when walked. DLL md5 bddb0de9a058a312d05f678d67c0e5a1 (built 14:30). release build + check.sh PASS. Awaiting user manual re-run (I cannot self-drive the system-quit->load-profile->confirm->return-title menu sequence).

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
QUIT-TO-DESKTOP CLEAN KILL (user directive 2026-07-08, after j74t fix confirmed). Run with the j74t fix held (guard fired list_removed=true, no 0x7ada87/0x7adb28/0x733f80) but unmasked a pre-existing QUIT crash: on quit->return-to-title the world teardown unloads the MenuOffscrRendParam param table, then the rebuilt TitleTopDialog's CSMenuProfModelRend (0x140bbe010) -> LookupMenuOffscrRendParam -> GetParamResCap(repo,MenuOffscrRendParam,0)==NULL -> DLPanic at MenuOffscrRendParam.cpp:0x23 (crash rva 0x1eb9999 via 0x1409af43f/0x140bbdf66/0x140d3exxx). User wants quit-to-desktop to save+kill fast, not the slow native teardown/crash.

FIX (chosen via AskUserQuestion: after-confirm + save-then-kill): hook the inner LookupMenuOffscrRendParam (deobf 0x140d3ed90) and reproduce the game's table-presence check (repo *(base+0x3d81ee8); GetParamResCap@0x140d4cc50(repo, type=0x4e, 0)); when the table is ABSENT (only on a quit teardown -- resident through loads, proven by the 3 successful loads) ExitProcess(0) for a clean fast exit instead of the DLPanic. Native confirm dialog untouched (teardown runs post-Yes); native quit-save issued before the rebuild + ExitProcess flushes file buffers = save-then-kill. Self-distinguishes quit (table gone -> kill) vs load (table resident -> forward). DLL md5 80da0442344735a15ea3ab08556c3bc3 (built 15:16). release build + check.sh PASS. Awaiting user validation: clean exit + save persistence. Residual: if the native save isn't flushed by the exit point, add an explicit save-flush wait; if ExitProcess hangs on CRT/DLL cleanup, switch to a hard kill.

title: Back from custom Load Profile menus returns to Quit tab
description: User report: from System > Quit custom rows, pressing Back in either Load Profile or Load Save Profiles currently collapses/escapes too far instead of returning to the previous menu. Expected previous menu is always the OptionSetting Quit tab. Investigation points at system_quit_restore_real_system_windows restoring only IngameTop and deliberately leaving OptionSetting hidden after ProfileSelect closes. Static Ghidra evidence says native ProfileSelect/child-menu Back is MenuJobResult::Failed via FUN_1407ac980 and MenuWindowJob::Run cleanup only removes the child owning MenuWindow from its window list; parent windows stay in place. Need restore/repair OptionSetting Quit tab on custom ProfileSelect backout, including save-picker backout, without reintroducing stale/cross-populated pane bugs.
close_reason: Fixed: Back from custom Load Profile / Load Save Profiles returns to Quit tab with correct Quit pane contents; validated by user live check and release build/checks.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Implemented second-pass fix after user live report: Back now reached the Quit tab label but showed the tab immediately to the left. Runtime telemetry from artifact /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/manual-no-teardown-quit-tab-back-20260708-105603 showed pane-reapply selected cache slot 8 for visual tab 8, while natural Quit rows were on dialog 0x18b0fc080 at cache slot 9; cache slot 8/dialog 0x18b0e2080 was the left tab content. Fixed reapply to keep visual tab index 8 for the tab strip but use composite pane_index=tab+1 for composite+0xb8, native refresh, and SetVisible. Checks passed: cargo fmt --all -- --check; bash scripts/check.sh; cargo xwin build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc. Relaunched with fresh DLL via me3 no-teardown artifact /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/manual-no-teardown-quit-tab-paneindex-20260708-110112; DLL attached/env/game task/telemetry all true, watcher still fails portrait_window_publish_failure, game left running PID 684434.

title: In-game native file-picker menus replacing both OS GetOpenFileNameW dialogs
description: Goal (user, 2026-07-07): replace (1) the startup missing-save OS picker (path_hooks.rs open_missing_save_file_picker + MessageBoxW prompt on helper thread) and (2) the system-quit Load Save Profiles OS picker (system_quit_dialog_handlers.rs system_quit_open_env_save_dir) with in-game menus embedded in the game's visual system. Both must: filter .sl2 vs .co2 by seamless residency, remember last opened save dir (preferred_save_picker_dir in er-effects.toml already exists for site 1), share design language, and eliminate OS window context switches. Branch: feat/game-native-file-picker (worktree .worktrees/game-native-file-picker).
notes:
2026-07-07: Both pickers implemented + staged for user runtime test. Site B (System>Quit Load Save Profiles): OS dialog replaced by 05_010 directory-browser rows (synthetic ProfileSummary records, flags set per RE; in-place rebuild 0x9a4ed0 for navigation; pick -> ingest pipeline -> reopens as slot view). Site A (startup missing-save): Win32 MessageBox/OFN flow deleted; natural no-save title boot (save job passes through; SetState denies only 4/5 while pending); auto-open fires native Load Game row when menu interactive; pick -> validate+BND4 -> save redirect installed -> native SetState(2) title reload. Both share preferred_save_picker_dir memory (session+toml) and expected_save_extension() (ersc latch + ER_EFFECTS_SAVE_MODE_HINT tiebreak). oracle_save_picker_* telemetry added. Branch feat/game-native-file-picker commits ca846fa+52bab5e; DLL 5a25fd1132092338 staged to main checkout target path for ~/Elden/launch.sh. Worktree gotchas fixed: vendor/ symlink + third_party submodule init (check.sh was silently failing before). Cupcake false-positive (commit message mentioning the Seamless DLL name) being fixed by background agent.
SPEC CLARIFICATION (user, 2026-07-07) for the STARTUP no-save picker — the acceptance behavior, must match the old OS-picker flow exactly except in-game: (1) the picker must come up PRIOR to the DLL-built 'checking for the save file' loading bar/cover surface; (2) it must PAUSE all threads that depend on a save being present (the hold IS desired, same as the OS flow blocked save IO + held SetState + CONTINUE-looped the save-data ShowProgressJob), keeping the full product_autoload suppression stack ARMED (press-start hidden, native menu suppressed, audio muted via IN_WORLD_REACHED!=YES); (3) when the user selects a save, loading RESUMES. WRONG (current) approach: letting the title boot proceed to a native menu (audio/menu run) and firing a nonexistent Load Game row. Audio 'regression' was a MISATTRIBUTION — user heard normal in-world audio from a SAVE-PRESENT autoload run; the mute hook (layout_global_hooks sound_post_event_core_hook, muted=IN_WORLD_REACHED!=YES) is untouched and only unmutes when a real player exists in-world. Open RE: is the cover-built 05_010 ProfileSelect renderable AT the held save-check stage, or must the picker draw on the same custom render layer as the 'loading bar we built' (title/loading cover surface)? Workflow wf_b048532e-0ed mapping this.
2026-07-07 STATUS: Both pickers working and user-confirmed A-grade. Site A (startup no-save): DLL-drawn overlay file browser composited on the boot loading bar (gpu_readback/save_picker_overlay.rs), OS-read input (GetAsyncKeyState + XInputGetState), boot held at save-check via the save-data ShowProgressJob CONTINUE-loop while missing_save pending (product_autoload stays armed; own_stepper_enabled gated dynamically on missing_save); pick installs redirect -> clears pending -> hold releases -> autoload resumes; overlay disarms. SAVE CHECK bar marker moved to the real pause (570 permille) with the fill clamped there so tick+fill coincide; SAVE CHECK/SAVE LOAD labels gated to never overlap. Site B (in-world System>Quit Load Save Profiles): native 05_010 directory browser (unchanged, works in-world where menu assets exist). Empty-container default-save guard added (native no-save boot creates a full-size empty ER0000). Both share preferred_save_picker_dir memory + expected extension (seamless latch + ER_EFFECTS_SAVE_MODE_HINT). Branch feat/game-native-file-picker, PR #40, all commits pushed through 633c1ed. Merged origin/main (four-button quit menu) mid-work.

title: System->Quit load-target confirm no-ops then Back restores Graphics-tab view into Quit menu
description:
User-reported manual repro (2026-07-07):
1. Start the game with an `.sl2` file.
2. After the character loads in, open/load a new save file.
3. Navigate to the screen where the next load target can be picked from the opened save file.
4. Try to confirm load on any character: confirmation has no effect.
5. Press Back.
6. The Quit Game menu is now populated with the view from the adjacent Graphics tab instead of the initial customized Quit Game menu we navigated from.

Impact:
- New character load fails.
- UI enters an unexpected but recoverable state.

Initial classification:
- Related to the already-open repeat save/slot switch family (`er-effects-rs-f2t`), but distinct symptom: no crash/fail-fast reported here; instead the native menu stack/view state appears mismatched after a failed confirm and Back.
- Not enough evidence to attribute to `feature/experiments-runtime-seams`. A closely related repeat-switch issue predates that branch (er-effects-rs-f2t, 2026-07-05), so treat this as likely pre-existing until reproduced against main vs the lifecycle branch.
- Needs a focused repro/probe around System->Quit->Load-Profile menu stack ownership, selected save source, confirm action dispatch, and back-navigation view restoration.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: User directive during investigation (2026-07-07): do not run any more auto-teardown runtime probes for these bugs; the user needs to test manually. Future runtime validation on this branch should be no-auto-teardown/manual-control only (e.g. RUNTIME_NO_TEARDOWN=1), and should not use watcher-owned teardown unless the user explicitly asks.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Investigation patch on feature/experiments-runtime-seams:
- c2ds: publish-failure attribution now resets `PROFILE_RT_SRV_COPIES_WINDOW` per loading window and maps formerly `cause=0` failures to actionable classes: checker, multi-model, unpaired provenance, no RT->SRV copy, or no color/depth provenance. Reset log now includes copies/provenance.
- pl4u: ProfileLoad job Run fallback now directly native-closes ProfileSelect after save-safe block and latches `SYSTEM_QUIT_PROFILESELECT_NATIVE_CLOSE_FIRED`, so the flow does not depend on a later confirm-lambda callback. Direct slot activation close path now sets the same latch. Save-swap selected-slot prepare path logs its silent skip states.
- Script: `RUNTIME_NO_TEARDOWN=1 --dry-run` now accurately reports no readiness watcher / no agent-owned teardown.
Validation: `bash scripts/check.sh` passed; release build passed (`cargo xwin build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc`). No runtime auto-teardown probes were run after the user directive; manual validation still required.
Manual no-teardown command shape:
`ARTIFACT_DIR=/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/manual-no-teardown-bugtest-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S) RUNTIME_NO_TEARDOWN=1 bash scripts/run-product-continue-direct-probe.sh`

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: User identified the likely root cause of pl4u/no-op load in Seamless: starting Seamless without an active save opens the missing-save picker, but it allows picking `.sl2`; later the in-game save picker also only offers `.sl2`. In Seamless mode the active save should be `.co2`; loading an `.sl2` under Seamless then trying to load another `.sl2` leaves the save-swap/commit path mismatched and explains the observed no-op/Access denied failures. Fix should make Seamless picker/filter/default target `.co2` and prevent `.sl2` selection in Seamless unless explicitly converting/copying to `.co2`.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Guard retrospective: while fixing the Seamless `.sl2`/`.co2` picker mismatch, the first implementation used a sleep-based ERSC settle loop and was correctly blocked by `scripts/check.sh` no-timeouts policy. Replaced it with a concrete launch/profile save-mode hint. That introduced a new `ER_EFFECTS_*` env var and was correctly blocked by the env-gate allowlist; because this pre-ERSC missing-save picker genuinely needs a signal known before `ersc.dll` is PEB-registered, the fix deliberately sanctioned `ER_EFFECTS_SAVE_MODE_HINT` in `.auto/env_gate_comment_baseline.json` and added the required ENV-GATE RATIONALE comment above the enclosing function. Also updated `/home/banon/Elden/launch.sh` dry-run/smoke path to pass `seamless` for `-s` and `vanilla` otherwise.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Manual user retest after a CO2 initial load and a CO2 System->Quit profile selection still stalled at ProfileSelect. Current log evidence shows the picker mode was fixed (`ER_EFFECTS_SAVE_MODE_HINT=seamless`, `.co2`), but confirm still failed because System->Quit save-swap armed the active/original path as appdata `ER0000.sl2`:

`system-quit-save-swap: FAILED to commit foreign save for slot 0 path='C:\users\steamuser\AppData\Roaming\EldenRing\76561197986456766\ER0000.sl2': Access denied. (os error 5); blocking activation to avoid loading stale/original bytes`

Root cause: the missing-save/direct-file picker source was stored in `SAVE_DIRECT_SOURCE_FILE`, but `system_quit_env_save_path()` ignored it and re-derived an appdata default via `configured_or_default_save_file()`. In a Seamless/manual direct-file flow this writes to stale `.sl2` instead of the active picked `.co2`, so confirm appears to no-op and ProfileSelect job loops.

Patch: add `active_save_file_for_system_quit()` resolving direct-file source first, then configured/default source; System->Quit now uses it. Default save discovery also honors the save-mode hint for `.co2` first under `-s`. Commit/recommit writes now clear read-only attributes before overwrite to avoid the same Access denied class on read-only save files.

Validation: `bash scripts/check.sh` and `cargo xwin build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc` passed. No runtime probe launched.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Checkpoint 2026-07-07: added boot-progress `SAVE LOAD` marker at the save-data pause before existing `NATIVE` handoff; fixed missing-save picker resume deadlock. Root cause: picker helper thread opened/read the user-selected source save while `MISSING_SAVE_DIALOG_STATE=PENDING`, and the save IO hook blocked that same picker thread before it could publish READY. Fix marks the missing-save picker helper thread as picker-owned so its own selected-source metadata/read/copy operations skip the pending gate; native/game save IO still blocks until READY/CANCELLED. Also moved picker resolution to publish READY immediately after staging the selected source rather than waiting on a possible MinHook installer `Once` contention. Validation: `cargo fmt --all`; `bash scripts/check.sh`; `python3 scripts/test-cupcake-policies.py`; `cargo xwin build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc`. DLL rebuilt at `/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/release/er_effects_rs.dll`. Manual/user-assisted picker smoke still required for end-to-end UI because the real file picker needs user selection unless a deterministic picker-driver is added.

title: Same-slot foreign-save switch reloads the resident character (return-title save clobbers the commit)
description:
User-reported 2026-07-06 (run seamless-save-smoke-20260706-144801, two different .co2 picks): choosing a foreign save's character in the SAME slot index as the resident character reloads the resident character -- the actual world character, not just the loading screen. FACE-IDENTITY MISMATCH #1 fired at the build kick (the new semaphore caught it in RAM).

ROOT CAUSE (log-proven): system_quit_save_swap_prepare_selected_slot commits the foreign bytes at slot activation (+98.921s), then the native return-title chain sets saveRequested and the game SAVES (+99.31 bc4=1 -> +99.34 save_state=1) -- rewriting the ACTIVE slot + profile summary into the active file over the commit. A same-slot pick therefore deserializes the original character; different-slot picks always survived because only the active slot's USER_DATA entry is rewritten (this is why every earlier switch QA passed).

FIX: re-commit the candidate bytes at the bc4-terminal point in title_tick_cover.rs (save provably finished, fresh deserialize still seconds away) via system_quit_save_swap_recommit_after_return_title_save(), idempotent per switch (recommitted latch, reset at each activation commit). No data loss: System-Quit already saved the old character into their own file before ProfileSelect; the return-title re-save was landing in the wrong (foreign) file anyway. Related: er-effects-rs-7q7 (timing-dependent save-dir).
close_reason: Fixed + runtime-validated twice (runs seamless-runtime-smoke-20260706-150435 and seamless-co2fix2-smoke-20260706-151021): same-slot foreign pick now loads the picked character (Da BEAST slot0 over resident slot0). Fix = re-commit the candidate bytes at bc4-terminal in title_tick_cover.rs after the game's return-title save finishes (system_quit_save_swap_recommit_after_return_title_save, idempotent via recommitted latch). Log shows commit -> game save -> RE-commit ordering both runs.

title: Loading portrait shows previous character on System-Quit switch load (stale bake bridge)
description:
User-reported 2026-07-06 (run jsm-slotstats2-switchqa-20260706-125259): second character load's loading screen kept showing character 1's animated head; stats text (fixed same day) updated correctly.

EVIDENCE (RAM/pixel, no screenshot reading): window-2 published 192 clean keyed frames from the correctly-kicked slot-1 renderer (kick #2, model live, anim bound), yet the window-2 2056x2056 RT dump structure-correlates 0.92 (48x48 luma grid) and 0.98 palette-intersects with window-1's character-1 dump -- same model, different anim phase. Telemetry: oracle_profile_renderer_spare_hits=1 (teardown-spare hook fired only at window 1), oracle_profile_spare_orphans_deleted=0, oracle_ownership_spared_outstanding=1 at run end.

ROOT CAUSE: loading_portrait_window_reset parks the spared prior-character renderer in PROFILE_SPARE_ORPHAN, whose only reclaim site was profile_renderer_teardown_spare_hook (native CSMenuProfModelRend teardown-all) -- which the System-Quit switch path never fires. The orphan's model+offscreen scene stayed registered, rendering char 1's head every frame through window 2; the readback published it. Related but distinct from er-effects-rs-qh7 (ac0 lag, addressed by portrait_target_slot).

FIX: reclaim the orphan (delay_delete_enqueue_renderer + ownership_release) at the switch confirm in system_quit_arm_quickload_autoload, with a backstop in maybe_build_profile_table_for_loading before the new window's builder runs; swap(0) keeps sites mutually exclusive; the teardown-spare hook reclaim remains for the Continue path.
close_reason: Fixed + user visual sign-off (run portrait-faceid2-switchqa-20260706-143508): second loading screen shows the picked foreign character. Root cause was the foreign-preview record writer cloning the ORIGINAL save's record as a template and only overwriting name/level/playtime -- FaceData/ChrAsm stayed the original character's. Fix: walker-based extraction of the foreign character's FaceDataBuffer + runtime-layout ChrAsm image from the save's variable-length sections (fixed runtime offsets false-negatived on every slot), written via native FaceData::CopyFromBuffer + ChrAsm copy. Face-identity semaphore live: oracle_portrait_face_identity_checks=1 mismatches=0 on the passing run. Also shipped in the same arc: clear-on-complete of the published head buffer (no stale bake/bridge), post-draw overlay-landing probe (96-97% head-match), orphan reclaim at switch confirm + table build.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
ROOT-CAUSE CORRECTION (run 2, portrait-orphanfix-switchqa-20260706-131119): the orphaned-spared-renderer theory is REFUTED. Run 2 had spare_hits=0 (no spared renderer ever existed, so no orphan; the confirm/table-build reclaim sites correctly found orphan=0 and the fix code was verified present in the artifact DLL) yet the user saw the identical wrong head.

PIXEL PROOF the readback is INNOCENT: cross-run same-character baseline corr(run1.slot0, run2.slot0)=0.9995 (48x48 luma grid) vs corr(slot0, slot1)=0.92 within each run -- window 2's readback content is OUTSIDE the same-character band, i.e. it IS the new character. The publish pipeline produced char-2 frames (352 fresh overlay re-uploads, 0 GPU failures) while the user saw char 1.

ACTUAL MECHANISM (display side): the now-loading background is forge-baked ONCE per artwork decode from LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_RGBA, which at window-2 decode still held char 1's make-before-break bridge frame (kept by design, user 2026-07-03). maybe_update_gfx_loading_portrait is a no-op (re-forge removed), so the baked char-1 art persists all window. The Present-overlay's char-2 draws have NO on-screen proof (head_probe_count=0; the existing probe is gated on GFX_PORTRAIT_BAKED>0 which stays 0, and samples pre-draw anyway) -- whether the overlay lands is UNVERIFIED.

FIX APPLIED (user-directed 2026-07-06, reversing 2026-07-03 make-before-break): loading_portrait_window_reset now CLEARS LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_RGBA + PROFILE_HAVE_KEYED_FRAME at load completion, so no stale character exists to bake or bridge; next window starts head-less until the new character's first keyed frame. Plus: unconditional POST-draw backbuffer head-match probe (mutually exclusive with the candidate-A pre-draw probe) so the next run yields a RAM semaphore for whether the overlay head actually reaches the presented frame. The orphan reclaim at confirm/table-build is KEPT (real leak when the spare engages: run 1 leaked one undeleted renderer; historical GX-queue overflow at ~switch #4).

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
TRUE ROOT CAUSE FOUND (user visual ID 2026-07-06: rendered dumps showed BANON in all three windows, incl. the slot-1 window). The foreign-save ProfileSelect preview (system_quit_apply_foreign_profile_summary_preview -> write_profile_summary_record) clones a FALLBACK RECORD from the ORIGINAL save's first active slot as a structural template, then overwrites ONLY name/level/playtime/runes/map. The record's FaceData (+0x38) and ChrAsm (+0x1a8) -- what the portrait renderer builds the model from -- stayed the ORIGINAL character's (Banon). So the pipeline faithfully kicked/built/rendered/published the right SLOT with the WRONG character's face+equipment; stats text was correct because name/level ARE overwritten. This also retroactively explains the earlier 0.92-0.98 cross-window pixel correlations (Banon-from-record vs live Banon).

FIX (build-validated, awaiting runtime QA): write_profile_summary_record now fills the real visual blocks from the FOREIGN character's PGD via the game's native helpers -- FaceData::CopyFromBuffer (0x252f70; saved wrapper header differs from live, so never memcpy) + ChrAsm copy (0x245c00) + gender/archetype/gift/c4 bytes -- mirroring the proven seed_profile_summary_slot_from_staged_save; RVAs promoted to shared constants (player_correctness.rs).

FACE-IDENTITY SEMAPHORE (user directive: the run must detect a wrong rendered character itself): the preview stores an FNV-1a64 fingerprint of each written slot's inner FaceDataBuffer (PROFILE_PREVIEW_FACE_HASH, reset at preview start + snapshot restore); kick_target_profile_slot re-hashes the record at build-kick time; drift bumps oracle_portrait_face_identity_mismatches (+ _checks) and logs 'FACE-IDENTITY MISMATCH'. Telemetry-only per default posture; watchers can fail-fast on it.

Kept from earlier this session (all pending the same QA run): clear-on-complete of LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_RGBA (no stale bake/bridge reservoir), post-draw overlay-landing probe, orphan reclaim at confirm/table-build.

title: Loading-screen stats show first character during second character load
description:
User-reported 2026-07-06: the loading-screen player-stats text (er-effects-rs-jsm feature) does not update when loading a second character via System Quit -> Load Profile; character 1's stats render through character 2's whole loading screen.

Root cause (two compounding defects in src/experiments/startup_hooks/stats_loading_text.rs):
1. read_loading_screen_stats() used ac0-first portrait_loaded_slot_confirmed(); during a switch ac0 still names the resident OLD character until deserialize, and the still-resident char-1 PGD name/level-validates against char-1's record -> a 'live' char-1 bitmap builds at window start.
2. maybe_build_stats_text() one-shot STATS_TEXT_LIVE latch re-armed AFTER stats_text_window_reset() (force_profile_render_tick keeps ticking until load_done+cover-down), re-latching char-1 live stats; load 2 then skipped every rebuild.

Fix: slot source switched to the make-before-break priority (SYSTEM_QUIT_QUICKLOAD_SELECTED_SLOT first, then ac0/stepper, then best_active_slot), and the bitmap is now CONTENT-KEYED (rebuild iff formatted lines differ from the cached ones) so stale builds self-heal; no per-window latch. STATS_TEXT_BUILT is now a cumulative build-count oracle.
close_reason: Fixed: content-keyed stats bitmap + selected-slot-first read (commit pending push). User visual QA passed 2026-07-06 on no-teardown switch run jsm-slotstats2-switchqa-20260706-125259: stats updated correctly for the second character.

title: Seamless: input-block never releases when autoload stalls -> user stuck with no control (add watchdog)
description: Audit finding (POSSIBLE) + LIVE-OBSERVED. The DInput8 GetDeviceState + XInputGetState/Ex block (input_block.rs, input_blocker.rs) is armed by default because autoload is armed; it zeroes real input until in-world (release keyed to player presence). Under co-op the autoload stalls on the wrong-save bug and in-world is never reached, so the block never releases -- observed 2026-07-05: player_available=false after 200s, game covered + uncontrollable. FIXES: (1) when seamless_coop_loaded(), do not arm the boot autoload until it is proven against the .co2 flow -- un-arming autoload un-arms the block for free; (2) UNCONDITIONALLY add a bounded watchdog that calls release_input_block_now() if in-world is not reached within N seconds, so a stalled autoload can never leave the user unable to play. simulated_button_presses_total stayed 0 (zero-input contract intact).

title: Seamless: autoload targets ER0000.sl2 not .co2 -> loads default char, stalls, leaves game covered+input-blocked
description: STATUS 2026-07-06 (feature/seamless-compat, commits 7b7cb49 co2-target, ad8853e ToS-suppress+latch, dd5af19 gap-fix): (1) active save extension resolves co2 under co-op via sticky-latched seamless_coop_loaded(); (2) DllMain default-save existence gate now falls back across .sl2/.co2 (default_save_file_names) so a co2-only profile enters DEFAULT-USER-SAVE instead of the boot missing-save picker -- co2-only gap CLOSED; (3) world-load rides native Continue (game reads own save, module-redirected to co2), so our reads are display-only, no contamination. Vanilla path unchanged. All built + fmt-clean + pushed. LIVE co-op validation still PENDING -- blocked on runtime (concurrent vanilla-probe session removes the co-op module); awaiting user go-ahead. Success semaphore: 0 TosTitle builds + boot-view->CONTINUE + co2 read + world, zero MessageBox/input, no crash.
close_reason: DONE + LIVE-VALIDATED 2026-07-06 (feature/seamless-compat b9465d5). Under real Seamless the zero-input autoload loads a real .co2 character to the world: no crash, no ToS, no MessageBox (oracle_msgbox_total_builds=0), no missing-save picker, simulated_button_presses=0. DLL-gated on co-op-module detection (seamless_coop_loaded), no env/config. co2 target + .sl2/.co2 default-save fallback + foreign-SteamID acceptance (validated_save_file_path is existence+size; load normalizes id) all confirmed. Full chain fixed: swapchain crash (fd1bde4) -> co2 target (7b7cb49) -> ToS ctor-suppress+sticky-latch (ad8853e) -> co2 fallback (dd5af19) -> job-sequence advance past ToS + co-op post-PAB MessageBox (b9465d5).

title: Seamless compat: BFS swapchain fallback latches foreign/stale swapchain under ERSC and self-present crashes
description: Crash repro 2026-07-05 21:37 (~/Elden/launch.sh -s, me3 quicksave-seamless profile, ersc.dll updated 21:19, er_effects_rs.dll built 21:05 from loading-screen-refinements@8021296). VEH crash log: AV at +831ms, RIP=0x6ffffc9005eb (dxgi.dll code, 0x405 before resolved Present8=0x6ffffc9009f0), rcx=0 fault_addr=0 read. PDB-symbolized call chain: present_overlay::boot_present_pump -> gpu_readback::composite_boot_progress_self_frame -> composite_boot_progress_inner -> first swapchain method call faults. Debug log delta vs same-build vanilla runs: vanilla latches swapchain 0xc191e0 via g_GxDrawContext chain (tries 34-36, ~+950-1100ms, no BFS line); Seamless run logged 'FOUND game swapchain 0x8d77e0 (vtable=0x6ffffcc388f0) via BFS after 12897 objs depth=12' at +831ms then crashed on first self-present. Root cause: find_game_swapchain_bfs accepts ANY heap object whose vtable[8]/[22] match the shared DXVK CDXGISwapChain vtable; under Seamless, ERSC's own DXGI activity leaves a swapchain-shaped (likely released dummy) object reachable from garbage pointers, its internal state dead -> first GetCurrentBackBufferIndex/GetBuffer derefs null. Fix: never latch a BFS-found object as GAME_SWAPCHAIN; trust only the RE-confirmed g_GxDrawContext chain (BFS removed, throttled miss diagnostics added). Degraded mode on future chain drift = no overlay, never a crash.
close_reason: FIXED + LIVE-VALIDATED (2026-07-05 22:11, feature/seamless-compat commit fd1bde4). Removed the BFS swapchain fallback in find_game_swapchain; the RE-confirmed g_GxDrawContext output-vector chain is now the only accepted source (miss -> None -> retry next frame; degraded mode = no overlay, never a crash). Live proof under Seamless via me3: DLL attached, present-overlay VMT-swap game swapchain 0xc17a40 tries=63 resolved via the chain (NO via-BFS line), boot-pump self-presented, game PRESENT(8) hook fired + handed over cleanly, boot progressed GAME->OFFLINE->TITLE->MENU, process ran 200s+ with ZERO access-violation. Prior crash was AV at +831ms on a dead BFS-latched swapchain object.

title: RE: default remembered sort option per menu
description: Investigate how Elden Ring persists menu sort selections across character loads but resets on process/power cycle, then identify DLL-safe hooks/state writes to default armaments, talismans, and armor menus to order-of-acquisition rather than item type when each menu opens. Static RE first; runtime tracebreakpoint probes only after static candidates are grounded. User has live game available for attach-only probes.
close_reason: Implemented configurable menu sort defaults and user-verified armaments/armor/talismans Order of Acquisition ascending behavior

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Static RE checkpoint for default menu sort investigation:

- Branch: feature/menu-default-sort-order.
- SortMenu builder: dump 0x1407fa410 -> deobf 0x1407fa320, loads GFX 02_032_SortMenu.
- Sort caller flow: row+0x8 is the current/session-persisted sort option. If negative, vanilla resolves MenuEquipTableData[row[0]].+0x10 as the default sort option. The same effective option feeds both the displayed selected option (r8d to SortMenu) and adapter object via 0x14084f1c0, so changing the fallback/default before adapter build keeps UI and comparator consistent.
- Order of Acquisition is FMG GR_MenuText id 6190 / 0x182e and sort/comparator id 0x5140 / 20800. Screenshot labels line up with table type 4: Item Type 6105, Order of Acquisition 6190, Weight 6111, Attack Power 6130, Guarded Damage Negation 6140.
- Static rows: Armaments MenuEquipTableData row 0x29 @ 0x143b37428 has label 40550 and sort type/default +0x10 = 4; Armor row 0x2a @ 0x143b37460 has label 40551 and type/default +0x10 = 6. Talismans likely resolve through category 40509 and sort type 8, but exact row needs one more bounded trace/static mapping.
- Candidate implementation direction: when opening target sort menus, if effective row+0x8 < 0 or row default is item-type, write 0x5140 to row+0x8 before 0x14084f1c0, or patch relevant static row +0x10 fallback(s). Prefer RAM write/hook over data patch until talisman row and unintended categories are proven.

Artifacts:
- /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/subagents/menu-sort-static-context.md
- /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/subagents/menu-sort-assets-and-ids.md

Constructor initializes entries to 0x80005141 (Item Type) except known special defaults; order-of-acquisition is 0x5140. Added one-shot write for sort types [4 armaments, 6 armor, 8 talismans] only when the current low bits are still Item Type, preserving any non-default/user state. Runtime proof: /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/menu-sort-defaults-full-20260704-203234; me3-smoke-verdict reports watcher_pass=true, dll_attach=true, game_task=true, crash_free=true. title: Repeat slot switch trips portrait identity fail-fast
description:
Repro/context from no-teardown manual run:
- User loaded a save from another arbitrary save file, then after the character loaded in, tried to load another slot from that same save file.
- Crash occurred on fade-out / second load transition.

Artifact:
/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/no-teardown-loose-same-dll-save-20260704-192759

Evidence:
- Process was absent after the user-reported crash.
- No er-effects-crash.log was written, but the last DLL debug line is a portrait identity fail-fast:
  PORTRAIT-IDENTITY-SEMAPHORE FAIL: target_slot=0 record(name_len=5 map=0xe000000) vs loaded(name_len=16 map=0x23000000) name_match=false map_mismatch=true
- Immediately before that, the second continue/load path fired:
  system-quit-quickload: fresh picked-slot deserialize OK #2 slot=0 -- forwarding continue_confirm so SetState5 streams
- Source in src/experiments/startup_hooks/loading_cover_save_slot.rs documents that this semaphore deliberately faults when crash logging is enabled.

Initial classification:
- Not evidence against initial arbitrary save-file direct_file redirect: first smoke/load passed and direct staging was valid.
- Likely repeatable profile-switch/portrait identity path bug: after switching to another slot from the same configured save, the target slot record and live loaded character identity diverge, tripping the fail-fast during fade/stream.
- Needs a focused fix/probe around slot selection/record targeting for repeated switches, not save redirect fallback.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Implemented arbitrary configured save-file support on refactor/file-size-sanity.

Behavior change:
- `save_file` may now be any readable `.sl2`/`.co2` path >= 1MiB. It no longer has to live under an `EldenRing/<steamid>` directory.
- Actual save-file opens (`.sl2`/`.co2`) redirect to the configured file itself.
- Directory/existence probes still redirect to a private staging tree next to the configured file (`er-effects-save-redirect-stage`) and lazily populate `eldenring/<active steamid>/er0000.sl2` when the game's native path reveals the SteamID. This keeps native save discovery happy without requiring the user's path to contain SteamID or EldenRing.
- Backup `.bak` paths redirect beside the configured file.

- cargo fmt --check: PASS
- cargo xwin check --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc -p er-effects-rs: PASS

Note: Rust does not have a native UTF-16 bytestring literal; current path filters still use u16 ASCII constants/helpers. A later cleanup can collapse those into an ascii-wide helper/macro, but the user-facing blocker is fixed in the redirect logic.
