title: ErStats push crash: first execution UAFs in SetText wrapper at in-world ProfileSelect open
description:
Run system-quit-repro-pause-at-menu 2026-07-04 07:08 (HEAD 8942ea1 + pause-at-menu harness): first-ever execution of the assign-driven ErStats push (push_stats_text_on_row, startup_hooks.rs ~7730) crashed ER with 0xc0000005 at +37836ms, 5ms after the 10 face-bind redirects, during the FIRST in-world System->Load-Profile ProfileSelect open. No stats-text log line ever printed (crash before push's own log; title-phase populate never runs the trigger, so in-world open is the path's first execution). Crash chain (deobf RVAs, matches our constants exactly): SetText wrapper 0x74a000 (PROFILE_SETTEXT_RVA) entry does rcx=*field; call *0x8(vt) -- ret 0x74a012 on stack; the stale *field pointer 0x7ffe9e52cc90 (menu-job arena; == our submitted 05_010 wrapper job 0x7ffe9e52cc80 + 0x10, freed after pump consumed it) has heap garbage vt 0x7ffe9858e8e0; the bogus vt+0x8 slot dispatched into ~CSScaleformValue 0xd7f850 (ret0 0xd7f88c = its call *0x10(rax) at 0xd7f889), which jumped to .rdata 0x142cbf2dd -> AV. fault_addr 0x96f05bc8 = low-32 truncation of heap ptr. Row-populate FUN_1408758d0 (dump) in stack (deobf ret 0x875814). So: assign resolve for 'ErStats' returned out!=0 but out+0x8 (SCENE_OBJ_PROXY_SCALEFORM_VALUE_OFFSET) held a dangling pointer into the freed job allocation. Open questions: (1) does assign leave the value slot unwritten/garbage when the child is MISSING from the movie instance (was the in-world 05_010 instance really our edited movie? runtime_edit_serves=1, served at +14853ms title phase; in-world AcquireMenuResource total=15 may have reused a cached instance) (2) is the proxy layout for a resolved DefineEditText child different (value not at +0x8)? Fix directions: validate the resolved value (vtable in .rdata range + dataType) before calling SetText; decompile binder not-found path; log assign's out vs our buffer. Evidence: target/runtime-probe/system-quit-repro-pause-at-menu/{er-effects-crash-log.txt,er-effects-autoload-debug.log}. NOT az9 (different signature/region; az9=repeated-switch 0x1ae GX region).

## Context
Found by the new pause-at-menu harness (ER_EFFECTS_SQ_REPRO_SWITCHES=0) on its first proof run -- user predicted the crash-before-pause outcome.
notes: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED via A/B (2026-07-04). The crash is NOT the ErStats push (that never runs: oracle_stats_text_row_populates=0). It is the NATIVE ProfileSelect row-populate FUN_1408758d0 (deobf 0x8758d0), which UNCONDITIONALLY resolves 'Icon_0' and 'Icon_0/m_trialFaceIcon' via assignComponentWithName, operates on them (FUN_140749a80/FUN_14074bdb0), and releases the resulting CSScaleformValue via ~CSScaleformValue (0xd7f850). Our stats-panel 05_010 GFX edit REMOVED Icon_0's PlaceObject from the row template (title_05_010_edits.rs edit#3: 'Icon_0'->'ErStats' placeobject; make_05_010_stats.rs row.remove(icon)). With Icon_0 unplaced, the resolve yields an invalid value and ~CSScaleformValue's 'call *0x10(rax)' hits a garbage vtable -> AV at .rdata 0x142cbf2dd. The generator comment 'Icon_0 setters are dataType-guarded so unplaced is a safe no-op' is FALSE. PROOF: staged er-effects-no-stats-panel.txt (stats-panel OFF, Icon_0 vanilla-present) -> harness navigated to ProfileSelect, sq_repro_paused_at_profile_select=1, state=DONE, ER ALIVE 40s+ paused, ZERO access-violation. FIX (user direction 2026-07-04: OMIT the boxes, don't show them): keep Icon_0 a resolvable PLACED instance but alpha-0 (CxformWithAlpha mult [256,256,256,0]) so it renders nothing and frees the strip for text -- native resolve/release stays valid. Regenerate movie+edit table+EDITED fingerprint.

title: ProfileSelect stats panel: runtime smoke validation
description: Runtime smoke for the finished stats panel: (1) per-field routing FIXED via row-populate hook (deobf 0x8757e0) that pushes the attribute line onto the GFX-added ErStats field BEFORE calling orig (orig callee-dtors the row proxy); pointer-ring SetText substitution removed (natives keep native text). (2) 05_010 GFX edit via er_gfx::title_05_010 (6 content-addressed edits: Icon_0 face box removed, char67 deco repurposed as 630x40 22px MenuFont_01 ErStats EditText at (-300,15), PlayerName/Level-caption/Level moved left into freed strip), served in-place at Scaleform file-open (mirror of 05_000 runtime strip). Oracles: oracle_stats_text_row_populates/_settext_subs/_push_failures + oracle_profile_05_010_runtime_edit_armed/_serves/_failures/_input_class/_output_validated. Smoke must show: edit_serves>=1, subs>=1, push_failures==0, no MessageBox, no crash; screenshot at portrait-cover moment for user layout review.
notes: 2026-07-04 pause-at-menu proof run: the runtime smoke REACHED the in-world ProfileSelect open and CRASHED on the first ErStats push execution -- see er-effects-rs-7e7 (UAF in SetText wrapper via stale resolved value). Smoke validation FAILS until 7e7 is fixed.

title: ProfileSelect stats panel: blank render + register per-slot texture + redirect binds
ProfileSelect stats panel: CPU stats-panel texture (bitmap font + DdsImage)
Switch feed-deserialize save-dir is timing-dependent; portrait per-frame work masked it (softlock when portraits off)
description:
ROOT (2026-07-03, exposed by disabling the portrait pipeline, run br93fa9dv): the System->Quit switch's continue_confirm hook drives a fresh feed-deserialize of the picked slot before SetState5 (startup_hooks.rs ~10128, own_load_feed_deserialize, fail-closed). Its save-dir resolution is TIMING-DEPENDENT: the game's save-dir path-DB field populates LATE (after submit -- see own_stepper.rs:497 'pre-submit population REFUTED' + the cold-char-mount SAVE-DIR notes). When continue_confirm fires BEFORE the path-DB is populated, own-load falls back to the default AppData dir (C:/users/steamuser/AppData/Roaming/EldenRing/{steamid}/) which is EMPTY in a staged/redirected-save run -> 'own-load: no .sl2/.co2 file under dir ... cannot read save' -> deserialize FAILS -> continue_confirm BLOCKS (correctly refuses to stream stale) -> product-core-autoload waits forever at owner_state=10 -> SOFTLOCK on switch #1.
TRIGGER: disabling the portrait pipeline (four render gates default-OFF, commit b67f351, REVERTED bb63c08). The portrait per-frame work (force_profile_render_tick each frame) was incidentally providing TIMING SLACK that delayed continue_confirm until the save-dir was populated, so the feed-deserialize read succeeded. Removing it fires continue_confirm earlier -> empty AppData -> fail. So the switch autoload is NOT independent of portraits (my 'cosmetic-only' claim was WRONG). lib.rs:198 explicitly says the DLL must NEVER read the default user save dir -- the AppData fallback is the bug.
FIX (proper, unblocks disabling portraits): make continue_confirm's feed-deserialize either (a) READINESS-GATE on the save-dir path-DB being populated (poll [io20+0x20]/the dir field != default before reading, retry until ready), or (b) read the REDIRECTED/staged save path directly (ER_EFFECTS_SAVE_FILE / the save_redirect resolution) instead of resolving via the timing-dependent game path-DB. Then portraits can be disabled without the softlock. Do NOT rely on portrait per-frame work as a timing crutch. Verify with a portraits-off 10-switch smoke.

title: Loading portrait: intermittent per-window publish starvation freezes prior character through the load
description: Soak fence-relax-1 (2026-07-03, 10 switches, commit c6df0fc): with the #1 drive freeze fixed (all 11 windows animated ~1:1 with display), a few windows still FROZE visually on the PRIOR character through the whole loading screen (user-observed windows 5, 6, 11 = transitions 3rd->4th, 4th->5th, 9th->10th; all other windows flawless live animation). Mechanism: publish-side starvation -- the new model's frames never pass the publish gate, so the make-before-break bridge holds the previous character's last clean frame. Window 6 is attributed: tear-gate storm, EVERY keyed frame scored tear=39-40 > threshold 10 (65 rejects, first-ever torn skips of the run; suspiciously CONSTANT score suggests a persistent seam, e.g. two producers writing the RT or a stable mid-write row, not stochastic scanline noise). Windows 5 and 11 are UNATTRIBUTED: no torn/pin/incoherent lines; suspects are an unkeyed streak (depth pairing failing all window -> frames never keyed; skipped_unkeyed=608 cumulative) or multi-model stable-gate skips (multi=366 cumulative) -- the cumulative oracles cannot localize. INSTRUMENTATION ADDED (this commit): loading_portrait_window_reset now logs per-window publish[clean/torn/unkeyed/multi/pin_moves/fence_skips] deltas; a frozen window shows clean=0 + its dominant skip class. NEXT: rerun the 10-switch soak, read the frozen windows' class, then fix that class at the root (tear storm -> real readback sync vs game render; unkeyed -> depth-pairing repair after retarget invalidate).
notes: VALIDATION SOAK (2026-07-03, commit a6f3358 target-slot binding): PASSED on every window it reached before az9 killed the run at switch #4. All 4 completed windows published clean with ZERO torn / ZERO unkeyed: boot 175, win2 309, win3 298 (this exact window class stalled at clean=92 wrong-identity in the attribution run), win4 243 (this class had the torn=214 storm). Both prior failure classes (frozen-on-prior identity publishes + constant-39/40 tear storms) are GONE with the target-slot binding. Run ended by er-effects-rs-az9 (same 0x1ae-region Scaleform-teardown AV, +170480ms, right after retarget slot3 -- switch #4, matching both prior firings). Residual work on THIS issue: none identified; keep open until a full 10-switch soak completes crash-free (blocked on az9).
close_reason: CLOSED on provenance soak v2 (commit 76897d8, 2026-07-03 ~21:04): full 10-switch soak, crash-free, ALL 11 windows published the correct character live (clean 105-298/window), zero unpaired/scan-provenance publishes (src[color bundle=*/scan=0 depth chain=*/bfs=0] every window -- the bundle chain never missed), zero full-window freezes, zero green-face. The frozen-on-prior-character bug class is resolved by the stack: target-slot binding (a6f3358) + strict bundle-provenance publish gate (c8c0225/76897d8) + drive/teardown fence (c6df0fc) + MZ-vcall scan hardening (235f613). User verdict: 'pretty good, with one very tiny exception' = a single ~0.5s missing-depth-mask moment, spun off as its own issue (unkeyed-streak residual, windows 10/11 unkeyed=31 each; user hypothesis: Patches boot/menu-path renderer lifecycle differs on reload).

title: Repeated-switch ProfileSelect Scaleform teardown use-after-free crash
notes: ATTRIBUTION CORRECTED (2026-07-03 static+PDB forensics): the LAST TWO 'az9' firings (+154662ms on a3d63b4, +170480ms on a6f3358 -- garbage RIP 0x300905a4d, stack 0x1aeaa9f/0x1af1411) were NOT this Scaleform-teardown bug. PDB symbolization of the self frames: ret0 = gpu_readback::find_d3d12_resource_ex+0x642, caller readback_offscreen_fast_coherent+0x214 -- OUR portrait readback's D3D12 heap scan. Root: the scan's in_ranges(vt,&d3d) vtable check accepted vt == d3d module BASE, so a freed heap chunk reused to hold a d3d module HANDLE passed as a 'd3d object' and the QueryInterface vcall executed the PE header as code -- crash RIP 0x300905a4d is literally the 'MZ\x90' signature bytes (0x5a4d='MZ'). The game-side 0x1ae stack RVAs are an allocator/pool path under the scan (disasm: call 0x141aeae60 alloc + store), not Scaleform teardown. FIXED: d3d_vtable_ok in find_d3d12_resource_ex now requires vtable AND its QI slot >= 0x1000 into a d3d module before any vcall. Ghidra ground truth also corrected the hook target naming: FUN_1411aaf10 is a scalar-deleting destructor (calls dtor FUN_1411a8920 then allocator-frees), not a per-frame Run body. WHAT REMAINS OF az9 PROPER: only the ORIGINAL firing (fault_addr=0x0 inside FUN_1411a8920's intrusive-list walk, run stable-base1-noteardown-20260703-120137) -- unreproduced since the GX-overflow/spared-renderer fixes (48afa18) across 24+ switches. If the next soaks stay clean with the scan hardening, close this as (a) original list-walk crash plausibly a downstream casualty of the since-fixed renderer leak family, (b) recent firings = the now-fixed scan bug.
close_reason: CLOSED after root-cause + crash-free proof (2026-07-03). (1) The recent firings (+154662/+170480, garbage RIP 0x300905a4d) were NOT this bug: PDB symbolization = our portrait readback D3D12 scan vcalling through a d3d module BASE stored in a reused freed chunk (RIP is the 'MZ\x90' header bytes). Fixed by d3d_vtable_ok hardening (commit 235f613): vtable + QI slot must sit >=0x1000 into a d3d module. Proof: full 10-switch soak on 235f613 completed CRASH-FREE (11 windows). (2) az9 proper (the single FUN_1411a8920 intrusive-list dtor fault, run stable-base1) has not reproduced across 30+ switches since the GX-overflow/spared-renderer leak fix (48afa18) -- consistent with it being a downstream casualty of the since-fixed renderer-leak family. Ghidra correction recorded: FUN_1411aaf10 is the scalar-deleting dtor (dtor + allocator free), not a per-frame Run body. Reopen ONLY on a new fault INSIDE FUN_1411a8920 with fault_addr=0x0 list-walk signature.

title: Loading portrait: cross-slot RT swap + early pop-out (IN_WORLD false positive)
description: Run strip-default-drive-20260702-194018: (1) portrait swaps to another save-slot character mid-load: engine rebuilds ALL 10 slot models post-Continue (summary marks persist from menu; global refresh builds every marked slot despite mark-only-target), and readback_offscreen_fast picks largest-BFS-reachable RT fresh each frame -> flips to foreign slot RT when late slots (7/8/9) finish building. (2) portrait pops out at +27.8s via IN_WORLD_REACHED (PlayerIns loading-screen false positive), before loading bar fills; also latches forever killing portraits on subsequent reloads. Fix: suppress non-target model builds in our post-Continue window (renderer +0x754/755 latches), pin readback candidate once confirmed non-checker, replace overlay stop with NowLoadingHelper-update-cessation window logic.

title: Silence PAB + Continue activation SFX during product autoload title flow
description:
User requirement 2026-07-02 (follow-on to er-effects-rs-dl0): the title flow still PLAYS SOUNDS on press-any-button advance and Continue confirm even though the visuals are now stripped. Silence exactly those activation sounds on the product zero-input path. NO prior sound RE exists in bd (user-confirmed 2026-07-02).

TRIGGER SITES (what fires them): (1) PAB advance -- readiness pab-advance hook drives the native PAB node-update 0x1407ad1c0 / accept path; (2) Continue confirm -- native title accept byte -> TitleTopDialog in-update open-menu, and continue_confirm wrapper 0x140b0e180 (also the System-Quit clean-title path). Elden Ring audio is Wwise-based (sd/ .bnk banks); menu UI SEs are likely fired natively via a play-sound-event helper with an SE id from the decide/OK handlers (menu decide/cancel SE ids possibly data-driven via a menu param), OR movie-driven from the (still-running) 05_000 AS3 shell -- determine which first.

INVESTIGATION (static first, Ghidra dump ermaporch has real names): (a) symbol sweep for Sound/PlaySe/SoundEvent/CSSound in the persistent project; (b) decompile the PAB dismiss + open_menu/continue_confirm paths and find the sound call + its id argument; (c) map deobf addresses via scripts/dump-deobf-shift.py before any hook/patch. PRODUCT LEVER SHAPE: suppress only those SE plays while the product autoload drives the title (tie to the same autoload-state gate as the strip; no global mute, no audio-device tampering, save-safe, zero-input preserved). Add a RAM telemetry counter oracle_title_se_suppressed (or similar) so runs prove the suppression fired. ACCEPTANCE: product boot with autoload -> no PAB/Continue activation SFX audible (user confirms), all other audio unaffected, T_controllable + zero-msgbox + zero-input oracles unchanged.
notes: First-pass RE done 2026-07-02 (see bd memory title-activation-sfx-native-not-movie-2026-07-02): sounds are NATIVE Wwise, NOT movie (stripped movie has 0 sound tags + 0 AS3 sound refs). PlaySE/CSSoundImp cluster is world/positional layer, wrong for menu. continue_confirm has no sound call -> menu decide handler plays it on accept-byte transition. Remaining: runtime trace at AK PostEvent (event-id + caller_rva) during title to pin the two ids, then whitelist-suppress. Ghidra GUI locked the headless project; used MCP.

title: Product-default in-memory stripped 05_000_title.gfx: kill title-UI animate-in drag at the asset layer
description:
# Goal

Eliminate the title-UI animate-in cost (PRESS ANY BUTTON, CONTINUE menu, footer/copyright, version block) at the ASSET layer: serve a stripped `05_000_title.gfx` from memory at Scaleform load time so the visual placements never exist and never animate, instead of hiding each element after it is built. User steer 2026-07-02: "Many of these assets are usually on disk and we edit them in memory" -- verified below; treat direction as confirmed, but validate each sub-claim during implementation.

# Measured drag to eliminate (run 2026-07-02 18:55, debug log timestamps)

- Second title visit (System-Quit switch): native `SetState(title, state=2)` +42303ms -> "PRESS BUTTON component ready; armed native title accept byte" +45297ms (**2.99s** of title build/animate before the product can arm accept) -> continue_confirm forwarded +46964ms (**+1.67s** more). ~**4.7s** title overhead per character switch.
- Cold boot: 05_000_title.gfx file-open +15135ms -> bind/build +15.6s -> menu portraits baked +17168ms -> T_controllable +27153ms.
- Part of the 3s is GFX/FMG disk load + build, part is the FadeIn/menu-open/footer animate-in; the existing title-anim levers already skip FadeIn->Loop (log +15641ms, hook 0x140b0d400/0x1407499e0) and speed pab_dismiss->menu_open 4x (`TITLE_ANIM_SPEEDUP_DEFAULT`, `src/constants.rs:452-472`), so the REMAINING drag is dominated by what still animates: menu items, footer, version block. Asset-strip removes those timelines entirely. Measure before/after via the same log events (SetState(2) -> accept-armed -> continue_confirm deltas) as the RAM-telemetry proof.

# Machinery that already exists (all verified in-repo)

- Embedded stripped asset: `src/title_05_000_text_suppressed_bytes.rs` -- `TITLE_05_000_TEXT_SUPPRESSED_GFX`, "Generated from target/custom-gfx-lab/title-text-suppressed/05_000_title.native-ui-stripped.gfx. Stripped 05_000_title.gfx: removes visual title/menu/text/progress placements while preserving the native GFx shell for game interactivity." Included via `src/constants.rs:1965`.
- In-memory replacement path: Scaleform MemoryFile-backed file-open replacement (`SCALEFORM_MEMORY_*` consts `src/constants.rs:1947-1963`, selection in `src/experiments/startup_hooks.rs:66,114`), currently opt-in via env `ER_EFFECTS_TITLE_RESOURCE_MEMORY_GFX` (`src/experiments/gating.rs:881-885`). Run evidence the hook rides the right path: file-open observer sampled `data0:/menu/05_000_title.gfx` from disk at +15135ms.
- Diagnostic embedded variants (`embedded:minimal-magenta`, `embedded:minimal-magenta-counter`, `src/constants.rs:1967-1988`) prove the MemoryFile path renders and can carry a frame-counter marker for recorder-independent timing.

# Gating / policy constraints

- **No new env-gated features** (bd user pref): promote from `ER_EFFECTS_TITLE_RESOURCE_MEMORY_GFX` opt-in to product default TIED TO the existing autoload/product enablement state, mirroring how `title_native_menu_visual_suppression_enabled()` de-gated. Keep env only as explicit override.
- Branch interaction (`src/lib.rs:276-351`): today memory-GFX mode and Part-A suppression are mutually exclusive branches (`title_native_menu_visual_suppression_enabled()` returns false when memory-GFX is on, because old TitleBack hide hooks would hide the replacement surface). Product default must re-decide which per-element hooks remain as defense-in-depth (bind-hide of stray names, logo hooks) vs which conflict.
- Probe contract: `ER_EFFECTS_TITLE_RESOURCE_MEMORY_GFX` is listed in VISUAL_RESOURCE_MUTATION_ENVS (`scripts/check-runtime-probe-contract.py:337`); flipping default-on moves that mutation into every product run -- update the contract/tests deliberately (they must change together with the policy per AGENTS).
- Human-drive diagnostic runs (System-Quit repeatable switching) currently rely on the VISIBLE vanilla menu for the human to drive. Product-default strip must keep a diagnostic escape (existing env override suffices) and must not break menu INTERACTIVITY (stripped shell preserves the native GFx shell for game interactivity -- validate accept/menu-open still function with zero input).
- Do not regress: zero simulated input, zero MessageBoxDialog builds, save-safety, Seamless-Co-op compatibility (bd `autoload-dll-product-requirements`); title cover surfaces must be provably above PRESS ANY BUTTON / CONTINUE (AGENTS; related open issue er-effects-rs-trp).
- Verify the stripped asset actually covers the footer CopyrightText placement and decide 05_020_TitleInformation (version block) handling -- see the footer bug ticket.

# Acceptance sketch

1. Product run (no env overrides): RAM telemetry proves stripped movie served (`TITLE_SCALEFORM_MEMORY_GFX_REPLACEMENTS>0`, bytes==embedded len) on BOTH cold-boot and post-System-Quit titles; zero visible title/menu/footer text placements by RAM/pixel semaphore; SetState(2)->accept-armed delta measurably below the 2.99s baseline; autoload + System-Quit switch still complete end-to-end (T_controllable, zero msgbox, zero simulated input).
2. Loading-screen-portrait screenshot captured at the portrait-cover moment for USER review (agents do not read it).
3. `scripts/check.sh` + probe-contract tests updated and green.
notes: Follow-on user requirement 2026-07-02: PAB/Continue activation SOUNDS still play with visuals stripped; silencing tracked separately (see new audio ticket).
close_reason: Landed on feature/title-ui-hide-footer-loadtime (ee42259). Strip default-on tied to autoload state; v2 asset also removes Cursor glow (image char 6 in sprite 10). Runtime-validated: strip armed +41ms, memory_replacement=true bytes=11707, PressStart readiness intact, T_controllable, 0 msgbox, 0 simulated inputs; user visually confirmed clean title ('Perfect'). Reload-title visual re-check rides the next System-Quit run (movie is cached from the same replaced MemoryFile). Rust-native regeneration tracked in er-effects-rs-h7x.

title: [bug] Title footer CopyrightText + 05_020 version block never hidden (bind names never traverse hooked binder)
description:
# Symptom (user-reviewed frames, run 2026-07-02 18:55)

The title footer -- the copyright line "(c)Bandai Namco Entertainment Inc. / (c)2024 FromSoftware, Inc." -- is visible in BOTH user-reviewed frames (`frame-000854.jpg`, `frame-000911.jpg` under `/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/system-quit-reload-patches-noteardown/human-drive-20260702-185536/frames/`), and it animates in (user-observed), taking time to load and contributing to title-phase drag. Frame 911 additionally shows the bottom-right version block ("Shadow of the Erdtree / OFFLINE / App Ver. 1.16.2 / Calibrations Ver. 1.16.1").

# Why the existing hides miss it (verified this run)

1. **CopyrightText never traverses the hooked binder.** The named-child bind hook (0x14074a2f0, `title_scene_obj_proxy_named_child_bind_hook`) match-list includes `CopyrightText`, `StaticSystemText_101000`, `StaticSystemText_100100`, `PRESS BUTTON`, `Info`, `ProgressInfo`, `Install_ProgressInfo`, `PressStart` (`src/experiments/startup_hooks.rs:6149-6159`), but the debug log shows only 4 names ever bound through it (PressStart, Info, ProgressInfo, Install_ProgressInfo -- calls 1-4 cold boot at +15606ms, calls 5-8 second title at +42369ms). `CopyrightText` and both `StaticSystemText_*` names NEVER hit the hook in the entire run => the footer text object is created/bound through a different native path.
2. **05_020_TitleInformation cover hook installed but never fired.** Log +121ms: "hooked PAB/TitleInformation wrapper 0x14081f8d0; native 05_020_TitleInformation preserved and covered", yet telemetry `oracle_title_pab_information_visual_builds=0` / `any_built=false` while the version block IS on screen (frame 911). The version/info block reaches the screen via a path the wrapper hook does not see.
3. The catch-all for both would have been the 05_000_Title render-suppression, which never engaged this run (window latch 0x0 -- see the PAB/menu bug ticket).

# Investigation leads

- Static RE first (per AGENTS): find who binds/creates `CopyrightText` -- search the binary for the "CopyrightText" ASCII/UTF-16 string and xrefs (Ghidra persistent project `ermaporch`, `scripts/ghidra-query.sh`; ground any patch/call address with `scripts/dump-deobf-shift.py`). Compare with the binder 0x14074a2f0 callers to learn which sibling function binds footer children.
- Determine which movie hosts the footer: 05_000_title.gfx placements (the embedded stripped GFX generator claims to remove "title/menu/text/progress placements" -- verify CopyrightText placement is among the stripped ones in `target/custom-gfx-lab/title-text-suppressed/05_000_title.native-ui-stripped.gfx` provenance) vs 05_020_TitleInformation.gfx (version block).
- Confirm whether the version block is data-driven into 05_020 or a separate movie; the 0x14081f8d0 wrapper hook's build path clearly is not the one used on this flow (builds=0) -- find the real one.

# Fix directions

- Preferred: asset-level removal via the in-memory stripped-GFX replacement (feature ticket) so footer + version block never exist; verify the stripped 05_000 actually strips CopyrightText, and produce/strip 05_020_TitleInformation equivalently if the version block lives there.
- Fallback: extend the bind/build hooks to the real footer bind path found by RE.
- Add an `oracle_*` RAM semaphore proving footer/version placements are absent (e.g. count of live named text objects in the title movie), so probes can score this without screenshots.
close_reason: Superseded/resolved by er-effects-rs-dl0 asset-level strip: footer CopyrightText and the version block placements live in 05_000_title.gfx and are absent from the served stripped movie -- user-reviewed v1/v2 runs 2026-07-02 showed neither (v1's only residual was the Cursor glow, removed in v2). The unanswered RE question (which binder path CopyrightText used; why 05_020 wrapper hook saw builds=0) is recorded in the issue body for historical reference; no product need remains.

title: System-Quit switch: 3rd ProfileSelect open crashes (DAT_143d6d8d0 null preview-model)
description:
When our cloned in-world Load-Profile switch opens the native ProfileSelect a THIRD time in one session, the game crashes. Crash log 2026-07-02 (explore run, session aa5f5d1a): access-violation game+0x9aa6d4 reading [null+0x754], via MenuWindowJob::Run (0x7ad2bc) -> FUN_140820d50 -> FUN_14081ebc0 -> FUN_1409aa7d0. Process exit 0xC0000005.

ROOT CAUSE (Ghidra ermaporch dump): FUN_1409aa7d0 (dump; = deobf 0x1409aa6d4 region) is the per-frame ProfileSelect character-preview builder. It walks the 10-entry pointer array DAT_143d6d8d0 (one CSMenuProfModelRend per save slot, size 0xa30) and dereferences *(entry+0x754) WITHOUT null-checking the entry. On the 3rd open one entry is null -> [null+0x754] AV. Lifecycle: FUN_1409af4f0 (ProfileSelect SETUP) calls FUN_1409b2f00 (TEARDOWN: frees all 10 via CSDelayDeleteMan->FUN_140e77540, nulls them) then re-allocs 10 via HeapAlloc(0xa30,0x10,GLOBAL_GfxHeapAllocator); on alloc failure it stores 0 (uVar1=0) into DAT_143d6d8d0[slot]. So the null is either (a) a GfxHeap alloc failure on the 3rd setup, or (b) the per-frame builder running in the teardown->realloc null window. Our feature is the only thing that reopens ProfileSelect in-session (vanilla shows it once at boot), so we expose this latent native bug.

STATUS: found while user manually drove a 3rd switch during explore mode (SQ_REPRO_TARGET_SWITCHES=1 build). Blocks the 'drive our feature to load a third character' goal. NEXT: diagnostic to determine null origin (alloc-fail vs teardown/update race) via a driven 3-switch repro, then fix (ensure DAT_143d6d8d0 fully populated when the builder runs -- prefer root-causing the null over a bare null guard). Related: [[system-quit-gaitem-singleton-restore-RESOLVED-2026-07-02]] (same class: per-slot native resource degrading across our repeated in-session reopens).

title: Investigate ProfileSelect slot activation from in-world System menu
description: Follow-up from er-effects-rs-srz. Presentation/interactivity of System -> Quit Game -> Select profile to load is validated, but actual slot activation/load from that in-world ProfileSelect route was intentionally kept out of scope and earlier attempts crashed around live RVA 0x67141a. Investigate native semantics for selecting a slot from this injected ProfileSelect context, save-safe with staged gold save, and preserve zero accidental load behavior until explicitly gated.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Correction applied: the System -> Quit Game quick-loading button is now always enabled; it no longer depends on ER_EFFECTS_SYSTEM_QUIT_DUPLICATE_BUTTON / er-effects-system-quit-duplicate-button.txt. Added a default-on ProfileLoadDialog activation guard for the injected in-world ProfileSelect route: native slot activation is blocked save-safely by default, records cursor/bound/dialog telemetry, and only forwards with ER_EFFECTS_SYSTEM_QUIT_ALLOW_PROFILE_LOAD=1 for an explicitly staged crash-risk probe. Static RE pinned the prior crash site as CSGaitemImp::Deserialize live/deobf 0x14067141a (dump 0x14067150a), reached from GameMan load/deser chain 0x67be5c -> 0x256c32 -> PlayerGameData::Deserialize -> CSGaitemImp::Deserialize. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo xwin build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc; no-auto-teardown smoke /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/system-quit-always-on-noteardown-smoke-20260701-134000. comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Static RE checkpoint for System->Quit Game Load Character feature:

- Glorious Merchant source confirms a useful text-overlay pattern: hook `MsgRepositoryImp::LookupEntry` and return owned/static wide strings for selected `(msgbnd,msg_id)`; it does not patch the System menu directly.
- For this feature, first-pass text probably does not need FMG parsing: existing FMG IDs already cover usable labels: `GR_MenuText 288000 = Character Selection`, `GR_LineHelp 288000 = Select which character to use`, `GR_MenuText 406000 = LOAD GAME`.
- The in-game Quit Game tab is not a pure GFX surface: `OptionSettingTopDialog` adds a category from `FUN_14093c0e0` (`GetGR_MenuTextEntry(110500)`, category id 9). The tab content is built by `FUN_140958a00`.
- `FUN_140958a00` calls `CS::MatchingDialog::AddCancelButton` twice. First label helper `FUN_140957110` = `GR_MenuText 110510 Quit Game` + `GR_LineHelp 110500`; its action chain eventually calls `FUN_14094e9f0` -> builds quit-to-title chain via `FUN_14079d7f0` and dialog `GR_Dialogues 110000`. Second helper `FUN_1409570c0` = `GR_MenuText 110511 Return to Desktop` + `GR_LineHelp 110501`; action chain calls `FUN_14094d9f0` -> `FUN_14079d2e0` and `GR_Dialogues 110010`.
- `AddCancelButton`/`FUN_140920bc0` creates an `EditProperty`, initializes a `PropertyNewButtonController`, and pushes into `PropertyEditDialog.properties` (`DLFixedVector<EditProperty>` cap 16, so a third button is structurally plausible).
- Feasibility risk: constructing the third button needs valid MSVC `std::function` objects. The native stack objects in `FUN_140958a00` are cloneable through their native vtables (`0x142b12b48` quit-title action, `0x142b12bb8` desktop action, `0x142ac7760` bool predicate). We can either (A) call `AddCancelButton` with native-like std::function objects and detour the cloned action object for our third row, or (B) implement a tiny compatible function/vtable shim in Rust. Do not assume this is a GFX edit.
- ProfileLoadDialog/load character target remains the existing known factory path: deobf/live `0x14081ead0` (dump `0x14081ebc0`) and existing `fire_live_loadgame_node`/`LIVE_DIALOG_FACTORY_RVA` code.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Static RE update: simplest third-button proof path found.

Instead of constructing our own MSVC `std::function` or touching FMG/GFX, hook `CS::MatchingDialog::AddCancelButton` live/deobf `0x140920c90` (dump `0x140920d80`) and duplicate ONLY the second call from the Quit Game tab builder.

Evidence:
- Quit tab builder dump `FUN_140958a00` -> live/deobf `0x140958910`.
- First AddCancelButton call returns to live RVA `0x958a20` after adding `Quit Game`.
- Second AddCancelButton call returns to live RVA `0x958b37` after adding `Return to Desktop`.
- At the hook point all caller stack objects are still alive; destructors run only after the call returns (`MenuHelpLabelComponent` and std::function destructors follow the callsite). Calling the original trampoline a second time from the hook with the exact same five args should append a third row with the same label/help/action/predicate as the second native button.
- `PropertyEditDialog.properties` is `DLFixedVector<EditProperty>` capacity 16, count at `dialog+0x1af0`; original has only two rows, so a third row is structurally safe.
- This proof avoids the hard part: no custom callbacks, no FMG replacement, no ProfileLoadDialog/save semantics. It only proves the UI can fit and dispatch a third native button.

Recommended proof gate: enable by explicit env/file flag, duplicate the second button only, verify the System->Quit Game tab shows three rows and that the third opens the same native Return-to-Desktop confirmation dialog (do not confirm the dialog during proof).

title: IBL environment runtime-modify: reverse + lever for the offscreen portrait scene background/lighting
description: GOAL: reverse the IBL environment in our custom offscreen GXSgCompositeScene (renderer+0xa8+0x48) and add a runtime lever to modify it (ambient/env/exposure -> control/remove the warm 0x17140E opaque background). RE facts so far: clear=black (FloatVector4_14329e9b0), scene RT-holder=scene->vtable[3]() (GXSceneContext ~0xc4b0B); +0x9c=0x141414 tested NO visual effect; field10_0x50 zero-init; field20_0x68=CSEzDraw helper. RT alpha=255 everywhere (no cutout). Warm bg is IBL/ambient or a bg pass. Subagent RE'ing scene ctor FUN_141a0a880 + passes (GXSgPaintSceneImpl/GXSgDrawScene/...) + GXSceneContext fields. See bd portrait-bg-alpha-RE-opaque-IBL-env-2026-06-30.
notes: SOLVED the runtime env-modify lever: tonemap color-grade block filter+0x30..+0x11c (filter=*(ctx+0xbf50)) is copied to the per-frame CB and affects output. PROVEN: gamma +0x60 2.2->1.0 changed readback bg (23,20,14)->(1,1,0), head ->(92,65,40). User confirmed likes the lighting control. Levers kept. NEXT: neon-green panel + chroma-key for transparent bg (workflow RE in progress). See portrait-env-lever-WORKS-tonemap-colorgrade-block-2026-06-30.

title: GX subcontext pool epic: per-frame profile head render during world-load
description: Loading-screen portrait head refreshes only ~4x/screen because the GX subcontext pool (gxctx+0xf0 vector, base@+0xf8 top@+0x100, used-mask@+0x110 max32) is held by the world render during load. Cheap fixes exhausted (retry/phases/readback). Path under test: find subcontext count+alloc in Initilize@0x1419e7d10 (push FUN_1419ee6a0@0x1419e9269), seed an extra valid subcontext so profile draw always gets one. Pool vector auto-grows via FUN_1419ed080; 32-cap is on distinct subctx +0x2580 indices.
notes: RESOLVED the per-frame RENDER+REFRESH goal: rgba_version 4->150 (3 clean runs: 150/145/150), readback_checker ~175->14, screenshot + L/R 1024 dumps confirm the loaded char (Banon) renders live per-frame (hair physics vary). Two fixes: (1) disabled the every-frame drive(r) ClearRTV that was self-wiping the RT; (2) new readback_offscreen_fast re-resolves the content RT fresh each frame (the cached readback resource went stale/dangled after the 1024 resize). Pool-contention theory refuted (free_min=18). Cursor look-at TRACKING (head turning) still fails -> filed as follow-up. Ready to commit+push.

title: Native-shader object draw: ISG1 parser (er-shaderkit) + real FLVER passthrough draw + readback (er-objectkit)
description: Next milestone after er-flver. (1) er-shaderkit: parse the .vpo DX container ISG1 chunk -> Vec<Isg1Input{semantic_name, semantic_index, register}> (ISG1 confirmed present in ER DXIL containers). Feeds er-flver::RawVertexBuffer::match_isg1. (2) er-objectkit/passthrough.rs: upload raw FLVER vertex buffers verbatim, build pipeline via RawVertexBuffer::wgpu_layout(sig), synthesize engine cbuffers (MVP from studio camera) + material cbMtdParam, bind material textures, indexed draw of a real mesh, read back a NON-BLANK image proving geometry rendered through the native vpo+ppo. Open deps from er-flver spec open_risks: validate 0x2E=Snorm16x4, biased-byte (b-127)/127 vs hw snorm against a real .vpo decode, uvFactor=2048, parallel multi-buffer binding. Engine/material cbuffer field OFFSETS need the DX container RDEF chunk (cbuffer layout) — separate sub-task. Needs assets extracted (target/er-objectkit empty now; loader auto-extracts via wine bridge). Pick a base-game model+material+pass whose matbin resolves to a base-game .shaderbdle (c4800 was DLC-only).

title: Create er-flver crate: raw FLVER vertex declarations for native-shader passthrough render
description: Factor FLVER handling out of er-objectkit into a dedicated er-flver crate. Headline capability: expose RAW interleaved vertex buffers + full per-member D3D-style declarations (semantic, semantic_index, format code, struct_offset, stride, count) + a FLVER-format-code -> wgpu::VertexFormat mapping, so real FLVER geometry can be bound to the native compiled .vpo vertex shader (matched against its ISG1 input signature) for passthrough rendering. Keep a normalized decode (positions/normals/uvs/tangents) for the Bevy PBR / studio-camera path. Harvest from user's flveRS crate (github.com/chozandrias76/flveRS, src/helpers/flver_asset.rs) and fstools_formats FLVER reader. Host-only, pure Rust, no Oodle (decompression stays in er-soulsformats wine bridge). Unblocks the M3 native-shader render frontier.
notes: er-flver crate scaffolded + wired. Modules: error/semantic/format/layout/vertex/mesh + lib. WRAP fstools_formats reader (catch_unwind panic boundary in parse_structural). Two paths: parse()->ObjectModel (normalized, ported verbatim from old er-objectkit/flver.rs) and parse_raw()->RawFlver (raw interleaved buffer slices + VertexMember decls + map_format authoritative format table + match_isg1 ISG1 reconciliation; optional wgpu feature for VertexBufferLayout). er-objectkit keeps a 'pub mod flver { pub use er_flver::*; }' alias so loader/scene/material/viewer need ZERO edits. Compiling now.
close_reason: er-flver crate created, tested (13/13), and verified. Wraps fstools_formats reader (panic-safe catch_unwind boundary). Two paths: parse()->ObjectModel (normalized, ported verbatim) and parse_raw()->RawFlver (raw interleaved buffer slices + VertexMember declarations + authoritative semantic-keyed map_format format table + match_isg1 ISG1 reconciliation; optional wgpu feature builds wgpu::VertexBufferLayout). er-objectkit keeps 'pub mod flver { pub use er_flver::* }' alias -> zero edits to loader/scene/material/viewer; both build green. Full scripts/check.sh green (fmt + no-lossy-utf8 + cargo xwin DLL check unaffected; er-flver stays host-only).

title: Composite loaded-character portrait onto CSFakeLoadingScreen (loading screen)
description: Branch spike/boot-parallelization core deliverable. Draw the already-rendered offscreen character portrait (CSMenuProfModelRend renderer table 0x3d6d8d0 slot0 -> renderer+0xa8 CSEzOffscreenRend -> +0x10 CSRuntimeTexResCap(SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00) -> +0x78 CSGxTexture; sampler at src/experiments/title.rs:1389, ready post-SL2 via maybe_refresh_title_profile_cover title.rs:1461) onto the actual LOADING SCREEN surface CS::CSFakeLoadingScreen (loading bar + help text + full-screen background). USER DIRECTIVE 2026-06-28: this is the only surface late+long-lived enough (see memory portrait-target-is-csfakeloadingscreen-2026-06-28). Composite step does NOT exist yet; current code only renders portrait offscreen and manipulates the wrong (title) surface. Constraints: no new env gates (tie to existing autoload state), no startup-process change beyond the portrait layer, must use native/game-render-layer composite (static RE; runtime deref of loading-layer pointers is unsafe per loading-render-layer-deref-unsafe-2026-06-25). Proof = RAM/native/pixel telemetry tying portrait source + live surface + lifetime; logo-replacement screenshot diagnostic.
notes: STATE 2026-06-29: PROVEN+SAFE = the now-loading background injection mechanism (forge any TPF as the full-screen loading bg via FUN_140d69880 0x140d697d0 + CreateTpfResCap 0x140b83680, crash-free, screenshot confirmed magenta checker). Live offscreen CSGxTexture capture+swap also crash-free. BLOCKER for the REAL portrait = DEFINITIVE root cause (see memory now-loading-portrait-ROOT-CAUSE-req754-zero-2026-06-29): the Continue autoload NEVER requests the profile model render (renderer+0x754/755=0), and couples menu-open+load-commit (native accept-byte 0x144589bdc drain) so there's no render window; driving the menu render post-Continue crashes. FIX = re-sequence the autoload (open menu -> call refresh 0x9aa680 to request the model -> hold while it loads/renders pre-commit -> capture -> commit), a substantial+risky change to the CORE proven char-load. User directive: 'pursue the path that works' -> attempting the re-sequence (design agent aa881dcae running), probe-tested + revertible, must not break oracle_player_present. Current code: forge + capture + spare hooks installed (safe, falls back to checker); inert maybe_fire_tfc_continue hold removed.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Concrete implementation plan for black init surface -> custom portrait cover (2026-06-28, baseline commit 0f668ab):

0. Guard baseline: keep no-hudhook. Do not re-add hudhook/debug. Current baseline artifact product-continue-direct-20260628-105043 proves no-hudhook path completes when DInput ilhook install is skipped; fix input blocker separately before product proof.

1. Black cover proof: re-enable existing 01_900_Black custom cover path (TITLE_CUSTOM_COVER_BLACK_WRAPPER_RVA 0x793b20) from BeginTitle wrapper and MenuWindowJob::Run hook. It must run every frame after native 05_000_Title/PAB jobs and hide TitleBack/PAB as baseline already does. Add a watcher event capture for black-cover oracle (black built, run_calls>N, cover_window non-null, logo/PAB hidden) and pixel analyzer that specifically rejects visible footer/text/logo. This proves custom surface is on top of PRESS ANY BUTTON/Continue, not just that native title background is black.

2. Color/control proof: if black cover is on top, prove the same surface is agent-controlled. Preferred static-first path: RE 01_900_Black's loaded GFx value/tree and existing CSScaleformValue display-info setters; if color/fill cannot be updated in-place, embed or synthesize a minimal custom GFX with a full-viewport root rectangle and serve it via an in-DLL resource/load hook (no loose files, no hudhook). Prove by switching black -> obvious color and capturing/pixel-oracling the exact event.

3. Portrait source proof: keep existing profile renderer source path: init 0x1409af3a0, refresh 0x1409aa680, sample CSMenuProfModelRend table 0x143d6d8d0, renderer+0xa8 CSEzOffscreenRend, offscreen+0x10 TexResCap/SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00. This is source readiness only, not visibility.

4. Portrait binding: use a valid MenuWindow/custom-surface bind owner, not raw TitleBackViewParts. Prior RE says ProfileSelect ctor calls bind helper 0x1407451c0 on MenuWindow/ProfileSelect owner field +0x3b8 with owner+0x60 initialized. The custom surface must expose a root-reachable dummy/image symbol (not ProfileSelect's unreachable MENU_DummyProfileFace placeholders), then bind SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00 to that symbol through the valid owner. Do not call 0x1407451c0 against raw TitleBackViewParts+0x8.

5. Final proof gates: no hudhook; zero simulated input; no MessageBoxDialog; DInput/XInput blocker fixed and active; black/custom surface obscures footer/PAB/Continue; portrait source ready; SYSTEX bind hits custom root-reachable symbol; event screenshot/pixel oracle shows portrait at the init black interval; runtime completes within cap. Commit after each proof rung.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Gap inventory for the concrete portrait-cover plan:

A. Input-blocker gap (must be fixed before product proof): current no-hudhook baseline only completes with the new DInput ilhook install skipped. This is not acceptable final proof because it weakens user-input suppression. Need root-cause/fix src/input_blocker.rs DInput hook install/probe so keyboard+mouse suppression is active without crashing/hanging, then rerun the baseline.

B. Black-cover ownership gap: 01_900_Black can be built statically via wrapper 0x140793b20, but we still must prove its MenuWindowJob is actually above the native title/PAB/Continue layer for the whole init interval. Current native baseline can already produce a big black screen/footer with no custom black job, so black pixels alone are not proof. Need RAM oracle + event screenshot/pixel analyzer that distinguishes custom 01_900_Black surface from native title background and rejects visible footer/text/logo.

C. Surface-control gap: 01_900_Black may be a static GFx asset with no simple fill-color control. Need static RE of its Scaleform tree/value handles, or a custom in-memory/minimal GFX/resource-load hook. Success is not “black appears”; success is changing the same surface to an unmistakable agent-chosen color and proving it on screen.

D. Custom GFX/resource gap: if 01_900_Black cannot be recolored/repurposed, we need a no-loose-file, no-hudhook way to serve an embedded custom GFX/resource into the native MenuWindowJob loader. Need identify the resource/load hook boundary and prove lifetime/order matches BeginTitle/PAB.

E. Portrait source-vs-visibility gap: existing CSMenuProfModelRend/SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00 telemetry proves a portrait texture source can exist, not that pixels are drawn. Need a visible root-reachable image symbol on the custom surface and a valid bind owner/path that consumes SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00.

F. Bind-owner ABI gap: prior raw TitleBackViewParts bind attempts crashed/failed. Need prove the correct owner layout for bind helper 0x1407451c0: ProfileSelect-like MenuWindow owner field +0x3b8 with owner+0x60 initialized, or an equivalent custom surface owner. Do not call bind helper against raw TitleBackViewParts+0x8 until this is statically proven.

G. Root-reachable symbol gap: 05_010_ProfileSelect exports MENU_DummyProfileFace_01..10 but they are not root-reachable; binding them does not make pixels visible. Any custom/reused GFX must place the target symbol in the display list, and tooling must verify root reachability before runtime.

H. Proof-oracle gap: the current logo-replacement screenshot trigger only fires for portrait-cover oracles, not for black/color surface proof. Need add a separate event semaphore/capture for black/color cover and later portrait binding, with exact target-window validation and pixel/native classification. Screenshots remain diagnostic; final stop/score must come from RAM/native/pixel oracle fields.

I. No-hudhook regression gap: hudhook/debug must stay removed. Any plan step that seems to need overlay/DX12 draw must be rephrased as native Scaleform/MenuWindow/resource work or rejected.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
CONSOLIDATED IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (from 8 headless read-only investigation lanes, 2026-06-28)

Baseline/constraints:
- Baseline commit: 0f668ab (pushed) = no-hudhook title baseline.
- Do NOT re-add hudhook or debug. Any render path must be native Scaleform/MenuWindow/resource work.
- Current runtime-happy baseline artifact: target/runtime-probe/product-continue-direct-20260628-105043. Important caveat: it completed only because DInput ilhook install is skipped; this is not final product proof.

R1 — Fix the no-hudhook input blocker first (mandatory product-proof prerequisite)
- Finding from headless lane A: likely root cause is the new DInput ilhook live patch race. Vendored MinHook freezes other threads before patching; ilhook does not. A function the input thread is executing can run half-written bytes -> AV/hang, and catch_unwind cannot contain SEH AVs.
- Implementation direction: remove ilhook from the DInput GetDeviceState hook path. Keep the existing probe-device address discovery, but install keyboard/mouse GetDeviceState detours through src/mh.rs / MinHook, matching the XInput path that already works.
- Required code shape: static extern detours for keyboard and mouse GetDeviceState; AtomicUsize trampoline(s); call original trampoline then zero buffer / injected key. Hook keyboard_addr and mouse_addr with MhHook::new + queue_enable + MH_ApplyQueued. Do not reintroduce hudhook::mh.
- Gate: cargo xwin build; runtime probe with DInput block active; telemetry/log must show DInput keyboard+mouse hook installed, XInput hook installed or retried, zero simulated input, no crash, no MessageBoxDialog.
- Gap closed when: product baseline completes with full input block active. Until then, all visual/render runs are diagnostic only.

R2 — Restore/prove the custom black surface, but distinguish it from native black
- Implementation direction: re-enable existing 01_900_Black carrier:
  - TITLE_CUSTOM_COVER_BLACK_WRAPPER_RVA = 0x793b20, TITLE_CUSTOM_COVER_BLACK_NAME = 01_900_Black.
  - Build it from title_native_menu_visual_begin_title_hook using the same rdx/MenuWindow host args as native title.
  - Pump it from title_custom_cover_menu_window_run_hook after native title/PAB jobs every frame, not as a one-tick artifact.
- Required RAM/native oracles:
  - black_builds > 0, black_last_job plausible, black_last_ret nonzero, run_calls increasing, run_last_cover_job == black_last_job, run_last_cover_window non-null.
  - cover window menu_id read from cover_window+0x180; cover visible flags from CSMenuMan+0x90+menu_id have draw bit set.
  - native title/PAB/logo hidden/suppressed oracles remain true: logo hide calls, press-start hide calls, native title visible flags cleared.
- Gap: native baseline already shows black+footer with custom black disabled. Therefore black pixels alone are invalid. The proof must pair RAM identity of 01_900_Black with event screenshot/pixel classification that rejects visible footer/text/logo.
- Optional stronger gap closure: static RE CSMenuMan draw sequence / z-order for cover_window vs native title/PAB window. If not completed, acceptable temporary proof is “all lower native title/PAB visual surfaces are suppressed and custom cover is the only draw-enabled surface with RAM identity.”
- Gate: runtime event capture at black-cover moment; telemetry proves custom black job active; analyzer says black surface visible and footer/PAB/Continue/logo absent.

R3 — Add separate watcher/oracle events for black and color cover proof
- Current scripts/er-readiness-watch.py only captures logo-replacement/portrait-cover when portrait oracles assert. Add separate stages, not piggybacked on portrait:
  - black_cover predicate: custom black built, run_calls >= small threshold, cover_window non-null, cover draw bit set, native title/PAB/logo suppression true.
  - color_cover predicate: same surface/control path plus color-sentinel telemetry.
  - portrait_cover predicate: existing portrait source/bind/visibility fields.
- Artifacts should be stage-named (e.g. cover-black-screenshot.*, cover-color-screenshot.*, cover-portrait-screenshot.*), with matching event JSON and analysis JSON.
- Analyzer must be stage-aware:
  - black_cover: high black32 ratio, low bright ratio, no footer/text/logo markers.
  - color_cover: rejects all-black, expects chosen sentinel color/bright ratio.
  - portrait_cover: rejects placeholder/chrome-only/ProfileSelect false positives.
- Gap: screenshots remain diagnostic. Stop/score must use RAM/native/pixel fields, not image-only observation.

R4 — Prove agent control of the surface via color
- First attempt: static RE 01_900_Black’s Scaleform tree/value handles. Find whether the full-screen rectangle/display object can be addressed by SceneObjProxy / CSScaleformValue and recolored or alpha/color-transformed. Existing setters include position/scale/visible; color/fill needs RE, not assumption.
- Required proof: change the same surface from black to an unmistakable color (e.g. magenta/green), with RAM identity unchanged (same black/custom cover job/window) and stage-aware pixel/native oracle confirming not black.
- Gap: 01_900_Black may be static and not recolorable. If color cannot be changed in-place, pivot to R5 custom GFX.

R5 — If needed, implement embedded custom GFX/resource path (no loose files, no hudhook)
- Goal: a minimal full-viewport custom Scaleform surface with a root-reachable fill/image symbol, loaded through native MenuWindowJob/Scaleform machinery.
- Static-first tasks:
  - Trace 0x1407acbf0 / 0x1407ad070 MenuWindowJob load path to the actual resource/file open or GFX bytes ingestion boundary.
  - Identify safe hook point to serve embedded custom GFX bytes for a synthetic menu filename/resource name, without loose files and without patching game install.
  - Keep lifetime/order identical to BeginTitle/PAB: constructed from BeginTitle wrapper args and pumped by MenuWindowJob::Run.
- Pre-runtime validation: extend/require scripts/gfx-abc-dump.py summary to prove custom target symbol is root-reachable and placed with visible alpha.
- Gap: we do not yet have a proven hook boundary for replacing GFx bytes in-memory. This is a static RE subtask before coding.

R6 — Prepare the portrait source, but do not confuse source with visibility
- Existing source path to keep/use:
  - init profile renderer table: 0x1409af3a0.
  - refresh profile render targets: 0x1409aa680.
  - sample renderer table: 0x143d6d8d0, CSMenuProfModelRend vtable, renderer+0xa8 CSEzOffscreenRend, offscreen+0x10 TexResCap / SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00.
- Gate: profile source telemetry says renderer/offscreen/TexResCap ready after profile/SL2 readiness, not early title construction.
- Gap: source readiness is not visible pixels. It only unlocks binding work.

R7 — Bind portrait texture into a valid visible custom surface
- Do NOT bind raw TitleBackViewParts+0x8. Prior attempts crashed/failed.
- Required ABI proof from lane E:
  - bind helper deobf 0x1407451c0 / native registrar path expects a ProfileSelect/MenuWindow-like owner.
  - owner rcx should be MenuWindow+0x3b8 bind-owner subobject; owner+0x60 must be initialized fixed-vector; MenuWindow ctor initializes this structure and +0xa20/+0xa28 vector count.
  - registrar dereferences singleton+0x8 GFx movie handle and returns no-op if null; bind must occur after movie handle exists.
- Implementation direction: custom surface’s MenuWindow owner must expose a root-reachable symbol and call bind helper with DLString pair (visible_symbol -> SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00) against that valid owner. Reuse ProfileSelect-like bind owner layout if custom MenuWindow has it; otherwise statically prove equivalent.
- Gate: bind observer hits custom visible symbol, target SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00, owner matches custom cover MenuWindow owner, source TexResCap ready, and event/pixel oracle sees portrait.

R8 — Root-reachable GFX checks before any runtime portrait bind
- Required static checks for any custom/reused GFX:
  - target symbol is in root_reachable_external_images, not only external_images.
  - placement_counts[target_cid] > 0.
  - placing frame has visible alpha (identity or full), sane transform/viewport coverage.
  - if runtime-fed image, also require feeder/source telemetry; exported BitmapData alone is not enough.
- Known false positive to avoid: 05_010_ProfileSelect exports MENU_DummyProfileFace_01..10 but they are not root-reachable, so binding those does not render pixels.

R9 — Final proof / commit cadence
- Commit after each rung that passes: input blocker fix, black cover RAM+event proof, color control proof, custom GFX hook if needed, portrait binding proof.
- Final gates: no hudhook/debug; full DInput+XInput suppression active; zero simulated input; no MessageBoxDialog; no postload modal; custom surface obscures footer/PAB/Continue/logo; portrait source ready; valid bind-owner ABI; root-reachable target symbol; SYSTEX bind hits custom visible symbol; event pixel/native oracle confirms portrait; runtime completes within cap.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
SECOND-WAVE ADDENDUM + FFDEC NOTE (2026-06-28)

Durable headless outputs:
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave2/I2_min_hook_dinput_design.txt
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave2/D2_resource_hook_boundary.txt
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave2/P2_portrait_bind_timing.txt
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave2/Z2_draw_order_oracle.txt

I2 DInput implementation is now concrete:
- Replace ilhook DInput hooks with vendored MinHook, not hudhook.
- Add module statics in src/input_blocker.rs:
  - DINPUT_KB_GET_STATE_ORIG: AtomicUsize
  - DINPUT_MOUSE_GET_STATE_ORIG: AtomicUsize
  - DINPUT_KB_ALSO_MOUSE: AtomicBool for keyboard_addr == mouse_addr.
- Use plain extern "system" detours, no closure hooks. Detour ABI: unsafe extern "system" fn(device: u64, size: u64, data: *mut u8) -> usize. Call trampoline, then if hr == 0 and flags active, zero buffer; keyboard detour writes INJECTED_KEY if set.
- Install with MhHook::new(keyboard_addr, dinput_kb_get_state_hook), optional mouse hook if mouse_addr != keyboard_addr, then MH_ApplyQueued. Keep retry-until-success or only set DINPUT_BLOCK_INSTALLED on success; do not one-shot fail permanently if dinput8/device creation is too early.

D2 resource boundary remains partly open:
- Wrappers pass UTF-16LE name pointer inside descriptor to factory/ctor; confirmed names:
  - 0x142ac3c90 = 05_000_Title
  - 0x142a9b808 = 01_900_Black
- The actual byte load is downstream when MenuWindowJob::Run 0x1407ad1c0 runs through SceneObjProxy/MenuResource/Scaleform load. Candidate hook boundary for embedded custom GFX is still the by-name -> file/open/decode resolver inside that run path, not the wrapper itself.
- Need static Ghidra trace of 0x1407ad1c0 callees to identify the exact VFS/EBL open-by-name call and not-found branch before coding embedded bytes.

P2 portrait bind timing is now sharper:
- Required order:
  1. owner at MenuJobWait + profile_summary loaded.
  2. custom cover MenuWindowJob built and distinct.
  3. cover job pumped inside native title pump; after run(cover_job) returns, cover_window/movie handle becomes meaningful.
  4. profile renderer init 0x1409af3a0 then refresh 0x1409aa680 after SL2/profile readiness.
  5. sample source asserts renderer/offscreen/TexResCap/ready_754/ready_755.
  6. bind helper 0x1407451c0 against valid cover MenuWindow+0x3b8 owner and a root-reachable visible symbol -> SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00.
- Gap: safe bind is not visible unless the target symbol is in the cover movie root display list.

Z2 draw-order/on-top proof recommendation:
- True draw-list order is still not pinned. Use exclusive-visible RAM oracle as the product gate:
  - cover_window = cover_job+0x130 valid; cover_id = *(cover_window+0x180) < 0x47.
  - CSMenuMan+0x90[cover_id] & 0x3 == 0x3.
  - every competing title/PAB/logo/native title id has CSMenuMan+0x90[id] & 0x3 == 0.
- This makes z-order moot: if the custom cover is the only draw-enabled title-band window, it is effectively on top.

FFDEC / JPEXS exploration path:
- Local ffdec exists at /usr/sbin/ffdec; Docker also exists at /usr/local/bin/docker. No ffdec Docker image was present in local image list at check time.
- Use scripts/gfx-abc-dump.py first for repo-consistent summaries. It already shells out to ffdec and computes external_images, placement_counts, root_placed_character_ids, sprite_children, and root_reachable_external_images.
- If a containerized JPEXS run is preferred, use the user-supplied shape with absolute paths, e.g. docker run --rm -v /abs/input:/work/input -v /abs/output:/work/output ffdec [args]. Do not write extracted copyrighted assets into git; only commit summaries/checks.
- Plan impact: add a pre-runtime GFX gate that runs FFDEC over any candidate/custom GFX and fails unless target portrait symbol is root-reachable, placed, visible-alpha, and viewport-covering. This directly closes the ProfileSelect DummyProfileFace false-positive class.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
THIRD-WAVE ADDENDUM (2026-06-28)

Durable headless outputs:
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave3/R3_menuwindow_run_resource_trace.txt
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave3/C3_black_surface_color_control.txt

- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave3/B3_black_cover_oracle_patch_plan.txt

R3 MenuWindow/GFX resource boundary is now much sharper:
- Primary seam: deobf 0x140d78630 (dump 0x140d786e0), labeled by lane as AcquireMenuResource.
  - Args: rcx = MenuResourceManager ([0x143d83148]+8 FD4Singleton), rdx = MenuDesc* (id/type/wchar* name), r8b = acquire flag (MenuWindowJob::Run passes 1).
  - Returns MenuResource*.
  - Equivalent earlier seam: 0x140d6a8e0, same args before +8 manager deref.
- First UTF-16 name deref / lookup-key build: 0x140d7a630 (dump 0x140d7a6e0), a find/cache lookup, not the load.
- Actual miss-path construct/load/open/decode: 0x140d6fc80 (dump 0x140d6fd30), called by cache-miss allocator path; args include rcx=new resource object, rdx=mgr, r8=&map-entry, r9=&MenuDesc.
- Not-found behavior: not a clean null-on-miss path. 0x140d7abc0 lazily creates+loads on cache miss (alloc 0xe8 -> 0x140d6fc80) and registers into mgr+0xbe8 DLFixedVector<...,32>. Null only in a type==2 unload-empty edge. Therefore embedded custom GFX must either return a substitute MenuResource* or intercept before/inside 0x140d6fc80; do not rely on archive miss returning null.

C3 in-place color/control route:
- Existing display helpers confirmed:
  - 0x140d83e20 SetPosition(x,y) -> DisplayInfo flags X=0x1, Y=0x2.
  - 0x140d84090 SetScale(x,y) -> XScale=0x8, YScale=0x10.
  - 0x140d83a50 SetEdgeAAMode -> flag 0x4000.
  - 0x140d844d0 SetVisible -> flag 0x40.
  - 0x140d85220 commits ObjectInterface::SetDisplayInfo via vtable slot *0x1a8 on obj *(CSScaleformValue+0x28)+0x88.
- Likely true recolor route is GFx Cxform / SetColorTransform on the same ObjectInterface, not SetDisplayInfo. Because black multiply remains black, sentinel recolor should use additive cxform terms: magenta = R_add=255,B_add=255; green = G_add=255.
- Remaining exact gap: SetCxform/GetCxform vtable slot not yet pinned. Need static RE on the same ObjectInterface vtable as SetDisplayInfo slot *0x1a8.
- Runtime handle/oracle plan:
  - CSScaleformValue for black/title logo surface is SceneObjProxy+0x18; existing notes tie TitleBackViewParts+0x88 to SceneObjProxy+0x18.
  - SCENE_OBJ_PROXY_CTOR_RVA 0x74a700 stores MenuWindow back-ref at proxy+0x20 and should fire for 01_900_Black during ctor 0x1407acf80.
  - Ready gate: (CSScaleformValue+0x20) & 0x8f == 0xa.
  - Obj ptr: *(CSScaleformValue+0x28)+0x88. Use GetCxform read-back as RAM/native sentinel; screenshot remains diagnostic only.
  - 0x140d82570 current-frame reader can be used as liveness check.

F3 FFDEC/GFX gate is now concrete:
- Local FFDec: /usr/sbin/ffdec v26.2.1. Docker is optional, not needed for current workflow.
- Existing cached GFX paths:
  - target/autoresearch/gfx-cache/menu/05_000_title.gfx
  - target/autoresearch/gfx-cache/menu/05_001_title_logo.gfx  (cover target)
  - target/autoresearch/gfx-cache/menu/05_010_profileselect.gfx  (known false-positive class)
  - target/autoresearch/gfx-cache/menu/05_020_titleinformation.gfx
  - target/autoresearch/gfx-cache/menu/02_903_nowloading2.gfx
  - target/autoresearch/gfx-cache/menu/02_904_nowloading3.gfx
- Use scripts/gfx-abc-dump.py as the repo-consistent gate: it wraps FFDec -swf2xml/-export and emits external_images, placement_counts, root_placed_character_ids, sprite_children, root_reachable_external_images, per-timeline rows with alpha_mult_term + matrix.
- 05_001_title_logo.gfx finding:
  - external images include MENU_Title_GR(0 placements), MENU_Title_EldenRing(0), MENU_DS3_LOGO(0), MENU_Title_EldenRing_01(char 4, placements=1).
  - root_placed_character_ids = ['6','7']; sprite 7 places char 4 MENU_Title_EldenRing_01 (2532x1532 image, scale 0.5 -> approx 1266x766 px).
  - root_reachable_external_images = {char 4}; other logo images are dead/unreachable false targets.
  - Depth-3 container alpha ramps 0->256 and holds opaque through Title_TopMenu frame 112; 256 means opaque.
  - Practical candidate target symbol for bind/control work: MENU_Title_EldenRing_01, not the dead logo names.

Z3 CSMenuMan flags semantics:
- Bit0/draw semantics and table location/indexing are high-confidence from static writer catalog; +0x90 flags carry no z-order.
- Exclusive-visible oracle is sufficient only in the per-id sense:
  - cover id bit0 == 1.
  - competitor ids bit0 == 0.
  - Do NOT assert only one whole-table bit, because title/logo/PAB can legitimately have bit0 simultaneously in normal flow.
- If a competitor still has bit0==1, flags cannot prove cover is above it. Therefore product proof must suppress competitors, not rely on z-order.

B3 minimal black-cover oracle patch plan:
- Add/emit these telemetry JSON fields:
  - oracle_title_custom_cover_black_cover_window
  - oracle_title_custom_cover_black_cover_menu_id
  - oracle_title_custom_cover_black_cover_flags
  - oracle_title_custom_cover_black_cover_draw_bit_set
  - oracle_title_pab_information_visual_current_menu_id
  - oracle_title_pab_information_visual_current_flags
  - oracle_title_pab_information_visual_current_draw_bit_set
  - oracle_title_custom_cover_black_exclusive_visible
- Primary go/no-go: oracle_title_custom_cover_black_exclusive_visible == true.
- Supporting diagnosis: black build/run counts > 0, cover draw bit true, native title draw bit false, PAB/title-information draw bit false.

Immediate recommended implementation order after wave 3:
1. Implement DInput MinHook replacement first; restore full input blocker proof.
2. Re-enable 01_900_Black and add B3 exclusive-visible telemetry only (no portrait bind yet).
3. Use F3 gate against 05_001_title_logo.gfx / MENU_Title_EldenRing_01 for any bind/control experiment; reject unreachable names.
4. If black exclusive-visible passes, pin SetCxform/GetCxform slot for a color sentinel, or pivot to R3 embedded resource hook at 0x140d78630/0x140d6fc80.

comments.text: Black-cover stall hypothesis from user observation (2026-06-28): the custom/native GFX/MenuWindowJob we add may innately stall the title flow because it lacks the expected on-end / advance epilogue. Runtime artifact product-continue-direct-20260628-121039 supports this: after wiring MenuWindowJob::Run pumping, 01_900_Black built and ran 1644 times, but the probe timed out with player_present=false/world_stable_samples=0. Investigate static MenuWindowJob completion semantics / asset timeline epilogue before adding more cover surfaces; do not assume a visually-valid surface is flow-neutral.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Static confirmation of black-cover stall (2026-06-28): MenuWindowJob::Run 0x1407ad1c0 only enters its finalization/result path when job+0x130 is null (no live window). The failed pump-hook run product-continue-direct-20260628-121039 had cover_window nonzero and 1644 custom-cover run calls, so it stayed in the live-window path and never supplied the title-flow completion/epilogue; timeout/player_present=false matches this. Treat separate 01_900_Black pumping as a stalled route unless we can prove/force completion semantics. Prefer epilogue-neutral approaches: mutate/cover an existing title surface with native title epilogue, or hook the resource used by an already-scheduled title job rather than adding an independent persistent MenuWindowJob.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Strategy pivot accepted (2026-06-28): build a small custom GFX on disk only as a diagnostic/provenance oracle, let the game load it through the native MenuResource/Scaleform path, observe exactly how it lands in memory/globals, then embed/copy those bytes from the DLL for the product path instead of shipping a loose on-disk file. This avoids independent MenuWindowJob epilogue stalls by targeting an already-scheduled title resource/load path and preserves the final no-loose-file/no-hudhook requirement.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Found epilogue-neutral route (2026-06-28): target the already-scheduled TitleBackViewParts / 05_001_Title_Logo resource, not a new MenuWindowJob. Evidence: 05_001_title_logo.gfx has native title timeline labels FadeIn/TextFadeIn/Title_TopMenu/FadeOut and frame_end=134, and is embedded in TitleTopDialog at +0xaa8 with GFx handle TitleBackViewParts+0x88. Runtime memories show reusing the existing dialog-owned TitleBackViewParts is stable, while duplicate TitleBack construction crashes and independent 01_900_Black pumping stalls. Diagnostic plan: create/modify a custom on-disk 05_001_title_logo.gfx sentinel only to observe native load/provenance, then move to a DLL-embedded resource response at AcquireMenuResource 0x140d78630 / load construct 0x140d6fc80 for the product path. Root-reachable target in the stock logo asset is MENU_Title_EldenRing_01; dead logo names must be ignored.

title: Investigate manual telemetry crash without overclaiming DLL causality
description:
Correction / evidence standard update (2026-06-26): the previous wording overclaimed causality.

Observed facts only:
- Multiple DLL-loaded manual telemetry variants exited rc=5 during press-any-button / Load-menu attempts.
- The staged save was initially copied read-only; after chmod/copy to a writable user save, a vanilla/offline launch with LazyLoader disabled did not hit the same immediate crash point.
- A later reduced DLL run still exited rc=5, but the comparison was not controlled enough to isolate whether the root cause was DLL load, CSTask registration, telemetry reads, launch state, save state, timing, or another environmental factor.

Do not describe the evidence as proving product-DLL/CSTask causality. The task is to design a cleaner investigation and decouple telemetry only if the stronger evidence supports it.

Potential next evidence paths:
- vanilla/offline + external /proc memory sampler, no DLL loaded;
- a truly minimal DLL smoke that only loads/unloads, no CSTask and no telemetry;
- a staged ladder: DLL load only -> telemetry file write only -> task registration only -> specific reads.

Artifacts from the overclaiming attempt:
- /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/manual-telemetry/offline-load-menu-clean/
- /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/manual-telemetry/offline-load-menu-minimal/
- /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/manual-telemetry/offline-load-menu-nodetour/
- /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/manual-telemetry/offline-load-menu-jsononly/

- Tracks profile-face hits, slot mask, post-world slot mask, first/last owner, last pair, last slot index, and first/last bind sequence.
- Emits `load-menu-semaphore: profile-face-bind ... slot_mask=... post_world=... all_slots=...` log lines.
- Adds telemetry JSON fields `oracle_load_menu_profile_face_*` for next manual run after loading a character and quitting to main menu.
Validation so far: cargo fmt; cargo xwin check; cargo xwin release build. Runtime validation requires next manual run to the Load Game menu.

title: Guard false positive blocks exact Elden Ring runtime cleanup
description:
Cupcake/OPA guard false positive during autoresearch runtime cleanup.

Blocked command attempted an exact /proc cmdline scan and SIGTERM/SIGKILL cleanup for `eldenring.exe` and `start_protected_game.exe` after `.auto/measure.sh` runtime probe timed out. The repo AGENTS.md explicitly allows process detection/teardown of stale `start_protected_game.exe`; the forbidden action is launching `start_protected_game.exe` directly or via Steam/Proton. The command did not launch anything; it only read /proc and killed exact matching processes after a runtime timeout.

Observed block text: "Cupcake blocked this Elden Ring EAC launcher command. Do not run `start_protected_game.exe` directly or via Proton/Wine/Steam from agent workflows. Runtime work must avoid the EAC launcher and use only approved offline/direct eldenring.exe probe paths."

Needed fix: refine the guard to distinguish launch/exec commands from post-probe detection/cleanup/teardown commands, or provide an approved cleanup helper that can inspect/terminate exact Elden Ring/protected-game process names without tripping the launch guard.

Impact: forced a cleanup pivot during autoresearch run after `run_experiment` timeout; the run still needs to be logged as crash.
close_reason: Fixed by policy/comment clarification and regression coverage for exact /proc SIGTERM/SIGKILL cleanup; launch forms remain denied.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Active repo: /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs. Fix applied in .cupcake/policies/claude/bash_elden_ring_launch_guard.rego plus Rego and engine regression tests. Exact blocked-command symptom recorded on this issue: a Python /proc cmdline scan plus SIGTERM/SIGKILL cleanup for eldenring.exe and start_protected_game.exe was denied with Cupcake blocked this Elden Ring EAC launcher command, even though it was teardown-only and did not launch the protected executable. Validation: opa test .cupcake/policies/claude/bash_elden_ring_launch_guard.rego .cupcake/tests/bash_elden_ring_launch_guard_test.rego => PASS 34/34; python3 scripts/test-cupcake-policies.py => cupcake policy regression tests passed.

title: Chase down bugs surfaced by correct .NET plumbing (param-rows + tooling now testable)
description:
Now that both SoulsFormats bridges build+run on this host (TFM auto-detect + RollForward, commit 1631896), we can actually exercise the host tooling and real bugs surface. Track and fix all of them here.

Known so far:
1. er-param-inspect 'rows/validate' -> bridge prints 'Param not found: SpEffectParam' (crates/soulsformats/bridge/Program.cs). Suspected Linux-only bug: Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension does NOT treat '\' as a separator on Linux .NET, so Windows-style binder entry names don't normalize to the bare param name. Fix: normalize backslashes before stem compare; also improve the not-found error to list available param names.

Add new bugs as they're found while testing.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Bug 1 FIXED (commit 68f65f6): param-rows 'Param not found: SpEffectParam' was a Linux-only Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension separator bug (backslash not treated as a separator off-Windows). Now normalizes to forward-slash before the stem compare; not-found error lists available params. Verified: rows -> SpEffectParam 11325 rows, id 4330 found; validate -> all 3 seeded effect IDs resolve. Scanned both bridges: shaderbridge runs under wine (Windows separator) so its Path.GetFileName is correct; no other instances. Host suite green (er-soulsformats 19, er-param-inspect 3, shaders e2e passes). No further plumbing bugs on the currently-testable surface; keep open for later phases.

title: Learn ER shader RE/build/edit pipeline
notes: Goal: reverse-engineer, build, edit ER shaders. Worktree .worktrees/er-shaders (branch research/er-shaders). Findings so far: ER ships /shader/ (DX11) and /shader_d3d12/ (DX12, the live path; vkd3d-proton.cache confirms DX12). Containers: *.shaderbnd.dcx (DCX BND4) per domain (gxflvershader, gxposteffect, gxshader, gxgui, grass, speedtree, gxraytracing, gxffxshader, gxdecal, gxgui, gxrenderershader) + shaderbdle*.shaderbdlebnd.dcx bundles + pipelinestatecache.dat.dcx. Extraction path: Smithbox binary DLLs at ~/.local/share/smithbox/app (Andre.IO.VFS.ArchiveBinderVirtualFileSystem mounts BHD/BDT; native bhd5_decrypt_rust.dll does RSA). UXM dict at app/Assets/UXM Dictionaries/EldenRingDictionary.txt (130882 paths). Host .NET 10 SDK present. Building .NET extractor under scripts/shaderlab/. NEXT: confirm bytecode DXBC vs DXIL by dumping a shaderbnd member.

title: Resolve CORRUPTED-SAVE SEMAPHORE (GR_System_Message 401106) in working autoload
description: NEW bug (NOT pre-existing) per working-milestone-bugs-are-new-not-preexisting-2026-06-24: the zero-input autoload now reaches the playable world (runs product-continue-direct-20260624-103059 headless, 103555 on-screen), but the save-override layer still fires CORRUPTED-SAVE SEMAPHORE -- GetGR_System_Message id 401106 (save data is corrupted) during validate/write of the staged gold copy. Today it is INTERCEPTED (oracle_msgbox_total_builds=0, no dialog, did not block the load) but it must be ROOT-CAUSE RESOLVED, not suppressed. Evidence: er-effects-autoload-debug.log line 'save-override: CORRUPTED-SAVE SEMAPHORE ... id=401106 ... gold save was read but rejected on validate/write'. Source save (save-files/150-Banon/ER0000.sl2) has NO companion files, so it is not a missing .bak/steam_autocloud.vdf copy gap. Root-cause leads from save-write-fail-ROOTCAUSE-INVESTIGATION-2026-06-23: save-op result handler callers 0x826ba7/0x826bb8 (Updating save data step), in-place write integrity, or steamid-binding mismatch at runtime. GOAL: gold save validates and writes cleanly so 401106 never fires. Verify with the on-screen probe (RUNTIME_ONSCREEN=1) -- a clean run shows oracle_msgbox_total_builds=0 AND no CORRUPTED-SAVE SEMAPHORE line in the debug log.
close_reason: Not a corrupted save -- confirmed false write-back validate flag. Corrected save (save-files/150-Banon) loads the real L150 Banon into the playable world across headless+on-screen runs; 401106 fires only on validate/write, is intercepted (msgbox=0), and does not block. Closing per user; will re-report if it recurs.
