title: RE: title row-build chain — open_menu posts MenuJob chain, MenuWindow::Update pumps it
notes:
Static RE 2026-06-23 (deobf VAs, base 0x140000000).

SAVE-UPDATE (task 1-3, now MOOT per coordinator: read-only staged copy was the cause):
- Save-update step gate FUN_14082cfa0 (deobf): returns (TitleFlowContext[+0x148] regulationVersion) < (CSRegulationManagerImp[+0x44] regulationVersion). Singleton GLOBAL_CSRegulationManager @ deobf 0x143d86b08 (dump 0x143d86c58). If gate true -> save-data migration job (FUN_140826b40 factory, msgs 401112/401106); if false -> FUN_1407a7250 builds a clean MenuJobResult::SetResult(Success) no-op. Orchestrator FUN_14082f700 (deobf), step dispatcher FUN_14082db10.

ROW-BUILD CHAIN (the real blocker):
- open_menu = FUN_1409b24e0 (deobf) BUILDS a chain of MenuJobs via CS::MenuJob::ChainMenuJobs and POSTS it into the MenuWindow job queue (MenuWindow+0x10 ring, active slot +0xa28) via FUN_1409aa580/FUN_14078e1d0/FUN_1407928c0.
- The rows are NOT built synchronously by open_menu. They build when the posted chain's Run() is PUMPED.
- PUMP: title update FUN_1409aac10 (deobf, TitleTopDialog::update, vtable slot2) -> tail calls MenuWindow::Update FUN_140745520 (deobf; dump 0x140745670) which at its body calls ExecuteMenuJob(&MenuWindow+0xa28) + FUN_1407a9460(&MenuWindow+0x10) — the FD4 job pump that runs the chain's vtable[2] Run each frame.
- The row-build LEAF is FUN_1409ac760 (deobf; dump 0x1409ac8b0): reads GameDataMan::GetProfileSummary + GetMenuSystemSaveLoad()->saveSlot, FUN_140261c30(summary,slot) presence check; in else-branch builds the Continue/load MenuJob via FUN_140826600 factory (the golden 0x1409ac9cb site) and ChainMenuJobs onto param_2 (the TitleTopDialog). Reached via vtable-Run chain: FUN_1409a7940(lambda invoke,vt slot 0x144902450) -> FUN_1409a80d0 -> FUN_1409a71a0 -> FUN_1409a92a0 (gates on titleFlowContext+0x14c==0 & MenuWindow+0x1e8 ShouldContinue) -> FUN_1409a9260 -> FUN_1409ac8b0. All are std::function lambda-invoke / MenuJob Run vtable methods.
- CRITICAL: the native title update pump FUN_1409aad60 ITSELF calls open_menu FUN_1409b2630 at line152: if(FUN_140e85f60() /*accept-pressed getter*/ && dialog->field_0xa40==0) open_menu(dialog). So natively open_menu runs INSIDE the pump frame; the SAME frame's MenuWindow::Update drains the posted chain. Our maybe_auto_open_menu calls open_menu from the recurring GAME-TASK thread (out of pump context) — the chain is posted but the title pump must still tick MenuWindow::Update to drain it.

DELIVERABLE (zero-input): rows build if MenuWindow::Update (dump 0x140745670) ticks the MenuWindow that open_menu posted into, on frames after open_menu. Either (a) call open_menu from inside a hook on TitleTopDialog::update 0x1409aac10 so post+drain share the frame (the native order), or (b) ensure the title update pump keeps ticking after our out-of-thread open_menu (it should, since it runs every frame) and confirm we post into the LIVE MenuWindow (owner+0xe0 dialog's super_MenuWindow). The continue-scan finding 0 MenuMemberFuncJob nodes suggests the posted chain is not being drained in our flow — verify the title update 0x1409aac10 is still being ticked and that our open_menu used the live dialog as receiver.

title: RE: title offline-mode MessageBox gate = notReleaseFlag55 / Menu.IsEnableOnlineMode
description: Title-menu-open offline popup (GR_System_Message 401170 'Starting in offline mode', vtable msgbox) is gated by TitleFlowContext+0x18c notReleaseFlag55. FUN_14082fda0(deobf, offline step) takes clean no-popup branch FUN_1407a7250 iff ctx->notReleaseFlag55 != 0. The ctx-init step FUN_14082d0d0(deobf) computes notReleaseFlag55 = !Menu_IsEnableOnlineMode(). Menu_IsEnableOnlineMode (deobf 0x140e56310) returns cached debug-property bool (global byte Menu.IsEnableOnlineMode @0x144588afc, default TRUE, init-guard DAT_144588b00 @0x144588b00). So with online-mode enabled + offline (no login) the title flow shows the 401170 popup before building Continue/Load/NewGame rows. ZERO-INPUT FIX: hook Menu_IsEnableOnlineMode(deobf 0x140e56310) to return false (or set global byte @0x144588afc=0 with guard @0x144588b00=-1) BEFORE TitleTopDialog ctor (deobf 0x1409a8180) runs its FUN_14082d0d0 call; then notReleaseFlag55=1 and the menu rows build with zero MessageBoxDialog. Alt: set TitleFlowContext+0x18c=1 directly (singleton = TitleTopDialog+0xa38). 401110 is just a generic ProgressTypeInfo loading label, not a popup. GhidraScripts: scripts/ghidra/AutoloadGate[2-8].java

title: OWN-LOAD final wall: world-resource streaming stalls at loading screen (player never spawns)
description: After continue_confirm/SetState5 fires cleanly (commit 8e70cd6) the engine reaches the LOADING SCREEN for the real char but STALLS -- player_present never flips true, full 110s alive (reason=timeout_seconds_budget_exhausted, NOT a crash). Stall telemetry: fd4_io_pool/worker/stream_task all present, io_device present but IDLE (inflight_10=0, reqhandle_20=0 -- not blocked on IO), now_loading logic-flag=0 while the visual loading screen persists. So world resources never finish streaming, but the iodev isn't waiting -- the stall is in the world-load/MoveMapStep pump, not the save-IO we bypass. INVESTIGATE: (1) capture mms_state (MoveMapStep / CSFeMan state) at the stall -- memory predicted WorldResWait(13). (2) Does the menu-free SetState5 skip a world-load step the native menu performs (a map-load request never enqueued, MoveMapStep not advanced, or owner+0x138/+0x300 post-continue calls 0x14082cd60/0x140ca7660 need state we lack)? (3) Try the emk-resman streaming-driver coldbuild lever (bd emk-resman-streaming-driver-coldbuild-stub-lever-2026). (4) Rule out the 110s cap by reading whether ANY stream progress counter advances over the run. Compare against a GOLDEN native-menu load's per-frame mms/stream telemetry. Relates: own-load-reaches-loading-screen-2026-06-22, coldmount-c30 (world-stream completion open item), b80-* (world-res may reuse the streaming subsystem).

After own_load_drive's native 0x67b290 parse yields a REAL c30 + real char, fire the GUARDED SetState5/continue_confirm 0x140b0e180 to stream the character into the playable world. GUARD (save-safety ABSOLUTE -- SetState5 autosaves): only fire if c30 != 0xa010000 (default) AND c30 != 0xffffffff AND char fingerprint real (level/stats non-default) -- all already verified true in the proven run. Then validate with a gated probe targeting world-stable: oracle_player_present=true + oracle_now_loading=0 + correct c30 + char fingerprint, presses=0, no msgbox, save md5 unchanged (backup/restore). This is now LOW-RISK: c30 is real so streaming goes to the right map (the prior crash cause -- c30=default collision -- is gone). Add as a phase after PHASE_DONE in own_load_drive, file-gated (e.g. own_load_continue=1) so verify-only stays the default. Relates: coldmount-c30-is-the-single-key, continue-fire-is-continue_confirm.

title: OWN-LOAD verify-only: hook 0x67b100 to feed sliced .sl2 body into native 0x67b290
description:
GOAL: prove the OWN-LOAD buffer-feed mechanism end-to-end, SAVE-SAFE (no SetState5, no autosave). This is the cleanest "reuse native fns" form of OWN-LOAD step 3 (see bd reuse-native-fns-not-reimplement-2026-06-22) and is fully de-risked by static RE this turn.

MECHANISM (validated static, 2026-06-22):
- Hook the FSM-gated read 0x67b100(rcx=out_buf, edx=size): when our one-shot gate flag is set, memcpy our sliced plaintext slot body into out_buf (length = min(edx, body.len())) and return 1 (al=1); else call original. Gate is REQUIRED: 0x67b100 has 4 callers (own-load-67b100-callers-static-2026-06-22) -- ours (0x67b290) + 3 native menu loader; never intercept the menu path.
- Get the slot body from er_save_loader::bnd4::slot_body(sl2_bytes, slot) (DONE, tested). Read the .sl2 path natively (builder 0x140e0e680 gives the EldenRing/<id> folder) -- do NOT hardcode (bd dont-hardcode-savefile-tied-constants-2026-06-22).
- Set gate, call native 0x67b290(slot) in-process UNCHANGED. It does NOT re-check b80 after the read (only test al,al; je fail @0x67b2de), so our al=1 flows into native parse: 0x2567b0 -> 0x261b80 -> GameMan+0xac0=slot -> 0x67bd70 c30 -> 0x140258840 stream deserialize -> char-apply 0x140e365c0. Clear gate.
- The c30-write gate [0x143d68078] is built AT BOOT (GameMan master init FUN_140679970), present at the menu (bd own-load-c30-gate-built-at-boot-2026-06-22) -> 0x67bd70 should write the REAL c30.

VERIFY (read-back, NO SetState5):
- GameMan+0xc30 == real map (NOT 0xa010000 default) and NOT 0xffffffff.
- PlayerGameData char fingerprint (level/stats) == the save's real char (Banon RL150 golden, or the 45-Slots fixture slot's real values), NOT level-9 default.
- Log via append_autoload_debug; publish to telemetry oracle; presses==0; save md5 unchanged (backup/restore harness).

IMPL NOTES:
- New gated function in src/experiments.rs, armed via the reliable er-effects-autoload.txt channel (new key, e.g. own_load=1 + slot=N), mirroring own_stepper/cold_char_mount wiring (SaveLoadRequest fields + experiments arming ~line 324-341).
- MinHook via hudhook::mh (MhHook/MH_Initialize/MH_ApplyQueued) like existing detours; install on base+0x67b100.
- Reuse transmute(base+RVA) call pattern. Addresses are deobf-authoritative (grounded this turn): 0x67b290, 0x67b100, GameMan global 0x143d69918, c30 @ GameMan+0xc30.
- ONLY after this verify-only probe passes: add the GUARDED SetState5/continue to reach the playable world (separate follow-up), guarded on c30-real + fingerprint.

HARNESS: gated runtime probe (Steam MUST be up), .auto contract (timeout <= .auto/runtime_timeout_cap_seconds=120), save-swap+restore, evidence-teardown via er-readiness-watch. Relates: own-load-step3-hook-67b100-feed-buffer-2026-06-22, own-load-67b100-callers-static-2026-06-22, own-load-c30-gate-built-at-boot-2026-06-22, coldmount-c30-is-the-single-key-write-conditions-and-recipe-2026.
close_reason: PROVEN at runtime 2026-06-22: gated 0x67b100 hook feeds sliced .sl2 body into native 0x67b290 -> c30 0xa010000->0x1c000000 (real map), full real char (level7 Hero) applied to PlayerGameData, zero input, no msgbox, no SetState5, save md5 unchanged. See bd own-load-buffer-feed-PROVEN-runtime-2026-06-22.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Subagent harness fix applied after run 67b638d2-2125-4463-be37-dfda37132733 stale-run failure: root cause was async runner startup failing with `Cannot find module '@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent'` from `pi-subagents/src/shared/utils.ts` when launched under the package-local jiti/node context. The package marks `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` as an optional peer dependency, but `utils.ts` imported it at module top level, making async runner startup hard-fail before status/result files could be written. Fixed installed package file `/home/banon/.pi/agent/npm/node_modules/pi-subagents/src/shared/utils.ts` to lazy-load the optional peer via `createRequire` and fall back to `.pi` when absent. Validation: direct async runner smoke using a minimal config now reaches completed status (`min-debug`) instead of stale-run repair; `subagent status min-debug` shows State complete and log/output paths. This is an installed-package hotfix; it will need upstreaming/reapplying if the npm package is upgraded.

title: Fix autoload oracle false positives for post-load modal and non-rendered player
description: Current north_star_score can remain 1400 even when the run is not actually acceptable: a post-load MessageBoxDialog/modal may remain and require OK, or save/player identity data may be present while the character is not visibly rendered/fully controllable. Add oracle checks that fail/decrease north_star_score for post-load blocking popups and missing visible/rendered player. RE the additional address/state that proves or enables player rendering, beyond existing save identity/player-data fields.

title: Prove DLL character load is correct + faster than native menu (vanilla offline)
description: New goal: with er_effects_rs.dll hooked into vanilla ER offline, drive a COMPLETE, CORRECT character load via the DLL (in-context functor invokes allowed -- the zero-input purity bar is dropped) and PROVE it is (a) correct (same char/map/state as a native load) and (b) faster than normal menu navigation. Implement STAGE 2 of own_stepper (invoke d180 Load-Game functor -> ProfileLoadDialog -> load_activate slot N -> native pump mounts c30=real+ac0+char via io18/io20 -> continue_confirm -> SetState(5)). Add frame-stamped timing + correctness instrumentation. Capture native baseline. Document for Ghidra. Save-safety: never SetState(5) until c30 real-mounted.

comments.text:
Runtime trace decision, 2026-06-27 profile-row/portrait investigation:

At the LOAD GAME profile row, the feature-relevant information for the product/autoload investigation is already on screen and already captured by native in-process trace dumps. Do not press E/OK from that row when the immediate question is portrait/profile-row population.

Why: E on the highlighted LOAD GAME row is the native OK/confirm action for the selected save slot. It leaves the row-population/portrait-builder state and advances into the selected-profile load/confirmation path. That path is useful only for later load-chain tracing; it is a different question from how the visible ProfileSummary row gets populated and how the native portrait/equipment renderer receives face/equipment data.

- No hit on old 0x25f900 hypothesis.
- ProfileSummary container dump: slot0 = container+0x18, slot stride = 0x2a0, name Banon at slot+0x0, level 150 at slot+0x24, FACE buffer begins at slot+0x40, equipment/ChrAsm source begins at slot+0x1a8, next slot name Dark Moon Bean at slot+0x2a0.

Use E from this state only after deliberately pivoting to load-path tracing: confirmation dialog, selected-profile load job, or save-read variants. Do not use it while still collecting profile-row/portrait population evidence.

title: RE: what populates/opens the title main-menu (Continue/Load d180) into the CSMenu registry owner+0xe0
description: STAGE 1c proved SetState(2)=BeginLogo (10->2->3->10) builds the CSMenu registry container at owner+0xe0 (holder vt 0x142b26468, update 0x1409aac10, count+0x60, registry sub-obj [holder+0xa60]) but does NOT register/open the Continue/Load(d180)/NewGame entries -- only the 3 title VIEWS (c000 BackScreen/c140/c280 TitleTopDialog) tick. Decode: (1) STEP_BeginTitle 0x140b0c5b0 -- does it register menu entries or just build the container? (2) WHO/WHAT registers Continue/Load/d180 into the CSMenu registry [holder+0xa60] and what TRIGGERS the open (a SetState, a callable functor/action on the TitleTopDialog vt 0x142b26468 ctor 0x1409a8180 update 0x1409aac10, or genuine input?). Find a ZERO-INPUT trigger (SetState/native action), per no-compromises-standard. (3) Decode the CSMenu registry iteration in 0x1409aac10 ([holder+0xa60] map structure, entries dispatched [entry+0x38]->vt[+0x10]) so the own-stepper can walk it to enumerate entries + locate d180 (functor->dialog_factory 0x14081ead0). Seed: invoke beads via /home/banon/.local/bin/bd directly; read memories with 'bd recall <key>'.
notes: CORRECTION (2026-06-17, objdump-verified): the registrar entry VA is 0x1409b24e0 (RVA 0x9b24e0, file offset 0x9b1ae0), NOT 0x1409b1ae0 -- the original subagent slipped the foff->VA conversion by 0xa00 (VA = foff + 0x140000a00, so foff 0x9b1ae0 -> 0x1409b24e0). Disasm at foff 0x9b1ae0 confirms the registrar: 'mov byte [rcx+0xa40],1; add rcx,0xa60; lea rdx,desc(0x142b264f0); call set_state(0x1407499e0)'. STAGE 1d runtime (2026-06-17, NO crash, NO write): calling the WRONG 0x1409b1ae0(dialog) fired cleanly but did nothing (still only the 3 title views tick, menu not populated) -- which CONFIRMED the dialog identification + guards are correct (holder vt 0x142b26468 matched, latch [+0xa40]==0, state machine [+0xa60]=0x7ffe.. non-null) and isolated the bug to the function address. All OTHER agent addresses (vtable 0x142b26468, latch +0xa40, state machine +0xa60, desc 0x142b264f0, set_state 0x1407499e0) verified correct. Retesting with 0x1409b24e0.
close_reason: Decoded: menu-open trigger = TitleTopDialog registrar 0x1409b1ae0, input-free, directly callable with rcx=owner+0xe0. STAGE 1d implemented.

Poll GameMan+0xc30 for real (!=0xa010000 default for non-m10). STRICTLY NO-WRITE until c30 is genuinely real-mounted.

title: Impl: own-stepper zero-input menu nav (invoke c000 -> Load entry -> d180 dialog functor)
description: In own_stepper_idx10, after SetState(3): drive the multi-level nav by invoking menu-item action functors (mirror 0x1407ad2b9: rcx=[item+0xa8], rax=[rcx], rdx=item+0x10, call [rax+0x10]), gated on [item+0x10]==0. Step 1 invoke c000 functor -> main menu; step 2 invoke captured Load-Game entry functor -> Load dialog; step 3 invoke d180 functor -> ProfileLoadDialog. Use the menu-item Update hook (functor_chain_hits_factory) to identify each level robustly. NO-WRITE: no SetState(5) anywhere in this issue.
notes: DECODED TitleTopDialog::update 0x1409aac10 (foff 0x9aa210) FULLY (2026-06-16, static RE on decrypted on-disk eldenring.exe 1.16.1). STATE MACHINE on [dialog+0xa60] (FD4 SM). State name-keys are INLINE ASCII at the descriptor VAs (is_in_state 0x140749b20 / set_state 0x1407499e0 compare node.name==desc.name AND require node flags&0x8f>=2): 0x142a90500='FadeIn', 0x142a8f9e8='Loop', 0x142b264f0='TextFadeOut' (the menu-list/open key the registrar 0x1409b24e0 sets). update flow: if is_in_state(sm,'FadeIn') -> run FadeIn anim predicate; the OR-fallbacks 0x14078ae50/0x14078ae40 are STUBBED 'xor al,al;ret' (always false) so only the per-state std::function anim-complete predicate (built by 0x14075a4d0/0x14075a360) can set sil=1=ready. When sil=1 it set_states sub-SM to 'Loop' (0x1409a62d0: add rcx,0x88; set_state(...,'Loop')). The MENU-OPEN registrar 0x1409b24e0 is called from TWO sites in update: (A) 0x1409aade8 gated by the Loop-ready/anim path; (B) 0x1409aae17 gated by 0x140e85f50 = 'movzx eax,byte[0x144589bdc]; ret' = THE ACCEPT/INPUT BYTE. So native press->menu is INPUT-GATED on 0x144589bdc. set_state 0x1407499e0 AND its longer sibling 0x140749a20 (foff 0x749020) are BOTH conditional (flags&0x8f>=2 gate at 0x140749a5f & 0x140749aac) -> NO unconditional set_state exists (option c DEAD). STUCK STATE at our SetState(2)->3->10 path = the FadeIn intro/animation node (flags&0x8f<2 = transitioning/not-settled); its anim-complete predicate never fires under our forced pump (and the accept-byte path needs input). LEVERS: (a) write the accept byte [0x144589bdc]=1 for one frame -> update site B fires registrar zero-input-ish but that IS synthesizing input (NO per no-compromises). (b) force node flags [node+0x20]|=2 then call registrar -> brittle. (c) DEAD. (d) BEST ZERO-INPUT: call the registrar 0x1409b24e0 directly BUT first ensure the dialog SM has SETTLED past FadeIn to a flags>=2 node, OR bypass set_state by calling the item-builders directly. CLEANEST: let the native pump tick update for enough frames so FadeIn anim completes naturally (flags>=2) THEN our direct registrar call's internal set_state('TextFadeOut') proceeds. DIAGNOSTIC read path (read-only): sm=[dialog+0xa60]; inner=[sm]; node=inner.vtable[+0x8](inner) (rcx=[sm];rax=[rcx];call[rax+0x8]); flags=[node+0x20]&0x8f; name=node-name-getter 0x140d82310(node). Log flags<2 vs name to see stuck state.

title: RE: identify+decode main-menu 'Load Game' entry functor (docall 0x14078ee60) -> opens Load dialog d180
description: After the main menu builds (c3c0/c640/cdc0, docall 0x14078ee60), identify WHICH entry is 'Load Game' and decode its +0xa8 action functor: does invoking it open the Load submenu/build the ProfileLoadDialog item d180 (functor->dialog_factory 0x14081ead0)? Map the iterator 0x1407aa1f0 sibling enumeration so the own-stepper can locate the Load-Game entry robustly (functor-chain classification, no offset guessing). Cite disasm. Give the invoke shape.
notes: DONE: Load-Game entry = item d180, _Do_call 0x140820c60 (add rcx,8; jmp dialog_factory 0x14081ead0) -- the ONLY caller of dialog_factory. So functor_chain_hits_factory==0x14081ead0 uniquely+robustly IDs Load-Game; NO +0x58 descriptor guessing. Generic dispatcher 0x14078ee60 = forwarder jmp [[cap+0x10d8]+0x10]; items c3c0/c640/cdc0 = Continue/NewGame/Settings/Quit (non-dialog). owner+0xe0 holder = CSMenu REGISTRY (vt 0x142b26468, update 0x1409aac10): registry sub-object at [holder+0xa60], entries dispatched via [entry+0x38]->vtable[+0x10] -- NOT a [+0x18]/[+0x60] Sequence; do NOT offset-walk owner+0xe0. Leaf Update 0x1407ad1c0 fires functor only when [item+0x130]==0 && [item+0xa8]!=0 && [item+0x10]==0. CRITICAL SEQUENCING: at bare BeginTitle only c000(BackScreen) ticks; d180 does NOT exist until the main menu is built. Zero-input d180 won't TICK (hook caught it in the user-drive only because user navigated). So to LOCATE d180 zero-input: walk the CSMenu registry [holder+0xa60], OR drive menu so d180 ticks. title: Fix Cupcake env guard shell-variable false positive
description: While validating .auto script smoke paths, the Bash inline environment guard denied a multi-line shell script because it contained a normal shell variable assignment (rc=0) after a command. The fallback regex should distinguish inline environment assignments like FOO=bar ./cmd from ordinary shell variable state inside a script.
close_reason: Fixed Bash env guard false positive by requiring AST env_setting statements to have a command_name and narrowing the fallback regex to conventional uppercase env assignments before a same-line command. Added Cupcake regressions for AST inline env denial and shell variable bookkeeping allow cases. Validated cupcake validate, cupcake verify, and scripts/test-cupcake-policies.py; the previously blocked .auto script smoke commands now run.

title: Build deterministic Elden Ring smoke-test driver
description: Create a helper/automation path for driving Elden Ring menu/test flows and capturing structured evidence instead of relying on LLM/OCR-guided clicking. It may use hooks/memory reads where needed for branching state.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Retrospective: first driver version over-mashed Enter/click after the game was already loaded because telemetry was still reporting player_available=false. Root cause: telemetry writes from render path hard-coded player_available=false and task path was not enough as an oracle. Fixing driver to use hook-side player availability/status telemetry from render and reducing blind menu input to bounded nudges rather than continuous mashing.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Pivot task list:
1. Stabilize hook-side telemetry: make player availability, menu/load phase, selected call states, and last driver command visible in structured JSON without render-path false negatives.
2. Add a driver command channel: let the Linux helper request apply/remove/toggle actions through a small file or named pipe that the injected DLL consumes, so proof toggles do not depend on ImGui clicks.
3. Investigate direct-load/continue entrypoint: inspect fromsoftware-rs/debug symbols and local mod references for a safe game-side continue/load-latest trigger; prefer calling a known function over menu navigation.
4. Implement bounded launcher flow: launch with LazyLoader, wait for telemetry phases, send only sparse prompt-dismiss nudges if direct-load is unavailable, and fail with artifacts instead of mashing.
5. Validate checkbox/menu behavior separately: once loaded, prove overlay checkbox on/off semantics with screenshots and telemetry; if command-channel toggles pass but UI clicks fail, isolate hudhook input/coordinate handling.
6. Record evidence and cleanup: capture before/on/off PNGs, telemetry JSON, chains-debug.log, and driver logs; stop Elden Ring cleanly and leave a concise Beads handoff.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Closed plan unknowns:
- Menu coordination can be avoided for smoke runs by launching eldenring.exe directly through Proton instead of Steam's start_protected_game.exe; current start_protected_game.exe is the Seamless Co-op ersc launcher, which adds modal prompts. Direct Proton launch preserves the overlay/DLL and reaches saved-character loading without Seamless prompts.
- Added a deterministic DLL command channel via ER_EFFECTS_COMMAND_PATH / er-effects-command.txt. Supported commands: apply_all, remove_all, apply <index>, remove <index>, set <index> on|off, toggle <index>. Driver no longer depends on ImGui click coordinates for effect proof.
- Stabilized telemetry via ER_EFFECTS_TELEMETRY_PATH / er-effects-telemetry.json: player_available, current_animation_id, network_sync, last_driver_command, and call enabled/active/apply_failed states.
- Direct game-load entrypoint remains not identified in local fromsoftware-rs RVAs or strings; no safe known function to call yet. For now, direct Proton launch + sparse nudges is the safer closed path.
- Validation artifact: target/smoke/driver-direct-command-20260612-085432. The driver reached player_available=true and command-channel set 0 on/off succeeded; final telemetry records last_driver_command="ok: set 0 off" and call 0 inactive.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Correction from user: direct Proton eldenring.exe launch is the wrong escape hatch for this project. The target is Seamless Co-op launch, but with a deterministic way to bypass Seamless + vanilla intro/menu flow, ideally by passing save-file and slot arguments or equivalent state into the exe/DLL. Re-scope driver around Seamless launch compatibility and investigate whether args/env/command channel can trigger load-slot while keeping DLL injection.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Closed argv/Seamless unknown: tested launching ersc_launcher.exe through Proton with args '--er-effects-probe save=foo slot=7'. The resulting eldenring.exe /proc/<pid>/cmdline was only 'eldenring.exe' with no forwarded args. Therefore raw save/slot exe arguments will not reach code running inside eldenring.exe through the current Seamless launcher. A DLL path can still work: launcher-created process should inherit env and can read files, so save/slot should be provided to our injected DLL via ER_EFFECTS_* env or command file, then the DLL must invoke/patch the in-game load-slot path after Seamless initializes.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Added ER_EFFECTS_START_COMMAND as the first env-based DLL control path. The DLL consumes it once after PlayerIns is available, using the same command grammar as er-effects-command.txt. This confirms the intended transport pattern: launch Seamless normally while passing automation intent via inherited env/file data to the injected DLL. Save/slot autoload still requires mapping the actual game load primitive; env is the right carrier but not by itself the loader.

title: Overlay can't render: hudhook 0.9.1 DX12 command-queue detection fails on ER 1.16.1
description: In-game smoke test (er-offline.exe, DLL auto-injected via dllMods/LazyLoader) showed the DLL injects and runs stably, but the ImGui overlay never appears. chains-debug.log spams every frame: 'hudhook::hooks::dx12: Couldn't find command queue pointer in swap chain struct (512 out of 512 pointers were readable)' followed by 'Initialization context incomplete' and 'Render error: HRESULT(0xFFFFFFFF)'. Reproduced with ALL other mods disabled (only er_effects_rs.dll loaded), so it is not a mod conflict. The pinned hudhook is commit 6a361de (0.9.1); its DX12 hook scans the swapchain struct for the D3D12 command queue pointer and never finds it on this game build/GPU, so the renderer context never completes. Root cause is upstream in hudhook, shared via the fromsoftware-rs 'debug' crate (both it and this DLL depend on the same hudhook git rev, so they must be bumped together).
design: Fix options, in order of preference: (1) Bump hudhook to a version whose DX12 hook obtains the command queue by hooking ID3D12CommandQueue::ExecuteCommandLists instead of scanning the swapchain (later hudhook reworked this); requires coordinating the bump in fromsoftware-rs since the debug crate pins the same rev, and adapting to any ImguiRenderLoop API changes. (2) If staying on 0.9.x, investigate passing the command queue explicitly. (3) Confirm whether a DX11-on-12 path is viable. Note: ProDebug._dll in dllMods is a native (non-hudhook) tool, so it is not a usable comparison.
acceptance_criteria: Overlay renders in-game on ER 1.16.1; chains-debug.log shows a 'Found command queue pointer ... at offset' line and no 'Initialization context incomplete' spam.
close_reason: Fixed for this repo via Banon-Labs/hudhook er-proton-cursor-poll branch: DX12 fallback renders overlay in ER, and input follow-up verified clicks work. Upstream fallback PR opened as veeenu/hudhook#257.

Blocker: in-game smoke test cannot copy DLL into ER Game/dllMods because /mnt/win-c is mounted read-only: /dev/mapper/win-c on /mnt/win-c type ntfs3 (ro,...). title: Re-ingest final FastSpEffectRecon report and triage candidates
description: The recon report copied into docs/recon/ghidra-fast-speffect-recon.txt is a mid-run snapshot (complete=false, 112 symbols, string section not yet reached). Once the Ghidra run finishes, re-copy the file from %LOCALAPPDATA%/Temp, re-run 'cargo run -p er-param-inspect -- recon docs/recon/ghidra-fast-speffect-recon.txt', and triage the candidate SpEffect IDs into data/effects.json.
acceptance_criteria: docs/recon contains a complete=true report; candidate IDs reviewed; worthwhile ones added to data/effects.json.
notes: Recon temp file still mid-symbol-scan (27.8K, no done marker) as of this session; re-copy and re-run ingest when Ghidra finishes. Candidates can now be checked directly: cargo run -p er-param-inspect -- recon <file> '/mnt/c/SteamLibrary/steamapps/common/ELDEN RING/Game/regulation.bin'
close_reason: Final report ingested (complete=true, 341 symbols/139 strings). 63 candidate IDs extracted; 14 validate as real SpEffectParam rows but all name-empty, and most candidates are face-gen field numbers (noise). Seeded eye-red IDs absent from literals (runtime-derived). Saved docs/recon/ingest-results.md + final report; nothing auto-added to data/effects.json — candidate triage is a human judgment call (left to owner).

title: Add unit tests for er-soulsformats pure helpers
description: The repo has zero tests. crates/soulsformats/src/lib.rs contains pure, easily testable functions: escape_xml, powershell_quote, parse_row_ids (in the tool), path_to_string, and the BRIDGE_PROJECT_TEMPLATE substitution. Add unit tests covering escaping/quoting edge cases (embedded quotes, ampersands, non-UTF8 paths) to lock in behavior before the bridge grows.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests exist for escape_xml, powershell_quote, and row-id parsing, including edge cases; cargo test passes.
close_reason: 18 unit tests across er-soulsformats (escape_xml, powershell_quote, template substitution, write_if_changed), recon parser, er-effects-data, and row-id parsing

title: Validate seeded SpEffect IDs against SpEffectParam via the soulsformats bridge
description: The er-param-inspect tool and er-soulsformats crate can query param rows from regulation.bin through the Smithbox/Andre.Formats bridge, but nothing wires this into the effects workflow. The three hardcoded SpEffect IDs (4330, 20018100, 20018101) are never checked against SpEffectParam. Add a validation path that confirms each seeded/added ID exists as a row before it is presented as applicable.
acceptance_criteria: Seeded SpEffect IDs are checked against SpEffectParam; missing IDs are flagged rather than silently applied.
close_reason: er-param-inspect validate checks data/effects.json against SpEffectParam and exits non-zero on missing rows; in-game, apply results are verified against the live SpEffect list instead of silently assumed; follow-up filed to execute against a real regulation.bin

title: Ingest FastSpEffectRecon Ghidra output into the project
description: The FastSpEffectRecon.java Ghidra script reconstructs SpEffect-related data from the game binary. There is currently no defined format, storage location, or parser for its output. Define where recon results live in the repo (e.g. docs/recon or a data file), a stable schema, and a way to consume them so the seeded SpEffect list can be validated and expanded from real data instead of guesswork.
acceptance_criteria: Recon output has a documented location + schema in the repo; a parser or import step turns it into usable SpEffect data.
close_reason: docs/recon/README.md documents drop location + full schema; er_soulsformats::recon parses reports; er-param-inspect recon subcommand extracts candidate IDs (verified against the live mid-run report); follow-up filed for final-report triage

title: Prototype DS1 Mushroom Child as ER c0000-rigged full-body armor
description: Build first implementation proof by importing DS1/DSR c2280 mesh, retopology/scale/orient as needed, transfer/paint weights to Elden Ring c0000 skeleton, author as a full-body armor/body part using ER materials/textures, and package loose files for ModEngine2. Prefer c2280 before c2270 unless asset inventory shows c2270 is cleaner.
design:
Detailed implementation plan for Route A: DS1 Mushroom Child as ER c0000-rigged full-body armor/body.

Objective:
- Produce the first playable proof by making the player character visually become a DS1 mushroom person while retaining ER's native player control, movement, combat, equipment, lock-on, stamina, camera, and hitbox semantics.
- First target is DS1/DSR c2280 Mushroom Child. Use c2270 Mushroom Parent only if c2280 inventory is unsuitable or if the user explicitly prefers parent proportions after c2280 proof.

Non-goals for first proof:
- Do not preserve original DS1 mushroom attack animations yet.
- Do not implement a custom runtime player controller.
- Do not use simulated host input.
- Do not launch either game during offline authoring.
- Do not modify base game files; use loose mod/output folders.
- Do not commit game-derived binaries to git.

Phase 0: prerequisites and source locations
1. DSR source assets expected after extraction/install:
   - chr/c2280.chrbnd(.dcx)
   - chr/c2280.anibnd(.dcx)
   - chr/c2270.chrbnd(.dcx)
   - chr/c2270.anibnd(.dcx)
2. ER target references needed from extracted/loose files or tooling:
   - c0000 player body/skeleton reference.
   - One sacrificial armor/body partsbnd as the first replacement target.
   - If a full-body part is not enough, the standard part slots are HD/BD/AM/LG (head/body/arms/legs) with male/female variants where applicable.
3. Tooling priority:
   - Unpack/repack: WitchyBND or Nuxe/UXM-style extracted files.
   - Model edit/export: Soulstruct-Blender and/or FLVER Editor 2 + FbxImporter.
   - Inspection: SoulsFormats/SoulsFormatsNEXT, soulstruct, er-flver for ER FLVER read sanity where applicable.

Phase 1: offline mushroom asset inventory
1. Unpack c2280.chrbnd and c2280.anibnd.
2. Record:
   - FLVER file names and count.
   - Texture/TPF file names and whether textures are internal or shared.
   - Material names/paths and DS1 MTD references.
   - Skeleton bone names/count/hierarchy.
   - Dummy polygon reference IDs and attach bones.
   - Bounding box and approximate scale.
   - Mesh count/submesh/material slot count.
   - Vertex bone-weight validity from the source.
   - HKX animation IDs and whether skeleton.hkx is present in ANIBND/CHRBND.
   - TAE files/event types for later Route B.
3. Repeat the same for c2270 only enough to compare feasibility.
4. Choice gate:
   - Choose c2280 if it imports cleanly, has simpler proportions, and covers the body sufficiently.
   - Choose c2270 only if c2280 has broken assets or the parent has dramatically easier mesh/material/skeleton handling.

Phase 2: ER target part choice
1. Pick a single sacrificial body armor part for first proof.
   - Prefer body armor (BD_M_####) if the mushroom mesh can be a full-body shell attached to c0000 skeleton.
   - If helmet/arms/legs must be independently visible, split later into HD/BD/AM/LG after the proof.
2. Avoid new EquipParamProtector rows at first.
   - Overwrite a known loose mod part so the player can equip an existing item and see the mushroom body.
   - Add param rows only after mesh proof is stable.
3. Export/open the target ER FLVER and preserve a copy as the donor for material layout, vertex buffer layout, MATBIN path patterns, and part binder structure.

Phase 3: Blender/armature conversion
1. Import DS1 c2280 FLVER into Blender.
2. Import ER target player/armor reference with its c0000-compatible armature.
3. Normalize transforms:
   - Align vertical axis and facing direction to ER reference.
   - Scale mushroom so feet/root and visual height are reasonable against c0000.
   - Keep mushroom silhouette; do not overfit to human proportions for the first proof.
4. Mesh cleanup:
   - Remove unused internal/LOD meshes only if they confuse export.
   - Preserve normals/UVs as much as possible.
   - Merge or keep submeshes based on export tool requirements, not aesthetics.
5. Weight transfer/paint:
   - Bind mushroom mesh to ER c0000 armature.
   - Coarse first-pass weights are acceptable: pelvis/root for lower trunk, spine/head for cap/body, thigh/calf/foot for legs, upper/lower arm/hand only if mesh has arm-like geometry.
   - Every animated vertex must have at least one and no more than four weights.
   - Normalize all weights.
   - Prefer deformation that looks stable during idle/walk/roll over strict anatomical correctness.
6. Dummy/attachment strategy:
   - For first proof, rely on native ER player equipment/weapon dummies from the underlying skeleton/part setup.
   - Only add/translate mushroom dummy polys if missing dummies cause obvious SFX/weapon attachment failure in later validation.

Phase 4: ER material/texture conversion
1. Fast proof material strategy:
   - Use a known-good ER material/MATBIN from the donor armor/body.
   - Map DS1 mushroom diffuse/normal/spec where available into compatible ER texture slots.
   - Accept imperfect shading for first visible proof.
2. Better material pass after proof:
   - Convert/author DDS/TPF texture outputs with unique loose paths.
   - Edit MATBIN/texture path references with Witchy/FLVER Editor as needed.
   - Preserve ER material conventions rather than trying to port DS1 MTD semantics directly.
3. Validation rules:
   - Reopen the exported part/FLVER and ensure no missing material path crashes the editor/tool.
   - Check texture path casing and folder placement match ER loose-file expectations.

Phase 5: export and package
1. Export FBX/FLVER through the chosen toolchain:
   - If using FbxImporter: import the prepared FBX into the donor ER FLVER/part container so ER vertex buffer/material layout remains valid.
   - If using Soulstruct-Blender: export FLVER and repack with WitchyBND/compatible binder tooling.
2. Package as loose files in a ModEngine2-style structure, likely:
   - parts/<chosen_part>.partsbnd.dcx and any needed texture/material files.
   - regulation.bin only if adding a new equip row; avoid for first proof.
3. Record manifest in Beads, not a legacy markdown file:
   - Source DS1 files and hashes/fingerprints.
   - Donor ER part ID/file.
   - Export tool versions/settings.
   - Output loose file paths.

Phase 6: offline validation before any runtime run
1. Open/reparse exported FLVER/partsbnd with at least one independent tool.
2. Check:
   - FLVER loads without parse errors.
   - Expected armature/bones exist.
   - All vertices have 1-4 normalized weights.
   - No extreme bounding box explosion.
   - Material count/path references are sane.
   - Texture files exist in the loose package.
   - Mesh is visually positioned around the player skeleton in Blender/tool preview.
3. If animation preview is available without launching ER:
   - Preview idle/walk/roll or a small c0000 animation set and inspect deformation.
   - Fix only catastrophic deformation before first runtime proof; polish comes later.

Phase 7: later runtime validation (not for current no-game session)
1. Use approved non-surprising ER runtime path only when authorized/appropriate.
2. Equip the donor/replacement armor and verify:
   - Mushroom mesh appears on the player.
   - Controller movement/combat still uses normal ER semantics.
   - No crash/message box.
   - Weapons/lock-on/camera remain functional.
   - Deformation is acceptable enough to proceed.
3. File separate polish issues for clipping, material quality, split equip slots, original mushroom attacks, and armor compatibility.

Expected first implementation artifacts:
- Beads manifest/comment with source and output paths.
- Loose mod folder containing the replacement part and textures/materials.
- Offline validation output from parser/editor/tooling.
- Later, runtime proof artifact only after user-approved/appropriate game launch.

Known risks and mitigations:
- Risk: mushroom proportions deform badly on human skeleton. Mitigation: treat first proof as full-body mascot shell; coarse weights and scale are allowed. If necessary, add helper geometry or split lower/upper weights.
- Risk: DS1 material/texture semantics do not map directly to ER. Mitigation: reuse ER donor material/MATBIN first, then improve custom materials.
- Risk: equipment/armor layering clips. Mitigation: first proof overwrites one body/armor part; armor compatibility is a later variant.
- Risk: source c2280 lacks clean textures or animations. Mitigation: route only needs mesh for first proof; c2270 is fallback.
- Risk: exported FLVER has invalid weights/layout. Mitigation: use donor ER FLVER through FbxImporter or Soulstruct-Blender validation and reopen before runtime.
acceptance_criteria: A loose-mod, no-base-file prototype exists that makes a DS1 mushroom mesh appear as a player-controllable ER character through the native c0000/player armor-body pipeline; offline validation proves FLVER/material/bone-weight sanity before runtime validation.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Route A implementation prep advanced offline:
- ER player references extracted from installed ER archives with fstools_cli into target/worktrees/mushroom-man/target/er-extract-player.
- ER c0000.chrbnd unpacked with WitchyBND: c0000.flver, c0000.HKX, c0000.bsipwv, c0000.hkxpwv. c0000.flver has no visible mesh/material payload, which is expected/useful: visual proof should target armor/body parts rather than direct c0000 mesh replacement.

- Donor part bd_m_1010 extracted/unpacked: BD_M_1010.flver/tpf/anibnd/collision/cloth-ish files. er-flver summary: 16 materials, 18 meshes, 17,349 vertices, 24,446 triangles, bbox min [-0.5611687, 0.5094859, -0.20016085], bbox max [0.56116855, 1.5417724, 0.39051092]. This is a credible first donor armor body part for the mushroom-shell proof.
- Implication: first proof should import/weight the c2280 mushroom mesh into a donor ER part FLVER like BD_M_1010, not replace c0000.flver directly.

8. Authoring target:
   - Source mesh: c2280 FLVER.
   - Target donor: $WORKTREE/target/er-extract-parts-sample/bd_m_1010-partsbnd-dcx/BD_M_1010.flver and BD_M_1010.tpf.
   - Keep donor BND manifest/file names initially; replace/import mushroom mesh into donor FLVER and use donor material slots/TPF structure for first proof.
9. First export validation before runtime:
   - Reopen repacked bd_m_1010.partsbnd.dcx with WitchyBND.
   - Re-run er-flver summary on output FLVER.
   - Confirm nonzero mesh/material counts, no missing TPF textures, sane bbox near donor scale, and no >4 weight vertices in the authoring tool.
10. Runtime boundary:
   - Stop before launching ER. At that point commit/push the exact offline prototype state if code/repo files changed, then ask for/coordinate runtime validation.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Implementation-prep helper added:
- scripts/route_a_mushroom_offline.py is now the repeatable offline entrypoint for Route A asset prep.
- It makes the exact next command simple once DSR assets are installed/visible:
  python3 scripts/route_a_mushroom_offline.py --extract-dsr --output target/mushroom-route-a-offline
- For ER donor/reference prep, use:
  python3 scripts/route_a_mushroom_offline.py --prepare-er --output target/mushroom-route-a-offline
  Note: full --prepare-er may exceed the default 30s foreground shell cap because Witchy unpacking can take longer; run it as a bounded background step or split extraction/unpack phases if needed.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Exact asset-specific Route A implementation plan after unpacking DSR assets:

Chosen source: c2280 Mushroom Child.
Reason: c2280 is already the right scale class and simplest first proof: 1,108 verts, 31 bones, 28 dummies, clean <=3 weights/vertex, bbox height 1.2579. c2270 is much larger/heavier (bbox height 2.5486, 43 bones, 70 dummies) and should be a later variant.

Chosen ER donor: bd_m_1010.partsbnd.dcx / BD_M_1010.flver.
Reason: c0000.flver has no visible mesh/materials, while BD_M_1010 is a real ER body armor part with 16 materials, 18 meshes, 17,349 verts, clean weights, TPF texture payload, and c0000-compatible player bones. Route A should replace/import into this donor, not direct c0000.flver.

Exact source files:
- c2280 mesh: target/mushroom-route-a-offline/dsr/dsr-loose-mushroom/c2280-chrbnd-dcx/c2280.flver
- c2280 textures: target/mushroom-route-a-offline/dsr/dsr-loose-mushroom/c2280-chrbnd-dcx/c2280-tpf/{c2280.dds,c2280_n.dds,c2280_s.dds}
- c2280 skeleton reference: target/mushroom-route-a-offline/dsr/dsr-loose-mushroom/c2280-anibnd-dcx/hkxx64/Skeleton.HKX plus c2280-chrbnd-dcx/c2280.hkx
- ER donor: target/er-extract-parts-sample/bd_m_1010-partsbnd-dcx/BD_M_1010.flver and BD_M_1010.tpf
- ER player/body skeleton references: target/er-extract-player/c0000-chrbnd-dcx/c0000.flver, c0000.HKX, target/er-extract-player/c0000-anibnd-dcx-wanibnd/hkxx64/Skeleton.hkx.

Authoring path:
1. Open/import c2280.flver and BD_M_1010.flver in the model authoring toolchain (preferred: Blender + Soulstruct-Blender or FLVER Editor/FbxImporter if Blender path is not available).
2. Treat BD_M_1010 as the target FLVER/binder structure. Preserve its ER FLVER version/material layout/binder manifest as much as possible.
3. Bring c2280 geometry into the BD_M_1010 scene. Initial transform:
   - Use c2280 native orientation as imported, then align feet/base to ER world floor.
   - Initial scale target: c2280 bbox height 1.2579259 -> ER body/top proof height around 1.54, so start uniform scale ~1.225. Adjust visually/offline only enough to sit around c0000 bones.
   - Keep mushroom silhouette wide; do not force it into human proportions.
4. Bind c2280 mesh to ER/c0000-compatible bones from the donor part. First proof weights should be coarse and stable, not anatomically perfect.
5. Initial bone-weight mapping:
   - c2280 root/model/global -> ER Pelvis/RootRotY context, but final mesh should use the donor FLVER bone table.
   - c2280 Pelvis/LLeg1/RLeg1/LLeg2/RLeg2 -> ER Pelvis, L_Thigh/R_Thigh, L_Calf/R_Calf, L_Foot/R_Foot as nearest lower-body supports.
   - c2280 Spine1/Spine2/Spine3/Neck/Head -> ER Spine, Spine1, Spine2, Neck, Head.
   - c2280 LArm*/RArm* and digit bones -> ER L_UpperArm/L_Forearm/L_Hand and R_UpperArm/R_Forearm/R_Hand; finger-level mapping can collapse to hand bones for first proof.
   - Any cap/body vertices without clear limb ownership -> Spine2/Neck/Head blend, max 4 weights.
6. Weight validation gates before export:
   - every vertex has >=1 and <=4 nonzero weights;
   - weights normalized;
   - no reference to source c2280 bone indices remains in the final ER FLVER unless those bones are intentionally added to the donor skeleton and proven acceptable;
   - bbox remains sane (not infinite/exploded, roughly player-sized).
7. Material/texture first proof:
   - Use one donor material slot initially, preferably a fabric/leather slot from BD_M_1010, to avoid solving matxml authoring first.
   - Substitute/point the diffuse/normal/spec texture slots to c2280.dds/c2280_n.dds/c2280_s.dds or converted equivalents inside the donor TPF structure.
   - Keep donor MATBIN/matxml path conventions; do not attempt direct DS1 MTD port for first proof.
8. Repack/export:
   - Export/import the prepared mesh into BD_M_1010.flver using the selected toolchain.
   - Repack bd_m_1010.partsbnd.dcx with its original manifest names.
   - Place loose mod output under a target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/parts-style folder first; do not overwrite game files.
9. Offline validation gates after export:
   - WitchyBND can unpack the generated partsbnd.
   - SoulsFormats/er-flver can parse the generated FLVER.
   - FLVER has nonzero mesh/material counts, sane bbox, clean weights, and expected texture references.
   - Optional authoring preview with c0000 idle/walk pose if tool supports it without launching ER.
10. Stop before runtime validation. At that point commit/push tracked scripts/metadata and ask for runtime launch coordination.

Runtime proof later is only to answer: does the native ER player pipeline render the mushroom donor armor and keep normal controller behavior? It should not be used to decide the offline Route A plan anymore.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Runtime negative evidence from user: launched the asset-only ME3 profile, created/used Wretch (level 1, 10 all stats, only club equipped), unequipped everything possible after spawning, and saw no visible mushroom change in character creation preview or in-game.

Interpretation: current payload is not sufficient for the user-visible goal. Treat as negative evidence, not success. Most likely causes to investigate offline before another runtime ask:
1. /parts/bd_m_1010*.partsbnd.dcx is not the visual asset path used by Wretch/naked body or character creation preview.
2. The asset package/profile did not load, though ME3 parsed the profile and the package path exists.
3. The replacement row/path requires an equipment/param/msg edit or a different body/part ID, not just replacing BD_M_1010.

Next corrective step: identify the exact protector/body part IDs and asset filenames used by naked Body Type A/Wretch/new-game preview, then retarget the asset package to that path or add a minimal asset-only param/msg package if needed. Do not ask the user to retry until the target path is grounded from regulation/msg/asset evidence.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Exact failure attribution for the no-visible-change run:
- The asset-only profile loaded a package that primarily replaced /parts/bd_m_1010*.partsbnd.dcx.
- EquipParamProtector inspection shows Wretch/naked base rows are not bd_m_1010:
  - row 10100: equipModelId=0, equipModelCategory=2, equipModelGender=3, bodyEquip=1 => naked body slot /parts/bd_m_0000, and extracted BD_M_0000/BD_M_0000_L have 0 meshes/materials.
  - row 10200: equipModelId=0, category=1 => AM_M_0000, also 0 meshes/materials.
  - row 10300: equipModelId=0, category=6 => LG_M_0000, visible lower/body component.
  - the full naked character/base customization mesh is FC_M_0000/FC_M_0000_L: extracted FLVERs have 42 materials, 42 meshes, 26,155 vertices, bbox [-0.685,-0.0026,-0.1598] -> [0.685,1.7157,0.1335].
- Rows with equipModelId=1010 are equipment rows such as 40000/40100/40200/40300, not Wretch naked. Therefore replacing BD_M_1010 could not affect Wretch with no gear. That is the exact wrong assumption.

Corrective offline package built:
- Added FC_M_0000 and FC_M_0000_L as the primary naked/full-body targets.
- Patched FC_M_0000/FC_M_0000_L using donor mesh index 13 (capacity 1500) with the c2280 mushroom geometry; kept mesh material index 0 (skin/body material) and degenerated the other FC face sets.

  - target/.../prototype/mod/parts/fc_m_0000.partsbnd.dcx (1,916,960 bytes)
  - target/.../prototype/mod/parts/fc_m_0000_l.partsbnd.dcx (925,696 bytes)
- Existing BD_M_1010 high/low files remain in the package only as armor-body fallback, not the Wretch naked primary path.

Verification of repacked/unpacked FC files:
- FC_M_0000.flver and FC_M_0000_L.flver parse as FLVER 0x2001A, bbox [-0.7509472,-1.25e-07,-0.5046694] -> [0.7509471,1.5409594,0.51967007], badWeightVertices=0, mesh13 material index 0.

This avoids leaving the mushroom geometry on the original human skin texture set while still using the ER FC material/shading path.

In this worktree, the previous PowerShell path failed because Windows Cargo could not follow the WSL sibling `../fromsoftware-rs` symlink; the cargo-xwin path follows the Linux symlink and matches the repo's documented xwin workflow.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
User runtime report after corrected FC package: legs rigging is pretty good; arms are still off but somewhat there; visible human hair/face is unacceptable/creepy. Treated as authoritative negative visual evidence and continued offline without launching either game.

Implemented follow-up offline changes:
- Added `scripts/route_a_mushroom_hide_flver_faces.rs`, a Rust helper that zeroes all FLVER face-set index buffers while preserving binder/file shape.
- Extracted/unpacked `facegen/facegen.fgbnd.dcx` offline and added a facegen override path to the asset-only ME3 package.
- `face.flver` inside the prototype facegen binder now has all 12 face/eye face sets zeroed (`hidden_indices=18360`, 16-bit index sets only). Packed/unpacked validation showed `nonzero_index_bytes=0` for the facegen face-set index buffers.
- Added `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/mod/facegen/facegen.fgbnd.dcx` to the mounted package payload.
- Improved arm weighting offline by adding root blending through ER shoulder/upper-arm/forearm/hand chains instead of putting most distal arm mass directly on Hand. Packed FC histogram now includes L/R_Shoulder, L/R_UpperArm, L/R_Forearm, L/R_Hand with no missing arm targets; legs remain on the previous thigh/calf/foot split.

Validation evidence:
- `scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_assets.sh` smoke rebuilt FC/BD/facegen folders.
- WitchyBND packed outputs despite the known exit-82 KeyPress quirk; packed artifacts were produced.
- Repacked FC TPF unpack proof still SHA-matches c2280 diffuse/spec/normal sources.
- Repacked facegen unpack proof: `face.flver` face-set index bytes all zero.
- `bash scripts/check.sh` passed via cargo-xwin path; `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/check-sh.out` final line reports Finished dev profile.

Still not agent-launched/runtime-proven. Next runtime/user gate should reuse the same asset-only ME3 profile and specifically check: face/eyes/hairlike overlay gone, arms improved enough, legs not regressed.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Follow-up after user runtime report + screenshot path `/tmp/pi-clipboard-4f7d1a3f-c0de-4dbe-aa74-ec06585ff080.png` (not agent-inspected as image; user report treated as authoritative): the previous facegen-only override did not visibly remove the head/hair, arms animate but have little volume/poor pose, bow animation no longer crashed.

Offline attribution before retest:
- The failed head/hair suppression targeted `/facegen/facegen.fgbnd.dcx`, but the ER dictionary also has rendered generated-face part binders under `/parts/fg_a_*.partsbnd.dcx`.
- Extracted `/parts/fg_a_0000_m.partsbnd.dcx` offline; it contains `FG_A_0000_M.flver` with one rendered mesh and skin material `P[ChrCustom_Skin]_SSS.mtd`. This is a stronger match for the visible generated head/hair than the base facegen template alone.

Implemented and pushed in commit `45fe573 feat: hide mushroom face parts offline`:
- Added `scripts/route_a_mushroom_hide_flver_faces.rs` and wired build script to zero both `facegen/face.flver` and `parts/FG_A_0000_M.flver` face-set index buffers.
- Added packed payload `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/mod/parts/fg_a_0000_m.partsbnd.dcx` alongside the existing FC/BD/facegen payloads.
- Added modest vertical stretch (`scaled_height=1.756693602`) and arm-volume swell in the exporter; this raises the mushroom cap/body without widening the shoulder span as much as a pure global scale, and gives L/R arm targets more geometric volume.

Offline validation:
- Repacked/unpacked `fg_a_0000_m.partsbnd.dcx`; `FG_A_0000_M.flver` has `face_sets=6`, `nonzero_index_bytes=0`.
- Repacked/unpacked `fc_m_0000.partsbnd.dcx`; FC bbox is now min `[-0.77601558, ~0, -0.50466943]`, max `[0.77601546, 1.75669372, 0.51967007]`.
- `bash scripts/check.sh` passed.
- Branch `investigation/ds1-mushroom-playable` pushed to origin at `45fe573`.

Next runtime gate: rerun the same asset-only ME3 profile and check whether the visible generated head/hair is gone. If it remains, the next offline attribution target is the specific selected `/parts/fg_a_<id>_m.partsbnd.dcx` variant rather than `/facegen/facegen.fgbnd.dcx`.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
User runtime report: mushroom is very close, but shoulders/upper arms are buried inside the body in idle; arms become visible only after elbows. User suggested maybe taller, but noted it could hurt shoulder rigging.

Implemented offline follow-up in commit `4966177 fix: push mushroom arms to body edge`:
- Did not further global-scale the model; kept height at current `scaled_height=1.756693602` to avoid changing the already-good leg/shoulder relationship unnecessarily.
- Added per-arm outward offsets after the existing arm-volume swell so arm geometry is pushed toward/outside the mushroom body edge:
  - shoulder outward offset `0.33`
  - upper-arm outward offset `0.27`
  - forearm outward offset `0.10`
  - hand outward offset `0.02`
- Fixed side selection to use the ER arm target side (`L_`/`R_`) instead of raw vertex x sign, so left/right arm vertices are pushed toward their intended side even when source vertices sit near/inside the torso.

Offline validation:
- Rebuilt and repacked the asset-only ME3 payload.
- Repacked FC unpack check: `FC_M_0000.flver` bbox now min `[-0.871343, ~0, -0.504669]`, max `[0.871343, 1.756694, 0.519670]`, which reflects wider visible arm extents without changing height again.
- `bash scripts/check.sh` passed.
- Branch pushed to origin at `4966177`.

Next runtime gate: rerun the same ME3 profile and check whether idle shoulders/upper arms are now visible at the mushroom body edge without regressing legs or combat/bow animation stability.

Per direct-tool fallback, parent inspected Kitty remote-control help and ran `kitty @ action --self clear_terminal scrollback active`, which returned empty/success output recorded at `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/kitty-clear-scrollback.out`. comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Offline follow-up after user asked about GUI/Frida/Cheat Engine and reported arms idle like hands-on-hips/distorted:
- Recommendation from available tooling/prior art: Blender/Soulstruct-style FLVER rigging is the right GUI class for manual weight/pose editing, but it is not installed/configured here. FLVER Editor/DS Anim Studio are useful for inspection/preview, but less direct for authoring c0000-compatible weight paint. Frida/Cheat Engine could support live diagnosis later, but the current issue is baked FLVER geometry/weights, so the durable fix should stay in the asset pipeline first.
- Implemented one more offline asset-pipeline pass before asking for runtime: stub-arm mode now collapses mushroom palm/digit source bones to ER forearm instead of hand, removing direct hand-bone influence that made idle look like hands-on-hips. Forearm-to-upper-arm threshold now keeps a forearm segment while anchoring inner arm mass to upper arm/shoulder.
- Rebuilt/repacked current asset-only ME3 payload. Current arm weight summary from TSV: L_Shoulder 80 rows / 41.852 weight, L_UpperArm 206 / 101.292, L_Forearm 208 / 138.001; R_Shoulder 79 / 41.005, R_UpperArm 206 / 101.292, R_Forearm 206 / 136.001; no Hand targets remain.
- Repacked FC unpack check: bbox min `[-0.966281, ~0, -0.504669]`, max `[0.966281, 1.756694, 0.51967]`, reflecting wider arm extents.
- Validation: `python3 scripts/check-no-magic-numbers.py`, `python3 scripts/check-no-lossy-utf8.py`, `shellcheck scripts/check.sh scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_assets.sh`, `bash scripts/check.sh`, and Pi Lens session diagnostics passed/no issues. Evidence: `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/check-sh-stub-arm-before-runtime.out`.
- Commit pushed: `bcf2ef5 fix: reduce mushroom arm hand-pose distortion`.

Next step requires runtime/user visual check of the current asset-only ME3 package. Specifically test whether idle arms no longer resemble hands on hips / buried hands while combat and bow animations remain stable enough.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Offline arm-tuning gate reached after user reported they cannot meaningfully describe the remaining arm distortion from screenshots.

Implemented:
- Parameterized Route A exporter arm controls (`--arm-x-swell`, `--arm-z-swell`, outward offsets, arm threshold controls, vertical stretch) so future tweaks no longer require code edits for every value change.
- Added `scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_arm_sweep.sh`, which builds asset-only ME3 variants without launching the game. It supports selected labels so each variant build stays under the 30s shell-script smoke budget.
- Built three current profiles under `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/arm-sweep/`:
  - `edge-stub`: current/default stub-arm baseline.
  - `more-out`: pushes shoulders/upper/forearms farther outward.
  - `less-contorted`: reduces arm x/z swell while keeping outward offsets, intended to reduce blobby/contorted arm volume.

Validation:
- `shellcheck scripts/check.sh scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_assets.sh scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_arm_sweep.sh` passed.
- `python3 scripts/check-no-magic-numbers.py`, `python3 scripts/check-no-lossy-utf8.py`, and `bash scripts/check.sh` passed; evidence `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/check-sh-arm-sweep.out`.
- Pi Lens reports no issues across diagnosed files in this session.
- Each variant has packed FC high/low, fg_a_0000_m, and facegen payloads plus an asset-only `.me3` profile.
- Commit pushed: `07782a2 feat: add mushroom arm tuning sweep`.

Next step requires user runtime testing. Ask user to test the three variant profiles rather than describing another screenshot: start with `less-contorted`, then `more-out` if shoulders still begin too far inward, and `edge-stub` only as baseline comparison.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
User reported that running the new configurable slider command produced the normal character body with no mushroom body visible. Attributed offline before asking for another retest:
- Inspected generated `live-tweak` profile/payload. The profile was generated with a relative package path (`target\mushroom-route-a-offline\arm-sweep\live-tweak\mod`) instead of an absolute UNC path. This made ME3 package resolution dependent on Windows process CWD.
- The configurable profile also omitted the BD_M_1010 fallback payloads that the known-working prototype profile includes; if the tested character was on an equipment/body path rather than naked FC_M_0000, that variant had no mushroom override for it.

Fix in commit `f150bdf fix: make configurable arm profiles load full payload`:
- Custom arm profiles now write absolute UNC package paths via `wslpath -w $(realpath -m ...)`.
- Custom arm profile launch commands now print absolute UNC profile paths.
- Custom arm variants now include the already-packed BD_M_1010 / BD_M_1010_L fallback payloads from the prototype package, in addition to FC_M_0000/FC_M_0000_L, FG_A_0000_M, and facegen payloads. Slider parameters still affect FC_M_0000 naked-body path; BD fallback is present to prevent a normal-body no-op if the runtime path is not FC.

Validation:
- `shellcheck scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_arm_sweep.sh` and `bash -n` passed.
- Direct configurable command smoke completed within 30s for `cli-smoke-fallback`; generated profile now contains absolute UNC package path and payload includes BD high/low + FC high/low + FG + facegen binders.
- `python3 scripts/check-no-magic-numbers.py`, `python3 scripts/check-no-lossy-utf8.py`, `shellcheck scripts/check.sh scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_assets.sh scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_arm_sweep.sh`, `bash scripts/check.sh`, and Pi Lens diagnostics passed. Evidence: `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/check-sh-configurable-fix.out` and `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/arm-sweep-cli-smoke-fallback.out`.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Responding to user request for torso sliders / skeleton-vs-weapon-position problem:
- Clarified implementation direction in code: asset-only FLVER tuning cannot move the vanilla weapon socket/club handle independently; those remain driven by the vanilla character hand/weapon transforms. To avoid jumping to skeleton/weapon edits yet, added torso/cap geometry sliders so the mushroom body can be narrowed/deepened while leaving arms/weapons closer to vanilla hand locations.
- Added exporter parameters: `--torso-x-scale`, `--torso-z-scale`, `--cap-x-scale`, `--cap-z-scale`, `--torso-start-norm-y`, `--cap-start-norm-y`.
- Updated `scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_arm_sweep.sh` help examples so one PowerShell/WSL command can act like sliders.
- Optimized configurable variant builder: it now reuses prepacked FC texture TPFs and static BD/facegen/FG fallbacks, so a selected custom profile builds within the 30s agent shell budget rather than repacking all static binders.
- Smoke built `torso-smoke` with `--torso-x-scale 0.88 --cap-x-scale 0.92`; generated profile/payload exists and summary records the torso/cap slider values.
- Validation: `python3 scripts/check-no-magic-numbers.py`, `python3 scripts/check-no-lossy-utf8.py`, `shellcheck scripts/check.sh scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_assets.sh scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_arm_sweep.sh`, `bash scripts/check.sh`, and Pi Lens diagnostics passed. Evidence: `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/check-sh-torso-sliders.out` and `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/arm-sweep-torso-smoke-fast.out`.
- Commit pushed: `3c17cb9 feat: add torso sliders for mushroom tuning`.

Next runtime gate: user can run a single WSL-from-PowerShell command with torso sliders. Suggested first test keeps current arm values and narrows torso/cap: `--torso-x-scale 0.88 --cap-x-scale 0.92`; if club/hand still cannot align, the next path is no longer pure body-silhouette tuning and should investigate weapon/socket/skeleton offset deliberately.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
User runtime feedback after using last settings: club/handle appears at the elbow. Root cause from current exporter/pipeline: the user's thresholds `--arm-upper-to-shoulder-abs-x 1.0 --arm-forearm-to-upper-abs-x 1.0` push nearly all c2280 arm geometry up to shoulder/upper-arm ownership, and the previous stub-arm path had no way to anchor distal mushroom palm/digit geometry to ER `Hand`. Making the visible mesh/skeleton taller (`--vertical-stretch`) would not move the vanilla weapon socket; the club is driven by ER hand/weapon transforms.

Implemented commit `cee7f11 feat: add hand anchor slider for mushroom grip`:
- New exporter/build slider `--arm-forearm-to-hand-abs-x` maps outer/distal forearm/palm/digit vertices onto ER `L_Hand`/`R_Hand` when source abs-x crosses the threshold.
- Default is `0.99`, effectively preserving previous stub-arm default.
- Low values (suggested first test around `0.46`) hand-anchor distal arm vertices so the visible mushroom arm can follow the weapon grip/socket instead of ending around the elbow/upper arm.
- Help/docs updated in `scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_arm_sweep.sh`.

Smoke/validation:
- Built `hand-anchor-smoke` with user's last settings plus `--arm-forearm-to-hand-abs-x 0.46` and torso sliders. Output profile/payload exists; weight summary showed `L_Hand=314`, `R_Hand=312`, shoulder/upper distribution preserved for the rest.
- Validation passed: `python3 scripts/check-no-magic-numbers.py`, `python3 scripts/check-no-lossy-utf8.py`, `shellcheck scripts/check.sh scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_assets.sh scripts/route_a_mushroom_build_arm_sweep.sh`, `bash scripts/check.sh`, Pi Lens diagnostics. Evidence: `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/hand-anchor-smoke.out` and `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/prototype/check-sh-hand-anchor.out`.

Suggested next user command should keep their last settings and add `--arm-forearm-to-hand-abs-x 0.46`; if too much hand-on-hips distortion comes back, increase toward `0.52`/`0.58`; if club is still too elbow-ish, lower toward `0.40`.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Runtime/user visual feedback: with the hand-anchor slider test, arms look like an L shape and the club appears to jut from the bicep. Interpreted as too much distal arm geometry being pulled directly to the hand while the user's `--arm-upper-to-shoulder-abs-x 1.0 --arm-forearm-to-upper-abs-x 1.0` settings leave no forearm bridge: L forearm vertices were split between upper-arm and hand, creating a sharp upper/hand bend. Next command recommendation should reintroduce a forearm middle zone: reduce upper-to-shoulder/forearm-to-upper thresholds from 1.0 to around 0.55-0.58 and raise hand threshold from 0.46 to around 0.72-0.78 so only the outer tip anchors to the weapon/hand.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Agent kicked off user-requested live-tweak launch using the recommended L-shape correction settings:
`--arm-x-swell 0.6 --arm-z-swell 0.6 --arm-shoulder-out 0.01 --arm-upper-out 0.01 --arm-forearm-out 0.02 --arm-hand-out 0.02 --arm-upper-to-shoulder-abs-x 0.58 --arm-forearm-to-upper-abs-x 0.56 --arm-forearm-to-hand-abs-x 0.76 --torso-x-scale 0.82 --torso-z-scale 0.92 --cap-x-scale 0.88 --cap-z-scale 0.95`.
Preflight saw Windows Steam running and no prior eldenring/me3 process. After launch, Windows processes were observed: `eldenring` PID 16616 with title `ELDEN RINGT`, `me3` PID 15900. Agent-side launcher PID was 3355530.
Note: the first background log path was placed under `arm-sweep/live-tweak/`, and the build script deletes/recreates that variant directory, so the log FD became a deleted file. Use a log path outside the variant directory for future agent-owned launches.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
User feedback: current slider/runtime loop is not yielding predictable corrections; neither agent nor user has a direct enough understanding of how levers affect the model. User proposed setting up Blender/Souls importer to compare mushroom relative scale/proportions against the real ER character model, constrain the mushroom to literal player model bounds, and only then tweak. Treat this as a pivot away from blind slider iteration. Added AGENTS guidance in commit `docs: require direct asset deformation feedback`: when slider changes do not match steering, establish a direct 3D authoring/feedback surface with ER donor/player body and source model before more runtime tuning or skeleton/weapon-socket edits.

Local setup evidence: Windows Blender 4.4 exists at `C:\Program Files\Blender Foundation\Blender 4.4\blender.exe`; Soulstruct add-on folders exist under `C:\Users\choza\AppData\Roaming\Blender Foundation\Blender\4.4\scripts\addons\io_soulstruct` and `io_soulstruct_lib`. Headless import of `io_soulstruct` reaches add-on load but fails in background mode at `gpu.shader.from_builtin("UNIFORM_COLOR")`, which is a background/GPU drawing limitation rather than proof GUI load fails. Next non-runtime work: make a Blender comparison scene or GUI import workflow that overlays ER FC_M_0000/player reference and the mushroom mesh with measured bounds/scale.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
User ran `bash scripts/build_mushroom_blender_edit.sh --label live-blender-edit --launch` after saving Blender edits and got `Error: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound, message: "No such file or directory" }` / exit 1. Root cause found in `target/mushroom-route-a-offline/blender-edit/live-blender-edit/export-blender-edit.log`: Blender export failed before ME3 launch with `IndexError: bpy_prop_collection[index]: index 0 out of range, size 0` at `uv = mesh.uv_layers.active.data[loop_index].uv`. User edits preserved geometry but the editable mesh's active UV layer had zero loop data.

Fix pushed: `ab2a773 fix: fall back to raw mushroom UVs for Blender edits`.
- `scripts/export_mushroom_blender_edit.py` now detects empty/missing UV loop data on `EDIT_ME_c2280` and falls back to the locked raw `c2280` reference mesh's UV loop data when topology/loop count matches.
- Summary records `uv_source` (`raw-reference` in the validated fixed build) and still records `uv_v_flipped_for_flver=true`.
- Validated with `bash scripts/build_mushroom_blender_edit.sh --label live-blender-edit-fixed` within 30s; output profile exists and export summary shows `uv_source=raw-reference`, source vertices 856, export vertices 1075, bbox dims `[1.41484, 2.106527, 0.836195]`.
- Also ran `py_compile`, shellcheck on related scripts, `check-no-magic-numbers`, `check-no-lossy-utf8`, `bash scripts/check.sh`, and Pi Lens diagnostics clean.

No relaunch was performed after this fix; next user-facing launch should use the same one-liner or the fixed script with `--launch`.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Created single-file Python installer and packaged current Mushroom Man ME3 payload.

Commit pushed: `e5aad95 feat: add mushroom man ME3 installer`.

Installer:
- `scripts/install_mushroom_man.py`
- No third-party Python dependencies.

title: Inventory DS1 mushroom assets offline
description: Offline-only asset inventory for DS1/DSR mushroom playable prototype. Extract or locate c2270/c2280 chrbnd/anibnd, enumerate FLVER names, textures/materials, skeleton bones, dummies, HKX animation IDs, TAE event types, and source format versions. No game launch required.
design:
Offline asset inventory plan for DS1/DSR mushroom characters.

Purpose:
- Make Route A implementation deterministic before touching ER runtime.
- Decide between c2280 Mushroom Child and c2270 Mushroom Parent from actual assets, not assumption.

Inputs:
- C:\Users\choza\DarkSoulsRemastered_1.3.1.0_dump.exe.gzf is available for executable semantics only.
- The asset files still need extraction/location from DS1/DSR install/archive:
  - chr/c2280.chrbnd(.dcx)
  - chr/c2280.anibnd(.dcx)
  - chr/c2270.chrbnd(.dcx)
  - chr/c2270.anibnd(.dcx)

Inventory commands/tools to prefer:
1. Use extraction/unpack tools without launching the game:
   - Nuxe/UXM/WitchyBND/Yabber-equivalent as available.
   - Soulstruct/SoulsFormats for binder structure when scriptable.
2. Use Python/SoulsFormats/Soulstruct where possible to emit machine-readable summaries:
   - Binder file paths, IDs, compression/DCX type.
   - FLVER version, mesh/submesh/material count.
   - Skeleton bones and hierarchy.
   - Dummy polys.
   - HKX animation IDs and skeleton.hkx presence.
   - TAE animation IDs and event type histogram.
3. Use Blender/Soulstruct-Blender only for visual/authoring after structural inventory is recorded.

Required fields to record in Beads for each of c2280 and c2270:
- Source file path and fingerprint: size + hash/fnv/sha if convenient.
- CHRBND contents: file names, IDs, decompressed sizes.
- ANIBND contents: skeleton entry, animation HKX entries, TAE entries, event counts.
- FLVER summary: version, bones, dummies, meshes, materials, textures, vertex count, face count, bind-pose flag if visible.
- Texture summary: TPF names, DDS names, color/normal/spec slots if detectable.
- Skeleton summary: bone count, root bone, major bone names, scale/orientation notes.
- Exportability test: can the FLVER be imported into the selected authoring tool without error?
- First-target recommendation: c2280/c2270 and why.

Expected decision rule:
- Choose c2280 for first prototype if it imports and has manageable mesh proportions.
- Choose c2270 only if c2280 import/export fails, has missing essential texture/model data, or has worse rigging complexity than c2270.
- Do not block Route A on animation inventory; Route A only requires a usable mesh/material path. Animation data is for the secondary retarget issue.

No-game constraint:
- This issue is complete without launching DS1/DSR or ER.
- Ghidra static queries and asset extraction are allowed; live process attach/probe is not needed.
acceptance_criteria: For c2280 and c2270, source binders are unpacked or conclusively unavailable, and Beads records FLVER/material/texture/skeleton/dummy/HKX/TAE inventory plus a c2280-vs-c2270 first-target recommendation.
notes: Blocked before any game launch: full DSR base assets are not present yet. Need chr/c2270/c2280 chrbnd/anibnd or DSR dvdbnd archives/full install visible to WSL to continue offline inventory.
close_reason: Completed Route A-relevant offline inventory for c2280/c2270; TAE event-type parsing deferred to animation-retarget issue.

title: Investigate DS1 mushroom-person playable model import
description: Investigate feasible paths to import a Dark Souls 1 mushroom-person character/model into Elden Ring as a playable character: either reuse/map DS1 NPC animations to controller actions, or retarget/rig the model onto an Elden Ring player skeleton/armor/weapon pipeline. Capture findings, risks, proof steps, and recommended next implementation path.
close_reason: Investigation produced a concrete Route A implementation plan, supporting offline evidence, and linked follow-up implementation/inventory/runtime issues.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Investigation checkpoint: user clarified Beads updates should be made from /home/choza/projects/er-effects-rs even though active code branch/worktree is target/worktrees/mushroom-man. I verified root Beads contains er-effects-rs-x7wh and will keep progress/evidence comments on that root issue.

Initial external/local evidence collected:
- DS1 mushroom IDs are c2270 Mushroom Parent and c2280 Mushroom Child (Souls Modding DS1 character list / model IDs).
- DS1/DSR character assets to inspect once installed/GZF is available: chr/c2270.chrbnd(.dcx), chr/c2270.anibnd(.dcx), chr/c2280.chrbnd(.dcx), chr/c2280.anibnd(.dcx).
- Elden Ring playable path involves c0000 player assets: c0000.chrbnd/c0000.anibnd plus c0000.behbnd/HKS/clip-generator registration for controller-triggered moves.
- Public prior art/tools: DS Anim Studio edits TAE/anibnd; ERClipGeneratorTool registers new Elden Ring hkbClipGenerators from custom anibnd entries; ModEngine2 file override can load loose modded files; SoulsFormats/SoulsFormatsNEXT parse BND/DCX/FLVER/TAE; soulstruct-havok has DSR and ER ANIBND/HKX support and a prototype retarget module.
