DEFINITIVE scheduler/FE-host decode (2026-06-15, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, FULL disasm). Resolves the 'scheduler-owned FE-host trigger' question AND CORRECTS two prior errors.

=== THE FE-HOST IS THE SimpleTitleStep, AND IT IS PRESENT AT THE TITLE ===
0x140ae7830 (FE-host alloc+ctor) allocs 0x3e8 and calls 0x140b0b1c0. 0x140b0b1c0 = the SimpleTitleStep CTOR: vtable 0x142b63bb0, binds its OWN step-fn table 0x143d71580 via 0x140b0b020, SetState(0) at end (@0x140b0b2d8 edx=0). So the object built by the 'FE-host' IS the SimpleTitleStep (the title/front-end menu controller). CORRECTION to outer-simpletitlestep-absent-at-title-wall: the SimpleTitleStep is NOT absent at press-any-button -- it IS instantiated and running. Its state-0 STEP_InitMenu 0x140b0cfa0 BUILDS the title menu (Continue/LoadGame items via 0x1407a91e0/0x140744a60, vtables 0x142b63ea8/0x142b63e70) at boot; at the press-any-button wall it sits at committed state 10 STEP_MenuJobWait (matches brief). The prior runtime find_outer scan gave a FALSE NEGATIVE because it searched wrong offsets: it looked for obj with +0x10==table 0x143d71340 and +0xc0==InGameStep, but the real table is 0x143d71580 (bound at obj+? via 0x140b0b020, not at +0x10) and the InGameStep CHILD is at obj+0x2e0 (slot key 0x10), NOT +0xc0. So 'outer absent' was a scan-offset bug, not reality.

=== EXACT SimpleTitleStep STEP TABLE 0x143d71580 (built by 0x1400a4f50; 12 (func,name) pairs stride 0x10; r8d=0xd0 reserves 13) ===
idx0 STEP_InitMenu 0x140b0cfa0; 1 STEP_BeginXR117Dialog 0x140b0c8d0; 2 STEP_BeginLogo 0x140b0c2a0; 3 STEP_BeginTitle 0x140b0c5b0; 4 STEP_BeginNewGame 0x140b0c540; 5 STEP_PlayGame 0x140b0d5b0; 6 STEP_GameStepWait 0x140b0cde0; 7 STEP_EndFlow 0x140b0cc00; 8 STEP_EndFlowWait 0x140b0ccc0; 9 STEP_NextLap 0x140b0d550; 10 STEP_MenuJobWait 0x140b0d400; 11 STEP_Finish 0x140b0cd70. (Names = UTF-16 'TitleStep::STEP_*' at 0x142b62d60+.) Object fields: +0x48 current_state(s32,-1=terminal), +0x4c requested_state(s32), +0x10 table base, +0xbc selection/request id (init -1), +0xc0 slot-holder (EndingStep job), +0xe0 menu-job multicast holder, +0x128/+0x130 selection queue+UI, +0x2e0 slot-holder for the InGameStep child (key 0x10, 0xb3870 bytes, ctor 0x140aeaaa0). SetState 0x140b0d960 writes +0x4c ONLY. Per-frame pump 0x140b0bd60 commits +0x4c->+0x48 and dispatches table[state].executor, draining up to 0x80 transitions/frame.

=== THE SCHEDULER/FACTORY (brief Q1) ===
0x140ae7830 has ZERO E8/E9 callers, ZERO ptr refs, ZERO rva refs (callscan+ptr_scan+refs all empty; only EH/.pdata). It takes rcx=PARENT and registers the new SimpleTitleStep into [parent+0x118] (FD4 slot-keyed holder, key 0xe, via insert fn 0x140eb55a0 -> indexed-slot worker 0x140eb2790, bounds <=0xa8). The global 0x143d872e0 it reads is the FD4 heap/allocator singleton (1777 refs), NOT a step factory. The SimpleTitleStep vtable 0x142b63bb0 is referenced ONLY by its ctor 0x140b0b1c0 and dtor 0x140b0b440 -- no RTTI/Create thunk. CONCLUSION: the FE-host instance is created by the FD4 task SCHEDULER invoking the PARENT step's init through the parent's step-table/vtable; the literal call to 0x140ae7830 is emitted by the parent's step routine at runtime (slot-keyed dispatch, key 0xe into [parent+0x118]). There is NO directly-callable static factory. (Unreversed remainder: the identity of that PARENT step and its slot-0xe init entry.)

=== THE TRIGGER / GATE (brief Q2) -- press-any-button does NOT SetState the title machine ===
STEP_MenuJobWait(10) 0x140b0d400 FULL decode: reads inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 (assert if null), sets inputmgr+0x6b0=1, PUMPS the menu-job multicast at owner+0xe0 (0x140733f20, passes frame time [rdx+8]) and the UI element at owner+0x130 (0x1407a9600), polls input 0x1407663c0(dl=1). Its ONLY SetState: cmp [0x143d856a0],0; je skip; edx=0xb; SetState(owner,11=Finish). So the title machine, while at state 10, ONLY ever advances to Finish(11=teardown) and ONLY when the QUIT-LATCH 0x143d856a0!=0. It NEVER SetStates to PlayGame/EndFlow. => The Continue/Load SELECTION is consumed by the MENU-JOB at owner+0xe0 (callback-driven), NOT by a title state transition. The selection callback writes the play-game request into the InGameStep child at owner+0x2e0 ([child+0x100]=map cmd, gate !=-1; request vector child+0xb35f0), and the CHILD's own step pump advances to its idx6 STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 which builds CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 (chain per csfeman-construction-chain-ingamestep-step6-2026). CSFeMan is NULL at the title simply because the child has not been driven to its MoveMap step yet -- NOT because any parent object is missing.

=== EndFlow(7)/EndFlowWait(8) ARE THE NG+ ENDING FLOW, NOT THE MENU BUILDER (corrects a mid-investigation hypothesis) ===
STEP_EndFlow(7) 0x140b0cc00 allocs a 0x120 JOB (ctor 0x140ae4540, vtable 0x142b5ad08) into owner+0xc0 (key 0xa). That JOB = the 'EndingStep' nested step machine (its base-ctor 0x140ae43a0 binds sub-table 0x143d6f9d0 with steps EndingStep::STEP_PlayEndingMovie/...Wait/NextLapQuestion/MenuJobWait/Finish). EndFlowWait(8) 0x140b0ccc0 waits via predicate 0x140eb5530 on owner+0xc0 = (held==null)||(held->vt[+0x20]()==0); held->vt+0x20 0x140ae5320 = (job[+0x48]==-1). When done -> tail-jmp 0x140b0e780/0x140b0e650 (selected by global 0x143d6f9c0; =1 route A is full first-build: bumps save counter [0x143d5df38]+0x120, inits singletons 0x143d68448/0x143d69d18/0x144588e98, SetState(5=PlayGame)). EndingStep COMPLETION IS INPUT-GATED: STEP_NextLapQuestion reads inputmgr+0x80+0x290 (NG+ yes/no dialog), STEP_PlayEndingMovieWait gated by 0x14080d5c0 reading inputmgr+0x90 keystate (action 0x43). EndFlow(7) is reached ONLY from GameStepWait(6) 0x140b0cde0 when [0x143d69918+0xb7c]!=0 (ending-played flag) -- i.e. AFTER beating the game, the NG+/credits path. It is NOT the press-any-button->menu path.

=== NATIVE NO-INPUT VERDICT (brief Q3) ===
The press-any-button->load path does NOT go through any latch/SetState we can force on the title machine; it is driven by the menu-job multicast at owner+0xe0 invoking a selection callback that writes the play-game request to the InGameStep child (+0x2e0). So the in-process no-input options are: (a) get a pointer to the live SimpleTitleStep, set the InGameStep child's map cmd [child+0x100]=slot/default and push to its request vector, then let the child's pump build CSFeMan -- requires finding the live SimpleTitleStep instance at runtime (the scheduler holds it at [parent+0x118] key 0xe; parent identity is the open piece) and replicating the exact selection-callback request submission (play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0 per prior notes); OR (b) drive the menu-job at owner+0xe0 with a synthetic 'Continue' selection so the native callback submits coherently (menu-task-manager-architecture recipe A). Forcing the quit-latch 0x143d856a0 only triggers Finish(11)=teardown (matches movie-dismiss-tears-down-not-builds). The SimpleTitleStep being PRESENT (not absent) at the title REOPENS option (a): we can locate it (its vtable 0x142b63bb0 is unique; scan committed heap for an object whose [0]==0x142b63bb0 and [+0x48]==10) and drive its InGameStep child directly -- this is the most promising no-input lane and supersedes the 'must replicate scheduler instantiation' conclusion, because the object already exists; only the child's play-game REQUEST submission (not construction) is missing. Related: csfeman-construction-chain-ingamestep-step6-2026, menu-task-manager-architecture, outer-simpletitlestep-absent-at-title-wall (CORRECTED), frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned-not-callable, movie-dismiss-tears-down-not-builds.

STEP-3 map-load-orchestrator STRUCTURE (2026-07-17 static RE, deobf). 0x14082dbf0 = THIN WRAPPER: mov args, call 0x14082faf0 (swaps: 0x82dbf0(rcx=ctx,rdx) -> 0x82faf0(rcx=old_rdx, rdx=old_rcx=ctx)), ret. Return addr into wrapper = 0x82dc1c (the June player-map-mount chain marker). 0x14082faf0 = the ACTUAL player-map mount work: builds request objects (descriptor vtables 0x142a93c00/0x142acb638) and dispatches via call 0x1407449e0 then 0x1407a7b60 (rbx=result); the mount reaches MountEblArchive through 0x1407a7b60 (contains the ~0x79171f chain link). CRITICAL: 0x82faf0's prologue has NO 'already-mounted, skip' self-guard -- it UNCONDITIONALLY builds+dispatches the mount. So on the load-2 warm reload the empty read is NOT because 0x82faf0 self-skips; it is because the STEP that calls it is not dispatched. Caller of 0x82dbf0 = 0x14082eb7e, itself a STEP-THUNK (pattern: movl /usr/bin/zsh,0x20(rsp) ... call ... movl ,0x20(rsp) ... ret = FD4 step executed-flag), one of a table of step thunks at 0x14082ebxx dispatched by the in-game step machine (InGameStep/MoveMapStep). So the player-map mount runs as a STEP; the warm reload either skips that step or runs it at a point where it no-ops. FIX PATH: (a) confirm via sw-bp census (does 0x82dc1c-chain mount fire on load2?); (b) if skipped, either satisfy the step-machine condition so the step runs on the warm reload, OR call 0x82dbf0(ctx) / 0x82faf0 directly for the m28 map at the load-2 stall with the correct step context (from the InGameStep/MoveMapStep pointers the oracle already reads). Need to RE 0x82dbf0's ctx arg (the step object) before a direct call. Links [[step3-run7-8-likely-root-skipped-map-load-orchestrator-0x82dbf0-warm-reload-2026-07-17]].

Therefore do not pass TitleBackViewParts+0x8/MenuResource to FUN_1407452c0. A valid bind owner is the ProfileSelect/MenuWindow field +0x3b8 shape with inline vector at owner+0x60. Next path: identify/build/reuse a minimal MenuWindow-like bind owner or construction-time custom GFX resource; do not retry TitleBack MenuResource bind, and update any telemetry sampler to distinguish pointer field vs inline vector.

RUNTIME (2026-06-23, user press -> instant crash). The build+post chain all fires: 9b24e0 (menu open) -> fire-tfc SET tfc+0x14c=1 -> selector 9a8eb0 -> dispatcher 9b3070 (build branch, our trampoline caller) -> PushBackJob to dialog+0x10 (ret=0x2). THEN CRASH: access-violation rva=0x1eba1c5 addr=0x141eba1c5, caller #0=our trampoline. 0x141eba1c0 is AtomicIncrement, called by ExecuteMenuJob (0x1407a9600 at 0x1407a9647 ) on cur+8 where cur=*(active slot). Our drain FUN_1407a90f0(rcx=dialog) read the active slot at dialog+0x8 and ran ExecuteMenuJob on it -> cur=*(dialog+0x8) is NON-null but NOT a valid MenuJob (its +8 is not a refcount) -> AtomicInc AV. So dialog (owner+0xe0, the TitleTopDialog) is NOT the FUN_1407a90f0 queue_owner the subagent assumed (+0x8 is some TitleTopDialog field, not a MenuJob active slot). PushBackJob to dialog+0x10 'succeeding' (ret=0x2) did NOT confirm dialog+0x10 is the right queue (PushBackJob only AtomicInc'd OUR job +OK + ring-pushed at dialog+0x18 which may be garbage).

FIX (next): do NOT use FUN_1407a90f0(rcx=dialog) (AVs on dialog+0x8) and do NOT rely on the dialog queue. PUMP OUR BUILT JOB DIRECTLY: call ExecuteMenuJob 0x1407a9600(rcx=&job_slot, rdx=&FD4Time) where job_slot holds out_job[0] (the dispatcher-built job from the selector). ExecuteMenuJob does cur=*rcx; AtomicInc(cur+8) [our job+8 is valid -- PushBackJob AtomicInc'd it OK]; cur->vtable[2](cur,&result,&time) [the job's REAL Run, via its own vtable -- correct for the dispatcher's chained job, unlike own_load_pump's fixed-RVA 0x826e10]; if !ShouldContinue *rcx=0. Drop PushBackJob (pump directly; avoids polluting the dialog queue + the native pump tripping on it). Store out_job[0] in a static TFC_DRAIN_JOB; each frame ExecuteMenuJob(&slot,&time) until *slot==0 (done) or cap. consts EXECUTE_MENU_JOB_RVA=0x7a9600. Relates: continue-load-drain-via-executemenujob-not-kick (the wrapper FUN_1407a90f0 is right for a real queue-owner, but the dialog is NOT one).

NATIVE-SHADER DRAW WORKING (vertex stage) on lavapipe software Vulkan, 2026. Real AEG301_012 FLVER geometry renders through the game's native CS[AMSN]_AEG301_012_Fwd.vpo vertex shader via wgpu SPIR-V passthrough -> non-blank silhouette (ORTHO proj 19.28% coverage, target/er-objectkit/native_draw.png). ZERO real-GPU risk (runs on llvmpipe/lavapipe via Headless::new_software / force_fallback_adapter). 

THE THREE SPIR-V PATCHES that made it work (er-shaderkit/src/spirv_patch.rs, applied post-dxil-spirv-translation, all spirv-val-clean + unit tested):
1. neutralize_draw_parameters: gl_BaseInstance/gl_BaseVertex OpLoad -> OpCopyObject const 0. wgpu passthrough doesn't enable VK_KHR_shader_draw_parameters so the builtin is garbage; localInstance=InstanceIndex-BaseInstance went ~4-billion -> SSBO OOB -> the HARDWARE GPUVM fault (0x8000742df000).
2. force_readonly_ssbo_loads_zero: loads from NonWritable StorageBuffer (g_InstanceIndexBuffer) -> 0. Single-instance slot is 0.
3. compact_descriptor_bindings: THE KEY for lavapipe. lavapipe+wgpu+passthrough NULLS descriptors at SPARSE bindings ({4,5,8,10,11,12,25}) -> null-deref segfault; remapping to contiguous {0..6} binds correctly (proven with clean-room probe: sparse 6-UBO = segfault, contiguous 6-UBO = green). Returns old->new (set,binding) map; caller remaps bind groups + uniform_writes.

TRANSFORM (the blank->silhouette fix): the vertex shader's world transform = VC_MatrixViewProj(cbSceneParam, orig b8, +384) * objMat * clothMat * mWorld(cbInstanceData orig b4, +0), where objMat=VC_aObjMatrix (orig b5) and clothMat=VC_aClothCancelObjMatrix (orig b12) are float4x3[] INDEXED PER-VERTEX by (matricesData + NORMAL.w)*3 vec4s. Writing identity only to slots 0/1 left NORMAL.w>=2 vertices reading zeroed slots -> collapse -> blank. FIX: fill obj+cloth buffers with the identity float4x3 (48B) repeated every 48 bytes (0..16KB) so ANY NORMAL.w index reads identity. dxil-spirv lays cbuffers out as FLAT vec4[] arrays (no ColMajor/MatrixStride decorations); byte offset 384 = vec4[24] still correct. 

STILL TODO for full 'what the game renders': native PIXEL shader (.ppo) + textures (from matbin, er-objectkit::material) + lighting/IBL cbuffers (zeroed lighting -> black). Currently uses a solid-red WGSL fragment (pixel_wgsl override) to isolate+prove the vertex projection. Camera: ORTHO fitting bbox works; perspective conventions need tuning (RH/LH only 0.11%). Diagnostic tooling: er-shaderkit/examples/{inspect_spirv,ubo_lavapipe_probe}, er-objectkit/examples/native_draw; spirv-dis/val/opt + gdb available.

After self-fire menu-open (textfadeout=true latch=1), owner+0xe0=TitleTopDialog (vt matched 0x142b26468), menu=dialog+0xa38, but [dialog+0xb08] bound=0 and [dialog+0xb0c] cursor=0 across all 8 throttled dumps (stable). NEXT RE TARGETS: (1) find the function that POPULATES the TitleTopDialog row vector (the lazy realize-on-focus builder) and call it directly zero-input; (2) OR re-derive the correct row-vector offset (the dialog+0xa38 +0x1290 guess is unconfirmed at runtime -- bound at dialog+0xb08=0 may be the real signal the list is empty regardless of offset); (3) OR accept a single bounded input event to realize+navigate the menu (the user has offered one fast interaction). The diagnostic dump_titletop_menu_entries (save-safe, owner+0xe0 row enumerator + Load-Game/Continue action classifier) is committed for the next probe. ALWAYS UPDATE NOTES BEFORE PUSHING + ALWAYS STAGE BEADS DATA (user directive 2026-07-17). Immediately prior to every push (and before committing after a runtime validation run per [[commit-immediately-after-runtime-validation-2026-07-17]]), UPDATE YOUR NOTES first: record the session's findings/decisions as bd memories (bd remember) so the knowledge is captured. Those bd writes update .beads/issues.jsonl, and you must ALWAYS STAGE .beads/issues.jsonl together with the code changes in the same commit -- never unstage/exclude the beads data (doing so was the mistake that prompted this). CANONICAL ORDER for wrapping up work: (1) bd remember the findings/notes, (2) git add the beads data (.beads/issues.jsonl) AND the code, (3) commit, (4) push (bd dolt push + git push per Session Completion). So the notes, the beads DB export, and the code always travel together and stay in sync across the fleet. WHY: beads is the shared cross-machine memory; a commit/push without the updated notes+beads leaves the next machine/session stale.

AUTORESEARCH FINDING 2026-06-25: MenuWindowJob::Run result path decode: dump 0x1407ad2b0 is CS::MenuWindowJob::Run. After load/init paths, it resolves/marks CSMenuMan state, calls FUN_140744ed0(this->owningMenuWindow), then calls FUN_140733ff0(this->field2_0x50). Later it reads aMStackX_8[0]=this->owningMenuWindow->field254_0x1e8 and calls MenuJobResult::ShouldContinue(aMStackX_8). If ShouldContinue is true, Run destructs the MenuWindowJob via FUN_1407adb30, copies owningMenuWindow->field254_0x1e8 into loadParams->jobResult, and returns; otherwise it sets loadParams->jobResult=Continue. This ties the prior TitleTopDialog field254_0x1e8 result path to the concrete MenuWindowJob Run return path. FUN_140744ed0 is not the row callback itself; it is a MenuWindow pump/visual activation helper that shows the scene proxy, plays FadeIn, invokes optional function at param_1[0x1b]+0x10, resolves param_1+0x30 through virtual +0x18 if 0xffff, and marks GLOBAL_CSMenuMan->field106_0x90. FUN_1407ac980 is a generic helper that sets a Failed MenuJobResult and calls vtable +0x60 on its object. Net: product-safe instrumentation should hook/passively observe owningMenuWindow->field254_0x1e8 after FUN_140744ed0/Run, not cold-call row builders. Next static target: decode the owning MenuWindow/CommandSelectDialog virtual +0x18 path used by FUN_140744ed0 and the vtable +0x60 result setter/row callback path that writes field254_0x1e8; this should identify the selected-row callback invocation that leads to lambda_896b/Continue.

NEW-GOAL recipe (2026-06-22, deobf disas, AUTHORITATIVE -- the Ghidra DUMP decompile of these save-IO fns is SHIFTED/wrong; trust deobf + live captures). The full save load can be replicated FSM-FREE by feeding the engine parser our OWN decrypted slot buffer -- this is the 'own the load orchestration' path for the new goal (DLL: launch -> real char in world, engine only for parse + world-stream).

0x67b290 (deobf, the REAL parser; bd was right, dump showed a shifted submit) body, ecx=slot:
  buf = ([0x143d872e0]->vt[0x50])(size=0x280000, 1)   // alloc 0x280000
  if(!0x67b100(buf,0x280000)) fail   // <-- THE READ, HARD-GATED b80==3 (reads iodev resident via 0x140e6e060/0x140e6e380). THIS is the FSM dependency we BYPASS.
  PS=0x1402567b0(); rec=0x140261b80(PS, slot)
  GameMan(0x143d69918)->ac0(+0xac0)=slot
  0x67bd70(rcx=GameMan, rdx=buf, r8d=0x280000)   // c30/header parse (gate [0x143d68078]!=0; streams over buf; c30=header+4)
  // stream over buf: [rsp+0x28..]=hdr struct; 0x141edfa20(&stream@rsp+0x48, buf, 0x280000); 0x141edfb60(&stream,&hdr,0x10); set stream vtables 0x1429d9628 then 0x1429d95b8; 0x140258840(hdr.field@+0x30) = consume/deserialize
  c30 resolve: 0x140679560(&out) -> 0x14067abd0(&out2, out)
  GameMan->[0x3c]=0; GameMan->[0x10]=1
  PGD=[0x144588268] (FD4-assert non-null); 0x140e365c0(rcx=PGD, edx=[rec+0x295], r8d=0)  // CHAR-APPLY
  finalize: ret=0x140e1a210(buf); call [ [ret] +0x68 ](ret, buf)
  return true

0x67b100(rcx=buf,edx=size): cmpl [GameMan+0xb80],3; jne FAIL(xor al). if [GameMan+0xdf0]!=0 fast (b80=0,ret1) else 0x140e6e060()->iosubsys; 0x140e6e380(iosubsys,buf,size) read; b80=0; ret al. => CONFIRMED b80==3 gated; FSM-dependent; we skip it.

BUFFER FORMAT: 0x280000 = ONE slot's DECRYPTED USER_DATA. ER .sl2 = BND4; entries USER_DATA_000..009 (10 slots, each 0x280000 decrypted, AES-CBC encrypted on disk, key = known ER-PC 16-byte static, IV = entry's first 0x10 bytes), +USER_DATA_010 (menu/profile), 011 (steamid). So WE: read .sl2 -> BND4 find USER_DATA_00<slot> -> AES-CBC decrypt -> 0x280000 buffer. (SoulsFormats/er-soulsformats does exactly this; reference for format+key+decrypt.)

IMPLEMENTATION (the DLL 'own load' fn): (1) read .sl2 (path .../EldenRing/<id>/ER0000.sl2 -- builder 0x140e0e680 gives the folder); (2) BND4 parse + AES-CBC decrypt slot N -> 0x280000 buf; (3) replicate 0x67b290 body above on buf (skip alloc/0x67b100; provide buf); (4) SetState(5)/continue_confirm to world-stream. NO menu, NO FSM, NO b80, NO ToS.

VERIFY-IN-IMPL: (a) does 0x140258840 (the big deserialize) read ONLY from the buf-backed stream (no iodev/FSM touch)? disas shows stream-over-buf -- confirm. (b) 0x140e365c0 scope (full char vs flag) -- the heavy char/world populate is likely 0x140258840; 0x140e365c0(PGD,rec+0x295,0) may be a smaller apply. (c) save-safety: this is READ-only of the .sl2 until SetState(5) (which autosaves) -- gate SetState5 on verified real mount (c30 real + char), backup/restore. COMMIT DISCIPLINE (user 2026-07-20): stop making reflexive per-script "feat" commits. A commit must earn its place in history -- it should record something PROVEN useful that a future reviewer should heed (a validated fix + its evidence, a milestone that actually delivered). I committed er-confine-wsl-cores.ps1 + boost-er-priority.sh as feats BEFORE proving they deliver the deterministic 3-load run -- unproven harness helpers = history noise. The commit-after-runtime-run rule means commit the tree that produced a run AS EVIDENCE once the run shows something worth preserving, NOT reflexively commit every new script mid-investigation. Prefer: keep working-tree changes uncommitted until a run PROVES they matter, then one meaningful commit with the evidence. Squash/avoid the incremental infra-noise commits. Applies going forward: do not narrate/commit each helper; commit when there is a proven, review-worthy result.

Scaleform GFx exporter (ER menu .gfx) is NON-MINIMAL in variable-width bit fields, not just RecordHeader length. Measured across the 114-file corpus: ~2,413-2,444 of ~153,265 MATRIX instances use MORE bits than minimal for NTranslateBits (a few NScale/NRotate over-wide too). CXFORM/CXFORMWITHALPHA (20,157) and RECT (124) were minimal in THIS corpus but treat as possibly-non-minimal. Therefore a byte-identical GFX codec MUST store each field's SOURCE nbits verbatim (Rect.nbits; Matrix scale_nbits/rotate_nbits/translate_nbits; Cxform.nbits) and re-encode with that stored width -- NEVER recompute minimal nbits (would corrupt the ~2,413 over-wide matrices). Bit padding to the byte boundary is always zero-fill (0 non-zero pads across 171,728 MATRIX / 20,157 CXFORM / 124 RECT); the reader rejects non-zero pad (fail-loud, GfxError::NonZeroBitPadding). Implemented in crates/er-gfx Tier 2 (BitReader/BitWriter MSB-first + exact-nbits Rect/Matrix/Cxform/CxformWithAlpha + typed PlaceObject2/DefineScalingGrid). Companion to bd memory gfx-recordheader-force-long-non-deterministic.

PHASE-4 PROLOGUE RECONSTRUCTION -- IMPLEMENTED & VALIDATED (2026-07-01, commit 7a25b79). capture_frame() in scripts/bake-function.py emulates the Arxan entry+return trampolines and recovers the real frame that recovery drops: frame_size = net rsp delta from entry to first game body instr; saved = callee-saved regs (rbx,rbp,rsi,rdi,r12-r15) stored in the prologue AND restored symmetrically in the FINAL return trampoline (restores reset on every game instr to isolate the true epilogue -- dispatch trampolines juggle callee-saved regs as scratch and would false-positive; observed on 0x140110820). Returns ok only on clean return + save==restore symmetry, else the reassembler FLAGS rather than synthesize a wrong frame (prevents wrong-frame vacuous passes). For arxan_ret functions the reassembler emits push <saved>; sub rsp,N prologue + add rsp,N; pop <saved reversed>; ret epilogue; the trampoline's gs:[0x58] security-cookie + cmovle dispatch noise is dropped (recovery already resolved the real target).

PROVEN CLEAN (uncontended, no concurrent bake-corpus): 0x140df9940 went verify-FAIL (frameless ref=128 vs recomp=0) -> PASS ref==recomp==0, ABI-faithful (revng_undefined__rbx in residue = rbx correctly modeled callee-saved). frame captured = 0x38 / saved=[rbx]. No regression: 0x140110820 (68==68), 0x1424fb244 (165==165 input-dependent) unchanged; real-ret functions skip the frame path.

NATIVE DRAW — pixel-shader phase status (next steps after vertex render works). The vertex render through the native .vpo WORKS on lavapipe (see native-draw-WORKING-lavapipe-2026, image target/er-objectkit/native_draw.png, ORTHO 41% coverage). To draw through the native .ppo PIXEL shader too (the full goal 'what the game renders'):

DONE: compact_descriptor_bindings_unified([&mut v_spv,&mut p_spv]) remaps BOTH modules against one shared map so a shared cbuffer (cbSceneParam) keeps the same compacted binding in both, and the whole vertex+pixel union is contiguous (lavapipe needs contiguous). er-objectkit/examples/native_draw.rs has a NATIVE_PIXEL const toggle (currently false = proven vertex render; true = native .ppo).

BLOCKER 1 (NATIVE_PIXEL=true segfaults): the .ppo samples ~23 textures expecting specific types (Texture2D/Cube/Array, float vs uint sample type), but draw_object_passthrough stubs EVERY texture as a generic 1x1 2D Rgba8 -> sampling a cube/array binding with a 2D stub -> SIGSEGV in the llvmpipe fragment thread (gdb: faulting data 0x3f800000=1.0f). FIX: make draw_object_passthrough reflect each texture's view_dimension + sample_type from the SPIR-V (OpTypeImage Dim/Depth/Arrayed/Sampled + the sampled type) and create a matching 1x1 stub (D2/Cube/D2Array, Float/Uint). er-objectkit::spirv_reflect already classifies kinds; extend it to carry the image dimension/sample type, or add OpTypeImage parsing.

BLOCKER 2 (visible output): even with correct texture stubs, zeroed lighting/IBL cbuffers -> the PBR .ppo outputs black. Need to fill cbSceneParam lighting fields (direction/color/ambient) — offsets unknown, get them from dxc -dumpbin sample.ppo. Real game-accurate lighting may need captured engine state; a synthetic directional light + ambient should give a visible lit result. Also bind REAL material textures (matbin -> TPF via er-objectkit::material + image_dds) for albedo, instead of gray stubs.

PERSPECTIVE camera: ORTHO works; RH/LH perspective conventions only 0.11% — tune look_at/perspective handedness + the col-major store. dxil-spirv lays cbuffers as flat vec4[] (no ColMajor decoration); offset 384 = vec4[24] is correct for VC_MatrixViewProj.

REAL bc4-PUMP GATE FOUND + OUR-GUARDS-STARVE-IT HYPOTHESIS (2026-07-16, Ghidra + runtime). The switch-load stall root chain (see switch-lock-unified-root-bc4-not-ready-functor): bc4 must pump 1->2->3 for our final functor to fire and set rt5d so the incoming child completes. The pump is done by the quit-save orchestrator FUN_140afb970, which FUN_140aff730 (MoveMapStep update) calls UNCONDITIONALLY every tick (so my old save-gate-diag conclusion 'orchestrator not called' was WRONG -- it IS called). FUN_140afb970 returns WITHOUT pumping unless: (a) BOOL_143d856a0 (force latch) == 0 [else a different path], (b) bVar5 == true, (c) FUN_14067a170 == true. FUN_14067a170 = GameMan->saveState==0 (true on the stall, save_state=0). So the gate is bVar5: bVar5 = ShouldSave() || FUN_140679460() || (GetRequestedSaveSlotLoad() != -1). ShouldSave (0x1406794c0) = GameMan->saveRequested(b72) && !CanShowSaveMenu() && FUN_14080d660()(menu_gate) && bc4!=3. FUN_140679460 (0x140679460) = GameMan+0xb73 && FUN_14080d660() && bc4!=3. GetRequestedSaveSlotLoad = GameMan+0xb78. So bVar5 needs one of: [b72 && !CanShowSaveMenu && menu_gate && bc4!=3] | [b73 && menu_gate && bc4!=3] | [b78 != -1]. On the stall (save-gate-diag showed menu_gate_ok=true, bc4=1!=3, force=0, save_state=0) bVar5 is somehow 0 -> orchestrator bails -> bc4 never pumps. PRIME SUSPECT: OUR OWN GUARDS starve bVar5: (1) the b78 guard (title_tick_cover.rs) FORCES GameMan+0xb78 = -1 (OWN_STEPPER_SLOT_NONE) while world_up -> kills the (b78 != -1) term; (2) if b72/b73 are also 0 (or CanShowSaveMenu()==true kills ShouldSave), bVar5=0. So MY accumulated fixes (b78=-1 guard for the reload-loop, bc4=0 clear) are likely fighting the game's own quit-save pump = the user's 'a world that didn't get torn down fully'. INSTRUMENTATION LANDED (DLL ea129345, save-gate-diag now logs b72/b73/b78/bVar5_est): next stall names the exact failing term. FIX DIRECTION (pending the b72/b73/b78 readout): stop OUR guards from starving the pump during the teardown window -- e.g. do NOT force b78=-1 (or ensure a save condition / let saveRequested drive ShouldSave) so bVar5=true -> orchestrator pumps bc4 1->2->3 -> final functor fires -> rt5d set -> child completes. Do NOT keep the bc4=0 clear (it skips bc4==3). Verify CanShowSaveMenu() too (if true it kills ShouldSave AND clears saveRequested).

Follow-up to router-this-select-layer-is-zeroinput-callable-lambda-2026. The router_this build-lambda's std::function functor (vtable _Func_base 0x142a9b958 / _Func_impl 0x142abbaf8, _Do_call 0x1407fd450) CAPTURES 0x10 bytes = 2 pointers at functor+0x8 (parent menu container + config/SceneProxy source); confirmed from the _Func_impl copy/move methods at 0x1407fe170 (and siblings 0x1407f8121/fb8b5/fc9a2 are the same lifecycle family, NOT the menu-storage site). _Do_call 0x1407fd450 reads [this+0x8],[this+0x10] -> passes as r8,r9 to factory 0x140907150 -> CSMenu ctor 0x1409060d0. Purely constructive, no input poll.
TWO-LAYER MODEL (key): (1) menu JOBS (Continue/Load-Game d180/NewGame MenuWindowJobs) are built ZERO-INPUT by registrar 0x1409b24e0 (TitleTopDialog::open_menu, sets [dialog+0xa40]=1). (2) the SELECTABLE/router layer (router_this CSMenu + fireable Load-Game node) is built by the build-lambda the framework invokes ON FOCUS. Firing the Load-Game node (run 0x1409aaba0 -> ProfileLoadDialog -> primes save-IO -> native load slot0) needs layer (2). Registrar self-fire alone opened a COMPETING dialog and reverted (user-observed flash-then-revert), because it does layer (1) not (2).
NEXT (precise resume): to wire the zero-input select-layer realize, need the RUNTIME pointers -- either (a) find the menu-build construction site that creates+stores this functor (to know the menu-structure offset to read it live), or (b) a runtime menu-structure dump at the rendered title to capture the functor + its 2 captured ptrs + the MenuWindow*/SceneProxy&, OR (c) find the framework's focus-invocation site and the focus-state it checks, then set that focus-state zero-input (ToS-style satisfy-the-side-effect -- cleanest). Then own_stepper invokes _Do_call 0x1407fd450(functor, MenuWindow*, SceneProxy&) or sets focus, build, verify-only 120s telemetry-only probe. Also fix native_fullread slot default 9->0. This is the branch's central multi-session wall; 2026-06-21 resolved that it is crackable (select layer is zero-input-realizable).

Static RE iteration 2026-06-28 after #620 decoded the ProfileSelect/Scaleform bind path. FUN_1407452c0(owner, pair) first appends the two-DLString pair to owner+0x60 fixed vector via FUN_140748700, then immediately calls FUN_140d6ac00(GLOBAL_CSScaleform, owner, target, symbol). FUN_140d6ac00 locks the Scaleform singleton and calls FUN_140d7c0c0. FUN_140d7c0c0 requires owner vfunc+8 to return a state whose flags at +0x20 satisfy (&0x8f)==10, requires a live root at state+0x18 -> vfunc+8, resolves both target and symbol names through FUN_140d7c850, calls FUN_140d7be60, and on success calls root/container vfunc+0x208. FUN_140d7be60 looks up the symbol display object via root vfunc+0xd8(symbol), requires object type low bits 0x100, takes the current child texture object from display_obj+0x18, and if it differs from the resolved target texture object, copies transform/size data, calls FUN_141148050(target,...), then FUN_140d60950(display_obj,target) to replace display_obj+0x48 and reset display_obj+0x18. Therefore #620's returned bind was not just queued; it found some object and applied/invalidation returned. The visual blank means the object reached by rewritten symbol MENU_FL_40135_Profile is not the visible portrait face object, or the replacement happens on a non-visible/offscreen instance. Next: stop asking whether bind applies; instead find the exact visible face symbol/display object in the aliased GFX (likely not MENU_FL_40135_Profile) or hook FUN_140d7be60 observe-only to record symbol, display_obj, display_obj+0x18/+0x48, target object, and success for native ProfileSelect construction versus our MenuWindow-only bind.

VERIFIED HEADLESS DISMISS (2026-06-17, objdump disasm) for CS::MessageBoxDialog (the boot connection-error / EULA / warning popups). IMPLEMENTED in er_effects_rs.dll.

MULTI-INHERITANCE: the runtime-captured vtable 0x142b03550 (RVA 0x2b03550) is the PRIMARY/RTTI base (mostly boilerplate). The functional logic is on the DIALOG-BASE SUB-OBJECT vtable 0x142b02750 (at [dialog+8]). Sub-obj vtable slots (VERIFIED): slot2(+0x10)=UPDATE 0x140927d30 (per-frame tick the menu calls); slot3(+0x18)=GetResult 0x14078dcd0 (returns [dialog+0x1260]); slot12(+0x60)=EmitResult-to-parent 0x140746e80 (sets closing latch [dialog+0x3b0]=1); slot13(+0x68)=OnDecide/finalize 0x140927ba0; slot14(+0x70)=0x1407b0cf0 = 'cmp [dialog+0x25e8],2; setge' = 'has >=2 BUTTONS' layout query (NOT a finished-poll -- that is why a hook on it never mattered).

FIELD CORRECTIONS (the prior field-write dismiss was WRONG + harmful): [dialog+0x25e0] i32 = CHOSEN/DEFAULT button index (builder 0x1409275b0 inits it ONCE from [msg_desc+0x40] at 0x140927644; sole writer in the image; for a 1-button OK modal this default IS the OK index; -1 = no choice). [dialog+0x25e8] i32 = BUTTON COUNT ((btn_end-btn_begin)/0x18 @0x14092767b) -- writing it corrupts the dialog. [dialog+0x3b0] u8 = 'result emitted / closing' latch (set=1 by EmitResult). [dialog+0x1bc0] u8 = confirm latch (set by input). [dialog+0x1260] i32 = result code. So +0x25e0/+0x25e8 are config/OUTPUT, never the close TRIGGER; the close is async (input -> +0x1bc0 -> commit 0x14078ef20 builds result functor into [dialog+0x10] -> next UPDATE sees handle ready -> emit stop via EmitResult).

THE DISMISS (preferred, VERIFIED): call OnDecide 0x140927ba0 with rcx=dialog. It does: cmp [dialog+0x25e0],-1; if != -1 -> tail 0x140738d40(rcx=[dialog+0xa38] input/cursor sub-obj, edx=[dialog+0x25e0]) = dispatch chosen button (=OK for the boot modal); if == -1 -> 0x140927e30(reset menu-input registrar, safe) then tail 0x14078dfd0(notify owner closed). This is the genuine OnDecide path a real OK press runs -> drives the dialog to EmitResult/stop so the parent MenuWindowJob tears it down. Leave [dialog+0x25e0] at its built default (OK). ALTERNATIVE (force-stop, skips OK action): 0x14078dfd0(rcx=dialog) -> if owner [dialog+0x1c80]!=0 tail owner->vt[+0x10](dialog) else StepResult(3=stop)+EmitResult; use only because the connection-error OK is a no-op dismiss.

CALL DISCIPLINE: call on a fully-built dialog (builder 0x1409275b0 return / our CONNECTION_ERROR_DIALOG capture) from the GAME/MENU thread (FrameBegin game task is fine -- OnDecide touches the menu-input-registrar singleton 0x143d6b7b0). Call each frame UNTIL [dialog+0x3b0]==1 (closing) OR the dialog is freed/reused (vtable [dialog] != 0x142b03550), then STOP -- calling after teardown = UAF. Crash risk LOW (no alloc, no save, only menu teardown). PARENT POLL: MenuWindowJob::Update 0x1407ad1c0 child-poll @0x1407ad571 calls UPDATE 0x140927d30 (returns 1 decided/2 cancel/3 stop) and on stop forwards via 0x1407ac890 -> EmitResult; the dialog must reach EmitResult/stop for the boot flow to proceed, which OnDecide achieves. IMPL: builder hook 0x1409275b0 captures CONNECTION_ERROR_DIALOG (no field writes); force_dismiss_startup_dialog calls OnDecide each frame gated on vtable + closing latch. Related: connection-error-is-messageboxdialog-menu-built-2026, online-disable-isonlinemode-getter-patch-2026, headless-autoload-no-startup-ui-2026.

THE HOOK WORKS: cap_menu_item_update_hook captures live menu-item 'this' pointers as the native pump ticks them, vt confirmed 0x142aa97e8 (MenuWindowJob), and functor_chain_hits_factory decodes the +0xa8 _Do_call chain. It does NOT reach dialog_factory 0x14081ead0 -> load_game=false. NO other distinct item ticks (sole item across 180+ frames). So at the BeginTitle screen only one menu item is active -- the Continue/Load-Game LIST items are NOT yet ticking (they live one level deeper / appear only after this item is activated). HYPOTHESIS: 0x7ffe9e52c000 is the press-any-button / 'enter main menu' item, and invoking its +0xa8 functor (-> 0x140763fc0) is the NATIVE zero-input 'advance to main menu' action (NOT input synthesis -- it is the game's own menu-action functor, compliant with no-compromises-standard). NEXT: (1) static RE 0x140763fc0 (the single item's action lambda) -- does it open the Continue/Load main menu (build the next-level MenuWindowJobs)? (2) ALSO hook the iterator Sequence::Update 0x1407aa1f0 to map the parent Sequence + enumerate siblings (the Continue/Load items if they're built as siblings). (3) If 0x140763fc0 advances the menu, invoke item 0x7ffe9e52c000's functor zero-input from idx10, then re-capture to find Load-Game. REFINED OWN-LOAD step-3 plan (2026-06-22, follows reuse-native-fns-not-reimplement principle) -- HYPOTHESIS, needs RE/runtime validation before commit. Instead of re-implementing 0x67b290's body in our DLL, do the MAXIMALLY-NATIVE thing: scoped-hook the ONE FSM-gated read 0x67b100(rcx=out_buf,edx=size) so that instead of reading the iodev resident (which requires GameMan+0xb80==3, the FSM dep), it memcpy's OUR sliced plaintext BND4 slot body (er-save-loader bnd4::slot_body, 0x280000 bytes) into out_buf and returns 1. Then CALL the native 0x67b290(slot) in-process UNCHANGED -- it allocs the buffer, invokes our hooked 0x67b100 (gets our bytes), then runs ALL the real native parse (0x67bd70 c30 + 0x140258840 deserialize + 0x140e365c0 char-apply + finalize) with zero re-implementation. This is a single scoped hook + one native call, far less own-code/drift than replicating the body. STATUS 2026-06-22 (static RE, see own-load-67b100-callers-static-2026-06-22): item (1) RESOLVED -- 4 direct callers (1 in our 0x67b290 @0x67b2d9, 3 in native menu loader incl menu_deser 0x82c240), NONE autosave; hook MUST be gated to our own-load call. item (2) RESOLVED -- 0x67b290 after the read does ONLY `test al,al; je fail` (@0x67b2de), NO b80 re-check; b80==3 gate lives solely inside 0x67b100, so a hook returning al=1 with our body in the alloc'd buffer (rcx) flows straight into native parse (0x2567b0 -> 0x261b80 -> GameMan+0xac0=slot -> 0x67bd70 c30 -> stream deserialize). REMAINING HARD DEP for save-safety: 0x67bd70's c30 write is gated on [0x143d68078]!=0 (save-data subsystem obj built by FSM boot 0x6798d0); if null cold (we bypassed FSM) c30 stays default -> wrong map -> must ensure that obj is built OR write c30 directly + GUARD before SetState5 (see coldmount-c30-is-the-single-key memory).

ORIGINAL OPEN ITEMS (now mostly answered): (1) verify 0x67b100's CALLERS -- is it called ONLY from 0x67b290, or also from autosave/other loads? If shared, gate the hook to fire only during our own-load (a one-shot/flag) so we don't corrupt other reads. (2) confirm 0x67b100 sets b80=0 on our path and nothing downstream in 0x67b290 re-checks b80==3. (3) save-safety unchanged: read c30 (out_buf+4) and char fingerprint from native PlayerGameData AFTER the call, GUARD before SetState(5). Relates: OWN-LOAD-buffer-fed-replication-recipe-deobf-2026-06-22, reuse-native-fns-not-reimplement-2026-06-22, ER-PC-SAVE-IS-PLAINTEXT-MD5-not-encrypted-2026-06-22.

SAVE-PICKER INPUT under Wine/Proton (branch feat/game-native-file-picker). DECISIVE runtime finding 2026-07-07: GetAsyncKeyState does NOT report the user's keys from a background std::thread under Wine -- only the game's own RENDER thread (where the D3D12 Present hook fires) reads them. Proof: a dedicated 8ms poll thread logged oracle_save_picker_overlay_poll_count=1089 but oracle_save_picker_overlay_held_polls=5 while the user mashed, and pick_count=0 (could not pick). The commit that moved picker input to a background thread (fix eaten inputs) made input WORSE and was reverted (66ad719).

CORRECT design: drive save_picker_overlay_input_tick from the Present hook (render thread) in present_overlay.rs; completion stays on the game task. Present starves to ~4fps during boot asset streaming (present_hook_hits~29 over ~8.7s), which DROPS fast presses if you only poll the GetAsyncKeyState HIGH bit -- that was the ORIGINAL 'inputs eaten while navigating' complaint. Fix: save_picker_sample() reads the LOW bit (0x0001, 'pressed since our previous call') for keyboard so a press between two slow polls is still caught; gamepad (XInput, no such bit) keeps high-bit edge detection vs SAVE_PICKER_OVERLAY_PREV_ACTIONS. Residual: navigation still PROCESSES at the render thread's ~4fps boot rate so the cursor steps coarsely and multiple presses within one frame collapse to one; presses are no longer LOST. If coarse nav is unacceptable, the next step is per-event capture (WH_KEYBOARD_LL low-level hook with a message pump) or re-driving the boot self-present pump during the picker -- both higher risk under Wine. Diagnostic oracles kept: poll_count (thread/hook ran) + held_polls (render thread sees keys -- should track presses now). DLL staged sha 652be0c7.

DECISIVE CORRECTION to titletop-confirm-route-static-validated-no-input-needed-2026: menu_obj is NOT owner+0xe0 (the TitleTopDialog). Per titletop-rowvector-vtable-corrections, TitleTopDialog+0x1290 = Scaleform/GFx MARKUP TEXT, NOT the row vector (confirmed: begin=0x143b68d60 static + end=0). The row vector [+0x1290 stride 0x210] + cursor [+0xb0c] live in the SEPARATE router_this CSMenu (vt 0x142afa070, ctor 0x1409060d0), which is the actual rcx the confirm router 0x14078e1c0 operates on. So fire_titletop_load_entry must operate on router_this, not owner+0xe0.

Boot-sequence CPU profiler (scripts/boot-profile-render.py + DLL er-effects-profiler thread, gated ER_EFFECTS_PROFILE=1) proves ER boot is SINGLE-THREAD-BOUND. Main thread (FromSoft names it via GetThreadDescription; tid 328 in 2026-06-24 onscreen runs) runs ~85-106% of ONE core for essentially the entire boot while 15/16 cores sit idle (all-core util 5-16%). Dominant phase: first_game_frame -> force_offline_cleared = ~12.3s of mostly main-thread CPU (NOT I/O wait: main accrued 34s CPU over 38s wall; CR2.Device(HDD)/CR2.Decomp_0 disk-decomp threads run only ~3.4s/1.5s alongside). Boot-to-in-world-controllable ~24-28s. This is the missed-parallelization the user hypothesized. RIP sampling (ER_EFFECTS_PROFILE_RIP=1, suspend+GetThreadContext, runs offline without crashing) captured 17890 samples; module not ASLR-relocated (base 0x140000000 => RIP==deobf VA). Thin 85-sample main-thread histogram in the 12s window: ~29% in Wine/system code (0x6fff...), top in-module 0x14251bee0/0x140047af0/0x141c90d10. NEXT: denser RIP run (ER_EFFECTS_PROFILE_RIP_EVERY=1) + Ghidra-dump symbolization to name the single-threaded engine-init function. Artifacts: target/runtime-probe/onscreen-capture-20260624-142138 (CPU) and -142507 (RIP). bd er-effects-rs-dzh, branch feat/boot-timeline-profiler.

CONCURRENCY SOLVED (2026-06-23, run 203349). ER runs FULL-SPEED offscreen alongside the user's other game via gamescope --headless -- game_task_ticks=745 (vs 1 unfocused), ran full 41s, 882 debug lines, never touched the user's monitor, never stole focus.

RECIPE (probe harness): set in .envs/gold-probe.env -> RUNTIME_SKIP_VISUAL_CAPTURE=1 (focus-independent watcher, --skip-visual-capture), GAMESCOPE_HEADLESS=1 (probe wraps the Proton launch in 'gamescope --backend headless -W 1280 -H 720 -r 30 --'; gamescope IS installed v3.16.24 -- non-interactive PATH may miss it), RUNTIME_EXTRA_WATCH_ARGS="--no-phase-watchdog --no-world-load-deadline" (MANDATORY: gamescope boot is GPU-contended/slower, surface created ~+6s; the title-stall phase watchdog else tears down at ~6.6s with game_task_ticks=1 BEFORE the game starts ticking -- that early-stall was a watchdog false-positive, NOT a present-block). Then bash scripts/run-product-continue-direct-probe.sh. Note: env file values with spaces MUST be quoted (RUNTIME_EXTRA_WATCH_ARGS="...") else sourcing runs the 2nd word as a command (exit 127).

KEY RE CORRECTION: the unfocused stall was diagnosed as present-block (unfocused surface gets no Wayland frame callbacks) -- that part holds (occluded ER on the user's monitor stalls), BUT gamescope headless gives an always-presenting offscreen surface so ER runs fine; the residual early-teardown was the phase watchdog, fixed by --no-phase-watchdog. Implementation: GAMESCOPE_HEADLESS support added to scripts/run-product-continue-direct-probe.sh (gamescope_prefix). scripts/run-gold-probe-headless.sh (Hyprland headless-output routing) is now SUPERSEDED by gamescope and can be ignored/removed (routing a Proton late-class fullscreen window never landed). Relates unfocused-throttle-is-present-block-not-code-2026-06-23, concurrent-er-launch-frame-gating-2026-06-23.

SAVE-DATA FRONTIER reframed (2026-06-17, deobf static + this-session runtime). The save-IO infra is NOT missing cold -- it self-constructs, same as the world-stream driver (worldres-driver-already-built-at-title-not-the-floor-2026). Save-IO worker MANAGER 0x144852f88 lazy-builds via 0x14240af40 -> 0x14240dee0 (mutex 0x141ec7220/0x141ed6210, NO session gate, NO FD4 assert); global pool 0x144853048 + iodev 0x144589390 likewise self-construct (async-io-b80-lane-mismatch). The world-stream worker task 0x144842d40 is ALSO present (non-null) at the title (this session's probe). So EVERY save/stream object exists cold.

THE WALL = ACTIVATION, not construction: the queued save read drains to RESIDENT (iodev request state 0x14240a1f0 -> 0x14, so poll 0x140679180 advances b80 -> 3) ONLY when the FD4 stream worker task 0x144842d40 is REGISTERED with the scheduler (key 0x59682f01) AND ticked. Our forced SetState(5) / cold initiators skip that registration, so the read never services (b80 stuck) -- the consistent wall across cold-b80-primitive-path-dead / primitive-b80-path-conclusively-dead / stream-worker-registration-is-the-b80-wall.

VERIFY: b80 reaches 3 (the make-or-break -- if the registered+ticked worker drains the read), then c30!=0xa010000 default (use a non-m10 char) + LOAD-CORRECTNESS oracle (name/level/stats) matches. owner=0x1445893b0 o18=0x7fff..856d0 (non-null) o20=0x7fff..85910 (non-null) container=[o20]=0x7fffa0e87200 (NON-NULL, built) h10=[o20+0x10]=0x7fff..6140 h10_deep=[h10+0x10]=0x4 fsm_index(0x14240a1f0(o20))=0x16 df0=[gm+0xdf0]=0x0 b80: 2 (init) -> 0 (by poll waits=0, stays 0).

There is an ACTIVE FSM NODE: 0x14240a1f0 returns a node's real index (via 0x14240d9e0) when a node is active, and 0x14 ONLY when current-node==0 (idle-ready). So a node is STUCK at index 0x16, node deep state *([o20+0x10]+0x10)=0x4, never advancing to 0x14. Empirically the native poll 0x140e6e080 must return >=2 at idx 0x16 (b80 bounced 2->0), so the prior jump-table decode claiming idx0x16->ret1 is suspect; trust the runtime: idx 0x16 is a NON-success parked state. READ BODY 0x14067b290 needs setter 0x140679180 to return 0 = poll==0 = idx 0x14 = NO active node. The stuck 0x16 node blocks it.

The save-dir INSTALL readback is still garbage (size=4294967296) so dir install unverified; the stuck node may be a failed/blocked read op. NEXT (static): decode 0x14240a1f0 (what produces 0x16 vs 0x14), 0x14240d9e0 (node real-index), the node object at h10 (vtable/type, what op, what state 0x4 means), and what advances/clears the node. GATE-B key source + registry lifecycle, byte-confirmed (deobf base 0x140000000):

KEY IS LIVE INDEX-0 LOOKUP (not a frame literal, not a fixed global): submit-builder 0x14240ad30. Save-request frame 0x140e6f4a0 (twin caller 0x140e6f69e): at 0x140e6f4cf call getter 0x141ee1110 -> registry; at 0x140e6f4e2 call 0x141ee10b0(rcx=registry, rdx=out, r8d=0) = KEY PRODUCER: walks [registry+0x28] counting from 0 to index, returns *(u32*)(node+0x10) of index-0 node via 0x141ee05f0; on empty/overrun returns 0xFFFFFFFF (helper 0x141ee0600). Then key copied to builder 0x14240ad30. GATE B then walks list for a node matching that id. => On empty registry: key=0xFFFFFFFF, empty walk, no match, discard. CONSEQUENCE: a single hand-spliced node would SELF-SATISFY the gate (it becomes index-0, key==its own id) -- but that likely passes the gate with NO REAL WORKER behind it to service the read (gate is a proxy for a real worker). Hence CLEAN worker-enroll is required, not a fake node.

REGISTRY LIFECYCLE: *0x144843038 written ONLY by initializer 0x141ee1720 (@0x141ee17a5), zeroed ONLY by teardown 0x141ee1590. ctor 0x141ee0bd0 called from exactly ONE site (init @0x141ee179c). Built once-lazily by FIRST of 26 getter(0x141ee1110) sites; submit-path getters (0x14240ace0, 0x140e6f4cf, 0x14240e0af) run first on cold path -> registry built stale-empty BEFORE save worker enrolled. REBUILD PRIMITIVE (proven): teardown 0x141ee1590() [no args; frees registry, NULLs *0x144843038, sets flag *0x144843040=1] then getter 0x141ee1110() rebuilds fresh (init checks flag@0x141ee1758, logs via 0x141eb97a0, clears@0x141ee1776). Non-blocking.

DEVICE MGR: getter 0x141ee1110 first builds device mgr: if(*0x144843030==0){if(*0x144842d40==0)*0x144842d40=0x141eceb10(); *0x144843030=*0x144842d40;}. 0x141eceb10->jmp 0x141f1d190-> singleton *0x144844008 (ctor 0x141f61d80, vtable 0x1430c7d30). Fresh device mgr worker list EMPTY (head@device_mgr+0x8 self-ref @0x141f61db1). vtable[+0x18]=0x141f62660 = movl 0x3b,(rdx);ret (status accessor, NOT worker enumerator).

REMAINING BLOCKER (agent a4b9fd421a85db12c tracing): the WORKER-ENROLL fn that adds save-read worker to device_mgr+0x8 list, and whether it is INIT-gated (callable cold) or MENU/Continue-gated. Clean fix = enroll worker -> teardown+re-getter rebuild registry -> submit. Worker id value is runtime-determined, not a static literal.

STEP-3 PIVOTAL USER OBSERVATION (2026-07-17): on the LOAD-2 loading screen (System->Quit->Load into the SAME angrE char), the character PORTRAIT does NOT clear/blank+switch and the STATS just POP IN (old data stays, new pops) instead of BLANKING then re-populating. A proper reload BLANKS the old save's portrait/stats then loads the new save's. So the game is SKIPPING the full save-data reload and REUSING load-1's character data because it is the SAME character. REFRAME OF ROOT: the bug is not merely the map EBL archive -- it is a WHOLE-CHARACTER reload short-circuit: loading the SAME character the game is already on triggers 'same char already loaded -> skip full reload', reusing portrait+stats+map(H1: map-mount step not dispatched = whole-char resident belief), but the map block DATA was torn down -> cap+0x90 null -> WORLD RES WAIT. Loading a DIFFERENT character clears+reloads properly (consistent: the self-drive loaded a DIFFERENT staged char and stalled DIFFERENTLY, NOT the m28 bug). FIX TARGET (higher-level than the map descriptor): find the CURRENT-CHARACTER IDENTITY / 'currently loaded save slot|char id' the game holds and compares on load; when it matches (same char) it skips the reload. Clear/invalidate that identity on System->Quit (or before load-2) so the game does a FULL fresh reload (blank portrait/stats + re-mount map). Candidate structures: GameMan/PlayerGameData current-character id, the save-slot/menu current-selection, or a 'loaded save fingerprint'. This is testable: loading a DIFFERENT char works (clears), same char skips. NEXT: RE the same-character reload short-circuit (the identity field + compare site) and clear it. Supersedes the narrow map-mount-descriptor framing. FindFuncImplCall shows lambda_cb31 vtable 0x142b26b68 has _Do_call FUN_1409b1180, which calls FUN_1409a9500(param_1+8,*param_2). FUN_1409a9500 is not the definitive default-selection setter: it first checks FUN_1407a9320(), then if GLOBAL_CSDlc->field53_0x43 is true it clears that flag and builds a side chain: a Success MenuFunctorJob (lambda_3fb20...), an empty/no-op job, a bool lambda_eaca..., a GR_System_Message 0x61f9e OK job through FUN_1407b7270, and stores/chains it via FUN_1407a9340(param_3+2,...). The better target is FUN_14078d860 and its internal item-list construction/selection fields, because FUN_1409abd80 passes the initialized row container auStack_1138 plus the 'ItemList/ItemList' component there to build pMVar10. RECONCILIATION (2026-06-17, static RE, two independent passes) of the d180 zero-input realize conflict. CORRECTS my earlier 'realize is downstream of accept byte' conclusion. KEY FACTS (fresh disasm): (1) Accept byte 0x144589bdc has EXACTLY ONE image-wide reference: reader 0x140e85f50 (movzx eax,[0x144589bdc];ret). ZERO RIP-relative writers anywhere; it is member +0x5c of the FD4 input-acceptance singleton @0x144589b80 (vt 0x142bfc3d8), written only via computed this-ptr by the input pipeline = the 'ACCEPT pressed this frame' flag. The boot/intro thread (0x140c80000-0x140ca0000) has ZERO refs to 0x144589bxx (unlike movie latch 0x143d856a0 which the intro writes at 0x140c8ff41) => 0x144589bdc is PURE INPUT, not a settable intro latch; writing it = BANNED. (2) The TitleTopDialog::update 0x1409aac10 reaches the registrar 0x1409b24e0 via TWO paths: the NON-INPUT Loop path (0x1409aad83->0x1409aadee) and the redundant INPUT path (0x1409aadf3, reads accept byte). Both call the SAME registrar. So realize is NOT hard-gated on the accept byte. (3) Loop-ready predicate [Loop_node_vt+0x10] leaves VERIFIED zero-input: 0x1407a9200=cmp dword[rcx],1;seta;ret and 0x1407a9210=cmp dword[rcx],2;sete;ret (node status-field compares); OR-fallbacks 0x14078ae50/0x14078ae40 are xor al,al;ret stubs; predicate refs NEITHER accept byte NOR inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 NOR session 0x143d83148. Trips when branch-gate node status [node+0]==2 (anim-complete/settled). (4) The registrar does NOT push rows into [dialog+0x1290]; it registers SPECS into [dialog+0xa48] (via 0x1407ab370/inner 0x1407aaf00, 0x70-byte node at [registry+0x38]). CONSTRUCTION-TIME at 0x140906430-0x140906488 a 0x60-byte bulk copy (mov rax,[rdi];call [rax+0x18];0x14073c690 -> 6x movups [rax+0..0x50]->[r14+0x1260..0x12b0]) copies an EMBEDDED std::vector struct (begin/end/cap = [+0x1290]/[+0x1298]/[+0x12a0]) from a source object into the dialog. CAVEAT (corrects the subagent): this copies the vector STRUCT, not rows -- a freshly-built empty menu copies an EMPTY vector (begin==end), which EXPLAINS (does not contradict) the runtime [dialog+0xb08]=0/[dialog+0xb0c]=0. The actual per-row PUSH is still a separate generic FD4 vector helper (no literal 0x1290 store at the push site). NET VERDICT: NO accept-byte hard gate forces input. Zero-input Continue path: do nothing -> let update 0x1409aac10 tick FadeIn->Loop on anim-complete -> poll is_in_state(main_sm[dialog+0xa60],Loop@0x142a8f9e8)&&latch[dialog+0xa40]==0 -> native non-input path self-fires registrar 0x1409b24e0 (realizes rows) -> set [dialog+0xb0c]=Load-Game idx -> load_activate via *(dialog[0]+0xa0)=0x1409a4670(rcx=dialog). PGD [0x144588268]!=0 precond. ProfileLoadDialog vtable=0x142b21bf8 (0x142b229f8=RTTI, sig 0x19930522, NOT vtable; runtime object[0]==0x142b229f8 vs on-disk 0x142b21bf8 is PE-rebase/dump skew ~0xe00, NOT multiple inheritance). REMAINING EMPIRICAL UNKNOWN: whether the Loop status field actually reaches ==2 (settles) in the headless flow zero-input -- if a probe shows it never settles, the ONE thing to satisfy is the anim-complete status-settle [node+0]==2, still NOT the accept byte. See titletop-rowvector-vtable-corrections-static-verified-2026, accept-byte-144589bdc-writer-inventory-and-realize-verdict-2026.

ROOT-CAUSE CORRECTION (2026-06-18, static deobf RE, subagent-verified). SUPERSEDES the 'tick Update to populate rows' hypothesis in direct-build-dialog-WORKS-but-rows-empty-need-tick-2026 -- that is WRONG. The direct-built ProfileLoadDialog's save rows are built SYNCHRONOUSLY INSIDE THE CTOR 0x1409a3d90 (NOT by Update, NOT async), and came up empty (bound [dialog+0xb08]=0) because a MENU-SCREEN-REGISTRY GATE inside the ctor's list-builder returned 0. Ticking Update 0x1409a3860 (=jmp widget-pump 0x140745570) does NOTHING -- it never re-runs the list builder. ABANDON the tick-Update plan.

THE POPULATE CHAIN (all inside ctor 0x1409a3d90, disasm-verified): ctor @0x1409a3dd3 call 0x140875590 (build the 10-slot ProfileSummary row-list, rcx=&out): @0x1408755cf movb 0x3c,[rsp+0x20] (query key=0x3c, the save-slot data source); @0x1408755d9 call THE GATE 0x140765030(rcx=&key)->al; @0x1408755e7 test al; if al==0 -> write count=0 ([rdi+0xa60]=0), return EMPTY (<-- what happened); if al!=0 -> loop slot 0..9: 0x1402567b0 (ProfileSummary getter) + 0x140261cd0 (slot-valid) + 0x1408752c0 (build 0x108-stride view-model) + append, finalize via 0x140874be0 (count [rdi+0xa58/0xa60]). Then ctor @0x1409a3e03 0x1409a51c0 builds GridControl @[dialog+0xa38]/[dialog+0x1260] + copies list->grid source vec [dialog+0x1268] via 0x140874be0, setting bound [dialog+0xb08]. @0x1409a3f5a 0x1409a5f20 self-search rows for [row+0x8]==active_slot.

THE GATE 0x140765030(key 0x3c): generic menu-resource lookup (60 callers); movsbq key; jmp *(0x143b34f50)[key*8]; handler walks a LINKED LIST from [query+0] comparing each node vs the ACTIVE MENU-SCREEN REGISTRY HEAD 0x143d6b800 (lazily built 0x1407668c0->0x140765930; note 0x143d6b800 is adjacent to inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0/CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 -- a menu/input mgr singleton). Match -> [query+8] (nonzero=TRUE); no match -> 0. For a STANDALONE direct-built dialog the key-0x3c save-data screen-node is NOT in 0x143d6b800 (it's registered only when the native menu-task ENTERS the Load-Game screen) -> gate FALSE -> empty list. THIS IS THE ENTIRE BUG: another lazy menu-SCREEN-registration gate, biting at ctor time.

NOTE set_items 0x14078ec90 (vtable 0x142aa2450 slot+0x10, std::_Func_impl GridIterator) -> rebuild_rows 0x14078d2c0 is the grid RE-FILL callback (items-changed re-layout), NOT the initial populate -- don't chase it for first-fill.

RANKED FIX OPTIONS: 
#1 (CORRECT, zero-input once solved): register the save-data screen node (key 0x3c) into registry 0x143d6b800 BEFORE the ctor, so the gate passes and the ctor populates rows synchronously. NEEDS the registrar (write side of the 0x143d6b800 linked list) + the key-0x3c node descriptor. Headless there is NO native Load-Game entry to learn this from -> requires a ONE-TIME user Load-Game entry with a read-only hook on the gate 0x140765030 ENTRY (log rcx=key node + the matched registry node) AND the registry writer, to capture the registrar + node (a dev RE measurement, NOT part of the zero-input deliverable). Then replicate registration zero-input.
#2 (BYPASS, fully zero-input, fiddly): after direct-build, REPLICATE 0x140875590's al!=0 branch ourselves (skip the gate): loop slots 0..9 calling ProfileSummary getter 0x1402567b0 + slot-valid 0x140261cd0 + view-model builder 0x1408752c0, append, then copy into grid [dialog+0x1268] via 0x140874be0 + set [dialog+0xb08]=count. AV risk: 0x1408752c0 + the row detail reader inside load_activate 0x1409a4670 FD4-assert session 0x144588268 + deref GameDataMan 0x143d5df38 (both non-null at title). Requires getting 0x1408752c0's ABI + the grid-copy exactly right.
#3 (BYPASS the dialog rows entirely): the mount only needs the SLOT fed to the selector-step builder 0x140826510 (gold capture: builder owner=arena, rdx=dialog+0x50, r8d=slot, r9=ptr) which builds selector 0x140826d50 that the live menu task pumps -> menu_deser 0x14082c240 mount. If builder 0x140826510 can be called directly with slot=0 (bypassing load_activate's cursor/bound read), rows are unnecessary. OPEN: the builder's rcx=menu-task arena + r9 come from the menu context (the low-addr FD4 arena) -- may itself need the menu-task context.
#3 ruled-IN-but-needs-RE. 

PRECONDS (all paths): GameDataMan 0x143d5df38 non-null, ProfileSummary +0x78 (vt 0x1429e60e8) populated, session 0x144588268 non-null -- all true at parked title. The ONE missing thing = registry 0x143d6b800 has no key-0x3c node. VAs: ctor 0x1409a3d90, gated list-builder 0x140875590, GATE 0x140765030 (key 0x3c, registry 0x143d6b800), registry lazy-head 0x1407668c0/0x140765930, grid-copy 0x140874be0(rcx=[dialog+0x1268],rdx=[list+8]), view-model 0x1408752c0, ProfileSummary getter 0x1402567b0/resolver 0x140261b80/valid 0x140261cd0 stride 0x2a0, bound [dialog+0xb08] cursor [dialog+0xb0c] GridControl [dialog+0x1260] grid-src [dialog+0x1268], builder 0x140826510 selector 0x140826d50 menu_deser 0x14082c240, load_activate 0x1409a4670. SELF-DRIVE last gap = OptionSetting TAB-SWITCH is mouse-only on native ER (2026-07-17, DLL e6f67b87, commit 5e9017b). The self-drive now navigates natively: boot->in-world->Esc(0x1b)->02_000_IngameTop->UP(0x26)+Enter(0x0d)->02_040_OptionSetting (lands on tab 0 = Game Options). TO_PROFILE must reach the Quit tab (OPTIONSETTING_QUIT_TAB_INDEX=8) where the cloned Load Profile row lives, but AUTO-DISCOVERY cycled 8 candidate keys Right(0x27)/Left(0x25)/Tab(0x09)/](0xdd)/[(0xdb)/PgDn(0x22)/Q(0x51)/E(0x45) and NONE moved OPTIONSETTING_CURRENT_TAB (stayed 0). So native ER has NO keyboard binding for menu tab-switching -- the user switches tabs by MOUSE CLICK (matches 'I click on the SL2 / the character'). ER reads the MOUSE via DInput (dinput_mouse_get_state_hook fires 10855x/run). SO THE FINISH is a MOUSE path, not keyboard: (option A) inject a left-click via the DInput mouse hook (add injection to dinput_mouse_get_state_hook like the keyboard set_injected_key) after positioning the OS cursor (SetCursorPos) over the Quit tab / Load Profile row -- needs the on-screen coords of those elements (read from the menu object layout or the ProfileSelect/OptionSetting dialog geometry); (option B, cleaner/no-coords) DETERMINISTICALLY trigger the Load-Profile-row activation the click performs -- the DLL's profile-load route (memory native-profile-load-msgbox-crash-1162: click -> 'profile-load route FIRE 05_010_ProfileSelect wrapper' -> SUBMIT job) -- invoke that route so ProfileSelect opens + SYSTEM_QUIT_QUICKLOAD_RETURN_CHAIN_SYSTEM_DIALOG gets set, then the existing TO_SLOT (cursor to slot 0 via UP/DOWN arrows, which DO work) + CONFIRM (Enter) arm the switch -> step-3 repro. Keyboard channel (Esc/arrows/Enter via SendInput to the real ELDEN RING window, overlay excluded) is SOLID for everything except tab-switch. See [[native-er-selfdrive-sendinput-result-2026-07-17]].

DEFINITIVE static RE (2026-06-15, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off) -- WHO constructs CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 + the menu, WHEN, and the native-replication verdict. Contradiction RESOLVED: CSFeMan is built during the LOAD/in-game-flow step (InGameStep step idx 6), which IS also the front-end bring-up -- both prior notes were right about different layers.

=== CSFeMan IDENTITY + WRITER (disasm-proven) ===
0x143d6b880 = CSFeMan singleton ptr (FD4Singleton, size 0x8420). SOLE writer = 0x140766010 (CSFeMan::Construct): cmp [0x143d6b880],0 / jne skip; if null -> alloc 0x8420 via 0x141eb9ed0, call inner-ctor 0x14076a6b0, then mov [0x143d6b880],rax (store @0x140766068). ptr_scan/rva_scan: no other writer. PRECONDITION (proven @0x140af2e06): before the Construct call it reads inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 and FD4-asserts (0x141eb97a0, FD4Singleton.h uninitialized-singleton) if NULL -> CSFeMan Construct REQUIRES inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 non-null. CALL ARGS @0x140af2e47: rcx=inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0, rdx=[owner+0xe8] (MoveMapStep field set from the spawner's r8). Inner ctor 0x14076a6b0(rcx=newCSFeMan, rdx=ptr, r8=inputmgr).

=== CALLER CHAIN (each link has exactly ONE static caller; verified via recon_callscan E8/E9) ===
0x140766010 CSFeMan::Construct  <- called ONLY by 0x140af2e47
  inside 0x140af28d0 = MoveMapStep/FeStep CTOR (sets vtable 0x142b5ff38 @0x140af2923; refs to that vtable = ctor 0x140af28d0 + dtor 0x140af3a90 only)  <- called ONLY by 0x140aec1f2
  inside 0x140aec120 = STEP_MoveMap_Init / spawner (allocs 0x4c0 MoveMapStep @0x140aec1ca then ctor)  <- NO E8 caller, NO 8-byte ptr ref. It is DISPATCHED as a STEP: registered as the idx-6 step-func at 0x143d70190 by the InGameStep step-table builder 0x1400a3710 (static init: lea 0x140aec120 -> mov [0x143d70190]). Table base 0x143d70130, (func,name) pairs; idx0=0x140aec010, ... idx6=0x140aec120.
The InGameStep step table 0x143d70130 is bound by the InGameStep CTOR 0x140aeaaa0 (vtable 0x142b5d408; @0x140aeaabc lea rdx=0x143d70130 -> 0x140aea840 bind; inits request vector +0xb35f0, map cmd +0x100=-1/+0x104=0)  <- called ONLY by 0x140b0b2a8
  inside 0x140b0b1c0 = OUTER SimpleTitleStep / FE-host TopStep CTOR (binds its OWN step table 0x143d71580 via 0x140b0b020; vtable 0x142b63bb0; allocs 0xb3870 then constructs the InGameStep child via 0x140aeaaa0 @0x140b0b2a8)  <- called ONLY by 0x140ae786f
  inside 0x140ae7830 = FE-host alloc+ctor (alloc 0x3e8 via 0x141eb9ed0 -> ctor 0x140b0b1c0 -> register child into [parent+0x118]; tail-jmp 0x140ae7d50)  <- NO E8 caller, NO ptr ref, NO rva ref (only its own .pdata/EH scope table @0x1436fe4d8). It is SCHEDULER-DISPATCHED (runtime step/task factory), NOT statically callable.

=== CONTRADICTION RESOLVED ===
Both prior notes were correct about DIFFERENT layers: (a) postaccept-csfeman-built-by-ingamestep-movemap is RIGHT that the ctor lives at InGameStep step idx6 (0x140aec120->0x140af28d0->0x140766010). (b) forcing-hits-session-singleton-cascade is RIGHT that CSFeMan must be non-null BEFORE the title save-load dispatcher 0x140afb880 runs -- because that dispatcher runs LATER in the SAME in-game flow, after STEP_MoveMap_Init already built CSFeMan. So CSFeMan is NOT a separate menu-entry object; it is built once the InGameStep flow advances into the MoveMap step. It is NULL at the press-any-button title because the OUTER SimpleTitleStep (0x140b0b1c0) -- the parent that constructs the InGameStep -- is not instantiated yet (outer-simpletitlestep-absent-at-title-wall).

=== REAL INPUT'S MENU-BUILD TRIGGER ===
On a real boot the scheduler instantiates the FE-host (0x140ae7830 -> outer SimpleTitleStep 0x140b0b1c0 -> InGameStep 0x140aeaaa0) as part of the title->menu transition. The outer SimpleTitleStep's STEP_IngameInit 0x140b0a1f0 (idx in outer table 0x143d71580) is the handler that, on selection, SUBMITS the play-game/map request: it reads selection [step+0x130]; if ==-1 uses default map string 'm60_42_34_00' 0x142b62c70 (resolved 0x14071fd60); sets inputmgr+0x6b0=1 (@0x140b0a249, the pump_ran flag); then calls play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0 (@0x140b0a3ec). play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0 is a REQUEST writer (NOT a builder): gate [rdx]!=-1 (@0x140aebe23), sets [owner+0xd8]=1, resolves map into [owner+0x100/0x104] via 0x14067aec0/0x14067abd0, appends to request vector owner+0xb35f0, writes GameFlow cmd to GameMan(0x143d69918)+0x14. The InGameStep step machine then advances to idx6 STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 which builds the MoveMapStep + CSFeMan. So: input -> scheduler builds FE-host -> outer SimpleTitleStep STEP_IngameInit submits request -> InGameStep advances to MoveMap step -> CSFeMan constructed. The 'press any button -> build the menu' decision lives in the SCHEDULER's title-step advance (the FE-host factory 0x140ae7830 dispatch), gated on the title-accept; it is NOT a directly-callable function.

=== NATIVE NO-INPUT RECIPE -- VERDICT: NO CLEAN STATIC TRIGGER ===
The construction entry 0x140ae7830 (and the spawner 0x140aec120) have ZERO static call sites and ZERO pointer/RVA refs (only EH/.pdata) -- they are instantiated by the FD4 task scheduler's runtime step-factory, so there is no function for our DLL to call to 'start the menu build'. This is the structural wall (frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned-not-callable), now confirmed by exhaustive ref scans of the whole chain.
MINIMAL native sequence IF one insists on building it in-process (HIGH RISK, replicates scheduler work; NOT recommended as the product path): (1) ensure inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 non-null (it IS non-null at the stable title per native-continue-and-slotn-recipe-2026); (2) alloc 0x3e8 + call FE-host ctor 0x140ae7830(rcx=buf) -- which internally allocs 0x3e8/0xb3870 and constructs outer SimpleTitleStep 0x140b0b1c0 + InGameStep 0x140aeaaa0, binding step tables 0x143d71580/0x143d70130; (3) REGISTER the resulting TopStep with the task scheduler so its update/step pump runs each frame (the missing piece -- the scheduler normally owns this; without registration the step machine never advances, so STEP_MoveMap_Init idx6 never runs and CSFeMan never builds -- matches the orphan/stall seen when forcing); (4) drive selection (set outer step+0x130 = slot or -1 for default) so STEP_IngameInit 0x140b0a1f0 submits via play_game_submit; (5) let the pumped InGameStep reach idx6 -> CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 becomes non-null (VERIFY: read 0x143d6b880 != 0). The blocker for (3) is that the scheduler-registration ABI (which CSTaskGroup/step-parent the FE-host attaches to, and the per-frame update entry) is the unreversed piece; just constructing the objects without scheduler-pumping reproduces the known stall. CONCLUSION: zero-input CSFeMan/menu construction requires REPLICATING the scheduler's FE-host instantiation+registration, which the game exposes only as a scheduler-internal factory -- consistent with the product decision to instead drive the NATIVE title-accept (autoload-dll-product-requirements) and let the scheduler build the menu. Related: postaccept-csfeman-built-by-ingamestep-movemap, forcing-hits-session-singleton-cascade, frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned-not-callable, outer-simpletitlestep-absent-at-title-wall, native-continue-and-slotn-recipe-2026.

STATIC RE (2026-06-16, objdump on decrypted on-disk eldenring.exe, ER 1.16.1 base 0x140000000, .text VA=foff+0x140000a00; helper scripts/disas-va.sh). DECODES the IfElseJob combiner child layout that blocked the owner+0x130 main-menu walk from reaching the Load-Game leaf d180 (the missing piece of Path A in mainmenu-built-at-owner130-not-pumped-zeroinput-2026). NO pump needed -- the child jobs are BUILD-TIME populated, so a static walk finds d180 even though owner+0x130 is not pumped zero-input.

=== TWO combiner node kinds under owner+0x130's MenuJobSequence (vt 0x142aa2958 upd 0x1407aa1f0 iterator, count [node+0x60], children [node+0x18+i*8]) ===

(1) PREDICATE node vt 0x142aa2938 upd 0x140792460: NOT a container. Reads inner condition [node+0x10], calls its vt[+0x10], writes a branch-index result {1 or 2} into [node+0x18]-pointed int via 0x1407a91e0 (which is just `mov [rcx],edx; mov [rcx+4],r8d; ret`). No child JOBS. Skip in the job walk.

(2) IFELSEJOB node vt 0x142aa2c38 upd 0x1407931e0: THE container holding d180. Layout (decoded from selector 0x140793390 called by the update):
   - INLINE entries array at [node+0x18], stride 0x10, each entry = { predicate_ptr @ +0x0 , child_job_ptr @ +0x8 }
   - entry COUNT at [node+0xa0]  (NOT +0x60 -- the generic [+0x60] read lands mid-array = the 'garbage 0x142bbb310' prior memory saw)
   - DEFAULT/else child job at [node+0xa8]
   - ACTIVE (runtime-selected) child at [node+0xb0] (set lazily by update 0x1407931e0; do NOT rely on it -- may be null if never pumped)
   Update 0x1407931e0: if [node+0xb0] active child set -> pump it (call active->vt[+0x10] frametime). Else evaluate [node+0x10] cond -> branch result -> 0x140793390 selects matching entry's child_job (or [node+0xa8] default) -> swaps [node+0xb0]. The entry child_jobs + default are PRE-BUILT and RETAINED at build time (0x140793390 does `mov rcx,[rbx+8]; add rcx,8; call 0x141eba1c0` addref) -- so they exist before any tick.

=== HOW TO WALK TO d180 (zero-input, no pump) ===
Extend diagnostic_job_tree_walk: classify node by update fn. update==0x1407aa1f0 (seq) -> children [node+0x18+i*8], count [node+0x60]. update==0x1407931e0 (ifelse) -> for i in 0..[node+0xa0]: child=[node+0x18+i*0x10+0x8]; PLUS default=[node+0xa8]; (optionally active=[node+0xb0]). Recurse; functor_chain_hits_factory(child)->dialog_factory 0x14081ead0 uniquely IDs d180. Pure reads, never call predicates. This finds d180 in owner+0x130's tree regardless of focus/active branch, so Stage-2 invoke_menu_item_functor(d180) can fire it zero-input. Related: mainmenu-built-at-owner130-not-pumped-zeroinput-2026, mainmenu-item-builder-into-iterator-tree-2026, zero-input-menu-open-milestone-2026.

SPIKE er-effects-rs-vbo graphics-phase (sec6-9 ~4s) structure via stack-scan sampling (profiler now captures partial call stacks; run onscreen-capture-20260624-150052, 142 main-thread stacked samples in window). Persistent stack frames (>=50%): LeaveCriticalSection/DLPlainLightMutex (57% -- a LOCK), _towlower_l (50% wide-char case-fold), FUN_14012bf60(DLRawStr*,..,wchar_t*) string op, FUN_141b037a0 (CRC hash util, 52 callers), GXCGShader region (FUN_141afc0f0). => the graphics phase is a SINGLE-THREADED, LOCK-GUARDED, string-keyed HASH/REGISTRY FILL (resource name -> towlower -> CRC hash -> insert under DLPlainLightMutex), per-item over many resources. PARALLELIZATION VERDICT: theoretically per-item/independent (structure is thread-safe -- it has a lock) BUT (1) a global light-mutex serializes the critical section -> Amdahl caps any win; (2) the loop is driven by FromSoft's internal resource-registration code -- parallelizing means HIJACKING that loop from our injected DLL to fan out across threads, racing engine state = deep RE, high risk, fragile; (3) lock is currently UNCONTENDED (only main thread) so parallelizing would ADD contention on the insert. Low feasibility / high risk from our DLL position. Combined spike conclusion: NO clean boot-parallelization win available to us -- ntdll mem ~5s irreducible, arxan ~3s unprovably-skippable/likely key-derivation, graphics ~4s lock-bound registry fill not safely parallelizable from a DLL. Updates [[boot-phase-structure-sequential-2026-06-24]].

VALIDATED COLD-BUILD LEVER for the world-stream driver (2026-06-17, deobf disasm + RTTI). The WorldResWait floor (0x143d7c088 null at forced title) is now cold-buildable with ONE call.

IDENTITY (RTTI): 0x143d7c088 = CS::CSEmkResManImp singleton (the EMK resource manager / world-resource streaming driver; ctor 0x140cdb040 sets vtable 0x142ba2748=CSEmkResManImp). Built by the CSResStep (vtable 0x142ba1690) tick/getter 0x140cd6c50. resmgr that WorldResWait reads = CS::WorldRes (vt 0x142a7e030); CS::WorldInfoOwner (vt 0x142a7de60) is the OWNER not the resmgr.

THE LEVER: call 0x140cd6c50(rcx = a PERSISTENT zeroed ~0x60-byte stub with [stub+0x48]=6, [stub+0x4c]/[stub+0x50] writable). PROOF it is safe with a stub this: the rsi(=this) register is used ONLY at the prologue (0x140cd6c68 mov rsi,rcx) and the TAIL (0x140cd7c64 mov rcx,rsi; 0x140cd7c67 call 0x140cd7f20(this); 0x140cd7c6c movb [this+0x50],1) -- the ENTIRE cluster-building BODY (0x140cd6c6d..0x140cd7c63) has NO this deref; it builds the whole EMK resman cluster via GLOBAL RIP-relative stores + boot allocators 0x143d872e0/0x143d87308/0x143d87360 (NO session/scheduler/latch/CSFeMan deref, NO assert on the success path; main ret 0x140cd7c7c). The CSEmkResManImp build (0x140cd75e9..0x140cd7623): r8=[0x143d872e0]; buf=0x141eb9ed0(rcx=0x500 size, rdx=8 align, r8=alloc); 0x140cdb040(rcx=buf, rdi=prior-built sibling -> [buf+0x488]=sibling); [0x143d7c088]=buf. The tail 0x140cd7f20(this): incl [this+0x4c]; if [this+0x48]>=6 -> build worker 0x144842d40 (0x141eceb10 if null) + register; then operates on the WORKER (not this). So stub+0x48=6 triggers the worker register too. ONE stub call = streaming driver 0x143d7c088 + stream worker 0x144842d40, both non-null, cold.

WHY IT WAS NULL: 0x140cd6c50 is the CSResStep's step method, invoked via the FD4 scheduler dispatch (0 static refs; registered by 0x1400acb30 dispatch table). Our forced SetState(5) never ticks the CSResStep, so the driver never builds. The stub call replicates the tick context-free.

NEXT (SAVE-SAFE first probe): from idx10 at the parked title (NO SetState, NO world load -> zero save-write risk), call 0x140cd6c50(&stub[+0x48=6]) once; read [0x143d7c088] and [0x144842d40] before (expect 0) and after (expect non-null); no crash = lever validated. THEN the full chain: SetState(5) builds CSFeMan+MoveMapStep -> our stub call builds driver+worker -> 0x14066e2e4(resmgr=WorldRes) enable -> feed real slot map owner+0x100 -> WorldResWait drains. BUT the full chain loads a world; do NOT run it until the SAVE-DATA/char mount is solved (else autosave blank char -- banned). Related: worldstream-half-cold-lever-set-static-validated-2026, worldres-enable-0x14066e2e4-decoded-receiver-and-driver-singleton-2026, session-activation-worldres-driver-cold-buildable-session-gated-2026, deobfuscated-er-image-tooling-2026.
