FINAL identity for the ToS-build fix (2026-06-21). The lazy-component-builder that builds the ToS (entry 0x1407ad1c0) is the REALIZE virtual method at vtable+0x10 of CS::MenuWindowJob (base vtable 0x142aa97e8; also overridden by CS::GaitemRepositoryMenuWindowJob 0x142aad548). So 'this' (rcx/rdi) is a CS::MenuWindowJob. Its layout for realize: +0xa8 = PRIMARY deferred build-lambda (std::function; the ToS/no-save surface), +0xe8 = FALLBACK deferred build-lambda (the normal save-present component), +0x130 = built/cached result, +0x10 = a guard (must be 0 to build). Build precedence in realize: if [this+0x130]!=0 skip; elif [this+0xa8]!=0 -> _Do_call [this+0xa8] (builds ToS) -> [this+0x130]; elif [this+0xe8]!=0 -> _Do_call [this+0xe8] (builds normal) -> [this+0x130]; else graceful skip 0x1407ad3ec.
THE COMPLETE ZERO-INPUT FIX: when the ToS-building MenuWindowJob is realized, CLEAR its +0xa8 so realize falls back to the normal +0xe8 component (no ToS built, no null-crash, no dismiss-input). The DLL ALREADY hooks MenuWindowJob ctors (oracle_menu_window_ctor / idle_ctor / native_ctor_b families, runs 240/241) -> use those to identify the ToS MenuWindowJob (the one with a non-null +0xa8 whose lambda builds TosMultiLangDialog) and zero its +0xa8 before realize (which runs from the menu update after the advance). 
IMPLEMENT: gated own_stepper/hook lever -> on MenuWindowJob ctor or each tick at the advanced title, for the title MenuWindowJob(s) with [+0xa8]!=0 AND [+0x130]==0, set [+0xa8]=0; verify-only 120s probe -> expect total_builds=0, menu_opened_latch=1, no exit, normal component built. This is the END of the ToS-on-advance problem -- fully readable, no Arxan, no input. Chain: title-global-accept-byte advance (lever-2/3) + clear-+0xa8 -> zero-input main menu with no ToS. Related: tos-fix-clear-this-a8-falls-back-to-this-e8-normal-component-2026, tos-build-gate-is-this-a8-nonnull-lazy-component-2026.

USER OBSERVATION 2026-06-24: native Windows boot is NOT meaningfully faster than the Proton/Wine boot. => the ~49% of main-thread engine-init CPU in Wine/ntdll memory code is NOT primarily a Proton emulation tax; it is REAL single-threaded game memory work (memcpy/decompress/memory-bound loading) that costs the same on Windows. Correction to the earlier 'native Windows would likely help' hypothesis in [[boot-profiler-coldwarm-everyboot-2026-06-24]] -- that hypothesis is FALSE per user. Implication: the boot is genuinely memory/CPU-bound single-threaded game work, so PARALLELIZATION (not environment, not caching) is the real lever. The engine already HAS idle worker threads during the 12s (FD4JobWorker:1/2, EzWorkPool_HIGHEST_0 observed mostly idle; CR2.Device(HDD)/CR2.Decomp_0 decomp threads run only ~3.4s/1.5s) -> the main thread bottlenecks while the engine's own job pool sits idle. Spike branch spike/boot-parallelization.

MAJOR REFRAME (RTTI byte-confirmed via docs/recon/deobf-rtti-classmap.tsv). The parked owner-FSM node (vtable 0x1431fc640) is .?AVSLSelectStorageSession@SaveLoad2@@ -- the console-era STORAGE-DEVICE-SELECTION session, NOT the .sl2 read. Earlier 'node+0x1d8 = async save-read job' was a MIS-ID. node+0x1d8 = the SLSessionRunnable op-handle for the storage-SELECT op; it reads NULL cold (my probe's 0xFFFFFFFF was a different field / format sentinel -- a true 0xFFFFFFFF would route into 0x142412890 and crash, not observed). 

WHY STUCK: advance 0x142411aa0 runs work-step 0x142415e30 (builds wide path, writes it into the runnable via 0x14240ece0->0x142412940 ONLY if *(node+0x1d8)!=NULL) then pump 0x142412ce0 (sub-state loop gated on node+0xb8 = completion of *(node+0x1d8)). Completion predicate 0x14240ecc0(&node+0x1d8): *(node+0x1d8)==NULL -> returns 3 (in-progress) -> op-state 4, re-park. So NULL runnable => perpetual 'in-progress' fixpoint at idx 0x15. NOTHING in advance/pump CREATES the runnable; it must be created by the SESSION-START which the cold menu-free path skips.

PIPELINE: SLSystemImpl (vtable 0x1431fc080) -> SLSessionManager (0x1431fbbf8) sequences sessions: SLSelectStorageSession (0x1431fc640) FIRST (resolve storage device; on PC must auto-resolve to local disk), THEN SLLoadSession (0x1431fc470) does the actual .sl2 read via FD4 job submit 0x140e6fb50 / thunk *0x144c0c594 (pools 0x143d87310/308/2e0/358). We never reach SLLoadSession because the select session never completes. Storage devmgr singleton 0x144844008 (=*0x144842d40, lazy 0x141eceb10->0x141f1d190, Win32 storage-enum via *0x144c0c8ac + drive-type jump table) IS available cold.

REAL FIX (revised, no-compromises): drive the SaveLoad2 pipeline kickoff -- find the SLSessionManager/SLSystemImpl SESSION-START that constructs the SLSelectStorageSession runnable op (writes node+0x1d8 non-NULL) so the existing advance/pump completes the select -> manager advances to SLLoadSession -> real read -> b80=3. Prefer the HIGH-LEVEL warm Continue SaveLoad2 'request load slot N' entry over hand-assembling sessions. Do NOT synth a read job on the select node (wrong layer). NOT byte-confirmed yet: (a) FD4 worker-thread start/cold; (b) the exact SLSessionManager op-constructor call. Supersedes b80-WALL-FD4-read-job-never-dispatched (refines: it's the SELECT session's runnable that's missing, upstream of the read). Code in cold_char_mount (warm-kick+finalize+pump) reaches idx0x15/deep0 but is pumping the wrong-layer select session.

POST-CONTINUE PORTRAIT -- WHY THE LOADING SCREEN IS BLACK (2026-06-29, branch spike/boot-parallelization). The own-renderer build path works (built[r=10 m=7..10], model renders), and the forge OWNS the visible now-loading background surface (user confirmed: full-screen CHECKER showed when we bound build_portrait_tpf's checker; stock art when untouched). But binding our live offscreen RT yields a BLACK loading screen. ROOT CAUSE proven by in-process D3D12 readback (lookat-spared-sweep 'loading-portrait-chain' line):

- The renderer's OFFSCREEN RENDER TARGET (reached by find_d3d12_resource BFS from renderer+0xa8) has the opaque portrait: rt[rgb_max=255 alpha_max=255].
- The SAMPLEABLE CSGxTexture our forge binds (renderer+0xa8 offscreen -> +0x10 CSRuntimeTexResCap -> +GX = the SAME ptr 0x..c6e0 we bind into the now-loading container) reads BLACK: boundtex[rgb_max=0 alpha_max=255]. Opaque-black, NOT alpha=0.

So it is NOT a colorspace/alpha/format issue (format is 28 = R8G8B8A8_UNORM, same as the checker; content is rgb255/alpha255 opaque in the RT). It is a RENDER-TARGET -> SHADER-RESOURCE RESOLVE GAP: the model rasterizes into the offscreen color render target, but that result is never resolved/copied into the sampleable SRV texture (offscreen+0x10's GX) that GFx samples. The menu's normal per-frame offscreen pipeline performs this resolve; our manual PROFILE_OFFSCREEN_DRIVE (FUN_140bb8d90 -> FUN_140bb73a0) rasterizes but does not resolve into the sampleable texture. GFx DOES re-sample our swapped GX each composite frame (else it would show the cached checker, not black), but the SRV it samples is the unresolved black one.

NEXT: find + invoke the offscreen RESOLVE step (RT->SRV copy) so offscreen+0x10's GX gets the content -- then the existing forge bind shows the portrait, no bind change needed. Candidates: a present/finalize call in the CSEzOffscreenRend pipeline, or a resolve inside the menu's per-frame offscreen-draw caller that our manual drive skips. Alternatively locate the render-target's OWN srv/CSGxTexture (the one with content the BFS finds) and bind THAT into the container instead of offscreen+0x10. Diagnostics committed: PROFILE_LOOKAT_RT_RGB/ALPHA_MAX + PROFILE_BOUND_GX_RGB/ALPHA_MAX, logged in 'loading-portrait-chain'. Builds on postcontinue-portrait-OWN-RENDERER-WORKS-2026-06-29.

DEFINITIVE VERIFICATION (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY disasm) of THE gate that blocks b80 engagement at our in-context loaded state. CONFIRMS async-io-b80-lane-mismatch + menu-load-path-is-movemaplist-self-driven with fresh disasm.

=== THE GAP: the MoveMapStep (world-load step) DOES NOT register the FD4 stream worker task 0x144842d40 ===
recon_callscan + disasm of: MoveMapStep ctor 0x140af28d0, spawner STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120, MoveMapStep update 0x140aff640 -- NONE of them contain a 0x142656b00 (FD4 scheduler register) call, the key immediate 0x59682f01, or a 0x144842d40 build (0x141eceb10). Grepped all three dumps: zero hits. So the world-load step that our SetState(5)/idx10 recipe builds runs the b80 dispatchers (0x140afbad0/0x140afb880/0x140afa6d0, all called unconditionally from 0x140aff640 @0x140aff720/728/730) but NEVER activates the async-IO worker LANE that services the queued slot read.

=== WHO DOES REGISTER IT: the MoveMapListStep menu helper 0x140af1b40 (VERIFIED) ===
0x140af1b40 (state-advance helper, 9 callers = the MoveMapListStep idx steps 0x140af06b3/07a6/0992/1440/17db/17ed/184a/1a24/1acf) DECODED: inc [owner+0x4c]; if [owner+0x48]+1 > 0xb (terminal-ish state) -> lazily build task 0x144842d40 via 0x141eceb10 if null, build a name-descriptor (string 0x1429d2b28 via 0x140116c20), then ecx=0x59682f01; CALL 0x142656b00 @0x140af1c05 (FD4 scheduler REGISTER, 269 callers, the same register fn IngameInit tail uses). So the in-game save-list MENU activates the stream worker on its own; the bare forced-title MoveMapStep does not. THIS is why menu-load completes (b80 reaches 3) but our forced-title b80 initiate stalls (submit 0x140e6ec70 enqueues onto a pool whose backing stream task 0x144842d40 is never ticked).

=== BUT the MoveMapListStep needs a LIVE SESSION (hard FD4 assert) ===
MoveMapListStep idx0 0x140af1720 DECODED: asserts SESSION singleton 0x144588e98 != 0 (0x141eb97a0 assert -> crash if null) AND resmgr 0x143d5b0f8 != 0, then tail-jmps 0x140af1b40 (register). So we cannot simply spawn the MoveMapListStep to register the worker unless session 0x144588e98 is live. (Also confirms current_slot_load gate g3 0x140256590() and save-mgr-update gate are session-scoped: the whole save-IO arming is session-gated.)

=== CONCLUSION (the exact blocker) ===
b80 never engages/advances at our in-context placeholder-world state NOT because of a flag we failed to set, but because the FD4 stream worker task 0x144842d40 is NOT REGISTERED with the scheduler (key 0x59682f01) at our forced FE-host. The world-load MoveMapStep relies on that registration being done elsewhere (IngameInit tail 0x140b0a980 +0x48>=7 arm, or the MoveMapListStep menu helper 0x140af1b40) -- both of which our SetState(5) shortcut SKIPS. The async submit 0x140e6ec70 may even succeed and set b80=1, but the queued 0x280000 read is never drained (no worker tick) so b80 is stuck and the deserialize 0x14067b290 (gated b80==3 + IO-resident) never runs. This is the SAME scheduler-registration ABI wall as frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned-not-callable / movemapstep-direct-pump-crashes-needs-scheduler.

=== RECIPE DIRECTION (verified-safe lever) ===
The cycle-breaker that AVOIDS the worker entirely (per async-io-b80-lane-mismatch lever b + slot9 recipe phase 2): the full deserialize 0x14067b290 does the SYNC read 0x14067b100, but 0x14067b100 is gated b80==3. So the deserialize cannot be called cold. The DATA path that worked before (slot9-deserialize-works) succeeded only AFTER SetState(5) had driven b80 to 3 -- which itself needs the worker. THEREFORE the only genuinely-zero-input complete path is: get the stream worker 0x144842d40 REGISTERED+TICKED so the native b80 machine (already driven by the ticking MoveMapStep dispatchers) completes 1->2->3 and the deserialize+stream run natively. The register call is 0x142656b00(rcx=??descriptor, ecx=0x59682f01) with task 0x144842d40 -- replicate the 0x140af1b40 tail. Open: the 0x142656b00 ABI (descriptor in rdx/r8) is the unreversed piece; decode 0x142656b00 args + 0x140af1b40's descriptor build (rsp+0x30 task ptr, rsp+0x38 name) to call it directly. DELIVERABLE (2026-07-01): scripts/deobf-recover.py -- full-function CFG recovery for Arxan-obfuscated functions, extending the single-path resolver (arxan-unicorn-dispatch-resolver-2026-07-01) to WHOLE functions. Completes the 3-tool progression: deobf-read.py (static, follow direct jmps, one path) -> deobf-emulate.py (Unicorn, resolve gadget dispatch, one path) -> deobf-recover.py (Unicorn, MULTI-PATH, full CFG).

METHOD: generational-search path exploration. Run under Unicorn forcing a schedule of real (game-.text) conditional-branch directions (set RIP to target/fallthrough in the code hook, flags ignored); each real jcc beyond the schedule spawns a child schedule with that branch flipped; BFS work-list, dedup schedules, stop after --paths (default 400) or 60 dry generations. Unicorn resolves the Arxan gadget dispatch (jmp [rsp-8], movabs-computed targets) concretely on EVERY path, so the union of game-.text instructions+edges = the real function CFG. Coalesces into basic blocks (single-succ/single-pred chains), folds obvious stack-gadget lines (lea rsp,[rsp+-8]; jmp qword [rsp]; xchg [rsp]), annotates movabs code-addr loads as dispatch targets, prints blocks with successors + call targets. Calls into game .text recorded+skipped (rax:=0).

PROVEN: 0x1424fb244 (branchy callee) -> PERFECT 4-block CFG: entry(decode encoded ptr; cmp; jne)->{two call-bearing branches}->join(cmove; ret) -- reads like clean compiler output, Arxan gone. 0x140110820 (flattened) -> clean linear 'call;call;ret'. 0x14011de10 (flattened branchy thunk) -> 2 paths, 3 blocks, je explored both ways, ret-gadget dispatch resolved to concrete 0x14011dee0.

HONEST LIMITS: forced branches (flags ignored) can create infeasible states -> some paths DERAIL (recovered up to the derail); heavily-flattened cmove-dispatch not fully collapsed to a clean if/else; recovery is best on moderately-obfuscated fns, partial on the worst. Clean functions recover to decompile-ready CFGs; the hardest need the derail-avoidance/seeded-inputs or symbolic (Triton/Miasm) next step. This is the 'full function execution' step past single-path dispatch resolution. Related: arxan-unicorn-dispatch-resolver-2026-07-01, arxan-cfg-trampoline-resolver-and-reader-2026-07-01.

ARXAN WALL (2026-06-21). The global ToS-accepted gate 0x140e4fda0 is a trampoline jmp 0x145ba1c41, and 0x145ba1c41 is ARXAN VM-PROTECTED / virtualized (stack-ptr juggling lea -0x8(rsp),rsp; mov r10,[rip junk]; jmp *-0x8(rsp) indirect dispatch; movabs scratch; self-modifying) -- the dearxan deobf pass did NOT unwrap it. So the accepted-state the gate reads is NOT statically decodable from disassembly. The ToS wrapper 0x9b6070 invoke during the advance is ALSO Arxan-dispatched (run 244: no static rel32/u64/lea callers). So 'decode the upstream accepted gate and set it zero-input' is blocked on the STATIC path.
NUANCE worth a runtime check: the ToS accept-predicate 0x1409b72b0 returns satisfied if (global gate 0x140e4fda0 OR owner+0x29c0). Run 243 showed owner+0x29c0=1 (accepted), so the predicate (the dialog's vtable+0x170 self-close check) WOULD return satisfied -> the dialog's OWN update may auto-close it NATIVELY (zero-input, NOT a simulated dismiss -- the game closing its own already-accepted dialog). BUT the watcher fires native_legal_popup_detected on the BUILD (total_builds=1), before any self-close, and the project bar (run 244) is 'the ToS must never BUILD', not 'must not be visible'. So build-then-native-auto-close still fails the build bar.
PATHS FORWARD (all runtime/empirical, not static -- Arxan blocks static): (a) hook the ToS predicate 0x1409b72b0 + the wrapper 0x9b6070 and let the run CONTINUE past the build to observe whether the dialog self-closes natively when owner+0x29c0=1 (if it does zero-input, reconsider whether build-then-native-close is acceptable, or relax the watcher to 'modal still present after N ticks' instead of 'built at all'); (b) find the PERSISTENT ToS-accepted storage (wine registry / GraphicsConfig / userdata) and ensure it is set so the native advance never invokes the build (this is what vanilla-with-accepted-ToS relies on); (c) compare the lever-advance state vs a natural(input)-advance state to see what the natural advance sets that makes vanilla-with-save skip the ToS. ZERO-INPUT ADVANCE IS SOLVED (levers 2+3). Related: tos-builds-on-any-title-advance-need-upstream-accepted-gate-2026, tos-multilang-modal-suppression-target.

GATE-B (0x141ee1240) registration mechanism, instruction-level (deobf image, base 0x140000000):

KEY: GATE B walks list [registry+0x28] (next ptr@node+0x00) and passes iff some node has *(u32*)(node+0x10) == key. key = *(u32*)arg2 of the submit frame, copied via helper 0x141ee05f0 (=mov eax,[rdx];mov[rcx],eax;mov rax,rcx;ret -- a 32-bit COPY, NOT a lock). Real lock acquire/release = 0x141ee0600/0x141ee0610; comparator = 0x141ee0620 (sete al). Frame 0x14240ad2f copies key at 0x14240ad73 then enqueues at 0x14240adb4 passing key as r8.

REGISTRY: getter 0x141ee1110 (no args, returns registry in rax) lazily builds singleton at *0x144843038 via initializer 0x141ee1720 -> constructor 0x141ee0bd0 (rcx=obj, vtable 0x1430b3b00, registers teardown 0x141ee1590 via 0x141ecd830). Constructor reads IO device-mgr *0x144843030, calls its vtable[+0x18] to enumerate worker context(s); each worker id -> node+0x10, spliced into [registry+0x28] via insert 0x141ee1650 (0x70-byte node from allocator 0x141eb9ed0 ecx=0x70/edx=8/r8=*0x144843030; constructor 0x141f4ee50 installs node vtable 0x1430c4488, ctx ptr@+0x58, sublock@+0x60, +0x64=0xFFFFFFFF, +0x68=0; marker 0x101@node+0x18). Splice fixups 0x141ee16c0/0x141ee15f0.

ROOT: registry populated ONCE lazily from workers *0x144843030 vends at first-call. Cold menu-free path fails GATE B because the save-read worker node is absent (IO-subsystem worker startup the title/Continue boot runs was skipped). key is request/context-scoped (copied from request descriptor), NOT raw OS thread id.

(2) clean IO-worker-startup fn that populates *0x144843030 (preferred over hand-splicing a node) + whether registry is already-built-empty vs not-yet-built on cold path. Installing our job there via Assign 0x1407a9460 (old_top non-empty, refcount-correct) -> the native FD4 pump (0x7aa2bb) PICKED IT UP and processed it. DECISIVE trajectory (getter, LOAD-2 ONLY offset 56612239): phase 0(cap null)->1->2 status 0->1->4 data STAYS 0. REFINED-GOAL-details-answers-3-2026-07-18: DRIVE SURFACE clarified. The DLL has MODES: (1) supplied a TOML near the exe with a save FILE + SLOT -> if both valid, AUTOMATICALLY loads that slot, user has NO control until the character loads in; (2) if TOML save/slot invalid or absent -> an interactive FILE PICKER appears (user-controlled). We ABSOLUTELY do NOT want to trigger/design-around the file-picker for this goal. SCOPE: we are ONLY interested in loads AFTER the first *automatic load* (the initial TOML-driven auto-load = the 'initial product load' that yields to the user). The proof target = repeated post-initial reloads of arbitrary (file, slot) done fast/read-only/in-memory-redirect landing fully-playable characters. GRANULARITY: per-character-within-file (vary slot) AND cross-file (vary file) both required. SPEED: log timings, do NOT gate pass/fail on speed this round (correctness first; record numbers for review). SEAMLESS: same acceptance criteria as vanilla (repeated loads after initial). Harness contract is therefore: set (file,slot) target -> trigger reload -> product loads -> verify identity+stats+gear + controllable -> repeat across >=3 files. Links [[REFINED-GOAL-details-answers-2-2026-07-18]].

USER-CONFIRMED load2 MOVABLE + framerate root (2026-07-19): user manually got into load2, moved around, everything worked (character movable/controllable) -- VALIDATES the cVar10=0 fix (clear warpRequested during mms13-18 -> fin stays 0 -> can_move ramps f60, exactly load1's movable window). BUT framerate was TRASH. ROOT: my fix holds cVar10=0 FOREVER, so load2's MoveMapStep never completes (stuck mms=18/fin=0, fake_cover=True) -> the game runs perpetual loading-screen/streaming/cover overhead every frame -> FPS tanks. load1 SETTLES (fin 5->9 -> mms=-1, cover drops) after its movable window, so its load overhead stops -> normal FPS. So framerate-trash and never-settles are the SAME root: the finalize must COMPLETE after the movable window. FIX: clear warpRequested only UNTIL can_move is confirmed this epoch (get the movable window), THEN release so the finalize runs like load1 (fin 5->9 -> mms=-1 -> cover drops -> FPS normal). Natural finalize walk (not forced) does NOT teardown -- it only stuck at 7 on saveState=3, so ALSO clear saveState=0 after can_move so 7->8 passes. Framerate is now a goal requirement (stable + comparable to load1 baseline); completing load2 is the framerate fix. can_move ramp confirmed at 99.6s run cvar10 (f0->f60).

Therefore if native-preserving runtime latches 05_000_Title job built but render/Run never fires, the missing edge is likely either `FUN_140b0e620` not installing that job into owner+0x130, owner+0x130 being overwritten before MenuJobWait, or `ExecuteMenuJob` ticking a different occupant. Next runtime-safe diagnostic: passively hook `FUN_140b0e620` and/or `ExecuteMenuJob` to log param_2 job, owner+0x130 before/after, current vtable, and whether it equals the latched BeginTitle 05_000_Title job; do not cold-call or assign manually.

PRODUCT LEVER (branch fix/loading-portrait-swap-early-popout, 2026-07-02): the now-loading background is now permanently replaced with a controllable texture on the overlay-head path. The MENU_Load_* forge (install_loading_bg_replace_bind_hook, hook loading_bg_replace_bind_hook -> forge_into_rti -> build_portrait_tpf) is now installed UNCONDITIONALLY in src/lib.rs (previously only when !portrait_lookat_enabled(), to avoid a second head -- but a transparent texture is not a head, so that restriction was dropped). On the portrait_lookat (present-overlay head) path, build_portrait_tpf returns build_loading_bg_replacement_tpf() = a 64x64 all-zero RGBA (transparent->black) texture. THAT HELPER IS THE SWAPPABLE LEVER: edit its pixels/dims to change the loading background later. Tied to portrait_lookat_enabled() state (no standalone flag, per the no-new-env-gates rule). Note: on this path maybe_reforge_loading_portrait is skipped and refresh_loading_bg_live_gx is hard-disabled (both crash vkd3d), so the transparent bind is a safe static one-shot.

Also: the cross-slot portrait SWAP bug was fixed by replacing the engine's global profile refresh (FUN_1409aa7d0, which kicks EVERY marked slot -> builds all save characters mid-load) with a per-slot replica kick_target_profile_slot() for ONLY the loaded slot; an earlier attempt to mute the global refresh by writing the +0x754/+0x755 latches on unconfigured renderers CRASHED (GX command-queue overflow, reserve_command_queue_slot null-slot write at deobf 0x141aeaf05). RT-pin (PROFILE_RT_PIN) prevents the readback scan from flipping to a foreign slot's same-size 1024 RT.

THE FIX, fully characterized (2026-06-21, readable static RE). In the menu-component lazy-builder (entry ~0x1407ad1c0), when this+0xa8==0 the je at 0x1407ad202 goes to 0x1407ad367 which is NOT a crash -- it FALLS BACK to an alternate build-lambda at this+0xe8: '0x1407ad367 cmpq [rcx+0xe8],0; je 0x1407ad3ec (both null -> graceful skip); else mov rcx,[rcx+0xe8]; mov rax,[rcx]; call [rax+0x10] (_Do_call); store -> this+0x130'. So this+0xa8 = PRIMARY build-lambda (the ToS/no-save surface, set on our lever-advance), this+0xe8 = FALLBACK build-lambda (the normal save-present component). Build precedence: this+0xa8 if non-null, else this+0xe8, else graceful skip.
=> ZERO-INPUT NO-ToS FIX: CLEAR this+0xa8 (set [this+0xa8]=0) BEFORE this lazy-builder runs. The builder then gracefully builds the NORMAL this+0xe8 component into this+0x130 instead of the ToS -- exactly the vanilla-with-save routing. NOT a null-suppress (result is the real fallback dialog, not null -> no caller crash). NOT a dismiss (the ToS is never built). NOT input.
REMAINING to implement: (1) identify 'this' so the DLL can reach this+0xa8 at runtime -- this+0x130 == owner+0x130 (title menu list) so 'this' is the title menu container (the title owner, or its menu holder owner+0xe0/TitleTopDialog -- CONFIRM the exact object whose +0xa8/+0xe8/+0x130 these are: identify the function entry + its this-vtable); (2) clear [this+0xa8] once at the right tick (before the lazy-builder runs during the advance) -- e.g. a new gated own_stepper lever that, after the advance is triggered, finds the title container and zeroes +0xa8; (3) verify-only probe -> expect total_builds=0 (no ToS), menu_opened_latch=1, no process exit, and the normal component built. This is the clean end of the ToS-on-advance problem. Related: tos-build-gate-is-this-a8-nonnull-lazy-component-2026, title-global-accept-byte-144589bdc-zeroinput-advance-2026.

KEEPALIVE per-frame DISPLAY -- ROOT CAUSE PINNED (2026-06-30, probe diag-12, instrumented). The displayed head refreshes only ~4x/loading-screen because the offscreen RT holds a real head only ~4x; the other ~175 frames it is PURE BLACK.

DECISIVE DATA (diag-12): oracle_profile_readback_some=179 (the cached readback SUCCEEDS ~per-frame), oracle_profile_readback_checker=175, version=4. The 175 'checker' frames are BLACK (dumped slot 103 = all-black 1024x1024; portrait_looks_like_checker classifies black as placeholder since black is 'pure' + top-2-colors>=85%). So 179 readbacks = 175 black + 4 head. render_drive_hits=230.

RULED OUT (do not re-investigate): (a) readback resource-creation cost -- cached RB_FAST_* readback made NO difference (still 4). (b) fence timeout -- 0. (c) checker false-positive on the head -- the head center is skin/beard mid-tones, not pure; the 'checkers' are genuinely black. (d) readback returning None -- it returns Some 179x.

ACTUAL CAUSE: the offscreen content RT (PROFILE_LIVE_RT_RES = find_d3d12_resource(off)) is BLACK ~96% of frames. Each frame the RT is cleared to black; drive(r) (PROFILE_OFFSCREEN_DRIVE_RVA, the offscreen rasterize, submitted to the GAME's GX queue) only lands a head ~4x. Our readback runs on OUR OWN queue with NO shared fence to the game's GX queue, so it reads the RT at an unsynchronized point (post-clear, pre/post-rasterize) -> mostly black, head only on lucky timing. This is a cross-queue GPU sync problem, NOT resource creation and NOT a checker.

FIX CANDIDATES (the keystone epic): (1) guarantee the offscreen RASTERIZE lands every frame AND read it after it completes -- needs the GX context flush/fence after drive(r) (find the GX flush-and-wait; the GX pool is FUN_1419e5850). (2) order the display copy ON the game's GX queue (after the rasterize) instead of our own queue -- needs the game's queue handle. (3) try a different draw phase for the realtime tick (er-effects-lookat-phase.txt selects PROFILE_LOOKAT_SELECTED_PHASE; cur=5 AdhocDraw) -- a phase where the GX frame is reliably recording might land the rasterize every frame (CHEAP to test, no rebuild). (4) GPU->GPU copy of the RT->backbuffer in the Present hook does NOT help alone -- the RT is still black ~96% there too unless the rasterize+sync is fixed first.

So 'see the character react until loading completes' (continuous) is BLOCKED on this cross-queue GX rasterize sync. The render IS alive and composites (overlay_draw_hits=180), and the head DOES track on the 4 good frames -- just not continuously. Relates: keepalive-render-alive-display-4x-gxframe-wall-2026-06-30 (refines it), interactive-face-levers-roadmap-2026-06-29 Tier-2.

The offscreen MODEL DRAW (FUN_140bb73a0 inside drive(r)) genuinely only produces head pixels ~4x per loading screen regardless of phase -- the RT is cleared to black each frame and the model rasterize lands ~4x. FPS ROOT = GLOBAL CORE CONTENTION, confirmed by numbers (fpscmp run 2026-07-20): oracle_fps per phase (BOTH loads under the user's parallel-cargo contention): load1 SETTLED(mms=-1, in-world) avg=23.6 min=20.1 MAX=66.2; load1 loading avg=21.1; load1 mms=18 avg=27.8; load2 loading avg=21.4. My earlier 'load2-specific stutter' read was WRONG; the can_move resets for load2 are contention FREEZE FRAMES (load2's window at 123-167s hit more cargo spikes than load1's 76-102s), not a code freeze. The high variance (20->66) = contention spikes. USER'S ORIGINAL parallel-cargo hypothesis is CONFIRMED. Consequences: (1) RenderDoc NOT the tool (CPU contention, not a GPU render-queue halt). (2) Framerate goal (load1-baseline-comparable) is MET at CODE level: load1 ~= load2, the cVar10 fix does not degrade load2 below load1; a clean absolute-60fps baseline needs a run WITHOUT parallel cargo. INTEGRATED-OBJECT VIEW feasibility (2026-06-25). Goal: a viewer tab that traces a shader -> the object(s) that use it, then re-renders the whole object with its real geometry+materials+textures+shaders as in-game. DETERMINATION: fully feasible OFFLINE in pure Rust. Asset chain (all native, no wine needed once decompressed): FLVER geometry, MATBIN materials (shader_path + sampler texture paths + FC_* param values), TPF textures. HARVEST SOURCES: (1) user's own https://github.com/chozandrias76/flveRS (Bevy FLVER viewer) gives FLVER->Bevy mesh conversion (vertex semantics, facesets) + 3D orbit/WASD camera; uses fstools_formats FLVER reader. (2) soulsmods/fstools-rs (MIT/Apache-2.0) crates/formats = pure-Rust parsers for flver, matbin (Matbin::parse(&[u8]) -> shader_path()/samplers()/parameters()), tpf, dcx, bnd4, bhd, param -- Bevy-independent, harvest directly. DECISIVE GATE RE (2026-06-16, objdump on-disk eldenring.exe). There is NO save-deserialize that bypasses GameMan+0xb80==3. 0x14067b290(slot) [the full deserialize]: allocs 0x280000 buf via [0x143d872e0], calls READ 0x14067b100(buf,0x280000); if it returns false -> BAILS at 0x14067b452 (NO c30 write); on success sets [GameMan+0xac0]=slot then c30-writer 0x14067bd70(buf) writes GameMan+0xc30=real map + parses save (0x141edfa20/0x141edfb60). THE READ 0x14067b100 @0x14067b110: 'cmp [GameMan+0xb80],3; jne 0x14067b174(xor al,al; ret)' = HARD-RETURNS FALSE unless b80==3 (no resident-container fallback). So 0x14067b290 cold (b80=0) cannot write c30. CORRECTION to slot9-deserialize-works-world-stream-needs-priming: its c30=0xa010000 'real map' was almost certainly the NEW-GAME DEFAULT (same 0xa010000 Path A produced with no deserialize), a FALSE POSITIVE -- 0x14067b290 would have bailed at b80=0, and the world never loaded to confirm the char. The menu deserialize 0x14082c240 ALSO uses 0x14067b100 (seamless-co2 memo) so it's the same gate.

NO session gate (unlike 0x14067b200 which gates on 0x1402567b0/0x140261cd0). So 0x14067b1a0 is the clean submit. Cold (RUN1 cold-b80 memo) it set b80=2 but io18=0 (queued to file-device-mgr, iodev request not populated) so poll 0x140679180 can't reach b80=3. STEP-3 FIX DIRECTION CORRECTED (2026-07-17, EBL-unmount RE subagent). PREVENT-UNMOUNT is the WRONG fix: (1) the archive is REFCOUNTED -- unmount teardown 0x141f23df0 is Archive::Release (lock xadd dec refcount @archive+0xc, finalize only at 0), so keeping it alive won't crash BUT (2) cap+0x90 (the bound data ptr) is written during the MOUNT/ORCHESTRATOR BIND, NOT looked up per-read from the registry, so a surviving-but-unbound archive still leaves cap+0x90=NULL (empty read persists); and (3) if the warm-reload guard skips the orchestrator BECAUSE it believes the map is still mounted, preventing the unmount makes that belief permanently true -> orchestrator stays skipped forever. Unmount teardown is PER-NAME (RemoveByName 0x141f48480 per archive, keyed by map name; Family-A per-map holder dtors 0x1426a4950/0x14265a060/0x1426a4a10 each unmount their one +0x18 wstring name; NOT a bulk clear). MountEblArchive descriptor is TRANSIENT (built per-load by orchestrator 0x14082faf0 as stack/heap task-chain objects; a captured rdx pointer goes stale) -- so you CANNOT synthesize/replay a descriptor; must let the orchestrator rebuild it. CORRECT FIX = satisfy/bypass the WARM-RELOAD GUARD so the game's own orchestrator (0x14082dbf0->0x14082faf0->...0x79171f->MountEblArchive) re-runs mount+BIND on load2, repopulating cap+0x90. Detour on 0x82dbf0 is UNSAFE (breaks load1 save-prep [[hook-hazard-2-detour-on-0x82dbf0-stalls-load1-use-swbp-2026-07-17]]) -> the fix is a DATA write that flips the guard predicate, not a detour. CENSUS now DISCRIMINATES: m28 ABSENT in registry = mount step skipped; m28 PRESENT but cap+0x90 null = bind skipped -- tells us WHERE the guard is. NEXT RUNTIME (subagent's asks): bp RemoveByName 0x141f48480 on load1 quit -> exact m28 name + teardown caller VA; bp inside 0x14082faf0 load1-vs-load2 -> the skip predicate (the guard to flip). Links [[step3-run7-8-likely-root-skipped-map-load-orchestrator-0x82dbf0-warm-reload-2026-07-17]] [[step3-driver-refinement-remount-via-mounteblarchive-not-orchestrator-2026-07-17]].

The full main-menu MenuWindowJob list (Continue/LoadGame=d180/NewGame/Settings/Quit) is built by the MAIN-MENU LIST BUILDER 0x14081f180, called EXACTLY ONCE from STEP_BeginLogo (idx2) at 0x140b0c43e. Signature: 0x14081f180(rcx=&out_sequence_root_local, rdx=owner+0xe0 holder). It builds 5 MenuWindowJob items in a tight loop via the per-item add helper 0x140820500 (calls at 0x14081f219/f264/f2b0/f301/f34d), each item: config-desc via 0x140751700, per-item predicate via 0x140765030, alloc 0x138-byte MenuWindowJob via item-builder 0x1407acb00 (writes vtable 0x142aa97e8 at [item]+0; stores rdx-ctx at [item+0x50]; functor descriptor xmm0 from [r8] into [item+0x58]), then 0x1407a7e30/0x1407a7bb0 link items into a sequence. d180 = the LoadGame item; its functor _Do_call 0x140820c60 = 'add rcx,8; jmp 0x14081ead0' (dialog_factory; VERIFIED bytes 48 83 c1 08 / e9 .. = jmp 0x14081ead0). dialog_factory 0x14081ead0 has only ONE caller (0x140820c64 jmp) so functor_chain_hits_factory==0x14081ead0 uniquely IDs d180.

=== WHY REGISTRAR/DIALOG-PUMP NEVER WORKS (confirms prior memories) ===
STEP_BeginTitle (idx3) 0x140b0c5b0 builds ONLY the 3 TITLE-COMPOSITION items via the menu-wirer 0x14081f9f0 (its SOLE caller is 0x140b0c70b inside BeginTitle): c000->0x140763fc0 (op-new 0xaa0 = CS::BackScreen), c140->CaptionView, c280->TitleTopDialog-builder 0x14081e5e0. BeginTitle does NOT call 0x14081f180. CRUCIAL: STEP_BeginLogo 0x140b0c2a0 at 0x140b0c356 branches on flag [owner+0xb8]: if SET it just SetState(3) at 0x140b0c3a8 (edx=3, call SetState 0x140b0d960) and RETURNS WITHOUT building the list -> this is EXACTLY why 'SetState(3) alone only built BackScreen / only c000 ticked' (milestone obs). The main-menu list is built only in BeginLogo's ELSE branch (0x140b0c3b2+): lea rdx,[rdi+0xe0]; lea rcx,[rbp+0x77]; call 0x14081f180; then commit via 0x140b0e530 (rcx=owner) which stores the assembled functor at owner+0x130 and SetState(owner,10). So registrar 0x1409b24e0 (only touches [dialog+0xa48] spec nodes) and the TitleTopDialog dialog pump 0x1409aac10 (only pumps [dialog+0x1f8] widgets) are STRUCTURALLY DISJOINT from owner+0xe0; they can NEVER make d180 tick.

=== THE TICK PATH (reconciles gold capture) ===
owner+0xe0 is a small-vector MULTICAST of jobs (count at [holder+0x48]); its pump 0x140733f20 iterates and calls each job->vt[+0x10](frametime in xmm1). One registered job leads to the FD4 menu-task pump 0x140791620 which reads pre-step functor [h+0x58] (call vt[0]) then ROOT [h+0x18] and calls root->vt[+0x10] (=Sequence iterator 0x1407aa1f0 if root is a Sequence: children [seq+0x18+i*8], count [seq+0x60], cursor [seq+0x10]) recursing to MenuWindowJob leaf Update 0x1407ad1c0. Gold stack 0xb0d526(MenuJobWait)->...->0x7a968e->0x7aa2bb(iterator)->0x745543->0x74684f->d180.functor@0x1407ad2bc matches: d180 ticks in THIS tree only after BeginLogo(2) populated it.

=== ZERO-INPUT VERDICT (Q3/Q4) ===
There is NO separate registrar/dialog-method that converts dialog state into the iterator tree. The ONLY function that builds the main-menu items into owner+0xe0 is 0x14081f180, and it is reached ONLY via STEP_BeginLogo(2). 0x14081f180 is technically directly callable (rcx=&local_out_root, rdx=live_owner+0xe0) BUT it returns a stack-local sequence that must then be committed via 0x140b0e530(rcx=owner) which also SetState(10); replicating BeginLogo's exact ctx (the rbp+0x77/0x67/0x7f closure descriptors, the [owner+0xb8] gate, session singleton 0x144588e98 assert at 0x140b0c2c3) by hand is fragile. CLEANEST zero-input path = let the native engine run it: from idx10 handler call SetState(owner,2=BeginLogo) via 0x140b0d960(rcx=owner,edx=2) PRECONDITION session singleton 0x144588e98 != null AND [owner+0xb8]==0 (else BeginLogo short-circuits to SetState(3) and skips the list). Native pump then runs 2(BeginLogo builds full list into owner+0xe0)->3(BeginTitle)->10, after which d180.Update 0x1407ad1c0 ticks via 0x140791620->0x1407aa1f0. THEN hand-invoke d180 functor (mirror 0x1407ad2b9: rcx=[item+0xa8], rax=[rcx], rdx=item+0x10, call [rax+0x10]; precondition [item+0x130]==0 && [item+0x10]==0). SINGLE FUNCTION our handler calls zero-input to make d180 live: SetState(owner,2) [0x140b0d960, rcx=live SimpleTitleStep owner (vtable 0x142b63bb0, [+0x48]==10), edx=2]; the engine's own STEP_BeginLogo->0x14081f180 does the build. Do NOT call 0x14081f180 directly unless reconstructing BeginLogo's closures+commit exactly. DECISIVE decode of 0x140c8fe90 (the SOLE latch writer, 2026-06-15, full disasm, the LAST untraced node). IDENTITY: it is NOT the press-any-button handler and NOT a menu/CSFeMan bootstrap. It is the BODY of a dedicated BOOT/INTRO worker thread -- its only caller is 0x140c90229 inside thread-trampoline 0x140c90153 (found via E8 rel32 scan; recon_refs.py reported 0 callers because it only scans rip-relative DATA refs, NOT E8/E9 relative calls -- new tools .auto/recon_callscan.py + .auto/recon_ptr_scan.py fix this gap). The trampoline does CoInitializeEx(,2) at 0x140c90215/223, calls the body, then CoUninitialize at 0x140c90230 -- classic FD4 worker thread. BODY WALK: (1) 0x140deb0b0 boot-init on FD4Singleton 0x144588de8 (gate 0x143d85738); (2) wait loop 0x140c8feb0-ef6 spins calling OS msg-pump 0x140e8ed70 while movie/intro tick 0x140dea370 returns true (0x140dea370 calls boot-singleton 0x14458b890 vtable+0x10 = movie is-playing/advance, increments frame ctr 0x143d8567c, computes fade alpha [sing+0x2cc]*[+0x268], calls fade 0x140ddfbf0); (3) play sound 0x80e8 via 0x141f0aa90; (4) 0x140c8ff41 mov byte[0x143d856a0],1 = THE LATCH, set UNCONDITIONALLY once the intro/movie wait is satisfied; (5) post-latch: 0xc8=200-iter fade loop (sound 0x80e8/0x21, frame-wait 0x140de9f50, 0x140dea280); (6) 0x64=100-iter fade-out loop (0x140ddfbf0 + sound 0x21); (7) teardown 0x140dea880 destroys boot singletons 0x143d855c8/0x143d67348/0x144589b58 via vtable+0x68. INPUT-GATE VERDICT: 0x140c8fe90 and ALL its direct callees reference inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 / input-session 0x143d83148 / CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 / session 0x1447ef360 ZERO times (grepped). So this node has NO internal input check and builds NO menu/session -- it is a pure intro/movie/fade timer that latches when the intro finishes. The press-any-button INPUT WAIT is NOT here; it is in MenuJobWait 0xb0d400 (per title-accept-lever). The boot thread sets the latch at BOOT, not at the title. Sole byte-imm writer of 0x143d856a0 confirmed = 0x140c8ff41 only (21 refs, 20 are cmp readers). ZERO-INPUT VERDICT: (a) calling/hooking 0x140c8fe90 gives NOTHING new -- it has no handoff to replicate (no CSFeMan/session construction); its only durable side-effect is the latch which we can already write directly. (b) The save-mgr autoload arm 0x14067f5d0@0x14067fa71 (if [mgr 0x143d69918 +0xac0]!=-1 AND [0x143d856a0]!=0 then [mgr+0xb72]=1) is itself GATED on input-idle: preceding it at 0x14067fa58 call 0x140765780(inputmgr) [true iff keystate bits at inputmgr+0x90 NOT pressed] AND 0x14067fa61 call 0x14080d570 [true iff inputmgr+0x80 sub-obj +0x290/+0x298 ==0]. These pass when input is IDLE (no press needed), so RECIPE A's slot+latch WOULD be consumed without a button -- but per [[outer-simpletitlestep-absent-at-title-wall]] the load then ORPHANS/crashes because the outer SimpleTitleStep/menu system that routes the load does not exist at the title. CONCLUSION: 0x140c8fe90 is decoded and is a DEAD END for new mechanism -- it is the intro-finish latcher, not a callable handoff. The wall is the absent menu-system construction (scheduler-owned, [[frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned-not-callable]]), not this node. Related: [[title-accept-lever-143d856a0]] [[native-autoload-slot-ac0-plus-force-flag]] [[outer-simpletitlestep-absent-at-title-wall]] [[frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned-not-callable]].

y22i crash 0xec95d1 (Scaleform CBV_SRV_UAV descriptor-heap null provider). STATIC RE (2 traces+reconcile, high conf): GAME-SIDE / DRIVER-INDEPENDENT. Fault fn deobf 0x140ec9530 advance(this,count) faults @0x140ec95d1 when provider=*(this+0x38) is null. Discriminator: crash needs capacity(this+0x20)==0 AND provider==null; capacity is set by GAME code before any D3D12 CreateDescriptorHeap call, so a driver-level create failure would leave capacity nonzero + take the fast path -- cannot cause this fault. TWO ROOT CAUSES FOUND+FIXED (both in src/experiments/startup_hooks.rs profile_lookat_realtime_draw_tick):
1. SELF-CLEAR: drive(r)=PROFILE_OFFSCREEN_DRIVE_RVA (FUN_140bb8d90->FUN_140bb73a0) is ONLY a ClearRTV (decompile-proven), NOT a re-rasterize as the original author believed. It ran every frame (render_drives~206), WIPING the offscreen RT to black on the ~200 frames between the engine's genuine paints. FIX: disabled the drive(r) clear (kept the counter for parity). 2. STALE CACHED READBACK: the DISPLAY path used readback_cached_content_rgba8 which cached PROFILE_LIVE_RT_RES (the resource) ONCE -> went stale (read black ~98%) because the offscreen RT is recreated (1024 resize) so the cached handle dangled. IN-RUN A/B PROOF: fresh publish readback[some=44 checker=12] (73% nonblack) vs cached deferred defer[some=48 nonblack=5] (~10%). Decisive: the cache was the bug.

Added per-frame model-draw drive (update+draw tasks, modeldraws=46) but it alone did NOT increase RT paints (the GX scene paint, not the tasks, does the rasterize).

FEEDBACK from user (chozandrias76, 2026-06-15) -- when faced with an equal/binary decision between approaches, the user's default preference is to try BOTH in parallel via background subagents, RATHER than picking one. Exception, only when the two options would NOT conflict with each other (shared mutable resources). How to apply -- for independent static RE questions, two code approaches in separate worktrees, two analyses, etc., fan out parallel background subagents (single message, multiple Agent calls, run_in_background=true) and synthesize. Do NOT parallelize options that contend on the same exclusive resource. Concrete example from this project -- two RUNTIME Elden Ring levers CANNOT run in parallel (single game install, single save set, single GAME_DIR IPC, single Steam instance, save backup/restore collision) so those must be serial, but their read-only static prep CAN be fanned out. Why -- the user values breadth/throughput and getting both answers over a possibly-wrong single pick. Related: [[dont-pause-when-authorized]].

DEEPER ISSUE found 2026-07-01 after approach-B partial success: the System->Quit->Load-Profile return-title transition crashes NON-DETERMINISTICALLY across runs (different crash each time), which means a timing/race bug, not one deterministic fault. Crash modes seen: (A) execute-fault to a garbage/ASCII RIP at the return-title handoff BEFORE ProfileSelect closes -- e.g. RIP=0x7463656a62 ('...bject'), rcx=0x7fffc9948658 whose 'vtable' 0x142c92c38 is actually SCALEFORM STRING DATA (decoded qwords: 'relatedObject','shiftKey','keyCode','Transform...' = GFx/ActionScript event property names) => a corrupted/type-confused Scaleform object had its vtable field overwritten with a string ptr and a virtual call jumped into the string. (B) later crash in the autoload's CSGaitemImp::Deserialize (0x67141a) when it gets past close+return-title-submit. ROOT INSIGHT (matches AGENTS.md 'recurring private pump is a red flag'): we drive the native ProfileSelect close (FUN_1407ac980) AND the return-title chain submit from OUR recurring game-task tick (system_quit_profile_select_top_menu_tick / product-core tick), CONCURRENTLY with the game's own menu/Scaleform pump. That concurrent poke corrupts Scaleform/menu state -> the non-deterministic crashes. WHAT WORKS: robust in-world load-job block; gaitem guards neutered (no more gaitemInsTable corruption); sometimes ProfileSelect closes + return-title chain submits. CORRECT NEXT ARCHITECTURE (per AGENTS.md 'reverse the native ownership path'): do NOT drive close/return-title/autoload from our concurrent tick. Instead trigger the return-title from WITHIN the native OK/confirm transition (menu-pump ownership) so it runs in the game's own menu frame, not racing it. i.e. hook the native confirm and enqueue/submit the return-title job in that native callback, and let the native menu pump own it. The current approach is the wrong integration boundary. Related: [[system-quit-load-profile-trigger-RESOLVED]] [[system-quit-profileselect-native-close-B-path]] [[menu-job-queue-pump-dequeue-mechanism]].

LOADING-SCREEN PORTRAIT -- ON-SCREEN PROGRESS (2026-06-30, branch spike/boot-parallelization). Goal: real character portrait on the now-loading screen. HUGE progress; one clean step remains.

PROVEN:
1. SURFACE BINDING WORKS. Force-checker isolation (er-effects-portrait-force-checker.txt -> forge binds the yellow/magenta checker, no live/baked swap) shows the checker FILLING THE ENTIRE now-loading tip-screen background. So the forge (loading_bg_replace_bind_hook on LOADING_BG_REPLACE_BIND_RVA=0xd697d0, building a TPF via build_portrait_tpf -> CreateTpfResCap) owns the visible surface. The screen background is pure black (0,0,0) when our texture is empty -- NOT the offscreen's 0x141414 clear -- confirming the displayed texture is the forge's name-registered one.
2. THE REAL PORTRAIT IS RENDERED BY THE MENU. profile-slot-dump shows all 10 profile slots rendered at 1024x1024 with IBL LIGHTING RESIDENT (renderer+0x760 env_obj != 0, ibl_region != 0) at ~16s DURING THE MENU (before our post-Continue rebuild). These are the real, well-lit character portraits (the post-Continue rebuild renders a DARK/NULL head -- no FaceData/IBL -- so DO NOT use it; use the menu render).
3. CAPTURE + BAKE WIRED: with portrait_real_pixels enabled, the slot-dump stores the TARGET slot's (OWN_STEPPER_SLOT) real IBL portrait into LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_RGBA (one-shot, gated on ibl_region!=0). build_portrait_tpf bakes that RGBA into the static TPF (PROVEN display path -- same as the checker).

THE BLOCKER (timing): the now-loading sprite commits to the FIRST forge bind (~15.5s), which fires BEFORE the menu IBL portrait is captured (~16.1s) -> the first forge bakes the CHECKER. The second forge bind (~17.5s) bakes the portrait but the sprite ignores it (committed to first). Post-hoc fixes FAIL: (a) re-forging the first rti with a new portrait container -- the Scaleform pump registers the texture by NAME only on first bind, ignores a swapped cap; (b) D3D12 pixel-upload (upload_rgba_to_texture) into the container GX AND into GetResCap(GLOBAL_TexRepository,name).gxTexture (TexResCap+0x78; GetResCap deobf 0x140b809a0, TEX_REPOSITORY_GET_RES_CAP_RVA=0xb809a0) -- both ok=true, no crash, but the screen stays checker (find_d3d12_resource picks among same-size textures non-deterministically, and/or Scaleform holds a separate decoded copy).

THE CLEAN FIX (next): DELAY the native Continue until PROFILE_BAKE_RGBA_CAPTURED is set, so the FIRST now-loading forge bakes the already-captured portrait via the proven TPF-bytes path. A PORTRAIT_HOLD already exists (PORTRAIT_HOLD_WAIT_TICKS / PORTRAIT_HOLD_MAX_TICKS=240, in maybe_fire_tfc_continue, gated on LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_GX_KEPT) but it is NOT on the native-continue path (this smoke uses PAB-advance + natural menu Continue, not fire_tfc_continue). Wire a hold into the native-continue trigger that waits on PROFILE_BAKE_RGBA_CAPTURED (cap ~240 ticks) before letting Continue fire. The menu portrait IBL-renders ~16.1s; Continue currently fires ~15.5s -- a ~0.6-1s hold is enough. Then build_portrait_tpf bakes the portrait into the first forge -> displayed. All infra committed (compiles, crash-free, check.sh passes). Flags staged: er-effects-portrait-real-pixels.txt (needed). Builds on postcontinue-portrait-OWN-RENDERER-WORKS + offscreen-render-task-RE + RT-SRV-resolve-gap.

STEP-3 WORLD RES WAIT ROOT CAUSE -- DEFINITIVE, decompile-verified (Ghidra 1.16.1, 2026-07-17). CORRECTS the b7c4/b798 field-reset hypothesis (WRONG). ROOT: our fast in-process System->Quit switch NEVER REBUILDS the world-res per-area block-res lists for the LEGACY destination map, so the incoming legacy block's load-state entry is never CREATED and the getter returns NULL forever -> STEP_WorldResWait (child step 3, 0x140af9de0) stalls. CHAIN: step3 advances 3->4 only when FUN_140624cb0 -> FUN_14066d4d0(worldInfoOwner,&curBlockId)==1 (check). Check+issuer FUN_14066d8d0 iterate the WorldAreaRes-ptr list worldres+0xb3030 (count +0xb3140), match areaId at *entry[1]+0xc, call block load-state getter via vtable slot +0x10, require loadstate +0x2d!=0 && +0x35==0x0a(10). Getter FUN_14062f550 (0x14062f550) = STATELESS search over the per-area WorldBlockRes array at WorldAreaRes+0xce0 (stride 0xb98, count +0xcd8) matching worldBlockInfo->mapId2 (*entry+0x34)==requested BlockId; NULL if no entry has mapId2==0x1c000000. So area 0x1c's WorldAreaRes IS in +0xb3030 (oracle blk_found=1) but its +0xce0 block-res array has NO 0x1c000000 entry -> getter null (oracle blk_ls=0x0). Those +0xce0 entries are built ONLY by CS::WorldInfoOwner::PopulateLists 0x14066aee0 (-> ResetAreaResLists 0x14066e9b0 + PopulateBlocksLists/PopulateGridLists) reached via Initialize 0x14066b030 driven by ProcessMsbLoadLists 0x14066b2c0 (parses the DEST map MSB loadlist into the BlockId set). The MoveMap step that runs this: CS::InGameStep::STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec210 -> _Common_Initialize 0x140aed910: if(IsNonDebugArea(areaId) && loadlistlistFileCap!=0) ProcessMsbLoadLists(&worldInfoOwner, loadlistlistFileCap, loadlistlistFileCap_dlc02); then FUN_14066da30 0x14066da30 pushes dest BlockId into b798. Grid-vs-legacy: areaId-0x32<0x27 (0x32..0x58=overworld grid; <0x32=legacy; 0x1c legacy, 0x3c overworld). NORMAL legacy load order: (1) _Common_Initialize->ProcessMsbLoadLists->Initialize->PopulateLists CREATES 0x1c block-res entry mapId2=0x1c000000; (2) FUN_14066da30 sets b798=target; (3) STEP_WorldResWait polls to LOADED. Our fast switch reuses boot m60 overworld lists + SKIPS (1) -> permanent null. FIX DIRECTION (verify at runtime first): on first overworld(area>=0x32)->legacy(area<0x32) switch, ensure InGameStep loadlistlistFileCap is the LEGACY dest map loadlist then re-run ProcessMsbLoadLists(&worldInfoOwner,fcap,fcap_dlc02) 0x14066b2c0 (runs ResetAreaResLists+PopulateLists -> creates entry) then FUN_14066da30(&worldInfoOwner,&destBlockId); or re-invoke _Common_Initialize/STEP_MoveMap_Init for that switch. TENSION TO RESOLVE AT RUNTIME: SWITCH-ORACLE shows ll_fcap NON-null + ll_size=35 -- so either STEP_MoveMap_Init ran with the STALE OVERWORLD loadlist (rebuilt m60 not m28) OR it was skipped for the legacy dest; instrument whether STEP_MoveMap_Init/_Common_Initialize runs on our switch and WHICH map's loadlistlistFileCap it uses before implementing. Why 'once any switch works then legacy works': first switch straight from native-booted overworld lacks the legacy block-res entry; any prior real map-move populates it. Supersedes step3-handoff-getter-re-blocked + the b7c4/b798 hypothesis.

STATIC RE FINDING (2026-06-22, scripts/find-xrefs.py over eldenring-deobf.bin, file_off==RVA base 0x140000000) -- de-risks own-load-step3-hook-67b100-feed-buffer item (1). 0x67b100 (the FSM-gated save READ helper) has exactly 4 DIRECT rel32 callers, ALL `call` (clean entry-detour hookable), NONE are autosave/write:
  - 0x14067b2d9  -> inside OUR parser 0x67b290 (+0x49). The call we WANT to feed.
  - 0x14082a81a  -> native menu save-load region.
  - 0x14082abbd  -> native menu save-load region.
  - 0x14082c2f1  -> inside menu_deser 0x14082c240 (+0xb1), native menu load.
Confirmed pattern at the menu_deser site (0x14082c2d0): mov (rcx),eax; mov edx,0x280000; call [rax+0x50] (alloc buf via vt[0x50]); test rax; je fail; mov edx,0x280000; mov rcx,rax; call 0x67b100 -- IDENTICAL to 0x67b290's body (same vt[0x50] allocator, same 0x280000 size in edx). So 0x67b100 is a SHARED read helper between our parser AND the native menu loader; size is passed by the caller in EDX (our hook reads edx for the copy length -- data-driven, no literal needed).
CONCLUSION: the step-3 hook on 0x67b100 MUST be GATED to fire only during our own 0x67b290 call (set a one-shot flag immediately before calling 0x67b290, clear in/after the hook), so it never intercepts the native menu path's 3 reads -- required to coexist with Seamless/other mods that may use the native menu. Caveat: rel32 scan catches only direct E8/E9; indirect (FF/2 via pointer) or vtable refs not covered, but this leaf read helper is called directly at all 4 sites. Tool: scripts/find-xrefs.py <target_va_hex>. RETROSPECTIVE / TOOLING RULE (2026-06-21, user-directed). A watcher edit (telemetry.get on a None telemetry) crashed the gated ER probe at runtime and BURNED A FULL LAUNCH (game booted + closed in seconds) -- pure waste of an expensive, save-safety-sensitive runtime launch + the user's time. ROOT CAUSE was a TOOLING GAP, not carelessness: there was no OFFLINE validation of the probe harness (watcher + probe scripts) before launch, so a Python None-deref / syntax error only surfaced after spending a launch. RULE: NEVER spend an ER runtime launch to discover a HARNESS bug. Before any runtime probe, run an offline preflight that (1) py_compiles every probe script (er-readiness-watch.py, run-product-continue-direct-probe.sh helpers), and (2) exercises the watcher's per-poll decision logic against synthetic telemetry fixtures INCLUDING the early states a real run produces: telemetry=None (file not yet written), {} (empty), and each terminal/oracle case -- asserting no exception + the expected exit reason. The established codebase pattern is telemetry_*_detected(telemetry) HELPER functions that centralize None-handling (that is why the existing native_legal/messagebox checks did NOT crash and the inline one did); new watcher exit conditions MUST follow that helper pattern, not inline telemetry.get(). Implemented this session: scripts/preflight-runtime-watcher.py (offline watcher self-test) wired into the probe launcher preflight so it FAILS CLOSED before launching ER if the harness is broken. GENERAL PRINCIPLE: when a runtime/launch step can be invalidated by an offline-checkable harness bug, build the offline check FIRST and gate the launch on it -- a launch is only spent when the harness is proven good. Relates: b80probe-blocked..., menufree-passive-boot-zero-tos-confirmed-plus-harness-lessons-2026-06-21.

STRUCTURAL BREAKTHROUGH (2026-06-17, runtime, explains years of d180-walk failures). The main-menu Continue/Load-Game/NewGame items are NOT FD4 MenuWindowJobs in the Sequence tree -- they are WIDGETS inside the TitleTopDialog's OWN menu system. PROOF: with the menu fully OPEN (self-fire, textfadeout=true latch=1, user could see+click Continue/Load), the MenuWindowJob Update hook 0x1407ad1c0 ticks ONLY THREE items forever: c000 (0x7ffe9e52c000, docall 0x140764b80->0x140763fc0 = BackScreen), c140 (docall 0x140820b30->0x14081e5e0 = TitleTopDialog builder), c280 (docall 0x140764b70->0x140763f10 = CaptionView). These are the 3 TITLE-COMPOSITION widgets. The Continue/Load items NEVER tick through 0x1407ad1c0, and the FD4 job-tree walk (owner+0x130) only finds the title-flow state machine (PressAnyButton SetState2 0x140b0e0e0 + Continue confirm 0x140b0e180 behind an IfElseJob) -- NOT the visual menu items. So d180 (the Load-Game item whose functor->dialog_factory 0x14081ead0) is NOT in the FD4 Sequence/MenuWindowJob tree at all. It lives in the TitleTopDialog (owner+0xe0, vt 0x142b26468 RVA 0x2b26468, update 0x1409aac10), pumped by that dialog's OWN element pump 0x140745570 over its widget vector [dialog+0x1f8]..[dialog+0x200] (stride ~0x140) and/or its [dialog+0xa48] registry -- a DIFFERENT subsystem from FD4 jobs. THIS is why every prior walk + the 0x1407ad1c0/0x1407aa1f0 hooks missed it. NEXT: decode the TitleTopDialog menu system -- where the Continue/Load selectable entries are stored, the cursor/selection field, and how a Load-Game pick routes to dialog_factory 0x14081ead0 (build ProfileLoadDialog) -> load_activate 0x1409a4670. Then headless = set the dialog's selection to Load-Game + invoke its confirm/action via a direct CALL (no input). Runtime routing seen: user Load-Game click -> ProfileLoadDialog (vt 0x142b229f8) at owner+0xe0 -> load_activate2 -> builder 0x140826510(slot) -> selector 0x140826d50 -> menu_deser 0x14082c240 mount -> continue_confirm 0x140b0e180 -> SetState5. INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION + corrections (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, READ-ONLY disasm) of the own-the-stepper control mechanism. Confirms step-table-control-mechanism-writable-data-2026 / step-dispatch-decode-and-patch-point-2026 / own-stepper-statemachine-driver-and-streaming-verdict-2026 and corrects two points.

=== Q1 CONTROL MECHANISM (VERIFIED by disasm) ===
DISPATCHER 0x140b0bd60 (FD4StepTemplate::Execute) decoded: rcx=owner(rbx), rdx=frame-ctx(rsi). Per-frame: movsxd rax,[rbx+0x4c]; mov [rbx+0x48],eax (commit requested->committed); then movsxd r8,[rbx+0x48]; add r8,r8; mov rax,[rbx+0x10] (TABLE BASE from owner+0x10); call [rax+r8*8] (rcx=owner, rdx=framectx). So step entries are (func@+0, name@+8), stride 0x10, base at owner+0x10. Handler signature == fromsoftware-rs StepperFn: extern C fn(rcx=&mut Subject, rdx=&FD4Time). Loop drains up to 0x80 transitions/frame (cmp edi,0x80).
TABLE BUILDERS decoded (on-disk table bytes are RUNTIME-built garbage; decode the builder, not the image):
 SimpleTitleStep builder 0x1400a4f50 -> table 0x143d71580: idx0 InitMenu 0x140b0cfa0; 1 0x140b0c8d0; 2 0x140b0c2a0; 3 BeginTitle 0x140b0c5b0; 4 0x140b0c540; 5 PlayGame 0x140b0d5b0; 6 GameStepWait 0x140b0cde0; 7 EndFlow 0x140b0cc00; 8 0x140b0ccc0; 9 0x140b0d550; 10 MenuJobWait 0x140b0d400; 11 Finish 0x140b0cd70.
 InGameStep builder 0x1400a3710 -> table 0x143d70130: idx0 0x140aec010; 1 0x140aebfd0; 2 0x140aecc10; 3 0x140aebf90; 4 0x140aec570; 5 0x140aec660; 6 MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120; ...
SECTION FLAGS (recon_section.py): tables 0x143d71580 / 0x143d70130 / 0x143d70860 ALL in WRITABLE .data (chars=0xc0000040). Dispatcher 0x140b0bd60 = .text READ-ONLY (0x60000020). Step vtables (0x142b63bb0 etc) = .rdata READ-ONLY. Quit-latch 0x143d856a0 = WRITABLE .data.
PATCH POINT: FE-host sits committed state 10 at press-any-button, so idx10 func slot = base+10*0x10 = 0x143d71580+0xa0 = 0x143d71620 is dispatched EVERY frame. Store &our_handler there with a plain qword write (writable .data, no VirtualProtect needed; save+restore original 0x140b0d400). Scheduler then calls our_handler(rcx=live SimpleTitleStep, rdx=FD4Time) in proper step context. This is the cleanest control point (vs hooking .text dispatcher or patching .rdata vtable).

=== Q3 MINIMAL DRIVER -- CORRECTION: call the PlayGame confirm, do NOT raw-SetState(5) ===
PlayGame confirm 0x140b0e180 DECODED (the native Continue action; rcx=item, owner=[item+0x8]): cmp [owner+0x284],1 -> if ==1 edx=4 (NewGame/EULA path) else: call 0x140678a50 (slot info) + 0x140679560(rsp+0x30) (most-recent slot) -> [owner+0xbc]=[rax] (slot); edx=5; call SetState 0x140b0d960(owner,edx); then call 0x14082cd60(owner+0x138, sel) [UI write]; then call 0x140ca7660(owner+0x300, playmode_byte) [InGameStep CHILD REQUEST write]. So a raw SetState(owner,5) MISSES the child-request write (0x140ca7660) and UI write -- that omission is why prior external forcing left the child orphaned. CLEANEST deterministic driver from our idx10 handler: ONE-SHOT call 0x140b0e180 on the live PlayGame item (it does slot-select + SetState5 + child request coherently), then restore idx10 and return. At the title +0x284==0 so it takes the Load(edx=5) branch automatically. SetState 0x140b0d960 verified = mov [rcx+0x4c],edx only (writes requested_state). Child writer 0x140ca7660: vtable[+0x20] submit calls, sets child+0xd8=0/+0xda=0/+0xdb=playmode.
After the confirm, the NATIVE pump (our handler returns; scheduler keeps ticking the SimpleTitleStep + its InGameStep child + the MoveMapStep it builds) runs PlayGame(5) 0x140b0d5b0 -> child idx6 MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 -> CSFeMan + MoveMapStep -> b80 mount + WorldResWait. We pump NOTHING ourselves (avoids the orphan-stall).

=== Q4 STREAMING -- CORRECTION: 0x143d7c088 is a lazy singleton with ONE writer, not 33-reads-only ===
recon_refs 0x143d7c088 = 33 refs; 32 are loads BUT the last (0x140cd7623, inside getter/installer 0x140cd6c50) is a movq_STORE. So 0x143d7c088 self-constructs on first use (sole writer = its getter 0x140cd6c50), like the other IO singletons (async-io-b80-lane-mismatch...). It is NOT gated on a one-time activation event -> driving the load in-context (which calls the getter) builds it. This REMOVES the agent's worry that it 'never builds at a forced title'. VERDICT (Q4): driving via the idx10 handler -> 0x140b0e180 runs the genuine native selection->map resolution (real map into child, not placeholder) AND the native PlayGame->InGameStep->MoveMap activation in native order + the FD4 scheduler keeps ticking the resulting MoveMapStep every frame (proven it ticks CSTaskGroup-less per fd4-scheduler-no-group-active-gate-runs-all-2026). That supplies exactly what external CSTask trampolining lacked: in-context FD4Time/frame-ctx, the per-frame child-step pump, and native ordering. The ONLY residual is a runtime confirm that the world goes resident (static RE cannot prove the exact frame the stream worker 0x144842d40 drains), but every static precondition for it is satisfied by letting the native activation run in-context rather than poking flags cold.

VERIFIED STAGE-2 in-context character-LOAD drive (2026-06-17, objdump on decrypted on-disk eldenring.exe ER 1.16.1 base 0x140000000; .text VA = foff + 0x140000a00). This is the menu-drive that actually COMPLETES a load (Path B), implemented as own_stepper_stage2 in src/experiments.rs. After the opened main menu identifies the Load-Game leaf d180 (MENU_LOAD_GAME_ITEM via the leaf/iterator hooks), four phases drive the native menu load:

PHASE INVOKE: hand-fire d180 +0xa8 action functor (mirror native leaf Update 0x1407ad2b9: rcx=[item+0xa8], rax=[rcx], rdx=item+0x10, call [rax+0x10] = _Do_call add rcx,8 jmp dialog_factory 0x14081ead0 -> op-new 0x1cd0 -> ProfileLoadDialog ctor 0x1409a3d90 writes vtable 0x142b229f8). Precondition [item+0xa8]!=0 AND [item+0x10]==0. The built dialog comes back as the functor RETURN (rax) and/or [item+0x130]; VALIDATE by vtable==0x142b229f8. The menu cursor sits on Continue not Load, so the native pump does NOT fire d180 itself -> hand-invoke is required.

PHASE ACTIVATE: SEED CORRECTION 1 -- load_activate 0x1409a4670 (dialog vtable SLOT 20 = [dialog+0xa0]) does NOT take the slot as an arg; it READS the highlighted list index from the cursor [dialog+0xb0c] (= [dialog+0xa38+0xd4], getter 0x140739e20 = mov eax,[rcx+0xd4]) and clamps to [0,[dialog+0xb08]). So WRITE [dialog+0xb0c]=N (bounds-check N < [dialog+0xb08]) BEFORE calling load_activate(rcx=dialog). SEED CORRECTION 2 -- builder 0x140826510 runs INSIDE load_activate (constructs the descriptor + slot from the cursor, calls factory 0x140821bf0); never call the builder directly. load_activate registers the selector step 0x140826d50 as a child of the live menu task. Gate: PlayerGameData [0x144588268] non-null (load_activate FD4-asserts it).

PHASE MOUNT_POLL: do NOT call the selector/menu_deser yourself (menu_deser 0x14082c240 read 0x14067b100 is hard-gated b80==3 + asserts session/PlayerGameData -> cold-call AVs). Just pass-through STEP_MenuJobWait each frame so the native FD4 menu task ticks the registered selector: first tick (step+0x68==0) calls installer 0x140828270 -> installs delegate at step+0x70 + POPULATES iodev io18 [iodev+0x18]/io20 [iodev+0x20]; later ticks pump it -> menu_deser 0x14082c240 reads slot from [step_ctx] first dword -> set_save_slot 0x14067a810 (GameMan+0xac0=N) -> deserialize -> GameMan+0xc30 = real saved map + character applied. THE MOUNT IS io18/io20-driven, b80-INDEPENDENT (b80 stays 0; mount is NOT the b80 machine). MOUNT ORACLE = (GameMan+0xac0 == want_slot) AND io18/io20 were set then cleared (consumed). ac0 is PRIMARY (set_save_slot runs only in the mount path); c30 is secondary (ambiguous for m10 chars whose real map == new-game default 0xa010000).

PHASE CONFIRM: HARD save-write guard (the ONLY save-write risk in the whole sequence) -- proceed ONLY if (ac0==want_slot && c30==latched-at-mount && c30!=unset). Then continue_confirm 0x140b0e180 via a {[+8]=owner} shim (rcx=shim, owner=[shim+8]): cmp [owner+0x284],1 (==1 NewGame edx4 else LOAD edx5; owner+0x284 is 0 -> LOAD), getter 0x140679560 copies c30 -> owner+0xbc, SetState 0x140b0d960(owner,5=PlayGame). Native pump then streams the real world. idx6 (STEP_GameStepWait) is a read-only watcher of the stream (csfeman/mms_state/c30/b7c1). FAIL-CLOSED: any phase failure/timeout -> stay at the menu, NO SetState(5), NO save write.

VERIFIED VAs/offsets: dialog vtable 0x142b229f8; load_activate vtable-slot-20 offset 0xa0 = fn 0x1409a4670; cursor [dialog+0xb0c]; bound [dialog+0xb08]; item +0xa8 functor / +0x10 ctx / +0x130 result; selector 0x140826d50 (+0x68 flag,+0x70 delegate); installer 0x140828270; menu_deser 0x14082c240; set_save_slot 0x14067a810 (mov [gm+0xac0],ecx); continue_confirm 0x140b0e180; SetState 0x140b0d960; GameMan 0x143d69918 (c30=+0xc30, ac0=+0xac0, b80=+0xb80, name_is_empty=+0xe78); PlayerGameData [0x144588268] (level+0x68, rune_count+0x6c, rune_memory+0x70, chr_type+0x98, name u16x17 +0x9c, stats vigor..arcane +0x3c..+0x58). NOTE 0x140e365c0 is a 2-byte FLAG setter, NOT the stat-apply (the deserialize reader chain applies stats). NEW-game default map 0xa010000. SWITCH-STALL ROOT CONFIRMED + FIX APPLIED (2026-07-16, Ghidra+runtime+workflow wf_ed658913). The MoveMapStep child leaves its resident STEP_MoveMap(18) step ONLY when the 'ending request' sub-machine (MoveMapStep.field25_0x12a, cases 0..8, in FUN_140afa7c0, ticked every frame by the MoveMap update task FUN_140aff730) walks to case 8, which calls FUN_140af9e80 to advance the parent step 18->19->20->-1; that -1 is what makes InGameStep::STEP_MoveMap_Update set ig_d8=2 and lets the world tear down. THE FREEZE: case 7->8 requires FUN_14067a170()(saveState==0) AND ShouldSave()==false AND FUN_140679460()==false AND CSRemo not-in-cutscene. ShouldSave(0x1406794c0)=saveRequested(GameMan+0xb72) && !CanShowSaveMenu() && menu_gate && bc4!=3. FUN_140679460=GameMan+0xb73 && menu_gate && bc4!=3. Our return-title REQUEST (FUN_14067a490) SETS b72(saveRequested) and b73 (it intends a quit-save). The NATIVE quit clears them BY SAVING; we SUPPRESS the save (no-save-on-quit by design) but never clear the flags, so b72/b73 stay 1 -> ShouldSave/FUN_140679460 stay true -> ending sub-machine hangs at case 7 forever -> child never finishes -> world never tears down = the 'MOVE MAP 18/20' stall. RUNTIME PROOF (DLL 504a7ce9): the STUCK switch2 had b72=1 b73=1; the switch1 that tore down had b72=0 b73=0 (its save happened to run after I cleared bc4=0, clearing b72). DISPROVEN this session: bc4/the +0x4b8 gate (gate was READY 1/0 at bc4=0 and the child still didn't advance; +0x4b8 is the world-VISIBILITY flag not the step advance); our loading-cover/CSFade/CSNowLoadingHelper (workflow: DLL has ZERO CSFade code, all loading hooks are read-only passthroughs, cover activity identical across boot/switch1/switch2). FIX (DLL built after 504a7ce9): title_tick_cover.rs POST-COMMIT DORMANCY block, right after the b78 write, when world_up: clear GameMan+0xb72=0 and +0xb73=0 every teardown frame (const GAME_MAN_SAVE_REQUESTED_B72_OFFSET=0xb72, GAME_MAN_SAVE_REQUEST_COMPANION_B73_OFFSET=0xb73, semaphore SYSTEM_QUIT_TEARDOWN_SAVEREQ_CLEAR_COUNT). Makes ShouldSave()/FUN_140679460() deterministically false -> ending sub-machine advances case 7->8 -> child finishes -> world tears down -> switch completes, with NO disk write (exactly the no-save-on-quit design). Kept: bc4=3-at-REQUEST (fires final functor -> sets rt5d = the cVar10 input that STARTS the ending sub-machine at case 0) + bc4->0 after functor (new world visibility). Note the 'ending request' cVar10 START inputs (case 0->1) = OR of: CSSessionManager WaitReload, CSEventMan deadReset==2, BOOL_143d856a0, menuData+0x5d(rt5d, set by our functor), warp_requested, GameMan b7c/b7d, FUN_140e62b50==8. Per-case fade/sound waits (case1->2, 5/6->7) use CSFade ch2 FadePlateTimerHasEnded + CSSound. Builds on movemapstep-completion-chain-ig-d8-needs-child-minus1 + switch-real-root-movemapstep18. NEXT: user runs the multi-switch; semaphore SYSTEM_QUIT_TEARDOWN_SAVEREQ_CLEAR_COUNT>0 + player goes true->false on each switch + SWITCH-ORACLE mms_step 18->19->20 + LOADED_STABLE + 3+ consecutive loads. If still stuck at 18 with b72=0 b73=0, the block is at a DIFFERENT sub-machine case (re-read field25_0x12a value in oracle -- ADD it).

STATIC RE (2026-06-22, Ghidra dump + deobf grounding). FULLY RESOLVED what drives a requested map block's loadstate to phase 0xa (the thing WorldResWait/readiness 0x14066d3e0 requires). The 'loadstate' object returned by block->vtable[+0x10]() is a CS::WorldBlockRes (super_WorldBlockResBase); fields: [+0x35]=phase byte, [+0x2f]=asset/IO completion gate, [+0x2c]=requested flag (RequestBlock 0x14066d8d0 sets it=1), [+0x2d]=registered flag, [+0x3c]=countdown, [+0x40]/[+0x48]=FD4FileCap ptrs. THE DRIVER (per-frame phase machine): FUN_1406148e0 (real entry 0x140614870; WorldBlockRes::Update) = switch(loadstate[+0x35]) dispatching one handler per phase: case0->1 (if [+0x2f]!=0 && [+6]==0), 1=FUN_140615420, 2=FUN_1406158d0, 3=FUN_140615890, 5=FUN_1406157a0, 6=FUN_140615980(->7 or 5), 7=FUN_140615c60, 8=FUN_1406153a0, 9=FUN_1406151b0, 10=FUN_140615350, 0xb=FUN_140615d20, 0xc->5. THE 0xa TRANSITION: phase-9 handler FUN_1406151b0: if loadstate[+0x2f]!=0 -> sets [+0x35]=10(0xa), [+0x3c]=2, notifies via thunk_FUN_1454ff158; else --[+0x3c] and on underflow calls vtable[+0xb8] and sets [+0x35]=8. (Sibling at deobf 0x1406150d0 also does [+0x2f]!=0 -> [+0x35]=0xa,[+0x3c]=2.) KEY GATE: FUN_1406148e0 recomputes [+0x2f] at top each tick: [+0x2f]=[+0x2d] ONLY IF *(block[3]+0x1c)==6 (the file/IO request object state==6 'loaded') AND virtual [+0x78](block)!=0; else [+0x2f]=0. So phase reaches 0xa ONLY after the FD4 file-load (GLOBAL_CSFile / FD4FileManagerImp -> FD4FileCap, unloaded by FUN_140615050) completes and the IO-request state becomes 6. WHO TICKS IT (the per-frame block loop that is MISSING/late on our path): FUN_14062f840(WorldAreaRes*) iterates [+0xce0] array (count [+0xcd8], stride 0xb98) calling FUN_1406148e0 per block; richer variant FUN_14063a930 iterates the block red-black tree at [+0xd00]/[+0xd08] calling FUN_1406148e0 per block, then drives overworld neighbor blocks via FUN_140614790. Both are CS::FieldArea/WorldAreaRes members (use GLOBAL_FieldArea, CS::BlockId::IsOverworldBlockId, CS::GameMan::CalcGetReplaceMapIdByDisaster, CS::WorldBlockInfo::GetMapId). IMPORTANT: the WorldResWait worker (deobf 0x140624bd0 = the STEP_WorldResWait body) only calls request(0x14066d7e0)+request2(0x14066da60)+pump(0x140629d80)+readiness(0x14066d3e0); it does NOT tick WorldBlockRes::Update. That tick comes from the FieldArea/WorldAreaRes per-frame update (FUN_14062f840/FUN_14063a930), scheduled by the in-game FieldArea task. THE GAP/LEVER: our menu-free continue_confirm/SetState5 path registers+requests the block but the FieldArea/WorldAreaRes per-frame block-update loop and/or the CSFile FD4 async file-load (sets IO-request state=6 -> [+0x2f]) never runs/completes, so [+0x35] never reaches 0xa. To replicate native Continue: ensure the FieldArea/WorldAreaRes update task is registered+ticking AND the CSFile FD4 IO/file-load actually runs+completes for the block's assets. Dump vs deobf: ~0x10 per-region shift; deobf addrs authoritative (grounded via disas-deobf.sh). HOOK POINTS for logging WHAT each phase loads (for parent agent): FUN_1406148e0@0x140614870 (per-block phase tick; log loadstate[+0x35] + map id via FUN_140660fe0/0x140660f70 'm%02d_%02d_%02d_%02d' formatter or block->WorldBlockInfo::GetMapId), FUN_1406151b0@0x1406151b0 (the ->0xa transition), FUN_140615050@0x140615050 (CSFile UnloadFileCap of FD4FileCap [+0x40]/[+0x48]), and GLOBAL_CSFile FD4FileCap load path. RECONCILED ZERO-INPUT MENU NAV RECIPE (2026-06-17, static RE pa8+zbb on decrypted on-disk eldenring.exe, ER 1.16.1 base 0x140000000). For er-effects-rs-av4/h86 own-stepper Load drive. SUPERSEDES the wrong premise that c000's functor advances the menu and that Load-Game shares the generic docall.

=== THE ADVANCE IS SetState, NOT a functor invoke ===
The 3 title items at BeginTitle are composed VIEWS, none advances: c000 _Do_call 0x140764b80->0x140763fc0 builds CS::BackScreen (vt 0x142aa0080, op-new 0xaa0); c140->0x140763f10 builds CS::CaptionView (vt 0x142adf4a0); c280->0x14081e5e0 builds CS::TitleTopDialog (vt 0x142b26468, op-new 0xd78). The native press->main-menu advance = engine's OWN SetState(owner,2=BeginLogo) at 0x140b0b6d9 inside press-job callback 0x140b0b6b0 (gated by press predicate 0x140765030; only called on real input). So zero-input we replicate it: SetState(owner,2) from our idx10 (game's own call, NOT input synthesis). The parked press-any-button screen is the FIRST state 10; SetState(2) -> native pump advances 2(BeginLogo)->3(BeginTitle)->10, building the FULL main menu. SetState(3)=BeginTitle ALONE (skipping BeginLogo) only built the BackScreen (runtime: only c000 ticked) -- the full sequence is needed. CAVEAT: BeginLogo(2) 0x140b0c2a0 hard-asserts session singleton 0x144588e98 at 0x140b0c2c3 -- read it live at the parked title; SetState(2) only if non-null, else SetState(3) fallback. Save-safe either way (menu-UI build, no save write; only SetState(5) writes).

=== LOAD-GAME = item d180, uniquely IDed by the EXISTING classifier ===
The Load-Game main-menu entry IS item d180: functor [d180+0xa8], _Do_call 0x140820c60 = 'add rcx,8; jmp 0x14081ead0' (dialog_factory, the ONLY caller). So functor_chain_hits_factory==0x14081ead0 uniquely+robustly IDs Load-Game; NO +0x58 descriptor guessing. The other main-menu items c3c0/c640/cdc0 use generic docall 0x14078ee60 (forwarder jmp [[cap+0x10d8]+0x10]) = Continue/NewGame/Settings/Quit (non-dialog). d180 does NOT exist until the FULL main menu is built (not at bare BeginTitle).

=== LOCATING d180 zero-input: the CSMenu registry ticks ALL entries ===
owner+0xe0 holder = CSMenu registry (vt 0x142b26468, update 0x1409aac10): registry sub-object at [holder+0xa60], iterates ALL registered entries and dispatches each entry's [entry+0x38]->vtable[+0x10] EVERY frame. So once the main menu is built, d180's leaf Update 0x1407ad1c0 is ticked by the registry -> our menu-item Update hook (cap_menu_item_update_hook on 0x1407ad1c0 + functor_chain_hits_factory) CAPTURES d180 with NO navigation/highlight needed and NO offset-walk of owner+0xe0 (it is NOT a [+0x18]/[+0x60] Sequence). PLAN: SetState(2) build full menu -> hook captures MENU_LOAD_GAME_ITEM=d180. (Iterator 0x1407aa1f0 cursor+0x10/count+0x60/base+0x18/stride8 applies only to Sequence nodes, not owner+0xe0.)

=== INVOKING d180 (STAGE 2): functor-invoke ABI ===
Mirror 0x1407ad2b9: rcx=[item+0xa8] (std::function), rax=[rcx] (_Func_impl vtable), rdx=item+0x10 (ctx out-slot), call [rax+0x10] (_Do_call: add rcx,8; jmp lambda). Result (built ProfileLoadDialog) -> [item+0x130]. Implemented as invoke_menu_item_functor in src/experiments.rs. PRECONDITION (native leaf Update BUILD path 0x1407ad1ec): fire functor only when [item+0x130]==0 && [item+0xa8]!=0 && [item+0x10]==0. zbb recommends LETTING THE NATIVE PUMP tick the leaf (it does the [item+0x68] window-resolve + [item+0x10] wiring before the functor) rather than hand-invoke; but zero-input the cursor is on Continue not Load, so hand-invoke of d180's functor (or calling its full Update) is likely needed -- TBD at runtime. d180 functor -> dialog_factory 0x14081ead0 -> op-new 0x1cd0 -> ProfileLoadDialog ctor 0x1409a3d90. Then load_activate 0x1409a4670(dialog,0,0,5) -> builder 0x140826510 -> selector 0x140826d50 -> menu_deser 0x14082c240 mounts ac0=N + c30=real + char (io18/io20, b80-independent). Save-WRITE risk ONLY at the final continue_confirm 0x140b0e180/SetState(5); guard: never SetState(5) until c30 real-mounted. Related: gold-userdrive-full-load-recipe-with-callers-2026, menu-item-tree-walk-and-functor-abi-decoded-2026, menu-item-hook-works-one-item-at-begintitle-2026, parked-title-menu-not-built-until-beginstate3-2026, owner-e0-holder-is-csmenu-not-sequence-pivot-to-hook-2026.

FIX RECIPE for the b80 cold full-read discard wall (2026-06-21, static RE subagent + my decode). The cold/menu-free save full-read is DISCARDED at gate 2 of the worker post 0x14240e420 (runtime-confirmed: not enqueued). MECHANISM fully decoded:

GATE 2 = membership test 0x141ee1240(rcx=registry, rdx=key_ptr): walks intrusive list [registry+0x28] (registry = *(0x144843038) = obj 0x144843050), comparing via 0x141ee0620 (mov eax,[rdx]; cmp eax,[rcx]; sete al = 4-BYTE DWORD compare) of *(key_ptr) vs *(node+0x10). Returns true iff a node's +0x10 dword == our key dword. If false => read DISCARDED via 0x14240a000 (status 0xe), b80 2->0, never resident. Our save-read's key is NOT registered cold (its owner object is built only on the live-session path; 'User.SigninStatus' 0x140e73e00 is a registered reference client, self-registered once at subsystem init from 0x140defca9).

KEY DERIVATION: 0x141ee05f0(rcx=out,rdx=in) = '*(out)=*(in); ret out' (copies the 4-byte key). In submit 0x14240ad30 the key passed to 0x14240e420 as r8 (=r14) is *(arg2) routed through stack buffers ([rsp+0x80]->[rsp+0x88]) built in 0x140e6f430 via 0x141ee10b0 (which iterates registry [reg+0x28]). The key is a TRANSIENT 4-byte dword during submit -- NOT stably readable from the request object after submit returns.

REGISTER ROUTINE (callable cold from game-task thread, self-contained): reg = getter 0x141ee1110(); then 0x141ee12d0(rcx=reg, rdx=&key_dword) (-> find-or-insert 0x141ee0e50 -> node alloc+splice 0x141ee2070, sets node+0x10=key, bumps count [reg+0x10]). Idempotent (returns false if key already present).

REMAINING: the KEY VALUE (4-byte dword). Subagent: 'read r8 at the submit, do NOT guess.' Capture at runtime at the gate: break at 0x14240adb4 (call 0x14240e420) and read r8 + *(u32*)r8; OR at gate 0x14240e4ba read rdx + *(u32*)rdx; OR at the compare 0x141ee0620 read *(rdx). Use the linux-x86-debug tracebreakpoint tool (being fixed for ER) or the in-process INT3 engine (er-effects-breakpoints.txt). THEN: in cold_char_mount, register that key via 0x141ee12d0 BEFORE submit, and submit -> gate 2 passes -> worker reads -> b80=3 -> deserialize. Gate 1 ([worker_mgr+0x19]==0) already confirmed clear at runtime. Relates: b80-worker-queue-unchanged-leans-discard-gate2-2026-06-21, linux-x86-debug-tracebreakpoint-tool-for-wine-runtime-RE-2026-06-21, b80-DEVICE-MOUNT-REFUTED-dev40-already-set-read-actively-fails-2026-06-21.
