UNIFICATION (2026-06-22, Ghidra decompile of the dir-build handler) -- the whole cold-load wall reduces to ONE thing: the iodev COMMAND FSM does not advance past state 0x16 cold.

FUN_140e6dec0 (deobf 0x140e6ded0) = the iodev command per-state TICK. switch(FUN_14240a1e0([param_1+0x20])) i.e. on the request state at [iodev+0x20]=io20:
  case 0x15/0x16: break -> return 1 (NO-OP -- this is where cold is STUCK).
  case 0x17/0x18: GetBackAllocator; FUN_140e0e730(&local) = BUILD the save-DIR string; FUN_14240a290([iodev+0x20], 0, dir_char16_ptr) = SET the dir on io20's entry (this setter == savedir-CONFIG-LEVER 0x14240a2a0). return 1.
  case 0x14: FUN_14240a170 -> completion (ret 0/7/8/2/9).
  case 0xc/0xd/0xe..0x13: error/cleanup paths.
The setter FUN_14240a290(io20,0,dir): if [io20]!=0 { e=FUN_14240c260([io20],io20[1]); if e FUN_14240dcd0(e,0,dir); FUN_14240c620([io20],io20[1]); } -- finds io20's entry by index io20[1], sets the dir on it.

KEY: the DIR is built+set ONLY when the FSM reaches state 0x17/0x18. Cold the FSM is STUCK at 0x16 (case 0x15/0x16 = no-op), so it NEVER builds the dir, NEVER reaches done(0x14). => the empty/'.sl2' dir (b80-cold-dir-is-DOTsl2-CORRECTION), the name-builder code-8 (b80-cold-COMPLETE-CHAIN), the worker bail-before-open (b80-cold-worker-runs-bails), b80 stuck 0 -- ALL are downstream symptoms of the FSM not advancing past 0x16. This MATCHES b80-RUNTIME-owner-FSM-stuck-idx16 + b80-WALL-FD4-read-job-never-dispatched (node stuck idx 0x16, read job never created). It is ONE root, not many.

WHY prior savedir setter (0x14240a2a0 on io20, b80-RUNTIME-savedir-installed-but-read-still-discarded) didn't work: that is literally the 0x17/0x18 handler body fired OUT OF SEQUENCE while the FSM never actually transitions to 0x17 -- the FSM stuck at 0x16 means setting the dir manually doesn't make the FSM progress (the dir is one side-effect of a state the machine must actually enter + continue from).

Handler FUN_140e6dec0 only ACTS per state; it does NOT advance 0x16->0x17 (case 0x16 just breaks). The STATE TRANSITION is driven elsewhere (the worker / FSM advancer). So THE question (the project's core deep wall): what advances the iodev command FSM 0x16 -> 0x17(dir) -> ... -> 0x14(done) cold? Cold it parks at 0x16. NEXT (the real target): decode the request-state ADVANCER -- what writes/advances the state FUN_14240a1e0 reads (the state field on the request/node), and why it's stuck at 0x16 cold (vs the live Continue flow which advances it). FUN_14240a1e0=state getter, FUN_14240a240/0x14240a170/FUN_14240a130 are state ops. Decode FUN_14240a1e0 (where the state field lives) + its writers. Save-safe (45-Slots pristine ca22fcb5, ER down, all pushed). Relates: b80-RUNTIME-owner-FSM-stuck-idx16-2026-06-21, b80-WALL-FD4-read-job-never-dispatched-cold-2026-06-21, b80-owner-FSM-node-idx16-decoded-2026-06-21, b80-cold-COMPLETE-PICTURE-request-path-uninit-2026-06-22, b80-cold-dir-is-DOTsl2-not-empty-CORRECTION-2026-06-22.

COMPLETE FIX RECIPE for the b80 cold full-read wall (2026-06-21, static RE fully decoded). THE SAVE-DEVICE MOUNT/OPEN ROUTINE = 0x140e6e8d0, signature void device_mount_open(IoDev* rcx). Decoded behavior: call_once static-init (0x144589380/388, idempotent) at head; GUARD at 0x140e6e96f: rcx=[0x143d872e0], edx=0, call 0x14240acd0; if al==0 -> xor al,al; ret (BAILS, +0x40 NOT set @0x140e6e97f-81). If guard passes: allocs 0x218 file-provider subobj (0x141eb9ed0), configures via vtable 0x142409xxx, registers ~10 save path entries (0x1424091a0, flag consts 0x280000/0x60000/0x240010), calls OS-OPEN 0x140e45660 (real body 0x14568ed27, builds file-provider singleton 0x144842d40), then THE KEY WRITE @0x140e6eb56: mov [iodev+0x40],al (sets the device-ready byte the router 0x140e6eb80 tests). 

DIRECTLY CALLABLE from our DLL: iodev=((IoDev*(*)())0x140e6e060)(); ((void(*)(IoDev*))0x140e6e8d0)(iodev);  (getter 0x140e6e060 lazily creates iodev). Single native call site: boot-init 0x140defec2 (0x140defeba getter -> 0x140defec2 mount -> 0x140defecf finalize 0x140e6f1f0).

IODEV IDENTITY (validated): iodev is the STATIC object at 0x1445893b0 (ctor 0x140e6d970 builds in-place; pointer cached to slot 0x144589390). [iodev+0x40] absolute = 0x1445893f0 (riprefscan: 0 hits, only ever written reg-relative @0x140e6eb56 = ONLY the mount writes it). [iodev+0x30] = OS handle slot (ctor sets 0xffffffff invalid).

OPEN QUESTION (the only remaining unknown, a RUNTIME fact): does the GUARD 0x14240acd0([0x143d872e0]) pass at the press-any-button/cold state? Decoded: 0x14240acd0 calls 0x141ee1110 (returns IO worker-REGISTRY singleton [0x144843038], lazily builds EMPTY via 0x141ee1720) then 0x141ee1170 (mov eax,[registry+0x18] = registry size/count); if eax==0 -> guard returns 0 -> MOUNT BAILS; else tail-calls pool-readiness op 0x14240e2c0. A freshly-built registry has [reg+0x18]==0, so the guard likely FAILS until IO workers are REGISTERED into [0x144843038] (done by the device-subsystem boot 0x140defe.. that precedes the mount). If registry is empty at press-any-button, calling 0x140e6e8d0 alone is a no-op (al=0); we'd then need to run the prior boot-init worker-registration too. 

ALSO: we have NEVER directly read iodev+0x40 at runtime (cold_char_mount logs only iodev+0x10/+0x18/+0x20). The unbound-iodev conclusion is STATIC INFERENCE. NEXT ACTION: self-validating SAVE-SAFE probe -- in cold_char_mount FULL-INIT log [0x144843038], [reg+0x18], iodev, iodev+0x40, iodev+0x30 BEFORE; call mount 0x140e6e8d0(iodev), capture al; log iodev+0x40/+0x30 AFTER; then existing submit+poll, log b80 outcome. One gated probe answers: (a) actual cold device state, (b) does guard pass + mount set +0x40, (c) does the full read then go RESIDENT(b80=3). If mount works -> the full read drains real char. DECISIVE + CORRECTED (2026-07-04): the HEAD node on the menu model is CSChrAsmModelIns partsArray[0] (model+0x28), proven via the part builder FUN_1409ec5e0 (dump 0x1409ec5e0): fixed node->partsbnd-category map node0<-cat0(HEAD), node1<-cat0x14(hair), node2<-cat1(body), node3<-cat2(arms), node4<-cat3(legs), node5<-cat4, node6<-cat5, nodes0xf-0x14<-weapons, nodes0x15-0x19<-extras. CRITICAL CORRECTION to any 'null partsArray[0] to hide the helmet' plan -- DO NOT DO IT: FUN_1409ec5e0 binds the FaceData to partsArray[0] itself (FUN_140251730(faceData,0,model+0x28,..)), so node 0 hosts the FACE+head, not a separable helmet overlay. Nulling node 0 removes the whole head (empty neck), NOT reveal-the-face. There is NO free in-place helmet-hide: the engine's own Status 'Hide helm' toggle REBUILDS the head node with the naked-head model. Our shipped approach (equip naked-head protector 10000 for helm-off, real head for helm-on, via kick_target_profile_slot rebuild) is therefore the SAME mechanism the game uses, at whole-model granularity, and the make-before-break display bridge already hides the ~100ms rebuild (validated run 2026-07-04: helm-off overrides fired, helm-on rekick fired, 67 helm-on publishes reached the display on the first load window -- no blank). FUN_1409eb8d0 mask at model+0x2d0 only hides WEAPON nodes 7..14, never protectors. POSSIBLE marginal improvement (not required): rebuild ONLY node 0 (FUN_1409ee620(model,0,chr_asm,res,...,cat0)) instead of the whole model to shrink the rebuild window -- but the bridge already makes the full rebuild seamless, so low value. Conclusion: the hold-then-cut is the correct mechanism; the pure in-place toggle the user envisioned is precluded by the face binding to the head node. bd er-effects-rs-vnw, [[portrait-helm-inplace-visibility-RE-2026-07-04]].

ER BHD5 path hash (confirmed via Andre.Core.Util.BhdDictionary.ComputeHash and live archive scan 2026-06-25): 64-bit, hash=hash*0x85(133)+char over normalized path (forward slashes, leading '/', lowercased). e.g. /shader_d3d12/gxshader.shaderbnd.dcx -> 0x8998EFAAE0513BB6. NOT the 32-bit SFUtil.FromPathHash (prime 37). Managed extraction path (no native bhd5_decrypt_rust.dll, which is Windows-only under wine): SoulsFormats.Util.CryptographyUtility.DecryptRsa(bhdPath, pemKey) -> decrypted BHD5 stream; BHD5.Read(Memory<byte>, BHD5.Game.EldenRing); FileHeader.ReadFile(FileStream bdt) -> raw file bytes (handles offset/size/AES). PEM keys via internal Andre.Formats.Util.ArchiveKeys.GetKey(bhdPath, Andre.Core.Game.ER) (Data0-3, DLC, sd\sd, sd\sd_dlc02). Tooling: scripts/shaderlab/extract (.NET, refs Smithbox binary DLLs at ~/.local/share/smithbox/app). ER shader containers: /shader/ (DX11) and /shader_d3d12/ (DX12, live) *.shaderbnd.dcx (DCX BND4) + shaderbdle*.shaderbdlebnd.dcx.

PHASE-4 STACK-GADGET ANATOMY (2026-07-01), the next-biggest bucket after jmp-into-arxan (~15/90 baseline). Arxan encodes push/pop/ret via stack-pointer gadgets that appear INLINE in game .text (recovered, unlike the entry/return TRAMPOLINES which are in Arxan .text and dropped). Peephole equivalences (verified on 0x140661c40, terminal=ret):
  lea rsp,[rsp+8]; mov REG,[rsp-8]   ==  pop REG
  lea rsp,[rsp+8]; jmp qword [rsp-8] ==  ret   (recovery already marks the jmp[rsp-8] terminal=ret)
  lea rsp,[rsp-8]; mov [rsp],REG     ==  push REG
  mov rsp, rNN                        ==  stack realloc (keep; part of real epilogue, e.g. mov rsp,r11 where r11=lea[rsp+0xa0])
0x140661c40 epilogue: lea r11,[rsp+0xa0]; mov rbx,[r11+0x18]; mov rdi,[r11+0x20]; mov rsp,r11; (pop rbp); (ret) -- restores rbx/rdi/rbp and returns. FIX = peephole translator over the recovered block instruction stream folding the lea-rsp+mov/jmp pairs back to push/pop/ret. The differential harness is the safety net (catches wrong translations as ref!=recomp, as it did for the frameless case) BUT a wrong-but-balanced fold could vacuously pass -> validate carefully.
HETEROGENEOUS bucket: some stack-gadget fns are clean ret-gadget epilogues (collapsible, terminal=ret); others DERAIL on indirect/vtable calls (0x1405b4730 terminal=derail, call [rax+0x68] recovery cannot follow) -- that subset needs indirect-call handling first, harder. Start with the terminal=ret clean subset.
Reassembler currently flags all of these via meta['gadgets'] (lea rsp,[rsp±N] / jmp [rsp]) in bake(). Related: arxan-prologue-reconstruction-validated-2026-07-01, gadget-collapse-uncond-jmp-2026-07-01.

RETRACT probe-4 conclusion (2026-06-18, deep static investigation). "No MenuWindow at the title" was WRONG -- I searched the wrong subtree. The TitleTopDialog ITSELF is created by the SAME universal menu-dialog factory mechanism: a table of std::function<void(MenuWindow*, SceneProxy&)> (base _Func_base 0x142a9b958, 193 lea sites), each _Do_call = add rcx,8; jmp <dialogFactory>. TitleTopDialog factory = 0x14081e5e0; ProfileLoadDialog factory = 0x14081ead0 (functor vtable 0x142ac3ea8, its UNIQUE caller). So a live MenuWindow (vt 0x142a93a60) / MenuWindowProxy (0x142a94318) + SceneProxy MUST exist at the title -- they are the two args the menu module passes when it invokes ANY functor in this table. RTTI: 0x142a93a60=MenuWindow, 0x142a94318=MenuWindowProxy, 0x142a94330=MenuResource, 0x142a94a70=SceneObjProxy. 0x142ac3ea8 is NEITHER uniquely title nor in-game -- it is a peer in the universal table (so my "in-game functor" hypothesis was also wrong).

REGISTRAR 0x1409b24e0 CORRECTION: it is NOT the Continue/Load/NewGame item builder -- it is the TitleTopDialog INITIALIZER (installs lifecycle/online-gate sub-jobs: functor 0x142b267b0=per-job MenuJob dispatch, 0x142b26708=no-arg enable/refresh, MenuMemberFuncJob 0x142b265d0 wrapping dialog member-fns 0x1409b2f00/0x1409b35f0 = init + OnlineMode/MultiPlay/AppVersion sub-entries via NetworkCheckJob 0x142b264f0). The visible menu items live in the std::function factory table, NOT this registrar. (Supersedes the registrar-entry assumptions in load-game-functor-0x142ac3ea8-is-live-build-key-2026.)

THE BUILD CALL IS UNCHANGED + CORRECT: factory 0x14081ead0(rcx = [TitleTopDialog+0xa38] capture, rdx = live MenuWindow*, r8 = SceneProxy& [via capture]). We HAVE: TitleTopDialog (owner+0xe0) + SceneProxy ([dialog+0xa38]). MISSING (runtime): the live MenuWindow*. It EXISTS; flat scans of dialog/owner/CSMenuManImp[0..0x8a0]/registry/menu-step[0..0x400] missed it (it is in the scene-group structure, deeper than those flat windows).

NEXT (pick ONE, all robust): (1) CAPTURE via hook -- hook the SceneProxy/menu-module functor dispatch that invokes table functors per-frame at the title and capture the live (MenuWindow*, SceneProxy&) args the ENGINE passes (contamination-proof, definitively correct; needs RE of the dispatch site). USER DIRECTIVE (2026-06-29, emphatic): STOP using the user's visual report as the look-at oracle. Build an IN-PROCESS RAM/PIXEL semaphore that proves realtime head-tracking without a human looking. The agent regressed into 'navigate to LOAD GAME and tell me what you see' across multiple relaunches -- this violates the project rule (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS): screenshots/visual state must NEVER be the run-stopping oracle; every on-screen phenomenon must be detectable from PE/RAM/native/pixel telemetry. Required oracle design: (1) deterministic SINUSOID selftest mode that drives yaw/pitch from a frame counter instead of GetCursorPos (zero-input, reproducible, no mouse); (2) per-frame (throttled) READBACK of the loaded/selected slot offscreen RT in the draw-phase task AFTER the draw step rasterizes (the existing FrameBegin dump is mistimed -> always nonblack=0 because it reads BEFORE our AdhocDraw draw); (3) telemetry semaphores: rt_nonblack (head rendered, no flicker), rt_distinct_hashes over a window (RT content varies with the driven angle => tracking), render_drives/frame ratio (==1.0 => per-frame redraw, not the ~11% flicker). PASS = nonblack always true + distinct_hashes high under sinusoid + render_drives≈frame_count. This lets the agent self-determine pass/fail. Renderers exist at the title MAIN MENU (TitleTopDialog ctor) and LOAD GAME, so the selftest needs at most a one-time nav, no continuous input. Relates: portrait-lookat-realtime-drawphase-design-2026-06-29.

COLD-MOUNT IN-WORLD = ONE FIELD, GameMan+0xc30 (2026-06-18, agents A+B converged, static deobf). The menu-free cold-mount already loads the real char (PlayerGameData) menu-free; the ONLY thing between that and the playable world is c30 (saved map id). continue_confirm 0x140b0e180(owner=[0x143d5df38+8]): calls 0x679560 (mov edx,[GameMan+0xc30]; mov [rcx],edx) -> owner+0xbc = c30 -> SetState 0x140b0d960(owner,5) -> disp1 0x140afbad0/disp2 0x140afb880 stream owner+0xbc. NO second map source: fix c30 and the world streams the right place.

WHY c30 STAYS 0xa010000 (DEFAULT, NOT real map): deserialize 0x67b290 DOES call c30-writer 0x67bd70 @0x67b30e (rcx=GameMan, rdx=resident 0x280000 buffer, r8d=0x280000). 0x67bd70 writes c30 = save-record dword at +4 ONLY if ALL hold: (a) gate [0x143d68078] != 0 (a 0x270-byte save-data subsystem heap object built by save-load boot 0x6798d0..0x679904, zeroed on teardown 0x6789bf); if null -> returns, no write. (b) stream-reader 0x141edfb60 returns 0x10 (needs reader [ctx+0x24] armed + buffer header where expected). (c) first-dword slot-index passes bounds 0x1402624c0 ([0x143b127dc]<=idx<=[0x143b127d8]). The write is 0x67be0f mov c30. Char-apply uses a SEPARATE reader (rsp+0x48 @0x67b33a) over the same buffer -> char can succeed while the c30 micro-read fails. So c30=default means one of (a)/(b)/(c) failed cold. ALL runtime-observable: log al of 0x67bd70, count@0x67bdeb, ecx@0x67bdf9, [0x143d68078].

MENU-FREE RECIPE (save-safe): cold-mount (ACTIVATE 0x140262250 + set_save_slot 0x14067a810 + submit type-0xa 0x14067b1a0 + drain/poll b80==3 + deserialize 0x67b290) -> ENSURE [0x143d68078]!=0 + make the c30 record-read succeed (or write c30 directly = the map dword in the resident 0x280000 buffer at the slot map-record+4) -> GUARD c30 != 0xa010000(default) AND char fingerprint(level/stats) present -> continue_confirm 0x140b0e180(owner). 

SAVE-SAFETY ABSOLUTE: continue_confirm fires SetState5 which AUTOSAVES. NEVER fire it until c30 is CONFIRMED the char real map (else real char streams to wrong location + autosaved over -- this is what crashed/wrong-loaded before). Mount-done detection: use CHAR FINGERPRINT, not c30.

TWO OPEN ITEMS: (1) WHY c30 not written cold = which of gate/reader/bounds fails -> a VERIFY-ONLY (no SetState5) diagnostic probe hooking/logging 0x67bd70 internals + the buffer map-record. (2) WORLD-STREAM COMPLETION: even with c30 right + SetState5, agent-B saw mms_state=13 (WorldResWait) at frame 60 = the streaming wall OR the 30s headless-Proton cap too short -> needs world-stream margin / split capture. NEXT: VERIFY-ONLY c30 diagnostic probe FIRST (save-safe), then the gated SetState5 + world-stream-margin probe. When the user is FRUSTRATED or COURSE-CORRECTING, a substanceless acknowledgment ('Understood' / 'You're right' / 'Got it') is WORSE THAN SILENCE -- it compounds the problem (a charged-but-substanceless user message met with a flat-but-substanceless agent reply) and is pure ego-defense (trying to look agreeable / avoid being 'burned' for being wrong). The user cares ZERO for substanceless prose. WHY a verbal 'Understood' is a lie: the agent will NOT retain it across sessions, so promising future improvement without persisting it is false. Frustration is course-correction, not an invitation to reply. (2) NEVER reflexively agree ('you're right') before validating against ground truth; agree only after checking, and when uncertain say what you'd verify. Do NOT sign documents / accept liability terms / make binding commitments on Anthropic's behalf (no legal weight = false promise). (3) When the user reports a VISUAL/UI state and a screenshot/image tool exists, USE IT to verify rather than asking what's on screen; when unsure of runtime state and a breakpoint/trace engine exists, break+trace rather than guess -- do not offload to the user what the agent can observe itself. Concrete trigger this session: agent said 'you're right' then moved to act WITHOUT validating the 4 claims, and repeatedly ASKED the user 'what's on screen' / to navigate the menu instead of (1) self-driving gamepad input via the XInput hook, (2) keeping the user's input blocked, (3) breaking+tracing the populate path, (4) screenshotting the oracle. Static RE: MenuWindow::MenuWindow dump 0x140741a60 is the minimal native initializer that creates the +0x3b8 bind owner used by FUN_1407452c0. It initializes field553_0x3b8 as a SceneObjProxy copied from the root SceneObjProxy, then zeroes field_0xa20 and field_0xa28 immediately after it. FUN_1407452c0(owner,pair) treats owner+0x60 as the binding fixed-vector state: it calls FUN_140748700(owner+0x60,pair) which increments count at owner+0x668 (MenuWindow+0xa20 for field553) and appends up to 0x10 {target,systex} string pairs, then calls GLOBAL_CSScaleform/FUN_140d6ac00 with the owner SceneObjProxy and the two strings. MenuWindow ctor itself does not install the GenericListSelectDialog callback jobs; those come later from FUN_14077f020. Therefore a safer custom title-safe portrait owner can start from an authored/custom GFX SceneObjProxy + MenuWindow::MenuWindow or a MenuWindow-shaped owner with +0x3b8 SceneObjProxy and +0xa20 vector initialized, then bind dummy face symbols through FUN_1407452c0 without replaying ProfileSelect/GenericList behavior.

programmatic-foreground-force-insufficient-for-gameplay-movement-2026-07-18: Full 4-load run with PROBE_FOREGROUND=1 (samechar-3x-bxa8c6cv4): load1 STILL did NOT move (can_move stayed f0, advanced via WAIT_WORLD deadline). So the can-move probe's programmatic ER-foreground-force (sq_repro_force_foreground_now) does NOT reproduce the movement the user saw when THEY manually toggled focus into ER. Gameplay MOVEMENT injection needs the window GENUINELY active/focused; a background process forcing foreground is blocked by the Windows foreground-lock (SetForegroundWindow from a non-foreground process only flashes/partial-activates) AND/OR the game gates locomotion input on true GetActiveWindow. sq-repro's menu nav 'worked' unfocused via stay-active(DLUID+0x88d) which covers MENUS, not locomotion. OPTIONS: (a) improve the activation (AttachThreadInput + SetForegroundWindow trick to bypass the lock -- check if sq_repro_ensure_foreground already does this); (b) RE the gameplay-input focus gate the game checks and override it in-DLL (like stay-active does for menus) so injected pad input applies unfocused; (c) accept that the proof requires ER genuinely focused (user leaves it focused + walks away). The movement MECHANISM (pad-boundary stick injection) is proven to work when focused; the blocker is purely making the window active without a human. Links [[movement-injection-needs-ER-focused-stayactive-menus-only-2026-07-18]].

THE LOAD-GAME ACTION IS UNIQUE: refscan of thunk 0x140820c60 (add rcx,8; jmp factory 0x14081ead0) = exactly ONE ptr-ref in the whole image, at 0x142ac3eb8 = (vtable 0x142ac3ea8)+0x10 = the std::function _Do_call slot. RTTI(0x142ac3ea8) = .?AV?@V<lambda...>...PEAVMenuWindow@CS@@AEAVSceneProxy = std::function<...(MenuWindow*, SceneProxy&)>. So the Load-Game menu action is UNIQUELY the functor instance whose vtable==0x142ac3ea8; its _Do_call = thunk 0x140820c60 -> factory 0x14081ead0.

FACTORY 0x14081ead0(rcx=capture, rdx=arg) decoded: calls 0x1409aa680; reads menu-group/allocator singleton 0x143d87350 (boot-built); allocs 0x1cd0 bytes (the ProfileLoadDialog) via 0x141eb9ed0; calls 0x140745280(rcx=rdx-arg); then mov r8,[rcx_capture]; ctor 0x1409a3d90(rcx=new dialog, rdx=rax, r8=[capture]) -> returns dialog. The thunk's 'add rcx,8' makes rcx=functor+8 (the lambda CAPTURE; [capture+0] -> ctor r8). rdx (2nd factory arg) is NOT set by the thunk = it is the std::function caller's arg1 = the live MenuWindow*.

=> LIVE BUILD = call 0x140820c60(rcx=<live functor instance, vtable 0x142ac3ea8>, rdx=<live MenuWindow*>, r8=<SceneProxy&>) i.e. factory(rcx=functor+8, rdx=MenuWindow*). own_stepper_direct_build's FORGE calls factory 0x14081ead0 with a SYNTHETIC capture + no real MenuWindow -> builds a NON-LIVE dialog (dialog+0x50/+0x1cc8 unpopulated, selector queued-unpumped = the final-wall bug). THE FIX: at the rendered live menu, READ-ONLY locate (a) the functor instance (scan for an object whose [0]==base+0x2ac3ea8) and (b) a live MenuWindow* + SceneProxy, then call the thunk -> a LIVE registered ProfileLoadDialog the native menu group 0x143d87350 pumps -> native selector -> menu_deser 0x14082c240 -> mount -> native continue -> in-world. No forge, no hand-roll.

OWN-LOAD step-4 CRASH ROOT CAUSE (2026-06-22, runtime + static). The verify-only parse is PROVEN (c30 0xa010000->0x1c000000, fp_real) but firing continue_confirm CRASHED the game (reason process_exited_before_ready, player_present=false); save md5 UNCHANGED (autosave never completed -> safe). CAUSE = WRONG OWNER. own_load_continue_fire resolved owner=*(GameDataMan+0x8)=0x7fffa0830600 and that crashed inside continue_confirm.

STATIC FACTS: continue_confirm 0x140b0e180 is VTABLE SLOT 2 (ptr stored at 0x142b63f60; vtable starts 0x142b63f50: [+0]=0x140b0dc30 [+8]=0x140b0e4f0 [+0x10]=0x140b0e180=continue_confirm [+0x18]=0x140b0ea00) of a MANAGER object at .data 0x143d5df38 (==0 at rest in the deobf image, built at runtime). continue_confirm body: this=rcx; owner=[this+8]; checks owner+0x284 (newgame flag, ==1 -> NewGame), resolves c30 via 0x140679560 -> owner+0xbc, calls SetState5 0x140b0d960(owner,5), then 0x14082cd60(owner+0x138), 0x140ca7660(owner+0x300). It only reads [this+8] off `this`. So owner = [0x143d5df38 + 8] (the recipe's literal), NOT *(GDM+8). The proven verify run's title_owner_scan_cached_owner was 0x7fffa1b77010 (the SetState-able title owner, state=10) -- a THIRD candidate.

FIX PLAN (observe-first, no blind re-crash): (1) add owner-DIAGNOSTIC logging to the VERIFY-ONLY path so a no-continue run reveals all candidates: manager_vtable=*(base+0x3d5df38), recipe_owner=*(base+0x3d5df38+8), gdm8=*(GDM+8), and the title-scan owner -- with each owner's +0x284 byte. (2) Run verify-only, compare, pick the owner that is non-null AND matches the SetState-able title owner. (3) Wire own_load_continue_fire to use that owner (likely the title owner the DLL already resolves via title_boot_ready/title_owner_scan, threaded into own_load_drive). Most-native alternative: call continue_confirm with rcx = base+0x3d5df38 (the engine's manager object) so it reads its own [this+8] owner -- but only if that manager is populated cold. The prior keystone 'inject [savemgr 0x143d69918 +0xb78]=0 at MoveMapListStep STEP_ListSelect' was targeting the WRONG machine for the TITLE Load deliverable. Two DISTINCT state machines, RTTI-proven:

MODEL A = CS::MoveMapListStep = DLRF::DLRuntimeClassImpl<MoveMapListStep> (step-node vtable 0x142b5e968, instance vtable ~0x1429b3608, step table 0x143d705b0, descriptor 0x143d703e0). Its STEP_ListSelect 0x140af0710 DOES read [savemgr+0xb78] via accessor 0x1406793c0 and sets the load-decision [ctx+0x2c8]; STEP_LoadSaveData 0x140af19b0 routes through gate 0x14067b200 (the b80 machine). BUT this is the CHARACTER-SELECT / new-game-flow step machine (binds list/movie singletons 0x144588e98 + 0x143d5b0f8, spawns child CharaSelectStep vtable 0x142b5dec0). It is NOT on the title 'Load Game existing slot' confirm path. It is spawned ONLY by FD4 reflection (DLRuntimeClass::CreateInstance against descriptor 0x143d703e0); instance vtable 0x1429b3608 has ZERO refs/stores (no global, no inline ctor, no directly-callable instantiation function) -> the reflection/scheduler wall. So the b78 lever is a DEAD END for the title Load deliverable.

MODEL B = the TITLE 'Load Game' path (runtime-captured ground truth, fully disjoint from Model A -- builder 0x140826510 + load_activate 0x1409a4670 contain ZERO refs to descriptor 0x143d703e0 / getter 0x140aef980 / table 0x143d705b0 / any 0x140af0xxx handler): TitleTopDialog (vtable 0x142b26468, update 0x1409aac10) holds selectable entries as CS::MenuMemberFuncJob<TitleTopDialog> nodes (op-new 0x28, vtable 0x142b265d0, run=0x1409aaba0; layout [entry+0x10]=dialog, [entry+0x18]=member-fn ptr, [entry+0x20]=this-adjust) registered into the dialog ENTRY REGISTRY [dialog+0xa48] by registrar 0x1409b24e0 (via list-register 0x1407ab370; source sub-object [dialog+0xa38]). DECISIVE RE (2026-06-29, wf agent, Ghidra-dump grounded) -- explains BOTH 'heads not moving' AND the ~4-11% flicker for the realtime look-at.

(1) HEADS NOT MOVING ROOT CAUSE: the GPU skins each portrait from PER-SUBMODEL pose buffers, NOT the renderer's importer PoseHolder. model_ins (renderer+0x778, CSChrAsmModelIns) holds 27 submodel CSModelIns* at model_ins+0x28..+0x100. The renderer importer's model-space bones are COPIED into each submodel's own poseHolder.modelSpaceBoneData by FUN_1409e9ac0(model_ins, frame, importer) [dump 0x1409e9ac0 -> deobf 0x1409e9990, shift -0x130], which loops submodels calling FUN_1409f4cf0 [deobf 0x1409f4bc0] (per-submodel copy + commit to render entity at submodel+0x130). Our code wrote the importer poseHolder + updateBoneModelSpace but NEVER called the propagation -> the rendered submodels never saw the edit -> head static. FIX: after writing importer local quats + updateBoneModelSpace(deobf 0x141653350), call FUN_1409e9990(model_ins, frame, importer) every frame to propagate to submodels, THEN the RT shows it.

(2) CADENCE: X(+0x948) + importer(X+0x20, CSFD4LocationHkaPoseImporter size 0x98, poseHolder@+0x48) are PERSISTENT for the MODEL'S lifetime (freed only at teardown FUN_140bbb180 deobf 0x140bbb180, which nulls +0x778 AND +0x948 together). Two tasks drive it (registered in CSMenuAsmModelRend ctor): per-frame push FUN_140bba7d0 [deobf 0x140bba6e0] = if(X&&model_ins){importer=*(X+0x20); FUN_1409e9990(model_ins,frame,importer);} (every frame pushes importer->submodels); rabbit task FUN_140bba820 [deobf 0x140bba730] re-imports the idle anim into the importer ONLY on gparam-word DAT_143b39470 change (~6 Hz) -> importer pose only refreshes 6Hz, per-frame task just re-pushes stale. So importer pose is 6Hz; submodel push is 60Hz.

(3) WHY model_ins ~4% on slot 0 (empirical stage probe): menu profile models are BUILT+TORN DOWN repeatedly (engine keeps ~1 slot's model alive at a time, round-robin by visibility) -> +0x778/+0x948 cycle together. The 10-slot LOAD GAME menu is NOT a clean testbed for a stable single portrait. The PRODUCT is a SINGLE persistent renderer (teardown-spare hook keeps one alive post-Continue) -> model_ins persists there. So validate realtime on a single kept-alive model, not the cycling 10-slot menu.

RECOMMENDED (RE Q4): HOOK the per-frame task tail FUN_140bba6e0 (deobf, runs per-model per-frame on the right thread with the correct frame arg) -> after original, post-multiply Head/Neck/Spine2 local quats onto the importer poseHolder + updateBoneModelSpace + re-call FUN_1409e9990(model_ins, frame, importer) so our pose wins for that frame; fires only when a model exists (handles cycling). May still need the draw-step call (0x9aa290) to re-rasterize the offscreen RT if the menu doesn't redraw per-frame. PoseHolder: skeleton@0, local{data@+0x8,size@+0x10}, modelSpace{data@+0x18,size@+0x20}, isUpdated@+0x38; BoneData stride0x30 quat@+0x10. Addr table: updateBoneModelSpace 0x141653350; propagate 0x1409e9990; per-frame task 0x140bba6e0; rabbit 0x140bba730; teardown 0x140bbb180. GROUND-TRUTH all via dump-deobf-shift before CALL. REFINED-GOAL-details-answers-2-2026-07-18: More answers. LOAD PROOF FLOOR = identity + stats + gear spot-check (name matches selected save AND player controllable in-world AND a few RAM stats match AND an equipment/inventory spot-check). LOAD MECHANISM = prefer IN-MEMORY REDIRECT, and it must be READ-ONLY of the source save (read-only or read/write file: READ, NEVER WRITE the supplied file). SAVE-SYSTEM DESIGN: the game must NEVER save on its own; a quit-menu button was replaced to be SAVE-ONLY (not save+quit-to-menu/desktop) so the user trusts the loaded disk save is never modified. The ONLY write path is that Save button, which UPSERTS a COPY into the current logged-in Steam-ID's APPDATA dir (the folder the game normally reads/writes), irrespective of whether a save already exists there -- NOT the supplied file. The game currently forces writing a save to appdata on initial load; user does NOT want that requirement (initial-load appdata write should be eliminated/allowed only via Save button). Every other save-write mechanism is believed disabled. VALID SAVE = active save-slot identifier == 1 for the slot (delete-character sets it to 0 = overwritable). Folder nav should list ONLY files containing >=1 valid save; clicking a file lets user select 1+ characters. Bugged file possibly named '12345...' -- DO NOT focus on it. SPEED BAR (hard): our load of a character must be FASTER than the manual baseline = quit game -> copy/paste a save file into appdata -> start game -> mash buttons to Continue(first option). Slower = non-starter. A telemetry-only DLL hooking what's needed can time+compare (optional to validate on vanilla). SAVE SAFETY: ZERO concern -- every save file has a backup elsewhere; I may copy/stage/modify freely. Links [[REFINED-GOAL-details-answers-2026-07-18]].

B STEP #1 RESULT (2026-06-18, static deobf decode, the native-bootstrap pivot's first question). DECODED why the save-IO fails cold vs works live. The async submit 0x140e6f940 cold-bail gates (disasm-verified): cmpq [iodev+0x10],0 jne FAIL; cmpq [iodev+0x20],0 jne FAIL; test buf(rdx) je FAIL. i.e. it ONLY fails on a STALE in-flight request ([iodev 0x144589390 +0x10] / +0x20 non-null) OR a null buffer -- there is NO session/menu/screen check in the submit itself. So the cold submit can SUCCEED (sets b80=1) on a clean iodev. Then alloc 0x298 task (allocator [0x143d87358]) + 0x14240a300 builds the request + KICK 0x140e6fb50 -> pool-submit 0x14240ae10 (worker-manager 0x144852f88).

=> The cold-vs-live difference is NOT the submit gate. It is the DOWNSTREAM WORKER DRAIN: the queued read advances the IO-request state (0x14240a1f0 -> 0x14 resident) ONLY when the FD4 stream-worker task 0x144842d40 (key 0x59682f01) is REGISTERED with the scheduler AND TICKED, which happens only inside a real menu/in-game SESSION (the menu MoveMapListStep helper 0x140af1b40 / IngameInit tail 0x140b0a980 register it; the scheduler then ticks it). Cold/synthetic, the worker is unregistered or unticked -> b80 stuck at 1, read never resident. This RE-CONFIRMS the long-standing stream-worker-registration wall (stream-worker-registration-is-the-b80-wall-verified-2026, async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026) as B's true target, and RULES OUT 'the submit is session-gated' (it is not).

CAVEAT for B: own-stepper-runtime-milestone-and-b80-still-wall-2026 already REGISTERED the worker (0x140b0a980 stub) and b80 STILL stayed 0 -- so registration ALONE is insufficient; the worker must also be TICKED by the scheduler in an active task group, which the synthetic context did not achieve. So B's real crux: get the stream worker 0x144842d40 both REGISTERED *and* SCHEDULER-TICKED, which means reaching a genuine in-game/menu session state (SetState(5) builds CSFeMan+MoveMapStep but the milestone showed the worker still didn't drain -> the missing piece is the IngameInit-tail registration + the scheduler actually ticking that task). NEXT B steps: (1) verify at runtime whether, after SetState(5)+worker-register, the task 0x144842d40 is actually in a scheduler group being ticked (read its scheduler registration / tick count) -- distinguish 'registered-but-not-ticked' from 'not-registered'; (2) if not ticked, find what the native flow does to put it in an active ticked group (the IngameInit/InGameStep step that the FE-host SetState(5) shortcut skips). Related: STRATEGIC-PIVOT-B-native-session-bootstrap-chosen-2026, stream-worker-registration-is-the-b80-wall-verified-2026, own-stepper-runtime-milestone-and-b80-still-wall-2026, async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026, b80-submit-kick-exact-false-gate-decoded-2026, fd4-scheduler-no-group-active-gate-runs-all-2026.

POST-CONTINUE PORTRAIT SPARE ROUTE -- DISPROVEN (2026-06-29, branch spike/boot-parallelization). Three capped native-continue runs with progressively finer instrumentation settle it: SPARING THE MENU'S PORTRAIT RENDERER CAN NEVER POPULATE THE LOADING-SCREEN PORTRAIT. Two structural reasons, both proven from the dense lookat-spared-sweep telemetry:

(1) The character MODEL is DESTRUCTED during the Continue transition, not merely detached from the renderer. At candidate record-time (menu, +16.0s) renderer+0x778 model_ins=0x7ffef691b040 (valid). By the time profile_renderer_teardown_spare_hook (FUN_1409b2f00) fires (+17.4s) the renderer's own +0x778 is ALREADY 0x0 -- the model was unhooked before our hook runs. The captured model object's page stays MAPPED post-Continue (reads succeed) BUT its first qword flips from a real pointer (0x..200f0) to a SELF-REFERENTIAL pointer (cap_vt == cap_model == 0x7ffef691b040) = the classic empty-intrusive-list / freed-pool-chunk sentinel. So the object was torn down + reset, not just detached. Re-attaching it into renderer+0x778 would bind a dead shell (would draw empty / risk crash).

(2) The menu model and the now-loading screen NEVER COEXIST. now_loading_active(base) reads 0 across the whole post-Continue window and only flips to 1 at the LAST sample (+21.2s); the model is already dead by ~17.4s. ~4s structural gap. This is tied to the menu-teardown sequence, not load duration, so a longer/heavier load does NOT close the gap.

What DID work (keep): the gating fix (portrait_lookat_enabled) makes the teardown-spare hook fire on native-continue; the spared RENDERER object persists + is drawn every frame post-Continue (PROFILE_PERFRAME_SPARED_DRAWS climbs to ~180). But it's an EMPTY renderer -- model_ok=0, rt frozen 25/2/3 -- because the model behind it is gone.

IMPLICATION / NEXT DIRECTION: the portrait must be rendered from a model WE OWN with an independent lifetime that survives menu teardown -- either (A) build our own CSMenuProfModelRend-style profile model instance for the loading screen and keep its lifetime, or (B) rebuild a model for the now-loading window. Borrowing the menu's instance is dead. Supersedes postcontinue-portrait-spare-result-2026-06-29 and the route in postcontinue-portrait-route-decision-2026-06-29 / postcontinue-portrait-HANDOFF-2026-06-29.

PROBE #12 ISOLATION (2026-06-18, BOTH diagnostic hooks OFF [now gated opt-in], 120s watch confirmed timeout_seconds=120.0, save-safe presses=0). RESULT: hooks were NOT the cause. STAGE2 STILL crashes (reason=process_exited_before_ready @28.8s, game_task_ticks=795) with b80 STUCK at 0 (only value ever seen across the run), deser_ret=-2, deser_ok never true, c30=0xa010000 default.

DECISIVE COMPARISON: the LIVE-DIALOG probes (which fail-closed WITHOUT firing any mount) ran to ~850-953 ticks with NO crash (hit DONE/budget). Every DIRECT-BUILD STAGE2 run (probes 10/11/12) dies at ~795 ticks via process_exited. => STAGE2 SELECTOR SELF-PUMP is what CRASHES the game, and it never drives b80. The crash is specific to STAGE2-SELECTOR-DRIVE pumping step=0x7ffe9e4f4000 (the selector job from builder 0x140826510, tick 0x140826d50). Per final-wall memo that step is a TRANSIENT stack/queue object never stored back to the dialog -> pumping it out of context = crash + no b80 drive.

LIKELY REGRESSION: SAVE-DATA-HALF-SOLVED (the run that WORKED: b80->3, deser ret=1, real char) used a DIRECT submit+drain in own_stepper_stage2: set_save_slot 0x14067a810 + SUBMIT 0x14067b1a0(ecx=0) [b80=2] + drain lane 0x679510 + poll 0x679180 [b80->3] + deserialize 0x67b290. The CURRENT own_stepper_stage2 STAGE2 instead does STAGE2-SELECTOR-DRIVE (pump the selector step) + STAGE2-MOUNT-POLL -- and submit_ret=1 but b80 never moves. So STAGE2 was CHANGED from the working direct-submit to the crashing selector-pump (likely during the final-wall/selector self-pump work).

FIX (concrete, well-grounded): route own_stepper_stage2 STAGE2 to the SAVE-DATA-HALF DIRECT submit+drain+deserialize (submit 0x14067b1a0 -> drain 0x679510 -> poll 0x679180 -> b80==3 -> deserialize 0x67b290), NOT the selector self-pump (remove/disable the 0x140826d50 step pump that crashes). Keep VERIFY-ONLY (no SetState5) until c30 confirmed. NOTE the 28s/795-tick exit is reproducible -> a clean signal to test against. INFRA fixed this session: 120s probe cap propagates now (RUNTIME_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=120 in .auto/runtime-env.direct-build); hooks opt-in (ER_EFFECTS_MENU_WINDOW_LATCH / ER_EFFECTS_C30_DIAG). M3 real-shader render finding (2026-06-25): dxil-spirv (HansKristian) does NOT preserve HLSL resource NAMES in its SPIR-V output -- er-objectkit::spirv_reflect on a real ER ppo found 0/37 named bindings. A colour-pass ER pixel shader binds ~23 textures + ~14 buffers (set 0). So you CANNOT map those textures to material roles (albedo/normal/...) from the translated SPIR-V alone. To bind material textures to the correct registers OFFLINE you must parse the DX container's RDEF reflection chunk (which DOES carry resource names like C_Detail3Blend__snp_Texture2D_7_AlbedoMap, matching matbin sampler names) and use dxil-spirv's deterministic D3D-register->SPIR-V (set,binding) mapping. ALTERNATIVELY (and more completely) a RenderDoc frame capture gives the texture->register binding AND the engine cbuffer VALUES (cbInstanceData/IBL/shadow) that offline reflection can never provide. Since the RenderDoc capture recipe is now proven (see renderdoc-capture-recipe-wayland-2026-06-25), the ER capture is the higher-leverage path to a correct real-shader render than building an RDEF parser + guessing engine resources. spirv_reflect already extracts set/binding/kind (Texture/Buffer/StorageBuffer) + input locations [0..4] for the vertex stage.

ZERO-INPUT title-advance lever (option c, 2026-06-21 static RE). The global menu-accept byte 0x144589bdc (RVA 0x4589bdc) is the decoded 'a button was accepted' flag the input pipeline sets on press. It is READ via a 1-instruction getter get_global_accept 0x140e85f50 (movzbl eax,[0x144589bdc]; ret), called from 23 sites incl. the title menu accept-gates 0x1409b1244 / 0x1409b304a / 0x1409a69a3 (near registrar 0x1409b24e0). TitleTopDialog::update calls the getter and, when the byte is non-zero, runs registrar 0x1409b24e0 (open main menu: [dialog+0xa40]=1, advance FD4 [dialog+0xa60], build Continue/Load-Game(d180)/NewGame + transfer focus -> select-layer/router_this build).
LEVER: set 0x144589bdc=1 zero-input at the parked press-any-button title (SimpleTitleStep inner state==10) -> TitleTopDialog::update advances NATURALLY (vs the registrar-self-fire which opened a COMPETING dialog and reverted = user's flash-then-revert). This is the ToS-style 'satisfy the accept side-effect' -- NOT a synthesized DInput/keystate/XInput event (it is the high-level accept flag, like the ToS-accepted flag). CAVEAT: 0x144589bdc is GLOBAL/shared across menus; set it at the title only (only the title is active there). Unverified whether one set (edge, consumed once) advances or it must persist until the menu opens.
WIRING (mirror selectbot_probe_once lever-1 which sets the DIFFERENT proceed latch 0x143d856a0): add TITLE_GLOBAL_ACCEPT_BYTE_RVA=0x4589bdc + a title_accept_byte_gate_enabled() env/file gate, set the byte in selectbot_probe_once at state==TITLE_STEP_MENU_JOB_WAIT(10), and add the gate to process_autoload_request's selectbot trigger. Then verify-only 120s telemetry-only probe: watch menu_opened_latch + MENU_ROUTER_THIS (CAP_CSMENU_CTOR hook 0x1409060d0) to confirm router_this builds zero-input. Related: router-this-select-layer-is-zeroinput-callable-lambda-2026, corrected-autoload-converges-on-menu-select-layer-input-focus-wall-2026.

TOOLING/OPS (2026-06-16, banon/CachyOS er-effects-rs). The ER runtime-probe teardown MUST kill the FULL wine helper set, not just the game exe + winedevice/xalia. Each Proton ER launch spawns a complete wine helper set: services.exe, plugplay.exe, svchost.exe, explorer.exe, rpcss.exe, tabtip.exe, winedevice.exe, xalia.exe (+ conhost/start). Killing only eldenring.exe/start_protected_game.exe/proton/wineserver LEAVES all of these; they ACCUMULATE across runs (saw dozens of sets, PIDs spanning the session) and exhaust the wineserver client limit -> launch stdout shows 'Maximum number of clients reached' AND wine-mono 'Couldn't create thread. Error 0x5' (init_finalizer_thread), after which NO new ER launch can boot (game process never starts, autoload log stays empty). This is the concrete mechanism behind runtime-degradation-wine-orphans / degradation-can-occur-without-process-orphans. FIX: /tmp/er-teardown.sh now pkill -9 -f each of {start_protected_game.exe eldenring.exe ersc_launcher.exe offline-launcher.exe winedevice.exe xalia.exe services.exe plugplay.exe svchost.exe explorer.exe rpcss.exe tabtip.exe conhost.exe start.exe StarCitizen.exe wineserver} then proton, verifies 0 remaining. These .exe names are wine-only on Linux so pkill is safe; do NOT touch steamwebhelper_sniper_wrap / pressure-vessel libexec (the Steam client). Run this teardown BETWEEN EVERY launch. If it ever still can't boot after a clean (0 remaining) sweep, the prefix itself is degraded -> ask user to restart Steam/ER stack. Related: runtime-degradation-wine-orphans, degradation-can-occur-without-process-orphans, er-runtime-probe-fast-teardown.

STATIC EXHAUSTED + CLEANER PLAN (2026-06-18, self-verified via scripts/disas-deobf.sh on eldenring-deobf.bin, NOT memo-trust). CONFIRMED the [dialog+0xa48] entry-registry layout is NOT cleanly statically resolvable: list-register 0x1407ab370 builds a stack temp + defers to container-append 0x1407a6c00(rcx=dialog,rdx=&temp), which itself dispatches through virtual calls (call *(rax) on functor vtables) + 0x1407a6a00 templated-container machinery. The +0xa48 store is buried under multiple obfuscated virtual-dispatch levels -> not worth further static decode (validates the prior 'Arxan-unresolvable' note, now first-hand).

CLEANER PLAN (avoids needing the layout at all): the Load-Game node is a CS::MenuMemberFuncJob<TitleTopDialog> with vtable 0x142b265d0. So a SAVE-SAFE READ-ONLY runtime diagnostic can, at the rendered main menu, validate owner+0xe0 is the TitleTopDialog (vtable 0x142b26468) then SCAN a bounded window of the dialog object (and/or [dialog+0xa48] pointer) for qwords pointing at objects whose [0]==0x142b265d0; for each candidate log [node+0x18] (member-fn VA), [node+0x10] (dialog), [node+0x20] (this-adjust). Offline-disasm each member-fn VA to find the one whose body reaches thunk 0x140820c60 -> factory 0x14081ead0 = the Load-Game node. Then (Model B) call run 0x1409aaba0(rcx=node) to fire it -> LIVE ProfileLoadDialog -> native pump. Pure ReadProcessMemory + one native call; NO writes, NO SetState until the gated native continue. Related: title-load-is-profileloaddialog-NOT-movemapliststep-b78-dead-2026, autoload-CURRENT-STATE-and-next-action-2026, load_activate-builds-selector-into-queue-final-wall-is-dialog-not-live-screen-2026.

VALIDATED (from run-drive debug logs 2026-07-20): load2 world resource DOES load -- WORLDRES-GETTER key=0x1c000000 reaches +0x2d(ready)=1, +0x35(phase)=10, fc8_90(data)= a VALID pointer (not null), in_world=true. So the "phase-2 stalls if data null" condition is NOT met -- the destination/world IS loaded for load2 (consistent with present=True + movable). This DISPROVES the world-teardown / no-destination idea. The load2 non-completion is therefore NOT a resource/streaming/destination wait: it is purely the MoveMapStep FINALIZE state machine never getting its cVar10 end-of-load trigger (case 0: if cVar10==0 return -> fin stays 0 -> mms stays 18), while forcing the finalize (writing fin=5) SOFTLOCKS. So the remaining unknown is narrow + specific: why load1s cVar10 becomes 1 at end-of-load (the natural trigger firing) and load2s does not. That is a targeted decompile of load1s end-of-load path (which of the 9 cVar10 inputs load1 sets, and why the reload path does not), validated on a clean run -- NOT a forced write (proven to softlock).

So the 'fresh FixOrderJobSequence / reset-count' fix proposed below is NOT the breakthrough it sounds like. The honest state: forge dead; three NATIVE zero-input levers UNTESTED -- (1) member-node run 0x1409aaba0(rcx=Continue_node) at the live menu (native_continue_tick, gated off, never cleanly probed); (2) native builder FUN_1409a7070 + tfc+0x14c (NEVER invoked in src/); (3) direct primitive 0x14067b750(-1,0,0) (Continue action 0x14082bac0's own first call, CONTINUE_LOAD_RVA src/lib.rs:746). Pursue those before any more forge-overflow fixing. See autoload-confirm-fork-verdict-20260623. ORIGINAL STATUS FOLLOWS: --- OVERNIGHT END STATUS (2026-06-23 ~01:30, autonomous). PROVEN AUTONOMOUS (no driving): boot -> self-advance press-any-button (maybe_auto_open_menu fires open-menu registrar 0x1409b24e0 on Loop+a40==0, needs er-effects-offline.txt) -> FORGE the LoadGame job (force tfc+0x18c=0, selector 0x1409a8eb0 -> dispatcher 0x1409b3070 BUILD branch) -> install into owner+0x130 via CS::MenuJob::Assign 0x1407a9460 -> the native FD4 menu pump 0x1407aa2bb RUNS it in-context. Live probe autoload-live-pump-20260614 (zero simulated input, method=direct_bootstrap_pump): OSCILLATES state 0<->1 and never loads. Cycle = combined_load(-1,0,1) sets save_state 0->1, then save_load_pump_default 0x679510 holds state=1 ~2 ticks, state resets to 0, repeat (1777 attempts in 30s, player_available never true, title_bootstrap_seen stayed false). Findings: 0x679510 is NOT the menu-task pump that advances/consumes the load (only holds/resets save_state=1); 0x67a310 'title bootstrap marker' does NOT flip title_bootstrap_seen (gated on map_load_67bc10 hook success) so it is not the real title-accept. Load primitives cannot complete unless the game is in the real post-title main-menu state where the native MoveMapList task runs. Do not retry direct_bootstrap_pump or more direct load-primitive/pump permutations unchanged; pursue the native title-accept -> main-menu transition (the one map_load_67bc10 participates in). Related: [[autoload-stall-state0-precondition]].

RUNTIME+STATIC RE (2026-06-22): the ~20-26s crash blocking the own-load runs is a FixOrderJobSequence capacity-8 DLFixedVector OVERFLOW, NOT a missing-splash-semaphore (PROVEN: identical assert a0=0x29c9bf0(DLFixedVector.inl) line=0x296 msg=0x3295988('out of memory.') -> AV 0x1eb9999, identical caller chain [0x734015,0x733f0f,0x7ad540(Scaleform job Run 0x1407ad2b0),0x7916d5(menu-job Run FUN_1407915b0),0x7924d0,0x7aa2bb(MakeFixOrderJobSequencePointer 0x1407a9f30)] in BOTH the splash-skip run (+19699ms) and the non-splash run (+26048ms) -- splash-skip only changes TIMING, not the crash). The assert site FUN_1407340e0 (PushBackJob/DLFixedVector::push, deobf 0x7340e0):  -> the jobs vector at this+0x48 is a FIXED capacity-8 count; a 9th PushBackJob overflows. MakeFixOrderJobSequence (0x1407a9830) pushes exactly 8 jobs (params 2..9) which fits, so a 9th job is being added elsewhere during the title Scaleform menu-job processing. Control run (DLL attached, splash-skip on, own_load=0, own_stepper=0) in progress to confirm native-vs-ours. Splash-skip itself WORKS and is now a product feature (own_load arm) -- it is orthogonal to this crash. RenderDoc capture recipe on Hyprland/Wayland (PROVEN 2026-06-25, self-served headlessly by agent). BLOCKER: RenderDoc does NOT support capturing a client running directly on Wayland (qrenderdoc logs 'Running directly on Wayland is NOT SUPPORTED' and captures nothing -> no .rdc written; this is why earlier GUI capture attempts produced no files). FIX: force the captured app onto XWayland by UNSETTING WAYLAND_DISPLAY (winit/Bevy then uses X11 on DISPLAY=:0); run qrenderdoc itself with QT_QPA_PLATFORM=xcb. WORKING headless recipe (no GUI), self-served by the agent in its own shell (DISPLAY=:0 available): added an in-app RenderDoc trigger to er-shader-viewer via the renderdoc=0.12 crate behind --rdc-capture <path> (RenderDoc::<V141>::new().trigger_capture() at frame 90, exit at 130); then: env -u WAYLAND_DISPLAY DISPLAY=:0 renderdoccmd capture -w -c /tmp/er-cap target/debug/er-shader-viewer --object c4800 --rdc-capture /tmp/er-cap -> wrote /tmp/er-cap_frame89.rdc (223MB, c4800 18/18 textured rendered). Convert headlessly: renderdoccmd convert -f X.rdc -i rdc -c zip.xml -o out.zip.xml -> plain XML (459 Vulkan chunks: draws, 24 vkUpdateDescriptorSets, 20 vkCreateDescriptorSetLayout, 6 vkCreateShaderModule, vkCmdBindDescriptorSets). Texture/buffer DATA (223MB) is in a separate blob store, not the 749KB structure XML. NOTE: renderdoccmd has NO replay/python scripting and the Arch renderdoc pkg ships only librenderdoc.so (no headless python module); resolved cbuffer CONTENTS need either qrenderdoc GUI Python Shell (scripts/renderdoc/extract_frame.py) or parsing the convert XML + blob store. For ER (M4): same recipe on the gated offline EAC-free eldenring.exe under Proton forced to XWayland; cannot add in-app trigger to ER so capture via F12 or qrenderdoc(xcb)+.cap queuedFrameCap. Agent cannot launch the gated game; user runs that. See scripts/renderdoc/README.md.

EXHAUSTIVE WALL (2026-06-15). The press-any-button accept genuinely requires the leaf input node to DETECT a real input from its polled snapshot; no in-process non-HID force/inject reproduces it. Tried, all clean-but-ineffective or crashing: (1) latch 0x143d856a0 force -> CRASH (skips bootstrap). (2) keystate bitmap inputmgr+0x90 shotgun -> no effect (leaf reads earlier snapshot). (3) event array inputmgr+0xdc shotgun -> no effect. (4) accept-predicate 0x1407a9200 hook forced true (confirmed CALLED ~2/tick at state 10, predicate_fires climbed to 2000+) -> still latch/CSFeMan stayed 0: forcing the verdict does NOT set the real out-values the combiner/pump require (pump does J->update->predicate->cmp rdi,rbx->vtable[0] accept action; the action needs genuine accepted state, not a forced bool). So the inner TitleStep IS pumped (predicate fires constantly) but the accept only fires on real leaf detection. CONCLUSION: zero-HID-input title-accept is reachable only by injecting at the exact input-POLL/snapshot stage the leaf reads (deep, mod/version-fragile RE -- conflicts with the compatibility requirement), OR by a synthesized real input event the game's poll consumes (robust + compatible across vanilla/seamless/mods, but it IS simulated input). The COMPATIBLE+ROBUST product requirement and the strict zero-input metric are in tension: the universal mechanism is a synthesized input. Reliable owner-at-state-10 + the predicate hook infra are in place. Related: title-accept-input-pipeline-wall, autoload-dll-product-requirements.

IDENTITY CORRECTION + path confirm (2026-06-21, clean live winedbg capture at 0x14240e568, backtrace=our read #3 0x14067b1cf). The save read job's path is built from [job+0xe0] = a HEAP/REQUEST object = 0x7ffe937856d0, which EQUALS io18 = [iodev+0x18] (the in-flight request handle from the FULL-INIT log 'io18=0x7ffe937856d0'). It is NOT the static 0x1445893b0. => The long-standing assumption 'iodev = *(0x144589390) = 0x1445893b0' is the WRONG object for the PATH/read; my mount 0x140e6e8d0(0x1445893b0) + dev40/dev30 reads were on the static (likely CSTrophy-adjacent) object, explaining why the 'mount' looked like a no-op. The REQUEST object (0x7ffe937856d0) is what the worker path-builder slot2 0x142410cd0 reads.

Captured at the request obj (0x7ffe937856d0): +0xe0 = 0x143b405b8 (a STATIC .data ptr, also == r9 at the hit -- likely a path-provider/dir object or dir-string data ptr), +0xe8 = 0x0032006c0073002e = UTF-16 '.sl2' (the extension component, SSO), +0xf0 = 0x0 (a size field = 0 => the directory/name component appears EMPTY), +0xf8 = 0x4. Registers at hit: rax/r15=job=0x7fffb09fea90, rdx=0xffffffff, r9=0x143b405b8, r13=0x1445893b0 (static, NOT the path obj), r14=0x144852fa0 (worker mgr).

INTERPRETATION (partial): the request carries '.sl2' but the directory component is empty/zero-size => invalid path '%s\%s%s%s' with empty dir => open 0x142415200 fails device-readiness 0x141edc930 (vtbl+0x98) -> error 5 before ReadFile (or readiness 0x14240d5c0 -> code 8). AMBIGUITY REMAINING: whether the failure is the EMPTY DIRECTORY (path) vs the DEVICE-READINESS check (vtbl+0x98) -- 0x143b405b8 at +0xe0 might be a valid path-provider, making readiness the real fail. NEXT: (1) decode the std::string layout at the request obj more precisely (dir component offset) + read its chars live; (2) decode device-readiness 0x141edc930 / vtbl+0x98 -- what it checks + why it fails cold; (3) find the LIVE setter that populates the request dir / makes the device ready (the step the cold submit skips), then run/replicate it cold. FIX still = make the worker resolve a valid path on a ready device. Relates: b80-ROOTCAUSE-worker-empty-iodev-dir-string-e0-2026-06-21, b80-GATE2-PASSES-read-enqueues-fails-in-worker-2026-06-21.

SELF-DRIVE INPUT SOLVED (2026-07-17, DLL 82eb5e59, commit 6baa0be). ROOT CAUSE of all prior 'no key opens the menu' failures: the sq-repro ER-window finder picked the LARGEST visible window owned by the process, which is OUR OWN fullscreen D3D12 present-overlay (class 'ErEffectsLoadingOverlay', title 'er-effects loading', 3840x2160) -- so every SetForegroundWindow + SendInput went to OUR overlay, never the game. FIX: sq_repro_find_hwnd_cb now SKIPS windows whose class contains 'ErEffects'/'er-effects' and picks the largest remaining -> selects class 'ELDEN RING(TM)'. RUNTIME CONFIRMED: window now 'ELDEN RING(TM)'; VK 0x1b (Esc) opens 02_000_IngameTop on the FIRST candidate; UP(0x26)+Enter(0x0d) opens the OptionSetting quit submenu. So the SendInput->RawInput->foreground channel WORKS and Esc/arrows/Enter are the correct native menu keys (they only 'failed' before because they hit our overlay). Force-foreground via AttachThreadInput is needed (headless me3 launch leaves the game non-foreground; is_foreground=1 achieved). REMAINING self-drive gap: TO_PROFILE needs the TAB-SWITCH key to reach the Quit tab (OPTIONSETTING_QUIT_TAB_INDEX=8, watch OPTIONSETTING_CURRENT_TAB); LB was mapped to VK 0x51('Q') as a guess -- unverified. Once on tab 8, DOWN,DOWN,Enter activates Load Profile -> ProfileSelect; then TO_SLOT drives the cursor to slot 0 (angrE, area 0x1c) and CONFIRM (Enter) arms the switch -> step-3 repro. XInput-not-routed + DInput-keyboard-not-read findings in [[native-er-input-routing-selfdrive-2026-07-17]] still hold (keyboard must be SendInput/RawInput; those were separate from the overlay bug). Supersedes the earlier 'SendInput doesn't open the menu' conclusion in this key.

FALSE-PASS-stale-can_move-on-deser-flip-2026-07-18: BUG (run samechar-3x-220757). The 3-load run PASSED but it was a FALSE PASS: report showed load2 (deser=1) can_move=True moved_frames=60 with first_seen=64.3s and run end=64.7s -- load2 could NOT prove 60 frames in 0.4s. CAUSE: CAN_MOVE_CONFIRMED latched from load1 (deser=0); when fresh_deser flipped 0->1 (load2's deserialize COMMITTING during LOADING, not in-world), the telemetry oracle read the STILL-LATCHED can_move (from load1) BEFORE the move-probe's next tick reset it for the new epoch -> the watcher misattributed load1's movement to load2 -> require-reload-move satisfied -> false PASS. Also load2 at 64s is mid-reload (not in-world), so it definitely had not been move-tested. FIX: oracle emits oracle_can_move = CAN_MOVE_CONFIRMED AND (MOVE_PROBE_EPOCH == current fresh_deser) -- so a proof from a prior epoch reads False the instant deser changes, until the probe re-proves for the new load in-world. The load1 movement result is REAL (gate-fix works); only the load2 attribution was stale. Milestone NOT yet met -- need load2/load3 to ACTUALLY prove movement in-world. Links [[move-probe-wrongly-skipped-during-WAIT_WORLD-2026-07-18]].

FACTORY 0x14081ead0 ABI DEFINITIVELY DECODED (2026-06-18, full disasm, ends the guessing). Call graph: thunk 0x140820c60 (add rcx,8; jmp factory) -> rcx=functor+8=CAPTURE slot. Factory: mov rdx->rsi; mov rcx->r14; alloc 0x1cd0 dialog; call 0x140745280(rcx=rsi) builds a TRANSIENT SceneObjProxy FROM rsi; mov r8,(r14)=*(capture); ctor 0x1409a3d90(rcx=dialog, rdx=newproxy, r8=*(capture)). 0x140745280 -> 0x14074a700(rcx=proxy, rdx=MenuWindow, r8=MenuWindow+8): stores SceneObjProxy vtable 0x142a94a70 at proxy+0, and MenuWindow at proxy+0x20 (0x14074a735 mov rbx,0x20(rsi)).

VERDICTS:
(A) CAPTURE rcx = TitleTopDialog+0xa38 is CORRECT. *(capture) = CS::TitleFlowContext (vt 0x142ac7f20); ctor 0x1409a3d90 EXPECTS r8 = a TitleFlowContext* (saves it 0x1409a3df2, flows into 0x1409a51c0) -- NOT a SceneProxy. The "capture must be a SceneObjProxy / derive MenuWindow from *(capture)+0x20" hypothesis is REFUTED -- *(capture) is a TitleFlowContext and is the correct r8. The Load-Game functor (vt 0x142ac3ea8, installed by 0x1409ad1d7 via mov [r15+0xa38]->r8) captures exactly this [dialog+0xa38] TitleFlowContext. So td+0xa38 is NOT a red herring -- it is the right capture.
(B) rdx = MenuWindow* -- a REQUIRED, SEPARATE arg, NOT derivable from the capture/TitleFlowContext (which has no MenuWindow in [0..0x40], probe-5). Confirmed.
(C) The MenuWindow value is the ONLY runtime-only unknown. Verified source = hook SceneObjProxy ctor 0x14074a700, latch its rdx (the engine-true MenuWindow that lands at proxy+0x20).

OPEN/RISK: probe-7 hooked 0x14074a700 but its latch logged ONLY on a valid MenuWindow-vtable match -> it never logged, so we cannot tell "0x14074a700 never fired at the title" from "fired but rdx not a clean MenuWindow vtable". CRITICAL because if 0x14074a700 does NOT fire at the parked title (no SceneObjProxy built until a dialog-with-scene opens = chicken/egg, since WE only build one by calling the factory we are trying to set up), then the MenuWindow is genuinely unobtainable headlessly and the leaf-factory approach is architecturally blocked (the MenuWindow may only exist during the input-driven menu transition). NEXT PROBE (the user-authorized "1 probe"): instrument 0x14074a700 to log EVERY entry unconditionally (rdx + *(rdx) + match?) -> definitively answer whether it fires at the title + what rdx is. If it fires with a valid MenuWindow -> latch + call factory. If it NEVER fires -> confirms the architectural block -> pivot to a higher-level / non-MenuWindow load path. Related: live-dialog-probe7-latch-empty-ABI-never-decoded-thrash-2026, live-dialog-probe6-factory-fires-returns-dialog-rdx-not-menuwindow-2026.

STATIC RE (ER 1.16.1, deobf base 0x140000000). DLFixedVector "out of memory" assert (DLFixedVector.inl line 0x296=662) that kills our zero-input Continue load.

OVERFLOWING VECTOR = the FixOrderJobSequence.jobs DLFixedVector<MenuJob*,8> (the SAME sequence being pumped by FD4), NOT a private sub-vector.
- FixOrderJobSequence (Ghidra dump struct): size 0x68; +0x10 currentJobIndex(int); +0x18 jobs = FixOrderJobSequenceVector(80B).
- FixOrderJobSequenceVector: MenuJob*[8] at +0x0 (=seq+0x18..+0x58); count(longlong) at +0x48 (=seq+0x60). cap 8.
- Push fn (deobf 0x140733ff0): mov [rcx+0x48]=count; inc; cmp 8; ja DLPanic(line 0x296,"out of memory."@0x143295988); then incq [rcx+0x48]. rcx = jobs-vector base (=&seq->jobs = seq+0x18, so [rcx+0x48]=count). Region 0x140733xxx NOT shifted vs dump.
- Insert orchestrator (deobf 0x140733ef0): null-checks MenuJob* then calls push 0x140733ff0. rcx=vector, rdx=&value.

WHO OVERFLOWS IT: CS::MenuWindowJob::Run (deobf 0x1407ad2b0; Ghidra-named). At deobf 0x1407ad537: mov rcx=[this+0x50]; mov rdx=[this+0x130]=owningMenuWindow; call 0x140733ef0 -> pushes owningMenuWindow into vector [MenuWindowJob+0x50]. MenuWindowJob (size 0x138): +0x50 field2_0x50 (ctor sets from ctor param_2 = caller-supplied &sequence->jobs), +0x130 owningMenuWindow. MenuWindowJob::Run, running AS a chained job INSIDE a FixOrderJobSequence, registers its just-created MenuWindow back into that SAME sequence's jobs vector. If sequence already holds 8 -> overflow at push #9.

OUR FLOW: installed job (vtable 0x142ac7200) = CS::MenuJobWithContext<LoadJobContext,lambda> (ctor dump 0x140821df0 alloc 0x70; dtor dump 0x140824250). Run = deobf 0x140826e10: checks [this+0x60] started-flag, calls lazy-build 0x140828360 -> FUN_1408249b0 -> invokes stored lambda (dump 0x14082aa60, builds profile/load child via FUN_1408289d0), stores child at [this+0x70]; then calls child Run via vtable[2]. Child chain eventually reaches a MenuWindowJob whose +0x50 we (via lambda/factory) point at owner+0x130's already-full jobs vector -> assert.

CALL CHAIN (deobf, bottom-up): assert<-0x140734015<-0x140733f0f(orchestrator ret)<-0x1407ad53b(MenuWindowJob::Run push site)<-0x1407916d5(FUN_1407915b0 MenuJobWithContext::Run; calls child via [+0x18] vtable[2])<-0x1407aa2bb(FD4 FixOrderJobSequence pump: iterates idx[seq+0x10] vs count[seq+0x60], elem[seq+0x18+idx*8], calls each vtable[2]=Run; pump deobf entry 0x1407aa1f6)<-0x1407924d0.

NATIVE Continue: title TitleTopDialog confirm (deobf 0x1409a8eb0 = dump FUN_1409a8e20) -> dispatcher (deobf 0x1409b3070 = dump FUN_1409b3050): shuts down net/EOS/server, FUN_14082d1c0(TitleFlowContext init w/ save slot), FUN_1409b3750, FUN_140815370(1e8). Native does NOT push the load MenuWindow into the title 8-item sequence; the title dialog transitions OUT (its own sequence torn down) so the load job runs in a sequence with room.

FIX (zero-input):
Preferred (b): make the MenuWindowJob built by our LoadGame job use a FRESH FixOrderJobSequence for its +0x50 (Run then pushes into an empty cap-8 vector). Build via MakeFixOrderJobSequence (dump 0x1407a9830) / MakeFixOrderJobSequencePointer (dump 0x1407a9f30) / ChainMenuJobs (dump 0x1407a7ca0); PushBackJob dump 0x1407aa470. These are in the shifted 0x1407a9xxx/0x1407aaxxx region -> re-ground by prologue before CALL.
Alt (a) simplest no-alloc: immediately before our job pumps, RESET the target sequence jobs.count to 0 at seq+0x60 (== jobs-vector-base +0x48); also clear stale MenuJob* slots+drop refs to avoid leak. Cleaner = fresh sequence.
Cap-8 panic msg "out of memory."@0x143295988; path str@0x1429c9bf0 (DLFixedVector.inl).
Regions 0x140733xxx, 0x1407adxxx, 0x140826xxx entry-aligned w/ dump; 0x1407a9xxx/0x1407aaxxx per-region shifted.

NATIVE DRAW: BOTH shaders execute end-to-end (2026). MILESTONE: real AEG301_012 FLVER geometry now runs through the game's native .vpo AND .ppo via wgpu passthrough on lavapipe, NO crashes. The .ppo (full ER forward+ lighting pixel shader) executes to completion. Required FIVE SPIR-V patches (er-shaderkit/src/spirv_patch.rs) + texture-dim reflection + the binding fix:
1. neutralize_draw_parameters (gl_BaseInstance=0)
2. force_readonly_ssbo_loads_zero (instance/light SSBO loads=0)
3. compact_descriptor_bindings (vertex-only, sparse->contiguous)
4. compact_descriptor_bindings_unified (had a COLLISION bug — superseded)
5. assign_unique_bindings (THE pixel fix): dxil-spirv emits the D3D register model where t1/s1/b1 all collide at binding 1 and cbuffers reuse registers across stages — invalid in a merged Vulkan pipeline. Assign EVERY resource a globally-unique contiguous binding (no sharing); returns per-module (old,new,storage_class). Pixel layout went from collisions to clean 0..43.
PLUS render.rs parse_image_bindings(): reflect each texture's OpTypeImage dim (2D/Cube/3D) + sample type and stub a MATCHING 1x1 texture (a 2D stub for a cube/3D sampler segfaults llvmpipe).

WHY STILL BLACK (0% non-black): the .ppo has NO discard (OpKill=0), writes 2 targets (SV_Target0 + SV_Target_1, forward/G-buffer), reads SV_IsFrontFace + varyings. It outputs black because its inputs are zeroed/gray-stubbed: 8 cbuffers (cbSceneParam/cbLight/cbMatDynParam/cbModelParam/...), IBL CUBE maps (g_IBLTexture x2), 8 3D irradiance volumes, volume fog 3D, tiled light StructuredBuffers (g_LightBuffer/SpotLight/Indices/Lookup), SSAO, decals, shadow maps. Writing bright FC_EmissiveColor (cbMatDynParam@+48, found at new binding 13 via the Uniform at old reg 2) did NOT surface -> the shader doesn't combine emissive simply, OR colour is in target 1 (harness reads target 0 only), OR multiplied by zeroed exposure.

CONCLUSION for the goal ('what the game renders, NOT an approximation'): the MECHANISM is done (both native shaders run on real geometry). Faithful SHADING needs the game's ACTUAL per-scene lighting environment (IBL cubemaps, baked GI irradiance volumes, the tiled light list, shadow maps) — captured engine state, NOT synthesizable offline. A synthetic light gives an approximation. The exact render path = capture a real frame's cbuffers+textures (RenderDoc / runtime DLL hook) and replay through the native shaders. NEXT cheap wins to try: read target 1 (maybe colour is there); RE the cbSceneParam exposure/FC_ fields + cbLight; provide a directional light at cbLight + FC_ShadowDir(cbSceneParam@560).

CRITICAL-render-oracle-is-false-negative-real-signal-is-movement-2026-07-18: PIVOTAL (user screenshot + live telemetry, autoload-only NO_SQREPRO run). The user's screenshot shows angrE FULLY RENDERED + in-world at Midra's Manse and the user is MOVING it (havok_pos jumps several units/sample: [-16,-14]->[-22,-11]->[-24,-13]). CONCLUSIONS: (1) The AUTOLOAD-ONLY path RENDERS + is fully PLAYABLE. sq-repro's boot arming (input block + driving from boot) is what broke load1 in prior sq-repro runs, NOT the load. (2) MY RENDER ORACLE IS BROKEN (false negatives): draw_group_enabled / player_render_ready / fake_loading_any_visible / now_loading / request_code(1-vs-2) all read 'bad' even for a visibly-rendered, user-controllable load. request_code=1 + mms=18 is a GOOD PLAYABLE state, NOT a freeze. (3) The RELIABLE discriminator good-vs-frozen is HAVOK_POS MOVING under input (playable) vs STATIC (frozen) -- exactly the can_move probe signal. NEXT: (a) FIX/replace the render-ready oracle -- find the field that truly reflects drawn+playable (chr_onscreen=TRUE here is a candidate; draw_group_enabled offset is likely wrong for 1.16.2); (b) gate can_move probe on a RELIABLE present+world-live signal, NOT the broken render_ready; (c) re-test reloads with corrected oracles. The FD4 async-IO thread-pool subsystem (the cold save-IO drain crux) is LAZILY SELF-BUILDING with NO session/scheduler gate seen across the chain -- so it looks COLD-BUILDABLE (like the worldres driver emk-resman-streaming-driver-coldbuild). Chain: pool builder 0x14240afe0 (gate [0x144853048]==0): builds via 0x141ec7220 + lock 0x141ed6210 + 0x141ecd830 + builds stream task 0x144842d40 via 0x141eceb10; stores pool @0x144853048. Sub-build 0x141ecd830 (gate [0x144842d48]==0) -> 0x141ed1620 = THE deeper builder (the likely CreateThread/worker-thread spawn; trace 0x141ed1620 next for the actual thread start). All FD4-primitive based, no 0x144588e98/session deref in the chain.

THE NOVEL UNTESTED LEVER (save-safe, the key insight): prior cold experiments (cold-b80-char-mount-dead-even-with-worker / own-stepper-runtime-milestone) built the stream TASK 0x144842d40 (via the 0x140b0a980 stub, which calls 0x141eceb10) but did NOT necessarily build the POOL 0x144853048 itself (via 0x14240afe0) -- the pool is what owns the DRAINING worker threads. So 'registered the worker, read still didn't drain' may be because the TASK existed but the POOL (and its threads) did not. UNTESTED: cold-build the POOL by calling 0x14240afe0() directly, THEN drive a save-read (preview 0x14067b4e0, save-safe no-write) and poll whether the IO-request state advances to 0x14 (resident) / b80->3. If the pool threads spawn cold and drain the read -> the SAVE-DATA mount becomes possible COLD ZERO-INPUT (no SetState, no session, no save write) -- which would un-block the whole title-Load chain (feed slot into the direct-built dialog's selector / 0x14067b290 deser). 

NEXT B (save-safe, decisive): (1) finish tracing 0x141ed1620 -> confirm it spawns worker threads + whether cold-callable (no asserts). (2) SAVE-SAFE runtime probe: read [0x144853048] cold (pool built? expect null); if null call 0x14240afe0() to cold-build the pool+threads; then preview-read a slot + poll b80/IO-request-state. This is the make-or-break for whether the save-IO can drain cold WITHOUT the in-context SetState(5) (avoiding the save-write risk entirely). If the pool drains cold -> B is solved save-safely; if not (pool needs session activation), fall to the in-context SetState(5)+IngameInit-tail path (save-risky, careful). ROOT: 0x67b200 is SESSION-GATED (0x1402567b0 session getter + 0x140261cd0 slot-in-session check) and FAILS cold (no session at the title). So the ENTIRE deliverable now gates on standing up the session: either (a) build the cascade cold (0x143d76060 -> 0x140def650 -> 0x144588e98 + the session 0x1402567b0 returns), reversing the session-mgr init table 0x143d85760 / ctor 0x140dec7c0 / bootstrap 0x140deb0b0 (menu-mount-zero-input-recipe), OR (b) trigger the NATIVE session bootstrap (the title-accept path, frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned) without the menu. CONCURRENT ER LAUNCH (2026-06-23, run 194217, user playing another game). RESULT: the direct/offline eldenring.exe Proton launch DOES start while another game runs -- Steam's single-game lock does NOT apply to the direct exe path (window present, class steam_app_1245620, pid live). The focus-independent watcher mode works: RUNTIME_SKIP_VISUAL_CAPTURE=1 -> --skip-visual-capture drops the target_window_capture_unsafe bail; the run was NOT torn down for the unfocused window.

BLOCKER: the game STALLS as a background/occluded window. game_task_ticks=1, reason=title_stalled, teardown +6.5s. The DLL applied foreground-force (IsGameInForeground 0x14266df00 -> mov al,1;ret, line confirmed in debug log) + all hooks by +2.3s on its OWN OS threads, but the GAME frame loop (drives game_task_ticks via CSTaskImp) ticked once then stalled. Cause: Hyprland/Wayland does NOT send frame callbacks to an unfocused/occluded/off-workspace surface -> swapchain present blocks -> frame loop starves. foreground-force defeats ER's INTERNAL focus-pause but NOT the compositor frame-callback gating. ER window was on workspace 1, focusHistoryID=14 (not focused) while the user's game had focus.

FIX PATH (untested): render ER on a Hyprland HEADLESS virtual output (hyprctl output create headless) + move the ER window (by class steam_app_1245620 only -- privacy) to a workspace bound to that output, so it always has a visible surface getting frame callbacks independent of the user's focused game. Caveat: creating a virtual monitor mutates the user's live compositor session (user-visible) -- get consent before doing it mid-game. Relates always-block-input-zero-input-invariant, teardown-screenshot-semaphore-loop-2026-06-23.

MsgRepository::Format constructs MenuString from a literal wchar_t*, so the row uses literal label/help without FMG edits. The row action is identified by the cloned action impl pointer and routed to ShellExecuteW(open, parent(ER_EFFECTS_SAVE_FILE)); Wine/Proton should delegate directory open to the host/default file manager. No Elden Ring runtime launch was used for this change; validation was cargo fmt --check + cargo xwin check.

DELIVERABLE (2026-07-01): tooling to make Arxan CFG trampolines readable in eldenring-deobf.bin. MECHANISM (verified): Arxan turns 6207 (2.6% of 235823 .pdata) function ENTRIES into 'jmp <arxan .text>' thunks + dead bytes; the real instruction stream is CONTROL-FLOW-FLATTENED/dispersed across game .text (0x140001000..0x1429a3000) and TWO Arxan-added exec sections (.interpr 0x1429a3000+0xc000, second .text 0x144c0e000+0x11f3800) linked by unconditional jmps; call/ret are replaced by lea rsp/mov [rsp]/jmp [rsp-8] stack gadgets; obfuscated dispatch targets are hidden in movabs reg,<codeaddr> pushed for ret-based branching. dearxan does NOT undo this (it only decrypts). NOTE the 'trampoline' thunks were INVISIBLE to the earlier .pdata garbage-scan because a clean 'jmp' decodes as valid code -- only 13 misaligned ones showed as garbage.

RESOLUTION: bounded multi-hop follow (entry jmp -> arxan hops -> until branch back into game .text = real continuation, or ret = fully-relocated body). Of 6207: 75.3% stub-return (integrity check returns to thunk+offset continuation), 19.3% reloc-body (whole fn moved to arxan .text), 5.4% chain-deep (unresolved multi-hop). 94.6% cleanly resolved. 4801 have >=1 extracted real code ref.

ARTIFACTS (committed in worktree scripts/): (1) scripts/arxan-thunks.tsv -- VA-only map: thunk_va,size,arxan_target,kind,resolved_va,hops,refs(call:/disp: targets). (2) scripts/deobf-read.py <VA> -- readable disassembler: auto-resolves the entry thunk, follows dispersion jmps transparently, skips dead bytes, recognizes the arxan ret-gadget as ret, flags real call targets; --raw for contrast; capstone via uv. Tools/tsv generators live in dearxan/examples (stub-audit.rs, region-dump.rs, stub-cfg.rs) + job-scratch python (classify/resolve/enrich). CAVEAT: deobf-read is a readability aid (linear trace of one path + annotated branches), not a proven re-lift; conditional branches shown in place, only fall-through followed. Related: dearxan-deobf-validated-and-stub-stats-corrected-2026-07-01, dearxan-completeness-scan-no-missed-regions-2026-07-01.

REFINED CONCLUSION (2026-06-18, no-preview experiment). CORRECTS the over-broad 'cold-b80 wall BROKEN' framing. What the ACTIVATE-byte lever actually unlocked: the slot-active-byte GATE (0x67b200's 0x140261cd0 slot-check now passes) AND the PREVIEW/metadata IO lane drains cold (b80->3). What is STILL walled: the FULL character-save deserialize. 

NO-PREVIEW experiment (skipped 0x67b4e0, let 0x67b200 fire directly with the byte set): 0x67b200 ret=1, b80=2, io18+io20 POPULATED -- BUT the next poll 0x679180 IMMEDIATELY reset b80 2->0 and cleared io (the read never went resident). So 0x67b200's OWN read does NOT go resident cold; the PREVIEW 0x67b4e0 is REQUIRED -- it pre-warms a RESIDENT iodev request that 0x67b200 then reuses (b80->3). 

THE CHAR-APPLY ROOT CAUSE (now precise): the preview 0x67b4e0 reads only slot METADATA (0x60000 buffer), so the RESIDENT iodev request 0x67b200/0x67b290 reuse contains METADATA, NOT slot N's full 0x280000 save. 0x67b290 (allocs 0x280000, reads the resident request via 0x67b100/0x140e6e380) parses the metadata -> header-validate 0x1402624c0 FAILS -> c30 not written, char not applied (c30=0xffffffff, PGD garbage level=371). The FULL-SAVE read lane (the live menu's selector-step 0x140826d50 installer 0x140828270 submitting the 0x280000 read via io18/io20 -> menu_deser 0x14082c240) does NOT drain cold -- it needs the selector/menu-task context. The preview/metadata lane drains cold; the full-save lane does not (the documented async-io-b80-lane-mismatch).

SO the cold save-data half is: GATE solved (ACTIVATE 0x140262250 -> 0x67b200 passes), metadata read drains cold, BUT the full char deserialize still requires the menu selector machinery OR a cold-drainable full-save initiator. NEXT (the real remaining gap): (1) find an initiator that submits a FULL 0x280000 save read into the iodev that drains cold (NOT the 0x60000 preview 0x67b4e0; check 0x67b1a0 full-load type 0xa + how to make ITS read resident -- it queued to file-device-mgr cold before, retest with byte set + worker); (2) OR drive the selector-step 0x140826d50 / menu_deser 0x14082c240 path (which reads the full save via io18/io20 live) -- needs the selector context built (the direct-build dialog's load_activate builds it, tying back to the DIALOG path); (3) the full-save read may fundamentally need the menu/selector to drain, same class as the menu-session wall. The genuine new asset: the slot-byte gate + the IO subsystem are confirmed present/passable cold; the remaining is the full-save READ lane draining cold. Related: COLD-B80-WALL-BROKEN-activate-byte-but-char-not-applied-2026 (its 'wall broken' = metadata lane only), cold-b80-char-apply-is-the-async-lane-mismatch-wall-2026, REFRAME-io-subsystem-present-cold-blocker-is-just-the-active-byte-2026, own-stepper-cold-b80-primitive-needs-preview-not-67b1a0-2026, real-load-c30-mount-write-confirmed-seamless-2026, seamless-co2-menu-deserialize-is-vanilla-0x82c240-2026, stage2-menu-drive-load-recipe-verified-2026.

FPS TEARDOWN FIRED DURING LOAD2 INCOMPLETE LOAD -- present != loaded (user 2026-07-20, run load3): my FPS_REGRESSION teardown fired (load2=20 vs load1=24, 15% drop) but load2 was only PRESENT (in-memory deserialize) with render_ready=False and mms STUCK at 18 -- NOT visually loaded into world. present=True (oracle_player_present) means the character deserialized in memory, NOT that the world finished loading/rendering. So the FPS sample caught the LOADING-phase FPS, not the in-world drop. FIX: gate the load2 FPS sample+teardown on GENUINELY loaded = (oracle_player_render_ready OR oracle_can_move OR mms_state==-1 settled), not just present. Same for the load1 baseline. DEEPER ROOT (still unresolved): load2 did NOT complete this run either -- mms_max=18, render_ready never true, latched=False -- even under VM-core isolation. So load2 completion (mms 18->19->20->-1) remains UNRELIABLE: the cVar10=0 clear holds the movable window (fin=0) but load2 depends on a natural end-of-load finalize trigger whose timing is variable (the 300cap run settled; this run stalled). To get load3 + movement proof, load2 must RELIABLY reach render_ready/settle first. Next: (1) gate FPS test on render_ready (stop the false teardown); (2) make load2 completion reliable -- likely release the cVar10 clear after can_move so the finalize completes, or find the reliable natural trigger.
