See [[load2-flow-decoupled-into-harness-dll-orchestration-2026-07-19]].

SAVE FILE DISCOVERY = (b) DIRECT PATH BUILD+OPEN, NO directory enumeration (STATIC RE 2026-06-23, Ghidra dump decompile + deobf shift + PE imports). Answers 'game opens EldenRing\ dir but never <steamid> subdir / ER0000.sl2'.

CHAIN: SAVE_DIR_BUILDER deobf 0x140e0e680 = dump 0x140e0e730 (shift +0xb0). Decomp: SHGetFolderPathW(NULL,CSIDL_APPDATA=0x1a,NULL)->appdata (on fail uses L'.'); FUN_140e8d560(steamid str via %I64d); DLString::FormatW(out, L'%s/EldenRing/%s/', appdata, steamid). Builds full per-steamid DIR DIRECTLY from APPDATA+live steamid; does NOT enumerate EldenRing\ for the subdir. 5 callers = IO-device FSM (dump FUN_140e6dbe0/dec0/e130/e4e0/e770); in state 0x17/0x18 they call builder then FUN_14240a290(io_entry,0,dirpath)=install setter (->FUN_14240dcd0 writes dir@io_entry+0x48,state 0x16). OPEN: SLLoadSession builds dir+ER0000+.sl2 then FUN_1424156a0->FUN_1424151f0: DLFileOperatorContainer(path)+OpenFile@141edc920 -> vtable -> MicrosoftDiskFileOperator@141fc0ab0 (HANDLE fileHandle, OVERLAPPED, WIN32_FILE_ATTRIBUTE_DATA = pure Win32). Factory GetFileOperator@141f8fa20 -> ResolveVirtualPath@141fbff30 (only handles title:/install:/temp:/debug: prefixes; plain abs path just /->\ ) -> GetAbsolutePath@141fc04f0 (=GetFullPathNameW) -> CreateFileW.

API SET (PE imports KERNEL32): only file-open = CreateFileW; existence/attr = GetFileAttributesW/GetFileAttributesExW; path norm = GetFullPathNameW. FindFirstFileW/Ex/FindNextFileW EXIST but NOT on save-open path. No Nt*File imports (pure Win32).

CONCLUSION: opens EldenRing\<steamid>\ER0000.sl2 by DIRECT path (SHGetFolderPathW(APPDATA)+/EldenRing/+steamid+/ER0000.sl2 via GetFullPathNameW then CreateFileW). NO enumeration to discover the subdir. So runtime symptom (EldenRing\ opened, steamid subdir never) = built path's STEAMID component wrong/empty at open OR our SHGetFolderPathW redirect returned a different APPDATA than where ER0000.sl2 is staged OR FSM never reached install with non-empty dir (cold empty-dir bug b80-cold-COMPLETE-CHAIN). NOT a case/enum/FindFirst issue. Redirect needs only SHGetFolderPathW(APPDATA)+CreateFileW(+GetFileAttributes); NtQueryDirectoryFile/FindFirst coverage NOT required.

DECISIVE PROGRESS (user observation 2026-07-19, run bq0zmjqqp): after the probe gate fix, the driver DID work through two steps -- (1) the character MOVED FORWARD for the correct duration (move-proof PASSED, so oracle_did_move>0 and CAN_MOVE_CONFIRMED latched -- the pad-stick injection at FD4PadDevice::poll DOES move the char on native Windows; my 'pad stick ignored on kb+mouse' hypothesis was WRONG), and (2) the escape/system MENU OPENED (sq-repro advanced WAIT_WORLD->OPEN_MENU and the menu-open input worked). BUT then it did NOT perform any menu NAVIGATION (no UP/A to OptionSetting, no tab-switch, no Load-Profile, no slot select, no confirm), and the game TORE DOWN. So the current blocker is POST-MENU-OPEN NAVIGATION: after IngameTop opens, the next sq-repro transitions (UP,A->OptionSetting; tab->Quit; DOWN,DOWN,A->ProfileSelect; slot; A,A->load) are not firing. Candidate causes: (a) sq-repro's menu-window detection (SYSTEM_QUIT_INGAME_TOP_WINDOW latch) not firing so it never advances past OPEN_MENU; (b) the nav injection (menu eventIds) not reaching the open menu; (c) teardown (cap/stall) fired before nav. NEXT: read sq_repro_state + menu-window latches + supplied/did_move from run bq0zmjqqp telemetry to pin which. This supersedes the pad-stick-ignored theory in [[load1-not-stalled-oracles-false-negative-padstick-ignored-on-kbmouse-2026-07-19]].

MISSING-SAVE PICKER LOAD -- resolved root cause + implemented fix (branch feat/game-native-file-picker PR #40, commit a61c793, DLL sha b81584da). RE (disasm-verified 2026-07-07) overturned the whole prior premise: the 'Continue row built via idle ctor 0x1407acf80' telemetry was matching the WRONG object -- the passive 01_900_Black backdrop layer, NOT a Continue row. idle-vs-native ctor = 'is this window interactive', reads no save state. So product_continue's wait for a native-accept MENU_CONTINUE_ITEM could NEVER resolve (phantom). All my prior accept-byte/timing/ProfileSummary-gate fixes were chasing a non-existent Continue row (one even deadlocked: menu-open is what populates ProfileSummary, so waiting for it before opening hangs; reverted 7e23711).

REAL gate (verified in eldenring-deobf.bin): save-load 0x14067b200 -> call GetProfileSummary 0x1402567b0, call occupancy 0x140261cd0 (movzbl 0x8(rax,rcx,1) = ProfileSummary->saveSlotsStates[slot]), cmp slot<=9, IO/file check, then set GameMan+0xb80(saveState)=2. Empty ProfileSummary at a missing-save boot => saveSlotsStates[slot]==0 => refused. MarkProfileIndexAsUsed 0x262250 ABI = fn(profileSummary, slot)->u8 (codebase-proven in save_swap_profile_table.rs, NOT fn(slot) as the RE guessed).

FIX (a61c793): route missing-save flow (missing_save_picker_selected_slot().is_some()) through the existing native_fullread chain instead of product_continue, at both title_tick_cover.rs product_continue call sites (the GUARD-phase 912 block and the main ~1205 call). native_fullread_slot() returns the picked slot; SUBMIT calls MarkProfileIndexAsUsed(summary,slot) so the gate passes then submit 0x67b1a0/drain 0x679510+0x679180/deserialize 0x67b290; skip the title_menu_action_ready Load-Game-node scan for the picker (gate+log only, chain uses slot/gm/base); GUARD min_level=1 for the picker (c30_real+fp_real still block new-game); CONFIRM 0x140b0e180->SetState5 commits into the REDIRECTED STAGED save copy (not the user's original). native_fullread_commit_enabled() already true via product_autoload_enabled().

NEEDS LIVE SMOKE: launch, pick a real char; success = char loads to world + native-fullread log shows DESER c30=real (!=0xa010000 new-game) + GUARD PASS + COMMIT, no 'waiting for native-accept' spam. Residual the disasm can't settle: whether the redirected file reads real bytes (c30 real) and the IO pool drains at the menu.

DECISIVE ROOT CAUSE of the b80 cold full-read wall (2026-06-21, static RE triangulated by 3 independent disasm agents on eldenring-deobf.bin base 0x140000000). The cold/menu-free async full save-read NEVER goes resident NOT because submit fails (it succeeds) but because THE IODEV/SAVE-DEVICE HAS NO BOUND FILE in the cold context.

CHAIN: initiator 0x14067b1a0 -> iodev getter 0x140e6e060 (rax=[0x144589390] = ptr to heap iodev singleton) -> async loader 0x140e6eb80: cmpb [iodev+0x40],0; jne 0x140e6f430 (SYNC/bound branch); else jmp 0x140e6f5b0 (ASYNC branch). Cold iodev has [iodev+0x40]==0 so ALWAYS async. 0x140e6f5b0 genuinely succeeds: allocs 0x230 request -> [iodev+0x18] (via 0x14240a850, a slot-table mgr vtable 0x1431fb740 whose slots preset 0xffffffff=invalid), reserves slot idx 7 (0x14240ab30), allocs 0x30 wrapper -> [iodev+0x20], enqueues via 0x14240ad30 -> 0x14240e420 (post-work) -> builds 0x480 IO-job (0x142410a30, vtable 0x1431fc470, path wstr at job+0x478, fmt '%s\%s%s.bak' at 0x1431fc490) onto worker singleton *0x144852f88; sets [req+0x34]=7, returns true. => runtime: submit_ret=1 b80=2 io18/io20 set, worker registered. CORRECT.

WHY EMPTY: the iodev is zero-inited by ctor 0x140e6d970 ([iodev+0x40]=0, [+0x30]=0xffffffff, [+0xb0]=0, +0x50 subobj 0x14251c2e0) -- NO open file, NO OS handle, NO source path, slot table invalid. peek 0x140678a50 (the native Continue runs it FIRST) sets ONLY container+0x94 path string + GameMan+0xe70=1 (path-write 0x140678abc, e70 0x140678b00); it does NO CreateFile and NO iodev bind. The REAL open+ReadFile is deferred to the worker thread behind job vtable 0x1431fc470; the worker load step 0x1404807b0 gates immediately: cmpb [loader+0x38],0; je -> xor al,al; ret (NOT ready => returns false, does NO IO). So the cold worker completes the 0x280000 request with ZERO bytes in one frame, frees [iodev+0x18]/[iodev+0x20].

COMPLETION DECODE (B80_POLL 0x140679180): writes b80=3 if [GameMan+0xdf0]!=0 (cached fast-path @0x1406791a2) OR processor 0x140e6e080 returns 0 (@0x1406791da); writes b80=0 if processor returns !=0 && !=1 (@0x1406791fd). Processor 0x140e6e080 returns 4 (=>b80=0) iff [iodev+0x18]==0 || [iodev+0x20]==0 (handles freed). returns 0 (resident) only via case 0x140e6e28a (secondary status 0x14240a180==0 && byteArg==0). So b80 2->0 == request handles freed by poll time == empty completion. Lane driver 0x140679510 only ever resets b80=0 (when gate 0x140e6e430 !=1), bumps retry ctr GameMan+0xbc0; it never writes 1/2/3.

NOT in the submit path; located in the title->Continue mount flow. NEXT: find callers of peek 0x140678a50 (=the Continue handler) and what it calls AFTER peek to bind the device; find writers of iodev+0x40 / loader+0x38 / the +0x50 subobj path+handle. The BOOT LOAD was actually STUCK at WORLD RES WAIT (mms 3) for ~66s (45->111s) until the system-quit switch interrupted it. So GATE 1 (WorldResWait/loadlist) is STILL the boot's primary stall. MY HOOK BUG: oracle calls=2 fixups=0. The disarm-on-first-ARMED-call logic (swap(false) on entry) consumed the arm on the FIRST post-arm RequestMoveMap which had param_2=0x1c000000 ALREADY VALID (logged 'armed call NO-OP'), so the arm was gone before the ACTUAL load's RequestMoveMap (likely param_2=-1, the 2nd call, unarmed, unlogged) -> not fixed -> WorldResWait stuck. FIX: change arm from disarm-on-first-call to a BOUNDED-WINDOW countdown that disarms only on an ACTUAL fixup (or after N calls), so a valid intervening call does not consume the arm and the real stale call gets caught. VERDICT (er-effects-rs-jsm, static-RE proven 2026-07-05): The native loading tip text (and the Gauge_3 loading bar) CAN render ABOVE the custom character portrait -- but ONLY via an IN-MOVIE mechanism, not via the current Present-hook overlay. Proof + recipe:

1. Z-ORDER (proven, GFx display-list parse of 02_903_nowloading2.gfx): the save-load loading screen is ONE Scaleform movie 02_903_NowLoading2, owned by CS::KnowledgeLoadingScreen. Root depths: black plate 1 < BackImage artwork (MENU_DummyLoad, the MENU_Load_NNNNN replace-tex slot) 3 < Gauge_3 bar 5 < Main clip (tip title/body + key guide) 11. Lower depth draws first, so tips+bar ALWAYS render above whatever pixels the artwork texture holds. Any portrait delivered as that texture inherits correct native layering for free.

2. The Present-overlay (overlay_composite.rs) draws the head over the WHOLE backbuffer AFTER the entire GFx pass, so it necessarily covers tips+bar. It structurally CANNOT layer tips above the head. Its only fallback (D) is to alpha-cut the tip/bar rectangles out of the composite -- which shows the MOVIE ARTWORK (not the portrait) behind the tips, i.e. holes, not true "above". Static cut rects (1920x1080 design px, scale by viewport): tip title (112..1222, 696..744), body (112..1222, 749..1159), keyguide (112..1262, 1007..1046), bar (1409..1805, 1001..1042).

3. IN-MOVIE mechanism (candidate A, the real answer): the texture GFx actually DISPLAYS is a CS::CSTextureImage held in the Scaleform tex repository name-map at *(0x143d82510)+0x80, resolvable BY NAME (the forge's MENU_Load_NNNNN symbol) via resolver deobf 0x140d7c940 (arg1 ignored; (0,&out,nameW); out = AddRef'd CSTextureImage*; PANICS if the repo singleton *(base+0x3d82510) is null -> must null-check first). The GFx-sampled HAL texture is CSTextureImage+0x10; its ID3D12Resource accepts CopyTextureRegion (proven by the historical one-shot scan-found upload that DID display a head). Per-frame CopyTextureRegion of the live head into that resource puts the head INSIDE the movie -> tips+bar render on top natively. This reconciles the "mechanism A failed" history: those uploads targeted the CS-side GetResCap CSGxTexture (TexResCap+0x78), which Scaleform does NOT sample; the CSTextureImage+0x10 HAL texture is a different object the history never tested.

4. Prior crashes (gx-pointer swap into the movie container = vkd3d 0xc0000005; per-frame D3D12 global-object SCAN racing teardown) are AVOIDED by A: no pointer swap (content copy keeps object identity + descriptors stable), no global scan (resolve by name + bounded nest walk), and hold the resolver's AddRef on the CSTextureImage to keep the target alive for the window (release on movie reload / window end).

PORTRAIT LOOK-AT REALTIME FIX (design + RE, 2026-06-29, static-grounded via Ghidra MCP dump + dump-deobf-shift; runtime smoke PENDING). Goal: kill the ~4s head-tracking latency at the LOAD GAME screen.

ROOT CAUSE (corrected): the engine NEVER redraws profile thumbnails per-frame -- only on profile data-CHANGE. The profile draw step FUN_1409aa3e0 (dump VA; deobf 0x1409aa290, content-unique shift -0x150) loops the 10-slot renderer table base+0x3d6d8d0 and calls the offscreen-draw thunk 0x140bb8ca0 (real GX draw 0x140bb72b0) per non-null slot -- UNCONDITIONAL, no per-slot dirty gate. Its only code caller is FUN_14082bb20, which is a one-shot RESET/SAVE menu action (FUN_14067b5d0 creates a save+sets saveState; FUN_140255a70/ae0 reset profile slots + saveload menu state), redrawing thumbnails after data change. So thumbnails are static between rebuilds.

KEY INSIGHT: the prior 'game-task drive renders BLACK = wrong thread' was WRONG diagnosis. It's the wrong PHASE. Our recurring task ran at CSTaskGroupIndex::FrameBegin = BEFORE the GX frame records, so the GX subcontext-pool pop FUN_1419e5850(g_GxDrawContext) returns 0 (head==tail, pool empty) -> draw no-ops to black. The CS task scheduler runs on the same thread that pops GX subcontexts; it just must run during a DRAW phase (frame actively recording). updateBoneModelSpace (0x1653350) is the anim/sim phase (FUN_140401e80 deltaTime) -- pure pose math, NO GX -> right place for pose WRITE, wrong place for DRAW (confirmed: that's why driving draw from it would also be black).

FIX (implemented, builds clean): a SECOND cs_task.run_recurring in CSTaskGroupIndex::GameSceneDraw calling profile_lookat_realtime_draw_tick(base). Per frame: read live GetCursorPos; for each registered profile holder write base-quat (CHANGE!) compose: quat_mul(BASE_LATCHED, delta(yaw,pitch)) into Head/Neck/Spine2 local q -- NOT quat_mul(current,delta) which drifts at 60Hz; base latched ONCE per slot from the clean rebuilt idle pose (LookatSlot gained head_base/neck_base/spine2_base/base_latched; slot reset to None on rebuild re-latches). Mark all dirty|=2, isUpdated=0, call updateBoneModelSpace via trampoline to recompute model-space, then call draw step 0x9aa290 once to rasterize all portraits. The updateBoneModelSpace detour now SKIPS its own write when PROFILE_LOOKAT_REALTIME=true (passthrough) to avoid double-apply. We OWN the task -> cancel() is a fromsoftware-rs no-op + self-leaked Arc -> persists past Continue = the loading-screen keepalive port reuses this SAME mechanism (later: spared renderer's table entry is NULLED by teardown-spare hook so draw step skips it -> must restore entry or call thunk directly on LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_SPARED_RENDERER).

readiness-gated-xinput-driver-not-blind-2026-07-18: User correction (2026-07-18) on WHY the automated capture failed to reproduce load1->load2(freeze)->load3(recover): the automated driver fired the next load WITHOUT proper semaphores -- it never checked the character was RENDERED and that INPUT actually MOVED the character (input-causes-movement = readiness) before triggering the third save. FIX for the autonomous xinput driver (sq-repro autopilot): gate each load strictly on TWO readiness semaphores per goal doc SS4/SS6 -- (a) RENDER-READY (chr draw_group_enabled AND render_group_enabled AND enable_render), and (b) CAN-MOVE / input-causes-movement (player-control-enabled + world-sim advancing: havok position or anim time progressing while input is applied). Sequence: confirm load1 genuinely READY (rendered+movable, held >=5s dwell) BEFORE triggering load2; load2 freezes (render-ready false) -> that is the captured freeze; then trigger load3 which recovers to READY. This makes the automated run reproduce the user's manual sequence. Do NOT fire loads on menu-open or timers alone. This is the missing gate the earlier automated capture lacked. Links [[automated-capture-cannot-reproduce-user-load2-freeze-need-user-2026-07-18]] [[readiness]].

DEFINITIVE static RE (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY disasm) -- RECONCILES the apparent contradiction between save-mount-is-b80-inside-movemapstep-not-separate-2026 (claims ALL slot reads funnel through the b80==3 gate driven by the MoveMapStep dispatcher 0x140afa6d0) and the user's empirical observation that the in-game Load menu shows + loads a save WITHOUT a full world build. ANSWER: there ARE TWO independent b80 drivers. The MoveMapListStep load MENU drives the b80 1->2->3 advance ITSELF (its own step functions), separate from the MoveMapStep dispatchers.

=== THE MoveMapListStep STEP TABLE (builder 0x1400a3c90 -> table 0x143d705b0, stride 0x10, func@+0/name@+8) ===
idx0 0x140af1720 (asserts session singleton 0x144588e98 + resmgr 0x143d5b0f8 non-null -> this is the IN-GAME/session save-list menu, NOT a title menu), idx1 0x140af1800, idx2 0x140af04b0, idx3 STEP_ListSelect 0x140af0710 (4106 bytes; on slot pick calls play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0 @0x140af096a + @0x140af1216), idx4 STEP_LoadSaveData 0x140af19b0, idx5 0x140af1a30 (the b80 2->3 POLL + c30 read), idx6 0x140af1ae0, idx7 0x140af0320, idx8 0x140af03a0. Vtable 0x142b5e1e8. Table builder 0x1400a3c90 has 0 E8/E9 callers (registered via static-init like SimpleTitleStep builder 0x1400a4f50).

=== THE TWO b80 DRIVERS (disasm-proven) ===
DRIVER A (MoveMapStep, the world-load): dispatcher-1 0x140afbad0 (SOLE caller 0x140aff720) and b80 dispatcher 0x140afa6d0 (SOLE caller 0x140aff730) are BOTH called ONLY from inside the MoveMapStep update 0x140aff640. dispatcher-1 0x140afbad0 @0x140afbc3e is the ONLY caller of the FULL deserialize 0x14067b290 (recon_callscan: exactly 1). So the full per-slot deserialize that allocs 0x280000, reads the save, writes [GameMan+0xc30]=saved map AND applies the CHARACTER via 0x140e365c0 vs PlayerGameData 0x144588268 runs ONLY inside the ticking MoveMapStep.
DRIVER B (MoveMapListStep menu, self-contained): STEP_LoadSaveData idx4 0x140af19b0 calls 0x14067b200 @0x140af19fc which sets [GameMan+0xb80]=2 itself (df0!=0 fast path, else async read 0x140e6eb80 then b80=2). idx5 0x140af1a30 calls the async-IO worker poll 0x140679180(0,0) @0x140af1a46 which sets b80=3 when [GameMan+0xdf0]!=0 OR the IO device 0x140e6e080 reports ready; on eax==0 (complete) it falls to 0x140af1a93 -> reads [GameMan+0xc30] via 0x140679560 (pure reader: mov edx,[GameMan+0xc30]; [out]=edx) -> GameFlow resolver 0x14067abd0. The menu item callback 0x14082ba30 calls the LOAD INITIATOR 0x14067b4e0(slot) which sets b80=1 + begins async slot-IO. So the MENU drives b80 0->1 (initiator)->2 (LoadSaveData)->3 (idx5 poll) entirely within its OWN step functions; it NEVER calls 0x140afa6d0 or dispatcher-1 0x140afbad0, and NEVER calls the full deserialize 0x14067b290.

=== KEY: the MoveMapListStep registers the FD4 stream worker ITSELF (the missing piece from async-io-b80-lane-mismatch) ===
The MoveMapListStep state-advance helper 0x140af1b40 (jumped-to by idx steps after work) @0x140af1b6f references the FD4 stream task 0x144842d40, lazily builds it via 0x141eceb10 if null, then REGISTERS it with the FD4 scheduler via 0x142656b00 (key 0x59682f01) @0x140af1c05 -- the SAME register fn (269 callers) and SAME key the IngameInit tail / MoveMapStep use. So the menu path activates the world-stream worker on its own. This is why the menu's async slot-IO actually COMPLETES (b80 reaches 3) whereas a forced-title b80 initiate stalls: the menu registers the worker; the bare forced title does not.

=== WHO WRITES c30 IN THE MENU PATH (since the full deserialize 0x14067b290 is MoveMapStep-only) ===
c30 writer is the header-parse 0x14067bd70 (recon_callscan: 3 callers): (1) 0x14067b30e inside the full deserialize 0x14067b290 (MoveMapStep path), (2) 0x140677f2d (tailcall thunk), (3) 0x14067e215 inside a SECOND full-deserialize-class fn 0x14067e150 (allocs 0x280000 via scheduler 0x143d87310[+0x50], reads via 0x14067dc00, stores count to [obj+0xd68], calls 0x14067bd70(r8d=[obj+0xd68]) -> writes c30, then clears load-state fields b28/ac4/b5c..b5e/b68/c35/cb0/e70). 0x14067e150 sole caller is 0x14067a6e7 (a date/timestamp-comparison fn 0x14067a520 that tail-jmps it). This 0x14067e150 chain is a slot-record/preview deserialize independent of the MoveMapStep dispatcher -- a candidate menu-side c30 writer. NEEDS one more trace hop (who calls 0x14067a520) to fully confirm it is the menu highlight/confirm path, but it proves a c30 writer EXISTS outside the MoveMapStep.
Related: save-mount-is-b80-inside-movemapstep-not-separate-2026, async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026, real-continue-load-driver-recipe, own-stepper-control-verified-and-driver-call-2026.

DECISIVE DISCRIMINATION (2026-06-22, recurring block-scan observer, save-safe 899eed15). OWN-LOAD WorldResWait stall is a STREAMING-DISPATCH gap, NOT registration/handoff. It's STUCK not slow (inflight=0, not >0). RULES OUT: play_game_submit gap (+0x100 set), registration gap (block present). REMAINING GAP: the resource-streaming DISPATCH that takes a registered+requested block from 'requested' to IO-in-flight to phase 0xa. The fd4 io pool/worker-manager/stream-task + io_device are all PRESENT but idle. So a kick/enqueue/worker-tick between RequestBlock and the IO load is missing on the menu-free path. NEXT (decisive): GOLDEN compare -- on a vanilla load at WorldResWait, is io_inflight>0 + does the 0x1c block reach 0xa? what drives it (which worker/flag)? DECISIVE cold-load wall (runtime-measured 2026-06-21, after warm-kick+finalize+node-pump). The owner-FSM save-read NODE is found+advanced but the actual async device-read JOB is NEVER DISPATCHED cold. Node state (stable 240 frames, after finalize 0x140e6f200 + warm kick 0x14067b4e0(0) [ret=1,b80->1] + per-frame node advance 0x142411aa0):
node=0x7fffb09fe570 idx[+0x98]=0x15 (raw 0x800000015) io_job[+0x1d8]=0xFFFFFFFF (INVALID/-1 = no IO job) errb[+0xad]=0 (no error) op[[+0xa0]+0x10]=0x0 (deep gate CLEAR) pool310=0x143b41cf8 pool2e0=0x143b405b8 (FD4 job pools PRESENT). b80 STUCK 0, default level-9 char.

UNIFYING CONCLUSION: both the original cold state (idx0x16/op0x4) and the warm-kick state (idx0x15/op0/io_job=-1) mean the SAME thing -- node+0x1d8 (the FD4 async .sl2 read job) is never created/submitted, so the FSM waits forever. op=4 was the null-job branch of completion-predicate 0x14240ecc0 (NULL->3->op4); io_job=0xffffffff is the explicit no-job sentinel. The warm kick built the worker (owner+0x10 now set) + subscribed events + advanced the node 0x16->0x15 + cleared the deep gate -- real progress -- but the read job itself is not dispatched.

WHAT WORKS NOW (in src/experiments.rs cold_char_mount PHASE_POLL, all file-gated): finalize parked node (0x140e6f200(owner)) -> warm kick 0x14067b4e0(cl=0) ret=1 b80->1 (worker built, owner+0x10 set) -> per-frame node-advance pump 0x142411aa0(current node via 0x14240c270([io20],[io20+8])). Node reaches idx0x15, deep=0.

REMAINING (next session): WHY is node+0x1d8 (the FD4 read job) never created? The work-step 0x142412ce0 / 0x142415e30 should create+kick it. Decode: (1) what state idx the job-creation work-step runs in (idx 0x15 may be BEFORE it); (2) the FD4 job submit path 0x140e6fb50 -> submit thunk *0x144c0c594 and whether the FD4 resource-loader WORKER THREAD that executes the job is running cold (gs:0x58 / refcount 0x1445893a8 queue drain); (3) whether the save path/.sl2 must be installed on the NEW (post-kick) node's path-DB (the earlier SAVE-DIR install ran on the finalized node). DEFINITIVE dispatcher-2 0x140afb880 routing decode (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY) -- CORRECTS prior memories (real-continue-load-driver-recipe, native-autoload-slot-ac0-plus-force-flag) that said b72->current_slot_load 0x14067b570. THAT IS WRONG. Decoded full dispatcher (0x140afb880..0x140afbac1, 577 bytes):

PREDICATES read at top: r15b=0x1406793d0() (effective b72 force), bl=0x140679370() (b73), sil=(0x1406793c0()!=-1) (b78 slot present), r14b=0x140679100(), bpl=0x140679360().
cl = (r15|bl|sil|bpl)?1:0 (note r14 NOT in the OR).
ENTRY GATES (all before any load arm):
 (G1) cmp byte [0x143d856a0],0; jne 0x140afba9e. QUIT-LATCH must be 0 (CLEAR). If set -> cleanup, NO load.
 (G2) test cl; je 0x140afbaa8. At least one of b72/b73/b78/bpl predicate set.
 (G3) call 0x14067a080; test al; je 0x140afbaa8. 0x14067a080 DECODED = 'cmp [GameMan+0xb80],0; sete al; ret' -> requires [GameMan+0xb80]==0.
PRIORITY ROUTING (CORRECTED):
 r15(b72) SET & bl(b73) SET   -> 0x14067b940
 r15(b72) SET & bl(b73) CLEAR -> 0x14067b750   <== THE b72/Continue/autoload FULL-LOAD INITIATOR (NOT current_slot_load)
 r15 CLEAR & bl(b73) SET      -> 0x14067b570 (current_slot_load, ecx=0)  <== b73 path, not b72
 r15 CLEAR & bl CLEAR & sil(b78) -> 0x14067b200 path (deserialize-slot from b78)
 r15 CLEAR & bl CLEAR & bpl   -> 0x14067b030
 (each arm also: if byte[0x143d70848]!=0 it first vcalls [0x144c0c594] storing to 0x143d709c0 -- a telemetry/handle, not a gate.)
APPLY ARM @0x140afb9d7 (after the load arm returns al!=0 AND [owner+0x12a]==0): DEREF CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 (FD4-assert non-null 0x141eb97a0 if null -> CRASH), set [CSFeMan+0x82a8]=1, [owner+0x130]=0.

0x14067b750 (the b72 target) DECODED (0x14067b750..~0x14067b8fb): gate 0x14080d060() must be FALSE (else 0x14067b915 fail); [GameMan+0xb80]==0 (else 0x14067b903 fail); slot edi (passed ecx=-1 -> uses [GameMan+0xac0]); slot must be <=9 (else 0x14067b8fc fail, clears b72); allocs 0x280000 (BIG full-load buf, not 0x60000 preview!) via 0x143d872e0[+0x50]; reads save header 0x14067dc00; gets IO dev 0x140e6e060; ASYNC SUBMIT 0x140e6ec70(iodev,buf,r8d=slot,r9d=async) -> bl. FINAL: only if bl!=0 -> [GameMan+0xbb8]=1; [GameMan+0xb80]=1; [GameMan+0xb72]=0 @0x14067b8d4. If bl==0 (submit returns false) -> returns WITHOUT setting b80=1, b72 STAYS 1 (re-armed next frame).

ROOT-CAUSE CANDIDATE for 'b80 never becomes 1 with b72 set': the async submit 0x140e6ec70 returns FALSE at our forced in-context state, so the b80=1 write is skipped every frame while b72 stays armed. NEXT: decode 0x140e6ec70 to find why it returns false (worker/lane not ready). Also note: b72 path needs slot in [GameMan+0xac0] (we set it) AND b80==0 AND latch==0 AND 0x14080d060()==false. Related: b80-engage-gate-decode-current-slot-load-2026, menu-load-path-is-movemaplist-self-driven-not-movemapstep-2026.

Static RE of runtime crash #599: deobf crash 0x140e1a2a9 maps to dump 0x140e1a359 inside DLKR::DLRobustHeap::Free wrapper FUN_140e1a300. Caller chain maps 0x141124673 -> dump FUN_141124590, and 0x140ce8062 -> dump FUN_140ce80d0, the Scaleform RefCount/File destructor path that calls heap deallocate through PTR_PTR_144593250. The MemoryFile ctor is live/deobf 0x140ce7bb0 -> dump 0x140ce7c90; it constructs a 0x30-byte Scaleform::MemoryFile at the caller-provided address and sets refcount/vtable fields. Next route must allocate MemoryFile/File objects from the same Scaleform heap/allocator or avoid returning a synthetic File object; an observe-only loader hook is safe, and byte-identical override of existing native requests must not return static freeable objects.

Menu-task-manager RE (readonly, 2026-06-15): the inner TitleStep (vtable 0x2b63bb0, owner+0x4c state) is driven by an OUTER manager SimpleTitleStep (global 0x143d71340; state table at 0x1400a4c90: STEP_MenuInit 0xb0a4a0, STEP_MenuLoop 0xb0a5e0, STEP_IngameInit 0xb0a1f0, ...). The TitleStep per-frame update 0xb0bd60 (vtable slot3) only checks an owner+0x48==-1 guard via [vtable+0x20]=0xb0c240 then dispatches state_table[owner+0x4c](owner); it does NOT pump owner+0x2e8 (load job) or owner+0xe0 (menu jobs). The SimpleTitleStep MenuLoop pump 0xb0a5e0 is the real driver: it parses a SERIALIZED SELECTION STREAM (readers 0xe7b650/0xe7c780/0xe7c3e0) into owner+0x130, takes from queue owner+0x128 (title_queue_take 0x7a9560), validates stream 0x71fd60, submits menu task 0x733f20, and sets the next title state via title_queue_state_set 0xb0aa90 (calls at 0xb0a77d/0xb0a7a8) -- 'sets state5 on gate or finish'. So the NATURAL flow: input/selection -> SimpleTitleStep pump parses it -> sets TitleStep state (e.g. PlayGame=5) WHILE the pump keeps running each frame to pump the load job. Our DLL force-wrote TitleStep state=5 directly, bypassing the pump -> load job (owner+0x2e8, job+0xd8) never drained (orphaned). NATIVE ENTRY (next, ranked): (A) feed the SimpleTitleStep selection queue owner+0x128 / stream with a 'Continue/PlayGame' selection so the pump drives the load coherently; (B) reverse the selection producer (who pushes to owner+0x128) and call it; need the selection serialization format. Do NOT force TitleStep state. Related: [[force-play-game-sets-load-value]] [[autoload-stall-state0-precondition]].

3RD-LOAD FAILURE ROOT CONFIRMED (RAM oracle, 2026-07-16): consecutive in-process loads -- 1st + 2nd WORK, 3rd ALWAYS fails on Windows (COUNT-based, not same-vs-foreign-save). On the 3rd load: player=true (old world up), ig_d8=1 (InGameStep+0xd8 requestCode==1='MoveMap pending'), menu_job(CSMenuMan+0x798)=0, bc4 FROZEN at 1 for 990+ frames. save-gate-diag: EVERY save gate open (force=0, save_state=0, disableSaveMenu=0, menu_gate_ok, saveRequested=true, warp=false) -> blocked_by=NONE(orchestrator not called). This VALIDATES the very first workflow's root cause (wf switch2-savedrive) that I WRONGLY DISMISSED: the save orchestrator FUN_140afb970 (pumps bc4 1->2) is called only from FUN_140aff730 = the MoveMapStep step-update, which ticks only if a live MoveMapStep with its CSEzUpdateTask is registered in CSTaskGroup 20. On load 3 the MoveMapStep is never created/registered/ticked (InGameStep parked at requestCode=1; STEP_MoveMap_Init dump 0x140aec210 didn't advance it), so the save-drive never runs. So it is NOT disableSaveMenu, NOT the menu gate, NOT save_state, NOT bc4-pump-gates -- it is the MoveMapStep task never registering on the 3rd load, count-based (some state accumulates over 2 loads). Earlier runs where 'bc4 reached 3' were the 1st/2nd loads, which is why I mis-concluded the MoveMapStep hypothesis was disproven. New RAM oracle (SWITCH-ORACLE lines: bc4/player/ig_d8/menu_job/stable_frames/peak, resets per arm) is the reliable outcome semaphore; a watcher on it notifies on stable/drop/freeze. Workflow wf_f5ca222a cracking the accumulating cause + fix.

PHASE-4 KEY FINDING (2026-07-01): Arxan-flattened functions keep their REAL prologue/epilogue INSIDE the Arxan trampolines, which recovery DROPS (only in_game .text is recorded). So return-gadget collapse alone yields an ABI-BROKEN frameless function. Concrete: 0x140df9940 entry jmps to Arxan trampoline 0x14580bb98 which does push rbx (mov [rsp-8],rbx; lea rsp,[rsp-8]) + sub rsp,0x30 + security-cookie/SEH (mov [rsp+0x20],-2; mov rax,gs:[0x58]); the terminal exit 0x140df99dc jmps to return trampoline 0x1459f76e5 = add rsp,0x30; pop rbx (mov rbx,[rsp-8]); jmp [rsp-8] (=ret). Recovery records ONLY the dispersed game-.text body between them, so the reassembled fn uses rbx + writes [rsp+0x40] with NO push rbx / sub rsp,0x30 prologue. RESULT: bake 0x140df9940 -> ref=128 (varied) vs recomp=0 (constant) MISMATCH. Diagnosis: the function ignores its arg so it IS constant; the frameless clang REFERENCE clobbers rbx/caller-stack -> spurious varied=128, while rev.ng enforce-abi reconstructs a clean ABI -> constant 0. So the LIFT is more correct than the frameless ref; the differential verifier HONESTLY caught the ABI break (not a vacuous pass). FIX NEEDED (next phase-4 iteration): reconstruct prologue/epilogue -- either capture+translate the Arxan entry/return trampoline stack ops (push/sub rsp ... add rsp/pop/ret) as a normal prologue/epilogue, or synthesize from callee-saved-reg usage + frame size. Distinguishing prologue/epilogue trampolines (KEEP, translate) from pure dispatch/ret gadgets (COLLAPSE) is the crux. Two correct in-scope fixes already landed: (1) deobf-recover run() until=SENT -> SENT+0x1000 so the 'ret' terminal actually records (was dead code: emu_start stops before executing until=SENT, so the address==SENT hook never fired -> terminals always empty); (2) bake-function reassembler emits ret for a terminal arxan jmp with terminals[addr]=='ret'. Related: gadget-collapse-uncond-jmp-2026-07-01, bake-batch-reverify-2026-07-01, arxan-cfg-trampoline-resolver-and-reader-2026-07-01.

STRATEGIC UNIFICATION (2026-06-17, deobf static RE). CS::MoveMapListStep is the SINGLE state machine for the ENTIRE zero-input autoload -- it sequences BOTH the char-mount (save-data) half AND the world/map-stream half the goal previously split. Full pipeline (step idx -> handler -> role): 0 LoadList 0x140af1720; 1 LoadListWait 0x140af1800; 2 InitProfile 0x140af04b0; 3 ListSelect 0x140af0710 (gate: load iff [savemgr 0x143d69918 +0xb78]!=-1; else input-driven list UI); 4 LoadSaveData 0x140af19b0 (activates slot0 + gate 0x67b200 -> QUEUES async deserialize job at [savemgr+0x18], sets [savemgr+0xb80]=2); 5 LoadSaveData_Wait 0x140af1a30 (waits async load -> [savemgr+0xdf0] deserialize-ready); 6 MoveMapWait 0x140af1ae0 = THE WORLD/MAP-STREAM WAIT: rcx=[ctx+0xe8] (map-load coordinator subobj); if [subobj+0xd8]==0 -> req step 3; else call 0x140aebd30 (world/map-ready predicate) -> [ctx+0x2b8]=al; waits for world ready; 7 CharaSelect 0x140af0320 (movie-latch gate); 8 Finish 0x140af03a0 (teardown: cleanup [ctx+0xe0]/[ctx+0x100] via 0x140eb5530/54c0, checks [ctx+0xd0], singleton 0x143d5b0f8). 

=> DELIVERABLE DRIVE = drive MoveMapListStep to Finish with the ListSelect selection INJECTED ([savemgr+0xb78]=0). The machine itself carries you from title-load through save deserialize AND world/map stream into the playable world. World-stream half is NOT a separate problem -- it's step #6 of this machine (subobj [ctx+0xe8] is the map-load coordinator; find what builds it + what 0x140aebd30 reads). REMAINING (subagent a97a0824ae1b73f16): who creates+drives MoveMapListStep + cold-liveness + Continue/Load-Game trigger. WORKFLOW RULE (user, 2026-06-22, applies broadly): whenever work requires DRILLING DOWN into low-level detail or JUMPING SIGNIFICANTLY OUT OF the current context scope -- large/multi-file implementations, deep RE rabbit holes, build-fix loops, broad searches, iterative experiment cycles -- DELEGATE it to subagents (Agent tool, run_in_background) instead of doing it inline. Keep the orchestrator's own context SMALL and focused on the big picture / golden path / strategic thread. **Why:** inlining deep dives bloats the main context and loses the high-level plot; a focused subagent with a tight brief does the drill-down and returns only the conclusion. **How to apply:** give each delegated agent the precise de-risked plan (e.g. the relevant bd issue) + the bd memories to recall + hard constraints (save-safety, build-only vs probe, code style) + a concise structured report-back spec; run it in background (Agent tasks usually exceed 30s); then the orchestrator REVIEWS/integrates the result, updates memories/issues, and keeps steering. Reserve inline work for orchestration, small edits, and decisions. Iterate by spawning/continuing agents (SendMessage to continue one with its context intact), not by absorbing all detail yourself. Relates: autoload-CURRENT-STATE-2026-06-22.

So the Continue/Load(d180)/NewGame list lives at owner+0x130. owner+0xe0 only ever holds title composition (c000/c140/c280). All prior owner+0xe0 walks were structurally blind to d180.

=== owner+0x130 IS PUMPED EVERY FRAME (the 'not pumped' memo was a measurement artifact) ===
STEP_MenuJobWait(10) 0x140b0d400 pumps BOTH: @0x140b0d497 lea rcx,[rbx+0xe0]; call 0x140733f20 (owner+0xe0 multicast) AND @0x140b0d515 lea rcx,[rbx+0x130]; call 0x1407a9600 (owner+0x130). 0x1407a9600: rbx=[rcx]=held MenuJobSequence node; @0x1407a9678 mov rax,[rbx]; @0x1407a968b call [rax+0x10](rcx=node,rdx=&out,r8=&frametime) = the iterator 0x1407aa1f0. Selector iterates case vector base[node+0x18] count[node+0x88] stride0x10: each case [case+0]=predicate obj, calls [case+0]->vt[+0x10](edx=cond) -> al; first al!=0 -> matched child [case+8] stored to [node+0xb0]. USER CONTEXT (2026-07-16) on the switch-stall: the System->Quit profile-switch feature NEVER ran on native Windows before this branch -- the branch was started because Windows users CRASHED ON START. The hook-union (register_union_hook) fixed the crash-on-start (fewer competing minhooks) but the user confirms the switch stall PERSISTED after the union -- so minhook contention is NOT the switch-stall root; do not chase it for this symptom. IMPLICATION: the step-18 fade-in completion happens on Wine but NOT on native Windows => a native-vs-Wine difference in the FADE/PRESENT path, not the switch logic. Connects to y22i-windows-validation-2026-07-15: on native Windows our Present-VMT overlay NEVER installs (find_game_swapchain vtable-equality is a vkd3d/Wine-only invariant), so our custom loading cover / fade-composite path behaves differently on native. Workflow wf_ed658913 auditing whether our loading-cover/fade/CSNowLoadingHelper code holds the fade + is asymmetric native-vs-Wine. DEFINITIVE submit/KICK false-gate decode (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY disasm). Completes the b72->b80=1 path: pins the EXACT branch where the async submit returns FALSE so 0x14067b750 skips the b80=1 write @0x14067b8d4. Cited fresh disasm.

=== THE FULL b72->b80=1 WRITE CHAIN (decoded end-to-end) ===
MoveMapStep update 0x140aff640 calls dispatcher-2 0x140afb880 UNCONDITIONALLY @0x140aff728 (mov rcx,rbx; call -- no guard). Dispatcher-2 reads getter 0x1406793d0 (r15b=effective b72) @0x140afb89b; entry gates: latch [0x143d856a0]==0 @0x140afb8e1, predicate-set @0x140afb8ee, 0x14067a080 (b80==0) @0x140afb8f6; routes b72-set+b73-clear -> CALL 0x14067b750 @0x140afb967 (verified). 0x14067b750 sets [GameMan 0x143d69918 +0xb80]=1 @0x14067b8d4 ONLY if bl!=0 @0x14067b894, where bl = submit 0x140e6ec70 return (movzx ebx,al @0x14067b87c).

=== 0x14067b750 ARG SETUP to submit (decoded 0x14067b750..0x14067b8fb, raw past pdata cap of 97 bytes) ===
edi=slot (arg ecx; if ==-1 -> [GameMan+0xac0]); must be <=9 else @0x14067b796 jmp 0x14067b8fc clears b72 & fails. rbp=0x1402567b0()=[savecontainer 0x143d5df38 +0x78] (save-data ctx). rsi = buffer from [allocator 0x143d872e0][+0x50](edx=0x280000,r8d=1) @0x14067b7b6/b9; GATE rsi!=0 @0x14067b7c2 else jmp 0x14067b8e5 returns bl(=0) WITHOUT b80 write (EARLIEST silent fail; b72 stays armed). Then 0x14067dc00 reads save header (r14b). Submit call @0x14067b877: rcx=0x140e6e060() (IO subsys singleton 0x144589390), rdx=rsi(buffer), r8d=edi(slot), r9d=ebx(=r12b!=0, the async flag arg).

=== 0x140e6ec70 = THUNK on [iodev+0x40] ===
cmp [rcx+0x40],0; jne 0x140e6f760 (sync path, has gate cmp [rsp+0xd0],0xa @0x140e6efc5); else jmp 0x140e6f940 (ASYNC path, the one the initiator hits when iodev+0x40==0).

=== 0x140e6f940 (async submit) FALSE gates (decoded 0x140e6f940..0x140e6fb46) ===
al=0 (jmp 0x140e6fb27) iff: [iodev+0x10]!=0 @0x140e6f976 (stale request in-flight) OR [iodev+0x20]!=0 @0x140e6f981 (stale request handle) OR rdx(buffer)==0 @0x140e6f98a OR alloc 0x141eb9ed0 null & [iodev+0x10]==0 @0x140e6f9cf. esi=r8d=slot drives a FILENAME/lane dispatch @0x140e6fa30: esi==4 path, esi<=3 path (both build name via 0x14011e360 + enqueue 0x280000 read via 0x14240a5d0 then CALL KICK 0x140e6fb50 @0x140e6fae0, dil=al), ELSE esi>4 -> 0x140e6faea calls TEARDOWN 0x140e6f200 and xor dil,dil => RETURNS FALSE. (Open: confirm initiator passes slot vs a request-kind enum here; if literal save-slot 5..9 this would auto-fail -- but native menu-load works for all slots, so esi is likely a request-KIND, not the raw slot; the slot is carried in rdx ctx/0x14067dc00 header. FLAG for runtime confirm.)

=== KICK 0x140e6fb50 = THE PRECISE FALSE GATE (decoded 0x140e6fb50..0x140e6fc32) ===
Allocs req-wrapper 0x141eb9ed0 (gate non-null @0x140e6fba3 else bl path skipped); calls POOL-SUBMIT 0x14240ae10(rcx=wrapper, rdx, r8d=esi, r9d=[iodev+0x10]) @0x140e6fbe0; stores rax->[iodev+0x20] @0x140e6fbec; THEN @0x140e6fbf0: test [iodev+0x20]; if ==0 (pool-submit returned null) -> CALL TEARDOWN 0x140e6f200 @0x140e6fbf8 + xor bl,bl => RETURN FALSE. Else stores [iodev+0x38] (via vcall [0x144c0c594]) + bl=1 => TRUE. SO: submit returns FALSE iff pool-submit 0x14240ae10 yields null.

=== POOL-SUBMIT 0x14240ae10 (decoded 0x14240ae10..0x14240aeba) ===
Loads worker-MANAGER singleton 0x144852f88; if null lazy-builds via 0x14240af40 @0x14240ae67. Calls enqueue 0x14240e6f0(rcx=manager, rdx=wrapper, r8=r14, r9d=esi...) @0x14240ae94. RETURNS rax=rbx = the INPUT wrapper object (mov rax,rbx @0x14240aea2), which is non-null. So 0x14240ae10 returns NON-NULL whenever its wrapper arg is non-null -- it does NOT itself null out on a dead worker lane; it just enqueues onto manager 0x144852f88's pool (0x14240e6f0). Therefore KICK's [iodev+0x20] becomes non-null and KICK RETURNS TRUE, so b80=1 WOULD be written.

=== RECONCILIATION with runtime 'b80 stays 0' ===
Given the submit-success path DOES write b80=1, b80 flat at 0 means the chain fails BEFORE the b80=1 write, at one of: (1) getter 0x1406793d0 returns 0 (b72 filtered: [inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 +0x13c]!=0, or 0x14080d570()==0, or [GameMan+0xbc4]==3); (2) dispatcher-2 entry gate (latch 0x143d856a0!=0, or 0x14067a080 b80!=0); (3) 0x14067b750 early fail: 0x14080d060()==true @0x14067b76f, or slot>9, or rsi(buffer alloc [0x143d872e0+0x50])==0 @0x14067b7c2 (returns WITHOUT b80=1, b72 re-armed); (4) submit FALSE via [iodev+0x10]/[iodev+0x20]!=0 (a STALE request from the placeholder m60 load still resident on IO subsys 0x144589390 -- STRONG candidate at our placeholder-then-swap state) or the esi>4 teardown lane. The single MOST-LIKELY at the placeholder-world state: [iodev+0x10]!=0 / [iodev+0x20]!=0 (placeholder load left an in-flight/resident request on the IO subsys handle), making the async submit bail FALSE every frame so b80 never reaches 1. Even if it DID reach 1, the read never drains (worker stream task 0x144842d40 unregistered) so b80 sticks at 1. EITHER WAY the real-slot world never streams. VERIFY at runtime: read [0x144589390+0x10] and +0x20 (expect non-zero == stale request blocks resubmit), [GameMan+0xbc4] (expect !=3), [inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 +0x13c] (expect 0). RE-ORIENT: the real, hard, still-UNSOLVED task is to make the RELOAD path produce a movable character EVERY time -- find + clear the stale element / fix the finalize+render+control handoff so the 1st reload (load2) is movable just like load3, no freeze. Use MOVEMENT (injected pad stick -> havok delta) as the oracle, not the broken render flags. The movement-proof + focus-override + pad-injection + union + regression-revert all stand and are pushed; what remains is the actual freeze fix. Do NOT reproduce the parity; ELIMINATE it. ER FILE-EXTRACTION TOOLCHAIN (2026-06-17, cloned as siblings of er-effects-rs). For pulling PACKED game data (menu layouts, Lua scripts, UI text/FMG) out of Elden Ring for static RE of the front-end menu + online/offline flow. Full doc: docs/file-extraction-tooling.md.

TOOLS (all .NET): (1) Nuxe /home/banon/projects/Nuxe (https://github.com/JKAnderson/Nuxe, replaces UXM) = WPF GUI; unpacks ALL Data#.bhd/bdt BinderLight archives -> loose files + optional exe PATCH to load loose. Patch makes install NON-VANILLA + breaks online; Decrypt(Advanced) only decrypts BHD headers. (2) UXM-Selective-Unpack /home/banon/projects/UXM-Selective-Unpack (Nordgaren) = WinForms GUI .NET4.7.2; SELECTIVE unpack (pick files) and v2.5+ to an ARBITRARY OUTPUT DIR without patching -- the RIGHT tool for read-only RE (extract just menu/ msg/ script/ to a scratch dir, leave vanilla install+exe untouched). (3) WitchyBND /home/banon/projects/WitchyBND (ividyon, Yabber successor) = unpacks FromSoft CONTAINER formats (DCX, BND3/4, BXF3/4, FMG[UI text], LUAGNL/LUAINFO, PARAM, GFX->JPEXS deferred, LUA/HKS->DSLuaDecompiler deferred, TPF). CROSS-PLATFORM with a Linux CLI since v3.0.0.0 (--help); needs oo2core Oodle DLL (auto-fetched from Steam game dir) + .NET Desktop Runtime 10 on Linux/Wine. REQUIRES archives already unpacked by Nuxe/UXM.

PIPELINE: Nuxe/UXM-Selective (BHD/BDT -> loose) -> WitchyBND (BND/DCX/FMG/Lua -> readable XML/text/Lua).

ER FILES OF INTEREST (post-unpack): UI dialog text incl 'Unable to start in online mode'/'A connection error occurred' = msg/engus/menu.msgbnd.dcx + item.msgbnd.dcx -> WitchyBND -> FMG XML (gives the text ID to pin the C++ call site in eldenring.exe). Menu layout = menu/ GFx. ER Lua = script/ event/AI luabnd (the core load + b80/menu-deserialize mount are C++ NOT Lua).

DECISION: EXTRACT OFFLINE, SELECTIVE, TO A SCRATCH DIR (NOT patch exe, NOT loose files in Game dir) -- preserves the vanilla install the DLL targets. Runtime extraction impractical (Lua in memory = compiled bytecode; FMG/GFx packed). This box has NO dotnet/mono yet; install dotnet-runtime/dotnet-sdk via pacman (Nuxe/UXM GUI run via the Proton/Wine prefix; WitchyBND runs native Linux). Only run extraction when a need is confirmed (online flow proves Lua/data-driven, or to get the dialog text ID) -- the core load RE has been C++ in eldenring.exe and productive without extraction. Related: stage2-menu-drive-load-recipe-verified-2026, headless-autoload-no-startup-ui-2026, ondisk-eldenring-exe-path-for-objdump-2026, smithbox-lives-at-d-smithbox-mnt-d-smithbox.

fake_cover IS THE GAME'S NATIVE LOADING SCREEN (2026-07-19): fake_loading_screen_visible reads the game's own fake-loading-screen helper (fake_loading_screen_ptr + FAKE_LOADING_SCREEN_VISIBLE_OFFSET), NOT our overlay. So load2's fake_cover=True at mms=18 means the GAME still has its loading screen UP = the game is in LOADING MODE (rendering the loading cover + doing loading/streaming work) because the MoveMapStep never completes (mms=18). load1 settles (mms->-1) -> loading screen drops (fake_cover=False) -> normal render -> normal FPS. So the trash FPS is the game held in loading mode, which is architecturally entangled with the incomplete MoveMapStep: exiting loading mode = completing the load = the case-8 world teardown. The movable-window approach (hold mms=18) inherently pays the loading-mode FPS cost. Real FPS fix likely needs the game to EXIT loading mode without the map-transition teardown (deep). Measuring exact load1-vs-load2 FPS now via new oracle_fps/oracle_min_fps.

FEEDBACK from user (chozandrias76, 2026-06-15) on the er-effects autoresearch loop: the frame-task-rate (runtime_frame_task_hz) HARD GATE is NOT the right call -- do not auto-discard/zero a runtime probe on low frame-task hz. Why: runtime_frame_task_hz_min is a min-over-run, so cold boot, loading screens, and the actual save load we are trying to reach all legitimately drive ticks down -- hard-zeroing would auto-discard a genuinely successful autoload (punishes the success state) and conflates engine-busy phases with DLL-caused per-frame cost. How to apply: keep frame-hz as a SECONDARY observability metric only (a sustained low AVG during the title-idle phase still flags expensive per-frame DLL work like the title-owner VirtualQuery scan regression, worth investigating/fixing), but never as a discard/hard-zero. Removed the hard-zero bullet from .auto/prompt.md Hard Zero Gates on 2026-06-15. There is no code-level enforcement (measure.sh/rego/watcher never gated on it -- frame_task_hz.py only computes/emits). Related: [[title-owner-scan-fps-cripple]].

RUNTIME+RE (2026-06-16). COMPLETE load pipeline traced to its deepest layer. Zero-input chain WORKS up to: phaseA deserialize 0x14067b290(9) [c30=0xa010000 real map+char], phaseB SetState(5) owner+0xbc=c30 [builds CSFeMan + MoveMapStep on slot 9's map], phaseC build+register world-stream worker 0x144842d40 via IngameInit tail 0x140b0a980 with synthetic this(+0x48=7) [worker non-null, NO crash/assert]. RESULTS confirmed: csfeman built, WORLD SINGLETONS 0x143d691d8/0x143d69ba8 NON-NULL (MsbLoad 0x140af8f00 RAN), MoveMapStep (=*(InGameStep owner+0x2e8 +0xe8)) advanced its OWN step machine (table 0x143d70860, runtime-built by 0x1400a40c0) to step 3. b80 is IRRELEVANT (b80=0 or 1 -> same stall; the 0x14067b4e0 initiator sets a dead b80==1 lane; removed it). FINAL WALL: MoveMapStep step machine STUCK at idx3 = STEP_WorldResWait 0x140af9cf0 (names from table: 0=BeginInit 0x140af5140, 1=MsbLoad 0x140af8f00, 2=MsbLoadWait 0x140af9480, 3=WorldResWait 0x140af9cf0, 4=CurrentLodBlockWait 0x140af5900, 5=LeaveSessionWait 0x140af7c80). STEP_WorldResWait calls 0x140624bd0([MoveMapStep+0xf0]) which each frame requests resources (0x14066d7e0/0x14066da60) then checks readiness 0x14066d3e0([wrm+0x10]=resmgr, &block_coord): readiness REQUIRES [resmgr+0xb3140] (loaded-block count) > 0 AND a block in array [resmgr+0xb3030] (stride 8) whose [+0x8]+0xc == target area byte (rdx+3) AND that block's load-state (vtable+0x10) has [+0x2d]!=0 AND [+0x35]==0xa (load phase 10 = fully resident). It returns false -> mms parks at step 3 -> IsResident gate 0x140eb5530 (mms+0x48==-1) false -> InGameStep+0xd8 stuck at 1 -> child never drains. So the ACTUAL MAP-BLOCK GEOMETRY STREAMING (resmgr at [[MoveMapStep+0xf0]+0x10], block load phase->0xa) is the deepest remaining layer -- the resources are REQUESTED each frame but never reach phase 0xa. This is the core in-game resource-streaming subsystem (block registration + asset stream to phase 0xa). Next: read [resmgr+0xb3140] block count at runtime (registration gap vs streaming gap), and determine what drives a block's load-state to [+0x35]==0xa (the resource-load/decompress pipeline). WORLD-STREAM half detail (2026-06-17, deobf static RE). In CS::MoveMapListStep, the MAP/WORLD-LOAD COORDINATOR is the subobject at [ctx+0xe8]. World-ready predicate 0x140aebd30(ctx) = {rax=[ctx+0xe8]; if rax==0 ret 0; if byte[ [ctx+0xe8] +0x244]==0 ret 0; ret 1}. STEP_MoveMapWait#6 (0x140af1ae0): rcx=[ctx+0xe8]; if dword[subobj+0xd8]==0 -> req step3; else 0x140aebd30 -> [ctx+0x2b8]=ready. ALL [ctx+0xe8] accesses in the 0xaf0 cluster are READS (0x140af092c/0957 in ListSelect list-UI, 0x140af11e4/1203, 0x140af1ae9 in MoveMapWait) -- NO writer in-cluster => [ctx+0xe8] is a CONSTRUCTOR-built member of the MoveMapListStep object (created with it via DLRuntimeClass::CreateInstance). So the world-stream coordinator comes for free when the step is instantiated; driving MoveMapListStep to step #6 waits on world-ready [ctx+0xe8]+0x244. ROOT CAUSE (static RE Ghidra ermaporch dump + deobf ground-truth + live gm-snap telemetry): the native full deserialize FUN_14067b380 (dump) = our own_load feed parser 0x14067b290 (deobf, DESERIALIZE_SLOT) UNCONDITIONALLY does 'GLOBAL_GameMan->warpRequested = true' (GameMan+0x10, the SetCallForWarp field 0x14067af90). warpRequested is one of ~9 OR'd return-to-title triggers read EVERY FRAME by MoveMapStep::CheckReturnToTitle (dump FUN_140afa7c0, called from the MoveMapStep warpUpdate task FUN_140aff730): it writes the OR into menuData+0x5e and, while MoveMapStep+0x12a (return-title substate)==0, if any trigger is set it begins the quit-to-title teardown (0x12a->1/5 -> fades -> world tears down -> TitleStep SetState(2)). On the NORMAL in-world load the MoveMapStep warp machine consumes/clears warpRequested; our reload forwards the title Continue confirm -> SetState5 (a FRESH title->world stream, the STEP_PlayGame path) which never consumes it, so warpRequested stays true -> CheckReturnToTitle trips -> bounce.

MAJOR (300cap run 2026-07-20): LOAD2 SETTLES NATURALLY -- mms 18->19(CLEANUP)->20(FINISH)->-1 at 168s (user confirmed visually). So the cVar10=0 movable-window fix lets load2 complete cleanly: after the movable window a NATURAL non-warp ending trigger fires (NOT warpRequested, which we keep clearing) and the finalize runs 5->9 to mms=-1 without the catastrophic teardown seen when forcing. Load1 baseline: mms=18 movable at 79s, settled (mms=19) 102.6s (~23s window). Load2: mms=18 at 123s, settled 167s (~44s window, ~2x longer). CRITICAL BLOCKER = FPS/STUTTER, and it is LOAD2-SPECIFIC (same run, same parallel-cargo contention): load1 can_move ramps CLEAN f0->f60 (no resets); load2 can_move REPEATEDLY RESETS f13->f0->f1->f13->f0->f5->f9->f13->f16->f0 during its mms=18 window -- the injected forward stick moves the char only on SOME frames (intermittent = periodic stalls), so the 60-CONSECUTIVE-frame requirement never latches -> can_move never proven for load2 -> harness never drives load3. oracle_fps=20 (49.9ms) confirmed at load2. Load2's mms=18 window is 2x longer (more held-loading overhead) = worse FPS. The INTERMITTENT/periodic nature (some frames move, some do not) points to CPU-side periodic stalls (streaming/loading work load2 keeps doing at held mms=18), NOT steady GPU overload -- so RenderDoc (single GPU frame) may not reveal it; prefer finding the load2-specific per-frame CPU work first. NEXT: whitelist FPS run for exact load1-vs-load2 numbers; then fix the stutter so can_move latches f60 for load2 -> load3 drives.

ROOT CAUSE (RE): sq-repro/inject-nav input harness only worked with a physical Xbox controller because Elden Ring statically imports XInput (XINPUT1_4.dll, XInputGetCapabilities @deobf 0x3149f88 + XInputSetState @0x3149fa0 present; XInputGetState/Ex imported by ORDINAL) and ENUMERATES pads via XInputGetCapabilities. With no controller, XInputGetCapabilities(0)/XInputGetState(0) return ERROR_DEVICE_NOT_CONNECTED (1167), so the game stops polling slot 0 and the fabrication in xinput_get_state_hook never lands. Code already documented this ('XInput is not polled' without a controller: constants/system_quit.rs:44, mod/product_core_own_stepper.rs:1108). FIX (crates/er-effects-rs/src/experiments/input_block.rs): (1) xinput_get_state_hook forces slot 0 to report CONNECTED idle pad (SUCCESS + fresh private XINPUT_KEEPALIVE_PACKET) instead of DEVICE_NOT_CONNECTED when harness armed; (2) NEW xinput_get_capabilities_hook forces slot 0 to enumerate as connected standard gamepad (Type=1 SubType=1), wired into install_xinput_block. Both gated on system_quit_repro_enabled()||inject_nav_enabled() (env/file diagnostic opt-ins) -- never product/default, other slots untouched, zero button synthesis so simulated_button_presses_total unchanged. UNVALIDATED at runtime (live session running; DLL not built). Exercise w/o controller: ER_EFFECTS_SYSTEM_QUIT_REPRO=1 (or marker file), boot, watch debug for 'hooked XInputGetCapabilities' + sq-repro transitions. If still stalls, game may gate on WM_DEVICECHANGE hotplug scan; next lever = DInput-keyboard mirror via InputBlocker::set_injected_key with Xbox->DIK map.

B STEP #2 (2026-06-18, static deobf decode, native-bootstrap pivot). Narrows the worker-drain crux one level. The save-IO worker-MANAGER builder 0x14240dee0 (lazy via 0x14240af40) builds ONLY the sync structure: vtable 0x1431fc080, mutex 0x141ed6150 @+0x20, condvar 0x141ee0600 @+0x54, worker-ctx 0x142408380 @+0x68, ready-flag [mgr+0x28]=1. It SPAWNS NO THREADS. So the actual queue-DRAINING worker threads are NOT in the manager -- they are in the SEPARATE global engine THREAD POOL 0x144853048 (lazy via 0x14240afe0, which refs the FD4 stream task 0x144842d40). The enqueue path: KICK 0x140e6fb50 -> pool-submit 0x14240ae10 -> 0x14240e6f0(pool 0x144852f88/0x144853048, request, task). The cold read never reaches IO-request state 0x14 (resident) because the POOL THREADS that process the queue are not draining cold.

=> B's TRUE DEEPEST CRUX = the FD4 async-IO thread pool 0x144853048 not processing the queued read cold. NEXT B RE (static, save-safe, the decisive step): decode pool builder 0x14240afe0 -- does it CreateThread/spawn the worker threads, and are they spawned at boot (so cold they ARE running and the issue is task-registration/enqueue) OR only on menu/session entry (so cold they are absent and STARTING them is the lever)? Also decode 0x14240e6f0 (enqueue) -- what condition makes a pool thread pick up the task (is the FD4 stream task 0x144842d40 the thread BODY, and is it registered as a runnable in the pool cold?). If the pool threads run cold but don't drain, the gap is the task not being a runnable pool entry; if they don't run cold, spawning the pool is the lever. This distinguishes 'pool absent' vs 'task not enqueued-for-pool' -- the make-or-break for whether B's in-context drive can work, OR whether cold-priming the pool 0x14240afe0 is a standalone zero-input lever (analogous to the worldres-driver cold-build). CAVEAT: cold-b80-char-mount-dead-even-with-worker registered the stream TASK 0x144842d40 (via 0x140b0a980) and the read STILL didn't drain -- consistent with 'the POOL (0x144853048), not just the task, is what is missing/idle cold'. So focus on the POOL 0x14240afe0, not just the task registration. Related: B-step1-cold-submit-gate-is-worker-drain-not-session-2026, STRATEGIC-PIVOT-B-native-session-bootstrap-chosen-2026, async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026, stream-worker-registration-is-the-b80-wall-verified-2026, cold-b80-char-mount-dead-even-with-worker-session-is-the-keystone-2026, emk-resman-streaming-driver-coldbuild-stub-lever-2026.

RUNTIME-IDENTIFIED (2026-06-17, builder-hook diagnostic). The boot 'A connection error occurred / Unable to start in online mode' modal IS a CS::MessageBoxDialog: vtable 0x142b03550 (RVA 0x2b03550), built by builder 0x1409275b0 (4 reg args rcx/rdx/r8/r9 -> dialog in rax; live args rcx=dialog, rdx=low FD4-arena ptr ~0x10c848, r8=large value ~0x45adbea8 maybe msg-id/config, r9=low ptr ~0x10c808). Built ONCE via the MENU PUMP: caller stack FD4 Sequence iterator 0x1407aa1f0 -> MenuWindowJob::Update 0x1407ad1c0 -> functor invoke 0x1407ad2bc -> builder -- i.e. a MenuWindowJob +0xa8 action functor builds it (analogous to Load-Game d180 building ProfileLoadDialog) and stores it at [item+0x130], polling it each frame.

WHAT FAILED (do not repeat): (1) the agent's getter patch (force GameMan::IsOnlineMode getter 0x14067a030 -> 0 = online-disable-isonlinemode-getter-patch-2026) applied early but did NOT prevent the modal -- the boot login ATTEMPT still fires + fails regardless of the IsOnlineMode flag. (2) The finished-getter 0x1407b0cf0 (cmp [rcx+0x25e8],2; setge) is NEVER called for this dialog (a minhook on it never fired) -- so it is NOT this dialog's finished-poll; hooking it to auto-OK does nothing. (3) Writing the result fields [dialog+0x25e0]=0 (button) + [dialog+0x25e8]=2 (state) EVERY FRAME from the game task (CSTaskGroupIndex::FrameBegin) does NOT close the dialog (171+ frames of writes, modal stayed up). Hypothesis: those fields are OUTPUT (read by the consumer 0x140927ba0 cmp [rbx+0x25e0],-1 AFTER the dialog closes), not the close TRIGGER; and/or the dialog's own per-frame update (run later in the frame during the menu task tick) RESETS them because no OK input arrived -> a write-overwrite race the FrameBegin write loses.

IMPLEMENTED so far (er_effects_rs.dll): builder hook 0x1409275b0 captures the dialog into CONNECTION_ERROR_DIALOG atomic (vtable-validated) + force_dismiss_startup_dialog writes the fields each frame pre-in-world (gated online_disable_enabled, IN_WORLD_REACHED latch turns it off in-game). NOT WORKING (fields are not the trigger). own_stepper PHASE_MENU now WAITS while CONNECTION_ERROR_DIALOG != 0 (so it does not build the menu under the modal -> avoids the black-screen contention seen when it did).

NEXT (RE in progress): decode CS::MessageBoxDialog vtable 0x142b03550 -> its UPDATE method + the real CONFIRM/CLOSE trigger (the internal lifecycle/state field that makes it close as if OK pressed, surviving the per-frame update), or a confirm method to CALL with rcx=dialog. CORRECTED ACQUISITION WALK (2026-06-18, static deobf, explains probe-1 empty scan). 0x143d6d8d0 is NOT an array of screen objects -- it is 10 slots of menu GROUP CONTROLLERS (each *(group)==vtable 0x142b7f310, 0xa30 bytes, descriptor table 0x143b39840 stride 0x20). The live SCREEN object hangs off each group via: holder = *(group + 0xa8); screen = *(holder + 0x48); vt = *(screen). Verified by group destructor 0x140bb72b0 (mov 0x48(rcx),rcx -> virtual-call) + chain build 0x140bb7eb0. holder+0x48 may be a list head -> walk siblings for multiple screens. That is why probe-1 matching slot[0] directly against TitleTopDialog/MenuWindow vtables found 0x0 (it compared the GROUP vtable 0x142b7f310, not a screen).

DETERMINISTIC SCREEN-FIND LOOP: for i in 0..9: group=*(base+0x3d6d8d0 + i*8); if !group or *(group)!=base+0x2b7f310 continue; holder=*(group+0xa8); if !holder continue; scr=*(holder+0x48); if !scr continue; vt=*(scr); classify: 0x142b26468=TitleTopDialog, 0x142a93a60 / 0x142a94318 = MenuWindow/Proxy, 0x142b229f8=ProfileLoadDialog. (Walk holder+0x48 sibling list too.)

SINGLETONS: CSMenuManImp singleton = *(0x143d6b7b0) (vtable 0x142a9be58, size 0x8a0, ctor 0x1407650a0) -- does NOT hold MenuWindow/screen-list at a fixed offset (786 readers, none give it). Menu-system singleton 0x143d5df38 (+0x78 = scrmgr, getter 0x1402567b0): scrmgr+0x18 has 10 entries stride 0x2a0, enable byte +0x8 -- these are FLAGS/transition state, NOT screen ptrs. So the ONLY screen-object path is the group->holder->+0x48 chain above.

MenuWindow ctors (0x1407419a0 vt 0x142a93a60; proxy 0x140748bf1 vt 0x142a94318) have ZERO static callers (lazy factory dispatch vtable ~0x142ac3e00). So MenuWindow is found ONLY by the group->holder walk (match vt). A live MenuWindow stores menu-step singleton *(0x143d87350) into its +0x1f0/+0x210 (identity cross-check). Fallback: type registry 0x143d873a0 (count *(0x143d87388), stride 0x18, instance at entry-0x10, lookup 0x140e1a210).

CORRECTED CODE RECIPE: TitleTopDialog = reuse own_stepper STAGE1d dialog (the one it drives; OWN_STEPPER_DIALOG) OR the walk match 0x142b26468; MenuWindow* = the group->holder walk match 0x142a93a60/0x142a94318 (fail-closed if none); then factory 0x14081ead0(rcx=TitleTopDialog+0xa38, rdx=MenuWindow*). RUNTIME-ONLY residue: group+0xa8/holder+0x48 are LAZILY populated (null until the title screen is up) -> the corrected code should LOG each group/holder/screen/vt it walks (doubles as the diag) + walk siblings; confirm which group index yields MenuWindow vs TitleTopDialog at the rendered menu. New tool: scripts/lea-scan-deobf.py (rip-rel lea/mov reader scan). The REAL requirement: validate load2 by following a path CLOSE TO HOW A USER WOULD DO IT -- navigate System->Quit->Load-Profile WITH INPUT, respecting the game's input-enable DELAYS (the game gates when you can move / open the menu / press buttons after load). That user-like navigation WHILE RUNNING TESTS is what gives reasonable confidence a real user can validate the feature reaches genuine world readiness. So: (1) the DIRECT-ARM shortcut (system_quit_arm_quickload_autoload bypassing the menu) is WRONG -- it skips the exact user path (menu nav + input delays) that must be validated. (2) The harness DLL's DIRECT-MEMORY native-binding injection (input_inject inputmgr+0x90) IS the right mechanism -- it mimics a user's button presses at the native binding, respecting delays, and drives the real menu path. (3) Drive the FULL user path: wait for input-enabled/world-ready -> open in-world menu -> tab to Quit -> Load Profile -> ProfileSelect -> slot -> Confirm, each gated on the game's real input-enable timing. Update AGENTS.md 'No Compromises' to drop zero-input/simulated_button_presses=0 and replace with 'user-like input-driven navigation respecting input-enable delays'. Supersedes [[harness-port-direct-memory-mechanisms-not-xinput-tick-2026-07-19]] only re: the ANTI-input framing; the direct-memory MECHANISM guidance stands.

ROOT CAUSE (Ghidra, static): GLOBAL_CSGaitem (ptr at dump 0x143d69890 = data RVA 0x3d69890, stable across deobf) is a PROCESS-LIFETIME FD4 singleton -- constructed ONCE at boot by the giant repository-init FUN_140cd6d40 behind 'if(GLOBAL_CSGaitem==0)', NEVER rebuilt per world-load. CSGaitemImp::Deserialize (dump 0x140671220, live 0x671130) allocates gaitems from freeTableIdxQueue via GetGaItemHandleWeapon/etc -> GetUnindexedGaItemHandle (dump 0x140672530): pops freeTableIdxQueue[head]; when head==end (queue EMPTY) returns handle 0, caller skips the gaitemInsTable[i] store, the lookup handle stays 0xffffffff, and Deserialize dispatches gaitemInsTable[-1] -> AV at live 0x67141a. On a NORMAL quit-to-title the world/inventory teardown releases the player's gaitems back to the queue via RemoveCSGaitemIns; our lightweight return-title chain (0x67a3a0) SKIPS that, so char#1's items leak and char#2's reload exhausts the queue. finalize FUN_140671760 only drops the deserialize-transaction refs (scratch table field7_0x19028), NOT the persistent inventory gaitems.

FIX: in the continue_confirm hook, at clean title (player absent) BEFORE own_load_feed_deserialize, call own_load_reset_gaitem_singleton(base) (src/experiments/own_load.rs): sweep all 0x1400 gaitemInsTable slots; for each occupied slot (gaitemInsTable[i]!=0) call native RemoveCSGaitemIns(gaitem, &entries[i].unindexedGaItemHandle) (dump 0x140672650 -> live 0x672560, shift -0xf0 ground-truthed). RemoveCSGaitemIns destructs+deallocates the ins (no leak) AND returns index i to freeTableIdxQueue[++end]. entries[i]'s handle embeds index i (ctor seeds, alloc preserves), so it targets slot i. CSGaitemImp struct offsets (Ghidra, size 0x19038): _vfptr +0, gaitemInsTable +0x8 (CSGaitemIns*[0x1400]), entries +0xa008 ({u32 handle,u32 refCount} stride 8), freeTableIdxQueue +0x14008, head +0x19008, end +0x1900c. Struct/consts in constants.rs (GLOBAL_CSGAITEM_SINGLETON_RVA, CSGAITEM_REMOVE_INS_RVA, CSGAITEM_*_OFFSET). Fail-closed structural guard: heap-aligned singleton + head/end in [0,0x1400).

REFINEMENT (2026-06-23, count-reset fix FAILED, but pins the mechanism exactly). I reset dialog+0x50's count (at dialog+0x98) to 0 BEFORE the Assign install -- log showed 'was 0' (ALREADY 0 at +26114ms, right after self-open). Then it FILLED to 8 DURING the pump (+26114->+26135), and our load's MenuWindowJob push (hit5 +26135, rcx=dialog+0x50) is #9 -> overflow -> ASSERT 'out of memory' -> crash. So: the menu ITEMS (Continue/Load/NewGame/Settings/Quit/... ~8) are pushed into dialog+0x50 DURING the pump (same frame), and our load WINDOW pushes one more = 9 > cap-8. Resetting BEFORE the pump is too EARLY (count was 0, items fill after). A reset-to-0 from our game task cannot win the race -- the ~9 pushes (8 items + our window) all happen within the pump that frame, from 0 -> still 9.

ROOT: our dispatcher-built load job's MenuWindowJob targets dialog+0x50 (the SAME vector the menu items use; dispatcher built our job with r8=dialog+0x50 as ctx_parent -> MenuWindowJob+0x50=dialog+0x50). The NATIVE Continue avoids the overflow by the menu TRANSITION (TextFadeOut) CLEARING the menu items out of dialog+0x50 BEFORE the load window pushes -- our direct install skips that transition, so items + load window collide in dialog+0x50.

FIX OPTIONS (careful, not a 2am hack): (1) trigger the menu TextFadeOut transition (clear the items from dialog+0x50) before/with the load -- the native confirm does this; find + invoke the transition. (2) make our load's MenuWindowJob use a SEPARATE/fresh vector for +0x50 (not dialog+0x50) -- requires changing the dispatcher's r8/ctx or intercepting the MenuWindowJob build (the +0x50 set in the ctor from param_2). (3) a VEH special-case at the push 0x1407ad53b that resets [rcx+0x48] to 0 right before OUR load's push (gated on our-load-active + rcx==dialog+0x50) -- precise timing but VEH-handler code + risk. (4) skip building the menu items (set dialog+0xa40=1 directly instead of calling the open-menu registrar 0x1409b24e0) so dialog+0x50 stays empty -- but the selector/dispatcher may need the registrar's menu setup (risky). RECOMMEND investigating (1) the menu transition (most native-faithful) or (2) the separate vector. NOT a from-game-task count reset (loses the race). Relates: OVERFLOW-VECTOR-PINNED-dialog-plus-0x50, owner130-IS-drained-but-dlfixedvector-cap8-overflow.

IMPLEMENTABLE ZERO-INPUT AUTOLOAD DESIGN (2026-06-16, static RE + validated by the real Seamless .co2 capture). The real save mount runs in the TITLE Load-Game menu context at STATE 10 (post-BeginTitle 0x140b0c5b0), needing only session 0x7ffe.. + PlayerGameData 0x144588268 + slotmgr (ALL boot-built); NOT csfeman (csfeman+MoveMapStep build only later at PlayGame state5->6).

SLOT-INJECTION (how slot N reaches the mount): deserialize 0x14082c240 reads slot from job-context [rsi] (first dword) at 14082c377 (mov ecx,[rsi]) then 14082c379 call 0x14067a810 = setter 'mov [GameMan(0x143d69918)+0xac0],ecx'. The job-context dword comes from the menu item descriptor+0x8 (job factory 0x140824820: mov eax,[rcx+0x8]; mov [r8],eax). So to plant slot N: SIMPLEST = call 0x14067a810(N) to set GameMan+0xac0=N (exactly what the deserialize does internally); native = build the selector-owner step (factory 0x140821bf0, vtable 0x142ac71e0) with descriptor+0x8=N.

MENU ENTRY CHAIN: BeginTitle 0x140b0c5b0 builds Continue + Load-Game menu items (descriptors via 0x1407a91e0/0x140744a60, composed by 0x14078e0e0/0x1407927d0, registered via 0x140b0e530 into title owner+0xe0/+0x138). The selector-owner step (tick 0x140826d50, vtable 0x142ac71e0, cluster 0x142ac71c0..0x142ac7238) is instantiated lazily when the Load-Game item is entered (factory 0x140821bf0), then ticked by the menu-task pump (#7 tick 0x140791620, #6 child-iterator 0x1407aa1f0). First tick of 0x140826d50 (step+0x68==0) fires delegate-installer 0x140828270 (builds async job at step+0x70, sets +0x68=1); later ticks pump the job until 0x14082c240 runs: sets ac0=N (0x67a810), writes [rsi+0x38], mounts c30 (0xa010000->real via thunk 0x140677f20->0x14067bd70), loads PlayerGameData char; sets step result [rsi+0x4]=5/3/2/6.

CONTINUE ALONE IS INSUFFICIENT: confirm 0x140b0e180 only READS GameMan+0xc30 (via accessor 0x140679560 = mov edx,[GameMan+0xc30]) into owner+0xbc then SetState(5). c30 is populated ONLY by the mount path (0x14082c240). So a prior mount is MANDATORY before Continue/PlayGame; Continue without a mount would SetState(5) with unpopulated c30 + no char.

MOST-RECENT SELECTOR: 0x14067a520 (timestamp comparator over GameMan+0xb98/+0xba0 packed datetime + per-slot timestamps) sets only b72/b73 save-request bools (gated 0x143d856a0), does NOT write the slot index. No native one-call most-recent->slot injector exists; to autoload most-recent, replicate its scan to pick N, then plant N.

THE B80 MACHINE WORKS AT STATE 10 (validated by the capture): 0x14067b1a0(slot) set b80=2 + populated iodev io18+io20 (started the read), poll 0x140679180(0,0) advanced b80 2->3 (resident), all at state 10 in the live menu (the stream worker drained). So the slot-int primitive lane (0x14067b1a0 -> poll 0x140679180 -> b80==3 -> deserialize) is the live path.

MINIMAL OWN-STEPPER DRIVE (the next experiment to implement+run): in our idx10 handler (own-stepper, runs in-context as the live SimpleTitleStep step): PHASE1 call 0x14067a810(N) [ac0=N]; call 0x14067b1a0(N) [b80=2 + start read]; PHASE2 each frame poll 0x140679180(0,0) until GameMan+0xb80==3; PHASE3 call 0x14067b290(N) [the dispatcher-family full deserialize: read 0x14067b100 (gated b80==3) + header c30 0x14067bd70 + char apply 0x140e365c0 -> mounts c30=real + ac0 + char] (works for .co2 too: ERSC redirects the FILE IO below 0x14067b100, deserialize logic is vanilla); PHASE4 read GameMan+0xc30, set owner+0xbc=c30, clear owner+0x284, SetState(5) via 0x140b0d960 -> native pump streams the real map with the applied char. ACTIVE STEAM ACCOUNT ID runtime-read recipe (STATIC RE 2026-06-23, deobf disasm authoritative; dump for names). Use to read live steamId64 as a telemetry oracle and compare to staged dir steamid 76561197986456766.

DEOBF chain (deobf base 0x140000000; binary eldenring-deobf.bin):
- 0x140e8d520 (steamid-string getter, called by SAVE_DIR_BUILDER): thunk(stack); call *0x143b48ff0 (Steam-interface GUARD/ensure-init); call 0x140e8d590 (THE VALUE GETTER) -> rax; thunk(stack); ret rax. NOTE in DEOBF the value getter is 0x140e8d590 (dump version 0x140e8d5a0 is Arxan-garbled 'halt_baddata', unreadable -- trust deobf).
- 0x140e8d590 body (DEOBF disasm, exact):
    lea rcx, 0x143b48a00            ; Steam mgr/context arg
    call *0x144c0d0a4               ; getter -> rax = Steam-user singleton holder
    lea rdx, [rsp+0x30]
    mov rcx, [rax]                 ; rcx = *holder = ISteamUser-ish object
    mov rax, [rcx]                 ; rax = vtable
    call *[rax+0x10]              ; vtable slot +0x10 -> returns ptr to CSteamID (rax)
    mov rax, [rax]                ; rax = the 64-bit steamId64 value
    ret
- Then FUN_140e8d560 (dump 0x140e8d560) formats it: swprintf(out, L'%I64d', steamId64). So the dir's <steamid> = decimal of this u64. Encoding: full SteamID64 (e.g. 76561197986456766).

READ RECIPE for the DLL oracle: call the in-process getter (cleanest) = call deobf 0x140e8d590 (returns steamId64 in rax) AFTER game/Steam init. (It internally re-enters Steam ensure-init via call *0x143b48ff0; safe once SteamAPI is up.) Pointer-chain alt (no call): p = (*0x144c0d0a4)(0x143b48a00); obj = *p; vt = *obj; idptr = (*(vt+0x10))(obj); steamId64 = *idptr. The vtable call at +0x10 is required (it returns the CSteamID address); there is no flat static global holding the id. Both 0x143b48ff0 and 0x144c0d0a4 are import/thunk pointer slots (deobf addrs). lib.rs already references 0x140e8d550(~getter) and guard 0x143b48ff0 -- consistent.

Relates: savedir-DISCOVERY-direct-path-CreateFileW-no-enum-2026-06-23. Compare live id vs 76561197986456766; mismatch => our SHGetFolderPathW redirect + staged dir steamid disagree => fix staged dir name or redirect.

STEP-3 (WORLD RES WAIT) STALL GATE FULLY RE'd (Ghidra 1.16.1, 2026-07-16) -- user corrected: this is DETERMINISTIC, determinant unknown. STEP_WorldResWait (child step 3 handler, dump 0x140af9de0) advances 3->4 ONLY when FUN_140624cb0(MoveMapStep->fieldArea, dt) returns 1; else it calls _CheckEternityLoading (the game's OWN infinite-loading watchdog -- confirms we tripped the game's recognized 'world resources never finish' state, a known FromSoft online failure mode; likely inert offline = detect-not-recover). OFFSETS (from disasm): FieldArea = MoveMapStep+0xf0 (the oracle's MOVEMAPSTEP_WORLDRES_F0_OFFSET=0xf0 is actually FieldArea, mislabeled 'worldres'); currentBlockId = FieldArea+0x2c (BlockId u32); worldInfoOwner = FieldArea+0x10 (oracle's WORLDRES_RESMGR_10_OFFSET=0x10 = worldInfoOwner, mislabeled 'resmgr'); block count = worldInfoOwner+0xb3140 (== the oracle's 'blocks'=33); block list = worldInfoOwner+0xb3030 (array of block ptrs). FUN_140624cb0 each frame: FUN_14066d8d0(worldInfoOwner,&currentBlockId) [ISSUES the load: finds block by areaId, sets loadstate+0x2c=1; for overworld blocks adds to a list via FUN_14063c5a0], then FUN_14066d4d0(worldInfoOwner,&currentBlockId) [THE GATE = is block loaded]. FUN_14066d4d0: walk block list (count +0xb3140), for each block find one whose (*(u64*)(block+0x8))+0xc == currentBlockId.areaId; get its load-state via block->vtable[0x10](block); LOADED requires loadstate+0x2d != 0 AND loadstate+0x35 == 0x0a(10). So step 3 hangs when: (A) NO block in worldInfoOwner's list matches currentBlockId.areaId (target block never registered -- prime suspect: our switch teardown leaves the wrong block set / target block not added), OR (B) the matching block's stream-state +0x35 is stuck below 10. DETERMINANT to instrument (next build): currentBlockId (FieldArea+0x2c), and scan the block list (worldInfoOwner+0xb3030, count +0xb3140) reading each block's areaId (*(u64*)(block+0x8)+0xc) -- report whether currentBlockId.areaId is IN the list, and if so its loadstate+0x35 (needs the vtable+0x10 call OR find loadstate passively). USER DATA: same save c30=1c000000 loaded fine as a LATE switch (run bdd2bb03) but stuck at step3 as the FIRST switch (run bvn83tc70) -> determinant is the CONTEXT/outgoing-world block-list state, not the target save alone. currentBlockId.areaId likely == c30 high byte (STEP_MoveMap masks BlockId & 0xff000000 == 0x3d000000). This is the NEW primary blocker after the case-7 teardown fix (committed 3a54089). Builds on new-stall-worldreswait-step3.

POST-SWITCH RELOAD CRASH (er-effects-rs-3pc) ROOT CAUSE — proven by RIP, NOT the game-side save_state/gaitem theory.

The fatal AV during the System-Quit -> return-title -> reload-of-picked-slot transition is caused by OUR OWN telemetry oracle, not game code. Crash log (run b40ka2vyj 2026-07-02, +34283ms; identical signature in the pre-fix 07:52 run at +39672ms): access-violation, RIP outside .text, fault_addr=0x7ffe977c61b0, ret0=self+0x317bf, faulting instruction self+0x317bd = 'call *rdx'. That call site is er_effects_rs::telemetry::title_logo_gfx_current_frame (src/telemetry.rs): it reads handle=*(title_logo_back_view_parts+0x88), vtable=*(handle), resolve_value_addr=*(vtable+8), then transmute+CALLs resolve_value(handle).

Why it faults: safe_read_usize only rejects UNMAPPED pages; it returns mapped-but-garbage qwords fine. During the reload transition PRODUCT_CORE_LAST_TITLE_DIALOG points at a half-torn-down/reallocated TitleTopDialog whose embedded BackViewParts holds a STALE handle whose vtable (observed 0x7ffe96aa4238) is in the Wine heap, NOT the game image (base 0x140000000, +SizeOfImage). resolve_value_addr read from that garbage vtable = 0x7ffe977c61b0, a data address; calling it -> AV.

FIX APPLIED: guard both the vtable and the resolved call target with crate::experiments::vtable_in_game_image(ptr, base) before the transmute+call; return TITLE_LOGO_GFX_UNKNOWN_FRAME if either is outside the game image. Minimal, matches the codebase's existing in-module pointer test (own_stepper in_module).
