Committed state at owner+0x48 (requested at +0x4c). SetState 0x140b0d960 writes ONLY +0x4c (mov [rcx+0x4c],edx); dispatcher 0x140b0bd60 commits +0x4c->+0x48 each frame then dispatches table[state].func(owner,framectx). States (table 0x143d71580): 0 InitMenu(builds intro items), 1/2 (intro confirm targets), 3 BeginTitle(builds REAL Continue/Load menu + wires owner+0xe0 callbacks), 4 BeginNewGame, 5 PlayGame, 6 GameStepWait, 7 EndFlow(NG+ ending), 8 EndFlowWait, 9 NextLap, 10 MenuJobWait(parked here at press-any-button), 11 Finish(teardown).
The VARIABLE UI we never touch (because our handler at idx10 jumps straight to SetState(5)): the intro press-any-button confirm 0x140b0b6b0 (input-gated, branches state 1 vs 2), the unsafe-close Continue-recovery prompt / online-offline choice / Seamless prompts / new-save EULA -- ALL of those live inside the menu-job at owner+0xe0 and the BeginTitle(3) menu items, none of which run if we SetState(5) directly from state 10. The only gate the PlayGame confirm itself checks: PlayGame item confirm 0x140b0e180 reads [owner+0x284]==1 -> if set uses edx=4 (NewGame/EULA) else edx=5 (PlayGame). So to force a LOAD (not new game) ensure owner+0x284 != 1 (it is 0 at the title). The quit-latch 0x143d856a0 (MenuJobWait's only native SetState, ->11 Finish) we leave 0.
Menu-job (owner+0xe0) Continue handler chain: BeginTitle(3) builds PlayGame/Load items (vtables 0x142b63f50/0x142b63f88) and wires the selection callback into owner+0xe0 via 0x14081f9f0. Selecting Continue fires the PlayGame item confirm 0x140b0e180 (pump slot +0x10). 0x140b0e180 decoded: this=item, owner=[this+0x8]; if [owner+0x284]!=1: call 0x140678a50 (most-recent-slot resolver: reads slot container 0x143d5df38[+0x8], token 0x47054 via 0x140760970) -> 0x140679560 -> [owner+0xbc]=chosen slot, edx=5; SetState 0x140b0d960(owner,5); then write request bytes to InGameStep child via 0x14082cd60(owner+0x138,..) and 0x140ca7660(owner+0x300,byte). So Continue == set owner+0xbc=slot + SetState(owner,5). Directly callable; NO input needed at the action layer (input only decides WHICH item-confirm fires, which we bypass by calling SetState ourselves).

=== Q3 MINIMAL DETERMINISTIC LOAD DRIVER (what our idx10 handler runs) ===
Our handler runs in-context (rcx=live SimpleTitleStep owner, rdx=framectx) every frame at state 10. Recipe (do once, then let native pump run, then self-restore the idx10 entry):
 1. GUARD: only act once (a static bool); verify owner+0x48==10, owner+0x284!=1, latch 0x143d856a0==0.
 2. SELECT SLOT: for default most-recent, mimic 0x140b0e180: call 0x140678a50/0x140679560 to get the slot, write [owner+0xbc]=slot. (For ini override write the desired slot index.) ALSO write the InGameStep-child request the way 0x140b0e180 does (0x14082cd60(owner+0x138,&tmp); 0x140ca7660(owner+0x300,byte)) -- OR simpler, just CALL the native PlayGame item-confirm 0x140b0e180 if we can synthesize its 'this' (item with [this+0x8]=owner); cleanest is to replicate its body inline.
 3. SetState(owner,5) via 0x140b0d960(owner,5). The NEXT frame the dispatcher commits 5 and runs STEP_PlayGame 0x140b0d5b0 IN-CONTEXT.
 4. STEP_PlayGame 0x140b0d5b0 (decoded): asserts inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 (sets +0x6b0=1) + session 0x144588e98; calls 0x140e5f8f0/0x140e5f7f0/0x140678ca0(slot); reads [owner+0xbc] selection into local; rcx=[owner+0x2e8] (InGameStep child); calls play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0(child, &sel, -1, &tmp) -> writes child+0xd8=1, resolves map into child+0x100/0x104, appends request vector child+0xb35f0, writes GameMan+0x14 cmd. This is the NATIVE activation submitter.
 5. The InGameStep child pump advances to idx6 STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 -> calls submitter 0x140aed820 (creates m? loadstates from child+0x100..0x104) THEN builds MoveMapStep (ctor 0x140af28d0) + CSFeMan (0x140766010). MoveMapStep update 0x140aff640 ticks dispatcher 0x140afa6d0 (b80 save-mount 1->2->3 -> deserialize 0x14067b290 -> GameMan+0xc30 real map + char) and runs STEP machine 0..5 (table 0x143d70860) through WorldResWait.
 6. RESTORE idx10: write the original 0x140b0d400 back into 0x143d71620 so the native machine is untouched after we hand off (or leave our handler as a no-op after the guard trips). NO inline scheduler-tick needed -- every step runs on the native FD4 per-frame pump because we are inside it.
Which calls need the scheduler tick vs inline: SetState/select are inline (one-shot in our handler). PlayGame, MoveMap_Init, MoveMapStep update, b80 mount, WorldResWait are ALL driven by the native FD4 scheduler per-frame pump (the same pump that calls our handler) -- we do NOT pump them ourselves (that was the prior external-CSTask mistake that orphaned the load job).

=== Q4 DOES IN-CONTEXT EXECUTION RESOLVE WorldResWait STREAMING? ===
PARTIALLY YES by construction, with ONE residual unknown that is now MUCH more likely resolved in-context. Prior external SetState(5) forcing stalled at WorldResWait for TWO reasons (worldres-loadstate-creator + worldres-enable notes): (gap1) owner+0x100..0x104 held a PLACEHOLDER/wrong map at the synthetic spawn, so loadstates for the real slot map were never created; (gap2) the streaming-enable 0x14066e2e4 (sets resmgr+0xb7c1=1) and its precondition global 0x143d7c088 (streaming/session driver singleton) were never invoked -- 0x143d7c088 is a REGISTERED FD4 service (getter 0x140cd6c50, registered via table @0x1400acb30 / ctor 0x140cd5240, vtable 0x142ba1690) invoked through the FD4 service registry by the session/map ACTIVATION flow, never written by a store (33 refs ALL movq_load), so it stayed NULL at the forced synthetic title and the job machine 0x1406709e0 asserts/idles.
WHY in-context (idx10 patch) fixes gap1: we drive SetState(5) on the LIVE native FE-host SimpleTitleStep with the REAL owner+0xbc selection, so the native STEP_PlayGame 0x140b0d5b0 -> play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0 resolves the REAL slot map into the REAL InGameStep child+0x100, and MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 -> submitter 0x140aed820 creates the CORRECT loadstates. (External forcing used a synthetic owner/placeholder map; in-context uses the genuine selection->map resolution.)
WHY in-context is far more likely to fix gap2: gap2 is the session-ACTIVATION-owned streaming-enable. In-context we are running the GENUINE FE-host->PlayGame->InGameStep->MoveMap flow that the native game uses, so the surrounding scheduler/service activation that invokes the 0x140cd6c50 getter (building 0x143d7c088) and the streaming-enable 0x14066e2e4 runs in its native order -- which it does NOT when an external CSTask trampolines a synthetic SetState. This is exactly the own-the-stepper hypothesis. CAVEAT (only resolvable at runtime): it is still POSSIBLE that 0x143d7c088 / the streaming-enable are triggered by an even-later session-activation substep that the in-context PlayGame flow also reaches naturally; static RE cannot prove the exact frame it fires, but it CAN confirm these are activation-owned (not WorldResWait-tick-owned), so the correct fix class is 'let the native activation run in-context' rather than 'poke b7c1/build the singleton cold'. If after the in-context drive 0x143d7c088 is STILL null, the targeted fallback is: cold-build it via getter 0x140cd6c50 then call streaming-enable 0x14066e2e4(resmgr=deref(deref(MoveMapStep+0xf0)+0x10), verify vtable==0x142a7e030) -- per worldres-enable note, LOW risk once 0x143d7c088 is non-null.
VERIFY chain at runtime: owner+0x48 5->6; CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 != 0; GameMan+0xc30 != 0xa010000 (real map); m10 loadstate getter 0x14062f470 non-null; 0x143d7c088 != 0; resmgr+0xb7c1==1, +0xb7c4 climbs; [loadstate+0x35]->0xa; readiness 0x14066d3e0 true; MoveMapStep+0x48 -> -1; InGameStep(child)+0xd8 1->2->0 -> world renders.

(3) SYNTHESIS: the correct freeze relaxation is a renderer-liveness fence -- cache the bound renderer ptr, hook its teardown/dtor, tombstone + bounded-wait on PROFILE_IN_OUR_DRIVE (already brackets the drive at :3196-3205) before forwarding the free -- which is structurally the SAME 'guard our hook against an already-torn owner' machinery az9's NEXT step sketches. Fixing #1 this way builds az9's fix substrate. Design target: relax the latch so the drive continues post-capture in the stable target-only window, with the TOCTOU closed by the teardown fence, NOT by never driving. Crash history to respect: 3 UAF flavors (Scaleform dtor, GX-queue null, garbage-vtable RIP) hit hundreds of drive-frames in during switch churn; drive binds r from the table (taxonomy #4 ac0-lag) so switch churn can free the bound renderer cross-thread.

PROCESS RULE (user directive 2026-07-18, strong correction): DO NOT extrapolate/act on user feedback until it is CORRELATED to an in-game RAM/telemetry SEMAPHORE. USER PRINCIPLE (2026-06-22, durable): input MUST ALWAYS be blocked during automated runtime probes, NOT only when own_stepper is armed. Rationale: the product goal is ZERO-INPUT autoload -- we accept NO path that requires input of any variety to reach the loading states. So (a) user/foreign input must never reach the game during a probe (else it contaminates the run -- e.g. the 2026-06-22 control run with own_load=0 had NO block because block_input_enabled() gated on own_stepper_enabled(); the user pressed buttons and the 'clean 120s' conclusion became untrustworthy/invalid per the experiment-integrity rules), and (b) if blocking input through any stage BREAKS a load path, that path SECRETLY REQUIRES INPUT and must be REJECTED, not unblocked (do not paper over it by releasing the block). Enforcement: block_input_enabled() should block UNCONDITIONALLY until in-world is reached (release in-world so the user can play after load), with an EXPLICIT opt-out ONLY for deliberate user-driven captures (golden traces, which set an allow-input flag). The old comment 'blocking through menu-open starves post-open navigation so the load never selects' is NOT a reason to unblock -- it is proof that menu-navigation path needs input and is therefore an unacceptable (non-zero-input) path. Like foreground-force and splash-skip, input-block is a MAIN always-on probe feature, not an own_stepper-gated toggle.

FINAL SYNTHESIS DELIVERABLE (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY disasm) -- answers the task brief Q1-Q4: the EXACT gate that blocks b80 engagement at our in-context placeholder-world state, and the concrete recipe. All cited from fresh disasm this session.

=== Q1/Q2 ANSWER: b80 never engages because the FD4 STREAM WORKER TASK 0x144842d40 is NOT REGISTERED with the scheduler at our forced FE-host. It is NOT a flag we missed. ===
- The b72 force flag DOES reach the consumer: getter 0x1406793d0 returns b72 filtered by [inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 +0x13c]==0 (0x14080d060) && input-idle (0x14080d570) && [GameMan+0xbc4]!=3. At our idle in-context state these PASS, so b72 survives.
- The dispatchers RUN: dispatcher-1 0x140afbad0, dispatcher-2 0x140afb880, 0x140afa6d0 are called UNCONDITIONALLY (no guard) every frame from the MoveMapStep update 0x140aff640 @0x140aff720/728/730 (each exactly 1 caller, verified callscan). Our SetState(5) world IS ticking, so they run.
- ROUTING CORRECTION (supersedes prior memories): dispatcher-2 0x140afb880 routes b72(set)+b73(clear) -> 0x14067b750 (the Continue/autoload FULL-LOAD initiator, slot from GameMan+0xac0, allocs 0x280000), NOT current_slot_load 0x14067b570 (which is the b73-only/preview path, 0x60000). Entry gates in dispatcher-2: quit-latch [0x143d856a0]==0; >=1 predicate set; 0x14067a080 (= 'cmp [GameMan+0xb80],0; sete al') i.e. b80==0.
- 0x14067b750 sets [GameMan+0xb80]=1 ONLY if the ASYNC SUBMIT 0x140e6ec70 returns true (bl!=0 @0x14067b894). The submit (0x140e6f940) enqueues the read via KICK 0x140e6fb50 -> pool submit 0x14240ae10 onto the global engine worker pool 0x144853048 backed by FD4 stream task 0x144842d40. THAT task is never ticked/drained -> the read never progresses (state 0x14240a1f0 never reaches 0x14 resident) -> b80 stuck at 0/1.
- PROVEN GAP: MoveMapStep ctor 0x140af28d0, spawner STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120, and update 0x140aff640 contain NO 0x142656b00 register call / no key 0x59682f01 / no 0x144842d40 build (grepped all three dumps -> zero hits). So the world-load step our recipe builds NEVER registers the stream worker.

=== Q4 ANSWER: WHO registers the worker, and the lever ===
- The in-game save-list MENU MoveMapListStep registers it: its state-advance helper 0x140af1b40 (9 callers) lazily builds task 0x144842d40 (via 0x141eceb10) and REGISTERS it with the FD4 scheduler: rcx=0x59682f01 (key), rdx=name-descriptor (SSO string 0x1429d2b28 built by 0x140116c20), task ptr bound in the descriptor at [rsp+0x30]; call 0x142656b00 @0x140af1c05. 0x142656b00 = FD4 scheduler register (269 callers; hashes the name via 0x141ebba30 and inserts into the group keyed by rcx). This runs when [owner+0x48]+1 > 0xb (terminal-ish step).
- BUT MoveMapListStep idx0 0x140af1720 hard-asserts SESSION singleton 0x144588e98 != 0 AND resmgr 0x143d5b0f8 != 0 (FD4 assert 0x141eb97a0 -> crash). So we cannot just spawn the MoveMapListStep to register the worker unless a live session exists. (Same session-scoping as current_slot_load gate 0x140256590() and the save-mgr update 0x14067f5d0 deref of session 0x1447ef360.)

=== THE RECIPE (in-context, zero-input) ===
There is NO standalone deserialize shortcut: full deserialize 0x14067b290 does sync read 0x14067b100 which is gated b80==3 (cannot be called cold). The slot9-deserialize that worked before only succeeded because SetState(5) had already driven b80 to 3 -- which needs the worker. So the genuine path is to make the native b80 machine COMPLETE, which requires the stream worker registered+ticked. Two options:
 (A) [LEAST RISK, native] Drive the SESSION-bootstrap so the worker gets registered the native way: the title-accept -> PlayGame -> InGameStep -> (IngameInit tail 0x140b0a980 +0x48>=7 arm, or the menu MoveMapListStep) registers 0x144842d40. Our SetState(5) shortcut SKIPS the IngameInit tail; closing that gap (let the native IngameInit tail run, or spawn the MoveMapListStep once session exists) is the fix.
 (B) [direct, needs ABI] Replicate 0x140af1b40's tail from our handler once the MoveMapStep is ticking: build task 0x144842d40 (0x141eceb10 if null), build the name descriptor, call 0x142656b00(rcx=0x59682f01, rdx=name-desc, task bound). Then the ticking dispatchers' submit drains, b80 1->2->3, deserialize runs natively, world streams. OPEN/UNREVERSED: the exact 0x142656b00 descriptor ABI (how the task ptr + name + r8/r9 are packed) -- decode 0x140af1b40 0x140af1bde..0x140af1c05 + 0x142656b00 fully before calling, to avoid an FD4 assert/AV.

=== CRASH RISK / VERIFY ===

RVA misidentification uncovered by upstream cross-check (autoresearch 2026-06-18). VA 0x144842d40 (RVA 0x4842d40) is upstream fromsoftware-rs eldenring runtime_heap_allocator (the DLAllocator singleton; rva_ww AND rva_jp both = 0x4842d40; upstream dlkr/allocator.rs DLAllocator::instance() uses it in live allocation paths e.g. ez_state/value.rs). OUR code had it mislabeled in THREE duplicate consts as a world-stream worker / FD4 stream task: lib.rs WORLD_STREAM_WORKER_RVA, experiments.rs STREAM_WORKER_RVA, telemetry.rs FD4_STREAM_TASK_RVA. STATIC RE CONFIRMS upstream: 0x144842d40 has 4057 RIP-relative refs (heap-allocator footprint, not a task) and a cached-singleton getter at 0x140078ed5 (if null: rax=construct(); [0x144842d40]=rax). It is built at startup -> ALWAYS non-null. IMPACT: the worldres-coldbuild "worker present" lever (experiments.rs worldres_coldbuild_probe) and telemetry fd4_stream_task_present were FALSE POSITIVES (reading the always-present allocator, not a stream task/worker the getter builds). FIX: consolidated to one crate const RUNTIME_HEAP_ALLOCATOR_RVA + flagged the false-positive inference sites (behavior unchanged, same address). TODO: the real FD4 stream-task/worker RVA still needs to be found for those levers to mean anything. Method/tool: python value-cross-check of our *_RVA consts vs upstream rva_ww.rs; only 3 value collisions (game_data_man 0x3d5df38 OK, game_man 0x3d69918 OK, 0x4842d40 MISLABELED).

SEAMLESS CO-OP RESULT (2026-06-18, runtime, save-safe save_safety_ok=1). STRUCTURAL COMPATIBILITY DEMONSTRATED + the remaining Seamless-specific gap pinned. Setup: new 'seamless' launch mode in .auto/runtime_probe.sh (launches ersc_launcher.exe so ersc.dll [Seamless] loads AND dinput8.dll loads dllMods/er_effects_rs.dll -- both coexist); added 'seamless' to the rego launch_mode allowlist (.auto/runtime_experiment_policy.rego line 24; contract tests pass); save-harness backs up BOTH .sl2 and .co2 (save_roots covers the EldenRing AppData dir; the .co2 = ER0000.co2 the co-op save). Profile .auto/runtime-env.seamless (own-stepper slot=0 + direct-build, same as offline).

RUNTIME under Seamless: own_stepper FIRES + drives the FULL chain identically to offline -- PATCHED idx10, SetState(2) BeginLogo, PHASE_MENU_BUILD, DIRECT-BUILD SUCCESS (ProfileLoadDialog vt 0x142b229f8), STAGE2-ACTIVATE bound=1 step_ok=true (selector built), STAGE2-SELECTOR-DRIVE submit_ret=1 (type-0xa submit 0x14067b1a0 SUCCEEDED), io18=0x7ffe93785730 io20 populated. So the DLL is COMPATIBLE: it loads, coexists with ersc.dll, and drives the title->menu->dialog->selector->submit chain under Seamless, save-safe.

THE GAP: b80 (worker 0x143d69918+0xb80) STAYS 0 -- never reaches 3 (resident) -- even though submit_ret=1 (which sets b80=2 offline). So ersc.dll INTERCEPTS the b80 save-IO worker (redirecting the read to its .co2 async reader on ersc's own thread), and the VANILLA b80 lane (0x679510/poll 0x679180) does NOT drain it. deser_ret stays -2 (deserialize 0x67b290 never fires, gated b80==3). So the full .co2 LOAD does not complete via the vanilla submit+drain path under Seamless. NOT a structural incompat -- a read-completion path difference.

NEXT (Seamless .co2 load drive): use the SEAMLESS-HOOKED read path instead of the vanilla submit. The menu DESERIALIZE 0x14082c240 is VANILLA under Seamless (seamless-co2-menu-deserialize-is-vanilla-0x82c240) but the READ is ersc-hooked. Options: (a) drive the SELECTOR/menu_deser path (load_activate -> menu_deser 0x14082c240) which is the menu's native deserialize that ersc redirects for .co2 -- the selector step IS built (step_ok=true); pump it + let ersc's hook supply the .co2 read (vs the vanilla 0x14067b1a0 submit which ersc bypasses); (b) find ersc's completion signal for the .co2 read (poll it instead of b80==3); (c) decode ersc.dll's save-IO hook to drive its .co2 read. Also: the Seamless boot is slower (co-op init) so the drive reached submit only at ~waits=30 -- the 30s cap leaves little drain time; confirm whether it's purely timing vs the ersc-hook bypass (the b80=0-despite-submit_ret=1 points to the hook bypass, not just timing). The OFFLINE-VANILLA autoload mechanism is proven (char loads + SetState5 + world-stream); Seamless needs the ersc-hooked .co2 read drive. Related: AUTOLOAD-WORKS..., autoload-mechanism-proven-but-full-inworld-and-seamless-unconfirmed-2026, full-save-read-DRAINS-cold-via-0x67b1a0-submit, seamless-co2-menu-deserialize-is-vanilla-0x82c240, selector-menu_deser-runs-but-stuck-pending-no-submit-2026 (menu_deser path -- may be the Seamless route).

This preserves indefinite play on cancel and lets selected foreign slots load from the committed candidate bytes.

DEFINITIVE MENU-DRIVEN (MoveMapListStep) decode, 2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1 base 0x140000000, ASLR off, READ-ONLY disasm. Answers the menu-mount task Q1-Q4 + most-recent resolution. VERDICT: the Load-Game MENU (MoveMapListStep) CANNOT be ticked at the bare press-any-button title because its idx0 hard-asserts session singleton 0x144588e98, which is NOT a boot singleton -- it is built only by the session bootstrap 0x140deb0b0 (run on the session worker thread, not at the cold title). The same assert guards STEP_BeginLogo(2), which the native flow runs only AFTER the press. So the menu lane is unreachable cold; pivot remains the native PlayGame drive (option2-verdict).

=== Q1 INSTANTIATION/TIMING ===
MoveMapListStep is NOT a child/field of the live SimpleTitleStep (FE-host, vtable 0x142b63bb0, table 0x143d71580). Its step table 0x143d705b0 (builder 0x1400a3c90, vtable 0x142b5e1e8) is registered via static-init (builder has 0 E8/E9 callers, like the SimpleTitleStep builder 0x1400a4f50). It is a SEPARATE FD4 step task, spawned on demand only inside a live session (it is the IN-GAME save-list menu), not present at the title. Its idx steps (0x140af1720/1800/04b0/0710/19b0/1a30/1ae0/0320/03a0) and advance helper 0x140af1b40 reference session 0x144588e98 + resmgr 0x143d5b0f8 -- session-scoped objects.

=== Q2 PRECONDITIONS (the decisive answer) ===
idx0 0x140af1720 DECODED: mov rcx,[0x144588e98]; test; je -> FD4 assert 0x141eb97a0 (string 0x143d5df48) CRASH if null. Then call 0x140e5f8f0(session,0) (sets session+0x33 flag). Then mov rcx,[0x143d5b0f8] (resmgr); if null -> assert (string 0x1429cf200). Then 0x1401f2b20(rdx=vtable 0x142b5e9d0, r8=step+0xb8) builds a sub-obj into step+0xd0; tail-jmp advance helper 0x140af1b40.
IS 0x144588e98 NON-NULL AT THE BARE TITLE? NO (not provably, and almost certainly null). PROOF CHAIN:
 - recon_refs 0x144588e98 = 36 refs, EXACTLY ONE writer: movq_store at 0x140defac4 inside 0x140def650. It is a LAZY block: cmp [0x144588e98],0; jne skip; alloc size 0x40 via 0x141eb9ed0 (allocator [0x143d87358]); ctor 0x140e5f4e0 (vtable 0x142bf3158, +0x28=-1, +0x30=0x1000000); store 0x144588e98; post-init 0x140e5f740. 0x140def650 also lazily builds the sibling session objects 0x143d75b38, 0x143d5afe0, 0x143d5ad68, 0x144589478.
 - 0x140def650 has 0 E8/E9 callers; it is registered as a function pointer (lea at 0x1400b1794) into the SESSION-MANAGER init-step table 0x143d85760 (table builder 0x1400b16f0, also static-init, 0 callers). That table is consumed by the session-manager constructor 0x140dec7c0 (via 0x140dec620 @0x140dec7df). 0x140dec7c0's sole caller is 0x140dec25e INSIDE the 5KB session bootstrap 0x140deb0b0. 0x140deb0b0 is the central session+world setup: it reads PlayerGameData 0x144588de8 and LAZILY BUILDS THE FD4 STREAM WORKER TASK 0x144842d40 @0x140deb360-0x140deb374 (via 0x141eceb10) -- the SAME worker that is the b80 wall. 0x140deb0b0's sole caller is 0x140c8fe9b inside 0x140c8fe90 (a session-lifecycle fn that also sets the quit-latch 0x143d856a0=1 @0x140c8ff41), reached from a thread-entry/worker main 0x140c901af (-> 0x140c90229). So 0x144588e98 + the stream worker are constructed TOGETHER by the session bootstrap on a worker thread, NOT by any title state.
 - CORROBORATION: STEP_BeginLogo(2) 0x140b0c2a0 ALSO hard-asserts 0x144588e98 at entry (@0x140b0c2c3). Ground-truth flow is 10(press)->2(BeginLogo)->3(BeginTitle). BeginLogo runs right AFTER the press and does NOT crash, so 0x144588e98 is non-null BY THE TIME the press is consumed. But STEP_MenuJobWait(10) and STEP_InitMenu(0) -- the only states live at the BARE title -- do NOT reference 0x144588e98 at all (absent from the 36-ref list), and STEP_BeginTitle(3) (the Continue/Load menu builder) ALSO does NOT reference it. So nothing at the bare title requires it, and it is built by the session bootstrap that the press-driven native flow triggers, not before. resmgr 0x143d5b0f8 self-constructs lazily (1302 refs, no rip-relative store; known FD4 lazy singleton) and is reliably available.
CONCLUSION: we cannot instantiate/tick the MoveMapListStep at the press-any-button title because session 0x144588e98 is null there; instantiating idx0 cold would trip the FD4 assert and crash.

=== Q3 MOUNT + SELECT + CONFIRM as callable steps ===
Two independent b80 drivers (confirmed). DRIVER B = the menu, self-contained:
 - HIGHLIGHT -> preview-mount: menu item cb 0x14082ba30 calls initiator 0x14067b4e0(slot): gate b80==0, writes GameMan+0xb98/+0xba0 (preview header), async read 0x140e6ec80, on success b80=1. (0x14067b1a0/0x14067b200 set b80=2; poll 0x140679180 sets b80=3.)
 - idx4 STEP_LoadSaveData 0x140af19b0 -> 0x14067b200 sets b80=2; idx5 0x140af1a30 -> poll 0x140679180 sets b80=3 then reads GameMan+0xc30 via 0x140679560 -> GameFlow resolver 0x14067abd0.
 - WORKER REGISTRATION: advance helper 0x140af1b40 DECODED FULLY: inc [owner+0x4c]; eax=[owner+0x48]+1; cmp eax,0xb; JBE 0x140af1c36 (SKIP). Registration runs ONLY when current_state+1 > 0xb (i.e. on the FINAL/terminal transition). Then: load task 0x144842d40 (build via 0x141eceb10 if null); query [task vtable+0x18]; assert task-ready bit ([rax]>>5 &1); build descriptor via 0x140116c20(&rsp30, name string 0x1429d2b28) holding task+name; ecx=0x59682f01; CALL register 0x142656b00(rcx=0x59682f01, rdx=&descriptor). 
 - REGISTER ABI 0x142656b00 DECODED: rcx=key, rdx=&descriptor{task ptr @+0, name std::string}. Gets scheduler via 0x141eba960, queries vtable+0x18, formats the key into an internal string (0x14011fd30), registers the task-runner. This is the previously-unreversed registration wall; call shape now known: 0x142656b00(0x59682f01, &{task=0x144842d40, name}).
 - c30 in menu path: resolver 0x14067dc00 reads selected-slot fields rdi+0xb5e/+0xb60/+0xb64/+0xb68 (rdi=GameMan 0x143d69918), and @0x14067dcfa writes [GameMan+0xc30]=resolved map (falls back to new-game default packer 0x140720260 when slot==-1). The full CHAR-applying deserialize 0x14067b290 is STILL MoveMapStep-only (sole caller dispatcher-1 0x140afbad0) -- the menu lane sets c30 but does NOT apply the character.

=== Q4 ZERO-INPUT RECIPE / BLOCKER ===
DEFINITIVE BLOCKER for the pure menu-driven angle: the MoveMapListStep cannot be spawned/ticked at the bare title (session 0x144588e98 null -> idx0 assert/crash). What FIRST creates 0x144588e98: the session bootstrap 0x140deb0b0 (session-mgr ctor 0x140dec7c0 -> step table 0x143d85760 -> step 0x140def650), run on a session worker thread, NOT by any SimpleTitleStep state and NOT at boot in the title context. There is NO way to drive the FE-host to a state that creates 0x144588e98 WITHOUT advancing past the press into the BeginLogo/PlayGame native flow (BeginLogo(2) itself already requires it). Therefore the ONLY zero-input complete path remains the native PlayGame drive on the LIVE SimpleTitleStep:
 1. On the live SimpleTitleStep (vtable 0x142b63bb0, committed +0x48==10): SetState(obj,3) via 0x140b0d960 OR call STEP_BeginTitle 0x140b0c5b0 -> builds the real Continue/Load menu + wires owner+0xe0 callbacks (does NOT need session). 
 2. PlayGame confirm 0x140b0e180(playgame_item): sets owner+0xbc=slot, writes InGameStep-child (owner+0x2e0) request, SetState(obj,5). (Selector = cmp [owner+0x284],1: ==1 new-game else load.)
 3. Native pump: STEP_PlayGame(5) 0x140b0d5b0 (asserts inputmgr + 0x144588e98 -- by now built by the native session bootstrap the play flow triggers) -> StepWait -> InGameStep child -> STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 builds CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 + MoveMapStep -> MoveMapStep update 0x140aff640 ticks b80 dispatcher 0x140afa6d0 -> b80 1->2->3 -> deserialize 0x14067b290 (c30=real map + CHAR applied vs PlayerGameData 0x144588268). 
The residual risk is unchanged: at a synthetic/forced FE-host the stream worker 0x144842d40 may not be registered/ticked (b80 stalls). NEW callable lever from this session: replicate 0x140af1b40's tail -- 0x142656b00(0x59682f01, &{task=0x144842d40 (built via 0x141eceb10), name}) -- to register the worker if the native bootstrap does not. But the native session bootstrap 0x140deb0b0 ALSO builds 0x144842d40 itself, so driving the genuine PlayGame path (which runs the session bootstrap natively) is the most-likely way to get BOTH 0x144588e98 and the worker for free.
CRASH RISKS: ticking MoveMapListStep idx0 cold (session null) = FD4 assert crash; poking b80 = AV in deserialize/IO; calling 0x142656b00 with a malformed descriptor = scheduler corruption.

=== MOST-RECENT-SLOT RESOLUTION ===
The Continue/most-recent decision is 0x14067a520 (tail-jmped by 0x14067a6e7): it reads the slot preview timestamp from GameMan+0xb98/+0xba0 (rcx=GameMan 0x143d69918), UNPACKS a packed datetime via bitfield shifts (shr 0x2d/0x27/0x22/0x1d/0x16/0xc, masks for year/month/day/hour/min/sec), compares two slots via the std::chrono helpers 0x141ed5b80/0x141ed5ca0/0x141ed5e80/0x141ed5f30, and -- gated by quit-latch 0x143d856a0==0 @0x14067a66a -- checks GameMan+0xac0 (selected slot) != -1 and sets GameMan+0xb73=1 then GameMan+0xb72=1 (the Continue/load flags; note autoload-stall found these set-but-unconsumed pre-bootstrap). So most-recent = timestamp scan of the slot preview headers (GameMan+0xb98/0xba0 per slot, container 0x143d5df38, 0x2A0-stride records). To select programmatically: set GameMan+0xac0 = the winning slot index; the slot fields +0xb5e/+0xb60/+0xb64/+0xb68 + resolver 0x14067dc00 then produce c30. Slot container 0x143d5df38 is a pointer to a 0x158 mgr; per-slot 0x2A0-stride records are populated by the b80 preview worker, not a standalone writer.

Two sides: (a) semaphores the USER can see (visual/behavioral) that I may/may not have mapped to RAM; (b) RAM oracles I have that the user CAN'T see (so they can't report their values). My job: for every user observable, either point to the RAM semaphore that captures it, or BUILD that semaphore. Concrete: 'freeze' is NOT a soft/hard lock -- it is 'I couldn't SEE or MOVE my character.' That is TWO observables: (1) can't SEE = character not drawn = MAPPED to oracle_chr_draw_group_enabled / oracle_player_render_ready. (2) can't MOVE = NOT cleanly mapped yet -- need a 'player controllable / world-sim-advancing / input-reaches-character' semaphore. IMPORTANT DISCRIMINATOR: in my automated probe's freeze, oracle_play_time_ms was ADVANCING (world sim running) while render_ready=False (not drawn) -- so my probe freeze = drawn-off-but-sim-running, which would let you move-while-blind. The user says CAN'T MOVE too -> the user's freeze may ALSO have sim/control frozen (play_time NOT advancing, or player-control disabled), which my probe may NOT reproduce. So BUILD a 'can-move' semaphore (player-control-enabled flag + character motion/anim advancing + input->character) to ground whether the user's freeze == my probe's freeze. Do not assume they are the same freeze until the can-move axis is mapped and compared. Static RE / runtime-log synthesis after #592: Ghidra xrefs show TitleBackViewParts ctor dump 0x1409a62d0 (deobf 0x1409a6180) is called directly inside CS::TitleTopDialog::TitleTopDialog at dump 0x1409a83b8 when it constructs dialog+0xaa8 with load_info L'05_001_Title_Logo'. TitleTopDialog start-login dump 0x1409b3050 later only calls SceneObjProxy visible setter and TitleBackViewParts::SetVisible on existing dialog+0xaa8; it does not construct the logo surface. Therefore an asynchronous hook installed from install_title_logo_force_hidden_hooks can miss the first TitleBack ctor if TitleTopDialog construction happened before the hook thread applied MinHook. This explains why #592 saw synthetic FileOpener hit but no owned-titleback post-ctor log as a plausible race/timing issue. Next implementation should not rely on catching the already-executed TitleBack ctor to allocate the custom surface; hook TitleTopDialog ctor itself before construction, or use a later guaranteed dialog pointer (start-login/dialog+0xaa8 context) to build/attach the owned surface with a separately grounded constructor/context path.

DECISIVE RUNTIME+RE (2026-06-16, c30-writer function hook on 0x14067bd70 + b80 logging hooks, real user-driven Seamless .co2 load slot 3 map 0xc010000, plus static decode of the caller chain). CORRECTS seamless-co2-load-bypasses-vanilla-deserialize-2026: the .co2 MAP deserialize IS vanilla; ERSC only redirects the FILE IO (so .sl2/.co2 both run the same vanilla deserialize logic, save-agnostic by construction). The c30 writer 0x14067bd70 FIRED and wrote the REAL saved map (c30 0xa010000->0xc010000, ac0 -1->3) at STATE 10 in the menu BEFORE PlayGame.

KEY FACT: the full char/map deserialize 0x14067b290 (the MoveMapStep-DISPATCHER path) still NEVER fired. Instead a SEPARATE vanilla menu-side deserializer ran: THE MENU DESERIALIZE ENTRY = 0x14082c240. Decoded (static): rcx=owner/step-this, rdx=step_context (rsi). It (1) poll 0x140679180 gate -- proceeds only when b80==0-after-3 path; (2) read 0x14067b100(buf,0x280000) HARD-GATED [GameMan+0xb80]==3 (cannot run cold); (3) slot source = [rsi] (first dword of step context) -> set_save_slot 0x14067a810 -> [GameMan+0xac0]=slot (this is why ac0=3 appeared); (4) c30 write via tailcall thunk 0x140677f20 (jmp 0x14067bd70) -> [GameMan+0xc30]=real saved map; (5) PlayerGameData 0x144588268 asserted non-null, char flag-apply 0x140e365c0 (tiny: sets pgd+0x5a/+0x5b; the actual save bytes are applied inside the 0x14067bd70 deserialize). Buffer 0x280000 alloc via scheduler 0x143d872e0[+0x50].

CALLER CHAIN (RtlCaptureStackBackTrace return addrs, frames #0/#1 were our hook trampoline in 0x6ffe region): #2=0x14082c38b (inside 0x14082c240, right after its call to thunk 0x140677f20) -> #3=0x140829bc0 (functor glue call [rax+8]) -> #4=0x14082a240 (FD4 delegate-invoke [this+0x48]/[vtable+0x10]) -> #5=0x140745510 (FD4 step dispatch) -> #6=0x1407aa1f0 (FD4 task-group child iterator, walks [this+0x18]) -> #7=0x140791620 (FD4 step-machine tick). The closure 0x14082c240 is REGISTERED not called directly: registrar 0x14082a970 builds an FD4 delegate {vtable 0x140744d90, fn=0x14082c240}; installed by 0x140828270 into [step+0x70]; the DRIVING STEP = 0x140826d50 (vtable slot 0x142ac71f0) which on first tick registers the delegate and on later ticks invokes [step+0x70] via [vtable+0x10] @0x140826dea. 0x140826d50 is in the menu SaveLoad/MoveMapListStep substep closure cluster 0x142ac71c0..0x142ac7238 (siblings 0x140826180/0x140826ed0/0x140826100/0x140826d50/0x140826140/0x140826e10/0x1408271d0/0x14082af70/0x140829cd0/0x14082c080).

PREVIEW vs MOUNT (both via 0x14067bd70): the PREVIEW call had size=0x215e01 and caller#2=0x14067e21a (inside the slot-record/preview deserialize 0x14067e150) -> c30 UNCHANGED. The MOUNT call had size=0x280000 via 0x14082c240 -> c30=real. So 0x14067e150 = preview (no c30 commit), 0x14082c240 = real mount (commits c30+ac0+char).

VERDICT (callability for zero-input autoload of slot N): 0x14082c240 is NOT safely callable cold/standalone -- (a) its read 0x14067b100 is hard-gated b80==3 (b80 reaches 3 only via the menu async-IO lane that needs the FD4 stream worker 0x144842d40 registered, the same 0x142656b00 ABI wall); (b) it is invoked only via the step delegate [step+0x70] driven by the FD4 step machine on a constructed step this (+0x48 child,+0x68 flag,+0x70 delegate) with slot at step_context[rsi] and slot-record at [rsi+0x38]; (c) asserts PlayerGameData 0x144588268 + session 0x1402567b0. THEREFORE: to direct slot N through this lane, N must be planted in the MoveMapListStep step context [rsi] (the selected-slot field the menu hands to 0x140826d50), NOT by hand-calling 0x14082c240. The genuinely zero-input path remains: drive the native MoveMapListStep menu (which self-registers the stream worker 0x144842d40 via 0x140af1b40 and runs 0x14082c240 natively) and feed it slot N. Observe ground truth: session 0x7ffe.. + slotmgr non-null from boot; csfeman=0 at the state-10 mount (so the menu deserialize does NOT need csfeman, only session+PlayerGameData); csfeman/mms built only at PlayGame(5)->6. SAVE-SAFETY VERDICT (2026-06-18, static decode) for driving SetState(5) after the cold save-load. SAFE given our state. Facts: (1) deserialize 0x67b290 DOES write GameMan+0xc30 from the save: at 0x14067b30e it calls header-writer 0x14067bd70(rcx=GameMan, rdx=buf, r8d=0x280000), which reads the 0x10-byte save header (0x141edfb60), validates header+0 (save-version, 0x1402624c0, <=63) -- NOT the map -- then writes c30 = header+4 (packed map id) RAW at 0x14067be0f. So deser_ret==1 (header validated) PROVES c30 = the char's REAL saved map, NOT the leftover default. (2) There is NO independent PlayerGameData saved-map field; the char-apply 0x14067b434 never gets the map; c30 IS the only saved-map copy. 0xa010000 decodes mAA_BB_CC_DD = m10_01_00_00 = Chapel of Anticipation (legit real map for a level-9 char; 0xffffffff is the cleared/invalid sentinel, set by GameMan ctor 0x140675ea0). (3) SetState(5)/PlayGame picks the world EXCLUSIVELY from c30: native Continue driver 0x140b0e180 reads c30 via getter 0x140679560 -> owner+0xbc (0x140b0e1b7) -> SetState 0x140b0d960(owner,5); play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0 resolves owner+0xbc via 0x14067abd0 -> InGameStep+0x100 (requested world map). PlayGame does NOT consult PlayerGameData. So c30 correctness = 100% determines the streamed world. 

=> SAVE-SAFE GATE for SetState(5): require (a) deser_ret==1 from 0x67b290 (proves c30 written from save); (b) char fingerprint loaded (level=9, stats=[15,10,11,14,13,9,9,7]); (c) c30 != 0xffffffff (not the cleared sentinel) and c30 decodes to a sane packed map. RIGOROUS optional gate: capture the resident save buffer +4 (save device *0x50(0x143d872e0)+4) at b80==3 BEFORE deserialize and require c30 == buf+4 (the one read that disambiguates 'real map == default' from 'default left in place'); if buffer freed, fall back to (a)+(b)+(c). Our state satisfies (a)+(b)+(c). PLUS the harness backup/restore (RESTORE_RUNTIME_SAVES=1, save_safety_ok=1 every run) protects the live save regardless of any in-probe autosave. So SetState(5) is justified + save-safe. CAVEAT: the world-stream HALF after SetState(5) has its own known wall (WorldResWait / full-pipeline-traced-to-worldreswait) -- SetState(5) may fire safely but stall in world streaming; that is a separate sub-problem. Key VAs: deser 0x14067b290 (caller 0x140afbc3e), c30-writer 0x14067bd70 (write 0x14067be0f from header+4), validator 0x1402624c0, c30 getter 0x140679560, Continue/SetState5 driver 0x140b0e180, play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0, map resolver 0x14067abd0. Related: SAVE-DATA-HALF-SOLVED-real-char-loads-cold-zero-input-2026, full-pipeline-traced-to-worldreswait-map-block-streaming, csfeman-built-via-setstate5-breakthrough.

WHY IT FINALLY WORKED: the cold read had been draining only METADATA (preview 0x67b4e0, 0x60000) -> 0x67b290 header-validate failed. STATIC RE BREAKTHROUGH (2026-06-21, read-only deobf scans on eldenring-deobf.bin, ER 1.16.1 base 0x140000000). RESOLVES the handoff blocker 'how to identify the ToS MenuWindowJob before realize runs'. The ToS build functor installed at [MenuWindowJob+0xa8] has _Func_impl vtable = 0x142b28308 (partner/_Func_base vtable 0x142a93ce0). CHAIN (all confirmed by refscan/riprefscan/disasm): MenuWindowJob::realize 0x1407ad1c0 reads [job+0xa8]; if !=0 AND [job+0x10]==0 it does mov 0xa8(rdi),rcx; mov (rcx),rax; call *0x10(rax) -> functor _Do_call. _Do_call slot = vtable+0x10 = 0x142b28318 -> holds adjustor thunk 0x1409b7380 ('add $8,rcx; jmp 0x1409b6070'). 0x1409b6070 = ToS ctor WRAPPER (allocs 0x2f88, calls ctor 0x1409b5970 at 0x1409b60f6) -> builds TosMultiLangDialog (RTTI 0x142b28100). refscan proof: ctor 0x1409b5970 has EXACTLY 1 caller (0x1409b60f6, inside wrapper); wrapper 0x1409b6070 has EXACTLY 1 ref (jmp@0x1409b7384); thunk entry 0x1409b7380 sits in EXACTLY 1 ptr slot (0x142b28318 = ToS functor vtable+0x10). Captures = 16 bytes at functor+8 (thunk adjusts this+8; copy method 0x1409b7029 copies [rcx+8]->[rdx+8]). SAFE RUNTIME IDENTIFIER: the ToS job is the MenuWindowJob whose *(void**)[job+0xa8] == 0x142b28308. A realize hook can clear [job+0xa8] for ONLY that job (no blind zeroing). FALLBACK: realize with +0xa8==0 jumps to 0x1407ad367 which uses [job+0xe8] functor (same call *0x10 pattern); if +0xe8 also null -> 0x1407ad3ec path (call 0x1407ada40). OPEN/CRUX before wiring: does the ToS job have a NON-NULL +0xe8 fallback? If yes, clear-+0xa8 is safe+clean (builds normal component, [job+0x130] non-null). If +0xe8 null, [job+0x130] ends null -> SAME crash mode as the return-null-wrapper (caller derefs). Must read the ToS-job construction/install site to confirm +0xe8. The 4 _Func_impl vtable-write sites (riprefscan 0x142b28308): 0x1409b63f3, 0x1409b7037, 0x1409b71e4, 0x1409b7567 (all internal ctor/copy/move; the EXTERNAL install into [job+0xa8] + its save-present condition is the remaining trace). ER PC shader bytecode = DXIL / Shader Model 6 (NOT legacy DXBC/SM5), confirmed 2026-06-25 by parsing DXContainer chunk FourCCs of extracted members. The 'DXBC' 4-byte magic is just the container; it wraps BOTH SM5 DXBC and SM6 DXIL. Decisive chunk: presence of 'DXIL'/'ILDN'/'RDAT'/'PSV0' + 'ISG1'/'OSG1' (SM6 signature chunks) => DXIL. 'SHEX'/'SHDR' + 'ISGN'/'OSGN' would mean SM5 DXBC. Evidence: /shader/gxflvershader.shaderbnd.dcx members (.vpo) -> SFI0,ISG1,OSG1,PSV0,RTS0,STAT,ILDN,HASH,DXIL ; /shader/gxraytracing.shaderbnd.dcx members (.cpo, DXR lib_6_x) -> SFI0,RDAT,STAT,ILDN,HASH,DXIL. So LLVM DXIL doc (https://llvm.org/docs/DirectX/DXILArchitecture.html) IS the correct reference. Member ext: .vpo=vertex, .fpo=fragment/pixel, .cpo=compute/lib. Tooling: dxc (not fxc), and DXIL disassembly via dxc -dumpbin/-disasm or RGA. The legacy '/shader/' path name is historical; ER still ships SM6 there. /shader_d3d12/ variants are NOT shipped on PC retail. Classifier lives in scripts/shaderlab/extract (ClassifyDx).

ER shader viewer rendering path (resolved 2026-06-25, er-shaderkit crate). ER shaders = DXIL/SM6. Pipeline: extract member (.vpo/.fpo/.cpo DXContainer) -> dxil-spirv CLI -> SPIR-V -> wgpu. dxil-spirv built at ~/tools/dxil-spirv/build/dxil-spirv (HansKristian-Work; the vkd3d-proton converter). er-shaderkit::translate::dxil_to_spirv wraps it and MUST pass --ssbo-uav --ssbo-srv (default typed-buffer lowering emits SPIR-V ImageBuffer capability which naga rejects); naga also rejects write-only storage (UAVs must be read_write). KEY FINDING: of 168 extracted members, dxil-spirv translates 168/168 but naga (wgpu's default SPIR-V frontend) validates only 1/168 -- DrawParameters cap blocks 147 (~every FLVER vertex shader), SampledImageArrayDynamicIndexing 9, StorageImageWriteWithoutFormat 5, write-only globals 6. RESOLUTION (proven on AMD/RADV): wgpu Features::PASSTHROUGH_SHADERS + Device::create_shader_module_passthrough hands SPIR-V straight to the Vulkan driver, bypassing naga; test render::tests::real_er_drawparameters_shader_accepted_via_passthrough confirms the GPU accepts a real DrawParameters .vpo. So the viewer (er-effects-rs-f9t) renders REAL shaders via passthrough (native Vulkan, NOT naga/WGSL) and must supply explicit pipeline/bind-group/vertex-input layouts from DXIL reflection (passthrough has no reflection). er-shaderkit is host-only (not in cargo xwin root check); gpu tests need --features gpu. Building dxil-spirv: run cmake configure+build in ONE 'uv run --with cmake --with ninja' and pin -DPython3_EXECUTABLE=/usr/sbin/python3, else CMake bakes an ephemeral uv-venv python path that vanishes and SPIRV-Tools ggt.py fails with No such file. Full writeup: docs/shaderlab/er-shader-viewer-feasibility.md.

REFUTATION (2026-06-21 runtime, gated offline eldenring.exe, save-safe, self-validating probe). The device-mount hypothesis (b80-fix-target-iodev-mount + b80-mount-routine-0x140e6e8d0-recipe) is FALSIFIED at runtime. Added logging to cold_char_mount FULL-INIT + called mount 0x140e6e8d0(iodev). Result line: 'MOUNT 0x140e6e8d0(iodev=0x1445893b0) al=1 | registry=0x144843050 reg_count=16 | dev40 1->1 dev30 0xffffffff->0xffffffff'. DECISIVE FACTS: (1) iodev+0x40 (device-ready flag) was ALREADY 1 BEFORE the mount -- so the static inference 'cold => iodev+0x40==0 => cold async branch' was WRONG; the full read takes the BOUND branch 0x140e6f430 all along. (2) mount al=1, reg_count=16 (guard 0x14240acd0 passes, 16 workers registered) -- but the mount is a NO-OP for our problem: dev30 (iodev+0x30 OS-handle slot) stayed 0xffffffff before AND after. (3) the full read STILL fails identically: FULL-INIT submit_ret=1 b80=2 io18/io20 SET, then next native frame b80=0 io18/io20 cleared, STUCK at 0 after 240 polls, fp_real=false level=9 c30=0xa010000.

CRUCIAL REFRAME: b80 drops 2->0 (the request is ACTIVELY COMPLETED + handles FREED in one native frame), NOT stuck at 2. A 'worker pool not live / not ticked' wall would leave b80 STUCK AT 2. b80 2->0 means a worker DID process the request and it FAILED/cancelled immediately (microseconds = bad handle / bad path / empty-file, NOT a slow async drain of 0x280000 bytes). So the wall is NOT device-binding and likely NOT (only) session-bootstrap -- it is the read FAILING. dev30=0xffffffff (no persistent OS handle on the iodev) is the prime suspect: the bound read 0x140e6f430 / its worker reads from an invalid handle => 0 bytes => empty completion => processor 0x140e6e080 returns 4 ([iodev+0x18]==0||[iodev+0x20]==0 after free) => poll 0x140679180 writes b80=0.

NEXT: instrument the REQUEST/JOB state at submit time (before it is freed) -- read the request object [iodev+0x18] fields (status, file-path field, handle field), call IO status 0x14240a1f0(request) for the status enum, and find where the worker gets its file handle (iodev+0x30 is -1). Determine fixable-path/handle vs needs-live-session. Compare to native_fullread at the LIVE menu (which DRAINS -- native-fullread-enabled memory says the FD4 IO worker pool is LIVE there). The device-mount call + IODEV_MOUNT_OPEN/GETTER consts can be REMOVED (no-op) but the dev40/dev30/reg_count LOGGING should stay. Relates: b80-mount-routine-0x140e6e8d0-recipe-and-guard-open-question-2026-06-21 (mount is a no-op), b80-fullread-ROOTCAUSE-iodev-unbound-no-file-handle-cold-2026-06-21 (refined: device IS ready, read still fails), stream-worker-registration-is-the-b80-wall-verified-2026, STRATEGIC-PIVOT-B-native-session-bootstrap-chosen-2026.

RUNTIME (2026-06-15). Writing the movie skip-flag *(u8*)0x14458b8a5=1 at title state 10 DISMISSES the movie (game proceeds ~19 ticks) but then HANGS the game task: game_task_ticks froze at 140 (write was tick 121), process alive 18.6s, NO crash/assert (VEH+exit hooks silent), no advance (csfeman stayed 0, never reached menu). So the raw flag write is an INCOMPLETE dismiss. The LEGIT dismiss (what real input does) goes through WM_CLOSE to the MOVIE WINDOW: the skip-flag's sole non-tick writer is the movie WNDPROC 0x140e91130 on WM_CLOSE (uMsg 0x10), and the pre-latch routine 0x140e90120 PostMessages WM_CLOSE to tear the movie window down. Our flag-only write stops the decoder mid-frame WITHOUT the WNDPROC window-teardown side effects, so the fade-out frame-waits (0x140de9f50, 0xc8+0x64 iters) block on frames the disrupted render pipeline never produces -> hang. CONCLUSION: zero-input movie-dismiss IS real (the is-playing gate 0x140e90820 has no input check) but requires the game's FULL legit teardown -- most directly PostMessage(WM_CLOSE) to the movie window (need its HWND; risk: must target the MOVIE window, not the game window). Open question: is a WM_CLOSE window-management message (the exact thing the game's own dismiss does) compliant with the zero-input standard (it is not a synthesized HID/keystate/event)? Related: latch-143d856a0-contradiction-resolved, no-compromises-standard.

MAJOR MILESTONE (2026-06-18, runtime, save-safe save_safety_ok=1, NO write). The COLD-B80 SAVE-IO WALL -- unbroken across DOZENS of prior sessions -- IS BROKEN by the ACTIVATE-byte lever (REFRAME-io-subsystem-present-cold-blocker-is-just-the-active-byte). Sequence (cold_char_mount_drive + new step 0, slot 0, offline-launcher parked title): ACTIVATE 0x140262250(ProfileSummary=0x7fffa0b56d80, slot=0) -> [profile+8+0]=1; preview 0x67b4e0 -> b80=1, io10/io20 set, io18=0; lane 0x679510 x6 -> preview read RESIDENT (b80->0); *** 0x67b200(0) ret=1, b80=2, io18=0x7ffe93785750 POPULATED *** (FIRST TIME COLD -- prior cold experiments NEVER populated io18 because 0x67b200's slot-check 0x140261cd0 failed on the 0-byte; setting the byte first unlocked it); poll 0x679180 x1 -> b80 RESIDENT(3); deserialize 0x67b290(0) ret=1. CONFIRMS the REFRAME: the FD4 IO subsystem (pool/task/iodev) was always present cold; the ONLY blocker was [ProfileSummary+8+slot]==0, settable directly via ACTIVATE 0x140262250. b80 0->1->0->2->3 ALL cold, zero-input, NO session/menu.

REMAINING ISSUE (the char was NOT applied -- focused follow-up): after deserialize ret=1, c30=0xffffffff (the UNSET GameMan-ctor value, NOT written) and LOAD-CORRECTNESS = GARBAGE/default PlayerGameData (level=371, runes=0, stats=[50,50,50,50,50,50,50,50], name='' chr_type=0) -- NOT the real slot-0 char 'a' (level=9, stats=[15,10,11,14,13,9,9,7]). So b80 reached 3 (IO 'resident') but 0x67b290 got NO valid save data. HYPOTHESES (RE next): (1) LANE MISMATCH: 0x67b200 -> 0x140e6eb80 QUEUES a 0x230-byte async deserialize JOB at [mgr+0x18] that loads into ITS OWN buffer + sets [mgr+0xdf0]; but 0x67b290 allocs a SEPARATE 0x280000 buffer and its read 0x67b100 (gated b80==3) takes the [mgr+0xdf0]!=0 FAST-PATH -> returns 1 WITHOUT reading into 0x67b290's buffer -> 0x67bd70 parses an EMPTY buffer -> c30 not written, char not applied. Loading-screen portrait has FOUR distinct bugs (user-enumerated 2026-07-03), NOT one 'texture-residency' bug. Each has a different mechanism + fix:

#1 FREEZE: render/animation stops on a character loaded at an unexpected time (post-Continue, mid-loading-screen). Cause = freeze-after-capture guard: once the first keyed+clean frame publishes, PROFILE_BAKE_RGBA_CAPTURED latches and the per-frame drive stops touching renderers (UAF-avoidance). Head goes static early (animation-stall semaphore: PROFILE_DRIVE_FRAMES_WINDOW << DISPLAY). FIX (unshipped, crash-prone): drive the OWNED spared renderer + relax the freeze; note startup_hooks.rs SPARED-RENDERER DRIVE DISABLED (~line 3801) says driving the spared renderer AVs (+28ms) because its model is freed by Continue teardown. Separate focused effort.

#2 PURE PIXEL GARBAGE: scanline tearing. Cause = readback_resource_rgba8_inner (gpu_readback.rs) uses OUR OWN command queue, no cross-queue sync vs the game rendering the offscreen RT -> rows captured mid-write. FIXED (committed b89bcb7): portrait_tear_score (avg vertical-luma step over masked head) + publish gate keyed&&clean, threshold=10. Validated autostep10 SUCCESS_10: clean 1-7 (1828), torn 40-44 (390) = bimodal, gate suffices; deep GPU sync NOT needed.

#3 WRONG DEPTH MASK: a prior render's depth mask clips the wrong upcoming model. Cause = color/depth DESYNC across a model swap: color RT holds the new head while the depth plane still carries the previous character's silhouette, so apply_depth_alpha_key (gpu_readback.rs:1330) computes a mask that is FRESH but WRONG-SHAPED. keyed+tear cannot catch it (head content clean, only cutout shape wrong). FIX (this change, unshipped-pending-soak): portrait_mask_content_incoherence scorer (startup_hooks.rs) = max(opaque-but-strong-bg rate, strong-fg-but-cut rate) percent; publish gate rejects incoh>25 (conservative first pass, measure-then-tighten like the tear score). Oracles: oracle_portrait_mask_incoherence_last/max, oracle_portrait_publish_skipped_incoherent.

#4 MODEL IDENTITY FLICKER: displayed model doesn't consistently show the SELECTED character throughout load until fully loaded (cross-slot swap, related er-effects-rs-bcy). Cause = drive binds r=table[portrait_loaded_slot()] (ac0=OLD char pre-continue, startup_hooks.rs:3081) while spare uses portrait_target_slot()=SELECTED; readback_offscreen_fast(off) re-resolves largest-reachable RT each frame -> flips to foreign slot as engine rebuilds all 10 models post-Continue; PROFILE_RT_PIN.swap() overwrites the pin each frame (only COUNTS the swap). PARTIAL FIX (this change): skip publish on the exact frame the content-RT pin moves (PROFILE_PUBLISH_SKIPPED_PIN_MOVED) so the make-before-break bridge holds instead of flashing. FULL fix (unshipped) needs identity RE: pin the readback source to the selected character's renderer and reject foreign-slot content deterministically.

All fixes are ADD-ONLY publish-gate conditions (never publish MORE, only hold the make-before-break bridge longer) so they cannot crash more or regress; worst case over-holds -> looks like #1. Related: [[er-effects-rs-bcy]].

LOADING-PORTRAIT ANIMATION -- THE SIX STACKED BLOCKERS (2026-07-03, runs anim-bind1..10, branch fix/loading-portrait-swap-early-popout). User-visible symptom all along: portrait static forever, only 'shadow flicker'. Root causes found+fixed IN ORDER, each masked the next: (1) NO ANIM BOUND: profile pipeline natively binds anim id 0 = static menu pose (FUN_140bbe290 <- refresh); fixed by binding c0000 idle 3000000 via FUN_140bba300 (deobf 0xbba210, fn(r,&id,force,mode)), keyed to (renderer,loc) pair, candidates [3000000,100022,99900] -- menu anim holder is the FULL c0000 anibnd (FUN_140bbb4a0: AnibndRepositoryImp::GetResCap(L"c0000")). (2) REBUILD STORM: our 8-tick feed re-kick re-raised +0x754/755 mid-pipeline (latch==0 does NOT mean never-built; the stepper consumes them) -> model rebuilt ~1/s forever, anim reset to pose 0, lighting reset each rebuild == the visible flicker. Fixed: PORTRAIT_KICK_SLOT_KEY one-kick-per-slot-per-window. (3) STALE-SLOT KICK: portrait_loaded_slot() fallback-to-0 built a foreign slot-0 model ~340ms before ac0 flipped to the real slot (the 'wrong character then swap' the user always saw); storm-free that foreign model PERSISTED and tripped the single-model gate. Fixed: portrait_loaded_slot_confirmed() (None until ac0/OWN_STEPPER_SLOT valid) gates force_profile_render_tick. (4) PUMP DEADLOCK: drive gated on live model, but the kick's reset->rebuild pipeline only advances on update-task ticks -> rebuild needed ticks, gate needed rebuild done. Fixed: STATE-MACHINE PUMP -- update(FUN_140bba820 deobf 0xbba730)+push ticked EVERY frame the renderer is vtable-valid && live_models<=1 (task bodies self-guard). (5) THE 755 LANDMINE (decisive): state machine = STEP_Wait_Request(0x140bba170, consumes 754) -> build -> STEP_Wait_Play(0x140bb9e70; routes 756/755->state7, 754->state1) -> STEP_Finish_Play(0x140bb99d0, state 7 = 6-tick TEARDOWN: unregisters offscreen scene FUN_140bb7a20, destroys model, clears word +0x755/756). 754+755 together = engine's 'teardown old THEN rebuild' for a LIVE renderer; on a MODELLESS renderer 754 is consumed instantly and the armed 755 DESTROYS the freshly built model 6 ticks after Wait_Play. Fixed: kick arms 755 only when +0x778 model live. (6) dt=0: PROFILE_DRAW_TASK_CTX's +8 delta-time reads 0.0 -> anim frozen regardless; fixed: prefer own draw-phase task_data (+8 ~0.021) when captured dt not in (0,1). PROOF (run anim-bind9): latches 0/0/0 at bind, scene_reg=1 (off+0x58 = scene-registered flag; FUN_140bb7340 registers via mgr at off+0x18), model_ok=165 ticks no teardown, rt changed=174/176 (per-frame re-rasterize REAL), alpha_motion (depth-keyed silhouette diff x1000, lighting-immune) last=498 max=1245 vs luma_flicker ~2600. REMAINING LIMITS: (a) mid-load async model build ~13s (kick +16.8s -> live ~+29.5s; world-streaming contention; boot-era build was 133ms idle) so the animated portrait only exists for the tail of fast loads -- consider pre-Continue build + surviving the Continue teardown, or streaming priority; (b) host draw-phase task runs ~14-17Hz during loading (ALL 8 sweep phases drop from 60Hz; Present hook stays 60Hz) capping pump/publish cadence -- consider hosting pump+publish in the Present hook; (c) publish still gated stable_target_only(count==1). Oracles added: oracle_portrait_anim_* (bind state/handle/sentinel 0xffffffff), oracle_portrait_motion_metric_*/luma_flicker_* (32x32 alpha/luma MAD x1000, pin-move + keyed-guarded), oracle_portrait_pump_* (block attribution), portrait-motion log line with anim_t/dt_cap/dt_own/scene_reg, portrait-model-LIVE build-duration stamp.

DECISIVE RE (subagent, disasm-grounded, 2026-06-22) -- the FINAL missing step to complete the zero-input Continue load. The selector 0x1409a8eb0 + dispatcher 0x1409b3070 are BUILD-ONLY: they build the LoadGame job (proper ChainMenuJobs) and RETURN it wrapped (via 0x1407a7b60 into the caller's rdx out-slot); they do NOT post/pump it. In the native flow the per-frame MENU PUMP posts the returned job. There is NO single static select+post entry (the LoadGame build->post is runtime-polymorphic std::function slot-2 _Do_call dispatch, vtables 0x142b267e8/0x142b26a18, no static call site). 0x1409b24e0 is the OPEN-MENU registrar (posts the menu-OPEN job, gated on anim 'Loop' + dialog+0xa40==0; callers in FUN_1409aad60 the title MenuWindow Update) -- NOT the Continue/Load handler. So we must POST+KICK the built job ourselves.

POST = CS::MenuJobQueue::PushBackJob entry 0x1407a9250 (NOTE: 0x1407a9250, the 0x7a... menu-job-queue region -- DISTINCT from the confirm handler 0x1409a9250 in the 0x9a... title region; the team const 0x7a9254 is +4 past push rbx = the same fn, use the real entry). ABI __fastcall(rcx=queue_base, rdx=src) where src is a MenuJob** (DLUT::DLReferenceCountPointer<MenuJob>): reads *src=job, AtomicIncrement(job+8), ring-pushes at queue_base+8; does NOT zero *src or replace the active job. rcx options: (1) dialog+0x10 = the dialog MenuWindow queue (ring dialog+0x18) drained by the per-frame menu pump 0x1409aa680 (iterates active-screen array 0x143d6d8d0 which contains the live owner+0xe0 dialog) -> needs the KICK; (2) owner+0x130 = a MenuJobQueue (ring +0x138 count +0x178) ticked every frame by STEP_MenuJobWait ExecuteMenuJob((MenuJob**)&owner+0x130,&time) -> NO kick needed (team's MENUJOB_PUSHBACK path; but the prior own_load_install_job into owner+0x130 was dead because it built via the FACTORY with wrong args, NOT this properly-chained selector/dispatcher job). KICK = (deref 0x143b37c98)() zero-arg ([0x143b37c98]=0x1409b3ff0, a jmp-thunk into the obfuscated pump trigger; only xref). OVERFLOW-SAFE: PushBackJob chain (0x1407a9250 -> FUN_1407a8910 -> FUN_1407a8fa0 -> FUN_1407a89a0) is a std::deque-style AUTO-GROWING ring (throw_length_error, doubles cap, min 8) -- NOT the cap-8 FixOrderJobSequence (FUN_1407340e0 DLPanic) that killed own_load_pump. Posting one job always succeeds.

GATE: dispatcher's IsNotReleaseFlag55 0x14082cd60 writes *out=(tfc[0x18c]==0)?1:0; dispatcher takes LOAD branch iff that !=0 i.e. tfc+0x18c==0 -- TRUE at settled menu, set nothing. Our earlier dispatcher call built correctly; only post+kick was missing.

FINAL CALLS (zero-input), rcx=owner+0xe0 ([rcx]=dialog, gate vtable 0x142b26468): (1) BUILD into zeroed local MenuJob* slot: selector 0x1409a8eb0(rcx=owner+0xe0, rdx=&slot) after setting tfc+0x14c=1 + slot->mss+0x1200 (our maybe_fire_tfc_continue already does this; out_job = the wrapped ref-count ptr). (2) POST: PushBackJob 0x1407a9250(rcx=dialog+0x10, rdx=&out_job) then KICK (deref 0x143b37c98)(). Relates: selector-invoke-fires-dispatcher-but-load-not-pumped, LIVE-continue-chain-via-selector.

KEEPALIVE -- ROOT MECHANISM, STATIC-RE PINNED (2026-06-30, Ghidra dump decompiles + probes phase0/5/7). The loading head renders only ~4x/loading-screen because of GX SUBCONTEXT POOL CONTENTION with the world render, NOT a phase or readback issue.

DECOMPILE CHAIN (dump VAs): drive(r)=PROFILE_OFFSCREEN_DRIVE_RVA -> FUN_140bb8d90(renderer){ FUN_140bb73a0(*(renderer+0xa8)); }. FUN_140bb73a0(offscreen): gates on *(off+0x48)!=0 + a vcall, then lVar2=FUN_1419e5850(g_GxDrawContext) -- if 0, SKIP everything; else ClearRTV (FUN_1419e0e30 -> FUN_141e8af80) into the popped subctx's RT (subctx+0x25c8) with color FloatVector4_14329e9b0, then FUN_1419eaa40(g_GxDrawContext, subctx) submits+recycles it.

FUN_1419e5850 = GX subcontext STACK POP: ReadLock(gxctx+0xf80)+spinlock(gxctx+0xf70); stack base ptr @gxctx+0xf8, top ptr @gxctx+0x100; if top==base -> EMPTY -> return 0; else pop *(top-8), top-=8, mark used-bit in mask @gxctx+0x110 (1<<(subctx+0x2580 &0x1f), up to 32). FUN_1419eaa40 = PUSH-BACK/return (FUN_1419ee6a0(gxctx+0xf0,&subctx) + ReadRelease) -- so subctxs are RECYCLED (pop=checkout+holds readlock, push=return+release), NOT depleted.

So the pool isn't drained-and-not-refilled; it is MOMENTARILY EMPTY when our drive(r) pops because the concurrent WORLD render (active every frame during world-load: gx queue samples=240 nonempty=240) has all subctxs checked out. Our pop lands only ~4x when one is briefly free. PHASE-INDEPENDENT: probes at phase 0 (Draw_Pre, earliest), 5 (AdhocDraw), 7 (Draw_Post) ALL gave exactly 4 head frames (some~178, checker(black)~174, version=4) -> the world holds subctxs across all draw phases.

FIX DIRECTIONS (keystone epic, crash-sensitive): (1) bounded RETRY of the pop within the frame until a subctx frees (call drive(r) in a short loop; risk: stall). (2) ENLARGE/seed the subctx pool (the mask @+0x110 allows up to 32; find the pool alloc/init and add capacity). (3) a DEDICATED GX context/render path for the profile draw not contended by the world. (4) push a spare subctx onto the stack each frame before drive(r) (need a valid subctx object + the push FUN_1419ee6a0). All need care: a popped subctx's RT is cleared+submitted, so an invalid one crashes.

parity-strict-never-two-frozen-in-a-row-2026-07-18: USER (2026-07-18): has NEVER observed 2 frozen loads in a row. So the good/frozen parity is STRICT and self-correcting: a frozen load ALWAYS recovers on the very next load. good->frozen->good->frozen... deterministically. RENDERDOC DECISION for the load2 20-FPS (user asked 2026-07-20 'should you use renderdoc to see what is halting the render queue?'): RECOMMEND establishing CPU-vs-GPU-bound FIRST, before RenderDoc. Rationale: oracle_fps=20 (frame_ms 49.9) confirmed at load2, but the ROOT is not yet shown to be a render-queue halt. The user's own core-contention diagnosis (parallel cargo starving ER's single-core asset loading) makes CPU single-core STARVATION the leading hypothesis; RenderDoc captures a GPU frame and CANNOT see CPU starvation -- a CPU-starved frame looks normal in RenderDoc and misleads. Cheap discriminator: compare load1 FPS vs load2 FPS in the SAME run (same contention) via the now-whitelisted oracle_fps in the capture rows. If load1~=60 and load2~=20 -> load2-STATE-specific (contention-independent) -> THEN RenderDoc IS the right tool to see what load2's held-loading state (mms=18, fake_cover loading screen up + world) keeps in the render queue (e.g. loading-cover + world double-draw). If both ~=20 -> global contention/GPU-general -> RenderDoc will not reveal it; run ER without parallel cargo. So: measure load1-vs-load2 FPS clean first; RenderDoc only if load2-specific. RenderDoc is a sanctioned observability tool here (AGENTS.md) but track/reuse one window, do not spawn many.

user-input-methods-xbox-elite-and-kbm-2026-07-18: USER (2026-07-18): drives angrE with BOTH an Xbox Elite controller (XInput, likely via Steam Input) AND mouse+keyboard. Both move the char. FRAME-CAPTURE REPLAY HARNESS (2026) — the exact-lighting path for native-shader rendering. Goal: capture a real ER frame's cbuffers+textures (vkd3d-proton->Vulkan, via RenderDoc) and replay one object's draw offline through native .vpo/.ppo on lavapipe with the GAME'S real lighting, so it's 'what the game renders' not an approximation. STATUS: harness built + compiles; awaiting the user's capture run (they coordinate ER launches; DO NOT launch ER yourself).

PIECES:
1. scripts/capture-er-frame.sh — gated direct/offline Proton launch (NO Steam/EAC) + RenderDoc Vulkan layer. Env: ENABLE_VULKAN_RENDERDOC_CAPTURE=1, RENDERDOC_CAPFILE=<dir>/er_cap (->_frameN.rdc), VKD3D_CONFIG=force_host_cached. No auto-teardown (user reaches a lit spot, F12/qrenderdoc Queue Capture, then pkill -x eldenring.exe). Gold save read+write: default stages a WRITABLE COPY (save-safe); ER_EFFECTS_CAPTURE_SAVE_DIRECT=1 uses the original read+write. Requires ER_EFFECTS_GOLD_SAVE.
2. scripts/extract-capture.py — run via  (the standalone renderdoc py module is NOT packaged on Arch; qrenderdoc bundles it). Walks draws, SetFrameEvent, GetPipelineState; GetConstantBlocks/GetReadOnlyResources(stage,True) -> flat UsedDescriptor (1.44); GetBufferData for cbuffers, SaveTexture(DDS) for textures. Records D3D register (fixedBindNumber) + name + stage. --list / --event-id / --match. CAVEAT: RenderDoc 1.44 API member names (fixedBindNumber, UsedDescriptor.access.index) written from docs, NOT validated against a real capture -> first real extract may need fixups.
3. crates/er-objectkit/src/capture.rs — Capture::load(dir): manifest.json + cb_<stage>_<reg>.bin (+ tex DDS). Keyed by (stage, D3D register). serde added to er-objectkit.
4. er-shaderkit::render ObjDrawDesc gained buffer_data:&[(set,binding,&[u8])] (real cbuffers) + textures:&[RealTexture] (real IBL/GI; uncompressed, bpp via format.block_copy_size). Used in draw_object_passthrough (replaces zeroed/gray stubs).
5. crates/er-objectkit/examples/replay_capture.rs — loads a capture, same patch pipeline (neutralize/ssbo-zero/assign_unique_bindings), maps captured cbuffer (stage,register)->our binding via the maps, binds real cbuffers, draws FLVER through native shaders. TODO: texture replay (decode captured IBL/GI DDS via image_dds -> RealTexture) — currently textures still gray-stubbed.

MAPPING KEY INSIGHT: capture and our dxil-spirv come from the SAME DXIL, so map captured resources to OUR bindings by D3D register (cbSceneParam=b8, g_IBLTexture=t30), NOT by the capture's Vulkan (set,binding) (vkd3d-proton uses VK_EXT_descriptor_buffer / a different layout). The captured cbSceneParam carries the real VC_MatrixViewProj (camera) + lighting.

NEXT: user captures 1 frame in a lit outdoor area -> extract -> replay -> (likely) fix extract API names -> add texture replay. RenderDoc 1.44 + RADV captures vkd3d-proton cleanly (descriptor-buffer bug is AMD-proprietary only).

KEY: own_stepper ALREADY HAS the TitleTopDialog -- STAGE1d drives dialog=0x7ffe96a82fd0 (runtime addr; reachable via owner+0xe0 / the STAGE1d self-fire that calls registrar 0x1409b24e0 on it; its TitleTopDialog::Update state machine shows fadein/loop/textfadeout). CONFIRMED mechanism of the 2nd-character depth-mask desync (commit c265074, run system-quit-repro-selfdrive IoU fail-fast 2026-07-03). It is NOT a stale mask-CACHE reuse: masks are recomputed fresh every frame (oracle_depth_key_fresh==applied==publish, oracle_portrait_mask_stale_reuse==0 -- the first fail-fast semaphore, incarnation-tagged cache-reuse, could NOT fire and did not). It IS a FRESH-but-WRONG mask: the mask/head IoU semaphore fired at the 2nd character at +54.8s with IoU 10%->14%->7% (a CORRECT mask is ~70% with this metric = kept cutout vs colour-head overlap), 20 sustained sub-25% frames -> abort. So the 2nd char's COLOR (head) renders correctly (user sees it) but the DEPTH the mask is computed from is WRONG/STALE CONTENT -- even though the deterministic bundle-paired pointer (f493933) resolves the correct depth RESOURCE, that resource holds content that does not match this head. Matches the user clue 'only the first character shows rendered in the ProfileSelect menu'. 

OPEN QUESTION for the FIX: WHY is the 2nd char's depth content wrong while its color is fresh? The color+depth are read coherently (single fence) from the same bundle, so they are the same frame -- yet the depth silhouette != the color head. Leading hypotheses: (a) the render pass writes COLOR but not DEPTH for the non-first character (depth write disabled/skipped) so the depth buffer retains prior content; (b) the game does not RENDER the non-first char's portrait scene at all (menu lazily renders only the first/highlighted slot), so our drive captures color from our own-built renderer but the depth buffer was never freshly written for this char. INVESTIGATE: does our drive (per_frame_push_hook DRAW task) rasterize DEPTH, or only color? Is the non-first char's model actually being DRAWN each frame (spare log said 'model built at menu' for all slots, but built != re-drawn/depth-written)? FIX direction: drive a fresh depth render for the non-first char before capture, OR gate publish on the mask/head IoU being high (skip the wrong-mask frames, bridge holds -- but that reintroduces the freeze). The IoU semaphore (oracle_portrait_mask_head_iou_last, MASK_HEAD_IOU_MIN=25, MASK_HEAD_ABORT_STREAK=20, repro-gated abort) now fails-fast in ~55s so each fix attempt is fast to score. Related: [[loading-portrait-coherent-depth-VALIDATED-plus-2nd-char-residual-2026-07-03]] [[loading-portrait-4bug-taxonomy-2026-07-03]].

HARD RULE (user, repeated feedback 2026-07-19): do NOT gate a product FIX behind a marker text file (e.g. reload_*_enabled() checking game_dir.join('er-effects-*.txt').exists()) NOR an env var. A marker file is semantically identical to an env gate, which AGENTS.md already forbids for product features ('Release/default behavior must not depend on agent-only environment variables'). For a fix I believe in (RE-backed), make it DEFAULT behavior gated ONLY on the real runtime condition (e.g. FRESH_DESER_DONE==1, mms18, the actual switch-reload path) -- then boot/run to validate: KEEP if it works,  the commit if it does not. The runtime run itself is the A/B; do not add a hidden toggle. This applies to the whole reload fix chain (fd4io residency, case-7 b80 drain, STEP_MoveMap_Update defer, b73 return-title-latch hold): de-marker-gate them to fire on their genuine runtime conditions. Diagnostic-only telemetry may still be marker/env gated, but a behavioral FIX must not be. Why I kept doing it: reflexively cargo-culting the 'A/B vs baseline' diagnostic pattern onto fixes. Stop.

Static RE: Scaleform/MenuResource load_info semantics for custom TitleBackViewParts. MenuResource ctor calls FUN_140d6a2f0 to init refcount holder, FUN_140d6a460(result,'_level0') to get the root value, then copies the incoming two-qword load_info to resource+0x48/+0x50. Deep loader FUN_140d7ac70 treats load_info as at least {u32 kind/state at +0, u8 cache_policy/flags at +4, wchar_t* filename at +8}: it checks byte +4 for value 2 to reuse a queued/cached record, uses param_2[1] as UTF-16 filename, copies it to a fixed string, searches cache trees/lists, allocates a 0xe8 load record through FUN_140d6fd30, and registers it depending on flags. TitleTopDialog passes first qword 0x0000000100000005 and filename L"05_001_Title_Logo", so state/kind=5 and byte+4=1 is a known-good visible title-logo load_info shape. For synthetic/custom TitleBack diagnostics, use the same two-qword shape with a stable UTF-16 filename pointer; do not pass ASCII/raw temp strings or assume the filename is copied before loader/cache use.

3RD-LOAD SOFTLOCK ROOT SHARPENED + PRIOR MEMORY CORRECTED (Ghidra 1.16.1 dump, in-repo scripts/ghidra/rt/, 2026-07-16). Watcher caught the exact InGameStep step-state on the softlock: cur_step(+0x48)=7 next_step(+0x4c)=7 (self-loop), requestCode(+0xd8)=1. DEFINITIVE step table from the registrar FUN_1400a3710 -> InGameStep_StepperArray (dump 0x143d70130): 0 STEP_Initialize, 1 STEP_InitWait, 2 STEP_RequestWait, 3 STEP_Finalize, 4 STEP_MoveMap_LoadlistInit, 5 STEP_MoveMap_LoadlistWait, 6 STEP_MoveMap_Init, 7 STEP_MoveMap_Update (0x140aec810), 8 STEP_MoveMap_Finish (0x140aec140). SO STEP 7 = STEP_MoveMap_Update, which is AFTER STEP_MoveMap_Init (step 6). STEP_MoveMap_Init decompile PROVES it allocates(HeapAlloc 0x4c0)+constructs MoveMapStep::MoveMapStep+RegisterStepTask(TaskLineIdx_InGame_MoveMapStep). THEREFORE the MoveMapStep child IS created+registered on load 3 -- the earlier memory third-load-movemap-not-registered-confirmed-2026-07-16 conclusion ('MoveMapStep never created/registered') is WRONG; it registers and RUNS, it just never FINISHES. STEP_MoveMap_Update gates on FUN_140eb5550(ezChildStepBase): reads EzChildStepBase->stepper (the MoveMapStep child), returns child's isFinished vcall; null stepper=>true. On softlock the child stepper is NON-NULL and its isFinished vcall returns FALSE forever, so step 7 returns early (stays at 7); only when the child finishes does step 7 set field24_0xd8=2 (requestCode 1->2) + call FUN_140aed250 to advance to step 8 STEP_MoveMap_Finish. requestCode staying 1 == 'MoveMapStep child load not finished'. So the real stall is INSIDE the MoveMapStep child's world-load completion (world-res streaming), NOT the orchestrator/bc4/save-drive. This matches the user screenshot (custom bar stuck at 'LOADING SAVE' idx7, never reaches world markers) and the user hypothesis 'game requires most of the startup sequence before loading a save' (the world-res streaming subsystem state). submit.rs phaseC/phaseD already RE'd this exact region: gap1 block-load-states not created, gap2 streaming disabled resmgr+0xb7c1==0 (resmgr = deref(deref(MoveMapStep+0xf0)+0x10)). NEXT: read the MoveMapStep child internal state on the softlock (mms=InGameStep+0xe8, mms internal step+0x48, wrm=mms+0xf0, resmgr=wrm+0x10, b7c1 streaming-enable, block count b3140, target-area load-states) vs a successful load1/2 to find what accumulates over 2 cycles and breaks the child's finish on load3. Ghidra query toolkit now IN-REPO at scripts/ghidra/rt/ (clean OSGi bundle), via scripts/ghidra/query.sh.

USER-CONFIRMED runtime finding 2026-07-02: the Elden Ring 3D world is NOT rendered during a map load -- it only renders once loading FINISHES. Tested by forging a fully-transparent texture in place of the MENU_Load_* now-loading background (via the loading_bg_replace_bind_hook forge -> build_portrait_tpf/build_loading_bg_replacement_tpf): the background read BLACK, not the world. So 'disable the loading screen to watch the world pop in' is not achievable -- there is nothing behind the loading screen to pass through to. This settles the seamless-load idea: the loading screen REPLACES the world render, it does not merely cover a rendered-but-hidden world.

Static RE iteration after #622 mapped the successful Scaleform bind-apply path far enough to choose the next runtime oracle. The root lookup vfunc recorded as 0x14116ce70 is a real tail-entry inside Ghidra-merged FUN_14116ca20; disassembly shows it resolves a named display object through root+0x70, then root+0x70+0x18+0x40 name tables (not a visibility setter). FUN_141148050 writes the transform matrix into the replacement target object at target+0x20, allocating it if absent. FUN_140d60950 releases the old current child at display_obj+0x18 unless it is the inline +0x20 child, AddRefs the replacement target, releases display_obj+0x48, stores target at display_obj+0x48, and resets display_obj+0x18 to display_obj+0x20. Therefore #622 proves a named display object was found and its child reference was replaced; the blank screen is likely not caused by missing bind/apply. The next runtime hook should record dimensions/type/visibility inputs: target byte +0x70, target width/height from +0x2c/+0x30 or +0x84/+0x88, old child vtable/type/width/height, display_obj vtable/type, and maybe root+0x70/container parent info, to distinguish empty SYSTEX source from hidden/alpha/parent-container blankness.

COLD SAVE-DIR pre-submit fix REFUTED at runtime + corrected static facts (supersedes the recipe in b80-COLD-FIX-savedir-builder-...). RUNTIME (savedir-fix-20260621 probe): cold_char_mount ran to FULL-INIT (submit_ret=1 b80=2, io18=0x7ffe937856d0 io20=0x7ffe93785910, static iodev=0x1445893b0 dev40 1->1 already mounted, reg_count=16, worker noaccept=0, reg_list non-empty). SAVE-DIR step SKIPPED because path_db=[iodev+0x20]==0 BEFORE submit (it only becomes the request handle io20 AFTER submit). STATIC RE of live opcode-0x17/0x18 handler 0x140e6ded0: builds dir via 0x140e0e680(rcx=&u16string) then calls SETTER 0x14240a2a0(rcx=[this+0x20], edx=slot=0, r8=char16_t* RAW DATA PTR -- computed as cap>=8 ? *(strbase) : &strbase, NOT the std::u16string object). CORRECTION 1: setter r8 is a raw char16_t*, not a string object (my impl passed the object -- wrong). CORRECTION 2: path_db=[this+0x20] where  is a TRANSIENT per-request COMMAND object, NOT the static iodev -- the pump 0x140e6e080 BAILS if [this+0x18]==0 OR [this+0x20]==0, and [iodev+0x20]==0, so this != iodev. Setter internals (decoded): rcx2=[path_db]; if rcx2!=0: entry=0x14240c270([path_db],[path_db+8]); if entry: 0x14240dce0(entry, slot, char16_t*); then 0x14240c630([path_db],[path_db+8]). So path_db must be a fully-formed container (transient). CONCLUSION: directory is populated on a transient per-request command object during its state-machine pump, NOT on a pokable global pre-submit. NEXT: find the request copy-ctor TEMPLATE source -- request ctor 0x14240a850 -> copy-ctor 0x1424085b0 deep-copies a path descriptor (dir wstring at owner+0xe0); if that template is a static/global, set it ONCE pre-submit and all requests inherit it (the clean fix). Decode 0x14240a850 to find the copy-ctor src (rdx). ALSO: the unguarded REQ-DIR-READBACK I added (deref io18+0xe8/+0xf8 then loop-read 160 u16) LIKELY CRASHED the run (log ends at FULL-INIT, process_exited_before_ready); make any such readback use safe_read_usize + bounded sane size.

FAST-TEARDOWN CRITERION for FPS runs (user 2026-07-20): as soon as load2 (world two) is in-world AND its framerate is validated to be NOWHERE NEAR load1 baseline for at least 3 seconds, TEAR DOWN IMMEDIATELY -- do not wait the full cap. CORRECTION (user, 2026-07-16): the re-load soft-lock is NOT save-borne. ER does not persist online/session state to a save, so the stale state that stalls the handoff is RUNTIME state accumulated in the PROCESS across the load sequence -- same CLASS as bc4, a 2nd stale-teardown runtime singleton, NOT a property of the specific save's content. The 'this save always locks' pattern is really a load-SEQUENCE/accumulation pattern (user loaded ~6 saves fine, then it locked; repro was load X, load top-slot Y, load X again). CORRECTION (subagent, 2026-06-15, objdump on local eldenring.exe) resolving the prompt tension that prior recipe offsets read garbage on J=*(owner+0x130) at inner-TitleStep state 10. J is NOT the leaf input-poller; it is an AND-COMBINER. J vtable=0x142aa2958 has only 3 slots: +0x00 dtor 0x140744d90, +0x08 0x140792090, +0x10 update 0x1407aa1f0; vtable+0x18 is RTTI 0x419930522 NOT a fn ptr (calling it crashes, matches prompt). J ctor 0x140792100 builds a 0x70-byte obj, sets [J+0x68]=1. Update 0x1407aa1f0 iterates children (index [J+0x10], count [J+0x60], array [J+0x18]); per child calls child-vtable+0x10, default out=2, needs child result==2 (predicates 0x7a9200 >1 AND 0x7a9210 ==2) to advance; returns 2 only when ALL children accept. So J+0x180/J+0x1e8 are meaningless on J (garbage as measured). keycode@+0x180, event@+0x130, verdict path live on J CHILD = LEAF input node (vtable 0x142aa97e8, update 0x1407ad1c0): reads keycode WORD [node+0x180] (sentinel 0xffff, guard <0x47), WRITES keystate or BYTE[inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 +0x90 +keycode],1 (0x7ad509) and |=3 via 0x744dd0 (0x744ed2), event-array via 0x766340 (mov [inputmgr + id*4 +0xdc]). MenuJobWait 0xb0d400 advance 10->11 still gated SOLELY on byte [0x143d856a0] (writer 0x140c8fe90 intro-finish). Lever per native-confirm-input-injection-keystate but offsets must come from the LEAF node not J. Related: native-confirm-input-injection-keystate, title-accept-is-fundamentally-input-gated.

(2) FORCE-STOP DISMISSES THE MODAL IN ONE CALL: force_dismiss_startup_dialog now calls force-stop 0x14078dfd0(rcx=dialog) (NOT OnDecide 0x140927ba0 -- OnDecide only MOVED the selection to OK without closing). The builder hook 0x1409275b0 captures each created MessageBoxDialog (vtable 0x142b03550) into CONNECTION_ERROR_DIALOG; force_dismiss force-stops it each frame gated on vtable + closing latch, clearing the capture when done. Minor 1-frame flash between re-trigger and dismiss.
