DECISIVE CORRECTION (2026-06-30, runtime-measured): the GX SUBCONTEXT POOL is NOT the cause of the ~4x loading-head refresh. The handoff/keepalive-gx-subcontext-pool-contention-ROOT theory ('world holds all subctxs, our pop fails 96%') is REFUTED BY DIRECT MEASUREMENT.

ADDED a read-only oracle (profile_gx_queue_sample in startup_hooks.rs): pool free-depth = (*(gxctx+0x100) - *(gxctx+0xf8))/8 each frame, monotonic min = PROFILE_GX_POOL_FREE_MIN; +0x110 used-mask popcount = N. RESULT (postcontinue-lookat-smoke, Banon, autoload ON): gxpool[free_min=18 free_last=18] -- the pool had >=18 FREE poppable subcontexts on EVERY frame across the whole loading screen. The pop NEVER fails. Same run: render_drive_hits=223, readback_some=173, readback_checker(black)=169 (97.7% black), rgba_version=4, overlay_reuploads=4, char=Banon, msgbox=0, player_present. So: drive(r) submits the rasterize ~per frame, the pool is always available, yet the readback reads BLACK 97.7%.

=> The entire 'enlarge/seed the GX subcontext pool' epic (the handoff's recommended next step, find subctx count in Initilize@0x1419e7d10) is DEAD -- do not pursue it. GX Initilize sizing facts (still true, for reference): the 0xf0 pool count N = min(descriptor[0x80]_config + clamp(GetCPUThreadCount,2,16), 32), 32-bit used-mask at +0x110 caps distinct subctx indices at 32; on this 16-thread box N would hit 32 if config>=16. But MOOT -- pool isn't the bottleneck.

REAL ROOT CAUSE (now strongly indicated, confirming keepalive-readback-races-gx-rasterize-black candidate): CROSS-QUEUE GPU SYNC. readback_resource_cached_fast (gpu_readback.rs:674) creates its OWN DIRECT queue (RB_FAST_QUEUE), transitions the content RT COMMON->COPY_SOURCE and copies, signaling only ITS OWN fence -- it NEVER waits on the GAME's GX queue fence. So our copy reads the RT before the game's GX rasterize into it completes -> black 97%, head only on lucky 4x timing alignment.

IN FLIGHT: added a DEFERRED-READBACK diagnostic (read the content RT at START of the draw tick, before this frame's drive(r), capturing the prior frame's now-Presented=GPU-complete result) -> counters readback[defer_some defer_nonblack] in the lookat-phase-sweep debug line. If defer_nonblack>>4 => H2 cross-queue timing confirmed, fix = sync/read-at-settled-point (or cross-queue fence Wait on game's GX queue). If defer_nonblack~=4 => H3 rasterize genuinely not landing in this texture. Next run decides. Relates: keepalive-readback-races-gx-rasterize-black-2026-06-30 (now confirmed over the pool-contention theory), HANDOFF-portrait-keepalive-2026-06-30.

The COLD-B80 DRAIN IS SOLVED (b80->3 cold via ACTIVATE-byte, io18 populated -- COLD-B80-WALL-BROKEN), but the CHARACTER DESERIALIZE hits the documented async-IO lane mismatch (async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker). Diagnostics: after ACTIVATE 0x140262250 + slot-mgr-peek 0x140678a50 (save path set) + preview 0x67b4e0 + 0x67b200 (io18 populated, b80->3): deserialize 0x67b290 ret=1 BUT c30=0xffffffff (0x67bd70's header-validate 0x1402624c0 FAILS -> c30 not written) + PlayerGameData garbage (level=371, stats=[50x8]). df0=0 (not fast-path), c30_gate 0x143d68078 SET. So 0x67b290 read INVALID/empty data. ROOT: 0x67b200->0x140e6eb80 QUEUES a 0x230-byte async deserialize JOB at [mgr+0x18]; the iodev going resident (b80->3) is that JOB's read completing, NOT slot 0's full 0x280000 save in 0x67b290's buffer. 0x67b290 allocs its OWN 0x280000 + reads via 0x67b100/0x140e6e380 which reads the iodev request = the 0x230 job (wrong size/data) -> header invalid -> bail. The async JOB is supposed to mount the char itself (worker fills [mgr+0xdf0] + mounts), but df0 STAYS 0 cold = the job's deserialize step never runs (no in-game MoveMapStep dispatcher 0x140afbad0 ticking it at the parked title). 

NEXT (the precise remaining gap, save-safe): (1) drive the async job (mgr+0x18) to completion -- find what processes the 0x230 job (0x14240a850 ctor; the worker/dispatcher that fills df0 + mounts the char) and tick it cold; OR (2) make 0x67b290 read the FULL save: the preview/0x67b200 read must be slot 0's full 0x280000 valid save, not the 0x230 job -- determine which initiator/op reads the full save into a parseable iodev request cold (op/edx of 0x140e6eb80; the preview 0x67b4e0 is 0x60000); OR (3) use the MENU deserialize 0x14082c240 (live-mounts via io18/io20, real-load-c30-mount-write-confirmed) instead of the dispatcher 0x67b290 -- but it needs the selector-step context. The cold-b80 IO drain is the hard part and it is SOLVED; this is feeding/processing the right deserialize buffer. SAVE-SAFE throughout (no SetState, no write). THE REMAINING GAP (focused): the built dialog's SAVE-SLOT ROWS ARE EMPTY. STAGE2-ACTIVATE: 'want=0 target=0 cursor_now=0 bound=0 lav=0x1409a4670 ret=2 io18=0x0 io20=0x0' -- [dialog+0xb08] (row bound) = 0, so load_activate 0x1409a4670 had NO valid row -> registered no real selector step -> MOUNT-POLL: ac0 stayed -1, c30 stayed 0xa010000 (new-game default), io18/io20 never populated, mount_done=false through 180+ waits. ROOT CAUSE (hypothesis, high-confidence): the ProfileLoadDialog rows are populated by the dialog's OWN per-frame Update (ProfileLoadDialog::Update = [vtable 0x142b229f8 +0x10] = 0x1409a3860) driving the async 'Checking save data' scan (rebuild_rows 0x14078d2c0 / set_items 0x14078ec90 reading the save files). In the NATIVE flow the menu task ticks the dialog each frame so the rows populate. Our STANDALONE direct-built dialog is NOT registered with the menu task, so its Update is NEVER ticked -> the save-data scan never runs -> bound stays 0. stage2 'pass-through so the native pump ticks the selector' assumes the dialog/selector is menu-task-registered, which a direct-built dialog is not.

NEXT (the fix, focused): after DIRECT-BUILD, TICK the dialog's Update 0x1409a3860(rcx=dialog, rdx=&out, r8=&frametime) each frame (and/or register it with the menu task) until [dialog+0xb08] (bound) > 0 (rows populated), THEN call load_activate. (b) is there an explicit rows-populate fn (rebuild_rows 0x14078d2c0 / set_items) to call directly with the save data? (c) ProfileLoadDialog::Update ABI (rdx/r8) -- decode 0x1409a3860 before ticking. Movement analog stick input boundary (dump 1.16.1). Game reads player MOVE stick from a per-device normalized-stick field, NOT directly from XInputGetState. Chain: GLOBAL_FD4PadManager @ RVA 0x485dc20 (dump 0x14485dc20; +8 after GLOBAL_DLUserInputManager @ 0x485dc18 = our DluidInputManager) -> FD4PadManager.padDevices (DLFixedVector<FD4PadDevice*,4> at +0x18, entries inline at +0x18, count +0x40) -> FD4PadDevice -> +0x8 = concrete DLUserInputDevice -> normalized stick f32: +0x89c leftX, +0x8a0 leftY(FORWARD=+1.0), +0x8a8 rightX, +0x8ac rightY; +0x890 u16 buttons; +0x8bc/+0x8c0 triggers. Device poll = FUN_141f6bac0 dump / deobf 0x141f6bad0 (shift +0x10), vtable+0x38 method run per-device per-frame from FD4PadDevice::Update FUN_142663580 <- FD4PadManager::Update dump 0x142667c60 / deobf 0x142667c70. Poll writes stick from ONE of 3 sources chosen by device fields: +0x894 XInput user idx (>=0 => XInput, norm=2*axis/65535 in [-1,1], divisor DAT_1429fbee8=65535.0), +0x7f8 DirectInput dev ptr, +0xa0c ScePad handle / +0xa38 ScePad read callback. Manager: DLUserInputManagerImpl.useLibScePad toggles ScePad-vs-DirectInput enum (EnumerateDevices 0x141f295b0). XINPUT MYSTERY: FUN_141f29000/deobf 0x141f29010 polls XInput idx0-3 every cycle just for DETECTION regardless of active pad, so 361 XInputGetState polls do NOT prove XInput drives gameplay; if Steam Input presents pad via ScePad/DInput the player-padMap device reads +0xa38/+0x7f8, never XInputGetState -> hooking XInputGetState only touches a deprioritized phantom device. FAITHFUL injection: hook FUN_141f6bad0 (device poll), call original then overwrite this+0x89c/+0x8a0 -- single convergence point for all 3 sources, below OS/Steam, per-frame, consumed unchanged downstream. Field overwritten every frame so write must be post-poll each frame. Dump VAs are semantics-only; use dump-deobf-shift.py for any hooked address.

Static RE 2026-06-27: FUN_140749290 is a constructor-time MenuResource-to-SceneObjProxy resolver, not a runtime texture/display bridge. It reads MenuResource+0x60, derives a name from MenuResource+0x48, constructs a Scaleform object from MenuResource+8, optionally assignComponentWithName(name), and copies the result into param_2. TitleBackViewParts ctor FUN_1409a62d0 sets vtable, constructs MenuResource at this+0x8, then calls FUN_140749290(this+0x8,this+0x70); other callers are similar view-part constructors/update helpers. FUN_1407acbf0 builds MenuWindowJob objects around CSScaleformLoadInfo and callbacks, so a separate custom surface must be constructed as its own MenuWindow/Scaleform target or engine draw surface; rebinding the already-live 05_001_Title_Logo SceneObjProxy through FUN_140749290 late is the wrong layer.

Static RE of FUN_140ce8320 after allocator crashes: Ghidra shows its caller FUN_140d6b580 first looks up the requested Shift-JIS filename in GLOBAL_GfxRepository via FUN_140ce7810(GLOBAL_GfxRepository, wide_name), which calls FD4ResCapHolder::GetResCap<T>(GLOBAL_GfxRepository+0x78,...). Only if that lookup returns a nonzero resource-cap object does FUN_140d6b580 call FUN_140ce8320(rescap). FUN_140ce8320 is not a general File/MemoryFile allocator: it expects param_1 to be a native GFX resource-cap object with name/string fields at +0x18/+0x30 and payload pointer/size at +0x78/+0x80; it allocates a 0x30 MemoryFile via Scaleform allocator vfunc+0x50, constructs a Scaleform string ref from the rescap name, and calls MemoryFile ctor with rescap payload bytes. Therefore synthetic er_effects_portrait_cover.gfx cannot be served by calling FUN_140ce8320 unless a real GLOBAL_GfxRepository rescap exists or a fully native-shaped rescap is built/inserted. Manual MemoryFile construction was the wrong layer. Next route: either insert/register a GfxRepository resource cap for a custom GFX, or hook/substitute an existing native GFX request/resource-cap payload at/after repository lookup; do not return ad-hoc File objects from FileOpener.

PATH A STATUS (2026-06-22): the menu-free world-asset streaming is the DEEP SUBSYSTEM the RE predicted, now backed by 3 concrete walls hit in sequence: (1) DIRECT-ENQUEUE lever dead -- AddDefaultFileLoadProcess (deobf 0x142658c60) is 2-arg needing an FD4FileLoadProcess object we can't synthesize, and FD4TaskManager::PushTask needs a constructed ExecutableTask descriptor + the task-mgr singleton; phase-1's real requester uses CSFileImp::AddFileCap not this fn. (2) PHASE MACHINE TICKS but FROZEN -- WorldBlockRes::Update ran 550k times yet every block stuck at phase [+0x35]=2 (cap never loads). (3) Even READING the cap state CRASHES -- resolving the WorldBlockRes via block->vtable[+0x10]() (the native readiness getter) faulted in our menu-free context (process_exited_before_ready, ticks=767) when a block appeared. CONCLUSION: every Path A lever requires the in-game LOAD CONTEXT that only the native load constructs (FD4 load-process objects, the step-machine that enqueues + advances per-block, the getter being valid). So A converges on B: to complete the world-stream you must drive the native in-game load = Path B (native_continue, already built + clean). The FD4 IO worker THREADS are alive (common blocks stream at boot) -- the gap is purely that m28's per-block load is never enqueued/advanced without the native in-game step context. RECOMMENDATION: probe Path B (native_continue) for the actual playable-world result; A's pure menu-free version is fully RE-mapped (worldblockres-phase2-handler-assetload-lever / fd4-loadtask-worker-lever) but is a multi-session subsystem re-implementation with no cheap lever. TOOLING/WORKFLOW (2026-06-16, banon/CachyOS er-effects-rs). The Cupcake bash guard requires an explicit tool-level timeout (<=30s: Bash 'timeout' ms or 'timeout_seconds') on EVERY agent-run Bash invocation -- INCLUDING run_in_background:true commands. A backgrounded command with NO timeout is DENIED ('missing Bash tool timeout parameter'), same as a foreground one. So the pattern for long jobs (cargo build, runtime probes) that exceed the 30s cap is: run_in_background:true AND set timeout (e.g. 30000ms) AND redirect output to a log file (> /tmp/x.log 2>&1); the 30s cap applies to the FOREGROUND launch handoff, the detached job keeps running past it; then poll the log file / wait for the completion task-notification. Do NOT try to dodge by omitting the timeout on a background command -- it is rejected. Also: foreground 'sleep' is blocked as a sync mechanism; wait on the task-notification or poll an event/log file instead. Related: er-runtime-probe-fast-teardown, dont-pause-when-authorized.

NATIVE MENU-JOB QUEUE + PUMP mechanism (RE 2026-07-01, dump via Ghidra MCP; live=deobf addrs). QUEUE at system_dialog+0x10: queue[0] (=+0x0) = current running MenuJob* (the HEAD); queue+0x8 = FixOrderJobSequence of PENDING jobs; queue+0x30 = second slot. READY-to-submit gate FUN_1407a9320 (live 0x7a9230): returns 1 iff queue[0]==0 && queue[0x30]==0. SUBMIT FUN_1407a9340 (live 0x7a9250): AddRef job then FUN_1407a8910(queue+0x8, job) appends into the pending FixOrderJobSequence. PUMP = ExecuteMenuJob(MenuJob** head, FD4Time* dt) live 0x7a96f0 (dump 0x1407a96f0; called from many Update() fns): currentJob=*head; if(currentJob){ AddRef; (*currentJob->vfptr[2])(currentJob,&result,&dt,...)  // vfptr[2] == Run; bVar=MenuJobResult::ShouldContinue(&result); if(bVar && *head==currentJob){ unref; *head=0; } // POP head } }. ShouldContinue(MenuJobResult*) live 0x7a92f0 = (Continue < result->state) -- TRUE means TERMINAL (Success/Failed > Continue), so the pump POPS the head only when Run writes a TERMINAL result. MenuJobResult states: Continue is the lowest; Success/Failed are > Continue. SetResult live 0x7a92d0. CONSEQUENCE for the System-Quit-Load-Profile fix: the stuck ProfileSelect MenuWindowJob (0x7ad1c0, at queue[0]) never clears because with its load blocked/faked its Run returns non-terminal -> queue never ready -> return-title chain (builder 0x79d700) never submits. FIX: make ProfileSelect terminate cleanly so queue[0]->0 (preferably drive its native Cancel/Back close which also removes the owner MenuWindow via AddCancelButton 0x920c90 + list-push-restore 0x733ef0; forcing a terminal MenuJobResult in our MenuWindowJob::Run hook is the blunt alternative but risks leaving the menu-window stack inconsistent), THEN submit the return-title chain (or route to native Quit-Game action 0x140961640) with autoload target slot armed. Related: [[system-quit-return-title-queue-blocker]] [[system-quit-chrasm-crash-3way-fork]].

PARTIAL-RETRACTION of b80-REAL-rootcause-df0-gate over-claim that DLUP/GATE-B is 'irrelevant'. Direct disasm shows the owner FSM's state-machine handle owner+0x20 IS built FROM the DLUP registry key. deobf base 0x140000000.

owner getter 0x140e6e060: mov rax,[0x144589390]; if null je 0x140e6f6f0 (create); else ret. So *0x144589390 (=static obj 0x1445893b0) is the owner; lazily created via 0x140e6f6f0.

0x140e6da37 = owner+0x20 (state-machine handle) LAZY BUILDER (rcx=owner):
  if([rcx+0x10]||[rcx+0x18]||[rcx+0x20]) skip (jne 0x140e6db23);
  else: reg=0x141ee1110() (DLUP settings registry); 0x141ee10b0(reg,&key@rsp+0x60,r8d=0) (INDEX-0 key; =0xFFFFFFFF if registry empty);
  node=0x141eb9ed0(ecx=0x20,edx=8,r8=[0x143d87358]); 0x141ee05f0 copies key; handle=0x14240ac00(rcx=node,rdx=&key,r8d=0,r9=[owner+8]); [owner+0x20]=handle.
=> owner+0x20 state machine is keyed by DLUP registry index-0 id. Cold/empty registry -> key=0xFFFFFFFF -> handle may be malformed/not idle-ready -> poll 0x140e6e080 won't reach success index 0x14 -> b80 bounce.

owner ctor-ish static init 0x140e6d970 zeroes static obj 0x1445893b0: [+0..+0x28]=0,[+0x30]=0xffffffff,[+0x50..]=0x14251c2e0(rcx=+0x50,edx=0,r8d=0x60),[+0xb0]=0.

OPEN (agent tracing): (1) is 0x140e6da37 called on cold path / who drives owner init + owner+0x18 population? (2) does cold key 0xFFFFFFFF actually block reaching index 0x14 (i.e. does DLUP registry need a real entry -> earlier registrar/rebuild work 0x141ee1590/0x141ee1110 becomes relevant)? (3) 0x14240ac00 handle builder + 0x14240c270 current-node: idle-ready when key=0xFFFFFFFF? Minimal zero-input path to owner index 0x14. Recommend reconciling with b80-GATEB-* memories (NOT fully irrelevant after all).

STATIC RE FINDING (2026-06-22, Ghidra dump pc_eldenring_runtime.1.16.1, XrefSaveDataObj.java) -- DE-RISKS the c30-write gate for the OWN-LOAD hook path. QUESTION: 0x67bd70 (c30 writer) only writes the real map if gate object [0x143d68078] != 0; is that object built at game BOOT (so it's present when we OWN-LOAD at the menu) or only by the FSM save-load path we bypass? ANSWER: BUILT AT BOOT.

[0x143d68078] (deobf addr; dump obj+0x0) has exactly ONE non-destructor writer: FUN_140679970 = the GameMan MASTER INITIALIZER. It HeapAllocs GLOBAL_GameMan itself, then inits CSEventState, CSEventFlagMan (HeapAlloc 0x270 = the recipe's '0x270-byte save-data subsystem object'), CSNetworkUserManager, CSTestUserNameManager, CSBlockPlayerList, CSSessionManager, GeomFlagSaveDataManager, and registers GameMan's update task. A fn that allocates GLOBAL_GameMan is the boot-time core init, called ONCE at startup -- NOT the per-load FSM. The only other writer is CS::GameMan::~GameMan (0x140678970), which zeroes it at teardown. So [0x143d68078] is a GameMan-lifecycle global: present from boot through the whole session.

IMPLICATION: when our OWN-LOAD runs at the rendered menu (long after boot), [0x143d68078] is already non-null, so 0x67bd70 c30-write gate (a) PASSES without the FSM load. The remaining c30 conditions (b) stream-reader 0x141edfb60 returns 0x10 and (c) slot-index bounds 0x1402624c0 both operate over OUR provided buffer, so a valid slot body should satisfy them. Net: the 67b100-hook + native-67b290 path should yield a REAL c30 (not the 0xa010000 default), removing the recipe's main save-safety worry. STILL GUARD c30 != default + char fingerprint before SetState5.

CAVEATS: dump-derived semantics (addresses shifted ~0x10-0x11 vs deobf; not re-grounded to the exact deobf write site); did not 100% pin whether 0x143d68078 is GLOBAL_CSEventState vs another GameMan global, but it is definitively GameMan-lifecycle/boot-built either way, which is the answer that matters. Tool: /tmp/ghidra_scripts/XrefSaveDataObj.java. Relates: own-load-step3-hook-67b100-feed-buffer-2026-06-22, own-load-67b100-callers-static-2026-06-22, coldmount-c30-is-the-single-key-write-conditions-and-recipe-2026, OWN-LOAD-buffer-fed-replication-recipe-deobf-2026-06-22.

PERSISTENT PROBE-DESIGN (user 2026-07-17): for a runtime PROBE that only measures (does not fix the bug), I must know EXACTLY (1) which semaphore I am ADDING (the measurement), and (2) which semaphore the teardown listens for, and they must be the SAME RELIABLE event (or the teardown keys directly on the measurement's own log line). Tear down 1 second after that semaphore. NEVER tie teardown to a different, possibly-silent signal than the one carrying the measurement (the a935261a failure: census gated on the flaky WORLDRES-GETTER while teardown also waited on it, but it never emitted -> soft-lock). CORRECT PATTERN: DLL emits the measurement on a RELIABLE stall semaphore (e.g. the SWITCH-ORACLE mms_step=3 path that always ticks), logs a clear DONE marker, and the monitor tears down 1s after that exact DONE line. The measurement event IS the teardown trigger. Before launching any probe, state precisely: added-semaphore, teardown-semaphore (=same reliable event), and the 1s delay. Links [[monitor-census-flaw-arm-on-mms_step-not-only-getter-2026-07-17]] [[persistent-fix-run-design-before-rerun-no-wasted-drives-2026-07-17]].

MECHANISM CRACKED (2026-07-06, live diagnostic caller-chain capture [0xd6a8ff,0x7ad261,0x7aa2bb,0x793346] + Ghidra dump decompile). Under Seamless the autoload stalls because the online-service ToS is a JOB inside a CS::FixOrderJobSequence (Run @ dump 0x1407aa2e0). FixOrderJobSequence::Run iterates jobs at currentJobIndex, calls each job's vfptr[2] (Run), and ADVANCES to the next job ONLY when that job's MenuJobResult is BOTH ShouldContinue AND IsSuccess; otherwise it breaks (stays on the job). The ToS job is a CS::MenuWindowJob whose Run = dump 0x1407ad2b0 = deobf/live 0x1407ad1c0 (MENU_WINDOW_JOB_RUN_RVA=0x7ad1c0, the generic scaleform menu-window-job Run we already hook for system-quit). That Run sets loadParams->jobResult from owningMenuWindow->field254_0x1e8: while the ToS window is up it reports Continue (waiting for accept) -> sequence stalls. Our Option-A ctor-wrapper null-suppress (0x1409b6070) makes the build fail -> Run SetResult(Failed) -> also !ShouldContinue -> stall forever (observed +17s..+108s idle). FIX (not yet implemented): in the MenuWindowJob::Run hook, identify the ToS job and force loadParams->jobResult = Success (state that satisfies IsSuccess && ShouldContinue) so FixOrderJobSequence advances past the ToS -- the SAME pattern as network-check-shortcircuit (which already returns Success for NetworkCheckJob). Target the ToS job via our ToS ctor-wrapper (0x1409b6070, fires exactly for the ToS) latching the current MenuWindowJob::Run 'this' (job ptr), then return Success for that job ptr. Menu id is at owningMenuWindow+0x180 (<0x47). Ties to tos-privacy-never-valuable-official-servers-only (ToS is official-servers-only, safe to kill; ERSC relay independent). Deobf<->dump shift in 0x1409b region +0x150, in 0x1407a-0x1407d region +0xf0.

angrE-reload-FULL-causal-chain-and-fix-2026-07-18: COMPLETE end-to-end root cause of angrE goal failure (1/4 then crash), DLL 08939c99. CHAIN: (1) boot autoload navigates the ProfileSelect/LOAD-GAME flow to load angrE; (2) that trips system_quit_arm_quickload_autoload (system_quit_repro_guards.rs:1064; callers system_quit_hooks.rs:273 'ProfileSelectLoadJobRun' and system_quit_ownership_repro.rs:1230 'ProfileSelectSlotActivate') which STORES SYSTEM_QUIT_QUICKLOAD_PHASE=RETURN_TITLE_REQUESTED (line 1211) -- arming a post-load return-title reload; (3) load #1 completes and is stable in-world ~47s; (4) but because phase stays >=RETURN_TITLE_REQUESTED, the IN-WORLD branch task_registration.rs:254-275 keeps calling product_core_autoload_tick every frame while the player is present; (5) once queue-ready + ProfileSelect-closed align (~+97962ms) the return-title chain SUBMITS (profile_rows_system_quit_menu.rs:921 system_quit_submit_direct_return_title_chain), tearing down the GOOD load; (6) the reload sticks at MoveMapStep 18, force-advances (rt5d/force flags), tears down, repeats; (7) after several forced cycles -> game ASSERT + AV rva=0x1eb9999 -> crash. Note product_core_autoload_tick's own stable-proof CANNOT fix this: it lives in the player-NOT-present else-branch (task_registration.rs:40) so it never runs once a player exists; and its ig_d8==STABLE_IN_WORLD(2) never holds in-world (ig_d8==1 always while player=true, ==2 only during teardown). DESIGNED FIX (not yet implemented): in the in-world armed branch (task_registration.rs:254), detect a SPURIOUS arm by sustained continuous in-world presence while phase>=RETURN_TITLE_REQUESTED (a genuine user return-title tears the world DOWN within ~1-2s, so player would vanish; 47s of stable presence proves it's spurious). After a threshold (~300 frames/~5s) set QUICKLOAD_PHASE=IDLE + FRESH_DESER_DONE=1 + disarm b78/save_requested/warp_requested, so the return-title chain (gated on phase!=IDLE) never submits and load #1 persists. Loads #2-4 then come from user native System->Quit->Continue via the working boot-autoload path. Needs a new atomic counter + rebuild + live validation. Links [[angre-stable-proof-title-owner-gate-bug-2026]] [[angre-load1-stable-then-destructive-reload-cycles-2026]].

er-tpf Tier-4 in-memory texture wire-up, static-RE resolved 2026-06-28 (Ghidra pc_eldenring_runtime dump; CALL/PATCH addrs grounded to deobf via dump-deobf-shift.py). VERDICT: NO-DISK FEEDING IS FEASIBLE. The engine has a SYNCHRONOUS raw-(ptr,len) TPF->GPU path that needs no VFS/CSFile name and no disk: GXCGTextureBuilder_TPF (dump 0x141a004e0 / deobf 0x141a004c0) takes (this, tpfBytePtr=Tpf003Header*, tpfLen, ...) and builds a Tpf003FileAccessor straight over our in-memory bytes (it calls Tpf003Header::TextureCount + Tpf003FileAccessor over param_2; uncompressed TPF003 => accessor reads the embedded DDS directly, no Oodle). The wrapper FUN_140b83ec0 (dump 0x140b83ec0 / deobf 0x140b83dd0)(holderObj, tpfPtr, tpfLen, flags, count) loops it and for each texture calls FUN_140b81200 (dump 0x140b81200 / deobf 0x140b81110)(GLOBAL_TexRepository, name=NULL, builder, flags) which, when name is NULL, DERIVES the repo key from the TPF entry name (FUN_141a00950(builder)) and inserts a TexResCap into GLOBAL_TexRepository via FUN_140b80b70. So our er-tpf blob entry-name becomes the in-RAM TexRepository key (NOT a disk path). CONTRAST Route A (loading-screen ReplaceTexInfo): ReplaceTexInfo::LoadTpf (dump 0x140d69880 / deobf 0x140d697d0) opens by NAME via FUN_1401f55e0(GLOBAL_CSFile, name) (dump 0x1401f55e0) == same shape as LoadTpfFileCap (dump 0x1401f5110): it allocs a TpfFileCap, flags|=0x20, builds an FD4BasicHashString from the name, and AddFileCaps it with a LoadTask (FD4FileCap+0x80). With a null/default LoadTask the named load resolves through the mounted EBL/BHD VFS == DISK. The LoadTask IS the abstraction seam (DLMemoryInputStream exists, dump 0x141edf9e0) so an in-memory LoadTask would also avoid disk, but that is strictly more plumbing than the offscreen builder. STRUCT LAYOUTS: TpfFileCap size 0x98 = FD4FileCap(0x90){super_FD4ResCap 0x78; loadProcess@+0x78; loadTask@+0x80 = byte source; loadState@+0x88 (==4 means LOADED); flags@+0x89} + TexResCap* texResCap @+0x90. TpfFileCap does NOT own texels; TexResCap (ctor dump 0x140b826a0) owns a CSGxTexture* gxTexture (alloc 0x20 from GfxHeap) = the decoded GPU resource, plus fields @+0x80/+0x88/+0x90. CSScaleformReplaceTexInfo size 0x50 (ctor dump 0x140d74410 / deobf 0x140d74360): vfptr@0, refCount@8, DLString name@0x10(alloc@10,buf@18,len@28,cap@30), encodingType byte@0x38, tpfFileCap@0x40, READY byte@0x48, @0x4c=0. Q3 ANSWER: +0x38 is NOT a flag, it is the name DLString DLCharacterSet encodingType (ctor sets 1=UTF16; LoadTpf overwrites with name->encodingType). +0x48 READY: ctor sets 0; SET TO 1 by FUN_140d79150 (CSScaleformImp update tick, dump 0x140d79150 / deobf 0x140d790a0) which walks the ReplaceTexInfo list at CSScaleformImp+0x1df0, and when FUN_140d696c0(rti) reports the tpfFileCap loaded it registers each tex (FUN_140d69690 count/FUN_140d69640 get/FUN_140d7c9f0 bind) THEN writes rti+0x48=1. The DRAW tick GetReplaceTexInfo->Update (FUN_14090a270 dump 0x14090a270) just swaps whatever GetReplaceTexInfo (dump 0x140909f90 / deobf 0x140909ea0) returns into drawObj+0xc40 regardless of +0x48; +0x48 gates the texel-resolve/Scaleform-register in FUN_140d79150. RECOMMENDED TIER-4 ROUTE = B (offscreen, zero-disk): (1) er-tpf emits uncompressed TPF003 blob whose single Tpf003 entry name = our chosen SYSTEX key; (2) on the game render/resource thread call FUN_140b83ec0(holder, tpfPtr, tpfLen, flags, 0) [or GXCGTextureBuilder_TPF + FUN_140b81200(GLOBAL_TexRepository, NULL, builder, flags)] to build+register the CSGxTexture/TexResCap under the entry name, NO disk; (3) promote the bind observer we own (deobf 0x7451c0 = dump FUN_1407452c0) from observer to replacer: its real work is FUN_140d6ac00(GLOBAL_CSScaleform, targetName, symbolName) (dump 0x140d6ac00 / deobf 0x140d6ab50) -> mutex -> FUN_140d7c0c0 (dump 0x140d7c0c0 / deobf 0x140d7c010) -> FUN_140d7be60 (dump 0x140d7bdb0 = deobf 0x140d7bdb0; THE bind primitive, name resolved via FUN_140d7c850). Call/patch the bind with target = OUR TexRepository name so the menu image symbol (GFX tag 1009 ext image, MENU_*.tga) resolves to our texture. Route B is the ONLY path natively fed by raw (ptr,len) with zero VFS, the name is an in-RAM key not a disk path, and it reuses the seam we already own. Route A (hook GetReplaceTexInfo 0x140909ea0 to return a hand-built CSScaleformReplaceTexInfo) works for the LOADING SCREEN specifically but needs us to hand-populate tpfFileCap->texResCap (built via the same offscreen builder) + either set +0x48=1 ourselves or let FUN_140d79150 flip it == strictly more plumbing. DDS FORMAT: GetDDSFormat (dump 0x141e97ee0) for a DX10 header (pf flags&4 and fourcc==DX10) reads the DXGI enum at +0x80 and passes it through FUN_141e983a0 (validate/remap) -> so BC7(98) MUST use a DX10 header; legacy header masks map RGBA8->28, BGRA8->0x57/0x58, DXT1->DXT1 etc, so uncompressed RGBA8 can use a legacy header and skip FUN_141e983a0. REMAINING UNKNOWNS: (a) GXCGTextureBuilder_TPF/FUN_140b81200 create+upload a GPU texture so MUST run on the game render/resource thread with the live D3D12 device (confirm the existing CSTaskImp game-task thread is valid for GPU resource creation, else marshal to the render task); (b) confirm FUN_141e983a0 accepts DXGI 98/71/28; (c) exact Tpf003 per-entry name field offset the builder reads (FUN_141a00950). PORTRAIT PER-SCENE TONEMAP FILTER (RE, goal 2026-06-30). The portrait scene's tonemap/color-grading block is CPU-writable and per-scene: filter = *(GXSceneContext+0xbf50) where GXSceneContext = *(scene+0x38) and scene = *(off+0x48) (off = the renderer offscreen, renderer+0xa8). The block at filter+0x30..+0x11c is copied VERBATIM into the per-frame tonemap constant buffer by the RECORD fn FUN_141b8cc30 (set once at ctor, writes persist). Key fields: gamma at filter+0x60 (default ~2.2f), exposure SCALE at filter+0x8c (default 1.0f; shader exposure = global_exp * filter[0x8c]), and an UNTOUCHED validation field at filter+0x64 (~250.0f ctor default, use as a content guard: 100<guard<500). Env LIGHTS/IBL are GLOBAL, but exposure/tone/color-grading is PER-SCENE, so writing filter+0x8c changes ONLY the portrait. 

REMOVED from the render path 2026-07-03 (commit pending): the 'proof pass' that wrote gamma=1.0 + exposure=8.0 every drive frame. That 8x overexposure blew out the portrait for the few drive frames per window (freeze-after-capture limits the drive to a few frames, clustered at the transition) = the USER-OBSERVED 'luminosity spike a few frames pre/post transition, both characters'. It ALSO blew out the colours enough to break the mask/head IoU semaphore's fg/bg classification (false-low IoU 7-14% -> the MASK-HEAD-MISMATCH abort). Hypothesis under test: removing it fixes BOTH the luminosity spike AND the IoU false-fire. If a REAL per-scene exposure/tone change is ever wanted for the portrait cover, this is the field to write (tie it to product autoload state, not a proof-pass). Related: [[loading-portrait-2nd-char-desync-CONFIRMED-freshwrongmask-2026-07-03]].

DEFINITIVE static RE (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY). Q1 CONTROL MECHANISM for own-the-stepper. SECTION FLAGS (recon_section.py reading PE section Characteristics): ALL THREE step-fn tables are in WRITABLE .data -- SimpleTitleStep table 0x143d71580, InGameStep table 0x143d70130, MoveMapStep table 0x143d70860 all fall in section .data rva [0x3b11000..0x4862ab4) chars=0xc0000040 [INITIALIZED_DATA,READ,WRITE] -> WRITABLE. So a step-table function-pointer entry can be repointed at OUR fn with a plain store (no VirtualProtect needed; though VirtualProtect/restore is still good hygiene). By contrast: FD4StepTemplate::Execute dispatcher 0x140b0bd60 is in .text [CODE,EXECUTE,READ] READ-ONLY (hooking it needs a trampoline + page reprotect). The step-object vtables are in .rdata READ-ONLY: SimpleTitleStep vtable 0x142b63bb0, InGameStep vtable 0x142b5d408, MoveMapStep vtable 0x142b5ff38 all section .rdata chars=0x40000040 [INITIALIZED_DATA,READ] -> READ-ONLY (vtable patch needs reprotect). CONCLUSION: the cleanest control point = PATCH A STEP-TABLE ENTRY in the writable .data tables (option a), NOT hook the .text dispatcher or .rdata vtable. Which table+index to patch is the next question (which is dispatched at the title each frame). Related: scheduler-fe-host-is-simpletitlestep-decoded-2026, own-the-stepper-strategic-direction-2026.

STEP-3 ROOT LEAD FOUND (Ghidra 1.16.1, version-stable, 2026-07-16): the null load-state at STEP_WorldResWait is because the LOAD LIST was never built for the target area. STEP_MoveMap_LoadlistInit (InGameStep step 4, dump 0x140aec660) builds the loadlist ONLY inside 'if (param_1->worldloadlistlistVirtualPath.size != 0)': it calls CSFileImp::CreateLoadlistlistFileCap(CSFile, worldloadlistlistVirtualPath, &field_0xb3610) -> param_1->loadlistlistFileCap, and a dlc02 one from PTR_u_map__WorldMsbList_dlc02... -> loadlistlistFileCap_dlc02. If worldloadlistlistVirtualPath is EMPTY (size==0), the whole block is SKIPPED -> loadlistlistFileCap stays null/stale -> no world-res block load-states created -> block->vtable[0x10] getter returns null (blk_ls=0) -> STEP_WorldResWait (child step 3) waits forever. This EXPLAINS the measured null load-state WITHOUT needing the version-blocked block-getter RE. HYPOTHESIS: on the first switch (native-boot m60 overworld -> legacy m28), our feed/continue_confirm/SetState5 path leaves InGameStep->worldloadlistlistVirtualPath EMPTY or STALE (m60's path), so LoadlistInit builds nothing (or m60's list) and m28's block never gets a load-state. Later switches work because a prior load set the path. NEXT: (1) find worldloadlistlistVirtualPath + loadlistlistFileCap offsets in InGameStep (disasm 0x140aec660) and instrument them at the stall -- confirm empty/stale + loadlistFileCap null; (2) find WHO SETS worldloadlistlistVirtualPath on a normal map load (likely STEP_RequestWait/RequestMoveMap or the load-request that our switch bypasses) -- that setter is the fix target: ensure the target map's loadlist virtual path is set before LoadlistInit on our switch. InGameStep RVAs are 1.16.1-stable so this whole path is RE-able in the existing dump (NO 1.16.2 dump needed). Supersedes the getter-RE-blocked conclusion for the immediate next step. Builds on step3-handoff-getter-re-blocked-by-1162-version-mismatch.

CORRECTED understanding (2026-06-21, runtime, lever-3 commit a036de0). The ToS (TosMultiLangDialog) builds on ANY zero-input title->menu advance, NOT just the broad global accept byte. Three advance mechanisms all reached the main menu (menu_opened_latch=1) but ALL built the ToS (total_builds=1, native_legal_popup_detected): (1) global accept byte 0x144589bdc; (2) lever-3 = native handler 0x1409b1260's singleton-flag set [0x143d5dea8]+0=1 + registrar self-fire. Lever-3 advanced WITHOUT the process-exit crash (vs the accept+null-suppress combo), but did not avoid the ToS. So the ToS build is INTRINSIC to the title->menu advance sequence in this probe environment.
WHY (hypothesis): the persistent ToS-accepted state is not satisfied in the headless probe, so the advance INVOKES the ToS build. Run 243 showed the per-instance policy flag owner+0x29c0 before=1, yet the ToS still built -> the build-invoke is gated UPSTREAM on a DIFFERENT (persistent/global) accepted state, likely the global gate 0x140e4fda0 (a deobf trampoline jmp 0x145ba1c41) that the ToS accept-predicate 0x1409b72b0 checks. Run 244 proved the ToS wrapper 0x9b6070 builds UNCONDITIONALLY once invoked -> cannot gate it at the build; the gate is on whether the advance INVOKES it.
CONSTRAINTS (locked): cannot null-suppress the ToS in the advance path (caller derefs null -> crash); cannot auto-dismiss (= simulated input, user-forbidden).
NEXT (the real fix): RE the UPSTREAM decision during the title advance that invokes the ToS build -- decode the global accepted gate 0x140e4fda0 (follow the trampoline to 0x145ba1c41) and the persistent ToS/EULA-accepted storage (config/registry/save-bnk), and SET that accepted state zero-input BEFORE advancing so the native sequence naturally SKIPS building the ToS (the same way vanilla-with-accepted-ToS skips it). That is the only path that avoids the modal without suppress-crash or dismiss-input. ZERO-INPUT ADVANCE ITSELF IS SOLVED (lever-2 accept byte + lever-3, both menu_opened_latch=1, simulated_button_presses_total=0). Related: title-global-accept-byte-144589bdc-zeroinput-advance-2026, tos-multilang-modal-suppression-target, title-accept-to-registrar-narrow-path-143d5dea8-2026.

ROOT CAUSE (2026-06-15) of the 'session degradation' where the ER game task freezes/exits monotonically earlier each run (e.g. ticks 158, 154, 115, 107) before our code runs: the harness teardown SIGKILLs eldenring.exe / start_protected_game.exe but LEAKS the wine/proton infrastructure -- orphaned winedevice.exe (14 seen) and bwrap (7 seen) processes accumulate across the session (PIDs spanning the whole session). These stale wine processes lock/corrupt the Proton prefix state, so each new launch's game task stalls early. It is NOT memory pressure (53GB free), NOT Steam-client state (restarting Steam did not help), and NOT our DLL (the freeze happens before our tick gate). FIX/UNBLOCK: kill all orphaned winedevice.exe + bwrap + leftover srt-bwrap/pv-adverb (SIGKILL, none are needed once the game is gone), then relaunch -- the next run reaches full duration again. PERMANENT FIX NEEDED: make the runtime_probe.sh teardown kill the whole wine process tree (winedevice.exe, bwrap, wineserver, services.exe, explorer.exe, plugplay.exe), not just eldenring.exe, so orphans never accumulate. Until then, sweep orphans between runs.

DECISIVE DECODE (2026-06-16, subagent on decrypted on-disk eldenring.exe 1.16.1, base 0x140000000; .text foff<->VA: VA=foff+0x140000a00). WHY the own-stepper STAGE1d registrar call (0x1409b24e0) does not reliably open the main menu, and the zero-input save-safe fix.

=== SM: CS::TitleTopDialog::update 0x1409aac10 (foff 0x9aa210), SM at [dialog+0xa60] ===
State name-keys are INLINE ASCII at the descriptor VA (NOT vtables): 0x142a90500=FadeIn, 0x142a8f9e8=Loop, 0x142b264f0=TextFadeOut. is_in_state 0x140749b20 and set_state 0x1407499e0 BOTH end in a name strcmp gated by (current_node.flags & 0x8f) >= 2 (node must be SETTLED). States: FadeIn (intro fade anim) -> on anim-complete -> Loop (idle press-prompt loop, settled) -> registrar transitions to TextFadeOut (menu-list active).
FadeIn->Loop is NOT input-gated: it advances on ANIMATION completion (predicate built by 0x14075a4d0; OR-fallbacks 0x14078ae50/0x14078ae40 are STUBS xor al,al ret, so only anim-complete settles it). INPUT path (rejected lever): 0x1409aadf3 call 0x140e85f50 = movzx eax,byte[0x144589bdc]; ret (accept byte); if set & latch==0 -> registrar. Writing 0x144589bdc IS input synthesis -> banned.

=== WHY STAGE1d NO-OPS ===
At SetState(2)->3->10 forced pump the dialog current node is FadeIn UNSETTLED (flags&0x8f < 2). registrar 0x1409b24e0 internally calls set_state(sm,TextFadeOut) which re-checks flags&0x8f>=2 and bails. The registrar ALSO runs item-registration UNCONDITIONALLY but every entry is keyed to the TextFadeOut list ([r13+0xa48], desc 0x142b264f0 -> 0x1407ab370 @0x1409b2816) so entries stay DORMANT until SM enters TextFadeOut. Registering harder (option d) does NOT help. NO unconditional set_state (sibling 0x140749a20 also gated) -> option c dead.

=== FIX (zero-input, save-safe) ===
Let engine OWN per-frame update 0x1409aac10 tick the dialog until FadeIn anim settles node to Loop (flags>=2), then call registrar 0x1409b24e0(rcx=dialog). Detect via read-only probe. Fallback only if anim never fires: force [node+0x20] |= 2 then call (option b, brittle, log it). Save-safe: registrar only builds menu UI.

=== READ-ONLY PROBE (dialog=owner+0xe0) ===
sm=dialog+0xa60; inner=*(sm); vt=*(inner); node=vt[+0x08](inner) [call [rax+0x8]]; flags=*(u32*)(node+0x20) & 0x8f (>=2 settled); name=name_getter 0x140d82310(node). Stuck: flags<2 name=FadeIn; worked: flags>=2 name=Loop then TextFadeOut. Also accept byte 0x144589bdc, latch [dialog+0xa40]. All reads.

IMPLICATION own_stepper PHASE_MENU_BUILD: replace blind clear-latch+retry with: each frame read SM node flags+name; if flags&0x8f>=2 call registrar once; else RETURN (let native update tick FadeIn). cross-dll-union-A3-A4-must-land-together-2026-07-18: HAZARD when wiring the two-DLL hook union. A3 = trace DLL routes its ~40 hooks through the product's er_effects_union_register export (single MinHook instance). A4 = the PRODUCT DLL must ALSO install its OWN hooks on the OVERLAPPING addresses (0xb0e180 continue-confirm, 0xb0d960 title-SetState, etc.) through register_union_hook, NOT raw MhHook::new. If A3 ships without A4: depending on thread order, the trace's union dispatcher can be created on a shared address BEFORE the product's raw MhHook::new runs -> product's MH_CreateHook returns ALREADY_CREATED -> the PRODUCT's own critical detour (e.g. continue-confirm) is DROPPED -> product breaks. Same MinHook instance => no memory corruption, but silent handler loss either direction. RULE: do not run the two-DLL config until BOTH A3 and A4 are done. A4 requires each overlapping product detour to be <=4 int/ptr args (union ABI). Links [[two-dll-hook-union-required-2026-07-18]].

SETUP: at bare press-any-button state 10 (BeginTitle SKIPPED), idx10 PHASE_MENU: register stream worker (0x140b0a980 stub -> worker 0x144844020 built OK), set GameMan+0xb78=slot, preview 0x14067b4e0(slot), arm synthetic MoveMapStep owner (0x140-byte zeroed, +0x12a=1 to skip CSFeMan-apply, +0x12c=-1). PHASE_DRIVE: per-frame call dispatcher-1 0x140afbad0(synth) + dispatcher-2 0x140afb880(synth); success = c30 != 0xffffffff (deserialize wrote it).

RESULT (drive log): drive#0 b80=1 io10=0x7ffe.. io18=0 io20=0x7ffe.. -- the preview 0x14067b4e0 DID start the iodev read at the BARE title (save-IO subsystem works at state 10, no session needed for the read to START). BUT drive#60..540 b80=0 with io10/io18/io20 ALL CLEARED -- the b80==1 preview lane COMPLETED and TORE DOWN the iodev request, leaving b80=0 and NO resident request. dispatcher-2 NEVER transitioned b80 0->2 (b80 stuck 0 the whole 600 frames; c30 stayed 0xffffffff). drive TIMEOUT. THEN post-timeout pass-through showed c30=0xa010000 (default appeared late).

TWO HARD CONCLUSIONS:
(1) 0x14067b4e0 is the WRONG initiator for the mount: it is the b80=1 PREVIEW lane (iodev fields io10+io20) which completes+CLEARS the iodev request. The real-load capture's deserialize-ready state was the b80=2 lane (io18+io20, NOT io10) which the MENU sets via idx4 0x14067b200 (NOT 0x14067b1a0 = the failed TEST2 fn, and NOT 0x14067b4e0). Next attempt must use 0x14067b200 (the menu's STEP_LoadSaveData initiator) to get io18+io20 + b80=2, then poll 0x140679180 -> b80=3 -> deserialize 0x14067b290.
(2) *** THE SYNTHETIC-OWNER DISPATCHER CALLS WROTE THE SAVE *** -- after the run, ER0000.sl2 md5 changed a73c579f7bea25f430a0760e9e827e33 (vanillachar) -> 9a8ab7639b86d1c42a7c39152148600d. The 600 dispatcher-2 0x140afb880 calls on the synthetic owner (or the resulting c30=default partial state) triggered a save WRITE despite the drive timing out and NEVER calling SetState(5). So calling the MoveMapStep dispatchers with a synthetic owner has uncontrolled side effects (save write) -- UNSAFE, abandon. Restored vanillachar after.

DECISIVE RE (2026-06-23, Ghidra subagent er-effects-rs-e9e). WHY the Continue/Load/NewGame rows never build after our zero-input open_menu (continue-scan=0 nodes, stage 3): TitleTopDialog::open_menu 0x1409b24e0 only POSTS a MenuJob chain (CS::MenuJob::ChainMenuJobs) into the LIVE MenuWindow's job slots (MenuWindow+0x10 queue ring, active slot +0xa28) via 0x1409aa580->0x14078e1d0->0x1407928c0. It does NOT build rows synchronously. The rows build only when the SAME pump frame's MenuWindow::Update 0x140745520 (called at the tail of TitleTopDialog::update 0x1409aac10, vtable slot 2, every frame) DRAINS that chain via ExecuteMenuJob(+0xa28)+0x1407a9460(+0x10), running each chained job's vtable[2] Run. Row-build leaf = 0x1409ac760 (dump 0x1409ac8b0): reads GetProfileSummary + saveslot, presence-check 0x140261c30, builds the Continue/load MenuJob via factory 0x140826600 (golden site 0x1409ac9cb) and ChainMenuJobs onto the TitleTopDialog (NOT owner+0x130, NOT a separate CSMenuMan stack -> our TitleTopDialog scan looks in the RIGHT place). Reached as a posted-chain Run callback: 0x1409a7940(std::function invoke)->0x1409a80d0->0x1409a71a0->0x1409a92a0->0x1409a9260->0x1409ac8b0. NATIVE trigger: title update pump 0x1409aad60 calls open_menu ITSELF when accept byte DAT_144589bdc (via 0x140e85f60) set && dialog+0xa40==0 -> post+drain share the native frame. OUR BUG: we fired open_menu OUT-OF-CONTEXT from the recurring game task -> chain posted but NEVER drained. FIX (committed next): fire maybe_auto_open_menu IN-CONTEXT from the title_update_detour (hook on 0x1409aac10, TITLE_UPDATE_HOOK), installed in the pab_advance path (replaced the game-task maybe_auto_open_menu call). ORDERING CAVEAT: chain stepper 0x1409a92a0 gates on titleFlowContext+0x14c==0; if 14c!=0 (a load dispatching, e.g. our fire_tfc tfc+0x14c=1) it returns Failed -> ABORTS the row build. So tfc+0x14c=1 must be set only AFTER rows build; native_continue path does NOT set it (fires the built node via 0x1409aaba0), so safe. Save-update 'corrupted' popup was a READ-ONLY staged save (now writable). Relates rowbuild + offline-notice-fix-works-revealed-save-update-gate-2026-06-23, continue-blocker-EMPTY-menu-list-stage3.

FACTORY 0x14081ead0 ABI (disasm-decoded prologue + body): rcx=capture+8, rdx=incoming_ctx. Prologue: rsi=rdx(incoming_ctx), r14=rcx(capture+8), rbx=0. @0x14081eaf9 call 0x1409aa680(rcx=capture+8) (TitleTopDialog slot-refresh accessor, return ignored, side-effect). @0x14081eafe r8 = [0x143d87350] = THE OP-NEW ALLOCATOR GLOBAL (so the factory self-loads the allocator -- calling the factory avoids needing to supply it). @0x14081eb10 op-new: 0x141eb9ed0(rcx=0x1cd0 size, edx=8 align[lea 0x8(rbx)], r8=[0x143d87350] allocator) -> buf in rax. @0x14081eb2a call 0x140745280(rcx=rsi=incoming_ctx, rdx=&stack_out) builds the ctor ctx (copies the label SSO string from [incoming_ctx+8]; null/zeroed -> empty label, null-guarded -- cosmetic only). @0x14081eb41 mov r8,(r14) = r8 = *(capture+8) = the OWNER-MENU-OBJECT. @0x14081eb4a call ctor 0x1409a3d90(rcx=buf, rdx=built_ctx, r8=owner-obj). Returns dialog* in rax.

ZERO-INPUT CALL SHAPE (from own_stepper, owner=SimpleTitleStep owner param): (1) cap = a writable qword buffer holding the owner-menu-object pointer; pass rcx = &cap (factory does r14=rcx, r8=[r14]=*cap=owner-obj; and 0x1409aa680(rcx=&cap) -- VERIFY 0x1409aa680 tolerates rcx=&cap, it derefs [rcx]=owner-obj). (2) ctx_in = a zeroed ~0x40-byte buffer; pass rdx=&ctx_in ([ctx_in+8]=0 -> empty label). (3) call 0x14081ead0(rcx=&cap, rdx=&ctx_in) -> dialog in rax. (4) VALIDATE [dialog]==base+0x2b229f8. (5) write cursor [dialog+0xb0c]=slot (< [dialog+0xb08]), call load_activate [dialog->vt+0xa0]=0x1409a4670(rcx=dialog) -> selector job -> pump frames -> menu_deser 0x14082c240 mount (ac0=slot, c30=real map, char; io18/io20, b80-independent) -> guarded continue_confirm 0x140b0e180 -> SetState(5). own_stepper_stage2 already implements steps 4-5 from OWN_STEPPER_DIALOG.

THE ONE RUNTIME-DERIVED VALUE = owner-obj (ctor r8 = *(capture+8)). Gold capture: *(capture+8) = owner+0x138 (the SimpleTitleStep owner + 0x138, the BeginLogo menu container); the ctor reads the profile ROW-VECTOR at [r8+0xa58]=begin / [r8+0xa60]=count. CANDIDATES to validate read-only BEFORE the native call: owner+0x138 (address) vs *(owner+0x138). A read-only r8-candidate probe is now in scan_dialog_for_loadgame (logs [cand+0], rowvec_begin [+0xa58], rowcount [+0xa60] for both candidates) -- run it to pin which candidate has a plausible vtable + small nonzero row count. PRECONDITION: must run on a live open menu (owner valid + rows populated); ctor calls boot singletons (0x140256360->[+0x1200], 0x140256780) satisfied at open menu; stack-canary 0x143c5adb0 + allocator boot-built.

NEXT (implementation): once the r8 probe pins owner-obj, add an own_stepper STAGE (in the open-menu park, BEFORE S2_ACTIVATE) that builds cap+ctx_in, calls factory 0x14081ead0, validates [dialog]==0x2b229f8, stores OWN_STEPPER_DIALOG, sets phase S2_ACTIVATE. SAVE-WRITE risk only at continue_confirm (gated by verified mount). Static RE (subagent, 2026-06-15, objdump) of the native title-accept lever. Inner TitleStep MenuJobWait (state 10, handler 0xb0d400) pumps the press-any-button job (owner+0xe0 via 0x733f20, owner+0x130 via 0x7a9600) but its ONLY state-advance exit is gated solely on global byte [0x143d856a0]. At 0xb0d526: cmp byte [0x143d856a0],0 then je skip then mov edx,0xb (state 11) then call 0x140b0d960 (SetState writes [owner+0x4c]=edx). So MenuJobWait advances 10 to 11 (Finish) IFF [0x143d856a0]!=0. Sole writer 0x140c8ff41 in 0x140c8fe90 (intro/logo-finish routine, sound IDs 0x80e8/0x21, fade loop) latches it when the title/intro sequence is satisfied. About 20 cmpb readers across title/boot states treat it as a one-shot title-accepted/proceed-toward-ingame latch (pump 0xb0a78b takes the direct-PlayGame branch when it is 1). CORRECTS run 301 -- this byte is NOT downstream of the load, it is the title-accept/proceed latch, and setting it is a candidate zero-input lever (pending runtime verification). Ranked zero-input levers, lever1 = write *(u8*)0x143d856a0=1 once inner owner confirmed at state 10 (smallest/most native, caution global read by ~20 sites so set only at state 10), lever2 = call 0x140b0d960(owner,11) (direct, needs only owner ptr). Input path for reference -- press feeds inputmgr [0x143d6b7b0] event array +0xdc (writer 0x766340) and keystate bitmap +0x90, job-done predicate 0x7a9200 = job+0x0 GTE 2. Related: [[selectbot-pump-dormant-pre-title-accept]] [[autoload-stall-state0-precondition]].

LOADING-SCREEN PORTRAIT DEPTH-CULL TRANSPARENT BACKGROUND -- WORKS + UNGATED (2026-07-01, runtime-proven, run target/runtime-probe/postcontinue-lookat-smoke, screenshot confirms head over the see-through loading screen). Branch spike/boot-parallelization.

MECHANISM (src/experiments/gpu_readback.rs + startup_hooks.rs):
1. find_depth_resource(off) BFS-finds the offscreen scene's DEPTH sibling (fmt=19 R32G8X24_TYPELESS, SAME size as color RT, adjacent in the GX wrapper nest). find_d3d12_resource_ex gained a want_depth bool -> try_depth_texture2d acceptor.
2. readback_depth_resource_cached: cached (RB_DEPTH_*) plane-0 (subresource 0) copy, reinterpret 4 bytes/texel as f32. GetCopyableFootprints(&desc,0,1,..) gives the plane-0 footprint directly. COMMON->COPY_SOURCE works under vkd3d (same as color).
3. apply_depth_alpha_key: the portrait depth is BIMODAL -- background sits at depth 0 (far/cleared, IBL env writes no nearer depth), foreground (head+SHOULDERS) clusters near max, separated by a large EMPTY GAP. CORNERS ARE NOT ALL BACKGROUND: a centered portrait's shoulders fill the BOTTOM corners (measured 1024 run: tl=0 tr=0 bl=0.0183 br=0.0193, center=0.0194, gap bins 1..+114/128, thr=0.0089). So the mask = histogram largest-empty-gap threshold, KEEP the cluster the centered-head patch belongs to, CUT the other. Robust to Z direction (never assume near/far==0). masked ~40%.
4. CRITICAL -- the depth buffer only carries content on GENUINE re-render frames (~5/145); the COLOR RT persists the last render every frame. So the mask is RECALCULATED when fresh depth is available and CACHED (LAST_DEPTH_MASK) + re-applied on the dead frames -> cutout on 145/145 frames (oracle_depth_key_applied=145, oracle_depth_key_fresh=5). NOT a frozen one-shot; recalc+cache.
5. COMPOSITE: replaced the opaque CopyTextureRegion with a CPU alpha-blend (blend_portrait_over_backbuffer): read the live backbuffer region -> alpha-blend the portrait OVER it (bg alpha 0 = loading screen shows through) -> write back. Two submits on our own queue, reuses OVERLAY_BB_* cached buffers. NO PSO/shader needed. Runtime-proven no crash (145 blends/run).

RAM ORACLES: oracle_depth_key_applied / oracle_depth_key_fresh / oracle_depth_key_bg_pct.

STEP-3 FIX REDUCTION (2026-07-17, H1-gate RE). The stall reduces to: WorldBlockRes phase-2 handler 0x1406157f0 binds+advances (+0x35=3) ONLY when primary FD4FileCap[+0x40]->[+0x90] != null; on load2 +0x90==0 (and WorldBlockInfo(blockres+0x8)[+0x28]==0) so it falls through with no write -> parks +0x35=2 -> WORLD RES WAIT. THE FIX = make FD4FileCap[+0x90] legitimately non-null (real mount+read); faking +0x35=3 (empty world) or +0x35=5 (re-runs find-or-insert, refcount-bumps same stale cap, no re-read) are both proven-wrong. IMPORTANT NEW: the map-mount MenuFunctorJob (vtable 0x142acb638, Do 0x140836f30, builder 0x14082faf0) has NO static registration (dynamically-built std::function, pumped by 0x140791370) -> the H1 dispatch decision CANNOT be flipped by patching a call-site/static flag. The refuted detector 0x14082d5b0 is the TITLE save-slot Load-Game confirm-dialog path (caller 0x1408320e0 builds GR_System_Message 401106), correctly off our path. TWO FIX OPTIONS keyed on whether the archive BDT is still mounted: Fix A (CSFile cap re-enqueue: cap[+0x88]=0 then call 0x14269d7b0(queue,cap), queue=*(*(*(0x143d5b0f8)+8)+0xe0+((cap[+0x89]>>2)&7)*8)) -- ALREADY TRIED (DLL 9c835905) and FAILED (+0x90 stayed null) because the archive is UNMOUNTED. Fix B (re-mount): call MountEblArchive 0x1401efc00(rcx=CSEblFileManager, rdx,r8,r9) with the m28 descriptor args -- CENSUS confirmed registry R=deref(0x1448464a8) null on load2 => Fix B is the path. MountEblArchive 2 callers 0x1401eef17 (4-sub-archive loop) / 0x1401efaef; rdx/r8 static-ish ~0x1429cf6xx (may be replayable). NEXT (autonomous, load1 only, NO user drive): sw-bp 0x1efc00 with RAISED log cap + deep stack -> capture the m28 mount args (the 0x82dc1c-chain MountEblArchive on load1 at ~+85s) and whether rdx/r8 are STATIC (replayable on load2). Then Fix B DLL: at load2 stall (R null), CALL MountEblArchive with the m28 descriptor + re-enqueue the cap so 0x1406157f0 advances. Links step3-H1-CONFIRMED-mapmount-step-not-dispatched-on-warm-reload-2026-07-17 step3-census-registry-null-on-load2-mount-skip-confirmed-2026-07-17.

ROOT CAUSE + FIX (2026-07-15, native Windows, ER 1.16.2): The System->Quit profile-switch was fully broken (couldn't load a profile, ghosting/menu-not-hidden, and the earlier msgbox crash) because the MenuWindowJob::Run hook that does ALL the System->Quit work NEVER INSTALLED. Two detours target the SAME rva MENU_WINDOW_JOB_RUN_RVA=0x7ad1c0: title_custom_cover_menu_window_run_hook (startup_modals_menu_cover.rs:1050, title-cover face transform) and system_quit_menu_window_job_run_hook (profile_rows_system_quit_menu.rs:1402, the resource-map+HIDE + in-world-load-ABORT + save-picker-pump + return-title submit). MinHook allows one detour per address; title_custom_cover wins the race, so system_quit's install fails MH_ERROR_ALREADY_CREATED and its handler never fires (proven: 0 'MenuWindowJob::Run resource=' lines, 0 'real-system-window hide' lines across the whole appended log). Net: PROFILE_SELECT_WINDOW/INGAME_TOP/OPTION_SETTING never set (this is ALSO why the msgbox crash's switch_active was false), the System menu is never hidden (ghosting), and the return-title/load-abort that COMPLETE a switch never run (couldn't load a profile). FIX: extracted system_quit_menu_window_job_run_hook's post-original body into pub fn system_quit_menu_window_run_post(job,ret) and call it from the WINNING title_custom_cover handler after it runs the original (both share the same job,ret contract). VERIFIED: MenuWindowJob::Run now fires (IngameTop 53, OptionSetting 11, ProfileSelect 526), hide fires, and the switch now runs the full return-title chain (ARMED save-safe switch -> return-title SUBMIT -> product-core-autoload state=6/6). REMAINING: the actual character reload now CRASHES late (a native AV during the '2nd in-process load' -- old render shows, new render shows opaque/no-transparent-bg, screen goes black, then a delayed crash). This is the known-hard 2nd-in-process-load garbage-vtable AV (see the load-abort comments at profile_rows ~1545, 'the native GFx text setter dispatches the uninitialized object'). NOT skipped/msgbox: the switch genuinely executes now, it just crashes in the reload. NOTE ALSO: ToS-skip + CURRENT_MENU_WINDOW_JOB_RUN_JOB (Seamless-only) still live only in the dead hook -- fold them into system_quit_menu_window_run_post or the winning handler if Seamless co-op regresses. See [[native-profile-load-msgbox-crash-1162]].
For Elden Ring packed-data extraction, prefer Nuxe over UXM-Selective-Unpack when Nuxe works; do not recommend/use UXM as the happy path just because selective unpack is convenient.

FPS KILLER ROOT-CAUSED (2026-07-20, confirms user: code, not system): the injected DLL tanks in-world FPS ~3x (60->20-40) via a PER-FRAME synchronous GPU READBACK-WITH-WAIT in the present hook. Chain: present_overlay.rs present_hook (every game Present) -> composite_on_game_swapchain (present_overlay.rs:249) -> composite_boot_progress_on_swapchain -> composite_boot_progress_inner (gpu_readback/boot_progress.rs:1501). That fn copies the backbuffer to a readback buffer and WAITS on it (overlay_composite.rs:2097 readback_wait_start ... Map) to alpha-blend the loading bar/stats -- a hard GPU->CPU pipeline stall every frame. It is meant to STOP at the loading->world handoff (boot_progress.rs:1561 sets BOOT_VIEW_STOPPED on native_lit||world_handoff||hold_bail), BUT that stop block is gated by (loading_handoff || world_handoff) at line 1520, and for LOAD2 world_handoff is forced false by `!own_menu_active` (line 1518, because IN_WORLD_REACHED is a stale latch during an own-menu switch). So load2 stops only via loading_handoff = a FRESH loadscreen table build (loadscreen_builds > table_baseline); if that does not fire, the block never runs, BOOT_VIEW_STOPPED never sets, and the readback composites EVERY FRAME in-world -> 20-40 FPS + the variable stutter. BOOT_VIEW_OWN_MENU_LOAD_ACTIVE set at boot_progress.rs:337, cleared only at 1570 (inside the stop path). FIX: give load2 a reliable PER-EPOCH fresh in-world stop (not the stale IN_WORLD_REACHED latch): stop the boot-view compositing once the current fresh_deser epoch is genuinely in-world (player present + world-clock live/playtime advancing, or a fresh per-epoch world latch), so the readback stops promptly after the switch settles. Must not cut the cover during legitimate loading (keep native_lit/hold_bail). This is NOT contention -- the DLL readback runs regardless of cores. VALIDATION is contention-coin-flip right now; fix + verify with ER isolated/quiet.

BLANK GAME OPTIONS PANE bug (user-reported 2026-07-06) -- ROOT CAUSE + FIX + AUTONOMOUS HARNESS.

SYMPTOM: In the in-world System menu (CS::OptionSettingTopDialog, menu_id 0x25), after navigating to the last/Quit tab (index 8-9, where our system-quit-dup injects Load-Profile/Save-Game rows) and RETURNING to the Game Options tab (0), tab 0's row pane draws BLANK -- tab strip + footer caption render, row list is black. User: '1000% confidence the menu disappears ONLY after I navigate to the tab that contains our custom menu options'.

ROOT CAUSE (RE-confirmed, dump VAs): pane visibility is applied ONLY by the game's per-tab pass FUN_14093b850(composite=window+0x1768, tabIndex): it iterates the 10 cached pane dialogs at composite+0x68 and SetVisible(dialog+0x1200, current==dialog). On RETURN to a previously-shown tab, that pane's dialog+0x1200 proxy stays DisplayInfo.Visible=0 and is never re-shown (same dialog ptr 0x..564080 goes Visible 1->0 and never back). NOT the named WindowList children (CameraSetting/PadSetting/etc.) -- those are ALWAYS Visible=0 (the first semaphore measured them and false-fired at boot; corrected to read the current tab dialog+0x1200).

SEMAPHORE (validated): oracle_optionsetting_current_pane_visible reads dialog+0x1200's DisplayInfo.Visible (GetDisplayInfo vcall = objectInterface->vtbl[+0xd8], Visible byte at DisplayInfo+0xd6; CSScaleformValue: objectInterface+0x18, dataType+0x20 gate (&0x8f)==10, handle+0x28). oracle_optionsetting_real_blank_detected_count latches healthy(ever_visible)->blank so it cannot false-fire on boot. Gate 'actively_shown' = CSMenuMan flag bit 0x4. current tab = *(*(window+0x1870+0x10)+0xd4).

FIX (product, always-on, no gate): in the sample path, when actively_shown && current pane is a DisplayObject but Visible=0, re-assert it with the game's OWN SetVisible (deobf 0x733340) on current_dialog+0x1200. oracle_optionsetting_pane_fix_applied counts corrections. Diagnostic A/B disable: er-effects-optionsetting-fix-off.txt.

AUTONOMOUS HARNESS (reproduces WITHOUT user): extended the system-quit repro autopilot (system_quit_repro_guards.rs) with SQ_REPRO_STATE_TAB_RETURN, gated by er-effects-tab-return-repro.txt: in-world -> START -> UP+A (OptionSetting) -> RB to last tab -> LB back to tab 0 -> dwell, driven by OPTIONSETTING_CURRENT_TAB feedback. Needs save_file set in the GAME-DIR er-effects.toml (S:\...\Game, NOT target/release) so the autoload gets in-world without the picker; point it at a WRITABLE throwaway ER0000.sl2 (save-safe). Watcher scripts/er-user-session-watch.py <dir> <cap_s> <blank_threshold> auto-tears-down on oracle_optionsetting_real_blank_detected_count.

A/B RESULT (run B fix OFF): real_blank=10430, cur_vis=0 -> blank REPRODUCED autonomously. (Run A fix ON pending at time of writing.)

Separate earlier issue: cupcake cache misses were branch-scope only and self-healed once a main-branch run saved cupcake-Linux-27b94b8. Remember: GH Actions caches saved from PR scopes are invisible to other PRs; only main-scope saves are shared.

DEFINITIVE static RE (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY). Answers WHO creates a world-res block load-state, the streaming-enable gate, and the zero-input lever for slot 9's m10 block. Supersedes the "unknown driver" framing in worldres-block-loadstate-null-streaming-driver-floor-2026.

=== THE LOAD-STATE OBJECT + GETTER (block-entry vtable) ===
Block-entry array [resmgr+0xb3030] (stride 8, ptrs), count [resmgr+0xb3140]; entries are inline storage [resmgr+0xb7d0] stride 0xce8 (count [resmgr+0xb760]). Block-entry VTABLE = 0x142a79728, ctor 0x14062f390 (sets [be+0xce0]=loadstate-array base, [be+0xcd8]=loadstate count, [be+0xce0]... key fields). Readiness 0x14066d3e0: r9=be=[resmgr+0xb3030+i*8]; match [[be+8]+0xc]==area byte; rax=[be]; call qword[rax+0x10](be,&coord) = getter. Getter vtable+0x10 = 0x14062f4c0 -> thunk -> 0x14062f470: rax=[be+0xcd8](count); r11=[be+0xce0](loadstate array base, stride 0xb98); linear-search by key at +0x34; returns [be+0xce0]+idx*0xb98 or NULL if count==0/no match. *** GETTER IS PURE LOOKUP, NO LAZY ALLOC. *** loadstate fields: +0x2c request, +0x2d, +0x34 key, +0x35 phase(0..0xa). NULL getter == loadstate count [be+0xcd8]==0 == none were created.

=== WHO CREATES THE LOAD-STATE (the initiator) ===
Loadstate ctor (CREATOR) = 0x1406610e0 (vtable 0x142a7d4b0; phase +0x35 init 0, key +0x34). Called ONLY from block-region BUILDER 0x14066bb10 @0x14066bceb (per-block in a span inner-loop). Builder also creates block-entries via 0x14062f390 @0x14066bc47. Builder is driven by a REQUEST: rdx=request, list [rdx+8] count [rdx+0x10]; it iterates request entries pairwise (area byte [entry+0x13]) and for spans creates the per-block loadstates. INITIATE chain: 0x14066af40 (process request -> map/region lists -> builder) <- 0x14066adf0 (populate, fills 0xb3030 ptr array) ... and CRUCIALLY 0x14066af40 has only 2 callers: 0x14066b61b (resmgr-internal) and 0x140aed911 (the in-game submitter). The submitter fn 0x140aed820 builds the request from the owner's target map coord [owner+0x100..0x104] (valid iff area<0x59 per gate 0x140720210), RB-tree-inserts it (0x14066a400), then calls initiate 0x14066af40([owner+0x250]=resmgr, &req). 0x140aed820's SOLE caller is 0x140aec120 = the MoveMapStep SPAWNER (the InGameStep MoveMap_Init step): it (1) calls submitter 0x140aed820 FIRST (creating loadstates), then (2) allocs 0x4c0 + calls MoveMapStep ctor 0x140af28d0 (rcx=mms, rdx=resmgr=owner+0x250 -> [mms+0xb8], r8=[owner+0xb3628] -> [mms+0xe8]). So loadstates are created EAGERLY by the MoveMap_Init/spawner step BEFORE the MoveMapStep's own step machine runs WorldResWait. 0x140aec120 has 0 rel32 callers (registered as a runtime step callback, table built by 0x1400a40c0).

=== WHO DRIVES [loadstate+0x35] 0 -> 0xa (phase progression) ===
Phase state machine = 0x140615020 (jump table @0x140615094, phases 0..0xc; resident set at 0x1406150ed: [ls+0x35]=0xa, [ls+0x3c]=2 after reading file handle [ls+0x40]+0x90 = BHD/BDT IO completion). Per-frame path: resmgr update 0x14066da60 loops [resmgr+0xb768] stride 0xce8 calling be-vtable 0x142a79728 slot+0x18 = 0x14062f4d0 -> walks [be+0xce0] loadstates stride 0xb98 -> per-loadstate tick 0x1406147f4 -> loadstate-vtable+0x78 -> jmp 0x140615020. So once loadstates EXIST, the per-frame resmgr update 0x14066da60 (which DOES run at our forced title, called from STEP_WorldResWait via 0x140624bd0) ticks them through the phases via async file IO.

=== THE STREAMING-ENABLE GATE: [resmgr+0xb7c1] ===
resmgr update 0x14066da60 tail calls 0x1406709e0(resmgr) = state machine on [resmgr+0xb7c4](0..3) GATED by bool [resmgr+0xb7c1]. With b7c1==0 it perpetually takes the teardown branch (writes [0x143d7c088+0x488]=0, never advances). With b7c1==1: state0 registers resmgr ([0x143d7c088+0x488]=resmgr) + calls 0x140cdc750 (creates 3 streaming IO jobs/handles on resmgr+0x490/+0x4a8/+0x4b0 via singleton 0x143d5b0f8) + sets b7c4=1; state1 polls jobs 0x140cdc6b0 (byte[handle+0x88]==4 ready) -> b7c4=2; etc. resmgr ctor 0x14066cab0 zero-inits b7c0/b7c1 (0x14066cb57) and b7c4 (0x14066cb5e) => DISABLED by default. b7c1 is set to 1 ONLY by VIRTUAL enable method 0x14066e2e4 @0x14066e397 (also lazily builds session singletons 0x143d687a0 size0x258 ctor 0x1405e80a0, 0x143d67bd0 size0x128 ctor 0x140585af0); disable 0x14066e3aa @0x14066e3d0. Both virtual (0 rel32 callers) -> dispatched via resmgr-owner vtable by the map/session ACTIVATION path. Plausibly NEVER invoked at forced-from-title (no real session activation), so b7c1 stays 0 and the resmgr's own streaming job machine never starts. Singleton 0x143d7c088 (getter/ctor 0x140cd6c50) = global streaming/session driver the resmgr registers into.

=== ROOT CAUSE (two independent gaps, BOTH must be closed) ===
At our forced-from-title slot-9 load: the MoveMapStep exists + is scheduler-ticked to step 3 (WorldResWait), and the per-frame resmgr update 0x14066da60 runs. BUT (gap 1) the m10 loadstates were NEVER CREATED -- the request submitted by the spawner 0x140aed820 did NOT contain m10 (built from [owner+0x100..0x104] which at our SetState(5)/synthetic-spawn context was the placeholder/wrong map, or the spawner ran before slot-9's real coord was in owner+0x100), so builder 0x14066bb10 made block-entries but [be+0xcd8] loadstate-count==0 for m10. AND (gap 2) even if loadstates existed, [resmgr+0xb7c1]==0 (streaming-enable gate never set by the virtual activator 0x14066e2e4), so the resmgr's job machine 0x1406709e0 idles and async IO never starts. This is the architectural FLOOR: world-resource streaming is owned by the in-game map/session ACTIVATION flow (same class as frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned-not-callable).

=== ZERO-INPUT LEVERS (ranked, VAs/args/order + crash risk) ===
(LEVER A, structural-correct, addresses gap 1 + relies on existing per-frame tick) Ensure owner+0x100..0x104 hold slot-9 real coord 0x0a010000 BEFORE the spawner 0x140aec120 runs, so its 0x140aed820 submits an m10 request and 0x14066bb10 creates the m10 loadstates. We already land slot 9's real map in GameMan+0xc30; the missing wiring is propagating it into the MoveMap owner+0x100..0x104 at MoveMap_Init time (not just owner+0xbc for SetState). VERIFY: after spawn, [be(m10)+0xcd8]>0 and getter 0x14062f470 returns non-null. Crash risk LOW (uses native submit path).
(LEVER B, addresses gap 2) Set [resmgr+0xb7c1]=1 directly (resmgr=[[MoveMapStep+0xf0]+0x10] or [MoveMapStep+0xb8]) OR better call the virtual enable 0x14066e2e4(resmgr) to also build session singletons. This starts the job machine 0x1406709e0. Crash risk MEDIUM-HIGH: 0x14066e2e4 builds singletons that may assert if dependencies (0x143d5b0f8 IO mgr) absent; raw b7c1=1 poke may leave half-built state. MUST be combined with Lever A (no loadstates => nothing to stream).
(LEVER C, direct create) Call submitter 0x140aed820(owner) again AFTER writing owner+0x100..0x104 = 0x0a010000, then ensure per-frame 0x14066da60 ticks. This re-runs initiate 0x14066af40 -> builder 0x14066bb10 -> loadstate ctor 0x1406610e0 for m10. Crash risk MEDIUM (request RB-tree 0x14066a400 + builder allocate via [0x143d872e0] heap; needs valid resmgr+0x250 sub-objects already built, which they are since MoveMapStep exists).
VERIFICATION CHAIN: m10 loadstate non-null -> per-frame 0x14066da60/0x14062f4d0/0x1406147f4/0x140615020 drive [ls+0x35]->0xa (needs b7c1 streaming jobs OR the loadstate's own file IO [ls+0x40]) -> readiness 0x14066d3e0 true -> MoveMapStep+0x48 -> -1 (gate 0x140eb5530) -> InGameStep+0xd8 drains 1->2->0 -> world renders.

=== DEFINITIVE FLOOR STATEMENT ===
World-resource streaming requires the in-game map/session ACTIVATION flow to (1) submit the real-map block-load request via the MoveMap_Init spawner 0x140aec120/0x140aed820 with the correct coord in owner+0x100, AND (2) invoke the VIRTUAL streaming-enable 0x14066e2e4 (sets [resmgr+0xb7c1]=1) to start the resmgr job machine + session singletons. Both are owned by the activation flow, not the per-frame WorldResWait tick. The zero-input lever must replicate BOTH (correct coord propagation + enable), not just one. Related: worldres-block-loadstate-null-streaming-driver-floor-2026, worldreswait-33-blocks-streaming-gap-2026, full-pipeline-traced-to-worldreswait-map-block-streaming, async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026, csfeman-built-via-setstate5-breakthrough.

AUTORESEARCH FINDING 2026-06-25: title_native_ready predicate object is a CS::SceneObjProxy / Scaleform value readiness check, not a bespoke title state flag. Runtime vtable 0x142a94a70 resolves via Ghidra VtableOwner to RTTI .?AVSceneObjProxy@CS@@; dump vtable slot [0]=0x14074bbe0 maps to the runtime getter seen as 0x14074baf0 (regional dump/deobf shift). The hook implements original semantics: state=this->vtable[0](this); return (state->flags_20 & 0x8f)!=0. Decompile around the vtable family shows SceneObjProxy helpers reading CSScaleformValue.dataType at +0x20 and masking 0x8f; FUN_14074a0f0 similarly only writes text if (*(value+0x20)&0x8f)>1. Therefore title_native_ready_last_flags=0 means the underlying Scaleform/SceneObjProxy value for the title row/component is not materialized/valid, not that a high-level TitleTopDialog field is false. Caller 0x140745bf9 inside FUN_140745b30 is row presentation/text attachment path that obtains text via FUN_140752f60 and calls FUN_14074a0f0(param_1+0x358,...). Next target: find why the SceneObjProxy/Scaleform component backing native title rows is invalid under zero-input / current render-suppression timing, or whether Part A suppresses/clears the visual object before semantic rows become valid. Do not patch the dataType flags; they are a proxy validity oracle.

STATIC RE DECODE (2026-06-16, objdump on-disk decrypted eldenring.exe, READ-ONLY) of the Load-Game menu-item action-functor invoke + the item-tree walk. Answers the 3 decode targets for bd er-effects-rs-av4.

=== CLASS HIERARCHY (RTTI-confirmed via vtables) ===
The menu is an FD4 MenuJobSequence TREE of CS:: jobs. Polymorphic update slot = VTABLE+0x10 ('Update(StepResult* out, ctx*, FrameTimeStruct*) -> StepResult{[0]=status,[4]=payload}'):
 - CS::MenuWindowJob (vtable 0x142aa97e8; +0x10 = 0x1407ad1c0) = LEAF; its Update fires the action functor. Subclass CS::GaitemRepositoryMenuWindowJob (vtable 0x142aad548) overrides same slot to 0x1407ad1c0 too.
 - CS::MenuJobSequence::IfElseJob (vtable 0x142aa2958; +0x10 = child-iterator 0x1407aa1f0).
 - CS::FixOrderJobSequence (vtable 0x142aa8d78; +0x10 = 0x1407aa1f0) = ordered container.
StepResult ctor 0x1407a91e0 (mov [rcx]=edx,[rcx+4]=r8d). Status decoders: 0x1407a9200 = seta([res]>1), 0x1407a9210 = sete([res]==2). status 0=continue,1/2=advance/stop.

=== TARGET 1: function 0x1407ad1c0 (MenuWindowJob::Update, contains functor invoke @0x1407ad2b9) ===
Entry 0x1407ad1c0: rcx=item(->rdi), rdx=arg2(->r12), r8=FrameTimeStruct(->r14, [r8+8]=dt). Branches on item fields: [item+0x130](pending child, jne 0x1407ad540), [item+0xa8](action functor: !=0 AND [item+0x10]==0 -> BUILD path), else [item+0xe8](alt functor path 0x1407ad367). BUILD path (0x1407ad213+): copies 16-byte descriptor [item+0x58] to local; ensures window mgr singleton 0x143d83148 (FD4-assert-init 0x141eb97a0 if null); calls 0x140d6a8e0(mgr, &desc) -> stores result at [item+0x68] (the window/scene obj); wires [item+0x10] via 0x140d717f0 + 0x14073df50; THEN the action-functor invoke. So [item+0x10] is the dialog/scene CTX that the functor writes into (it is 0 before, populated by the functor's return stored at [item+0x130]).
MenuWindowJob item LAYOUT (offsets in use): +0x0 vtable; +0x10 dialog/scene CTX (ptr, lifecycle-managed, FD4 string/handle); +0x58 16-byte DESCRIPTOR (item identity/key -- this is what distinguishes Load-Game vs Continue, consumed by 0x140d6a8e0->0x140d78630 to resolve the window); +0x68 resolved window/scene obj; +0xa8 ACTION std::function (the load/dialog-build callback); +0xe8 alternate std::function (secondary action path); +0x130 last-returned child/dialog ptr (managed via 0x141eba1c0/0x141eba200 FD4 refcount).

=== TARGET 2: child-iterator 0x1407aa1f0 (Sequence::Update) ===
rcx=this(seq, ->rdi), rdx=out(->rsi), r8=FrameTime(->r15, [r15+8]=dt). Resets via 0x1407a91e0(out,2). COLLECTION: base = [this+0x18] (array of 8-byte child ptrs, with -(this+0x18)&7 align fudge r14), COUNT = [this+0x60], INDEX = [this+0x10] (s64). Loop while [this+0x10] < [this+0x60]: child = *(align(this+0x18) + index*8) i.e. effectively child=[this+0x18 + index*8]; refcount-acquire (0x141eba1c0); build FrameTime predicate-struct on stack ([+0]=tag 0x1429c8e58,[+8]=dt); DISPATCH child->vtable[+0x10](out2, &predstruct) ; read child status via 0x1407a9200/0x1407a9210; if continue -> inc [this+0x10] + release child (0x141eba200, vtable[0] dtor if last) + loop; else break. So it ticks each child's Update slot in order, advancing only while the child returns 'continue'(>1). To reach the Load-Game MenuWindowItem from owner: SimpleTitleStep owner+0xe0 = menu-job multicast holder; the menu task pump 0x140791620 ticks the root job: rsi=jobholder, [rsi+0x58]=pre-step functor (vtable[0]), [rsi+0x18]=ROOT child job -> calls [root_vtable+0x10] (=0x1407aa1f0 if root is a Sequence). Walk: root Sequence.[this+0x18][0..count] -> nested Sequences/IfElseJob -> MenuWindowJob leaf whose [item+0x58] descriptor == Load-Game key; invoke that leaf's [item+0xa8].

=== TARGET 3: functor-invoke ABI @0x1407ad2b9 ===
0x1407ad2a0: rcx=[item+0xa8] (std::function obj); null-check (else FD4 throw 0x1424d27f0+int3). 0x1407ad2b2: rax=[rcx] (the std::_Func_impl vtable, NO RTTI). 0x1407ad2b5: rdx=item+0x10 (the single arg = dialog ctx out-slot). 0x1407ad2b9: call [rax+0x10] = std::function _Do_call (MSVC _Func_impl_no_alloc layout: [0]_Copy [8]_Move [0x10]_Do_call [0x18]_Get [0x20]_Delete). Return rax stored to [item+0x130] (the built dialog). The _Do_call thunk does 'add rcx,0x8' (skip impl header to captured lambda state) then tail-jmps the lambda body. For the Load-Game item the lambda body tail-jmps DIALOG FACTORY 0x14081ead0 (thunk 0x140820c64 = 'add rcx,8; jmp 0x14081ead0'; the ONLY caller of 0x14081ead0). Factory 0x14081ead0(rcx=lambda-capture, rdx=ctx): op-new 0x1cd0 (0x141eb9ed0) -> ProfileLoadDialog ctor 0x1409a3d90 (writes vtable @0x1409a3e7e), reads capture [r14] for ctor param; returns dialog_this. So invoking [item+0xa8](rdx=item+0x10) BUILDS the ProfileLoadDialog -- matches cap_dialog_factory capture (factory rcx=0x7ffe9e52d1f8, rdx=rcx-0x68, ret dialog_this).

=== ZERO-INPUT REPLAY SHAPE ===
Given live SimpleTitleStep owner (vtable 0x142b63bb0, +0x48==10): owner+0xe0 job holder -> root job [holder+0x18] -> recurse Sequence children ([seq+0x18], count [seq+0x60]) to find MenuWindowJob (vtable 0x142aa97e8/0x142aad548) whose [item+0x58] descriptor is Load-Game -> set up nothing else, just call [item+0xa8] vtable[+0x10] with rcx=[item+0xa8], rdx=item+0x10 (mirror 0x1407ad2b9). That builds the ProfileLoadDialog natively. The leaf's own Update (0x1407ad1c0) ALREADY does the [item+0x68] window-resolve + ctx wiring before the functor, so calling the functor directly may need [item+0x10]==0 precondition (Update only fires functor when [item+0x10]==0). Safest = let the native pump tick the located leaf rather than hand-invoke. UNREVERSED: exact [item+0x58] descriptor bytes that key Load-Game vs Continue (resolved at runtime by 0x140d6a8e0->0x140d78630); capture or compare the two main-menu items' +0x58 at runtime. Related: menu-load-full-construction-chain-2026, pathB-profileloaddialog-lazy-menu-nav-wall-2026, scheduler-fe-host-is-simpletitlestep-decoded-2026.
