DECISIVE RTTI CORRECTION (2026-06-17, via dearxan deobfuscated image + scripts/rtti-deobf.py MSVC RTTI resolver). OVERTURNS titletop-confirm-route-static-validated-no-input-needed-2026 and titletop-rowvector-vtable-corrections: that whole 'confirm route' was built on a MISIDENTIFIED function.

RTTI-RESOLVED FACTS (deobf mapped image, offset==RVA, base 0x140000000; vtable[-8]=COL, COL+0xC=RVA(TypeDescriptor), TD+0x10=mangled name):
- 0x14078e1c0 (the alleged 'title confirm router') = CommandSelectDialog::Update -- it is vtable slot +0x10 of FOUR IN-GAME dialog classes: CommandSelectDialog (vt 0x142aa1fa0), ChrMakeCommandSelectDialog (vt 0x142ab5e10), CSEventListDialog (vt 0x142ae5d88), DetailKeyGuideDialog (vt 0x142b03698). NONE is the title menu. So cursor [+0xb0c] / rows [+0x1290 stride 0x210] / resolver 0x14078fbd0 are the CommandSelectDialog-FAMILY layout, NOT the TitleTopDialog's. Using them on owner+0xe0 is invalid -- which is why fire_titletop_load_entry CORRECTLY fail-closed (owner+0xe0+0x1290 read = GFx markup, not rows).
- owner+0xe0 vtable 0x142b26468 = TitleTopDialog (RTTI .?AVTitleTopDialog@CS@@; COL at 0x142b26460=0x1433275b8). 0x142b26468 IS the real on-disk vtable base (NOT a runtime-skewed 0x142b25668 -- 0x142b25668 is inside a lambda _Func_impl, NOT a vtable; the '0xe00 PE-rebase skew' claim in old memos is FALSE). TitleTopDialog vtable slots: [+0x10]=update 0x1409aac10.
- 0x142afa070 (mislabeled 'router_this' in d180-hover-vs-confirm / zero-input-load-two-layer-route-synthesis) = GestureEquipDialog. Another wrong label.

CommandSelectDialog vtable 0x142aa1fa0 has 0 static refs (E8 + ptr) in the deobf image -> its vtable install + the menu-routing call sites are obfuscated/templated even AFTER Arxan decryption (control-flow obfuscation remains; only encryption-at-rest was removed). So the LIVE object graph (how a dialog instance is reached from owner) is NOT statically traceable for these FD4 menu objects. Class IDENTITY is resolvable (RTTI), object LOCATION is not.

IMPLICATION: the title Continue/Load/NewGame menu is the TitleTopDialog itself (GFx/Scaleform, update 0x1409aac10), NOT a CommandSelectDialog. The CORRECT headless path is the TitleTopDialog confirm mechanism (mainmenu-items-are-titletopdialog-widgets-not-fd4-jobs-2026 / d180-hover-vs-confirm-runtime-verdict-2026: rows are TitleTopDialog widgets [dialog+0x1f8] pumped by 0x140745570; confirm builds the d180 MenuWindowJob -> dialog_factory 0x14081ead0 -> ProfileLoadDialog). fire_titletop_load_entry's CommandSelectDialog-offset approach must be REPLACED. Re-derive the TitleTopDialog widget/cursor/confirm offsets via RTTI+disasm of 0x1409aac10 / 0x140745570 on the deobf image, OR a self-validating runtime hook on the TitleTopDialog update to read its real fields.

TOOL: scripts/rtti-deobf.py <slot_va> resolves any vtable slot in eldenring-deobf.bin to its MSVC class name + vtable base + slot index. Use it to VALIDATE every function/object identity before trusting an offset. Static RE 2026-06-28 custom Scaleform MemoryFile return ABI: deobf 0x140ce8240 repository wrapper allocates the File object by calling allocator singleton at 0x144593250 through vfunc +0x50 with rcx=*(0x144593250), edx=0x30, r8d=0, then calls MemoryFile ctor deobf 0x140ce7bb0 with rcx=allocated_0x30_obj, rdx=&ScaleformStringRef, r8=payload_bytes_ptr, r9d=payload_len. The ctor writes MemoryFile vtable 0x142ba4c80, refcount=1, initializes the embedded filename/string object at +0x10 via 0x1411410f0, stores bytes at +0x18, size at +0x20, cursor=0 at +0x24, and valid byte at +0x28 if the passed string ref is non-empty and payload pointer/len are valid. Important hazard: ctor rdx is NOT a raw C string; it is a Scaleform string/ref object. The native wrapper builds that object on stack with deobf 0x141141390(rcx=&stack_string_ref, rdx=wide_or_char_name_ptr). Therefore a future synthetic Open hook should either build a proper string ref with 0x141141390 before calling ctor, or manually construct the object only if it also safely initializes +0x10. Passing the `url: *const i8` directly as rdx to 0x140ce7bb0 would be invalid and likely crash. Allocation/deallocation pair is allocator singleton 0x144593250 vfunc +0x50/+0x60; object size is 0x30. This finding enables an actual embedded-GFX MemoryFile return but still does not prove a title-safe render surface or portrait pixels.

STEAM DETECTION BUG (2026-07-18, cost a full overnight). On this WSL2 box Steam runs as a WINDOWS process (steam.exe / steamwebhelper.exe, visible ONLY via tasklist.exe), NOT a Linux process. So 'pgrep -x steam' ALWAYS returns no-match = FALSE NEGATIVE. I trusted that false negative as 'Steam is down' and blocked the whole night on a non-existent blocker while Steam was up the whole time. The SAME broken check gates run-product-continue-direct-probe.sh preflight (line ~190 'pgrep -x steam ... || fatal') and me3-launch-lib.sh, so the approved launcher would ALSO refuse to launch on this machine. FIX: Steam-running check must be WSL-aware = (pgrep -x steam on native-linux Steam) OR (tasklist.exe | grep -iq '^steam.exe') for Windows Steam. LESSON: never treat a brittle presence-check's negative as ground truth for a hard blocker -- verify the actual process signature (tasklist.exe on WSL) before declaring blocked. Also: the game (eldenring.exe) + Steam are Windows-native here; the recent target/product-runtime-manual runs were user-launched -- the exact launch invocation for this box still needs confirming. Why: a wrong environment-detection primitive silently converts 'ready' into 'blocked'. How to apply: use the WSL-aware steam check; when a runtime blocker is a simple presence check, cross-verify with tasklist.exe before stopping. Links [[morning-run-recipe-repeatable-multi-save-2026-07-18]] [[not-determined-not-nondeterministic]].

*** RETRACTED 2026-06-21 (validated wrong by direct disasm of 0x14067b1a0). *** This memory claimed the cold save-read is discarded at enqueue gate 0x14240e420 / GATE B (0x141ee1240) because an IO-worker context is unregistered. THAT IS FALSE. RTTI proves 0x14240e420's object *0x144843038 = DLUP::DLUserPreferenceService (user SETTINGS service, vtable 0x1430b3b00) and *0x144843030 = DLKRD::HeapAllocator (vtable 0x1430c7d30) -- NOT an IO-worker registry/device-mgr. GATE B gates a SETTINGS/preference read, not the character save read.

GROUND TRUTH (direct disasm 0x14067b1a0): the full-load INITIATOR does NOT call 0x14240e420 at all. It reads GameMan(*0x143d69918)+0xb80; if ==0 it gets the state machine via 0x140e6e060 then request_transition(rcx=sm, edx=0xa, r8d=slot) via 0x140e6eb80; on accept (al!=0) writes b80=2. Sibling 0x14067b200 checks GameMan+0xdf0!=0 then sets b80=2. BODY 0x14067b290 allocs the 0x280000 read buffer via *(0x143d872e0)->vtable[+0x50]. So the b80 save path is a STATE-MACHINE transition to state 0xa, NOT the preference enqueue.

REAL WALL (cold path b80 2->0, never 3): the state-0xa transition is accepted (b80=2) then bounced back to idle (b80=0) by the state machine update tick / state-0xa handler precondition -- NOT by GATE B. Next: trace 0x140e6e060 (get SM), 0x140e6eb80 (request_transition preconditions), and the state-0xa handler that should call body 0x14067b290 and set b80=3. See b80-GATEB-* memories: they are correct ABOUT THE SETTINGS SUBSYSTEM but IRRELEVANT to the save read.

VERDICT (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, static RE READ-ONLY, full VAs) on the brief's DECISIVE QUESTION: can the native Load-menu slot-deserialize land GameMan+0xc30=a slot's REAL map AND apply the character AT THE TITLE, WITHOUT first building+ticking a full MoveMapStep world-load? 

=== ANSWER: OPTION 2 IS NOT VIABLE AT THE BARE TITLE. The self-driven menu deserialize requires a LIVE SESSION (the MoveMapListStep is the IN-GAME save-list menu), and the CHARACTER-APPLYING full deserialize 0x14067b290 is reachable ONLY through the MoveMapStep. Pivot to OPTION 1 (SetState(5) build+tick MoveMapStep). ===

=== THE RECONCILIATION (why the user sees the menu load without an obvious 'world build') ===
There ARE two independent b80 drivers (see menu-load-path-is-movemaplist-self-driven-not-movemapstep-2026): the MoveMapListStep load MENU self-drives b80 0->1->2->3 within its own step fns (initiator 0x14067b4e0 via item cb 0x14082ba30; LoadSaveData idx4 0x140af19b0 -> 0x14067b200 sets b80=2; idx5 0x140af1a30 -> poll 0x140679180 sets b80=3 + reads c30 via 0x140679560) AND registers the FD4 stream worker 0x144842d40 itself (advance helper 0x140af1b40 @0x140af1c05 via 0x142656b00 key 0x59682f01). So the menu DOES drive its own b80 + worker -- the user is RIGHT that it does not need the separate MoveMapStep dispatcher 0x140afa6d0. BUT this MoveMapListStep is the IN-GAME / SESSION save-list menu: its idx0 0x140af1720 asserts session singleton 0x144588e98 + resource mgr 0x143d5b0f8 non-null; its deserialize-chain callers (0x140b02110 asserts session 0x143d65f88; 0x140766268 in the CSFeMan region) are all session/world contexts. At the bare press-any-button title these session singletons are NULL (per native-continue-and-slotn-recipe-2026), so the MoveMapListStep cannot be instantiated/ticked, and its self-driven b80 lane is unreachable. The user's empirical 'menu shows + loads the save' happens AFTER the front-end has bootstrapped (post title-accept) where the session objects exist -- it is NOT available at the cold title the autoload DLL targets.

=== WHY THE CHARACTER APPLY STILL FORCES THE MoveMapStep ===
The CHARACTER is applied (stats/inventory -> PlayerGameData 0x144588268 via 0x140e365c0) ONLY by the FULL deserialize 0x14067b290, whose SOLE caller is dispatcher-1 0x140afbad0 @0x140afbc3e, whose SOLE caller is inside the MoveMapStep update 0x140aff640 @0x140aff720 (recon_callscan: 1 each). The menu's idx5 0x140af1a30 reads c30 (0x140679560) but does NOT call 0x14067b290 and does NOT call the char-apply 0x140e365c0; the menu c30 is produced by a PARALLEL deserialize 0x14067e150 (-> c30 writer 0x14067bd70) used for slot RECORDS/preview, not the full char apply. So even granting the menu lane, landing the REAL character (not just c30) at the title still needs the MoveMapStep path. There is NO title-reachable, MoveMapStep-less function that both sets c30=real-map AND applies the character.

=== THEREFORE: PIVOT TO OPTION 1 (the proven 3-phase / native PlayGame drive) ===
The cleanest zero-input drive remains: from the idx10 handler on the live SimpleTitleStep, run the native PlayGame/Continue confirm 0x140b0e180 (sets owner+0xbc=map, child request, SetState 5) -- per own-stepper-control-verified-and-driver-call-2026 -- letting the native pump build CSFeMan + the MoveMapStep, whose update 0x140aff640 runs dispatcher-1 0x140afbad0 -> full deserialize 0x14067b290 (c30=real map + CHAR applied) -> dispatcher-2 apply-to-world. The ONE residual blocker is unchanged: at a FORCED/synthetic title the FD4 stream worker task 0x144842d40 may not be registered/ticked so the async slot-IO (b80 1->2->3) stalls (async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026). CRUCIAL NEW LEAD: the MoveMapListStep advance helper 0x140af1b40 shows the EXACT, callable worker-registration ABI (lazily build 0x144842d40 via 0x141eceb10 if null, then register with scheduler 0x142656b00 using key 0x59682f01) -- this is the same registration the brief/prior memories said was the unreversed wall. Driving the load via the genuine native PlayGame path (which runs in-context with real FD4Time/frame-ctx) is the most likely way to get this registration to happen natively; if it still stalls, replicate 0x140af1b40's 5-instruction register sequence (build+0x142656b00(0x59682f01,&task)) from the in-context handler.

VERIFY targets unchanged: [GameMan+0xc30] != 0xa010000 (real map), CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 != 0, child(owner+0x2e8)+0xd8 drains 1->2->0, PlayerGameData 0x144588268 = the slot's real character, NO blank-char write.

PRECISE OFFSET CHAIN + anomaly (2026-06-21, static decode + live capture, via scripts/read-tracebreakpoint.py). The worker path-builder slot2 0x142410cd0 builds '%s\%s%s%s' (dir,name,ext,ext) into job+0x478. The DIRECTORY %s source chain: rcx=[job+0xe0] (=the per-request object, live=0x7ffe937856d0, == io18); 0x14240aa60(rcx)='lea rax,[rcx+8];ret' -> request_obj+8; 0x1424090d0(rcx)='lea rax,[rcx+0xe0]; cmpq [rax+0x18],8; jb sso; mov rax,[rax] (heap); ret' -> reads the std::string at (request_obj+8)+0xe0 = request_obj+0xe8 (data SSO@+0xe8, size@+0xf8, cap@+0x100). 

LIVE CAPTURE (request_obj=0x7ffe937856d0): *(req+0xe8)=0x32006c0073002e = UTF-16 '.sl2'; *(req+0xf8)=0x4 (=size 4). => the DIRECTORY component the worker formats = '.sl2' (4 chars) -- MALFORMED (a directory of '.sl2' is wrong; should be the save folder path). So the cold read builds an invalid path ('.sl2\<name>...') => open 0x142415200 device-readiness 0x141edc930 fails => error before ReadFile => fast empty completion (b80 2->0). NOTE my earlier 'dir_size=0 at req+0xf0' was the WRONG offset (req+0xf0 is mid-SSO-buffer; the real size is at req+0xf8=4). 

CONFIRMS: cold/menu-free submit does NOT populate the read request's save-DIRECTORY string (request_obj+0xe8) with the real save folder; '.sl2' leaks in as the dir. FIX: find what the LIVE save-session sets as the request's directory string (request_obj+0xe8) -- the real save folder path (Steam userdata .../EldenRing/<steamid>/) -- and populate it cold before the read; OR run the live request-setup step the cold path skips. NEXT: capture request_obj+0xe8 full chars + the name component (0x14240d7e0(job)) to see the full malformed path; trace who sets request_obj+0xe8 in the live flow (RIP/ref scan + decode the request init). DECISIVE GOLDEN COMPARISON (2026-06-22, user-driven vanilla Continue vs our OWN-LOAD continue_confirm, same map c30=0x1c000000). The golden (native menu Continue, char Vagabond L45 slot2 ac0=2) loaded the player INTO THE WORLD in ~6s: frame750 mms_state=3 player=false (IDENTICAL observable state to our stall: block_count=33, target_block_present=true, req_blockid=0x1c000000, io_inflight=0) -> frame784 EVENT T_controllable player=1 + LOAD-CORRECTNESS name=Vagabond level=45 -> frame810 mms_state=13 player_present=TRUE -> frame840 mms_state=16. So mms_state(+0x48)=3 is a NORMAL TRANSIENT (golden passes through it in <1s), NOT a hard wall; +0x48 climbs 3->13->16 as the player spawns. OUR OWN-LOAD (continue_confirm only, Hero L7 slot0 ac0=0) sits at mms_state=3 FOREVER (900+ frames). At mms_state=3 the OBSERVABLE state is identical between golden and ours (block registered+requested, io_inflight=0) -- the difference is the m28 block's resource STREAMS TO RESIDENCY (load phase 0xa) in golden within ~2s and NOT in ours. RULED OUT (all identical in both): coord/req_blockid, block registration (present), block_count=33, io_inflight=0, csfeman/mms/resmgr built. THE GAP: the full native Continue drives the resource-stream of the registered block to phase 0xa (-> WorldResWait passes -> mms 3->13->16 -> player spawns); our continue_confirm/SetState5-only path registers+requests the block but does NOT complete its resource-stream. NOTE: io_inflight=0 in golden too (the in-flight window is between 30-frame samples; golden streams fast). NEXT: RE what the native Continue path runs (beyond continue_confirm/SetState5) that drives a requested block to 0xa -- the FD4 resource-load worker/dispatch + whether our menu-free SetState5 enters the in-game task group that ticks it. Candidate: drive more of the native in-game load (IngameInit table-0x143d71340 machine / the resource-stream task) rather than continue_confirm alone. Golden char data loads perfectly either way (char/buffer half fully solved). Save-safe (golden autosaved 6b333ddd -> restored fixture to 899eed15). Tool: scripts/run-golden-observe.sh (user-driven, no watcher). DECISIVE runtime finding (run 309, 2026-06-15) -- the outer SimpleTitleStep (the IngameInit-bearing, world-priming state machine) is NOT INSTANTIATED at the title screen. find_outer scan (object with +0x10 == SimpleTitleStep table 0x143d71340 AND +0xc0 == the live InGameStep) over all committed memory returns table_matches=1 but that single match is SPURIOUS (first_match=0x1000ffc58, a low static address, its +0xc0=0, not the InGameStep at 0x7fffa1ec9a40). The InGameStep DOES exist (inner TitleStep owner+0x2e8 non-null, from the TitleStep ctor) but its parent outer SimpleTitleStep does not exist yet. This finally explains pump_ran(+0x6b0)=0 across all runs -- the outer handlers never run because the outer object is not constructed at the title. CONSEQUENCES -- recipe B (drive the existing outer IngameInit 0x140b0a1f0) is IMPOSSIBLE (no outer this-pointer exists). Recipe A (set the title-accept force flag 0x143d856a0) CRASHES (confirmed twice). force_play_game submits an InGameStep load that orphans (no outer to prime/route it, stalls at step 7 MoveMap_Update because the world-streaming subsystems were never primed). So ALL in-process state/flag shortcuts to zero-input autoload are EXHAUSTED -- the world-priming (IngameInit/outer/menu system) only comes into existence AFTER the input-driven title-accept constructs the menu, and forcing/flagging that accept crashes. The known-good oracle (5 DirectInput Confirm pulses) works precisely because real input drives the title->menu->IngameInit construction. REMAINING FORKS (need user decision) -- (a) construct/activate the outer SimpleTitleStep ourselves (replicate the menu bring-up -- very deep), (b) accept the input-driven path (native keystate injection [[native-confirm-input-injection-keystate]] or the oracle) which is simulated input, (c) conclude that zero-in-process-input autoload from the title is blocked by this architecture. We got as far as -- load SUBMITTED + step machine pumped 4->5->7 via direct calls, stalling only on world-stream priming that requires the absent outer. Related: [[load-needs-ingameinit-subsystem-priming]] [[ingamestep-direct-pump-works-stuck-step4]] [[title-accept-lever-143d856a0]] [[native-confirm-input-injection-keystate]].

FIX (2026-07-15, native Windows, ER 1.16.2): The System->Quit ghosting (bug 2) AND the 2nd-in-process reload crash (bug 1, garbage-vtable AV at CSGaitemImp::Deserialize 0x67141a) were BOTH caused by a MinHook install REENTRANCY race. install_system_quit_menu_window_job_run_hook (system_quit_ownership_repro.rs:644) and install_system_quit_gaitem_deserialize_hook (system_quit_hooks.rs:531) used a  guard that did NOT block a reentrant call while the first was mid-install (INSTALLED set only on full success). So the install ran twice -> double MhHook::new (ALREADY_CREATED) + double MH_EnableHook -> MH_ERROR_ENABLED -> the handler non-deterministically didn't fire. When the MenuWindowJob::Run hook loses the race: no System-menu hide (ghosting) AND the return-title/continue_confirm reset+feed path never runs so the native deserialize crashes on char#1's stale gaitem table. FIX: replace the guard with an ATOMIC once-claim (compare_exchange NOT_INSTALLED->INSTALLED_YES; reentrant callers bail), accept MH_ERROR_ENABLED as success, roll back to NOT_INSTALLED only on real failure. Also switched from queue_enable+MH_ApplyQueued (shared-queue race) to atomic single-target MH_EnableHook (crate::mh::MH_EnableHook). VERIFIED live: hooked ...(immediate enable, created=true), MenuWindowJob::Run fires reliably (268 ProfileSelect), hide fires, ghosting GONE, and the FIRST profile switch loads cleanly (continue_confirm intercepted -> gaitem pristine-restore released=2572 -> own-load-feed slot=6 ret=1 -> char loads, 0 AVs). ALSO added a backstop reset in the gaitem-deserialize hook (system_quit_hooks.rs) before the native deserialize when SELECTED_SLOT<10. REMAINING (user gate: 'do not mark fixed until we can load more than one character'): the 2ND consecutive switch stalls -- return-title REQUEST+SUBMIT reach the title but continue_confirm never fires the 2nd time (product-core-autoload Continue driver is one-shot; rearm_boot_progress_for_own_menu_load re-arms only the loading BAR, not the Continue driver) -> parked at the covered title = black screen (not a crash). Bugs 2+3 fully fixed+confirmed; bug 1 first-switch works, consecutive pending re-arm. See [[system-quit-menuwindowjob-run-dead-hook-rootcause-2026-07-15]] [[game-is-1162-re-baseline-is-1161-2026-07-15]].

USER DIRECTIVE (2026-06-29, enforced): EVERY Elden Ring runtime run MUST be hard-capped at 45s (the canonical .auto/runtime_timeout_cap_seconds). Do NOT use RUNTIME_NO_TEARDOWN=1 / manual no-teardown runs that linger minutes -- that violates the cap and makes the user babysit. The agent repeatedly left ER up for minutes via run-lookat-smoke.sh / run-postcontinue-lookat-smoke.sh (both source manual-portrait-drive.env which sets RUNTIME_NO_TEARDOWN=1). CORRECT PATTERN: run with auto-teardown so the watcher hard-kills at <=45s and the background job COMPLETES (notifying the agent) -> read the artifact (telemetry + er-effects-autoload-debug.log) AFTER it self-terminates. No Monitor babysitting, no manual pkill. Override RUNTIME_NO_TEARDOWN=0 (or don't source the manual env) and rely on the 45s cap. CAVEAT for the post-Continue portrait work: zero-input autoload reaches the Continue teardown only ~30-40s in, so a 45s-capped run leaves a tiny/no post-Continue window -- the post-Continue spared-renderer oracle may not get data within the cap. Resolve by making the autoload reach Continue FASTER, or capturing the spared-renderer oracle in the brief post-Continue window, NOT by extending the cap. Relates: portrait-lookat-menu-model-cycling-blocker-2026-06-29.

CORRECTION + DEFINITIVE FINDING (2026-06-16, proven with the new hard input block as oracle). The d180 leaf-tick CAPTURE is INPUT-GATED, NOT zero-input. PRIOR CLAIMS in zero-input-menu-open-milestone-2026 / mainmenu-built-at-owner130-not-pumped-zeroinput-2026 that the leaf-update hook (cap_menu_item_update_hook) captured d180 (docall 0x140820c60->0x14081ead0) "zero-input" were CONTAMINATED -- the user was mashing keyboard/mouse during those runs. With the hard input block ACTIVE (hard-input-block-uncontaminatable-probe-2026; user mashed, zero effect), the own-stepper TIMED OUT at 3000 waits and d180 NEVER surfaced.

=== WHAT ACTUALLY HAPPENS ZERO-INPUT (clean, block-active) ===
- own_stepper SetState(2)=BeginLogo builds the list; STAGE1d self-fires the open-menu registrar 0x1409b24e0 at ~waits=176 (latch=1, genuinely zero-input). 
- BUT only the 3 TITLE-composition MenuWindowJobs tick (vt 0x142aa97e8): c000 docall 0x140764b80->0x140763fc0, c140 0x140820b30->0x14081e5e0 (TitleTopDialog builder), c280 0x140764b70->0x140763f10. Callers = the title pump (0xb0d526 MenuJobWait, 0x7a968e, 0x7aa2bb iterator).
- NO main-menu entry (Continue/Load d180/NewGame, docall family 0x14078ee60 / 0x14081ead0) EVER ticks. d180 does NOT appear. The user's clicks in the contaminated runs were doing the actual title->main-menu pump advance.
=> The title->main-menu PUMP-SWITCH is input-gated, CONFIRMED. The self-fire registrar alone does not register-and-pump the main menu zero-input.

=== STATIC WALK ALSO DOES NOT REACH d180 ZERO-INPUT (yet) ===
diagnostic_job_tree_walk is now IfElseJob/decorator-aware (ifelsejob-child-layout-decoded-b0a8-2026 + single-child decorator prologue-decoder decorator_child_offset: reads `mov rcx,[rcx+disp]` 48 8b 49 d8 / 48 8b 89 d32). angrE-slot-index-mismatch-selected-vs-saveslot-2026-07-18: The slot-aware disarm (DLL f5c884b4) FAILED because SYSTEM_QUIT_QUICKLOAD_SELECTED_SLOT and GameMan.save_slot use DIFFERENT index spaces. Live telemetry during angrE boot autoload: system_quit_quickload_selected_slot=1, autoload_slot=0, game_save_slot=0 (gm-snap save_slot=0 during load, -1 after teardown), product_core_callsite_last_slot=1. REAL ZERO-INPUT DRAIN: ExecuteMenuJob via wrapper FUN_1407a91e0(rcx=queue_owner, rdx=&FD4Time). queue_owner == the DIALOG (struct: +0x8 active MenuJob* slot, +0x10 the CS::MenuJobQueue we PushBackJob into, +0x38 pending indicator). FUN_1407a91e0: if active slot empty && +0x38 pending -> pop from +0x10 (FUN_1407a8870, pure ptr arith) -> CS::MenuJob::Assign into active slot -> ExecuteMenuJob(active_slot, time). ExecuteMenuJob (dump 0x1407a96f0): cur=*job; AtomicInc; cur->vftable[2](cur,&result,&{FD4Time vtbl,delta}) [the job's Execute]; if !ShouldContinue -> *job=0. ABI to call each frame: RCX=dialog, RDX=&{void* FD4Time_vtbl; f32 delta} (build a tiny stack FD4Time; caller FUN_1408c9db0 just stacks vtbl+delta), R8/R9 unused. This is THE standard menu-job pump (43 callers, mostly Update methods + STEP_MenuJobWait); zero input, no DInput/keystate/XInput on the path.

NATIVE DRIVER: FUN_1408c9db0(FrontEndViewRoot,delta) [from Update@0x1408c7727] pumps 3 sibling queue-owners (+0x250f0/+0x25138/+0x25180, stride 0x48) each via FUN_1407a91e0. CAVEAT: dump VAs are SHIFTED vs deobf and decode as stubs at the raw dump VA -- RE-GROUND on eldenring-deobf.bin by prologue near the dump VA before calling: FUN_1407a91e0 (dump 0x1407a91e0), ExecuteMenuJob (dump 0x1407a96f0). Anchor: PushBackJob deobf 0x1407a9250 (already grounded) -> derive relative to the same module map.

PLAN: keep maybe_fire_tfc_continue's build (selector 0x1409a8eb0 -> dispatcher build branch) + PushBackJob to dialog+0x10; DROP the kick; cache the dialog; add a per-frame drain in the recurring task: FUN_1407a91e0(rcx=dialog, rdx=&FD4Time) until the job completes / world streams. IMPLEMENTATION SPEC for the #3 fix (coherent color+depth readback) on the live path -- see [[loading-portrait-live-path-deep-fix-2026-07-03]] and [[loading-portrait-4bug-taxonomy-2026-07-03]].

ROOT confirmed by code trace: color is read at startup_hooks.rs:3451 readback_offscreen_fast(off) on the RB_FAST_* cached queue/fence (waits only for OUR copy); depth is read ~100 lines later at startup_hooks.rs:3554 inside apply_depth_alpha_key -> compute_depth_mask -> readback_depth_fast on the SEPARATE RB_DEPTH_* queue/fence. The model render (per_frame_push_hook) already ran earlier (~3130); between our two independently-fenced copies the game's OWN render thread runs async and can write the offscreen RT, so color=frameN, depth=frameN+1 -> mask shape mismatches the head (bug #3). gpu_readback.rs:522 comment literally notes the depth cache is 'separate from RB_FAST_* so color and depth never share a queue' -- that separation IS the desync.

FIX = single-command-list, single-fence readback of BOTH. New fn readback_offscreen_color_depth_coherent(gpu_child) -> Option<(u32,u32,Vec<u8>, u32,u32,Vec<f32>)>: (1) resolve color RT via find_d3d12_resource_ex(gpu_child,0,false,PROFILE_RT_PIN) and depth RT via find_depth_resource(gpu_child); (2) new RB_COH_* cached statics (queue/alloc/list/fence + colorbuf + depthbuf + sizes + fenceval); (3) reset alloc+list, barrier color COMMON->COPY_SOURCE + CopyTextureRegion color->colorbuf (subresource 0, GetCopyableFootprints like readback_resource_cached_fast), barrier depth COMMON->COPY_SOURCE + CopyTextureRegion depth PLANE0->depthbuf (GetCopyableFootprints(&desc,0,1,..) like readback_depth_resource_cached), barriers back, Close; (4) ONE ExecuteCommandLists, ONE Signal, ONE Wait; (5) Map both, de-swizzle color (swap_rb for BGRA per gpu_readback.rs:1082) + reinterpret depth texels as f32. catch_unwind; return None on any failure.

WIRING: split compute_depth_mask(gpu_child,w,h) into compute_depth_mask_from_depth(depth:&[f32],dw,dh,w,h)->Option<Vec<u8>> (the histogram-gap logic at gpu_readback.rs:1386-1477 unchanged, just take the buffer) + keep the old reader calling it. Add apply_depth_alpha_key_from_depth(w,h,cpx,depth,dw,dh) mirroring apply_depth_alpha_key (gpu_readback.rs:1330) but using the passed depth (still caches to LAST_DEPTH_MASK, fail-open). In the drive (startup_hooks.rs ~3451..3554): replace readback_offscreen_fast(off) + apply_depth_alpha_key(off,...) with readback_offscreen_color_depth_coherent(off) -> compute mask from the coherent depth -> apply to the coherent color -> existing keyed+tear publish gate. FALLBACK: if the coherent fn returns None, fall back to the current separate path (degrade, never crash). Keep the committed tear-gate (b89bcb7) as-is.

Static RE 2026-06-28 ScaleformStringRef helper ABI for safe MemoryFile return: deobf 0x141141390 (dump FUN_1411413b0) is safe to call as `init_string_ref(out: *mut u64, name: *const i8) -> *mut u64` for a normal null-terminated ASCII/C string. It initializes `*out` to the shared empty string block `0x143b6aa68`, increments empty refcount at `0x143b6aa70`, then if name is non-null calls deobf 0x141142b70 (Ghidra labels body as FUN_141142b90 due boundary shift). 0x141142b70 computes strlen(name) with 0x141175160, allocates a string block from the current allocator/state (or global allocator 0x144593250) via vfunc +0x50 with length+0x10, writes `{len, refcount=1, bytes at +0xc, trailing NUL}`, stores the block pointer plus low tag bits into `*out`, and releases the previous ref via 0x141140bb0 / allocator +0x60. Therefore a future synthetic `CSScaleformFileOpener::Open` hook can create `let mut name_ref: [usize; 2?]` on stack, call 0x141141390(&mut name_ref, SCALEFORM_SYNTHETIC_COVER_URL.as_ptr()), allocate 0x30 bytes through allocator singleton 0x144593250 vfunc +0x50, then call MemoryFile ctor deobf 0x140ce7bb0(obj, &name_ref, embedded_gfx_ptr, embedded_gfx_len). The name_ref must remain valid only through ctor because ctor copies it into object+0x10 via 0x1411410f0. This removes the earlier uncertainty: the helper does accept a normal char* name; do not pass raw char* directly to the MemoryFile ctor.

TOOLING + ROOT CAUSE (2026-06-15). Built an in-DLL crash/exit logger (opt-in ER_EFFECTS_CRASH_LOG): a vectored exception handler logging access violations (faulting RVA + caller stack via trace_callers_summary) plus MinHooks on ExitProcess/TerminateProcess/RtlExitUserProcess/NtTerminateProcess logging the caller of a clean exit. Harness: ER_EFFECTS_CRASH_LOG writes the trigger and copies er-effects-crash.log to the artifact dir; RUNTIME_SEH_TRACE exports WINEDEBUG=+seh. It immediately OVERTURNED the earlier (wrong) telemetry-I/O-stall theory. ACTUAL cause of the continue-drive ~12-19s self-exit: our continue_drive_tick calls the MoveMapList dispatcher 0x140afb880 with a SYNTHETIC owner, which fails a FromSoft validity ASSERTION. The assert handler (0x141eb98d0, wrapper 0x141eb97a0, mode global 0x143b4059c defaulting to mode 0) deliberately CRASHES via a null write mov dword ds:0x0,0xDEADBABE at 0x141eb9999 -> access-violation -> NtTerminateProcess(code=0xc0000005). Crash-log caller chain proved it: our DLL (high 0x6ffe.. base) -> dispatcher 0x140afba17 (inside 0x140afb880) -> assert wrapper 0x141eb97b8 -> assert crash 0x141eb9999. The lite/inert bisect was AMBIGUOUS (lite skipped BOTH the telemetry I/O and the dispatcher call) -- the crash hook disambiguated in one run. IMPLICATIONS -- (1) the synthetic-owner shortcut for the dispatcher is INVALID, the dispatcher asserts on it, so the genuine continue load needs a REAL MoveMapList owner (the recipe open question), not a synthetic buffer. (2) Many earlier research crashes attributed to session-degradation/watchdog/EAC were likely the SAME class -- our calls into game functions with synthetic/invalid context tripping FromSoft asserts that deliberately crash. (3) NEXT TOOL idea -- set the assert mode global 0x143b4059c to 1 (print+continue) to make asserts non-fatal and capture the assert message (what field/check failed), instead of crashing. Related: [[real-continue-load-driver-recipe]].

ER Continue load FINAL VERDICT (static RE complete, 2026-06-23): The push-target IS dialog+0x50 and our DLL's offset is CORRECT. Byte-confirmed in native FUN_1409b2630 (the TitleTopDialog title-flow builder) at deobf 0x1409b27de-0x1409b2801: with r13=dialog (live TitleTopDialog), it builds bundle{[0]=*(dialog+0xa38)=titleFlowContext, [1]=(lea 0x50(r13))=dialog+0x50} and calls the load builder 0x140833880 (=dump FUN_140833970). bundle[1]=dialog+0x50 becomes the MenuWindowJob.field2_0x50 push target. The selector 0x1409a8eb0 does mov(rcx),rcx then lea 0x50(rcx),r8 => (*owner+0xe0)+0x50 = dialog+0x50, SAME thing. A subagent deep-scan PROVED MenuWindow+0x50 is a DLFixedVector<ptr,8>, count at MenuWindow+0x98, init 0 by ctor 0x140741a60 (field130_0x98=0), APPEND-ONLY (the only erase 0x140733d79 is DEAD/0 callers; dtor never clears it), vtable slot10 (0x140734300: cmpq 0,0x98(rcx);seta) reads it as a count, and NO code ever writes a pointer/large value into a real MenuWindow's +0x98. THEREFORE: if we observe garbage (a ~0x7fff pointer) at dialog+0x98, the object is NOT a fully-constructed/live TitleTopDialog at that instant -- NOT a wrong offset. Our DLL constants verified correct: DIALOG_MENUWINDOW_VEC_50_OFFSET=0x50, COUNT_48=0x48, A38=0xa38, TFC 14c, vtable gate 0x142b26468 (=real, ctor 0x1409a82d0 stores it). FIX is NOT re-grounding; it is object-identity/lifecycle: ensure dialog=*(owner+0xe0) is the LIVE pumped dialog whose ctor ran (vtable-gate already done) AND that we are not racing the pump; the count-reset-to-0 hack (experiments.rs ~4059) papers over reading a non-dialog/garbage. Real fix: build the load via the native path that supplies dialog+0x50 from the SAME live dialog (invoke selector 0x1409a8eb0 with rcx=owner+0xe0, which is what maybe_fire_tfc_continue does) rather than a forged out-of-band MenuJobWithContext install at owner+0x130; or gate the fire on count being a sane small value (already added) AND on the dialog being in the Loop state.

When checking whether a user-visible Elden Ring launch actually spawned the game, run an immediate exact lightweight check such as pgrep -x eldenring.exe right after launch. Do not delay with Python/log/telemetry parsing first. If the user has had time to quit normally, a later empty pgrep only means the process is no longer running, not that launch failed.

For Elden Ring runtime probes in er-effects-rs, do not rely on slow manual/LLM-paced input timing. Scaleform GFx exporter (ER menu .gfx, all uncompressed GFX v0x0b) is NOT RecordHeader-length-deterministic: 14,766 tags across the 114-file menu corpus use the long form (0x3f-escape + u32 length) even though their body is <= 0x3e bytes, and it is not decidable per tag-code -- codes 26 (PlaceObject2) and 70 (PlaceObject3) each appear in BOTH short and over-long form with small bodies. Therefore a byte-identical GFX codec MUST capture a per-tag force_long bit at parse time and reproduce it on write; you cannot derive short-vs-long purely from body length. This is implemented in crates/er-gfx (Tag::Unknown{code,raw,force_long} + Tag::DefineSprite{force_long}); do NOT 'simplify' the force_long field away -- it is required for the 114/114 byte-identical round-trip test. End(0) is always short across the corpus. Movie RECT is preserved verbatim as raw bytes at Tier 0 (no bit re-encode). FileLength is always recomputed (placeholder+backpatch).

REVERSES the run-249 handoff plan to 'clear [ToS-job+0xa8] -> graceful fallback to +0xe8'. THAT PLAN IS UNSAFE -- it crashes the same way the rejected return-null-wrapper did. PROOF via the MenuWindowJob ctor 0x1407acb00 (allocs 0x138 bytes, writes vtable 0x142aa97e8 = MenuWindowJob vtable, realize 0x1407ad1c0 at vt+0x10): the job is 3 sub-objects each holding a functor at sub+0x38: (A) +0x70 -> functor at +0xa8 = cloned from caller arg r9/[r13+0x38] = the BUILD/override functor (ToS factory vtable 0x142b28308 for the ToS job); (B) +0xb0 -> functor at +0xe8 = HARDCODED 0 (mov r12,[rax+0x38] @0x1407acc5e, r12=0) and NEVER conditionally repopulated; (C) +0xf0 -> functor at +0x128 = 0x1407ad810 which is an AVAILABILITY PREDICATE (reads global 0x143d6b7b0, calls 0x140765f20, returns !bool) NOT a builder; +0x130 = built component (init 0). realize path: [job+0xa8]!=0 && [job+0x10]==0 -> build via +0xa8 -> [job+0x130]; else je 0x1407ad367 -> if [job+0xe8]!=0 build via +0xe8 else je 0x1407ad3ec -> if [job+0x130]==0 call default-builder 0x1407ada40. KEY LEARNINGS (the runtime recipe): (1) rev.ng lifts non-leaf fine -- real calls are PRESERVED as tail call @callee(ABI args) and callee-saved rbx round-trips resolve (opt uses %rcx directly). (2) opt CONSTANT-FOLDS inlined-constant branches (the cookie decode vanished). (3) THE @_rsp TRAP: the lifted body has DEAD but BALANCED (push/pop net-zero per call) emulated-stack stores through pointers derived from global @_rsp; opt CANNOT DSE them (may-alias) and internal-linkage prelude defs DON'T satisfy the external decls. Stub _rsp + dead helpers (bit_parity,lshift,opaque_64,revng_undefined__rbx return 0) + the REAL callee(s) in a rtstub.c linked into both ref and recompiled. (4) CLEAN pass = rename local_0x<va>->name, rename call targets, DELETE void @newpc calls (safe, drops revng.const refs), keep everything else; llc; nm -u tells you exactly what to provide.

STATE (2026-06-18, runtime, save-safe). The cold selector path now: BUILDS the selector cold (step_ok, vtable 0x142ac71e0), installer runs (job at step+0x70), and calling the job's execute DIRECTLY ([[step+0x70]]+0x10 = menu_deser 0x14082c240)(rcx=job, rdx=&ctx, r8=&FD4Time) RUNS each frame: md_ret=0x10FC48 (a low arena ptr return, not a status), tctx[0]=ctx_state=1, tctx[1]=ctx_done=0. Per selector-tick-drive-decode, menu_deser return consumed at 0x14082c279: 1=PENDING(keep ticking), 2/3=advance, 0=submit-needed. So menu_deser returns PENDING every frame -- it is POLLING for a resident read but NEVER reaches the submit state (0), so it never allocs+submits the 0x280000 read. b80 (worker 0x143d69918+0xb80)=0, c30=0xa010000 default, ac0=-1, io18=0 across 100s of ticks. Co-driving lane 0x679510 + poll 0x679180 each frame does NOT help (no in-flight read to advance). 

=> menu_deser is the POLL+DESERIALIZE stage; it expects the full-save read to already be SUBMITTED/resident, and polls. The SUBMIT is missing cold. The request state machine (menu_deser checks state via 0x140678d70 / 0x140679180) is stuck in a state that is neither submit-needed(0) nor resident(3) -- so menu_deser waits forever. NEXT RE (decode, precise): menu_deser 0x14082c240's state machine -- what field/state it polls (0x140678d70 returns the request state?), WHY it is 'pending' not 'submit-needed(0)' cold (what initial state the request/job is in), and WHAT advances it to submit (alloc 0x280000 + 0x67b100 read) OR resident. Likely the job/request needs an explicit SUBMIT/initiate call before menu_deser's poll succeeds (the live menu's selector installer 0x140828270 may do a submit we are not triggering, OR the request needs reset to state 0). Options: (a) find + call the submit/initiate the live path uses before menu_deser polls; (b) decode 0x140678d70 to see the state + force/reset it to submit-needed(0). HARD PART DONE: selector builds + menu_deser executes cold; remaining = make menu_deser's IO request reach submit/resident. SAVE-SAFE throughout (no SetState until gated mount). Related: selector-builds-cold-but-menu_deser-read-not-draining-2026, selector-tick-drive-decode, load_activate-builds-selector-into-queue-final-wall-is-dialog-not-live-screen-2026, COLD-B80-WALL-BROKEN-activate-byte-but-char-not-applied-2026.

CRITICAL WORKFLOW FOOTGUN (2026-06-15) that silently invalidated several runtime probes. .envrc sources EVERY .envs/*.env file alphabetically with set -a (last wins), and .auto/runtime_probe.sh sources .envrc AFTER inheriting the caller env. So if multiple .envs/*.env set the same var, the alphabetically-last file clobbers it -- including AUTO_RUNTIME_ENV_FILE that I exported on the measure.sh command line. I had created 3 pointer files (.envs/ingamestep-pump.env, selectbot-probe.env, title-proceed-gate.env) all setting AUTO_RUNTIME_ENV_FILE -- title-proceed-gate.env sorts last, so EVERY runtime run since was forced to .auto/runtime-env.title-proceed-gate regardless of which env I selected on the command line. Consequence -- the read-only selectbot control and the ingamestep-pump run BOTH actually ran the title-proceed latch experiment, not what I intended. FIX -- the runtime target now lives in a SINGLE file .envs/zzz-runtime-target.env (sorts last, wins), and the 3 pointer files are neutralized (comment-only). To switch experiments edit ONLY zzz-runtime-target.env. ALSO note rtk grep / ripgrep SKIPS .envs (gitignored) by default, so grepping .envs for a var silently returns nothing -- use Read or grep --no-ignore. Verify the active target by reading the artifact dir request-file copies (e.g. title-proceed-gate-request.txt present means that experiment ran). Related: [[runtime-session-instability-tick120-ceiling]] [[title-accept-lever-143d856a0]].

LoadJob 0x14082c240 internals (extends native-loadjob-menujobresult-loadjobcontext). Body 0x14082c374+:
- Reads ctx+0 dword (mov ecx,[rsi] at 0x14082c2ae/0x14082c326/0x14082c377) -> passes to helper 0x14067a810. ctx+0 is the only LoadJobContext field used for selection; ctx+4 is written =1 (status). - Completion functor 0x142ac76f0 = _Func_impl<lambda ... bool> (returns bool ok).
IMPLICATION for stepper: manager-side slot selection (set_save_slot on 0x143d872e0 family) is already understood/used by the DLL; the REAL-PATH change is to enqueue the native LoadJob (0x14082c240 via assembler 0x1408288e0 + enqueue 0x1407418d0) instead of calling continue_load_67b750/b80_deserialize directly (those are the forbidden direct shortcuts). STILL TODO: orchestrator this-class; exact ctx+0 meaning via 0x14067a810; how set_save_slot relates to ctx+0.

The memory asked: is the router_this (CS::CSMenu select/selectable-row controller) builder CALLABLE ZERO-INPUT or fundamentally input-poll-gated? ANSWER: CALLABLE ZERO-INPUT.
CHAIN (all purely constructive, NO input reads):
- router_this factory 0x140907150: pure ctor -- allocates 0x13d0 bytes (heap 0x143d87350 via 0x141eb9ed0) then calls CS::CSMenu ctor 0x1409060d0(rcx=obj, rdx=parent, r8d=flag, r9=cfg). Sole rel32 caller is its own wrapper.

IMPLICATION: the title-menu select layer (router_this + selectable rows + Load-Game node) does NOT require input -- only the framework's focus-triggered INVOCATION of the build-lambda. ZERO-INPUT LEVER: invoke the router_this build-lambda's _Do_call 0x1407fd450 directly (functor this + MenuWindow*/SceneProxy& from the rendered title menu context) to realize the select layer, then the Load-Game node exists -> fire run 0x1409aaba0(node) -> ProfileLoadDialog -> primes save-IO -> native load slot 0. STILL TODO (runtime): obtain the functor-this + MenuWindow*/SceneProxy& args at the rendered title menu, and verify the deeper ctor 0x1409060d0 / cfg-build 0x140745280 have no input read (likely constructive). Then wire an own_stepper that invokes the build-lambda zero-input. Fix native_fullread slot default (must be 0 not 9).

IN-PLACE HEAD-PROTECTOR VISIBILITY on the menu character model (static RE 2026-07-04, Ghidra dump). This is the mechanism the Status-screen 'Hide helm' X-toggle uses -- INSTANT, in place, ONE live model, NO rebuild. The CSMenu*ModelRend update task FUN_140bba820 (dump; deobf 0xbba730) steps ONE live CSChrAsmModelIns (renderer+0x778 == param_1[0xef]) every tick and RE-COMPUTES part visibility each tick via FUN_1409eb8d0(model, 1) (dump 0x1409eb8d0). FUN_1409eb8d0 walks the model's partsArray at CSChrAsmModelIns+0x28 (27 = 0x1b entries), and for each non-null part calls part->vtable[0x18](part, visible_bool). Visibility is gated by a SUPPRESS MASK at CSChrAsmModelIns+0x2d0: for the 8 equipment parts (partsArray indices 7..14, loop var uVar6=0..7), hidden if bVar4!=0 where bVar4 = (per-part enable byte partsArray[+0x119+uVar6]==1 ? (mask&1) : (mask>>1)&1) | (uVar6<4 ? (mask>>2)&1 : (mask>>3)&1). So mask bit2 hides equip parts 7..10, bit3 hides 11..14; bit0/bit1 gate by the per-part enable byte. Body parts (idx 0..6) and idx 15..26 are always visible (uVar5=1) unless param_2==0 (hide-all). Because visibility is recomputed EVERY update tick, writing the +0x2d0 mask (or per-part enable byte) takes effect the next frame with NO rebuild. RELATED CHANNEL: the ChrAsm SpEffect protector-display OVERRIDE fields unk0(+0x0)/unkd4(+0xd4)/unkd8(+0xd8) (ChrAsm struct in fromsoftware-rs eldenring player_game_data.rs; equipment_param_ids at +0x7c) are written by FUN_14065f090 from head-hiding SpEffects (stateInfo 0x60/0x72/0xc0) -- the game's own 'render a different/hidden head without changing equipment' path. UNVERIFIED: the exact partsArray index (or +0x2d0 bit) that is the HEAD protector specifically -- +0x2d0 bit2/bit3 are GROUP-of-4, not per-slot; the per-head control is likely the per-part enable byte at partsArray+0x119+head_slot, OR the head part is a known fixed index. Pin it with a bounded runtime PARTS-ENUMERATION probe: read model+0x28 partsArray, log each part ptr + its source protector param id, correlate the head against ChrAsm head protector param id (chrAsm+0x7c+(0+0xc)*4). CURRENT SHIP (branch feat/helm-load-transition): helm-off = equip default head into staged ChrAsm at build (rebuild); helm-on = re-feed real head + rekick rebuild; display = hold-then-cut (bridge holds old head through rebuild, no fade). The in-place toggle above would replace the rekick REBUILD with a live +0x2d0/enable-byte flip = truly one scene, no dead window, matching the Status screen. bd er-effects-rs-vnw.

The privacy-policy/Continue gate == IsAnySavedCharacterPresent (deobf ~0x140875a70) which iterates ProfileSummary slot-active bytes [ProfileSummary+8+slot] via FUN_140261d80. Those bytes are populated FROM DISK by the boot save-summary read, which runs as MenuJobs built in CS::TitleStep::STEP_InitMenu (deobf 0x140b0d090). STEP_InitMenu installs a MenuJob chain via FUN_140b0e620 (-> requestedState=10=STEP_MenuJobWait which pumps [step+0x130] via ExecuteMenuJob). The read jobs (FUN_14082a840 GameSettings 0x60000 read + FUN_14082c330 char 0x280000 read) call FUN_14067b1f0 (gated GameMan->saveState==3) -> FUN_140e6e110/FUN_140e6e430 = SLLoadSession; on a slot with data they call ProfileSummary::MarkProfileIndexAsUsed + activate 0x140262250 ([ProfileSummary+8+slot]=1).

TITLE FSM ORDER (step table builder FUN_1400a4f50, base 0x143d71580 stride 0x10): idx0 STEP_InitMenu, idx2 STEP_BeginLogo, idx3 STEP_BeginTitle(press-any-button), idx10 STEP_MenuJobWait, idx11 Finish. FUN_140b0da50(step,N) sets requestedState=N (destination step index, direct). Boot order: BeginLogo -> InitMenu (SAVE READ) -> BeginTitle (press-any-button) -> press triggers registrar open_menu 0x1409b24e0. So the profile read is sequenced BEFORE press-any-button and BEFORE open_menu. Press-accept handler 0x1409b1260 just sets keyconfig + calls registrar 0x1409b2630; the registrar builds the menu-entry/title-flow CHECK chain (FUN_140833970 -> ~11 ShowProgressJobs) and sets a40=1 -- it does NOT itself read the profile.

OUR open_menu / pab-advance do NOT preempt the read (both run at/after BeginTitle, downstream of InitMenu). THE CULPRIT IS THE SHOWPROGRESSJOB SHORT-CIRCUIT. ShowProgressJob::Run (deobf 0x1408349c0, dump 0x140834ab0) normally runs a per-type delegate at [job+0x20] (slot+0x10) for progressType [job+0x18]; ShouldContinue-> propagate/advance else Continue(wait). The SAVE-data check/load is one of these progressTypes. Our show_progress_job_run_hook (src/experiments.rs ~11337) REPLACES Run with immediate MenuJobResult(Success=2) for ALL progressTypes and NEVER calls the delegate -> the save read delegate never runs -> ProfileSummary stays empty -> IsAnySavedCharacterPresent=false -> privacy policy. Matches runtime: ZERO .sl2 opens, ZERO ER0000 existence checks. ShowProgressJob struct: +0x10 titleFlowContext, +0x18 progressType(uint), +0x20 delegate, +0x28 timer.

ZERO-INPUT FIX: make show_progress_job_run_hook progressType-AWARE. Read [rcx+0x18]=progressType; for the SAVE-data type CALL THE ORIGINAL (run the real read delegate so SLLoadSession fires + ProfileSummary populates from staged gold); only force Success for NETWORK/sign-in/login types (the offline-modal ones). Gate unchanged (online_disable_enabled, src/lib.rs ~3698-3709). Pin the exact save progressType id by reading the leaf ShowProgressJob ctor (FUN_140839xxx/FUN_14083axxx leaf builders under FUN_140833970/FUN_140838a00 set progressType via ctor immediate) OR log [rcx+0x18] for one run. NetworkCheckJob::Run 0x140821310 is a separate job, NOT the save read -- leaving it is fine. SECONDARY risk: STEP_BeginLogo logo-SKIP branch (if [step+0xb8]!=0 -> FUN_140b0da50(step,3) jumps to BeginTitle, BYPASSING InitMenu idx0) -- verify InitMenu actually runs when applying the fix.

Added a STALE-CE0 dump to the SWITCH-ORACLE (title_tick_cover.rs): iterates WorldAreaRes+0xce0[i] (stride 0xb98), reads *(entry+0x8)=worldBlockInfo, +0x34=mapId2 -- the exact structure the getter FUN_14062f550 (deobf 0x14062f470) searches. VERIFIED (2026-06-17, disasm) in-process injection to navigate the ER front-end MAIN MENU (to surface the input-gated Load-Game item d180). IMPLEMENTED as menu_input_drive in src/experiments.rs.

INPUT LAYER: the menu reads the KEYSTATE BITMAP at inputmgr+0x90+eventId (inputmgr = [0x143d6b7b0]), indexed by MENU eventId (NOT raw HID keycode; eventId < 0x47), edge-triggered (predicate does ). The logical event array inputmgr+0xdc+id*4 is the downstream/parallel copy (writer 0x140766340(inputmgr,id,value)); the leaf input PRODUCER 0x1407ad1c0 writes BOTH ( @0x1407ad509 + event-forward @0x1407ad491). So the leaf is a producer; the menu consumes from +0x90 (and the +0xdc poll copy). Predicates: confirm 0x140765d40 (eventId 0x3d), vertical-move 0x140765780 (eventIds 0x0 and 0x45). action-namer 0x140767df0 (mov word[rcx],dx; mov rax,rcx; ret).

EVENT IDS (verified): Confirm/OK = 0x3d (edge). Vertical-move = 0x0 OR 0x45 (edge; Down-vs-Up NOT split in the predicate -- inject BOTH, only Down moves the cursor down, Up saturates at top = harmless from Continue). Inject per-frame: *(u8*)(inputmgr+0x90+id) |= 1 (TAP: assert ~2 frames then a gap so it is a clean single edge + no auto-repeat; re-write each frame asserted, the bitmap is re-polled every frame). Fallback if +0x90 is the wrong stage (title-accept-input-pipeline-wall timing): 0x140766340(inputmgr, id, 1) into the +0xdc event array.

CURSOR: highlighted index = *(i32*)(listObj+0xd4) (getter 0x140739e20 = mov eax,[rcx+0xd4]; ret), count [listObj+0xd0]. listObj is at owner+0x130 (the BeginLogo commit target; verify whether owner+0x130 IS the listObj or holds it -- read both +0xd4). Mover 0x140905fe0(listObj,idx,dir): Down=idx+1 saturating at count-1, Up=idx-1 saturating at 0, NO WRAP. == WHAT IS DONE + PROVEN (committed/pushed) ==
1. Face-forward cursor-tracking portrait on the loading screen. The loaded character (Banon) renders front-facing on the actual now-loading screen and the head/neck/posture turn toward the cursor (visually + pixel-diff proven). Eyes ride the Head bone (ER has no eye bone).

== THE ONE REMAINING BLOCKER: CONTINUOUS per-frame reaction ==
The displayed head refreshes only ~4x per loading screen, NOT per-frame. ROOT CAUSE (exhaustively pinned, static RE + 15 probes): the profile head only re-renders when FUN_140bb73a0 pops a GX SUBCONTEXT (FUN_1419e5850). That subcontext pool (g_GxDrawContext+0xf0 stack, base@+0xf8 top@+0x100, used-mask@+0x110 up to 32) is SHARED by ~10 render paths; during world-load the WORLD render holds them, so the profile pop lands only ~4x. EMPIRICALLY RULED OUT: readback resource caching, fence timeouts (0), all draw phases (0/5/7 -> 4 each), 24x pop retry (-> 4). All cheap fixes exhausted.

== NEXT STEP (the GX-pool epic; START HERE) ==
Decompile the GX 'Initilize' function (Ghidra dump): it calls the pool push FUN_1419ee6a0 at dump VA 0x1419e9269 -- that fill loop reveals the subcontext COUNT. If the count is a simple constant, the LOW-RISK fix is to bump it so the ENGINE allocates extra (valid) subcontexts -> the profile draw gets one even when the world holds the rest. (decompile_function_by_address needs the function START; 0x1419e9269 is a call site inside 'Initilize' -- get the function start via list/disasm.) Other fix dirs (higher risk): a DEDICATED subcontext held out of the pool for the profile pass (pop one when free, never return; needs the offscreen-pass lifecycle RE'd to avoid AV -- note FUN_140bb73a0 = pop->ClearRTV(subctx+0x25c8, FUN_141e8af80)->FUN_1419eaa40 return; the pop holds a ReadLock(gxctx+0xf80) released only by the return), or a separate GX render context. CRASH-SENSITIVE: a bad subctx gets cleared+submitted -> AV; validate incrementally with the smoke.

SCOPE-CORRECTED 2026-06-23 (disasm + empirical). RETRACTION of the headline impossibility: the conclusion 'literal zero-input autoload from the bare title is ARCHITECTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE -- the session bootstrap requires an accept input' is an OVER-CLAIM and is WITHDRAWN. It generalizes a NARROW LATCH finding (about ONE forcing method) into a whole-goal impossibility, which later work REFUTES for the autoload path. // EMPIRICAL REFUTATION (kept as the headline correction): we now self-fire the press-any-button advance ZERO-INPUT. maybe_auto_open_menu (experiments.rs:3854) calls the native open-menu registrar 0x1409b24e0(dialog) on FD4 Loop state with the menu-opened latch dialog+0xa40==0 -> the game's OWN handler advances title->main-menu, logged 'autonomous press-any-button equivalent, NO input' (no HID, no DInput, no keystate, no pointer). So 'the press-any-button proceed gate is FUNDAMENTALLY an input keystate / there is no input-independent native proceed' is FALSE as a general statement: calling the native registrar advances it input-free. Further, FULL-AUTOLOAD-PIPELINE-FIRES-2026 drove the WHOLE save-load pipeline (deser ret=1, real char, SetState5, world streams) zero-input via direct game-fn calls -- additional evidence the 'accept input required' framing over-reaches. // STILL VALID (narrow, kept): the SPECIFIC forcing method tested here is genuinely broken -- inner TitleStep state-10 MenuJobWait (handler 0x140b0d400) advances only when the press-any-button job at [owner+0x130] (node update 0x1407ad1c0) reports its bound keycode [job+0x180] pressed via predicate 0x1407a9200 ([job+0x1e8]>=2); the node update writes inputmgr keystate (0x1407ad509 |=1, 0x140744ed2 |=3 at inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0+0x90+keycode). FORCING the post-accept latch 0x143d856a0 (the SetState(11/Finish) transition) DIRECTLY bypasses the job-accept bookkeeping so the front-end/session cascade (CSFeMan 0x143d6b880, 0x1447ef360, InGameStep->STEP_MoveMap) is never built -> CRASH. THAT specific bypass-the-latch method is dead. The Lever-1 keystate-bitmap injection described here is ALSO input (not zero-input) -- correctly flagged. // CORRECTED CONCLUSION: forcing the accept LATCH 0x143d856a0 is the wrong lever (bypasses bootstrap -> crash), but advancing press-any-button itself is achievable ZERO-INPUT via the native registrar (proven). 'Architecturally impossible' is withdrawn. The autoload zero-input question is open at the LOAD/deser layer (see autoload-confirm-fork-verdict-20260623), NOT settled-impossible at the title-accept layer. Relates: FULL-AUTOLOAD-PIPELINE-FIRES, autoload-confirm-fork-verdict-20260623.

COMPREHENSIVE offset-disagreement scan vs upstream fromsoftware-rs eldenring (2026-06-18, autoresearch). Method: compile-time offset_of! asserts of every hand-decoded offset that maps to an upstream NAMED PUBLIC field, across the only 3 upstream-covered structs our code dereferences by raw offset (GameMan, GameDataMan, PlayerGameData). Our code reads ChrIns/PlayerIns/FieldArea via upstream TYPED accessors (no raw offsets), and everything else (InGameStep/MoveMapStep/WorldRes/ResMgr/Block/MsgBox/InputMgr/IODevice/title-flow + GameMan unnamed flags) has no upstream named field to compare against. RESULT: character_name_is_empty (0xe78 -> 0xe70) was the ONLY disagreement (fixed, commit a26bc88). All others AGREE and are now offset_of!-bound: GameMan move_map_target(0x14) save_slot(0xac0) save_requested(0xb72) requested_save_slot_load_index(0xb78) save_state(0xb80); GameDataMan main_player_game_data(0x8); PlayerGameData vigor(0x3c) level(0x68) rune_count(0x6c) rune_memory(0x70) chr_type(0x98). Cannot-audit (upstream unnamed/private, left hand-decoded): GameMan c30/b73/b74/b75/b28/bb8/bbc/bc0/bc4/df0/12d/12e, GameDataMan profile_summary(0x78 private), PlayerGameData character_name(0x9c private). HYPOTHESIS: on the WARM System->Quit->Load reload, the guarded orchestrator 0x82dbf0 (player-map mount 0x82dc1c) is SKIPPED -- likely a guard that treats the map as still-mounted from load1, but System->Quit unmounted it -> archive not re-mounted -> empty read -> WORLD RES WAIT 3/20 park. NOTE load2 is the NATIVE System->Quit->Load path (golden native load DOES reach 0x82dbf0), so on load2 it is reached-but-guarded-out on the warm reload, not architecturally unreachable -> fix = satisfy/bypass the warm-reload guard. AUTORESEARCH FINDING 2026-06-25: runtime product-continue-direct-20260625-164342 with executable-map sampler captured the current pre-BeginTitle crash as a real game access violation at live/deobf `0x14074bbfd` (RVA 0x74bbfd) before `NtTerminateProcess(c0000005)`. Disassembly shows the fault site is in the title/SceneObjProxy Scaleform copy helper: after copying a ScaleformValue with object-bit set (`ecx` from stack+0x50, `shr ecx,6; test cl,1`), it loads objectInterface from `[rsp+0x48]` into rcx, stores it to output+0x10, then faults at `mov rax,[rcx]` before calling vtable+0x8 AddRef. This means the source ScaleformValue advertises object/interface ownership but its objectInterface pointer is invalid at that moment. The crash happens before BeginTitle/title-owner allocation in current runs, so the next safe diagnostic should avoid intercepting/calling this helper broadly and instead sample/guard the producer of the invalid ScaleformValue or add crash logging of rcx/stack value around this native fault. This is stronger than the earlier hypothesis: the broad a48 hook correlation is superseded by direct AV evidence at the native copy/AddRef path.

Static RE: MenuWindow::MenuWindow alone constructs the valid Scaleform bind owner used by native profile/status dialogs without GenericListSelectDialog modal/list behavior. Dump MenuWindow::MenuWindow 0x140741a60 maps to live/deobf 0x140741960. It initializes field553_0x3b8 via CS::SceneObjProxy::SceneObjProxy(param_2) (live call around 0x140741b1d), then immediately writes zero to owner+0x668 (live 0x140741b23, where owner = this+0x3b8). This is the fixed-vector count field that FUN_140748700 reads after FUN_1407452c0 adds +0x60 to the owner; for a valid MenuWindow owner, owner+0x60 is an inline fixed vector and owner+0x668 starts at 0. By contrast, TitleBackViewParts+0x8 MenuResource made FUN_140748700 read MenuResource+0x668 as bogus count and panic. MenuWindow ctor also initializes owner-related SceneObjProxy fields and base MenuWindow job/result fields, assigns MenuTitle/Text_0 and BaseChangeDstSelectDialog helpers if present, sets field_0xa30 from param_3, and touches GLOBAL_CSMenuMan field106_0x90 for the menu id; it does not install GenericListSelectDialog/ProfileSelect modal/list callbacks by itself. Therefore a future bounded diagnostic can try a DLL-owned MenuWindow-only object over a ProfileSelect-like SceneObjProxy/GFX, bind dummy profile symbols through this+0x3b8, and avoid GenericListSelectDialog behavior. Ground teardown/destructor before runtime and capture deeper crash trace evidence if probing.

THE BLOCKER IS THE INPUT DRIVER CADENCE, not SetState5: (1) the driver had a BIG DELAY driving the menu AFTER load2 froze -- my SQ_REPRO_FREEZE_RECOVERY_DEADLINE=1200f(~20s) IS that delay (it waits 20s before advancing to OPEN_MENU for switch #2); (2) after advancing (switch_idx 0->1) the sq-repro menu nav NEVER DROVE FAR ENOUGH to attempt load3 -- deser stuck at 1, menu nav never reached CONFIRM. So load3 was never even attempted. This matches the user's overnight framing: need an input driver whose frequency/cadence is SEMAPHORE-DRIVEN so it reliably navigates the menu from the frozen state to the confirm, fast. FIXES: (a) shrink/remove the freeze-deadline delay (re-drive promptly like the user does by hand); (b) DEBUG why sq-repro menu nav from the frozen load2 state stalls before CONFIRM -- read my-run-debug.log sq-repro state transitions (OPEN_MENU/TO_SYSTEM/TO_PROFILE/TO_SLOT/CONFIRM) to find where it stops; the frozen shell may not open the menu windows the nav gates on. Static RE (2 subagents, 2026-06-15) of the outer SimpleTitleStep state machine, with an UNRESOLVED title-idle-state contradiction needing a runtime read. Global 0x143d71340 IS the outer state table (6 entries stride 0x10, confirmed name SimpleTitleStep STEP_MenuInit at 0x142b62678) -- 0 MenuInit 0xb0a4a0, 1 MenuLoop 0xb0a5e0, 2 IngameInit 0xb0a1f0, 3 IngameStandby 0xb0a430, 4 Ingame 0xb0a1b0, 5 End 0xb0a170. Generic dispatcher 0xb0bd60 calls table[+0x48](obj) each tick and copies +0x4c into +0x48 (+0x48 working, +0x4c next). Base ctor 0xaea9b0 zeroes +0x48/+0x4c (0xaeaa24) so it BOOTS at state 0 and walks forward 0 to 1 to 2 to 3 to 4 via advance helpers 0xb0a980 (inc +0x4c) and 0xb0aa90 (set +0x4c=edx). CONTRADICTION on the title-idle state -- subagent A inferred state 4 STEP_Ingame (from runtime +0x6b0=0, since states 4 and 5 do NOT set [inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 +0x6b0]), but subagent B disasm says the idle is MenuLoop state 1 (title input lane that reads menu list [outer+0x128], polls input, stores selection to [outer+0x130], advances 1 to 2, quit forces 5). Runtime pump_ran(+0x6b0)=0 contradicts BOTH because states 0-3 incl MenuLoop set +0x6b0 -- so the outer may not be ticked at all at idle, or +0x6b0 reflects a different object. UNRESOLVED, must READ the outer step +0x48 at runtime. Find the outer object by its +0x10 == table base 0x143d71340 and its +0xc0 == InGameStep pointer (vtable 0x142b5d408). [outer+0xc0]+0xd8 is LIVE LOAD PROGRESS not a menu gate -- 0 at ctor, 1 after BeginLoad 0xaebdc0 kicks a load, settles when the async loading-task (InGameStep+0x108) is pumped to completion by STEP_ListSelect 0xaf0710. STEP_Ingame state 4 stays while +0xd8 != 0, which is the POST-LOAD tail reached only after a load is kicked, NOT a pre-menu gate. VERDICT -- do NOT clear +0xd8=0 (real load in progress, desync/crash) and do NOT force +0x4c past 4 (bounces to MenuInit). The real lever per [[ingamestep-load-job-needs-perframe-tick]] is to kick BeginLoad with a valid save id and get the InGameStep child step machine TICKED each frame to completion. Related: [[ingamestep-load-job-needs-perframe-tick]] [[title-accept-lever-143d856a0]].

Elden Ring HARD-THROTTLES an UNFOCUSED window to ~6 fps. The runtime probe launches eldenring.exe but does NOT focus it, so when the USER is actively focused on their own windows (Ghidra GUI, terminal, etc.) the ER window is unfocused -> game-task ticks at ~6/s -> title-boot-ready never latches within the 120s cap -> cold_char_mount never runs -> reason=readiness_poll_budget_exhausted, game_task_ticks~754, title_bootstrap_seen=false. SUCCESSFUL (60fps) runs instead reach cold_char_mount fast and tear down early (~927 ticks in ~15s). STEP-3 GUARD-FLIP FIX ATTEMPT 1 FAILED (2026-07-17, DLL 9f7d957a). The fix fired 20 MAP-MOUNT-GUARD-FLIP (map_mount_guard_flip_tick clobbering descriptor at singleton+0x1200, singleton=deref(deref(0x143d5df38)+0x60)) but did NOT force the mount: world never entered (max stable_frames=0), stalled at mms_step=3 WORLD RES WAIT, no crash. ROOT CAUSE of the failed clobber: every flip logged old_id=0x0 old_bits=0x2, and the clobber (write id=0, bits AND= ~0x79) is a NO-OP on this descriptor -- id was already 0, and bit1 (0x2) is not in the 0x79 mask (bits 0,3,4,5,6). So desc never DIFFERS from the controller -> detector 0x14082d5b0 returns unchanged -> mount never enqueued. The descriptor being STATIC id=0/bits=0x2 across all 20 flips => singleton+0x1200 is very likely the WRONG gate (one of 4 detector sites; the subagent did NOT confirm 0x140831f70 is the m28 map-mount gate -- it recommended runtime confirmation which I skipped). Also: to force 'changed' the clobber must make desc DIFFER from the CONTROLLER (not set it to a fixed value that may match); needs the controller state. OBSERVED: during the failed load2, mms_step cycled 1,2,3,18,19,20 then back to 1,2,3 (reached high ~20 then reset to WORLD RES WAIT 3). NEXT: instrument the change-detector 0x14082d5b0 (read-only forwarding hook -- leaf compare fn, lower risk than 0x82dbf0; note 4 sites incl tail-jumps) logging per call rcx=controller (+0x120 id,+0x128,+0x130,+0x131,+0x132,+0x133 bits), rdx=descriptor (+0x08 id,+0x04 bits), return al, tagged in_world -> the m28 gate = al=1 load1 / al=0 load2; then clobber THAT descriptor to a value guaranteed to differ from its controller. RUN-DESIGN worked: fix ran, monitor 10s stall window torn down at 35.9s (though verdict said CENSUS CAPTURED -- census still fired? check: census should be gated off in fix mode; verify). Links step3-THE-GUARD-changedetector-0x14082d5b0-descriptor-clobber-fix-2026-07-17.

RESOLUTION (subagent, 2026-06-15, exhaustive disasm) of the latch=0-vs-unconditional-writer contradiction for 0x143d856a0. COMPLETE WRITER SCAN (new tool .auto/recon_byte_store_scan.py covers C6/0 imm, 88/r reg-store, 00/08/20/28/30 RMW, 80/group1-imm, F6/FE inc/dec/not/neg, REX r8b-r15b; plus recon_ptr_scan=0 8-byte ptrs and no lea/movq addr-taking ref in .text): EXACTLY ONE writer of 0x143d856a0 in the whole image = 0x140c8ff41 (mov byte[0x143d856a0],1) inside boot-intro thread body 0x140c8fe90. All 20 other refs are cmp-byte READERS. NO input-driven writer exists; the latch cannot be written via computed pointer (its address is never loaded into any register). CONTRADICTION RESOLVED: unconditional was true only CONDITIONAL on the movie wait completing. The intro thread spins a wait loop 0x140c8fea0-fef6 calling movie-tick 0x140dea370; it reaches the store 0x140c8ff41 ONLY after movie-tick returns false. The only two loop exits (je 0x140c8fef8 / jne 0x140c8feb0) BOTH fall into the latch, so latch=0 for 1000+ ticks PROVES the thread is parked in the wait loop (movie-tick still true) -- the movie has NOT finished. movie-tick still-playing verdict = (vtable+0x10 of boot movie singleton 0x14458b890 returns nonzero) AND (byte [0x14458b8a5]==0). vtable resolved via ctor 0x140e8d780 -> vtable 0x142bfe088 (3 entries) -> +0x10 = 0x140e90820. DECODE of 0x140e90820 (is-playing/advance): ZERO input refs (grep of full body for inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0/session 0x143d83148/keystate/GetAsyncKeyState = 0 hits). It is a pure Bink/FD4 movie decoder driver: state-diff 0x140e93430, decode-submit 0x140e73b20/c80, OS msg-pump 0x14266df40 (Peek/Translate/DispatchMessage). The skip-flag 0x14458b8a5 has ONE writer 0x140e9117b inside WNDPROC 0x140e91130 set ONLY on WM_CLOSE (uMsg==0x10) when global 0x143d71c48!=0 -- i.e. window-close/AltF4, NOT a press-any-button. The pre-latch call 0x140e90120 PostMessages WM_CLOSE(0x10) to tear down the movie window. boot-init 0x140deb0b0 and movie-tick 0x140dea370 are called ONLY from 0x140c8fe90 (callscan: 1 and 2 sites, all in-thread) -- this is a single boot-time logo/legal-movie sequence owned by one FD4 worker thread, DISTINCT from the inner-TitleStep MenuJobWait press-any-button system. VERDICT: the title-accept latch is NOT input-gated by THIS thread -- it is gated on the boot intro MOVIE finishing. ZERO-INPUT VIABILITY: making the movie report finished natively is structurally available without input: set byte *(u8*)0x14458b8a5=1 (skip-flag) which makes vtable+0x10 (0x140e90820) take its stop branch (call vtable+0xc0 stop, then jmp epilogue) -> movie-tick returns false -> intro thread runs its REAL fade-out (0xc8 + 0x64 loops) + teardown 0x140dea880 + latch 0x140c8ff41 LEGITIMATELY (proper teardown, no orphan, unlike bare latch poke). PRECONDITIONS/CAVEATS needing runtime read: (1) confirm intro thread still ALIVE/parked at the press-any-button wall (latch=0 strongly implies yes); (2) confirm singleton 0x14458b890 non-null and vtable is 0x142bfe088 at runtime; (3) the skip-flag also drives window teardown imports in the WNDPROC path -- verify setting it does not kill the game window prematurely (the is-playing stop branch itself only calls vtable+0xc0 movie-stop, so it may be safe). BIG CAVEAT vs prior memory title-accept-is-fundamentally-input-gated: the INNER TitleStep MenuJobWait advance 10->11 reads the SAME latch, but the SESSION/front-end bootstrap may still need the press-any-button job accept separate from this movie latch. This resolution PROVES the movie gate is NOT input, REOPENING the zero-input path IF the movie-finish latch alone drives the inner MenuJobWait advance + session build. Needs runtime test: set 0x14458b8a5=1 at the wall; observe latch->1, MenuJobWait 10->11, CSFeMan 0x143d6b880/session 0x1447ef360 building without a button. Related: decode-0x140c8fe90-is-intro-thread-not-handoff, title-accept-lever-143d856a0, title-accept-is-fundamentally-input-gated.

STEP 2 + DIAGNOSIS CORRECTION (2026-06-29, run product-continue-direct-133301 + RE workflow). End-to-end autoload fired: force-profile-render kicked the model build at the main menu, captured offscreen gx, forge swapped it (used_portrait=true commits=2) onto MENU_Load now-loading bg. The now-loading screen showed a MAGENTA/YELLOW CHECKERBOARD.

KEY CORRECTION: the checkerboard is NOT the engine missing-texture fallback -- it is OUR OWN placeholder. build_portrait_test_tpf (src/experiments/startup_hooks.rs:2338-2342) builds DdsImage::checker(512,512,32,[255,0,255,255]=magenta,[255,255,0,255]=yellow). So the now-loading INJECTION/DISPLAY PATH IS PROVEN: it puts OUR texture full-screen on the now-loading screen, crash-free (commits=2). 

WHY THE gx-SWAP IS A NO-OP: the forge overwrites ONE pointer (tex_rescap0+0x78 = gx, startup_hooks.rs:2444-2447) and copies no descriptor/format. GFx/Scaleform samples an SRV the engine CACHED from the checker DDS bytes at CreateTpfResCap time -- it never re-reads the live gx pointer. So raw-gx-swap can NEVER work regardless of compositing/format. Proven: used_portrait=false and used_portrait=true render the SAME checker.

THE FIX (user-accepted: a real 3D-render frame via readback is fine since the live-pointer path is impossible): render head in menu phase -> D3D12 readback the committed RT (gx+0x10 GPU resource; if gx is CSOffscreenGxTexture wrapper vtable 0x142b80278 follow [this+0x18] else [this+0x10]; GetDevice; READBACK heap; CopyTextureRegion 256B row align per crates/er-shaderkit/src/render.rs:285; Map; de-swizzle RGBA8) -> DdsImage::from_rgba8(w,h,px) [ADD to crates/er-tpf/src/lib.rs, fields public] -> Tpf::single_pc -> existing CreateTpfResCap (0xb83680, PROVEN) -> DROP the +0x78 swap. Engine rebuilds a correct SRV from our real head bytes (same mechanism that makes the checker display correctly).

H1 (RT may be black at main menu -- composite task FUN_140bba7d0 gated +0x778 && +0x948 only ticks when renderer is DISPLAYED/enrolled in DAT_143d6d8d0 draw graph; force-built renderer is NOT enrolled, PROFILE_OFFSCREEN_DRIVE 0xbb8ca0 disabled) is still possibly true -- the D3D12 readback GetDesc+center-pixel-sum is the decisive oracle AND the pixel source. If black: enroll the renderer into DAT_143d6d8d0 / menu-phase drive 0x140bb8ca0 (NEVER post-Continue, crash) before readback. NEXT/STEP0: build the D3D12 readback helper + oracle_loading_bg_portrait_gx_nonblack (gated off) -- decisive measurement + RAM/pixel semaphore + pixel source. Runtime falsification (run 303, read-only probe, 2026-06-15): the outer SimpleTitleStep menu system is DORMANT at the press-any-button title idle, so the SelectBot selection-injection lane sits BEHIND the title-accept gate, not around it. Evidence: the DLL probe sampled [input_mgr 0x143d6b7b0 + 0x6b0] (the MenuLoop pump 0xb0a5e0 sets this byte to 1 every frame it runs). It stayed 0 across all ticks. Handler 0xb0a4a0 (outer MenuInit) ALSO sets the same byte at 0xb0a4f6, so 0x6b0=0 means NEITHER outer MenuInit NOR MenuLoop ran. The inner TitleStep stays at state 10 MenuJobWait with selection +0x130 never populated (garbage). Therefore injecting the live SelectBot registry [0x143d87360]=0x143b432a8 pre-accept is a NO-OP -- the consuming pump never runs until the title-accept advances the inner TitleStep out of MenuJobWait and spins up the outer menu/SelectBot system. REDIRECTION: the highest-leverage target is the native title-accept / MenuJobWait(state 10) advance with zero input (re-confirms the runs 292-293 'title-accept is the gap' verdict). The SelectBot/menu decode (runs 300-302) characterized everything DOWNSTREAM of accept. NEXT: reverse how inner TitleStep MenuJobWait 0xb0d400 advances (owner+0xe0 menu job / title-accept input node 0x7ad1c0 / input manager 0x143d6b7b0), and whether a bot-activation global starts the outer system earlier. That is why the force-finish fired but did nothing (stepper stayed, world unmoved). The REAL blocker: load2's MoveMapStep MOVE MAP step (18) does not complete the destination-map move, so it never advances to 19/20 and the InGameStep STEP_MoveMap_Update never sets requestCode=2. This is a DIFFERENT child than the testNetStep -- it is the MOVE MAP destination-block load/move (STEP_MoveMap_Update waits FUN_140eb5550(child); the destination BlockId at GameMan+0x14/+0xac8). finalize12a=7 is a substate within this. Static RE (subagent, 2026-06-15) -- the native zero-HID confirm-injection lever for the title-accept. The title-accept input node update 0x7ad1c0 does NOT read the keystate bitmap to decide accept, it WRITES it -- at 0x7ad509 it does or BYTE [inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 + 0x90 + keycode],1 (and 0x744dd0 ORs 3 = pressed+triggered), where keycode = WORD at job+0x180 (sentinel 0xffff = unmapped, guard keycode < 0x47). The accept verdict is one level up -- the press-any-button job at inner-owner+0x130, its result via job+0x1e8 then +0x0 > 1 (predicate 0x7a9200 seta i.e. >=2, companion 0x7a9210 ==2), job state word set only by 0x7a91e0 (mov [rcx],edx). LEVER (recommended, coherent, zero HID) -- on the game/task thread each title frame, OR the confirm keycode bit into the bitmap so the game's own event object -> job GetState -> node predicate -> outer chain run fully coherently. Pseudocode -- inputmgr = *(void**)0x143d6b7b0 (assert non-null), job = *(void**)(inner_owner+0x130), keycode = *(u16*)(job+0x180) (skip if 0xffff or >=0x47), *(u8*)(inputmgr+0x90+keycode) |= 3. CAVEATS -- the bitmap is repolled every frame so must re-inject each frame until accepted, input must be enabled (inputmgr+0x18 via 0x7663c0, MenuJobWait 0xb0d400 does this each frame), and read keycode live from the job not hardcoded. Fallback -- 0x766340(inputmgr, confirmEventId, 1) writes the event array inputmgr+0xdc[id]. Avoid directly setting job state=2 (shallow half-accept). TENSION -- this IS still a form of simulated input (native keystate level, not HID), so it may not satisfy the product goal simulated_button_presses_total=0, but it is the cleanest in-process accept and per [[outer-simpletitlestep-state4-ingame-gate]] subagent C's verdict the title-accept is fundamentally input-driven. Key VAs -- node update 0x7ad1c0, keystate write 0x7ad509/0x744dd0, event writer 0x766340, event getter 0x749960 (table 0x143d6a860), job predicates 0x7a9200/0x7a9210, job state setter 0x7a91e0, job pump 0x7a9600, input enable 0x7663c0. Related: [[title-accept-lever-143d856a0]] [[outer-simpletitlestep-state4-ingame-gate]].

VERIFIED ROOT of the residual color/depth desync (user still saw it AFTER the single-fence coherent readback; run system-quit-repro-selfdrive coherent-readback soak 2026-07-03). Debug log: 'depth-key: SKIP dims-mismatch color=1024x1024 depth=2048x1024 depthlen=2097152'. The COLOR RT is 1024x1024 but the resolved DEPTH is 2048x1024 -- DIFFERENT resources, NOT a matched pair. find_d3d12_resource_ex (gpu_readback.rs:320) for want_depth collects the LARGEST-AREA depth texture in the nest; the foreign 2048x1024 depth (area 2097152) beats the correct 1024x1024 portrait depth sibling (area 1048576). Then compute_depth_mask PINS it (PROFILE_DEPTH_PIN) once it yields a clean gap, so the drifted wrong-size depth 'wins outright' (prefer_v branch, gpu_readback.rs:395) every subsequent frame. When dims mismatch, compute_depth_mask returns None -> LAST_DEPTH_MASK (stale) reused on the new head -> the desync. Early in the run the depth resolved correctly (1024x1024 at +17s via the DSV chain) then drifted to 2048x1024 after switches. THE SINGLE-FENCE COHERENT READBACK (b89bcb7+working tree) did NOT fix this because BOTH the coherent and the old separate paths call the same find_depth_resource -> same wrong-size depth. This is a depth-RESOLUTION bug that predates the coherence work. FIX (implementing): constrain the depth resolution to the COLOR RT's area -- a DEPTH_WANT_AREA atomic that find_d3d12_resource_ex honors when want_depth (only accept area == want, ignoring a wrong-size pin); set it to cw*ch around the coherent depth resolution. ALSO add missing instrumentation: COHERENT_READ_OK vs COHERENT_READ_FALLBACK counters (oracle) because there was no way to prove the coherent path engaged vs silently fell back. Related: [[loading-portrait-coherent-readback-impl-2026-07-03]] [[loading-portrait-4bug-taxonomy-2026-07-03]]. Run also: 9/10 switches, no crash, ledger_violations=0; depth_key_fresh==applied==publish per frame (mask computed+applied every frame, but from the WRONG depth once drifted).

SIGNATURE (Ghidra-symbolized): bool FD4::FD4FileCap::AddDefaultFileLoadProcess(FD4FileCap* cap [rcx], FD4FileLoadProcess* loadProcess [rdx]). Win64 fastcall, 2 args, returns bool.
- arg1/rcx = the FD4FileCap (this).
- arg2/rdx = the cap OWN FD4FileLoadProcess which ALREADY EXISTS at cap+0x78. FD4FileCap struct (len0x90): +0x78 FD4FileLoadProcess* loadProcess, +0x80 LoadTask*, +0x88 loadState (4==complete), +0x90 bytes. We do NOT synthesize arg2 -> arg2 = *(cap+0x78). FD4FileLoadProcess (len0x28): +0x8 fileCap backref, +0x10 allocator, +0x18 fileLoadEventQueue, +0x20 fileLoadProcessor.

The FD4FileLoadProcessor_Default IS BUILT INTERNALLY: HeapAlloc(0x50,8,loadProcess->allocator); set FD4FileLoadProcessor_Default vftable; processor->fileLoadProcess=arg2; FUN_14265b270(processor); AppendFileLoadProcessor(arg2,processor). FUN_14265b270 calls RequestDCX(cap)->RSResourceFileRequest then enqueues via cap-loadProcess fileLoadEventQueue (FUN_1426ea0a0 else / FUN_1426ea4b0 if) which HeapAlloc RSResourceRequestEntry(0x160) and dispatch to GLOBAL_LoadManager (FUN_1401a2790) under mutex. SELF-DISPATCHES IO. => FD4TaskManager::PushTask is NOT needed (PushTask needs a constructed ExecutableTask descriptor; different mechanism, not on this path).

RequestDCX deobf 0x142658a80. PushTask deobf 0x142655d50 (NOT needed). AssignFileCap deobf ~0x1426511c0 = pure bookkeeping, NOT needed.

m28 FD4FileCap ACQUISITION (block 0x1c000000): resmgr=[[MoveMapStep+0xf0]+0x10]; block array at resmgr+0xb3030 stride 8; block=deref(slot); the array entry IS the WorldBlockRes (phase-1 FUN_140615420 reads *(WorldBlockRes+8)=WorldBlockInfo, area at +0xc).
