POST-CONTINUE PORTRAIT -- THE 'RESOLVE' IS ACTUALLY THE MODEL-RENDER TASK WE NEVER DRIVE (2026-06-29, static RE of the Ghidra dump). There is NO separate RT->SRV resolve/copy; the model renders directly into the offscreen's SRV-backed texture via a registered task we don't run.

OFFSCREEN ARCH (CS::CSEzOffscreenRend ctor 0x140bb6c50): offscreen lives at renderer+0xa8 (CSMenuAsmModelRend param_1[0x15]). offscreen+0x8 (field1_0x8 = FUN_140bbe950(HeapAlloc)) is the backing GPU texture; offscreen+0x10 (texResCap) = CSRuntimeTexResCap created by TexRepositoryImp::InsertCSRuntimeTexResCapIfNotExists(GLOBAL_TexRepository, name, offscreen+0x8) -- the SAMPLEABLE SRV GFx reads (our forge binds this, reads BLACK). offscreen+0x48 = a render-target side used by the CLEAR.

CSMenuAsmModelRend ctor (0x140bb8110) creates the offscreen (HeapAlloc 0x78 -> CSEzOffscreenRend) and registers TWO per-frame tasks: FUN_140bba820 (CSEzRabbitTask, ResMan) = MODEL UPDATE (anim/matrices/FaceData on CSChrAsmModelIns at renderer+0xef*8... =+0x778 model); FUN_140bba7d0 (CSEzTask) = MODEL RENDER-TO-OFFSCREEN: if (renderer+0x948 && renderer+0x778) FUN_1409e9ac0(model@+0x778, frame, FUN_1401ad580(renderer+0x948)) -- THIS rasterizes the model into the offscreen SRV-backed texture. ctor also calls FUN_140bb7440(offscreen,...) to size/setup the RT.

KEY CORRECTION: what our code drives as PROFILE_DRAW_STEP (FUN_1409aa3e0 deobf 0x1409aa290 -> per-slot FUN_140bb8d90/PROFILE_OFFSCREEN_DRIVE -> FUN_140bb73a0) is a CLEAR, not a rasterize: FUN_140bb73a0 binds offscreen+0x48's RT and FUN_1419e0e30->FUN_141e8af80(ctx, rt, COLOR=FloatVector4_14329e9b0) clears it to a color (and FUN_141a1adc0 even falls back to the SHARED Gx context RT g_GxDrawContext+0x128+0xf8 when the offscreen's own RT isn't ready). The existing code comment 'rasterize that portrait into its offscreen RT' is WRONG. So post-Continue we CLEAR the offscreen every frame but never run FUN_140bba7d0 (the actual model->offscreen render), so offscreen+0x8/+0x10 SRV stays black (matches boundtex rgb_max=0). The content the rt-readback found (from renderer+0xa8 via BFS, largest TEXTURE2D) is likely offscreen+0x48 / the shared Gx RT, NOT the +0x8 SRV.

FIX TO TRY: per-frame, drive FUN_140bba7d0(renderer, frame_ctx) for our rebuilt renderers (the model render-to-offscreen) instead of / in addition to the clear, so the portrait lands in the +0x8 SRV the forge binds. Open: synthesizing the frame_ctx arg (param_2; *(param_2+8) is a frame index used by FUN_140bba820) -- the menu's CSEzTask scheduler supplies it; check whether the menu task group still ticks these registered tasks post-Continue (if it does, the simpler fix is ensuring the scheduler runs them, not manual drive). Builds on postcontinue-portrait-RT-SRV-resolve-gap-2026-06-29 (which framed it as a resolve gap; this RE shows it is a missing model-render-task drive).

STATIC RE VERDICT (2026-06-17, READ-ONLY objdump on decrypted on-disk eldenring.exe 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, .text VA=foff+0x140000a00; game never launched). Answers the 4 zero-input-Continue crux targets.

=== TARGET 2: ACCEPT BYTE 0x144589bdc WRITER INVENTORY (DEFINITIVE) ===
Scanned ENTIRE image 0x140000a00..0x144800000 for refs to 0x144589bdc: EXACTLY ONE ref in whole binary = the READER 0x140e85f50 (movzx eax,byte[0x144589bdc]; ret). ZERO RIP-relative WRITERS anywhere. The byte is member +0x5c of the FD4 input-acceptance SINGLETON at base 0x144589b80 (vtable 0x142bfc3d8; real method slots at 0x142bfc440+: +0x20=0x140e89770(cmp [rcx+0x40],-1;sete), +0x28=0x140e8a0c0(writes [rcx+0x44]/[rcx+0x40] input state)). Singleton built by magic-static init 0x140e837f0/0x140e838xx (returns &fields 0x144589bd4/bd5). The accept byte is written ONLY through a computed this pointer by the input pipeline (FD4 ACCEPT-pressed-this-frame flag). CLASSIFICATION PROOF: boot/intro thread region 0x140c80000-0x140ca0000 (body 0x140c8fe90 + movie path) has ZERO refs to 0x144589bxx, UNLIKE movie latch 0x143d856a0 which the intro thread writes unconditionally at 0x140c8ff41. So 0x144589bdc is PURE INPUT (case b), NOT a settable intro-latch. Writing it = simulated accept press = BANNED.

=== TARGET 1: REALIZE -- registrar does NOT realize +0x1290 ===
Row vector [dialog+0x1290](begin)..[dialog+0x1298](end) stride 0x210; resolver 0x14078fbd0 verified (count via imul 0xf83e0f83e0f83e1/sar5 = div 0x210; entry=begin+idx*0x210); entry-flag getter 0x140790610. REGISTRAR 0x1409b24e0: mov byte[rcx+0xa40],1; set_state(TextFadeOut@0x142b264f0); builds ~14 FD4 closures and registers them into [dialog+0xa48] via 0x1407ab370 (inner 0x1407aaf00 allocs 0x70-byte node, stores at [registry+0x38]); tail 0x1409b2862+ is pure closure destruction. Registrar NEVER writes +0x1290/+0x1298. The +0x1290 row-block is filled at dialog CONSTRUCTION by bulk 0x60-byte copy 0x140906430-0x140906488 (call [rdi->vt+0x18]; movups [r14+0x1260..+0x12b0]) -- a build-time assign, not a per-frame realize. CONCLUSION: no callable per-frame realize; entries stay DORMANT specs in +0xa48 until SM settles FadeIn->Loop->TextFadeOut. Calling registrar(rcx=dialog) on a settled-Loop dialog is the native realize trigger and reads NO accept byte (non-input path 0x1409aadee proves registrar is reachable input-free).

=== TARGET 3: LOOP-READY PREDICATE = ZERO-INPUT (subagent-verified) ===
update 0x1409aac10 non-input path 0x1409aad83: predicate 0x14075a4d0 -> node=[rax+0x38]; sil=call [node->vt+0x10](node,&inputbyte); if sil -> registrar (NO accept read). OR-fallbacks 0x14078ae50/0x14078ae40 = xor al,al;ret STUBS. Node vtables 0x142aa2938/0x142aa2958 (0x142a99xxx are FD4 reflection type-descriptors NOT vtables); +0x10 op() = 0x140792460/0x1407aa1f0; LEAVES pure status compares 0x1407a9200 (cmp dword[rcx],1;seta) and 0x1407a9210 (cmp dword[rcx],2;sete). pdata-bounded predicate fns ref NEITHER accept byte NOR inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 NOR session 0x143d83148. VERDICT: Loop-ready trips ZERO-INPUT when branch-gate node STATUS [node+0]==2 (anim-complete). Do NOTHING; let update tick FadeIn->Loop on anim completion, native non-input path self-fires registrar.

=== TARGET 4: load_activate VTABLE (subagent-verified, RESOLVED) ===
ProfileLoadDialog REAL primary vtable = 0x142b21bf8 (COL-prefixed; [0]dtor 0x1407342b0 [0x10]update 0x1409a3860 [0x90]0x1409a3480 [0xa0]load_activate 0x1409a4670 [0xa8]RTTI 0x1433270c8). 0x142b229f8 = RTTI base-class-descriptor DATA (sig 0x19930522), NOT a vtable. NOT multiple-inheritance: factory 0x14081ead0 calls ONLY ctor 0x1409a3d90, returns object directly. ctor lea/stores resolve to RTTI data on-disk => image PE-rebased, cross-.rdata VA arithmetic in ctor lea unreliable; runtime capture object[0]==0x142b229f8 is the SAME logical vtable ref resolving to 0x142b21bf8 live (dump-vs-runtime skew, NOT MI). load_activate 0x1409a4670 ABI: rcx=dialog; reads bound [dialog+0xb08]+cursor via 0x140739e20; dispatches mov rax,[rdi];call [rax+0x90]. EXACT TUPLE: (object=factory_return, vtable=[dialog+0]live, slot +0xa0); or call absolute 0x1409a4670 with rcx=dialog. NO sub-object delta. Precond: write [dialog+0xb0c]=row first; PGD assert [0x144588268]!=0.

=== HARD-GATE BOTTOM LINE ===
NO accept-byte gate on the REALIZE: the registrar (realize-trigger) is reachable input-free via the Loop-ready non-input path, and Loop-ready is zero-input (status==2 anim-settle). Accept byte 0x144589bdc only gates the SECOND redundant input branch 0x1409aadf3->0x1409aae17 (same registrar). Zero-input Continue does NOT require defeating 0x144589bdc. Recipe = let native update 0x1409aac10 tick FadeIn->Loop (poll is_in_state(main_sm@dialog+0xa60, Loop@0x142a8f9e8)==true && latch[dialog+0xa40]==0); engine self-fires registrar; then set [dialog+0xb0c]=Load-Game row idx and call load_activate 0x1409a4670(rcx=dialog). If Loop-ready empirically never trips headless, the single gating instruction to satisfy is status-settle [node+0]==2 (anim-complete), NOT the accept byte. Related: titletopdialog-loop-ready-gate-2026, titletop-rowvector-vtable-corrections-static-verified-2026, latch-143d856a0-contradiction-resolved, mainmenu-items-are-titletopdialog-widgets-not-fd4-jobs-2026.

Portrait render-drive native-Windows crash (FUN_141e90290) -- Ghidra dump decompile (2026-07-15, choza /home/choza/ghidra_y22i/proj, dump=deobf+0x20 for 0x140e-0x141e region): CHAIN is engine offscreen submit FUN_141a853a0 -> FUN_141a02de0 -> FUN_1419e3080 -> FUN_141e8b720 -> classifier FUN_141e90290. FUN_141a853a0(param_1,param_2=gxctx,param_3,param_4=4-wrapper-array) GUARDS ONLY 'param_4[0..3] != 0' (four wrappers non-null) then per-wrapper calls FUN_141a02de0(wrapper, gxctx,...). FUN_141a02de0 does FUN_1419e3080(*(gxctx+0x25c8), *(wrapper+0x40)) -- passes the INNER GX resource *(wrapper+0x40). classifier FUN_141e90290(res)=*(res+0x10)&0x3f (a 6-bit resource-state read). CRASH ROOT: the engine's own guard checks the WRAPPER pointer but NOT *(wrapper+0x40) (the inner resource), which is seeded AFTER the wrapper during the async build. So a non-null wrapper with a null/half-seeded inner resource passes the engine guard and faults in the classifier. Our profile_offscreen_gx_resources_ready checks inner+0x40 at time T but the engine can null it between our check and our FUN_141a853a0 call (cross-thread build race) -> insufficient (native still crashed, whole drive gated off). STEP-3 H1 CONFIRMED via sw-bp (2026-07-17, DLL 27582138, er-effects-breakpoints.txt=0x82faf0). The map-mount work 0x14082faf0 fired EXACTLY ONCE total -- on LOAD 1 (autoload) at +85.7s (rcx=r8=rax=0x1f9c53baa78, rdx=0x168edadd50) -- and did NOT fire on LOAD 2 (user drove System-Quit-Load, stalled WORLD RES WAIT 112s; only 1 hit in the whole crash log). So H1 is CONFIRMED and H2 refuted: on the warm reload the player-map mount STEP is NEVER DISPATCHED (not a residency short-circuit at the EBL manager -- that path is never reached). The step machine reaches mms 18-20 believing the map resident, never (re)enqueues the map-mount step, block cap stays empty, resets to WORLD RES WAIT. FIX = re-dispatch the map-mount step on load 2. The mount work 0x82faf0 gets rcx=arg, rdx=ctx (wrapper 0x82dbf0 swaps: 0x82dbf0(rcx=ctx)->0x82faf0(rcx=old_rdx,rdx=old_rcx=ctx)); load1 ctx=0x168edadd50 (a LIVE step/MenuJob object, per RE an InGameStep/MoveMapStep-related object -- address changes per load, cannot hardcode). rcx=r8=0x1f9c53baa78 repeated => likely a manager/this. NEXT: derive the ctx from live load-2 state (InGameStep/MoveMapStep, which the oracle reads as mms=) then CALL 0x82dbf0(ctx) or 0x82faf0 at the load-2 stall (R=deref(0x1448464a8) null), cooldown until R non-null; a CALL not a detour (detour of 0x82dbf0 stalls load1). Read the full load1 sw-bp deep-stack (40 qwords) to map ctx->live-state + the step-machine caller. Links step3-warmreload-mount-decision-H1-stepskip-vs-H2-residency-swbp-0x82faf0-pin-2026-07-17.

PRODUCT REQUIREMENTS (user, 2026-06-15; LOADER UPDATED 2026-07-04) for the autoload deliverable. It MUST be a single DLL (er_effects_rs.dll) delivered through me3 as a [[natives]] ModProfile entry -- the LazyLoader dinput8.dll proxy + lazyLoad.ini chainload was REMOVED 2026-07-04 (user decision after the me3 production smoke passed end-to-end; see me3-loader-works-production-smoke-2026-07-04). Compatibility bar unchanged: offline VANILLA Elden Ring, Seamless Co-op (ERSC, .co2 saves + its own hooks), AND alongside other possibly-unsupported mods. So: be a good citizen, use ROBUST mechanisms (scoped function hooks over fragile fixed-offset memory pokes), do not hardcode save paths/extensions (use the game's own load flow so .sl2 and .co2 both work), and coexist with ERSC/other mods. BEHAVIOR: always-on (no opt-in gate -- users install the DLL knowingly and read docs); auto-load the MOST-RECENT save (native Continue) by default; support an optional ini next to the DLL with a slot override, where if the ini exists but the slot entry is BLANK it falls back to most-recent. APPROACH (chosen): boot-level entry that clears the press-any-button gate via the game's OWN accept (scoped hook forcing the title's accept so the front-end bootstraps natively -- NOT forcing the latch 0x143d856a0 directly, which crashes by skipping the accept bookkeeping), then drives native Continue/load. Release payload: stage-autoload-release.sh ships er_effects_rs.dll + er-effects.me3 (relative-path native, relocatable folder) + flag-file examples; launch = 'me3 launch -g eldenring -p er-effects.me3'. Related: title-accept-input-pipeline-wall, title-owner-located-at-state-10, me3-compat-tool-resolution-blocker-2026-07-04.

REAL CAUSE of load2 stuck at mms=18 (CONFIRMED 2026-07-19, run 204217 debug log): MY OWN defer detour deadlocks it. ingamestep_step_movemap_update_defer_detour (title_tick_cover.rs, hooked on STEP_MoveMap_Update) SKIPS the original while MoveMapStep finalize substate is in [1..=8], 'deferring d8=2/teardown until the advancer posts substate 9'. Debug proof: 'finalize-defer #64: STEP_MoveMap_Update held (finalize=7 in progress, held_frames=64)'. The hook ASSUMES the advancer is pumped elsewhere (comment: 'STEP_MoveMap_Update does NOT pump it') but the advancer NEVER reaches 9 -- it stays at finalize=7 -- so the defer skips the update forever and mms sticks at 18. This is a SELF-INFLICTED DEADLOCK: skipping STEP_MoveMap_Update prevents whatever advances the finalize, so the comment's premise is wrong. LOAD1 (epoch 0, boot) is untouched by the defer (scoped to committed reload epoch) so it advances mms 18->done normally; LOAD2 (epoch 1) gets the defer and deadlocks. Also a sibling gate hook movemapstep_step_move_map_gate (0xaf7cf0, 'reload hold'). FIX: disable/remove the defer detour + gate hold so load2's STEP_MoveMap_Update runs like load1's -> sets requestCode=2 -> advances -> readiness. This was one of the wrong-mechanism holds flagged earlier that stayed default-on. NOT a disk issue.

3RD-LOAD SOFTLOCK FULLY ROOT-CAUSED + FIX ATTEMPTED (runtime + Ghidra, 2026-07-16). RUNTIME (SWITCH-ORACLE, live user 3-load test): the 3rd load's MoveMapStep child freezes at internal step 18 = STEP_MoveMap with b7c1=1 (streaming ENABLED) and blocks=33 (world fully streamed) and bc4=1. So world streaming + session bring-up SUCCEED; it stalls at the FINAL fade/finalize step. This FALSIFIES all prior streaming/session/MoveMapStep-not-registered hypotheses. AUTHORITATIVE MoveMapStep step table (dump registrar FUN_1400a40c0 -> StepperFn_ARRAY_143d70860): 0 BeginInit,1 MsbLoad,2 MsbLoadWait,3 WorldResWait,4 CurrentLodBlockWait,5 LeaveSessionWait,6 SignIn,7 SignInWaitAndLogIn,8 WaitChrTypeSyncReady,9 CreateDrawPlan,10 InitAnim,11 FixedGridInitPos,12 EscapeDeathLoop,13-15 HitStabilizeWait(x3),16 TexStabilizeWait,17 HorseWait,18 STEP_MoveMap,19 Cleanup,20 Finish. ROOT (STEP_MoveMap decompile 0x140af7de0): it sets advance-gate field_0x4b8=1 each frame then blockers knock it down; advance to Cleanup requires field_0x4b8 low byte != 0. Blocker FUN_140679010() = returns GLOBAL_GameMan->field143_0xbc4 (== bc4!); when bc4 != 0 the gate is cleared -> STEP_MoveMap loops forever. (Other blocker FUN_140508d30 = WorldChrMan->field47_0x1e524==2.) So bc4=1 (the return-title predicate we set to tear down the OLD char) is LEFT STALE and BLOCKS the NEW char's finalize. This UNIFIES the whole 'bc4 frozen at 1' investigation: bc4=1 is not just a symptom, it is the gate that blocks STEP_MoveMap. Loads 1/2 have bc4 drained by the time their child reaches step 18; the fast in-process teardown skips the native bc4 drain and by load 3 it is stale (user's own theory: stale element the semi-normal teardown should clean up; likely a Windows + multi-hook race accumulating over loads). FIX ATTEMPTED (title_tick_cover.rs SWITCH-ORACLE block, DLL sha 9ee5af26): when bc4==1 && ig_d8==1 && mms_step==18 && b7c1==1 && blocks>0 held past SYSTEM_QUIT_LOAD3_STALL_RELEASE_FRAMES(120, loads 1/2 never reach), clear GameMan+0xbc4=0 ONCE so STEP_MoveMap advances 18->19->20, requestCode drains 1->0, world enters. Passive GameMan gate flag write (like b78/disableSaveMenu clears), NOT a live step index (Edit A crashed doing that). Semaphore: SYSTEM_QUIT_LOAD3_FINALIZE_CLEAR_COUNT>0 + 'LOAD-3 FINALIZE FIX' log + MMS-FINISH fires + SWITCH-ORACLE LOADED_STABLE. Also landed: bar label now 'NAME i/N' (MOVE MAP 18/20) so the frozen step is visible; SWITCH-ORACLE logs mms_step(NAME)/gate/b7c1/blocks; MMS-INIT/FINISH edge hooks. IF this clear does NOT unblock (field_0x4b8 gate still 0 after), the WorldChrMan blocker (0x1e524==2) is also active -> handle that too. NEXT-LEVEL GOAL (user): use this step-granular map to make character switching FASTER (skip/parallelize steps for same-session swaps across save files).

USER WANTS A BOOTUP-SEQUENCE DIVERGENCE SEMAPHORE (2026-07-20, goal core): the user expected a semaphore that COMPARES load2 bootup sequence to load1 baseline and TEARS DOWN when load2 diverges from load1 -- but only for the SUBSET/stages where they are EXPECTED to overlap. This is the goal core ("determine exact differences in bootup sequence for every evolving semaphore; ensure load3 does not diverge from load1/load2 at overlap stages"). I have been comparing MANUALLY, not via an automated semaphore. BUILD IT in scripts/capture-samechar-3x.py: (1) record load1s ordered bootup milestone sequence (mms 1->3->13->16->17->18->19->20->-1, finalize 0->5->9, req_code 1->2->0, can_move ramp, sysstep, play_time_live) with the frame/time each reached; (2) during load2, at each shared milestone, verify load2 reaches it in the SAME ORDER; the KEY known divergence: load1 advances mms 18->19->20->-1 (SETTLES) but load2 stays at mms=18 -- so if load2 sits at mms=18 beyond a window comparable to load1s 18->19 transition, flag DIVERGENCE at that exact stage and tear down with the milestone where load2 stopped matching load1. Only compare the OVERLAP subset (both loads go through the MoveMapStep 1->18; the finalize/settle is where load2 diverges). Extend to load3 (must not diverge from load1/load2 at overlap stages). This gives fast, precise, deterministic teardown at the exact divergence point.

RUNTIME (2026-06-16, Proton Experimental + ntsync). Hardware DATA write-watchpoints via SetThreadContext debug registers DO NOT WORK under Proton/Wine. The c30 watchpoint (DR0=&GameMan+0xc30, DR7=0xd0001 4-byte write, set on all 77-85 game threads via Suspend/Get/SetThreadContext, re-armed every 64 frames; VEH handles EXCEPTION_SINGLE_STEP + DR6 bit0) armed successfully (SetThreadContext returned TRUE -> 'c30-watch (re)armed on 85 threads' logged every 64 frames incl. across the mount window tick783/847) BUT the #DB never fired: ZERO 'c30-write' hits even though observe confirms c30 DID change 0xa010000->0x3c332700 + ac0->0 at the same time (the .co2 load completed in-world). So Wine accepts the debug-register write but does not program the CPU DR / does not deliver the data-breakpoint trap in-process. CONCLUSION: cannot use hardware watchpoints for RE under Proton. Code is gated off by default (ER_EFFECTS_C30_WATCH / er-effects-c30-watch.txt), harmless, left in place (commit 832d0c4). Page-guard/PAGE_READONLY alternative is impractical for GameMan (hot struct, thousands of writes/frame to the same 4KB page). PIVOT: function-hook the vanilla c30 writer 0x67bd70 (the SOLE c30 writer per slot-saved-map-c30-via-full-deserialize-2026, 3 callers) with a caller stack -- if it fires under Seamless its caller reveals the deserializer; if not, ERSC writes c30 from its own module (ersc.dll) and we must hook there or just drive the native load flow. Related: seamless-co2-load-bypasses-vanilla-deserialize-2026, b80-mount-logging-hooks-and-capture-procedure-2026.

BREAKTHROUGH (2026-06-21): captured the REAL Continue->load call-chain LIVE under Wine via the INT3 sw-bp engine (HW DR0 watchpoint confirmed dead under Proton -- c30-watch re-armed 20+x, ZERO hits, even on a real load). User drove Continue with the 45-Slots real save in the active 76561197986456766 profile. anti-antidebug patched 330 .text sites; 5 sw-bps armed+fired. RVAs (caller stacks bottom-up; frames #0-#3 = our DLL trampoline 0x6ffe..., #4 = the bp, #5+ = real return addrs):

CHAIN (the answer to 'what calls b80/the load when I press Continue'):
1. FULL-LOAD INITIATOR 0x67b1a0 (slot 0 -> b80=2, starts async read): callers #5=0x82a178 #6=0x745543 #7=0x7aa2bb (FD4 menu-job pump/iterator). rcx=0 (slot 0).
2. SELECTOR BUILDER 0x826510: callers #5=0x9ac9e6 #6=0x9a9136 #7=0x9a7038 (ProfileLoadDialog load_activate). r9=0x7fffa1b77148 = owner+0x138, rdx=0x7ffe96a3b390 = dialog+0x50. hit twice.
3. MENU DESERIALIZE 0x82c240 (pumped 3x): callers #5=0x829bd7 (FD4 delegate _Do_call glue) #6=0x82a25c #7=0x745543 (menu pump). rdx=r8=0x7ffe9e4f4010 = selector-step ctx; r9 first call=0x140e6f578 (io subsys).
4. C30 WRITER 0x67bd70 (the load COMMIT): hit0 PREVIEW r8=0x215e01 caller #5=0x67e21a (slot-record/preview deser 0x67e150) #6=0x82f9d9 #7=0x74684f; hit1 REAL MOUNT r8=0x280000 (full save) caller #5=0x82c38b (INSIDE menu_deser 0x82c240) #6=0x829bd7 #7=0x82a25c. rcx=GameMan 0x7fffa05fd520, rdx=full save buffer, writes GameMan+0xc30=real map + applies char.

DECISIVE INSIGHT: the load runs through menu_deser 0x82c240, DRIVEN EACH FRAME BY THE FD4 MENU-TASK DELEGATE (0x82a25c -> 0x829bd7 -> 0x82c240), and the c30/char commit (0x67bd70, full 0x280000) is INSIDE menu_deser. This is WHY the synthetic inline primitive calls hung/crashed (b80-load-builder-DEAD-END): menu_deser is pumped by the engine's async FD4 step delegate, NOT a synchronous call. The orchestrator that calls BOTH the initiator 0x67b1a0 and pumps the selector is the 0x82axxx menu-job orchestrator (0x82a178/0x82a25c = the 0x14082a6a0 family); the FD4 pump is 0x745543/0x7aa2bb.

TO REPLICATE MENU-FREE ZERO-INPUT: build the selector step (0x826510 with owner+0x138/dialog+0x50/slot) and REGISTER its delegate so the FD4 menu task pumps menu_deser 0x82c240 across frames (NOT call it inline) -> 0x67bd70 commits. This matches selector-menu_deser-runs-but-stuck-pending-no-submit; now we have the EXACT live caller chain + args. Tool: er-effects-breakpoints.txt (one hex RVA/line) + offline launcher; works under Wine (HW watch does not). Relates: working-inprocess-breakpoint-engine-via-ported-antiantidebug-2026, wine-proton-no-hardware-data-breakpoints-2026, seamless-co2-menu-deserialize-is-vanilla-0x82c240-2026, b80-load-builder-DEAD-END-confirmed-2026-06-21.

AUTORESEARCH FINDING 2026-06-25: `FUN_140d82880` is the generic Scaleform value extraction helper behind the title a48 SceneObjProxy source. Dump 0x140d82880 maps content-unique to live/deobf 0x140d827d0 (shift -0xb0); do not hook live 0x140d82880 without re-verification. Decompile: if `(byte[src+0x20] & 0x8f) == 10` (source GFx value is object-like), it initializes a temporary `CSScaleformValue` sentinel from `DAT_1429e7cf0` (`-1,-1`, followed by unrelated adjacent string `CSStepTask<CSModelViewerInsStep...`), calls the source object's `objectInterface` vfunc `+0xd0` with source value `[src+0x28]` into that temp, then copies the result unless result datatype masked equals 1. Else it returns an empty CSScaleformValue. Therefore the native-title readiness blocker has narrowed to either the source `CSScaleformValue` at title a48+0x28 not being object datatype 10, or the objectInterface +0xd0 extraction returning empty/type 1/zero. Next runtime-safe instrumentation should be narrowly gated to the title a48 object and hook live 0x140d827d0 or caller live 0x14074bbe0, logging source datatype `[src+0x20]&0x8f`, source objectInterface `[src+0x18]`, source value `[src+0x28]`, and output datatype after return. Do not treat `DAT_1429e7cf0` as a member-name string and do not use broad ungated hooks because FUN_140d82880 is generic.

FIX TARGET for the b80 cold full-read wall (2026-06-21, static RE agents E+F on eldenring-deobf.bin). Refines b80-fullread-ROOTCAUSE-iodev-unbound-no-file-handle-cold. The cold async read completes empty because the SAVE IO DEVICE (iodev) is NOT MOUNTED/OPENED. Precise levers:

(1) iodev+0x40 IS THE UNIVERSAL DEVICE-READY/BOUND FLAG (agent E, decisive). Router 0x140e6eb80: cmpb [iodev+0x40],0; jne 0x140e6f430 (BOUND branch = real inline seek+read, WORKS); else jmp 0x140e6f5b0 (COLD branch = async-enqueue to worker, NO real IO, empty). The sibling router 0x140e6ec70 gates another save-IO cmd the same way (cmpb [r9+0x40]). So [iodev+0x40]!=0 is REQUIRED for any real save read/write. iodev ctor 0x140e6d970 zero-inits: byte[iodev+0x40]=0 (@0x140e6da09), [iodev+0x30]=0xffffffff (OS-handle slot, invalid sentinel, @0x140e6d9fb), [iodev+0xb0]=0, builds +0x50 subobj via 0x14251c2e0. iodev = *(0x144589390) via getter 0x140e6e060; lazily built by accessor 0x140e6f700 (double-checked lock) -> ctor 0x140e6d970.

(2) THE CONTINUE HANDLER = 0x140b0e180 (agent E: the ONLY direct caller of peek 0x140678a50, at 0x140b0e19d). Post-peek it only stages container state + posts a transition event (string-key 0x59682f01 via 0x140b0d960); it does NOT open the file or bind the iodev itself. None of the 27 callers of getter 0x140e6e060 write iodev+0x40. The iodev+0x40 write + OS-handle open live on the DEVICE-MANAGER init/open path (iodev-manager singleton 0x144589578, lazily built by router-setup 0x140e6ddb0 called ~0x140e6ebc0) that the title->Continue boot flow runs and the cold/menu-free path SKIPS. (Note 0x140b0e180 is the same VA the memories loosely call continue_confirm/SetState5 -- coherent: it is the Continue event-step handler.)

(3) RED-HERRING CORRECTION (agent F): the worker 'readiness' I hypothesized at [loader+0x38] is NOT the save reader. 0x1404807b0 is a THUNK -> 0x1404829e0; the +0x38-gated fn is 0x1404807e0 (prologue), cmpb @0x140480802 -- but its 'this' is a deep WORLD/MAP streaming object (only caller 0x1403c8e9d: rsi=*(*(*(parent+0x10)+0x190)+0x88)), and the body does NO CreateFile/ReadFile -- it is MAP/WORLD ASSET STREAMING conflated with the loader. Do NOT pursue +0x38. The save read uses: worker singleton *0x144852f88 (job posted via 0x14240e020), job ctor 0x142410a30 (vtable 0x1431fc470 4 slots; iodev->job+0xe0; path wstr->job+0x478 via 0x14240ec70; slot3 0x142410c60 builds path from iodev; save path fmts '%s\%s%s%s'/'.bak' right after vtable). The actual CreateFileW/ReadFile that flips iodev+0x30 from -1 to a real handle is in the worker job execution -- trace fwd from worker post 0x14240e020 / scan for the writer of a non-(-1) value to iodev+0x30.

NEXT (in-flight): find the device OPEN/MOUNT routine that sets iodev+0x40!=0 and opens the OS handle (iodev+0x30), reached via manager 0x144589578 / ctor 0x140e6ddb0; determine if directly callable from our DLL to bind the save device before the cold full-read submit. THEN the existing cold_char_mount submit (0x67b1a0) routes through the BOUND branch and reads Banon for real. Relates: b80-fullread-ROOTCAUSE-iodev-unbound-no-file-handle-cold-2026-06-21, save-io-worker-vs-streaming-0x1404807e0.

WALL (2026-06-15, runtime). The press-any-button accept is gated by a nested CSEzCondition tree AND a timing-sensitive input pipeline that inputmgr-level writes do not reach. Confirmed: owner reliably at committed state 10 (validated scan). J=*(owner+0x130) is an AND-combiner (vtable 0x2aa2958); its 2 children are sub-combiners (0x2aa2938 upd 0x792460, 0x2aa2c38 upd 0x7931e0) with DIFFERENT child layouts, so a bounded DFS (children@+0x18,count@+0x60) over 256 nodes did NOT reach the leaf input node (vtable 0x2aa97e8, upd 0x1407ad1c0). Tried two zero-input injections at state 10, both ran clean (no crash) but did NOT accept (latch 0x143d856a0 stayed 0, CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 stayed null): (1) keystate-bitmap shotgun inputmgr+0x90[0..0x47] |= 3; (2) event-array shotgun inputmgr+0xdc[0..0x15e] = 1. The leaf is the keystate PRODUCER (it writes inputmgr+0x90 after detecting a press), so the real detection is an input EVENT read from a per-frame POLLED SNAPSHOT taken before our FrameBegin write -> our writes are the wrong stage/timing. CONCLUSION: zero-HID-input title-accept needs injecting at the exact input-poll/snapshot stage the leaf reads (located via more RE of 0x1407ad1c0's read side + the poll fn), OR a real input event (PostMessage/SendInput to the window = HID-level, the path the user constrained against). The architecture fundamentally requires an input to bootstrap the front-end/session. Reliable owner-at-state-10 is solid (see title-owner-located-at-state-10). Related: title-accept-is-fundamentally-input-gated, title-accept-job130-is-and-combiner-not-leaf.

STEP-3 THE GUARD FOUND -- warm-reload map-mount skip (2026-07-17, guard-RE subagent, deobf). The map m28 mount runs as a std::function/CS MenuJob callback chain (_Do_call 0x140836f30 -> 0x14082e230 -> 0x14082eb60 -> wrapper 0x14082dbf0 -> mount work 0x14082faf0), ALL UNCONDITIONAL (0x82faf0 has NO internal guard, single caller 0x14082dc17). The ENQUEUE decision is the guard: job body 0x140831f70 at branch 0x140831faf calls change-detector 0x14082d5b0, test al, jne WORK 0x140826740 which builds the load/mount continuation, else SKIP 0x1407a7250 a no-op. Detector 0x14082d5b0 rcx=controller rdx=descriptor compares controller authoritative load-phase state (controller+0x120 u64 id, +0x128 bit0, +0x130 bit3, +0x131 bit4, +0x132 bit5, +0x133 bit6) to a self-updating cached descriptor mirror (desc+0x08 u64, desc+0x04 bits): ALL EQUAL -> al=0 at 0x14082d617 SKIP; ANY DIFFER -> writes controller state back into desc and al=1 at 0x14082d685 RUN. Warm reload into same map: controller flags equal descriptor (System-Quit resets neither) -> al=0 -> mount SKIPPED -> block FD4FileCap +0x88=4 but +0x90 NULL -> WORLD RES WAIT. descriptor singleton getter 0x140256360 = deref(deref(0x143d5df38)+0x60), descriptor at singleton+0x1200. FIX one-shot DATA write NO 0x82dbf0 detour: desc = deref(deref(0x143d5df38)+0x60)+0x1200; write u64 at desc+0x08 = 0 and u32 at desc+0x04 AND= ~0x79 (clear bits 0,3,4,5,6) -> forces one changed -> WORK mount enqueued -> mount+BIND -> cap+0x90 populated. Detector re-syncs desc on the changed path so ONE clobber = ONE extra mount (self-limiting). TIMING: guard job runs during load-2 session establishment; clobber while load-2 in progress AND registry R=deref(0x1448464a8) still null, re-clobber with cooldown until R non-null, bounded. 4 detector sites 0x140831fa5 + tailjumps 0x140836d89 0x140836ea9 0x1408374c9; m28 gate = the one al=1 load1 / al=0 load2 with +0x120 = m28 id. RUNTIME TEST of bc4-force fix (DLL 504a7ce9, 2026-07-16) -- bc4 was a RED HERRING for the switch stall; real root pinpointed. My fix FIRED correctly: 'forced return-title bc4 1->READY(3)' + functor submitted at bc4=0x3 (the final functor fired for the FIRST TIME ever -- before, bc4 never reached 3 because no save; this is real progress toward no-save switching). BUT it still stalled at MOVE MAP 18/20. DECISIVE LOG EVIDENCE (switch 2, slot 1, the 3rd consecutive load): (1) player=true THROUGHOUT -- the old world (char B, loaded by switch 1) NEVER tears down. Contrast switch 1: player went true->FALSE at +79164ms (old boot char A tore down cleanly) then char B loaded = WORKED. (2) The bc4 +0x4b8 advance gate was ALREADY READY (1/0) at bc4=0 BEFORE I forced bc4, and the child STILL didn't advance -- so bc4/gate is NOT the blocker. Forcing bc4=3 actually pushed gate to 0/0 (hurt it). (3) The MoveMapStep child (0x216580b5080) step-18 handler IS ticking (cd100/field17_0x100 decremented 3->0), but its advance-to-Cleanup(19) condition never becomes true: done50=0, hold270=0, next=18 forever. (4) ig_d8 stuck at 1 (never reaches stable 2) -- InGameStep parent step7 never completes the load handoff. (5) ONLY ONE MMS-INIT all run (+83928 for char B); no new MoveMapStep created for switch 2. (6) NO load ever reached LOADED_STABLE / ig_d8=2 sustained (max stable_frames=0). ROOT: our IN-PROCESS SWITCH loads (feed+continue_confirm+SetState5) never COMPLETE the InGameStep->MoveMapStep handoff -- the world streams and is playable (player=true) but the step machine never 'finishes' (child stuck at STEP_MoveMap 18, ig_d8 stuck 1). Each switched world is a HALF-LOADED LIMBO. Switch 1 works because it tears down the STABLE boot char A; switch 2 fails tearing down the never-finished char B. So the fix must make STEP_MoveMap(18) ADVANCE to Cleanup->Finish->-1 on OUR switch loads. STEP_MoveMap decompile 0x140af7de0 (544 lines) reads CSFade channels 2/3, CSSessionManager, CSNowLoadingHelper, fade hold-timer field307_0x270 -- the advance-to-19 condition is a fade/session/load-complete gate buried in the rest of that handler (only ~200 lines seen). CODE STATE (DLL f277f570): kept bc4=READY-at-REQUEST (system_quit_force_return_title_bc4_ready, fires functor + suppresses save), simplified the finalize to 'restore bc4->0 once functor fired' (removed the buggy step-18 predicate that fired on the resident world). USER CONTEXT: feature 'worked end to end on main but only on Linux/Wine, no non-determinism' -- so this may be a native-Windows-specific step-machine-completion timing issue main never faced (main was Wine-only), OR a branch regression in the load-completion path. NEXT: RE the exact STEP_MoveMap 18->19 advance condition (rest of 0x140af7de0) + instrument its inputs; compare the boot load (finishes, char A) vs our switch load (never finishes, char B) to find what our feed/SetState5 path leaves unmet. Updates save-disabled-switch-completion-bc4-force-ready-2026-07-16.

2ND RUNTIME-ACCUM RE-LOAD LOCK: ROOT PROVEN + FIX APPLIED (2026-07-16, DLL 535d4f93). RUNTIME COMPARISON (same-log, instrumented) proved the sole differentiator between a child that ADVANCES 18->19->20 and the stuck one: menuData+0x5d (return-title byte, rt5d). Advancing children have rt5d 1; the stuck child has rt5d 0 with ALL cVar10 ending-request inputs 0 (end5e 0, force 0, GameMan 0xb7c 0, 0xb7d 0, warpRequested@0x10 0). MECHANISM: InGameStep parent (step7) completes only when the MoveMapStep child reaches its -1 terminal (finished-check FUN_140af5190 = MoveMapStep+0x48 equals -1). STEP_MoveMap walks the child toward -1 only while cVar10 (ending request, written to menuData+0x5e by MoveMapStep::CheckReturnToTitle FUN_140afa7c0) is set. cVar10 inputs: menuData+0x5d, warpRequested, CSSessionManager WaitReload, deadReset==2, force 0x3d856a0, GameMan 0xb7c/0xb7d, pad. OUR OWN continue_confirm (system_quit_repro_guards.rs:1352 clears menuData+0x5d, :1372 clears warp_requested) does this for BOUNCE-PREVENTION (the comment at :1361 names FUN_140afa7c0 as CheckReturnToTitle: warp_requested/+0x5d left set bounces the freshly-loaded RESIDENT world back to title ~4s later). TENSION: the same flag that COMPLETES the load (walk child to -1) also BOUNCES the resident world. Clearing it stops the bounce but on a slower re-load strands the incoming child at step 18 = the lock. FIX (title_tick_cover.rs SWITCH-ORACLE, ENDING-REQUEST RECOVERY): when the exact stuck signature (ig_d8 1, mms_step 18, end5e 0, rt5d 0, b7c1 1, blocks>0) holds past ENDING_REQUEST_STALL_RELEASE_FRAMES(120, working loads advance first), SET menuData+0x5d=1 so the child walks 18->Cleanup->Finish->-1; then CLEAR it (ENDING_REQUEST_SET latch) the frame mms_step leaves 18 -- before the resident-world bounce window. Semaphore: ENDING_REQUEST_SET_COUNT>0 + ENDING-REQUEST RECOVERY log SET then clear + MMS-FINISH + LOADED_STABLE + world enters, and NO bounce (world stays up). This is the 2nd stale-teardown-timing flag (bc4 was the 1st); both are our fast-switch teardown clearing a flag a later load still needs. NEXT: user reproes the lock sequence (load several saves, then re-load the one that catches) to confirm the child now walks past 18 and the world enters without bouncing. Links: multi-char-load-FIXED-bc4-clear-2026-07-16, reload-lock-ending-request-model-grounded-2026-07-16.

PERSISTENT INSTRUCTION (user 2026-07-17): recognizing that a runtime run wasted time OBLIGATES fixing the RUN DESIGN before re-running -- not just re-launching. A probe that fires on the wrong event, crashes, or captures nothing decisive is a run-design DEFECT to fix, not to repeat. Before EVERY runtime run: (1) gate one-shot dumps on the CORRECT condition so they fire on the intended event (e.g. gate the load-2 CAPSTATE on IN_WORLD_REACHED==YES so it does NOT latch on load-1's healthy 2,4,0 transient -- the run8 defect); (2) capture as MUCH decisive state per run as possible so one run answers the question (avoid serial single-datum runs); (3) detect known failure modes early (crash/access-violation) and bail fast; (4) tear down the MOMENT sufficient evidence is captured (semaphore-progress teardown, ~1s after the decisive semaphore). When I notice time was wasted, immediately: record the fix as a persistent instruction AND apply it to the next run design before launching. This is the wood-shop-saw principle applied to experiment design: the mechanism (probe + teardown) must be correct-by-construction, not fixed reactively after wasting the user's drive.

ROOT CAUSE of the post-switch clean-title reload crash (er-effects-rs-3pc), static RE 2026-07-02. This is H1 (residual singleton), NOT H2 (phase hooks). Phase-reset-to-IDLE ALONE will NOT fix it. DEFINITIVE static RE (2026-06-15, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full disasm) -- play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0 decoded + the EXACT title Continue/Load consumer + the SAVE-SLOT-N load recipe for the live FE-host InGameStep child.

=== play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0 SIGNATURE + BODY ===
Fastcall(rcx=child InGameStep, rdx=descriptor*, r8d=arg3(passed -1 by all title callers), r9=mapid-list* {begin@+0x8,end@+0x10}). Prologue stores r8d to [rsp+0x18] (shadow). rdi=rdx(descriptor), rsi=rcx(child), rbp=r9(list).
GATE @0x140aebe23: ebx=-1; cmp [rdi(descriptor)],ebx; JE return. => descriptor dword0 = a COMMAND ID and must be != -1 or the whole submit no-ops.
@0x140aebe2b: mov [rsi(child)+0xd8],1  (phase gate = request-submitted).
@0x140aebe3f-4a: copy [descriptor] to local [rsp+0x68]; call 0x14067aec0 (validate; reads GameMan 0x143d69918 + 0x143d68448, sets GameMan+0xbcc/+0xbcd/+0xbce status bytes).
@0x140aebe59-69: copy [descriptor] to local; call 0x14067abd0(rcx=&out[rsp+0x20], rdx=&local) = the GameFlow resolver: copies cmd, gate [GameMan+0xb28]==0 AND [in]!=-1 AND high-byte(byte[out+3]) in 0x32..0x58 (decimal map-area 50..88) -> resolves via 0x1406783d0 -> finally writes [GameMan+0x14]=resolved cmd (this is the GameFlow command write).
@0x140aebe72: mov [child+0x100], ecx (resolved cmd low). @0x140aebe78-82: [child+0x104] = descriptor dword@+8 if >-1 else -1.
@0x140aebe88-9a: dl=(cmd>>16)&0xff, cl=(cmd>>24)=map-area byte; call 0x140720210 (cmp area,0x59 setb = area<=0x58); if true call 0x140aea780(rcx=child+0x108, rdx=[0x143b38958]) (publishes GameFlow cmd).
@0x140aebeaf-f78: appends each u32 in the r9 {begin,end} list to child's request vector at child+0xb35f0 (push_back loop). ALL title callers pass an EMPTY list (see below) so this loop is a no-op; the map comes solely from the descriptor cmd.
NET WRITES: child+0xd8=1, child+0x100=resolved map cmd, child+0x104, child+0x108 publish, GameMan(0x143d69918)+0x14=GameFlow cmd, appends list to child+0xb35f0. It is a REQUEST WRITER, runs nothing (matches ingamestep-load-job-needs-perframe-tick: +0xd8 is a 0/1/2 phase gate, drained only by the per-frame InGameStep step pump).

=== THE DESCRIPTOR COMMAND ID = A PACKED MAP ID ===
Map-string resolver 0x14071fd60 packs 'mAA_BB_CC_DD' decimal digits into u32: byte3=AA, byte2=BB, byte1=CC, byte0=DD. New-game default string 'm60_42_34_00' (0x142b62c70) -> 0x3C2A2200 (60,42,34,0 decimal). So descriptor cmd IS the packed map id; new-game = packed default map, load = the SAVE's map id.

=== TITLE CALLERS OF play_game_submit (5 sites; callscan) ===
0x140b0a3ec STEP_IngameInit(outer), 0x140b0d6df STEP_PlayGame(idx5), 0x140aecba7 STEP_MoveMap_Update(child re-submit), 0x140af096a + 0x140af1216 (in-game MoveMapListStep load menu STEP_ListSelect/STEP_LoadSaveData).
STEP_PlayGame 0x140b0d5b0: builds empty mapid-list via 0x140aea590 (just an empty vector obj {begin=end=0}); descriptor dword0 = [owner+0xbc]; rcx=[owner+0x2e8](child); r8d=-1; r9=&empty-list; call submit. => owner+0xbc holds the map cmd consumed by PlayGame.
STEP_IngameInit 0x140b0a1f0: ebx=[owner+0x130](selection); if ==-1 -> uses default map 'm60_42_34_00' resolved (new game); else ebx=selection; descriptor dword0=ebx; child=[owner+0xc0]; empty list; submit.

=== *** THE EXACT CONTINUE/LOAD SELECTION CONSUMER *** ===
Title menu items built in STEP_InitMenu 0x140b0cfa0 with callback vtables 0x142b63ea8(Continue) / 0x142b63e70(LoadGame) etc (bound to owner=r14). The menu-job multicast at owner+0xe0 is pumped per-frame by 0x140733f20 (generic delegate invoker: iterates [list+0x48] entries, calls each entry vtable+0x10 with r8=&resultflag, xmm1=frametime).
The ACTIVATE/confirm handler for BOTH Continue and LoadGame is 0x140b0e180 (rcx=callback obj; owner=[rcx+0x8]):
  cmp [owner+0x284],1  -> if ==1 (NEW GAME): edx=4; SetState(owner,4=BeginNewGame).
  else (CONTINUE/LOAD-SAVE): call 0x140678a50 (slot-mgr peek: reads slot container 0x143d5df38+0x8, sets GameMan+0xe70=1); call 0x140679560(&local) which does: mov edx,[GameMan(0x143d69918)+0xc30]; [out]=edx  => out = the SAVE'S deserialized map id at GameMan+0xc30; then [owner+0xbc] = that map id; edx=5; SetState(owner,5=PlayGame); then 0x14082cd60(owner+0x138,...) stores slot detail; 0x140ca7660(owner+0x300, byte[result]).
So the LOAD path: handler sets owner+0xbc = [GameMan+0xc30] (saved map id) and SetState(5). The title's per-frame pump 0x140b0bd60 commits +0x4c->+0x48 and runs STEP_PlayGame(5) 0x140b0d5b0 which puts owner+0xbc into the descriptor and calls play_game_submit(child=[owner+0x2e8], descriptor={[owner+0xbc]}, -1, empty-list) -> writes child+0xd8=1/+0x100=map/GameMan+0x14=GameFlow; the child's InGameStep pump then advances to idx6 STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 which builds CSFeMan 0x143d6b880.

=== NEW-GAME vs LOAD distinguisher ===
owner+0x284==1 => new game (SetState 4, default map m60_42_34_00). owner+0x284!=1 => load/continue (SetState 5, map = [GameMan+0xc30] = the slot's deserialized saved map). The slot INDEX that actually streams is GameMan+0xac0 (slot 0..9; setter 0x14067a810: mov [GameMan+0xac0],ecx). current_slot_load 0x14067b570 reads [GameMan+0xac0] (inc;cmp 0xa;ja => valid 0..9, -1=none) and deserializes that slot via scheduler 0x143d872e0. GameMan+0xc30 (saved map) and +0xac0 (slot idx) are populated when a slot is highlighted in the load list (runtime-only); the title Continue handler 0x140b0e180 only READS +0xc30, it does NOT set +0xac0.

=== RECIPE: load SAVE SLOT 9 on the live FE-host child (zero input) ===
Preconditions (per native-continue-and-slotn-recipe-2026): GameMan 0x143d69918, slot container 0x143d5df38, inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 non-null (true at stable title); quit-latch 0x143d856a0 must be ==0; [GameMan+0xb28]==0 (submit gate); [GameMan+0xb80]==0.
Locate live FE-host: scan committed heap for obj where [0]==SimpleTitleStep vtable 0x142b63bb0 && [+0x48]==10 (committed state MenuJobWait). child = *(owner+0x2e8).
CLEANEST native action (replicates the Continue handler's load branch, NO state-forcing):
 (1) call 0x14067a810(ecx=9) to set GameMan+0xac0=9 (slot index that will stream).
 (2) ensure GameMan+0xc30 = slot 9's saved map id. Source it from the slot container 0x143d5df38 (its [+0x8]+... record holds the saved map) OR call the same peek the menu uses (0x140678a50 then read GameMan+0xc30); simplest is to read it live after a slot-peek -- it is a RUNTIME-ONLY value (the saved map of slot 9), NOT a literal.

Then DO NOTHING else -- the existing SimpleTitleStep per-frame pump 0x140b0bd60 commits the state and runs STEP_PlayGame which submits via play_game_submit and the child pump drives idx6 -> CSFeMan.
 ALTERNATIVE (even cleaner, fewest fields): directly invoke the native handler 0x140b0e180 with rcx = a callback obj whose [+0x8]==owner, after setting GameMan+0xac0=9 and clearing owner+0x284 -- it performs steps (2)+(3) natively (reads +0xc30, sets +0xbc, SetState 5). Requires a valid callback shim ({[+0x8]=owner}).
VERIFY: poll 0x143d6b880 (CSFeMan) becomes non-null; child+0xd8 cycles 1->2->0 as the InGameStep pump drains it.

=== WHAT force_play_game DID WRONG (vs this) ===
force_play_game wrote the title INNER state directly (owner+0x4c=5 / via the bare STEP_PlayGame submit) AND set owner+0xbc=9 (a SLOT INDEX, not a map id) and owner+0x3e1=1, bypassing the SetState transition-queue bookkeeping (0x140b0d960 enqueues into +0x48 count). Net: it pushed the title to GameStepWait(6) which polls child+0xd8 but the child step graph was never per-frame ticked (job orphaned, +0xd8 stuck at 1) -- per force-play-game-sets-load-value + ingamestep-load-job-needs-perframe-tick. Two faults: (a) owner+0xbc was a raw slot number 9, but play_game_submit treats descriptor dword0 as a PACKED MAP ID and gates on map-area byte 0x32..0x58 -- 9 fails the area gate so no valid GameFlow cmd; (b) forcing state skips the SetState queue + does not keep the pump driving the child, so even a valid request orphans. The CORRECT submission goes through SetState(5) on the LIVE owner (queued, committed by the existing pump) with owner+0xbc = a real packed MAP ID from [GameMan+0xc30], letting the existing SimpleTitleStep pump 0x140b0bd60 drive PlayGame -> child submit -> child pump -> MoveMap_Init -> CSFeMan. Submitting via the request (not forcing inner state) is what lets the existing pump build CSFeMan.

GameMan+0xc30 (slot's saved map id) and the per-slot saved map in slot container 0x143d5df38 are runtime-only (come from slot deserialize). owner (live SimpleTitleStep) and child=*(owner+0x2e8) are runtime pointers (heap scan for vtable 0x142b63bb0 + state 10). So [dialog+0xa48] is a container of 0x70-byte SPEC nodes (vtable 0x142aa91e0; +0x8 key/state, +0x38 inner ptr, +0x60 registry back-link) carrying std::function closures (the dialog ptr r13 captured at [rbp+0x148]/[rbp+0x88]). These are DORMANT DESCRIPTORS keyed to TextFadeOut, NOT CS::MenuWindowJob objects (vt 0x142aa89e8) and NOT ticked.

The element pump 0x140745570 iterates the WIDGET vector [dialog+0x1f8]..[dialog+0x200] stride dispatch (reads rec+0x38, calls rec vt slots) -- these are the dialog UI widgets (the 3 TITLE composition items), NOT the menu action items. CRITICAL: dialog update 0x1409aac10 NEVER calls 0x140791620 NOR 0x1407aa1f0. So registrar+dialog-pump can NEVER tick d180.
The MAIN-MENU MenuWindowJob items (Continue/Load d180/NewGame, vt 0x142aa89e8, update 0x1407ad1c0) live in a separate FD4 MenuJobSequence TREE pumped by the MENU-TASK PUMP 0x140791620: reads [holder+0x58] pre-step functor (call vt[0] @0x140791677), then [holder+0x18] ROOT job (@0x14079168e), builds FrameTime predstruct (tag 0x1429c8e58, dt=[arg3+8]), and calls root->vt[+0x10] @0x1407916d2 (= Sequence iterator 0x1407aa1f0 if root is a Sequence: children base [seq+0x18], count [seq+0x60], cursor [seq+0x10] stride8 -> recurse to MenuWindowJob leaf 0x1407ad1c0). This holder is the owner+0xe0 SimpleTitleStep job-holder, NOT the dialog. The gold capture proves d180.Update 0x1407ad1c0 runs in THIS tree.

There is NO instantiation step in the dialog update that converts [dialog+0xa48] specs into live MenuWindowJobs in the owner+0xe0 Sequence tree. The registry+0xa48 and the Sequence tree are DISJOINT. The full main menu (with d180) is built by the native SimpleTitleStep SetState sequence 2(BeginLogo)->3(BeginTitle)->10 (gold: state3 builder @0x140b0cdad asserts session singleton 0x144588e98; BeginLogo 0x140b0c2a0 hard-asserts it too). Registrar alone only (re)registers dormant +0xa48 specs and pumps +0x1f8 widgets -> that is exactly why our iterator hook only ever sees the 3 TITLE items (c000/c140/c280) and never d180. CONCLUSION: 'registrar fired + TextFadeOut' is NOT sufficient. Required steady state = the owner-e0 Sequence tree must be POPULATED, which only the native SetState(2)->3->10 does. The TextFadeOut->steady transition the runtime observed (latch=1, named states false for hundreds of frames) is the DIALOG SM settling; it does NOT build the action-item tree.

=== Q4/Q5: CONCRETE d180 WALK + INVOKE ABI ===
LOCATE (zero-input, after SetState(2)->3->10 builds the tree): owner+0xe0 holder -> root [holder+0x18] -> if Sequence (vt+0x10==0x1407aa1f0) recurse children [seq+0x18 + i*8] for i in 0..[seq+0x60] -> find MenuWindowJob leaf (vt 0x142aa89e8 or 0x142aac748) whose action functor chain hits dialog_factory: classify via [item+0xa8] -> [+0] -> [+0x10] _Do_call == 0x140820c60 which is literally add rcx,8; jmp 0x14081ead0 (VERIFIED at 0x140820c60/0x140820c64) -> dialog_factory 0x14081ead0 = Load-Game d180. functor_chain_hits_factory==0x14081ead0 IDs d180 uniquely; the other main-menu items use generic docall 0x14078ee60. === BOTTOM LINE for own-stepper ===
Do NOT expect the registrar/dialog pump to make d180 tick -- structurally impossible (+0xa48 specs and +0x1f8 widgets only). Must run native SetState(2)->3->10 (SimpleTitleStep owner) to populate the owner+0xe0 Sequence tree; THEN d180.Update 0x1407ad1c0 ticks via 0x140791620->0x1407aa1f0 and the iterator+classifier hook captures it, or hand-invoke [item+0xa8] with the [+0x130]==0 && [+0x10]==0 precondition. Separately, ProfileSelect dialog ctor `FUN_1409a5310` constructs `CS::ProfileSelectDialog` and explicitly binds `MENU_DummyProfileFace_01..10` to `SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00..09` via `FUN_1407452c0(&(dialog->MenuWindow+0x3b8), pair)`, whose helper calls Scaleform singleton `FUN_140d6ac00(owner, resource, target, symbol)` after preparing owner+0x60. This explains runtime #553: swapping 05_010_profileselect.gfx into the title GFX request rendered the shell with placeholder rows, because the title `TitleBackViewParts` did not run the ProfileSelect dialog constructor/bind path on that resource; the profile renderer source was ready, but no destination bind hit the swapped title surface (`oracle_title_scaleform_bind_observer_hits=0`, visible_surface_bind_rewrites=0). Next product path should either create a real ProfileSelect-like dialog/resource context so its ctor binds dummy faces to SYSTEX, or reproduce the binding on the actual swapped title resource owner; do not assume the GFX file alone contains the profile scene/model.

RUNTIME GROUND TRUTH (2026-06-16, user-driven real load, observe mode, frame-level b80/iodev instrumentation). Char in slot 1 at map 0xa010000 (m10). Full /tmp/er-observe.log frame sequence:

boot: session 0x7ffe.. built tick2, driver 0x7fff.. built tick2, iodev struct 0x1445893b0 + slotmgr present by tick80, b80=0.
tick123 state10 (press-any-button). tick161 state2 (BeginLogo, user pressed). tick162 state3 (BeginTitle). tick163 state10, c30 0xffffffff -> 0xa010000 (BeginTitle set c30 to NEW-GAME DEFAULT). 
BROWSE (state10, mms=0 the whole time -- the title Load-Game MoveMapListStep self-drives b80, NOT the InGameStep MoveMapStep): tick342 io20 appears (b80=0); tick344 b80=2 io18+io20 both present; tick347 b80=0 io cleared; tick511 b80=1 io10+io20 present; tick513 b80=0 cleared. These are slot-highlight PREVIEW cycles.
FINAL SLOT-1 MOUNT: tick1227 io18+io20 appear (b80=0); tick1229 ac0 -1 -> 1 (slot selected), io cleared. *** ac0=slot is set ONLY by the full deserialize 0x14067b290 (mov [GameMan+0xac0]=slot) -- so the deserialize for slot 1 RAN at ~tick1228, between the io-handle appearance (1227) and ac0=1 (1229); b80 went 2->3->deserialize->0 inside the 2-tick sampling gap. THE BROWSE/SELECT MOUNT *IS* THE CHARACTER-APPLYING DESERIALIZE, at state 10 in the menu, BEFORE PlayGame. ***
CONFIRM: tick1270 state5 (PlayGame), state at confirm = ac0=1, c30=0xa010000 (the only 2 values PlayGame needs). tick1271 state6. tick1275 csfeman 0x7ffe.. built + mms 0x7fff.. mms_state=1(MsbLoad). tick1277 mms_state=2 resmgr 0x7fff.. built b7c1=1 (streaming enabled NATIVELY). tick1279 mms_state=3(WorldResWait) -> in-world.

DECISIVE CONCLUSIONS: (1) c30 = 0xa010000 CONSTANT the entire load incl. fully in-world -- for an m10 char c30 NEVER disambiguates (m10 char map == new-game default). Need a NON-m10 char to SEE c30 change on mount. (2) The mount/char-apply happens at STATE 10 via the title MoveMapListStep menu deserialize (tell = ac0 set to slot), NOT at PlayGame. PlayGame(5) just STREAMS c30 map with the already-applied char. (3) The iodev request pair io18[+0x18]/io20[+0x20] populate on the preview read (0x14067b4e0) BEFORE ac0 is set -- confirms the decode: the preview read is the precursor that makes the iodev request resident, then b80 2->3 poll converges on it -> deserialize. (4) Our own-stepper SetState(5) loaded m60 because it used the m60 placeholder map (0x3c2a2200) in owner+0xbc and NEVER ran the mount deserialize, so ac0/char/c30 were the wrong/unmounted values. THE FIX: replicate the state-10 mount deserialize for slot N (ac0=N + char + c30=real map) BEFORE SetState(5). Related: menu-b80-mount-orchestration-sequence-2026, ground-truth-real-load-and-c30-is-newgame-default-2026, b80-mount-orchestration-is-the-blocker-not-worker-2026.

RUNTIME+RE (2026-06-16). The world-resource floor is DEFINITIVELY characterized. STEP_WorldResWait (MoveMapStep step 3, 0x140af9cf0) calls 0x140624bd0([MoveMapStep+0xf0]) which per-frame calls 0x14066d7e0 (set-flag), 0x14066da60, 0x140629d80, then readiness 0x14066d3e0. DIAGNOSTICS on the live forced-load: reqcoord=0xa010000 (CORRECT m10_01 requested, matches slot 9), [resmgr+0xb3140] block count=33, the blocks ARE the gameplay map (sample areas 0x0a/0x0b/0x0c/0x0d = m10/m11/m12/m13, slot 9's surroundings), slot 9's m10 block IS registered (found10=1). BUT the m10 block's LOAD-STATE is NULL: replicating 0x14066d3e0's tail (loadstate = blockentry->vtable[+0x10](entry, coord); ready iff [loadstate+0x2d]!=0 && [loadstate+0x35]==0xa), the getter returned NULL (m10phase=-1, m10flag=-1). KEY: 0x14066d7e0's block-found path (0x14066d8b4) gets the load-state via the SAME vtable[+0x10] and, IF non-null, sets [loadstate+0x2c]=1 (request/keep-alive) -- it does NOT CREATE the load-state. So nothing initiates the m10 block's actual load (load-state alloc + async IO); the blocks are REGISTERED but their LOADS are never STARTED. The block-load initiation = the in-game resource-STREAMING DRIVER (creates load-states + drives async IO based on the active in-game streaming loop / player anchor / budget), which does not run at the forced-from-title context. This fully explains the user's on-screen symptom: the load LOGIC runs (area/intro cutscene text+audio fire) but the world never renders (geometry never streams) and the title 'press to continue' view never tears down (FE-host parked at GameStepWait waiting on child_d8 waiting on WorldResWait). This is the architectural FLOOR: world resource streaming is in-game-state-owned (same class as frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned-not-callable), the deepest layer. Candidates next: find what CREATES a block load-state (the streaming driver / 0x14066da60 / 0x140629d80 / a per-frame resource-streaming update + its enable gate), and whether a spawned player/WorldChrMan anchor or a streaming-enable flag is the missing trigger. Related: worldreswait-33-blocks-streaming-gap-2026, full-pipeline-traced-to-worldreswait-map-block-streaming.

VISUAL CONFIRMATION (2026-06-18, grim screenshot of the ER window class steam_app_1245620 at the 30s deadline, save-safe). Added a save-safe capture_inworld_screenshot to .auto/runtime_probe.sh (privacy: queries+grims ONLY the ER window by class, before teardown). The offline autoload screenshot (inworld.png) shows the ELDEN RING LOADING SCREEN (black screen + ELDEN RING logo + Erdtree icon bottom-left) -- NOT the playable world. 

=> DEFINITIVELY CONFIRMS: (1) player_available=true was a LOADING-SCREEN FALSE-POSITIVE (PlayerIns exists during the loading screen) -- the honest correction was right. (2) The autoload MECHANISM WORKS end-to-end up to the world-load: the loading screen only appears because the char deserialized + SetState5 fired + the world-stream started with the loaded char (a failed autoload would stay at the title/menu, not show the in-game loading screen). (3) The full world-load (past the loading screen into the playable world) does NOT finish within the 30s policy cap: timeline is boot-to-title ~15s + menu-drive+deserialize to ~24s + SetState5 + world-load (loading screen at 30s, still loading). A real ER m10_01 world-load takes ~10-20s, so total ~40-45s > 30s.

So the OFFLINE autoload is FUNCTIONALLY PROVEN (reaches the world-load/loading-screen with the real char) but the PLAYABLE-WORLD visual confirmation EXCEEDS the 30s Rego cap (AGENTS.md: timeout_seconds<=30). This is a POLICY-CAP constraint, NOT a mechanism failure. To confirm the playable world either: (a) the user relaxes the 30s cap for a one-off verification run (deliberate policy change -- contract+rego+tests together), (b) the user manually launches the autoload once + watches the player reach the world, or (c) accept the loading-screen + full-pipeline evidence as functional proof. NEXT (if cap relaxed): re-run with a longer timeout, screenshot again -> expect the playable world (m10_01 Chapel of Anticipation, the char 'a'). The screenshot harness (capture_inworld_screenshot) is committed + ready. HOOK HAZARD 2 (2026-07-17, DLL 99a12f98): a read-only forwarding DETOUR on the map-load orchestrator 0x14082dbf0 STALLED the FIRST autoload at a NEW earlier point 'Preparing Save 6/11' (user-observed). Prior DLL d9c40b89 (without this hook) booted through load1 fine, so the 0x82dbf0 detour is the cause. 0x82dbf0 is dispatched as an in-game STEP (caller 0x14082eb7e step-thunk) and is load-critical/possibly hot; a MinHook/ilhook detour + mms_call_original forwarding perturbs it enough to wedge the load (no crash, just stall). LESSON (reinforces persistent-fix-run-design + no-double-detour): do NOT detour unfamiliar in-game step/orchestrator functions to OBSERVE them -- use the sw-breakpoint (INT3) facility instead (er-effects-breakpoints.txt), which does not trampoline/corrupt. To capture the player-map mount evidence SAFELY: sw-bp on MountEblArchive 0x1efc00 (proven safe in June golden-mount-trace; fires per-mount not per-frame so bounded) with deep stack (SW_BP_STACK_DUMP_QWORDS=40) -> the deep stack shows the 0x82dc1c/0x79171f orchestrator caller chain (confirms 0x82dbf0 involvement) AND MountEblArchive's rdx/r8 archive descriptor (the driver target for re-mounting on load2). Do NOT sw-bp 0x82dbf0 itself (step-dispatched => possibly per-frame hot => trap storm risk). Interesting datum: the 0x82dbf0 detour breaking load1 at 'Preparing Save' confirms 0x82dbf0 IS on the load-1 critical path. Links [[strategy-pivot-drive-map-load-orchestrator-0x82dbf0-serves-bugfix-and-ownload-2026-07-17]] [[hook-hazard-me3-already-hooks-mount-ebl-no-double-detour-2026-07-17]].

Then ingamestep_pump_tick calls 0x140b0bd60(InGameStep=*(owner+0x2e8), &FD4TaskData) directly from the CSTask each frame. NEXT -- RE the InGameStep step table 0x143d70130 step index 4 (what it is, its advance/wait condition), and add per-tick step_state logging to the pump to see if step_state moves at all. MMS18 EXTERNAL FINALIZE TRIGGER RESOLVED (static RE, all VAs ground-truthed via dump-deobf-shift, uniform shift=-0xf0 content-unique). STEP_MoveMap(step18, dump 0x140af7de0/deobf 0x140af7cf0, CS::MoveMapStep::STEP_MoveMap, size 0x120b) NEVER self-advances: full decompile has ZERO calls to the advance-helper and ZERO writes to field8_0x50(+0x50) or requestedState(+0x4c). FD4 advance idiom (proven from STEP_WorldResWait/MsbLoadWait/Cleanup): call FUN_140af9e80(this) [requestedState++, panic '移動先ステップ未定義'; dump 0x140af9e80/deobf 0x140af9d90] THEN set super_FD4StepTemplateBase.field8_0x50=true. Xref of FUN_140af9e80: EVERY STEP_* calls it EXCEPT STEP_MoveMap and STEP_Finish; the ONE non-STEP caller = FUN_140afa7c0 (dump 0x140afa7c0/deobf 0x140afa6d0 = the external finalize advancer; our code names it MoveMapStep::CheckReturnToTitle). It is ticked EVERY FRAME by FUN_140aff730 (dump 0x140aff730/deobf 0x140aff640 = resident MoveMapStep per-frame update, the one whose direct-pump crashes).
cVar10 PREDICATE (EXACT): FUN_140afa7c0 writes CSMenuMan->menuData->field_0x5e = cVar10 every frame; cVar10=1 iff ANY of: (a) FUN_140e2ab20()!=0 [pad] DEAD returns const0; OR CSEzSelectBot bot (offline false); (b) CSSessionManager->protocolState==WaitReload && MP-host (MP only); (c) FUN_1405ff030(CSEventMan->deadReset)==2 (death); (d) BOOL_143d856a0 [force-flag RVA 0x3d856a0, written ONLY by MainLoop dump 0x140c8ff80/deobf 0x140c8fe90 = a save/ending gate]; (e) menuData+0x5d [return-title byte]; (f) GameManIsWarpRequested()==GameMan->warpRequested@+0x10 [reader dump 0x140679900/deobf 0x140679810; setter SetCallForWarp dump 0x14067af90/deobf 0x14067aea0]; (g) GameMan+0xb7c [reader FUN_140679520/deobf 0x140679430; SET=1 ONLY by FUN_14067a7a0/deobf 0x14067a6b0 from _CheckEndingRequest dump 0x140afa0f0/deobf 0x140afa000 = ENDING flag, our const GAME_MAN_ENDING_FLAG_B7C]; (h) GameMan+0xb7d [reader FUN_140679530/deobf 0x140679440; SET=1 ONLY by FUN_14067a7b0/deobf 0x14067a6c0 from _CheckEndingRequest ending-flag 0x1e = ENDING flag B7D]; (i) FUN_140e62b50()==8 DEAD returns const0.
KEY: menuData+0x5e=1 is NOT itself the advance. FUN_140afa7c0 runs an INTERNAL sub-state-machine on param_1->field25_0x12a (0..8): case0 RETURNS immediately if cVar10==0; once cVar10==1 it walks 0->(1 or 5)->..->7->8 across many frames gated on CSFade timers/CSSound/CSRemo; ONLY case8 calls FUN_140af9e80 (the real 18->19 advance) + SetCallForWarp(false) + DAT_143b33d00=1 + frees load-time field tasks. case7->8 gate = FUN_14067a170() && !ShouldSave() && !FUN_140679460() && FUN_140a9ceb0(CSRemo). cVar10 does NOT read MoveMapStep+0x248/+0x100/+0x270/+0x4b8 (those are STEP_MoveMap's own fields, irrelevant to the advancer).
Q2 HEALTHY-LOAD INPUT: by elimination the ONLY live single-player load-in input is GameMan::warpRequested (b7c/b7d ending-only per our own const names + _CheckEndingRequest; force=MainLoop save-gate; death/MP/return-title not a fresh continue; pad+iVar15 DEAD). Native full deserialize (0x67b290=dump FUN_14067b380) UNCONDITIONALLY sets warpRequested=true; FUN_140afa7c0 consumes+clears it at case8 (SetCallForWarp false) -- which is why gm-snap reads warp=FALSE in the resident world yet warp is the trigger DURING step18.
Q3 WHY rt5d(0x5d) PARTIAL: menuData+0x5d makes cVar10=1 but routes case0 through the RETURN-TITLE variant (skips warp-target-commit FUN_14067a810 + save-profile block, sets serverLog=4); it correctly tears DOWN an old world (proven) but is return-to-title semantics, not a stay-in-new-world load finalize.
Q4 MINIMAL FIX: do NOT force menuData+0x5e or poke +0x50. The offline save_redirect load must present GameMan::warpRequested=TRUE at step18 so FUN_140afa7c0 finalizes 18->Cleanup(19, 0x140af5840)->Finish(20, dump 0x140af5b10/deobf 0x140af5a20, gated field_0xb0>=2 + testNetStep + CSRemo)->-1 -> InGameStep+0xd8 drains 1->2->0 -> render. OUR BUG: system_quit_repro_guards.rs:1681 (+ title_tick_cover.rs:1370) CLEARS warp_requested=false to prevent a title-bounce; for the save_redirect path this STARVES the finalize (cVar10 stays 0, step18 parks, end5e=0 = the exact grounded signature). Their bounce is a downstream unconsumed/invalid-target warp addressed by the existing RequestMoveMap BlockId fix; correct fix = LET warp_requested STAND (or SetCallForWarp(true) deobf 0x14067aea0) at step18 AND ensure the finalize sub-machine reaches case8. CORRECTION (2026-06-24, RTTI/xref RE + onscreen screenshots + user catch): the lever previously called pab_outro_skip is MISLABELED -- it is NOT a press-any-button lever, it is a TITLE INIT-READINESS OVERRIDE. The two flags it forces are init milestones, NOT button-press flags (nothing on either writer path reads input): (A) CSMenuMan+0x21 = "Scaleform menu resident-resources loaded", normally set by CS::CSScaleformStep::STEP_Wait_forResidentResourceLoad; (B) (*(TitleStep+0x2e8)=CS::InGameStep*)+0xdc = "InGameStep init-wait complete", normally set by CS::InGameStep::STEP_InitWait. The ACTUAL press-any-button dismissal is the global accept byte 0x144589bdc (read by CS::TitleTopDialog::update) + the BackScreen job+0x1e8 that pab-advance already pokes -- a SEPARATE mechanism. "PRESS ANY BUTTON" prompt + ELDEN RING logo both belong to CS::TitleTopDialog (05_000_Title, vt 0x142b26468); CS::BackScreen (vt 0x142aa0080) is the full-screen backdrop shown first, so press-any-button-backdrop-before-logo is the EXPECTED splash-skipped order, not a bug. IMPLICATION for the floor (menu-open-floor-is-boot-init-task-starvation-2026-06-24): forcing init-ready BEFORE resources truly load just moves the wait to the RENDER -- the menu cannot draw until the real Scaleform resident load completes (~15.4s floor). The override skips wait-poll scheduling (~1s real, validated), NOT the load. It remains save-safe (RE-blessed, validated gold-loads/zero-input/zero-MessageBoxDialog) and is KEPT default-on (user decision 2026-06-24) but RENAMED honestly: gating title_init_ready_override_enabled, fn title_init_ready_override, latch TITLE_INIT_READY_OVERRIDE_FIRED, log line title-init-ready-override; consts CSMENUMAN_SCALEFORM_RESIDENT_LOADED_21_OFFSET / TITLE_OWNER_INGAMESTEP_2E8_OFFSET / INGAMESTEP_INIT_WAIT_DONE_DC_OFFSET. Screenshots: +13.1s/+14.6s black (resources loading), +17.1s ELDEN RING logo + "Checking save data...". Capture timing must use telemetry markers (scripts/capture-er-at-marker.sh), NOT launch_epoch (wine startup offset). Supersedes the press-lever framing in pab-outro-skip-VALIDATED-residual-init-gated-2026-06-24 and pab-job-playout-is-the-3.5s-NOT-menu-build-2026-06-24.

DECISIVE root cause (subagent, 2026-06-15) of the step-7 map-stream stall plus the path forward. The InGameStep step machine advances 4->5->7 under our direct pump but STEP_MoveMap_Update step 7 (0x140aec720) sets job+0xd8=2 ONLY when gate 0x140eb5530 on the +0xe0 MoveMapStep child returns ready. Gate reads rcx=[MoveMapStep+0x8] and returns true if null else calls that sub-object vtable+0x20. Observed d8 stuck at 1 means [MoveMapStep+0x8] is a non-null pending stream task whose resident/complete check never returns true. WHY -- nothing drives that stream task to completion in the forced-title context. STEP_MoveMap_Update only calls the GATE on +0xe0, never MoveMapStep own Update 0x140aeb910 (vtable 0x142b5d3f8 +0x20), and MoveMapStep is a DIRECT MEMBER of InGameStep NOT an entry in the InGameStep child-list [+0x18], so the InGameStep child-walk 0x142656690 does not tick it either. The missing setup is IngameInit (outer SimpleTitleStep state-2 handler 0x140b0a1f0) which primes subsystems BEFORE submitting the load -- 0x1405d19a0 registers the in-game child steps into the InGameStep child list [+0xc0]+0xc0/+0xd0 (insert primitive 0x1405cb5a0), 0x1406e9250 rebuilds world/render objects (singletons 0x143d691d8/0x143d69ba8), zeroes GameMan 0x143d5df38+0x8 fields +0xc8/+0xd0/+0xf0/+0xf8, reads requested slot from [outer+0x130], writes [InGameStep]+0xb3868=0, THEN calls SetupLoad 0x140aebdc0 itself (at 0x140b0a3ec). force_play_game runs SetupLoad WITHOUT that priming, so the stream task is created (d8=1, area passes the under-0x59 gate 0x140720210) but never driven to resident. VERDICT -- NOT a hard wall, replicable. RECOMMENDED LEVER 1 -- stop forcing the INNER TitleStep to PlayGame, instead DRIVE THE OUTER SimpleTitleStep through state 2 IngameInit (call 0x140b0a1f0(outer, &FD4TaskData) or set outer +0x4c=2 if the outer is ticked) with a valid [outer+0x130] requested slot, THEN keep pumping 0x140b0bd60(InGameStep) each frame. Expected -- IngameInit registers in-game child steps plus primes world subsystems, the step-walk then ticks the stream, [MoveMapStep+0x8] reports ready, gate returns true, step7 writes d8=2, load completes past the 700 plateau. PREREQ -- need the OUTER SimpleTitleStep object pointer (its +0x10 == table base 0x143d71340, its +0xc0 == the InGameStep = inner owner+0x2e8), still need to locate it at runtime. RISK medium (IngameInit touches GameMan plus world singletons) but it is the game own designed path. Lever 2 (pump MoveMapStep Update 0x140aeb910 directly) is HIGH risk and needs the same priming first, not preferred. Key VAs -- gate 0xeb5530, MoveMapStep Update 0xaeb910 (vtable 0x142b5d3f8), SetupLoad 0xaebdc0, IngameInit 0xb0a1f0, subsystem init 0x5d19a0 and 0x6e9250, child-insert 0x5cb5a0, world-stream FD4 alloc 0x144842d40. Related: [[ingamestep-direct-pump-works-stuck-step4]] [[outer-simpletitlestep-state4-ingame-gate]] [[ingamestep-manual-tick-lever-b0bd60]].

PERSISTENT PAUSE-JUSTIFICATION RULE (user 2026-07-17, refines terse-pause): a blocked-pause message must state not only WHAT I am blocked on but WHY that blocker precludes doing OTHER foreground work right now. If I cannot precisely justify why the blocker blocks ALL other non-overlapping foreground work, then I am NOT truly blocked and MUST do that foreground work instead of pausing. A background subagent/RE result usually gates only ONE specific next step (e.g. the exact offset), NOT all foreground work -- so the default is to keep working (clean a gate, examine the fix integration point, build the success oracle, remove refuted code) and only pause when genuinely nothing independent remains, with the why spelled out. Extends persistent-pause-message-must-be-short-blocked-reason-only-2026-07-17 and the VERBOSEPAUSE policy.

USER DECISION 2026-07-18: same-slot self-reload must do the EXACT same thing as loading any other character -- a genuine full reload, NOT a no-op. This SUPERSEDES the earlier 'slot-aware disarm / no-op self-reload' prescription in angre-4loads-goal-met-but-switch-regression for the product route. So the fix for product-route-same-slot-reload-nowload-stuck must make own_load_switch_reload_fire complete GENERICALLY for any (file,slot) incl. same-slot: clear the stuck engine NowLoading flag after fresh_deser_done so the world resumes sim/render, and eliminate the MessageBox side effect (msgbox 0->1). Do NOT special-case same-slot as skip. Likely mechanism: same-slot reload has target map == current map (0x1c==0x1c) so the MoveMap teardown short-circuits and never clears NowLoading, whereas a different-map switch tears down fully; fix must force the full teardown/clear regardless of same-map. Links [[product-route-same-slot-reload-nowload-stuck-2026-07-18]].

DESIGN COROLLARY (user, 2026-06-22) to reuse-native-fns-not-reimplement: DON'T HARDCODE CONSTANTS THAT ARE TIED TO THE SAVE FILE OR ENGINE -- infer them from the data, or defer to in-game memory reads at runtime. Two cases: (1) SAVE-FILE-TIED + self-describing -> derive from the parsed file. BND4 is self-describing: header carries headerSize (@0x10) and per-entry fileHeaderSize/stride (@0x20); each entry carries its own entry_size (@0x08). So er-save-loader bnd4.rs now reads the strides from the header and derives body length = entry_size - 0x10 (MD5) per entry; the SLOT_BODY_LEN/SLOT_ENTRY_LEN literals (0x280000/0x280010) are kept ONLY as expected char-slot sanity references, never the slicing source of truth. (2) ENGINE-RUNTIME values -> read from the engine in-process, not literals: e.g. the 0x280000 alloc/read size the parser uses comes from the engine's own 0x67b100(size) call arg (our hook reads it), and RVAs resolve off the live module base. Keep literals only as validation bounds / expected-value asserts. Applies to the whole autoload/OWN-LOAD effort. Relates: reuse-native-fns-not-reimplement-2026-06-22, own-load-step3-hook-67b100-feed-buffer-2026-06-22, ER-PC-SAVE-IS-PLAINTEXT-MD5-not-encrypted-2026-06-22.

STATIC RE CONCLUSIVELY EXHAUSTED on the live-dialog registration (2026-06-18, 4th deobf subagent). THE PARADOX DISSOLVED: there is NO separate post-build enter/register routine. The Load-Game dialog becomes a LIVE registered ProfileLoadDialog (vtable 0x142b229f8) intrinsically WHEN the menu job-list pump invokes the entry std::function _Do_call (thunk 0x140820c60 -> factory 0x14081ead0) WITH THE LIVE CALL-FRAME ARGS rdx=MenuWindow* and r8=SceneProxy&. Those live args are what make the factory wire the dialog into menu-group 0x143d87350 + the active-screen set (factory pre-step 0x1409aa680 toggles active-screen array 0x143d6d8d0 visibility). There is NO [0x143d87350+0x18] list-push after the call -- registration is folded into the factory invocation under live args.

WHY run(node) AND the forge BOTH FAIL: run 0x1409aaba0 is a PARAMETERLESS MenuMemberFuncJob wrapper -- it calls the functor/factory WITHOUT the MenuWindow*/SceneProxy& args, so the factory builds a NON-LIVE dialog and run discards it. The synthetic forge (own_stepper_direct_build) likewise lacks live args. Same wall both ways.

TitleTopDialog::Update 0x1409aac10 is just the title-screen STATE MACHINE (compares state name at dialog+0xa60 vs "FadeIn"/"TextFadeOut"/"Loop" via 0x140749b20; on "Loop" w/ dialog+0xa40==0 calls entry registrar 0x1409b24e0) -- it BUILDS entries into registry [dialog+0xa48], does NOT dispatch confirms or register dialogs. The node run is dispatched only via the generic virtual job-list pump ([node+0]->[+0x10]); no static VA owns it.

THE ONE CALL own_stepper needs (zero-input live build): thunk 0x140820c60(rcx = live functor instance whose vtable==0x142ac3ea8, rdx = live MenuWindow*, r8 = live SceneProxy&) -- equivalently factory 0x14081ead0(rcx = dialog+0xa38 capture, rdx = MenuWindow*, r8 = SceneProxy&). This single call (with REAL args) builds AND registers the live dialog; the native pump then drives load_activate -> selector -> submit 0x14067b1a0 -> mount.

RUNTIME-ONLY RESIDUE (static cannot pin -- call-frame values, not globals): (1) the live MenuWindow* and (2) SceneProxy& the factory needs as rdx/r8; (3) the live functor instance location (heap registry [dialog+0xa48], Arxan -- locate by scanning for [obj]==0x142ac3ea8); (4) confirm dialog+0xa38 holds the right capture on the live dialog.

IMPLICATION FOR THE BUILT CODE (gated ER_EFFECTS_LIVE_DIALOG): it is INSUFFICIENT as-built -- fire_live_loadgame_node calls the parameterless run(node) (no live args) and the orchestrator reads owner+0xe0 (the dialog is never stored there). CORRECT path = at the rendered menu, read-only acquire MenuWindow* + SceneProxy& (from the menu owner chain / a MenuMan-SceneProxy singleton) + the functor instance, then make the single thunk 0x140820c60 call with those live args. NEXT (last static lead): pin where MenuWindow* + SceneProxy& come from (singleton/accessor/owner-chain offset) so the runtime read is deterministic; then it is a save-safe read-only runtime acquisition + one call. Related: live-dialog-not-registered-by-node-fire-pump-owns-lane-2026, load-game-functor-0x142ac3ea8-is-live-build-key-2026, model-B-live-dialog-IMPLEMENTATION-plan-2026.

LOAD1-PROVEN-loads-and-movable-but-needs-focus-2026-07-18: MILESTONE (run samechar-3x-211532, rc=0, PASS). LOAD1 IS PROVEN: boot autoload of angrE loads up AND the character is MOVABLE -- the pad-boundary stick injection (hook 0x141f6bad0, write this+0x8a0=1.0) walked the char 60 consecutive frames -> CAN_MOVE_CONFIRMED. NEXT: RE the gameplay-input focus gate (where the game checks GetActiveWindow / the active-window flag before applying locomotion/gameplay input, analogous to the menu flag DLUID+0x88d at 0x141f292bd that stay-active forces) and OVERRIDE it in-DLL so injected input applies while UNFOCUSED. REFINED-GOAL-details-answers-2026-07-18: Detailed answers to the goal-refinement questions. INPUT MODEL: Product loads ONLY the initial configured save, yields to the user at the FIRST genuinely-playable state (world ready; user could play single-player OR seamless co-op normally), then STOPS. Then the HARNESS takes over and pivots to a new save slot AND/OR save file. When the harness selects a save, the PRODUCT loads that next character 'the quickest valid way possible' = ANY fast path that lands a FULLY-PLAYABLE character with that save's real stats/equipment/everything -- explicitly NOT required to replay the vanilla teardown pathway (quit-to-menu -> load-character -> only-current-file -> click -> await). Striving for speed but ACCURACY OVER SPEED: if we cannot load multiple characters in a row proving teardown+startup works GENERICALLY, goal is NOT met. XInput is NOT required for any proof; it is just the closest/best autonomous path that mirrors real user flow (prefer it for fidelity where feasible, not mandatory). PROOF BOUND: test >=3 save FILES per game type (vanilla=3; +seamless=6). 20 switches is excessive. Edge cases appeared at load #1 and #3, so cross-FILE coverage matters more than raw count. CORPUS: any .sl2/.co2 under /mnt/a/Code Projects/Elden Ring Save Manager/data/save-files (Windows A:\Code Projects\Elden Ring Save Manager\data\save-files) is fair game; user has no preference which. Some saves have a KNOWN bug: loading FROM them to another save 'did not transfer to the screen where I could select a second character' for bugged cases. SEAMLESS: TIMEBOX -- if vanilla not solved in 12h, skip seamless; else seamless worth doing. REALLY timebox; self-time via 'date'. User values bd memories recording how long specific tasks took (my estimates) to gauge; expects mistakes; fine with autonomous non-cyclic overnight work. Timebox START = 2026-07-18 02:23 -0700. Links [[REFINED-GOAL-multi-save-repeatable-load-2026-07-18]].

Intended product behavior: quit == ALT+F4 -- the process terminates at once and the user gets full control of display/mouse/focus right after, no loading-screen/world-teardown visible. Third of three System->Quit-menu bugs reported together; see [[native-profile-load-msgbox-crash-1162]].

CORRECTION (2026-06-21, static decode of job ctor 0x142410a30). The 'job path size=0 at ctor' lead (b80-job-path-size-zero-at-ctor-lead) is a RED HERRING. So path_size=0 captured at 0x14240e568 (right after ctor) is EXPECTED, not proof of an empty path. Also my path offsets were unreliable (job+0x488=0x7fff309ffff2 cant be a string size).
