DECISIVE static RE (2026-06-15, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs) of IngameInit 0x140b0a1f0 -- its this/args/effect, the ordering, and the REAL world-priming root cause. CITES disasm. Supersedes the premise that IngameInit BUILDS the world.

=== IngameInit 0x140b0a1f0 IDENTITY + ARGS ===
this(rcx->rsi) = the SECOND SimpleTitleStep step machine bound to step-table 0x143d71340 (NOT our live FE-host table 0x143d71580, and NOT the InGameStep). IngameInit is idx2 of table 0x143d71340 (builder 0x1400a4c90: idx0 STEP_MenuInit 0x140b0a4a0, idx1 STEP_MenuLoop 0x140b0a5e0, idx2 STEP_IngameInit 0x140b0a1f0, idx3 STEP_IngameStandby 0x140b0a430; names all 'SimpleTitleStep::STEP_*' UTF-16 @0x142b62678+). This machine self-drives MenuInit->MenuLoop->IngameInit via the SetState helper 0x140b0a980 (inc [this+0x4c]). this-layout for IngameInit: +0x48 cur_state, +0x4c req_state, +0xc0 = DIRECT InGameStep ptr (0xb3870 obj, fields +0xd8 phase/+0x100,+0x104 map/+0xb35f0 req-vec/+0xb3868 flag), +0x130 = selection/slot (-1 => default map m60 'm60_42_34_00' 0x142b62c70 via 0x14071fd60). NOTE: this +0xc0 is a RAW InGameStep pointer; on our live FE-host (table 0x143d71580) the InGameStep is instead held at owner+0x2e0 slot-holder ([owner+0x2e8]=InGameStep via insert 0x140eb55a0 storing [holder+0x8]). So IngameInit's this is a DIFFERENT object class/layout than our FE-host -- only the synthetic-this (zeroed buf, +0xc0=InGameStep, +0x130=mapid, +0x48=0) satisfies it.

=== WHAT IngameInit ACTUALLY DOES (in order) ===
1. inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 assert-nonnull; sets inputmgr+0x6b0=1 (pump_ran). 2. 0x1405d19a0(rcx=[0x143d68448]) -- registers in-game CHILD STEPS into singleton 0x143d68448's child-list (+0xd0) via insert 0x1405cb5a0. (0x143d68448 ctor 0x140679880<-0x140defeb5, a GameMan-area flow obj.) 3. 0x1406e9250(rcx=[0x143d69d18]) -- WORLD/SCENE RE-SYNC: at +0x2c2/+0x2cf it does cmp [0x143d691d8],0 / je RETURN and cmp [0x143d69ba8],0 / je RETURN -- i.e. it EARLY-RETURNS (no-op) if world singletons 0x143d691d8/0x143d69ba8 are NULL. It only iterates/re-registers EXISTING scene objects; it does NOT construct the world singletons. 4. 0x1401cd400, 0x1406f9560. 5. zeroes GameMan(0x143d5df38)[+0x8] fields +0xc8/+0xd0/+0xf0/+0xf8. 6. 0x140257560(-1). 7. reads sel [rsi+0x130]; resolves map; sets [InGameStep+0xb3868]=0; 8. calls play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0(rcx=InGameStep) = SUBMIT request (sets InGameStep+0xd8=1, +0x100/+0x104=map, appends req-vec +0xb35f0). 9. tail jmp 0x140b0a980 (inc req_state; if cur_state+1>7 builds FD4 world-stream task 0x144842d40 + registers key 0x59682f01 via 0x142656b00 -- so +0x48>=7 needed for that arm; +0x48==0 SKIPS it = the synthetic-this safe path, hence false-positive placeholder).

=== ROOT CAUSE: IngameInit is NOT the world-builder. The MoveMapStep's OWN step machine is. ===
The IsResident gate that drains child+0xd8: STEP_MoveMap_Update 0x140aec720 @0x140aec8fc calls 0x140eb5530(rcx=InGameStep+0xe0); if al!=0 writes [InGameStep+0xd8]=2. Gate 0x140eb5530: held=[holder+0x8]=MoveMapStep; if null ret 1 else ret (MoveMapStep->vtable[+0x20]()!=0). MoveMapStep vtable 0x142b5ff38 +0x20 = 0x140af50a0 = { cmp [MoveMapStep+0x48],0xffffffff; sete al; ret } -- RESIDENT IFF MoveMapStep+0x48 (its OWN step-machine cur_state) == -1 (terminal). MoveMapStep's own step-table = 0x143d70860 (builder 0x1400a40c0): idx0 STEP_BeginInit 0x140af5140, idx1 STEP_MsbLoad 0x140af8f00, idx2 STEP_MsbLoadWait, ... STEP_MsbLoad 0x140af8f00 is what CONSTRUCTS the world singletons: 0x143d691d8 written by 0x140618bf0/0x140619be0 (both called from 0x140af8f00 region @0x140af8fc4) and 0x143d69ba8 by 0x14061e800 (@0x140af9546). So the world singletons IngameInit's 0x1406e9250 needs are built by MoveMapStep::STEP_MsbLoad, i.e. by the MoveMapStep advancing its OWN step machine to terminal.

=== WHO PUMPS THE MoveMapStep STEP MACHINE: the FD4 SCHEDULER, via task nodes ===
MoveMapStep update = 0x140aff640 (ref only @ ctor 0x140af28d0 +0x2be4: lea ->[node+0x20]). 0x140aff640 has 0 E8 callers -- it is dispatched as an FD4 task. MoveMapStep ctor 0x140af28d0 calls task-node init 0x140eb12d0 NINE times (node vtable 0x142c01740), registering its update fns (incl 0x140aff640) as scheduler tasks; it also calls CSFeMan::Construct 0x140766010 @0x140af2e47. STEP_MoveMap_Update 0x140aec720 does NOT call 0x140aff640 (verified: it only calls the gate). So the MoveMapStep's per-frame update + its own BeginInit/MsbLoad step machine run ONLY because the FD4 scheduler ticks those 9 registered task nodes each frame. In our forced/deserialize-first context the MoveMapStep object exists (CSFeMan built) but its scheduler task nodes are not being driven to advance the BeginInit->MsbLoad->terminal sequence, so MoveMapStep+0x48 never reaches -1, the gate never returns resident, and InGameStep+0xd8 stays 1.

=== ORDERING vs our deserialize+SetState(5) ===
On a real boot the table-0x143d71340 machine self-advances MenuInit->MenuLoop->IngameInit, and IngameInit primes the GameMan flow + InGameStep child-steps + SUBMITS the play-game request -- BEFORE/as the InGameStep advances its idx6 STEP_MoveMap_Init which builds the MoveMapStep (+ CSFeMan). The MoveMapStep then, under SCHEDULER ticking, runs BeginInit->MsbLoad (builds world singletons 0x143d691d8/0x143d69ba8) -> ... -> terminal (+0x48=-1) -> gate resident -> InGameStep+0xd8 1->2->0. Our SetState(5) path builds the MoveMapStep+CSFeMan but skips the self-driving title machine AND does not get the MoveMapStep's own step machine ticked to completion.

=== VERDICT / RECIPE DIRECTION ===
Calling IngameInit (real or synthetic this) does NOT build the world -- it no-ops 0x1406e9250 when the world singletons are null (= the false-positive placeholder). The genuine missing lever is: drive the MoveMapStep's OWN step machine (table 0x143d70860, BeginInit->MsbLoad...) to terminal (+0x48=-1) so STEP_MsbLoad constructs 0x143d691d8/0x143d69ba8 and streams the slot-9 MSB. Two real options: (a) ensure the live MoveMapStep's 9 scheduler task nodes are actually ticked (find/register them with the FD4 task scheduler so 0x140aff640 runs each frame) -- the scheduler-task-registration ABI is the remaining unreversed piece; or (b) directly pump MoveMapStep update 0x140aff640(rcx=MoveMapStep, rdx=&framectx with [rdx+8]=dt) each frame after CSFeMan/MoveMapStep exist and the slot is deserialized, which advances its own step machine through MsbLoad to terminal (HIGH risk, but it is the game's own update fn -- preferred over re-implementing MsbLoad). PRECONDS for (b): MoveMapStep exists (=[InGameStep+0xe0 holder +0x8]); slot 9 deserialized (GameMan+0xc30 real map, +0xac0=9); CSFeMan non-null; valid framectx. VERIFY: MoveMapStep+0x48 advances toward -1; world singletons 0x143d691d8/0x143d69ba8 go non-null after MsbLoad; gate 0x140eb5530 true; InGameStep+0xd8 drains 1->2->0; slot-9 area+char resident. Related: load-needs-ingameinit-subsystem-priming (REFINED: IngameInit re-syncs but does NOT build the world), slot9-deserialize-works-world-stream-needs-priming, ingameinit-synthetic-this-works-but-false-positive (EXPLAINED: 0x1406e9250 no-op + map=default = placeholder), csfeman-construction-chain-ingamestep-step6-2026, real-continue-load-driver-recipe.

CONVERGED ACQUISITION RECIPE (2026-06-18, 2 of 3 parallel agents agree; 3rd ruled out). The live MenuWindow* (factory 0x14081ead0 rdx) is read DETERMINISTICALLY from the SceneProxy we already hold:

RECIPE (at rendered title, all read-only safe_read): 
1. td = *(owner + 0xe0); require *(td) == base+0x2b26468 (TitleTopDialog).
2. proxy = *(td + 0xa38).   // the capture/SceneProxy pointer the factory consumes
3. require *(proxy) == base + 0x2a94a70 (CS::SceneObjProxy vtable). LOG *(proxy) regardless (self-diagnostic).
4. menu_window = *(proxy + 0x20).   // proven: SceneObjProxy ctor 0x14074a700 does mov [proxy+0x20]=rbx where rbx=the MenuWindow (0x14074a735). back-ref EXISTS at +0x20.

6. factory 0x14081ead0(rcx = td + 0xa38, rdx = menu_window).  // rcx is the capture SLOT (td+0xa38); factory r8=*(rcx)=proxy; ctor uses proxy as SceneProxy. rdx=MenuWindow.

WHY option 3 (scene-group walk) is DEAD: *(0x143d87350) is the FD4 named-heap registry entry for "MENU" (FD4MemoryHeapWrapper, vtable 0x142bde2d8; sole writer 0x140e19891 via DLInplaceStr map 0x140e15200). The dialog factories load mov r8,[0x143d87350] ONLY for alloc/registration context; they do NOT read a MenuWindow from it. MenuWindow ctor 0x1407419a0 stores the group as an ALLOCATOR-HANDLE backref at MenuWindow+0x1f0/+0x210 ({group,key,key,key}), NOT a registration -> the group has NO MenuWindow list (this is why probe-4 flat scan of *(0x143d87350)[0..0x400] found nothing). Do NOT pursue the group for window enumeration.

CAVEAT (runtime-only, self-diagnosed): the object at [td+0xa38] could be a peer proxy class, not SceneObjProxy -> step 3 verifies *(proxy)==0x142a94a70 and the code LOGS the actual vtable + the derived menu_window vtable, fail-closed if mismatch. If probe shows it is NOT a SceneObjProxy, fall back to option 2 (hook the menu functor dispatch to capture the engine MenuWindow*/SceneProxy& -- agent a84db266e818497bb). FALLBACK if menu_window vtable is wrong: scan the SceneObjProxy [0..0x40] for the MenuWindow vtable. BREAKTHROUGH (2026-06-23, autonomous diagnostic via sw-bp on the push site 0x1407ad53b, rcx=the vector). The cap-8 DLFixedVector that OVERFLOWS when our load's CS::MenuWindowJob::Run pushes its window = the vector at DIALOG+0x50 (dialog = *(owner+0xe0), the live TitleTopDialog). Pinned: the LAST push before the crash (hit5 +26304ms, AFTER our owner+0x130 install +26284ms) had rcx=0x7fff69e85020; the dispatcher 0x9b3070 hit showed dialog=0x7fff69e84fd0 and r8=0x7fff69e85020=dialog+0x50. The earlier title-window pushes (hits 2,3,4) went into owner+0xe0 (0x7ffffdffa9a0, a DIFFERENT vector) -- so dialog+0x50 is a SEPARATE cap-8 vector that our load's MenuWindowJob inherits as its +0x50 push target (the dispatcher built our load job with r8=dialog+0x50 as ctx_parent -> the factory stored dialog+0x50 into MenuWindowJob+0x50). dialog+0x50 was already full (8) -> push #9 -> DLFixedVector.inl:662 'out of memory' -> crash.

THE FIX (grounded): the push (0x140733ff0) reads count at [rcx+0x48] where rcx=the vector=dialog+0x50, so COUNT IS AT dialog+0x50+0x48 = dialog+0x98 (read as u64). Before our job pumps (e.g. right before the Assign install in maybe_fire_tfc_continue), RESET *(dialog+0x98)=0 (or a low value) to make room -> the MenuWindowJob push at dialog+0x50 succeeds -> load proceeds. Caveat: orphans the existing dialog+0x50 entries (acceptable, we are leaving the title menu). Verified push disasm at 0x1407ad530: mov 0x130(rdi),rdx (window); 0x1407ad537 mov 0x50(rdi),rcx (=vector); 0x1407ad53b call 0x140733ef0 (insert(rcx=vector,rdx=window)); rdi=the MenuWindowJob. Also note 0x1407ad4c3 reads CSMenuMan [0x143d6b7b0] + 0x1407ad509 sets +0x90 input bitmap (confirms 0x143d6b7b0=CSMenuMan w/ input bitmap at +0x90). Relates: owner130-IS-drained-but-dlfixedvector-cap8-overflow, menuwindowjob-run-dlfixedvector-overflow, WARNING-0x1407adxxx-region-addresses-unreliable.

synthetic-xinput-injection-does-NOT-move-character-2026-07-18: DECISIVE (autoload-only + move-probe, user-confirmed 'I saw no movement'). The move-probe injected a forward left-stick via the XInputGetState slot-0 hook on a fully-loaded, rendered angrE (Midra's Manse), but the character DID NOT MOVE. moved_frames only blipped to 3 from the load's settle-teleport ([-21,16,-54]->[-5,0,-5]) then reset to 0 -- no sustained walking. So synthetic XInputGetState(0) stick injection does NOT reach the game's MOVEMENT system on this setup. CRITICAL REALIZATION: sq-repro's menu nav that 'worked' did NOT actually validate xinput -- it opened menus via SendInput/RawInput KEYBOARD (VK keys) and fired the Load-Profile route via a NATIVE call; the xinput fabrication was never proven to drive anything. So xinput injection has NEVER actually driven the game here. Likely cause: Steam Input intercepts/remaps controller input (the game reads Steam's virtual pad, not the raw xinputN.dll our hook patches), OR a physical controller on slot 0 overrides, OR movement is read via a different path than XInputGetState. IMPLICATION: the movement-proof-by-xinput-injection approach is blocked until the REAL input path is found. The user 'uses xinput' and it works for THEM (real controller through Steam Input), so injection must target that same layer. NEXT: determine the input path (Steam Input? which xinput DLL does the game load? does GetState(0) even get polled for movement?) and inject there; OR prove playability via a reliable RAM control-enabled flag instead of injected movement. Links [[critical-render-oracle-is-false-negative-real-signal-is-movement-2026-07-18]].

PROVEN 2026-06-28: the ER window off-screen shift is NOT caused by the er_effects DLL. Symptom: window maps correctly on DP-1 at logical (2,23) size 1024x576 (=1280x720/1.25), then ~2s later jumps to (-3069,23) = exactly -3072 logical (one monitor width), off the left of DP-1. A/B: vanilla ER with the DLL DISABLED (scripts/launch-vanilla-offline.sh) launched DETACHED by the agent (setsid, non-interactive) shifts off-screen IDENTICALLY. So the real variable is interactive-user-launch (DP-1 focused) vs agent-detached/unfocused launch -- the user's offline launcher works only because they run it with DP-1 focused. Monitor layout: DP-1 @ (0,0) FOCUSED, DP-3 @ (3072,0) logical, both scale 1.25 (DP-3 physical x=3840). Mechanism (Ghidra dump, runtime stack via temporary user32 hook in src/experiments/window_move_diag.rs): during boot ~+12.4s the GAME calls MoveWindow(x=3839,y=-1,1286x752) from the DXGI/swapchain path FUN_141e9d480 (dump entry 0x141e9d480; deobf return RVA 0x1e9d506) -- x=3839 ~= DP-3 physical offset 3840 = it places the window on the NON-focused monitor. Separately CSWindowImp window-mode apply FUN_140e89a80 (calls AdjustWindowRect/GetWindowLongW/SetWindowPos, uses CS::CSWindowImp::GetWindowHandle@dump 0x14266df20) requests SetWindowPos(0,0). Caller stacks are PURE GAME CODE -- no DLL frames -- so no DLL patch (foreground-force/title-cover/save-redirect) is in the move path. FIX DIRECTION (not a DLL change): make DP-1 the focused/active or primary output when the game selects its DXGI output during boot, OR launch with the target monitor focused. Temporary diagnostic window_move_diag (lib.rs spawn + experiments/mod.rs decl) should be reverted once done.

movement-proof-must-use-real-input-not-ram-write-2026-07-18: USER CORRECTION (2026-07-18): 'Are you sure that will be sufficient given a user doesn't edit ram to move?' -- writing the player MOVE-VECTOR / locomotion output directly in RAM is INSUFFICIENT/cheating for the playability proof: it proves locomotion works, NOT that INPUT moves the char, which is what 'playable' means to the user (they move via controller/keyboard). A RAM move-vector write would FALSE-PASS a char that is frozen to real input. The proof MUST drive movement through the game's REAL input path, the same as the user's physical controller/keyboard. So: (1) the faithful, cheapest path is OS-level KEYBOARD W via SendInput (RawInput) -- same as the user pressing W -- which reached the game for menus; test it CLEANLY (now non-focus-stealing) to see if it drives movement. (2) synthetic XInputGetState(0) reaches the game's slot-0 poll (361 polls) but movement doesn't happen -> likely the game's MOVEMENT reads via Steam Input (ISteamInput virtual pad), not the raw xinput DLL we hook; injecting at the game's real input-read boundary (the pad state the locomotion reads, post-Steam-Input) is acceptable ONLY if it is the same data a real device produces at that boundary (simulating the device), NOT the locomotion OUTPUT. Do NOT prove movement by writing the move vector. Links [[correction-keyboard-w-effect-unconfirmed-2026-07-18]] [[user-input-methods-xbox-elite-and-kbm-2026-07-18]].

DEFINITIVE static RE (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY). RECONCILES the task-brief NEW evidence (deserialize 0x14067b290 returns the constant default 0xa010000 at the title for ALL saves) with the prior slot9 recipe (where it returned the real map). ANSWERS: there is NO separate save-mount / load-all and NO separate main-menu-build system. Both are the SAME machine, and the save mount is gated behind the MoveMapStep which only exists after PlayGame.

=== Q1 SAVE-MOUNT: there is NO synchronous load-all; the mount IS the b80 async machine, and it lives INSIDE the MoveMapStep ===
The per-slot full-deserialize 0x14067b290(slot) allocs 0x280000, calls save-READ 0x14067b100 into it, then header-parse 0x14067bd70 -> [GameMan 0x143d69918 +0xc30]=saved map, then applies char 0x140e365c0 vs PlayerGameData 0x144588268.
save-READ 0x14067b100 (decoded full): cmp [GameMan+0xb80],3; jne 0x14067b174 (-> xor al,al; FAIL). Only when b80==3: if [GameMan+0xdf0]!=0 -> async-complete path (sets b80=0, ret 1); if df0==0 -> jumps 0x14067b138: call IO-device getter 0x140e6e060 then sync read 0x140e6e380 (which REQUIRES IO-request state 0x14240a1f0==0x14 'resident', else fails), sets b80=0, ret read result. So at the bare press-any-button title b80==0 -> 0x14067b100 returns 0 -> deserialize jumps 0x14067b452 (frees buf, al=0) -> [GameMan+0xc30] STAYS at new-game default 0xa010000. THAT is exactly the brief's constant-default observation, for empty/.co2/vanilla alike: the save bytes are not resident because the b80 mount has not run.
ALL slot reads (full AND menu previews) funnel through the SAME b80==3 + IO-resident gate. The preview/highlight reader 0x14067b570 (current_slot_load: GameMan+0xac0 slot, allocs 0x60000, reads via 0x140e6e060/0x140e6ec70) only sets b80=1 (REQUEST); 0x140e6ec70 is async (tail-jmps 0x140e6f760/0x140e6f940). Other requesters: 0x14067b750 (single slot, b80=1), 0x14067b940 (full+preview, b80=1), 0x14067bc0c (all-slot 0x240010 header region, b80=1, ZERO xrefs). NONE read disk inline; they only set b80=1.
THE MOUNT DRIVER: the b80 1->2->3 advance + IO device tick is the save-IO request DISPATCHER 0x140afa6d0, whose SOLE caller is 0x140aff730 INSIDE the MoveMapStep per-frame update 0x140aff640. recon_callscan 0x140afa6d0 = 1 caller; recon_refs 0x140aff640 = 1 ref (0x140af2be4, the MoveMapStep ctor 0x140af28d0 binding it as the update fn). So the entire save-IO/mount machine runs ONLY when the MoveMapStep object exists and is being ticked by the FD4 scheduler. The MoveMapStep is built by STEP_PlayGame(5) -> STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 (which also builds CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 via 0x140766010@0x140af2e47). CONSEQUENCE: there is NO boot/menu-init synchronous reader that populates the slot container 0x143d5df38 at the title; the menu previews are produced by this SAME b80 machine being requested+ticked once the menu/MoveMap phase is active.
Slot container 0x143d5df38 is a POINTER to a 0x158-byte manager (single store: ctor 0x140255d40 -> 0x141eb9ed0(0x158) -> ctor 0x140254680 -> mov [0x143d5df38],rax @0x140255d7b). Its 0x2A0-stride per-slot records are filled from the parsed save buffers by the b80 worker, not by a standalone writer. 723 reads, ~all loads (hot handle). So 'populate the container directly' is not a thing; you must drive the b80 mount.

=== Q2 MAIN-MENU BUILD: it is the SAME SimpleTitleStep advancing state, NOT a separate CSMenuMan/FeMan ===
The FE-host SimpleTitleStep (vtable 0x142b63bb0, step table 0x143d71580) builds the title-INTRO at STEP_InitMenu(0) 0x140b0cfa0 (4 light item delegates via 0x1407a91e0/0x140744a60; sink 0x140b0e530 copies to owner+0x130 and SetState(10)=MenuJobWait). At the bare title it sits at state 10 with INTRO items.
STEP_BeginTitle(3) 0x140b0c5b0 IS the main-menu build: it constructs the real Continue/Load/PlayGame items (item vtables 0x142b63f88, 0x142b63f50 PlayGame/Load), wires the selection callback into owner+0xe0 (the menu-job multicast) via 0x14081f9f0 (rdx=owner+0xe0, r8=owner+0x138; std::function builder 0x1407acb00), then sink 0x140b0e530 -> SetState(10). So 'Continue/Load Game with previews' is the same object at state 3->10, NOT a new manager. Continue/Load item ENABLEMENT uses the preview reader: item-factory 0x14082ba80 calls 0x14067b570 and builds descriptor kind 2 vs 3 from al.

=== Q3 PRESS-TRIGGERED ACTION SEQUENCE (detection -> menu/mount), entry points ===
At state 10 STEP_MenuJobWait 0x140b0d400 pumps the menu-job multicast owner+0xe0 (0x140733f20) and the UI item owner+0x130 (0x1407a9600) every frame; its ONLY SetState is ->11(Finish) gated on quit-latch 0x143d856a0. The press advances the machine NOT via a title SetState but via the INTRO item confirm 0x140b0b6b0: it calls 0x140765030 (input/decision, jump-table on [rcx]) -> if al SetState(owner,1) else SetState(owner,2) via 0x140b0d960, tail 0x140bbf0d0. This confirm is INPUT-GATED (fires when the bound press-any-button keycode reads pressed via inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0+0x90 keystate; see prior title-accept-is-fundamentally-input-gated). State 1/2 funnel to STEP_BeginTitle(3) which builds the main menu. Selecting Continue fires the PlayGame item (vtable 0x142b63f50, pump slot +0x10 = 0x140b0e180): chooses slot via 0x140678a50/0x140679560 -> stores slot into owner+0xbc, writes request bytes into the InGameStep child owner+0x2e0 (via 0x140ca7660: child+0xd8/+0xda flags, play-mode child+0xdb), SetState(owner,5)=STEP_PlayGame. STEP_PlayGame(5) 0x140b0d5b0 asserts inputmgr + 0x144588e98, calls 0x140e5f8f0/0x140e5f7f0/0x140678ca0(returns slot), and drives the InGameStep child toward STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 -> builds CSFeMan + MoveMapStep -> MoveMapStep update 0x140aff640 ticks dispatcher 0x140afa6d0 -> b80 mount runs -> deserialize succeeds -> world streams. STEP_GameStepWait(6) 0x140b0cde0 waits on child(owner+0x2e8)+0xd8 and the load flags 0x140679430/0x140679440.
ENTRY POINTS to invoke directly on the live SimpleTitleStep (rcx=obj): SetState(obj,3) via 0x140b0d960 OR call STEP_BeginTitle 0x140b0c5b0 to build the main menu; then PlayGame confirm 0x140b0e180 (sets owner+0xbc slot + child request + SetState 5) to start the load; OR SetState(obj,5) after manually setting owner+0xbc + child request bytes.

=== Q4 ZERO-INPUT VERDICT ===
The press-triggered ACTION is NOT itself a hard input gate -- it is plain SetState calls (1/2 -> 3 -> 5) on the SimpleTitleStep, all directly callable. The ONLY input gate is the DETECTION: which item-confirm fires (0x140b0b6b0) is decided by the inputmgr keystate. Therefore zero-input drive does NOT require synthesizing input at the action layer; it requires bypassing the detection by directly advancing the machine:
RECIPE (zero-input, on the live SimpleTitleStep obj, committed +0x48==10; scheduler 0x143d872e0, slot container 0x143d5df38, inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 non-null, quit-latch 0x143d856a0==0):
 1. SetState(obj,3) [0x140b0d960(obj,3)] OR call STEP_BeginTitle 0x140b0c5b0(obj) -> next pump builds the real Continue/Load menu + wires owner+0xe0 callbacks. (Lets the native preview reader run later.)
 2. Drive the PlayGame/Continue path: either call the PlayGame confirm 0x140b0e180(playgame_item) (it sets owner+0xbc=chosen slot, writes the InGameStep-child request, SetState(obj,5)), OR set owner+0xbc to the desired slot (most-recent via 0x140678a50/0x140679560, or ini override) + write the child request, then SetState(obj,5).
 3. Let the native pump run: STEP_PlayGame(5) -> StepWait -> InGameStep child -> STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 builds CSFeMan + MoveMapStep. Once MoveMapStep ticks (0x140aff640), its dispatcher 0x140afa6d0 advances b80 1->2->3 and mounts the save (IO request -> 0x14240a1f0==0x14 resident). NOW 0x14067b290 (driven by dispatcher-1 0x140afbad0) succeeds: [GameMan+0xc30]=the slot's REAL map + char applied. VERIFY: [GameMan+0xc30] != 0xa010000; slot container records populated; CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 != 0; child+0xd8 drains 1->2->0.
KEY RECONCILIATION: the prior slot9 recipe's 0x14067b290(9) returned the REAL map ONLY because it ran SetState(5) FIRST (building the ticking MoveMapStep that advanced b80 to 3); at the bare title with no MoveMapStep, b80 stays 0 and the same call returns the 0xa010000 default. So the brief's constant-default is the EXPECTED pre-mount state, fully consistent.
The remaining risk is the SAME async-IO completion wall (b80 stuck at 1 because the world-stream worker task 0x144842d40 is not registered/ticked at a forced FE-host) per async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026 -- i.e. driving SetState(5) at the synthetic title still needs the IngameInit-tail stream-worker registration. But for the char/save DATA (the brief's slot-map question), the cycle-breaker is: once CSFeMan exists from step 1-3, call 0x14067b290(slot) directly OR let dispatcher-1 run it; the constant-default disappears the moment the b80 machine reaches state 3, which the ticking MoveMapStep provides. The action layer needs NO synthesized input.
Related: scheduler-fe-host-is-simpletitlestep-decoded-2026, slot9-load-phase-machine-b80-csfeman-less-2026, async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026, title-accept-is-fundamentally-input-gated, menu-task-manager-architecture.

er-tpf crate spec (static-RE 2026-06-28, in-memory texture replacement, NO disk/Kraken). WHAT IT PRODUCES: an UNCOMPRESSED in-memory TPF003 byte blob wrapping ONE DDS = exactly the bytes the game holds AFTER it Oodle-decompresses a menu .dcx; the RASTER analog of er-gfx's Scaleform GFX MemoryFile (VECTOR) swap. Host-testable pure-Rust byte builder (std-only, mirrors crates/er-gfx); the GAME's own TPF path materializes the CSGxTexture/TexResCap/TpfFileCap from our bytes -- er-tpf builds NO C structs. Format: TPF header ("TPF\0", u32 totalDataSize, u32 textureCount=1, u8 platform=0 PC, ... extFlag bit0 CLEAR -> entry table @+0x10) + one Tpf003 entry + DDS payload ("DDS "+DDS_HEADER[+DXT10]); DXGI BC7_UNORM(98)/BC1_UNORM(71) typical, R8G8B8A8_UNORM(28) accepted; format read by Tpf003FileAccessor::DDSFormat->GetDDSFormat (dump 0x141e97ee0). CORRECTION 1: NO real CPU texture upload exists in the DLL today -- the old draw_calls=317/drawn telemetry was the DELETED hudhook ImGui overlay placeholder (git 38e2da3 src/overlay.rs draw_title_overlay_cover); constants.rs 641-642 marks TITLE_OVERLAY_COVER_* as removed. Only proven in-memory swap is the er-gfx GFX MemoryFile (vector). CORRECTION 2 (my earlier premise was WRONG): the loading-screen drawn texture slot is replaceTexInfo @ CSNowLoadingHelperImp+0xd8 (returned by GetReplaceTexInfo deobf 0x140909ea0), NOT requestedReplaceTexInfo +0xe0 (that is only the OUTGOING cross-fade copy that Update deobf 0x1402a2b80 retires on countdown; writing +0xe0 changes nothing drawn). The texture is a CSScaleformReplaceTexInfo (size 0x50, ctor deobf 0x140d74360) with TpfFileCap* @ +0x40, DLString name @+0x10, ready flag @+0x48. INTEGRATION ROUTES (in-process; all dump VAs need scripts/dump-deobf-shift.py before call/patch; the one deobf RVA we own is bind observer 0x7451c0): PRIMARY packed-TPF = hook GetReplaceTexInfo (0x140909ea0) return our CSScaleformReplaceTexInfo, built via GetOrCreateReplaceTexInfo (0x140d7c310)+LoadTpf (0x140d697d0). SECONDARY offscreen = GXCGTextureBuilder_TPF (dump 0x141a004e0)+InsertCSRuntimeTexResCapIfNotExists (deobf 0x1401e96e0) own a CSGxTexture/TexResCap(+0x78) under SYSTEX_Menu_* name, then promote bind observer 0x7451c0 observer->replacer (rewrite pair+0x30 target DLString). TIER PLAN: T0 host DDS encoder (uncompressed RGBA DXGI 28), T1 host TPF003 wrap (+self round-trip), T2 host mip chain, T3 host BCn (BC7/BC1), T4 DLL wire-to-seam (runtime-probe-gated, the genuinely new work). OPEN-RE gating T4: TpfFileCap layout + LoadFromMemoryStream (dump 0x1401cd130) to feed bytes with NO on-disk name; who sets +0x48 ready; exact Tpf003 per-entry layout; GetDDSFormat acceptance of our DXGI enum. Companion to er-menu-texture-pipeline-tpf-kraken.

Fix 1+2 (return-gadget recognition, committed 8f76428) CONFIRMED to convert functions: 1405aa580 was reassemble:jmp-into-arxan-unresolved (FAIL) in the seed-1337 baseline -> constant_pass post-fix. PROLOGUE RECONSTRUCTION recipe (validated statically; clean bake proof still pending): for 0x140df9940 the faithful frame is "push rbx; sub rsp,0x30" (prologue) + "add rsp,0x30; pop rbx; ret" (epilogue). The hand-injected .s is STRUCTURALLY CLEAN: both je paths converge on the ret, no loop, balanced stack; body [rsp+0x40] correctly lands in caller shadow space (entry_rsp+8) exactly like the original. CAPTURE ALGORITHM to generalize: emulate the entry trampoline (the function-entry jmp into arxan) until the first GAME instr = real body start; frame_size = net rsp delta (0x38 here); saved_regs = callee-saved regs (rbx,rbp,rsi,rdi,r12-r15) STORED in the prologue AND RESTORED symmetrically in the return trampoline (rbx only here; the trampoline's other push/pop of rax/r10 are scratch for the security-cookie+dispatch, NOT true saves). Reconstruct: push each saved reg; sub rsp,(frame_size - 8*nsaved); body; add rsp,...; pop regs in reverse; ret. The entry trampoline ALSO contains a gs:[0x58] security-cookie check gating a cmovle dispatch to the real block -- DROP that (recovery already resolved the real target); keep only the push/sub frame.

NOT a real function hang (static .s has no loop). Re-run prologue proofs in ISOLATION (no concurrent bake-corpus) or the timing is invalid. Classic "validate everything, do not trust a contaminated experiment". Related: arxan-prologue-epilogue-reconstruction-needed-2026-07-01, gadget-collapse-uncond-jmp-2026-07-01.

SPIKE er-effects-rs-vbo key finding: the ~12s single-threaded engine-init is NOT one fan-out-able loop -- it is SEQUENTIAL single-threaded PHASES (per-second main-thread RIP region histogram, dense run onscreen-capture-20260624-143025): sec0-2 ntdll memory bootstrap (~2s); sec2-4 LOW-.TEXT CRYPTO ~3s (0x14004xxxx region, hot op = asymmetric bignum multiply FUN_14004c600 = SIGNATURE/integrity verify, NOT regulation AES which has no bignum => Arxan/DRM integrity, OFFLINE-SKIPPABLE); sec5 ntdll; sec6-9 GRAPHICS ~4s (shader CRC FUN_141c90cd0 + memcpy + _wcsicmp_l, from GXCGShader); sec10 other engine; sec11-13 ntdll memory. IMPLICATIONS: (1) Arxan/integrity is ~3s ~25% of engine-init (much bigger than the earlier 9% bignum-leaf estimate) -> biggest single lever, and it is a SKIP not a parallelize; (2) top-level phase fan-out is blocked by sequential dependencies; (3) the ONLY real parallelization candidate is the ~4s graphics CRC+memcpy phase, viable ONLY if it is per-shader independent (unverified); (4) ntdll memory phases ~5s are allocator-bound, hard to parallelize. Main thread is CPU-pegged (34s/38s) so adding workers won't help -- work must be MOVED off main thread. Updates [[boot-windows-not-faster-2026-06-24]] [[boot-profiler-arxan-vs-asset-resolved-2026-06-24]].

Autoresearch static RE finding 2026-06-27: Followed offscreen+0x68 CS::CSSgEzDraw/CSEzDraw branch. CS::CSEzDraw ctors 0x140bb35f0/0x140bb3660 call InitBuffers 0x14264ee80, which allocates drawContext and two FD4HkEzDrawCommandBuffer objects. Draw entry 0x142650030 locks commandQueuemutex, selects a double buffer, and calls command replay 0x14269b4f0, which replays bufferStart..writePtr entries as opcode + function-pointer commands. Producers such as 0x14264f050/0x14264f3d0 and DrawLine/DrawSphere/QueueText push primitive/text commands; handlers 0x142699970/0x142699cc0 render via FD4HkEzDrawContext. CSSgEzDraw render entry 0x140bb6c10 only calls CSEzDraw::Draw on the embedded CSEzDraw at +8, and RendMan phase calls use the same EzDraw debug/easy-draw queues. Conclusion: CSSgEzDraw/CSEzDraw is an immediate-mode/debug command queue, not the loaded-character portrait model render target or direct texture/SRV bridge. Next: inspect CSMenuAsmModelRend model-instance update calls (FUN_1409ecad0/FUN_1409ecb60/FUN_1409e9ac0 and renderer+0xee/+0xef/+0x778/+0x948) or GXSgCompositeScene/GXSgLayerFlat traversal/draw calls instead.

ER PRIORITY-HIGH ALONE INSUFFICIENT (boosted run 2026-07-20): boost helper confirmed ER PID prio=High, but FPS still ~25 (load1 settled avg 25fps) and load2 stalled early (mms_max=3, LOADING_PROGRESS_STALLED_60S at 140s). So raising ONLY ER's priority does not out-compete the WSL2 VM host (vmmem/vmmemWSL) which runs ALL the parallel cargo threads at Normal -- the scheduler still splits cores. NEXT LEVER: lower the WSL2 VM host process priority (vmmem) to BelowNormal/Idle OR cap its cores, so ER's High decisively wins -- this is the OTHER side of the contention. vmmem is likely SYSTEM-owned (may need admin/Task Manager). Testing powershell access; if blocked, user (who offered to help) sets Vmmem priority Below Normal in Task Manager Details tab, or reduces cargo -j / parallel agent count. affinity-confining ER alone does NOT isolate it (cannot set vmmem affinity without admin). bd deterministic-control-boost-er-windows-priority-affinity-powershell.

TELEMETRY at frozen state: oracle_char_name=angrE, oracle_player_present=True, oracle_saved_map_c30=0x1c000000, system_quit_continue_confirm_fresh_deser_done=1/count=1, system_quit_quickload_phase=0(IDLE, completion latch fired), system_quit_quickload_selected_slot=0, return_title_request_count=1, product_autoload_armed=True, BUT oracle_msgbox_total_builds went 0->1 (a MessageBox built during reload = hard investigation trigger per AGENTS.md). FOUNDATION PROVEN (2026-06-29, run product-continue-direct-151740, manual ProfileSelect). End-to-end capture of the loaded character's LIVE 3D head WORKS: force-build slot0 (Mark 0x262250 + refresh 0x9aa680 on base+0x3d6d8d0) -> the renderer composites the FaceData head into a 256x256 offscreen -> the scan-by-d3d-vtable picks the COLOR RT (0x..b300 fmt=28 R8G8B8A8) and correctly SKIPS the same-size DEPTH sibling (fmt=19 R32G8X24) -> D3D12 readback -> dump_portrait_rgba wrote portrait-capture.bin (256x256 RGBA8) -> converted to PNG = a REAL 3D character head (eyes/brow/nose/mouth/neck visible). PNG: scratchpad/portrait-capture.png (delivered to user).

KEY FIXES that cracked it: (1) scan from the OFFSCREEN (renderer+0xa8) not the gx sub-nest (gx+0x10 holds only 1x1 vkd3d dummies); (2) reject DEPTH formats / accept only color (28/29/87/91) so 'largest texture2d' doesn't grab the 256x256 depth buffer over the 256x256 color RT; (3) force-build IS needed (natural ProfileSelect leaves base+0x3d6d8d0 idle -- the displayed face comes from elsewhere, but our FORCED render of slot0's FaceData IS the character head we want).

CAVEAT: the captured head is UNDERLIT (mean RGB ~27/255, dark) -- the forced render lacks the menu's lighting/IBL setup. Lighting/exposure is a lever to fix.

This unlocks the interactive-face vision (bd loading-screen-interactive-face-product-vision-2026-06-29): the renderer + model_ins(renderer+0x778) are the handles for higher-res, custom camera, eye/face look-at, deformation. NEXT LEVERS (order): fix lighting; higher-res render (override offscreen size, per-slot w/h param DAT_143b39840); custom camera (RE renderer view matrix); display on now-loading (forge -- needs keep-alive for the LIVE/interactive version, the hard GxDrawContext-past-menu problem). Code: gpu_readback.rs (scan+readback+color filter), startup_hooks.rs (force_profile_render_tick enumerate + maybe_capture + dump_portrait_rgba), all gated force_profile_render/portrait_real_pixels (default OFF). Relates: base-3d6d8d0-is-title-table... (CORRECTED: force-build composites a real head even though the natural table is idle).

SEMAPHORE SPLIT DIRECTIVE (user 2026-07-19): differentiate THREE distinct signals that the current single can_move oracle conflates: (1) CAN_MOVE = CAPABILITY -- is the player in-world/controllable right now (control enabled, not in menu/cutscene/load), read from GAME STATE INDEPENDENT of our injection; (2) SUPPLIED_MOVEMENT_INPUT = did WE actually inject/supply movement input this window (our probe wrote the stick/key); (3) DID_MOVE = did the player's position ACTUALLY change (havok displacement > threshold). This disambiguates the current failure: user confirms can_move=TRUE (camera responds to mouse) but did_move=FALSE with supplied_movement_input=TRUE -> the injection is not taking effect (pad-stick ignored on native kb+mouse). Emit all three as separate oracle_* fields; gate sq-repro's WAIT_WORLD->drive on CAN_MOVE (capability), NOT on did_move (so a non-registering test-move doesn't block the whole load2 drive). Then the injection-layer bug (pad stick vs WASD keyboard/merged field input) is isolated to supplied=true+did_move=false. See [[load1-not-stalled-oracles-false-negative-padstick-ignored-on-kbmouse-2026-07-19]].

DEFORM LEVER DESIGN (user, 2026-06-29): for the mouse-based face deformation, use the SAME bounce-back / elastic spring physics as Super Mario 64's face-poke screen. Reference: https://github.com/n64decomp/sm64 (the SM64 title-screen Mario head is the 'goddard'/gd dynamic-mesh engine -- grab a vertex, it follows the cursor, on release it springs back with DAMPED oscillation). 

IMPLICATION: this is the TRUE MESH-POKE path (direct vertex displacement + spring-back), NOT the parametric FaceData-slider path the roadmap's 'deform' lever described. So the deform lever has two flavors and the user wants the HARD one: a custom spring/elastic vertex-deformation layer over the live model_ins mesh (grab nearest vertex to cursor -> pull -> release -> Hooke's-law damped return), modeled on SM64's gd_vec/spring code. Port the SM64 spring constants/damping for feel. This needs: access to the model's vertex/skeleton data (model_ins = CSChrAsmModelIns at renderer+0x778), a per-frame deformation pass, and the look-at/cursor mapping. Heavier than slider edits but it's the heart of the SM64-face vision. matbin->shaderbdle resolution is NOT a pure name rule (measured 2026-06-25 over 15103 matbins vs 750 base bundles). matbin shader names: C[DetailBlend] x3009, M[AMSN_V][Ov_N], M[AMSN_V], C[DetailBlend]_cloth, M[AMSN]..., i.e. prefixes C[..] and M[..]. Bundles are CS[..][quals]. Naive C[X]->CS[X]: only 1 exact, 5886/15103 (~39%) match by first-bracket-token prefix, 9216 unresolved. The bundle qualifier brackets ([Rich],[VA_Frame],[S2],[Ov_N]...) encode the FLVER VERTEX-ATTRIBUTE LAYOUT + quality tier, so the engine selects the compiled bundle from SPX + the mesh's vertex format, NOT the matbin name alone (same material -> different bundle per mesh vertex layout). Also only base shaderbdle.shaderbdlebnd extracted (750); +dlc01(84)+dlc02(118)+_[rt] variants exist (e.g. C[Detail3Blend] has no base bundle). IMPLICATION: M1 trace (shader/material -> objects) is fully solvable at matbin level (matbin binder path gives object family; FLVER materials ref matbins). Exact compiled-bundle selection is an M3 render concern -- resolve empirically per-FLVER: load FLVER vertex layout + material, match bundle by qualifiers, else list candidate bundles. Needs SPX file parse to be exact. See bd shaderbdle-is-per-material-compiled-bundle-2026-06-25.

AGENTS.md: every CS::MessageBoxDialog before/after char load is a hard trigger; zero-msgbox is product proof. Test: correlate oracle_msgbox_total_builds / msgbox-skip / connection-error events to the ~430ms pre-revert window on load2 vs load1; if a msgbox/network-error/unsaved-quit message builds only on load2, that is the interrupt. Fix direction: satisfy/skip the message's native side-effect (not UI), and ensure the reload is not treated as an online session or an unsaved-quit.

er-tpf Tier-4 Route-B 3-caveat static-RE CONFIRMED 2026-06-28 (Ghidra pc_eldenring_runtime dump; call/patch addrs grounded via dump-deobf-shift.py). VERDICT: GO with conditions on the Route-B in-memory TPF->GPU DLL call.

CAVEAT 1 DXGI ACCEPTANCE = PASS. GetDDSFormat(dump 0x141e97ee0)(ddsPtr): ddsPtr = start of a STANDARD DDS file INCLUDING 4-byte 'DDS ' magic (it reads pf.dwFlags @+0x50, fourcc @+0x54, RGBBitCount @+0x58, R/G/B/A masks @+0x5c/+0x60/+0x64/+0x68, DX10 dxgiFormat @+0x80). DX10 path (pf.dwFlags&4 && fourcc=='DX10'/0x30315844) reads dxgiFormat@+0x80 and passes it through FUN_141e983a0. FUN_141e983a0(dump 0x141e983a0) is a PURE IDENTITY passthrough for enums 1..0x63(99): returns input unchanged, default(0 and >=100)=0=UNKNOWN. So DX10 dxgi 28(R8G8B8A8_UNORM=0x1c), 71(BC1_UNORM=0x47), 98(BC7_UNORM=0x62), 99(BC7_SRGB=0x63) ALL ACCEPTED; >=100 rejected. LEGACY (non-DX10) uncompressed RGBA8: pf.dwFlags has DDPF_RGB(0x40) set, RGBBitCount==0x20, R=0xff G=0xff00 B=0xff0000 A=0xff000000(or 0) -> returns 0x1c(28) DIRECTLY and DOES NOT call FUN_141e983a0. => SAFEST first proof = uncompressed RGBA8 via LEGACY DDS header (bypasses the remap validator entirely); BC7 REQUIRES a DX10 header (still accepted).

CAVEAT 2 TPF003 ENTRY-NAME OFFSET = PINNED. Header (validated by FUN_141e94be0 dump 0x141e94be0): +0x00 'TPF\0'; +0x04 u32 dataSize(!=0); +0x08 u32 textureCount(!=0); +0x0c u8 platform==0(PC, selects AttributeAccessor_Win32); +0x0d u8 version==3 REQUIRED(TPF003); +0x0e u8 ENCODING (DLCharacterSet; ==UNICODE_STRING_ENCODING -> UTF-16LE names, else single-byte read as Shift-JIS/ASCII; malformed-header default = ENCODING_WINDOWS_31J). +0x0f u8 flag3: bit0 set -> entry table starts at tpf+0x30 instead of tpf+0x10. Per-entry name: AttributeAccessor_Win32 vtable[5] name-offset getter = FUN_141e93a20 returns *(u32*)(currentEntry+0x0c); name read FUN_141e93f40 returns name_ptr = tpf_base + that_u32 (ABSOLUTE offset-to-string from file base, NUL-terminated). Fixed entry-record header size = vtable[7] FUN_141e93a40 = 0x14 bytes. So: write the repo key as a NUL-terminated string somewhere in the blob and put its file-relative offset as u32 at entry+0x0c; for an ASCII key set encoding byte +0x0e to a non-Unicode value (ASCII-safe via Shift-JIS), or use UTF-16 with encoding==UNICODE. This name becomes the GLOBAL_TexRepository key when FUN_140b81110 is called with name=NULL (it derives key from FUN_141a00950(builder)).

CAVEAT 3 RENDER-THREAD AFFINITY = GO-with-conditions; NOT inline-D3D12 in create step. Chain: GXCGTextureBuilder_TPF ctor(deobf 0x141a004c0) is pure CPU TPF parse, no GPU, safe anywhere. FUN_140b81110(deobf)/FUN_140b80b70: TexRepo insert is mutex-protected (DLPlainLightMutex @repo+0xa0); allocs GXTexture2D(GfxHeap), TexResCap(MainHeap), CSGxTexture(GfxHeap); calls GXTexture2D vtbl[0x16]=FUN_1419efc70 -> builder vtbl[0x50]=FUN_141a00630 -> FUN_141e94190 (assembles CPU mip-ptr staging in DLTemporaryHeap/TLS + descriptor) -> factory FUN_141e87da0(default) or 0x141a007b0(TPF override) -> FUN_141e898a0 -> FUN_141ea9860 which only CONSTRUCTS a DLCG3::CGTexture2D wrapper (copies descriptor+staging ptrs, sets vftable) -- NO ID3D12Device::Create*/command-queue call inline; SRV built lazily (FUN_141e88370->CGShaderResourceView). => FromSoft CG layer is DEFERRED-upload: D3D12 residency happens later in the render pass, so the path is NOT render-thread-pinned at device level. CORROBORATION: the engine ITSELF drives this exact raw-(ptr,len) path: CreateTpfResCap(deobf entry 0x140b83770)(GLOBAL_TpfRepository, L'FaceGenTexture', BND4base+dataOffset, size,...) from the FaceGen/chargen model build (FUN_1400a3400->FUN_140ae98a0->FUN_1401ec840), i.e. off the present thread. CONDITIONS for our CSTaskImp call: (1) only after graphics system fully initialized (GLOBAL_RenderingSystemAllocator/GLOBAL_GfxHeapAllocator/GLOBAL_TexRepository live -- in-game/past title gfx init, NEVER DllMain/loader thread); (2) residual risk = GfxHeap + CG descriptor/upload-ring allocators (DAT_144801248 family) may assume serialized access vs the render thread's upload-drain -> race = crash vector; mitigate by making the first call guarded and ideally on a task slot serialized with the engine's own texture work, fallback marshal to render/resource task if unstable.

GROUNDED CALL/PATCH ADDRS (dump->deobf via dump-deobf-shift.py, content-unique): GXCGTextureBuilder_TPF 0x141a004e0->0x141a004c0(-0x20); wrapper FUN_140b83ec0 0x140b83ec0->0x140b83dd0(-0xf0); register FUN_140b81200 0x140b81200->0x140b81110(-0xf0). CAUSAL CHAIN of the render-handoff freeze (runtime-localized 2026-07-18, bd er-effects-rs-um9g), root traced to the WORLD-RES LOADLIST VIRTUAL PATH: redirect/fast load leaves worldloadlistlistVirtualPath (InGameStep+0x220, size field) EMPTY/stale for the target map -> STEP_MoveMap_LoadlistInit only builds the loadlist when +0x220.size != 0, so it SKIPS -> loadlistFileCap (InGameStep+0x238) stays 0 -> CS::WorldInfoOwner::ProcessMsbLoadLists (0x14066b2c0) never creates the dest block-res -> block load-state NULL (blk_ls=0) -> STEP_WorldResWait (MoveMapStep child step 3, [MoveMapStep+0x48]==3) never resolves -> product-core-autoload 'waiting for core readiness state=6/6' forever -> chr_ins.load_state.draw_group_enabled (ChrIns+0x1b4 bit0) never set + loading cover never lifts = present-but-invisible frozen world (world sim/physics/play_time STILL advancing; only render dead). REPRODUCED on a PURE save_redirect BOOT (mms 1->2->STUCK 3; own_load_continue_fired=False). Reactive ProcessMsbLoadLists at the stall AVs (mid-stream ResetAreaResLists). FIX must run at the INIT point: populate worldloadlistlistVirtualPath (+0x220) with the TARGET map's path (derived from GameMan+0xc30) BEFORE STEP_MoveMap_LoadlistInit, so the game's own LoadlistInit->ProcessMsbLoadLists builds the dest block-res and WorldResWait resolves and the render handoff fires naturally. RE agent (render-handoff-freeze-2026-07-18) resolving: which native call normally writes +0x220 from the map id, and draw-group ordering. Links [[step3-reactive-processmsbloadlists-crashes-init-point-fix-needed-2026-07-17]] [[runtime-render-gate-validated-boot-worldreswait-freeze-2026-07-18]].

LOAD2 TEST-DRIVE CRITERIA (user 2026-07-19, from live run samechar-3x-threedll-20260719-185504): the input driver must (1) MOVE THE PLAYER FORWARD for >=60 frames minimum (proves load1 is genuinely playable/can-move), THEN (2) NAVIGATE the menu to LOAD THE SAME CHARACTER (angrE) again = load2. FIX: presence-gate prove_movement_enabled() + probe_foreground_enabled() on harness_dll_present() too (same decoupled toggle as system_quit_repro_enabled). The move-forward-60-frames IS the can-move probe; verify its frame count matches the >=60 criterion. See [[goal-is-user-like-input-path-not-zero-input-2026-07-19]].

AUTORESEARCH FINDING 2026-06-25: GenericListSelectDialog ctor decode corrected the prior initial-index interpretation. `FindByName GenericListSelectDialog` resolves the ctor at dump 0x14078c560. Signature: GenericListSelectDialog(param_1, SceneObjProxy* param_2, SceneObjProxy* param_3, byte param_4, undefined8 param_5, undefined1 param_6, undefined1 param_7). FUN_14078c2e0 passes literal 0 as param_6, but the ctor does not store it as an obvious selected-row index. Instead it constructs MenuWindow, sets GenericListSelectDialog vtable, constructs a SceneObjProxy from param_3, then calls FUN_140735b90(&param_1->field1_0xa38, sceneObj, param_6, auStack_b0, ...), constructs field1986_0x1200 SceneObjProxy, and calls FUN_14077f020(param_1,param_7). FUN_140735b90 initializes a list/control subobject: early fields param_1[0x19]=0, param_1[0x1a]=0, *(param_1+0xd8)=1, *(param_1+0xdc)=1; param_3 (the literal 0 from FUN_14078c2e0) is passed into FUN_140733740(..., "LeftArrow", param_3, ...), while additional helpers bind arrows/hit arrows and scene object proxies. FUN_14077f020 chooses different animation/function chains based on param_7, not row selection. Therefore literal ctor argument 0 is NOT yet proven to be an initial selected-row index; prior memory `autoload-commandlist-constructor-initial-index-zero-2026-06-25` should be treated as over-strong/static-evidence-only and superseded by this correction. Remaining evidence still proves Continue is appended as row 0 when a saved character exists, but the selection/default behavior must be found in GenericList/List-control state/update paths, likely field1_0xa38 vtable/update or MenuWindow run (`FUN_140733ff0`, `FUN_140735b90` subobject, `FUN_1407ad070`/`Run`). Next target: decode field1_0xa38 list-control vtable/update or add a passive runtime oracle at the constructed GenericListSelectDialog to log active/selected row; do not treat param_6=0 as selection proof and do not cold-call row builders.

PER-SLOT STATS FROM SAVE (verified offline 2026-07-04 against real save-files/*/ER0000.sl2 via er_save_loader::bnd4::slot_body). ER PC .sl2 is PLAINTEXT BND4 (no AES); USER_DATA{000..009} bodies = 0x280000 bytes. PlayerGameData in the slot body: name@PGD+0x9c (UTF16), level@PGD+0x68 (i32), 8 attributes@PGD+0x3c (contiguous i32: VIG MND END STR DEX INT FAI ARC). CRITICAL: the PGD base offset in the slot body is NOT fixed (seen 0xe0b6,0xe44e,0xe750,0xe81c,0xe894,0xe8a4 across slots/saves) -- variable-length pre-PGD data. The fixed continue_load.rs SAVE_BODY_PLAYER_GAME_DATA_OFFSET=0xebae is WRONG for per-slot reads (gave garbage). LOCATE the PGD dynamically via the ER invariant RUNE LEVEL == sum(8 attributes) - 79 (holds EXACTLY for every class/level tested: lvl6 Astro, lvl7 Hero/Pro, lvl9 Vagabond/Menace, lvl45, lvl90 Dark Moon Bean): scan the body for 8 consecutive i32 all in [1,99] where i32@(pos+0x2c)==sum-79 (0x2c = level 0x68 - statbase 0x3c); name at pos+0x60. Empty slots have no such block. INFRA already in repo: own_load.rs own_load_read_sl2_bytes(base) locates+reads the live .sl2 at runtime; er_save_loader::bnd4::slot_body(sl2,slot) slices plaintext bodies. Row->slot mapping: FUN_1408758d0(param_1 row-model, param_2 proxy) has slot index at *(int*)(param_1+8) (drives Icon_0 face frame = profile slot). Related: [[profileselect-per-slot-stats-not-native-2026-07-04]] [[profile-select-05010-layout-fonts-RE-2026-07-04]]

REAL CONTINUE/LOAD DESERIALIZE DRIVER (re-verified 2026-06-15; prior content of this key was accidentally clobbered by a bodyless 'bd remember <key>' and is rewritten from fresh recon). One per-frame load-request manager (singleton 0x143d6b880) arbitrates ALL load lanes via dispatcher 0x140afb880 + driver 0x140afbac4 (both called from parent 0x140aff640 at ~0x140aff720). Dispatcher 0x140afb880 (gated [0x143d856a0]==0 i.e. latch CLEAR) priority branches: r15b=getter 0x1406793d0 (+0xb72 force) -> current_slot_load 0x14067b570 (autoload/Continue, async, reads slot [GameMan+0xac0]); bl=0x140679370 (+0xb73) -> 0x14067b940; sil=(0x1406793c0 returns [GameMan+0xb78]!=-1) -> 0x14067b030; bpl=0x140679360 -> sync. Driver 0x140afbac4: gate1 0x140679ff0 (== [GameMan+0xb80]==2), gate2 0x140679180 (async worker poll on [GameMan+0xdf0]; sets +0xb80=3 on fail), then 0x140afbc3e calls SYNC DESERIALIZE 0x14067b290(ecx=slot from [mgr+0x12c]) -- the SOLE call site (recon_callscan confirms 1). 0x14067b290: allocs 0x280000 buf, reads save (0x14067b100), WRITES slot into [GameMan+0xac0], calls 0x14067bd70 (writes saved map id->[GameMan+0xc30], gated [0x143d68078]!=0), applies stats/inventory 0x140e365c0 vs PlayerGameData [0x144588268]. The MoveMapListStep menu lane (STEP_LoadSaveData 0x140af19b0) only POLLS [GameMan+0xb80]/[+0xdf0] via 0x14067b200 and advances steps; it never calls 0x14067b290 directly -- the actual byte-deserialize always happens in driver 0x140afbac4. So menu-load and autoload CONVERGE on the same deserialize. The whole graph is SESSION-scoped (CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 + session 0x1447ef360 null at the pre-bootstrap title). Related: native-autoload-slot-ac0-plus-force-flag, native-continue-and-slotn-recipe-2026, continue-load-subsystem-session-scoped.

CORRECTED 2026-06-15 -- the earlier diagnosis (environmental tick-120 ceiling) was WRONG. The real cause was the title-accept latch write crashing the game every run. Due to the .envrc bulk-source clobber ([[envrc-bulk-source-clobber-footgun]]), every runtime run after 303 was forced to .auto/runtime-env.title-proceed-gate, so they all fired title_proceed_gate -- which writes [0x143d856a0]=1 at inner state 10 tick ~90, and the game exited ~tick 105-120 (about 15 ticks / 0.25s later) every time. The supposed read-only control was NOT read-only (same clobber), which is why it also exited early and falsely looked environmental. CONCLUSION -- the session was NOT unstable. The latch poke (lever 1) RELIABLY CRASHES the game shortly after the write (confirmed across runs 304 attempt2, control, and the mislabeled ingamestep run). This matches the static prediction that poking the latch decouples the presenter-finalize and runs Finish/PlayGame with un-set-up presenter singletons. Lever 1 is FALSIFIED at runtime, not inconclusive. Runs 302/303 ran the full 30s because they predated the clobber (read-only selectbot, no latch). REFINED DIVERGENCE + CORRECTION HYPOTHESIS (verified, run 20260720-101944 SWITCH-ORACLE timestamps). Distinguisher between the two loads at MoveMapStep step 18:
- LOAD1 (boot autoload, +76330ms): mms_step=18 with rt5d(menuData+0x5d)=1 then end5e(menuData+0x5e=cVar10)=1, WARP=0 -> inner finalize (FUN_140afa7c0) walks, mms_step advances 18->19(CLEANUP)->20(FINISH). COMPLETES via the NON-WARP rt5d/end5e path. bc4 3->0.
- LOAD2 (reload, +81871ms): STARTS at mms_step=1(MSB LOAD) player=false with WARP=1 (the reload is a warp to saved pos); by the time it reaches mms_step=18 (player=true) warp is consumed AND rt5d=0 end5e=0 -> ALL cVar10 inputs 0 -> case 0 returns -> FROZEN at 18.
ROOT: the load2 handoff commit ("native Continue handoff commit OK #1") AUTOCLEARS menuData+0x5d/+0x5e (rt5d/end5e) AND warp_requested. Clearing warp was the post-switch teardown-loop fix, but it ALSO clears rt5d/end5e -- the exact driver load1 uses to finish. So we OVER-CLEARED: killed load2's finalize driver.
KEY PROOF the correction is safe: LOAD1 completed 18->19->20 with WARP=0 (rt5d/end5e drove it, no warp) -> the finalize CAN complete via the non-warp path WITHOUT the case-8 warp-reload teardown loop. 
CORRECTION HYPOTHESIS (to test, not yet applied): for load2 at mms18, keep WARP cleared (avoid the warp-reload loop) but DO NOT autoclear menuData+0x5d/+0x5e -- let rt5d/end5e stand (or set end5e=1) so the finalize advances 18->19->20 the same non-warp way load1 does. Verify on a run: load2 should reach mms_step 20(FINISH) and become movable (can_move f60) without a teardown/warp-loop. New epoch/fin12a tags added to SWITCH-ORACLE (uncommitted build) to make the load1-vs-load2 split unambiguous next run. Supersedes the "keep cVar10=0 for load2 movable" premise entirely. RUNTIME DOUBLE-FALSIFICATION (2026-06-16, own-stepper FORCING runs, vanilla .sl2 swap). The cold slot-int b80-primitive mount at the bare title is a DEAD END in vanilla -- the deserialize-ready iodev field io18 [iodev 0x1445893b0 +0x18] is NEVER populated cold, and the poll 0x140679180 (which reads io18+io20) cannot advance b80->3 without it.

RUN 2 evidence (/tmp/er-ownstep.log, slot=1, save-files/25): drive-init preview 0x14067b4e0(1) -> b80=1 + populated io10=0x7ffec6b854b0 + io20=0x7ffec6b85750 but io18=0x0. drive 0: b80=1 io10+io20 set io18=0. drive 60: b80=0, io10/io18/io20 ALL CLEARED -- the b80==1 lane driver 0x140679510 (called when b80==1) drained the preview read to resident AND TORE DOWN the request (reset b80->0, cleared io). drive 60..540: b80=0, io all 0 -- 0x14067b200(1) (called when b80==0) NEVER advanced b80 0->2 (session-gated: it checks 0x1402567b0/0x140261cd0 which fail at the bare title, so no b80=2 + no io18). drive TIMEOUT at 600, c30 unset. No SetState -> no save write (save hashes byte-identical before/after, confirmed).

RUN 1 evidence: 0x14067b1a0(slot) cold -> b80=2 but io10=io18=io20=0 (file-device-mgr queue, no iodev population).

CONCLUSION: io18 (the b80=2 deserialize-ready handle the poll reads) is populated ONLY by (a) the menu/session machinery -- 0x14067b200's session-gated lane in a live menu/session, or the MoveMapStep dispatcher orchestration on the REAL owner; or (b) ERSC under Seamless (the capture showed 0x14067b1a0 populate io18 because ERSC redirects the IO). NEITHER vanilla cold initiator (0x14067b4e0 preview = io10+io20 only; 0x14067b1a0 = nothing; 0x14067b200 = session-gated no-op cold) populates io18. The preview lane driver 0x140679510 TEARS DOWN the request, so 'preview then reuse' does not work cold either.

VERDICT: stop hand-driving b80 primitives cold. The mount fundamentally needs the in-context native flow. Two real paths remain: (A) set GameMan+0xb78=realslot BEFORE SetState(5) so the REAL MoveMapStep's NATIVE dispatcher-2 b78-route (real owner, NOT synthetic -> no save-write bug) populates io18 + deserializes the real slot in-context (the own-stepper SetState(5) milestone already builds the real MoveMapStep whose real dispatchers DO populate io18 -- it loaded the placeholder map; the fix is feeding the real slot via b78); (B) drive the native MoveMapListStep menu (autoload-native-menu-mount-drive-design-2026). Path A is the cleaner next experiment: minimal, uses the real owner/dispatchers, no synthetic owner. RUNTIME (2026-06-23, user press -> crash on owner+0x130 attempt). TWO findings: (1) owner+0x130 is NOT a valid MenuJobQueue -- PushBackJob(rcx=owner+0x130) ACCESS-VIOLATED at 0x1407a8a8e (inside the deque-push internals 0x7a89xx). owner+0x130 is the menu ITEM LIST (TITLE_OWNER_MENU_LIST_130_OFFSET, where BeginLogo commits Continue/Load/NewGame), NOT a job queue -- the post-primitive subagent was WRONG about owner+0x130 being a queue. dialog+0x10 IS the valid queue (PushBackJob there returned ret=0x2, no crash). REVERT post target to dialog+0x10.

(2) KEY HYPOTHESIS (matches user timing hint 'cant add the job until after press, immediately after'): the builder 0x1409ac760 has NEVER fired in ANY run, AND posting the selector-built job to dialog+0x10 produced NO load (only menu preview 67bd70 r8=0x215e01). => the dispatcher 0x1409b3070 is taking its ABORT branch (0x9b30d5: call 0x1407a7250 builds an EMPTY/wait job), NOT the BUILD/LOAD branch (0x9b30eb). The branch is gated by IsNotReleaseFlag55 0x14082cd60: LOAD branch (jne 0x9b30eb) iff (*ret)!=0 iff *(u8*)(tfc+0x18c)==0. At our timing (lever fires at a40==1, AFTER the open-menu registrar ran) tfc+0x18c is likely SET non-0 by the menu-open path (subagent noted the open-menu sets a tfc+0x18e byte near 0x18c) -> dispatcher aborts -> empty job -> posting does nothing. This is why all attempts produced no load + no builder/factory hits.

FIX: in maybe_fire_tfc_continue, FORCE *(u8*)(tfc+0x18c)=0 BEFORE calling the selector (guarantees IsNotReleaseFlag55->1->dispatcher BUILD branch -> real LoadGame job 0x140826510). Keep post to dialog+0x10. Then the builder 0x9ac760 + factory 0x826510 should fire, the job is real, and the pump drains it -> deser+world. const TFC_NOT_RELEASE_FLAG_18C_OFFSET=0x18c. RUNTIME HARNESS TRAP (2026-06-17). .envrc auto-sources ALL .envs/*.env in ALPHABETICAL glob order (set -a; source each). .envs/zzz-runtime-target.env is the RUNTIME TARGET SELECTOR -- it sets AUTO_RUNTIME_ENV_FILE and, being 'zzz', sorts LAST so it WINS over any other .env's AUTO_RUNTIME_ENV_FILE. .auto/runtime_probe.sh sources .envrc THEN sources $AUTO_RUNTIME_ENV_FILE as its profile. So to select which runtime-env profile the probe uses, EDIT .envs/zzz-runtime-target.env (NOT just your own .env -- yours is overridden by zzz). Symptom of the trap: probe ignored my .envs/savedata-snapshot.env (AUTO_RUNTIME_ENV_FILE + LAUNCH_MODE) and ran whatever profile zzz pointed at (was .auto/runtime-env.safe-confirm -> LAUNCH_MODE=direct), wasting 2 launches. FIX: set zzz-runtime-target.env AUTO_RUNTIME_ENV_FILE='.auto/runtime-env.<your-profile>'. Original value before my snapshot work = '.auto/runtime-env.safe-confirm' (backup at target/zzz-runtime-target.env.bak) -- RESTORE it after snapshot work if the prior session's safe-confirm target should persist. To verify resolution: bash -c '. ./.envrc; RUNTIME_ENV_FILE=${AUTO_RUNTIME_ENV_FILE}; . $RUNTIME_ENV_FILE; echo $LAUNCH_MODE'. Related: offline-launcher-and-direct-mode-dinput-crash-2026.

SOAK RESULT (2026-07-05, branch feat/boot-progress-view, commit 6f80359): the boot-view early self-present pump + crash-proof swapchain validator is STABLE -- 5/5 clean runs across 5 save files (Stink Bean/90-Hero, Da BEAST/200-BEAST, Vagabond/25, Vagabond/45-Slots, Banon/150-Banon) and BOTH slot 0 and slot 1. Every run identical: boot bar first-draw ~+1.08s (was +3.8s detour-only, ~2.6s sooner), swapchain found+hooked at pump+~0.56s, 166-169 self-presents on the game swapchain via the ORIGINAL Present, clean handoff to the detour path (oracle_boot_view_pump_stop_reason=1) at ~+3.85s when the game's own render loop starts presenting, all milestones latched (mask 0x1f), loading-portrait window picks up (oracle_overlay_draw_hits>0), zero crashes, zero msgboxes. KEY FIX: the swapchain FIND must NOT QueryInterface early-boot candidates -- half-constructed objects have garbage vtables and QI dispatches through them -> SEH access violation catch_unwind can't catch (crashed twice at ~+700ms). swapchain_vtable_matches() reads vtable[8]/[22] and compares to the resolved shared DXVK Present/Present1 addrs (all dxgi swapchains share one vtable under vkd3d-proton) -- pure read+compare, never a call. Soak used ER_EFFECTS_BLOCK_INPUT=1 (force input lock) + teardown at the boot-view->loading-window handoff; force-block did NOT starve the native continue. See bd continue-present-stall-no-frames-setstate5-to-keyed-2026-07-05 for why CONTINUE stays oracle-only.

CAMERA LEVER RE -- VERIFIED (2026-06-29, corrects interactive-face-levers-roadmap-2026-06-29 which was WRONG). The roadmap's camera FUN_ addresses were garbled (dump-vs-deobf-space confusion). GROUND TRUTH from Ghidra dump pc_eldenring_runtime.1.16.1.exe + dump-deobf-shift.py:

CSMenuProfModelRend (renderer R, BYTE offsets): +0x9b0 i32 slot; +0x9b4/+0x9b8/+0x9bc Vec3 TARGET (look-at), +0x9c0 f32 w=1.0; +0x9c4 f32 DISTANCE (used sign-flipped = cam behind target, smaller=closer); +0x9c8 f32 PITCH(radians); +0x9cc f32 YAW(radians); +0x9d0 CSPersCam subobject; +0x9e0..+0xa1f 16xf32 VIEW MATRIX (== CSPersCam view matrix CSCam+0x10); +0xa20 f32 FOV(radians)=CSPersCam.fov; +0xa24 f32 ASPECT=CSPersCam.aspectRatio (far=10000 near=0.05 defaults).

The camera routine is dump FUN_140bbe190 (called from ctor 0x140bbe010 @ +0xbbe0f7) -- it Lookup's MenuOffscrRendParam(DAT_143b39858[slot*0x20]) and writes orbit fields (fov/yaw/pitch = param * GLOBAL_DegreeToRadian), then builds matrix and pushes. THIS is the 'clobbering' fn (roadmap mislabeled FUN_140bbe0a0) -- NEVER call it after overriding (re-reads param, resets orbit).

CALL THESE (deobf addrs, shift-verified content-unique, shift=-0xf0):
- MATRIX BUILDER: dump FUN_140bbe480 -> deobf 0x140bbe390 (RVA 0xbbe390). sig fn(R /rcx/, out:*mut f32[16] /rdx/)->*mut f32. Pure orbit->view-matrix math (sinf/cosf of pitch/yaw, target, -distance), reads R+0x9b4/9c4/9c8/9cc, writes 16 f32 to out. No ctx/alloc/lock.
- PUSH: dump FUN_140bba550 -> deobf 0x140bba460 (RVA 0xbba460). sig fn(R /rcx/, persCam=R+0x9d0 /rdx/). -> FUN_140bb7da0(*(R+0xa8)=offscreenRend, persCam): copies persCam matrix(+0x10..0x4f)+proj(+0x50..0x5c) into offscreenViewState(offscreen+0x30) then FUN_140b12260 (bakes viewport 1280x720 + matrix) + FUN_141ad94e0->FUN_140cca450 (pure struct copy). ENTIRE path verified pure CPU state -- safe on CSTaskImp game thread.

APPLY RECIPE (replicate FUN_140bbe190 tail, skip the param read so no clobber): latch baseline orbit ONCE/slot (renderer obj persists across model rebuilds; only model rebuilds), then per tick write target/dist/pitch/yaw/fov = baseline+offset, call matrix builder -> copy 16f32 to R+0x9e0, call push(R, R+0x9d0). Re-apply per tick = clobber-proof. NOTE the roadmap NUMBERS 0x140bbe390/0x140bba460 were right as DEOBF call addrs but mislabeled as dump FUN_ names. Relates: interactive-face-levers-roadmap-2026-06-29, live-portrait-HIGHER-RES-1024-DONE-2026-06-29.

angrE-stable-proof-title-owner-gate-bug-2026-07-18: MECHANISM for why load-success proof never fires (title_tick_cover.rs product_core_autoload_tick). The stable-proof block (line ~808+, incl. the sf==30 -> phase IDLE reset at ~1302) is gated at line 751 on title_owner(module_base)==Some -> else early-returns true at 759. title_owner exists at TITLE / return-title states but is None during stable in-world gameplay, so during load #1's stable ~47s the proof block DOES NOT RUN (matches the 47s SWITCH-ORACLE logging gap). Additionally, the proof requires ig_d8==STABLE_IN_WORLD(2) but runtime shows ig_d8==2 ONLY when player=false (teardown transition); during player=true gameplay ig_d8==1 always. So the proof conditions (title_owner present + ig_d8==2 + menu_job!=0) describe a transition, never co-occur to reach sf==30, and load success is never latched -> QUICKLOAD_PHASE stuck non-idle -> destructive reload cycles -> crash. FIX must detect stable load from an in-world signal that is valid WITHOUT a title owner (player_available/player-present + world loaded + not streaming, sustained) and latch phase IDLE + disarm the return-title chain there. Links [[angre-load1-stable-then-destructive-reload-cycles-2026]].

FALSIFICATION (subagent RE, 2026-06-15, disasm-verified). The hypothesized 'native accept ACTION' at pump 0x1407a9600's call [rax] (0x1407a96c0) DOES NOT EXIST. Decoded: 0x7a9600 is a smart-ptr evaluate-and-release: AddRef J+8 (0x141eba1c0), call J->update 0x1407aa1f0 (writes verdict to out-struct), predicate 0x1407a9200 (reads *out>1, the combiner verdict word -- NOT a forced bool/latch), then atomic Release (0x141eba200); ONLY if old refcount==1 it does rax=*rdi (J's vtable); call [rax] = J->vtable[0] = DESTRUCTOR 0x140744d90 (allocator-free), then nulls the slot (consume job). So call [rax] is ~Node(), not OnConfirm. The combiner update 0x1407aa1f0 is a PURE evaluator (iterates children idx@+0x10/count@+0x60/array@+0x18, ANDs verdicts, zero state mutation, no SetState, no CSFeMan construction). The REAL advance is SetState 0x140b0d960(owner,0xb) inside MenuJobWait 0x140b0d400, gated SOLELY on latch byte 0x143d856a0 != 0 (latch written by intro-finish 0x140c8fe90, not by accept) -- this is exactly the force-state path that CRASHES (skips the input-driven session bootstrap). CONCLUSION: there is NO input-independent native OnConfirm to call at the accept layer. The leaf 0x1407ad1c0 reads its press state from input-SESSION singleton 0x143d83148 (resolved via 0x140d6a8e0, queried call [rax+0x10]) -- the pre-poll snapshot; writing THAT is the only in-process way to make the game's own detection report a press, but it IS synthesized input (forbidden by no-compromises-standard). REMAINING zero-input fork: replicate the front-end/session BOOTSTRAP natively (construct outer SimpleTitleStep + CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 (ctor 0x140766010 @caller 0x140af2e47) + session mgr 0x1447ef360), bypassing the input-gated title-accept. Related: title-accept-needs-genuine-input-detection, outer-simpletitlestep-absent-at-title-wall, no-compromises-standard.

Cold-vs-warm vkd3d shader-cache A/B (isolated VKD3D_SHADER_CACHE_PATH, runs onscreen-capture-20260624-144057 cold / -144153 warm): NO meaningful difference. engine-init 12642 vs 12275ms, main-thread CPU 34410 vs 34470ms, CRC 40 vs 39 hits, vkd3d-thread CPU 2270 vs 2030ms. => the ~12.6s single-threaded engine-init is an EVERY-BOOT CONSTANT cost, NOT a one-time shader-cache fill; caching is not the lever. Main-thread engine-init RIP breakdown (~530 samples): ~49% Wine/ntdll memory ops 0x6fff (partly Proton overhead), ~33% other in-module engine (incl memcpy ~9%), ~9% Arxan/DRM bignum crypto FUN_14004c600, ~8% graphics-shader CRC FUN_141c90cd0. NB the CRC has 3 DATA xrefs in addition to the GXCGShader code path -- some CRC use may be Arxan checksumming via pointer table, so Arxan's TOTAL share (bignum+CRC-via-DATA+decrypt memcpy) is likely >9%. PHASE-4 START (2026-07-01): implemented Arxan gadget-collapse for the UNCONDITIONAL-JMP-DISPATCH class in scripts/bake-function.py reassembler. KEY INSIGHT: deobf-recover.py ALREADY emulates THROUGH the Arxan dispatch and records the real successor in r.edges -- e.g. for 0x140110820 the literal entry byte is 'jmp 0x145ccb6b2' (Arxan 0x144c0e000-0x145e01800) but r.edges has 0x140110820->0x140110838 (the resolved real block); recovery produced 12 clean instrs, 0 instrs inside Arxan, 0 Arxan-touching edges. The OLD reassembler flagged the fn because it read the literal branch immediate instead of consulting r.edges. FIX: build succ={a:[b...]} from r.edges; for a branch whose literal target is Arxan, if mnemonic=='jmp' (unconditional) AND exactly one recovered non-Arxan in-fn successor -> rewrite to 'jmp loc_<succ>' (arxan_jmp map, also drives label emission); else still flag. Conditional-branch-into-Arxan and call-into-Arxan STILL FLAG (a cond branch has resolved-taken + fall-through successors and edges don't disambiguate which; call edges record the RETURN successor not the call target). PROVEN: 0x140110820 went NOT-BAKED(flagged) -> BAKED ref==recomp==68 (non-vacuous non-zero), collapsed .s = jmp loc_140110838 / call sub_1416de0c0 / nop / jmp loc_1424fb294 / sub rsp,0x28 / call sub_1424fb244 / neg rax / sbb eax,eax / neg eax / dec eax / add rsp,0x28 / ret. callees 0x1416de0c0 & 0x1424fb244 (the latter is the already-BAKED security-cookie fn). input_dependent=False only because return-0 stubs neutralize the real callee 0x1424fb244 (which returns its arg for even inputs) -> using REAL callees (next phase-4 item) would make it input-dependent. No regression: fns w/o Arxan branches leave arxan_jmp empty (unchanged path). Also symlinked eldenring-deobf.bin into the worktree root (was only in main checkout) so scripts/disas-deobf.sh & tooling resolve it. Related: bake-batch-reverify-2026-07-01, bake-function-oven-phases-1-3-2026-07-01.

GROUND TRUTH (2026-06-16) from a USER-DRIVEN real load (read-only observe) + decode of confirm 0x140b0e180. Overturns several assumptions. ER 1.16.1 base 0x140000000.

REAL LOAD STATE SEQUENCE (observe, user drove normal flow, char in slot 0 at m10): boot builds session 0x47ef360 + streaming-driver 0x143d7c088 + slotmgr by tick ~80 (ALL non-null FROM BOOT, NOT null-at-title -- corrects continue-load-subsystem-session-scoped / frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned). state 10 (press) -> press -> state 2 (BeginLogo) -> 3 (BeginTitle: builds menu AND sets GameMan+0xc30) -> back to 10 (c30 now 0xa010000) -> user browses Load Game (b80 cycles 2->1->0 = slot preview deserialize/mount) -> selects slot0 (ac0=0) -> confirm -> state 5 (PlayGame) -> 6 (GameStepWait) -> csfeman+MoveMapStep build -> mms_state 1(MsbLoad)->2(MsbLoadWait: resmgr built, b7c1=1 streaming-enable set NATIVELY)->3(WorldResWait) -> world resident -> in-game. So b7c1 streaming-enable happens natively at mms_state=2 (the entire worldres-enable-0x14066e2e4 manual-poke RE is MOOT). The visual real load = a dark m10 interior (Stranded Graveyard).

CONFIRM 0x140b0e180 DECODE: selector is literally cmp dword [owner+0x284],1 -> ==1 NEW GAME (edx=4), else LOAD (edx=5). owner=[item+8]. The LOAD branch is PURELY DATA-DRIVEN by GameMan+0xc30: getter 0x140679560 copies c30 -> owner+0xbc; SetState(5); PlayGame idx5 0x140b0d5b0 reads owner+0xbc -> load arm 0x140aebdc0 whose ONLY gate is map != -1. NO save-validity check, NO mount check, NO menu-index check. So clearing owner+0x284 was a no-op (it was already !=1) and is NOT the lever.

CRITICAL: 0xa010000 (m10_01) IS THE NEW-GAME DEFAULT START MAP, not save-derived. Written by 0x14067aac0 (sole caller 0x140b0e89e in 0x140b0e780, run by the BeginTitle/NewGame chain) via 0x140720260 -> 0x14029a950 packer, edition-gated by global 0x143d87220. The user test char is ALSO at m10_01, so its real saved map == the new-game default == 0xa010000 -- c30 value alone CANNOT disambiguate load vs new-game for an early character. The save-slot c30 writer is 0x14067afa0 (sets c30 = the slot record map), GATED on 0x14067fbc0 returning a POPULATED slot record (produced by the b80 preview-deserialize / mount); driven by the Load-Game-list step 0x140b016a0 / resolver 0x14067dc00 (reads selected-slot fields +0xb5e/+0xb60/+0xb64/+0xb68/+0xbca, +0xac0).

WHY OUR own-stepper got the NEW-GAME CUTSCENE: we did SetState(3=BeginTitle) [c30 = new-game DEFAULT 0xa010000] then called confirm 0x140b0e180 COLD without mounting any slot. The load branch loaded c30=0xa010000 as a FRESH map with NO save data applied -> new-game intro at m10. The real flow had MOUNTED the slot during the Load-Game browse, so c30 held the SAVE map AND the character data was applied by the b80 deserialize.

THE FIX (mandatory): before confirm/SetState(5), MOUNT the most-recent slot so (1) the slot record is populated (0x14067fbc0 true) -> 0x14067afa0 sets c30 from the save, and (2) the character data is applied by the b80 deserialize 0x14067b290 (needs b80->3). The b80 mount is exactly what the Load-Game MENU (MoveMapListStep, which ALSO registers the stream worker 0x144842d40 via helper 0x140af1b40) does during slot highlight. Our PlayGame-confirm path skips the menu, so the save is never mounted. NEXT: drive the native Load-Game slot mount (MoveMapListStep / 0x14067afa0 + b80 deserialize) BEFORE the confirm, instead of the cold PlayGame confirm. Verify MoveMapListStep idx0 session assert 0x144588e98 (DIFFERENT from 0x47ef360) is non-null at the title. Related: own-stepper-direct-initiator-sets-b80-and-c30-2026, stream-worker-registration-is-the-b80-wall-verified-2026, menu-load-path-is-movemaplist-self-driven-not-movemapstep-2026, option2-verdict-menu-deserialize-needs-session-not-title-2026.

IN-WORLD LOAD TRIGGER FULLY TRACED (2026-07-01, answers 'investigate DoSaveStuff trigger first'). CS::MoveMapStep::DoSaveStuff (dump 0x140afbbc0) is a per-frame tick; its LOAD branch fires when GameMan::IsSaveState2() (== GLOBAL_GameMan->saveState==2) and loads slot=MoveMapStep+0x12c via FUN_14067b380(slot) (dump 0x67b380 -> AllocateAligned 0x280000, read save file, GameMan->saveSlot=slot, FUN_14067be60 DESERIALIZE == crash in PlayerGameData::Deserialize 0x260070 -> CSGaitemImp::Deserialize 0x671220, then warpRequested=true). KEY: GameMan.saveState IS the b80 field (constants.rs GAME_MAN_LOAD_IN_PROGRESS_B80_OFFSET == offset_of!(GameMan, save_state)); saveState/b80==2 is the 'deserialize arm'. WHO ARMS IT = OUR OWN AUTOLOAD, not the native confirmed path. In title.rs ~1990: as soon as SYSTEM_QUIT_QUICKLOAD_PHASE>=TITLE_OWNER_SEEN(3) it flips phase->AUTOLOAD_HANDOFF(4) and calls product_continue_autoload_tick (continue_load.rs:271), which in FULLREAD_PHASE_SUBMIT does set_save_slot(slot)+submit_native_continue_item_action -> the native Continue fires the load -> b80=2 -> DoSaveStuff deserializes. BUT 'title owner appeared' != 'world torn down': for the SWITCH the return-title chain (just submitted) is still tearing down the OLD world, so the Continue deserializes into a LIVE world -> crash. BOOT autoload works only because title is CLEAN (no old world). Return-title-request 0x67a3a0 only sets saveRequested+bc4 (NOT the load); load-job builder/Run don't arm it either. FIX (our code, minimal): gate product_continue_autoload_tick's Continue-submit (or the phase 3->4 handoff) so for the System-Quit switch it WAITS until the old world is actually torn down / at clean title (e.g. world player absent / return-title teardown complete), not merely when the title-owner object exists. Boot path (no System-Quit phase) unaffected. Related: [[system-quit-load-profile-progress-2026-07-01]] [[menu-job-queue-pump-dequeue-mechanism]].

MENU/TITLE ANIMATION SPEED LEVER (static RE, subagent 2026-06-22, deobf base 0x140000000). Goal component (2) 'faster animation playback'. 

MECHANISM: The title flow has NO per-state duration constant -- it is a Scaleform/GFx animation driven by the FD4 FRAME-DELTA f32. Path: STEP_MenuJobWait tick reads TitleNativeJobTaskData+0x08 (frame_delta ~1/60) at 0x140b0d50a (movss 0x8(rdi),xmm0) -> stores to local FD4TaskData -> 0x140b0d521 call 0x1407a9600 (dialog update dispatcher) which re-reads delta at 0x1407a966d (movss 0x8(rsi),xmm0) -> 0x1407a968b call *0x10(rax)=dialog vtable[2] update -> CS::TitleTopDialog::update 0x1409aac10 gets delta in xmm1 (0x1409aac40 movaps xmm1,xmm6), re-feeds child-window updates 0x140745570 / 0x1409b3dd0 -> drives the Scaleform movie frame counter. Completion (FadeIn->Loop) is CHECKED not timed: predicate 0x14075a4d0 -> lambda vtable 0x142a995a8[2] = 0x14075ce00 (helper 0x14075cea0) reads anim obj [obj+0x08], current frame 0x140739e20, total frame 0x140736c90, returns sete(current==total). Settle gate: node eligible when (*(u32*)(node+0x20) & 0x8f) > 1.

LEVER (recommended, option a): SCALE the title frame-delta f32 at TitleNativeJobTaskData+0x08 by K (~3.0-4.0) before consumption. Cleanest: hook the title native job and write K*(1/60) into +0x08 each tick, OR scale the value written to the local FD4TaskData+0x08. ENGINE-SAFE: every downstream consumer (Scaleform tick, FadeIn/TextFadeOut frame advance, the current==total completion predicate 0x14075ce00, the (flags&0x8f)>=2 settle gate) is satisfied NATURALLY -- the anim just reaches its end frame in fewer ticks; no predicate bypassed, load does not desync. NO global timescale multiplier competes on this path (delta flows raw from +0x08 to dialog update, no mulss against a global) -> +0x08 is the single clean choke-point, and scaling the TITLE job's own delta copy affects only the title flow not the whole engine. REJECT (b) writing anim progress/[node+0x20] directly (trips settle gate / skips compare frame) and (c) shrinking a duration const (none exists; durations live in the .gfx asset 05_001_Title_Logo). Confidence high on delta path + predicate chain. Relates: titletopdialog-fadein-gate-blocks-menu-open-2026, autoload-RECONCILED-PLAN-ride-flow-inject-focus-2026-06-22.

PICKER-BOOT CRASH ROOT CAUSE + FIX (2026-07-08, live-validated, draft PR #42, branch fix/fullread-stale-request-disarm off main@562b96f). SYMPTOM: missing-save picker boot crashed 6/6 at ~22s, at world arrival -- AV live 0x67141a (CSGaitemImp::Deserialize gaitemInsTable[-1] OOB, NtTerminateProcess 0xc0000005).
