DECISIVE FINAL-WALL LOCATION (2026-06-18, static decode + runtime, save-safe). After the ACTIVATE-byte + dialog-rows-populate convergence, the LAST wall for the cold save-data load is now precisely located: the direct-built ProfileLoadDialog is not a LIVE MENU SCREEN, so two data fields the selector build needs are unpopulated, and the queued selector job is not pumped.

CONFIRMED (load_activate-gate-inverted re-verified): load_activate 0x1409a4670 with [rec+0x295]>=1 && accessor 0x140e362c0([rec+0x44])==2 (our set values) DOES take the build branch 0x1409a48d6 -> calls builder 0x140826510 @0x1409a4980. So load_activate BUILDS the selector. BUT the built MenuJob (CS::MenuJobWithContext<LoadJobContext,lambda>, vtable 0x142ac71e0, RTTI-confirmed) is placed in a TRANSIENT DLReferencePointer on load_activate's STACK ([rsp+0x58]) and HANDED TO THE MENU-JOB QUEUE -- it is NEVER stored back into the dialog. That is why scanning the dialog's first 0x400 qwords for vtable 0x142ac71e0 found NOTHING (step_ok=false, selector_step=0x0) -- the self-pump-from-dialog approach cannot find it.

THE BUILDER IS CALLABLE COLD (allocator/session singleton 0x143d87350 is boot-built, sole writer 0x140e19891; the factory 0x140821bf0 vtable+0x18 assert is non-fatal + passes cold; alloc 0x141eb9ed0(0x78) fine). But the HARD BLOCKER IS DATA: builder 0x140826510(rcx=&owner_cell[0x10, zeroed; step ptr returned at [cell+0]], rdx=dialog+0x50, r8d=slot, r9=[dialog+0x1cc8]) requires: (a) dialog+0x50 = a POPULATED MenuJob DESCRIPTOR (3 ints @+0/+4/+8, a valid std::vector @+0x10 [begin/end/cap, factory 0x140823c50 reads ([+0x10]-[+0x8])>>2 as count], qword @+0x30, int @+0x38); (b) [dialog+0x1cc8] = a valid LoadJobContext ptr (>=0x18 bytes, factory copies movups (rbx)+movsd 0x10(rbx) -> step+0x50..0x60). On a cold direct-built ProfileLoadDialog these are NULL/garbage -- they are populated by the dialog's LIVE menu-screen init, which never runs cold. The dialog ctor builds the ROWS (with ACTIVATE) but NOT +0x50/+0x1cc8.

=> THE PRECISE FINAL WALL: make the direct-built dialog a LIVE MENU SCREEN so (1) its init populates dialog+0x50 (descriptor) + [dialog+0x1cc8] (LoadJobContext), and (2) the MENU task-group (0x143d87350) pumps the queued selector job -> menu_deser -> full-save mount. NEXT RE (the real target, save-safe): find what WRITES dialog+0x50 (the MenuJob descriptor) and dialog+0x1cc8 (LoadJobContext) during the dialog's live mount -- trace the ProfileLoadDialog init/update path (ctor 0x1409a3d90 sub-builds 0x1409a51c0/0x14078c470, and the screen-register/enter path). If those are populated by a callable init fn, call it on our direct-built dialog, THEN build the selector (0x140826510 with the now-valid +0x50/+0x1cc8) + self-pump tick 0x140826d50, OR register the dialog with the MENU group so the native pump drives it. The selector self-pump infra (OWN_STEPPER_SELECTOR_STEP, tick 0x140826d50 w/ FD4Time result+ctx) + the dialog-scan are wired in own_stepper_stage2 (inactive until the step is found). EVERYTHING ELSE IS SOLVED cold: slot-byte gate (ACTIVATE 0x140262250), IO subsystem present, dialog rows populate (bound=1), load_activate reaches the builder. Related: convergence-ACTIVATE-populates-dialog-rows-but-selector-mount-still-walled-2026, load_activate-gate-inverted-live-mount-is-nonbuild-path-2026, selector-tick-drive-decode, REFRAME-io-subsystem-present-cold-blocker-is-just-the-active-byte-2026, direct-build-dialog-WORKS-but-rows-empty-need-tick-2026, stage2-menu-drive-load-recipe-verified-2026.

LOADING-SCREEN PORTRAIT -- EXHAUSTIVE: in-pipeline display is a DEAD END; the deterministic path is a D3D12 PRESENT OVERLAY (2026-06-30). After ~50 capped runs, all forge/Scaleform/TexRepository routes are ruled out for DETERMINISTIC display:

1. FORGE-BAKE (bake the real portrait into the first/displayed TPF): TIMING RACE, unwinnable. The now-loading background forge (loading_bg_replace_bind_hook) PRE-BINDS at +15.4..16.2s (varies run-to-run) and the engine DECODES the TPF bytes into the displayed texture AT FORGE TIME. The menu portrait can't render before ~15.7s (needs the menu up). Dropping the IBL-residency gate to capture the first nonblack (~0.3s earlier) wins SOME runs but not the early-forge ones -> nondeterministic. The network-check hold does NOT reliably gate the forge.
2. RE-FORGE the displayed rti with a portrait container: FAILS -- the CSScaleform pump registers the texture by NAME on first bind only.
3. D3D12 PIXEL UPLOAD (upload_rgba_to_texture) into GetResCap(GLOBAL_TexRepository,name).gxTexture: lands correctly (post-upload readback verify_center_rgb=(37,38,37) = the dark portrait, NOT the bright checker) but the SCREEN STAYS CHECKER. So the engine decodes the TPF into a SEPARATE Scaleform GFx-renderer texture at forge time; the CS-side TexRepository CSGxTexture we can reach is NOT the sampled texture. Triply confirmed.

PROVEN GOOD (reuse): surface ownership works (force-checker fills the whole tip screen); the real 1024x1024 menu portrait IS captured into LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_RGBA (build_portrait_tpf bakes it); checker via TPF bytes displays. Autoload intact (player=1) through all changes. Infra built + committed: forge_into_rti, upload_rgba_to_texture (CPU->GPU CopyTextureRegion), readback_offscreen_rgba8/readback_excluding_rgba8, find_d3d12_resource_ex (exclude-aware), GetResCap call (TEX_REPOSITORY_GET_RES_CAP_RVA=0xb809a0, TexResCap+0x78), bounded network-check portrait hold.

THE PATH (next session, per CLAUDE.md 'native D3D12/game-render-layer path'): hook IDXGISwapChain::Present, and when now_loading_active(base), draw the captured portrait (LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_RGBA, already a GPU texture via upload_rgba_to_texture) as a fullscreen quad onto the backbuffer RTV (root sig + a trivial fullscreen-triangle PSO + the portrait SRV). This bypasses the forge/Scaleform/TexRepository entirely and is deterministic. Sized ~250-300 lines + a swapchain vtable hook -- a fresh, well-scoped subsystem; do NOT keep iterating the in-pipeline routes (all dead ends above). Reuse the existing D3D12 device-access + command-list/fence patterns in gpu_readback.rs. Supersedes postcontinue-portrait-timing-wall-2026-06-30 and ONSCREEN-progress.

USER PRINCIPLE (2026-06-24, durable, emphatic correction). 'Pre-existing bug' is a claim about the PAST and it EXPIRES at a working milestone. Once a feature is PROVEN to work (e.g. zero-input autoload reaches the playable world), any bug that recurs inside that working feature is NEW -- a regression to RESOLVE, not history to wave off. Do NOT label a recurring bug 'pre-existing, not my problem' after the feature works. WHY: history goes forward in time; tolerating a known bug under the 'pre-existing' label lets regressions ride along inside a shipped milestone. HOW TO APPLY: when a working-milestone run surfaces a bug seen before, treat it as NEW, file it, fix it -- do not suppress/tolerate. First application: CORRUPTED-SAVE SEMAPHORE GR_System_Message id 401106 fires in the now-working autoload (gold save read but rejected on validate/write); it must be ROOT-CAUSE resolved, not just suppressed.

POST-CONTINUE PORTRAIT SPARE -- RUN RESULT (2026-06-29, branch spike/boot-parallelization). Ran scripts/run-postcontinue-lookat-smoke.sh twice (added a dense 'lookat-spared-sweep' every 20 ticks while LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_SPARED_RENDERER!=0 because the coarse 240-tick lookat-phase-sweep never sampled the ~2s post-Continue now-loading window on the fast gold-save load).

PROVEN WORKING: (1) the teardown-spare GATING FIX is correct -- gating on portrait_lookat_enabled() (not just product_autoload) makes profile_renderer_teardown_spare_hook FIRE on native-continue. Log: 'loading-portrait: pre-recorded spare candidate renderer=0x.. slot=0 (model built at menu)' at +15.7s then 'loading-portrait: SPARED slot0 renderer=0x.. (candidate matches) from teardown' at +17.2s. (2) The spared renderer PERSISTS past Continue: spared[ptr] stays set across frames 260->420 (+17.7s->+20.8s), spanning now-loading INTO world-load (T_controllable player=1 name=Banon level=150 at +19.1s). (3) It is DRAWN every frame post-Continue via the offscreen thunk PROFILE_OFFSCREEN_DRIVE_RVA: PROFILE_PERFRAME_SPARED_DRAWS climbs 16->176.

BROKEN (route blocker): model_ok=0 the ENTIRE post-Continue window. The oracle reads spared+PROFILE_RENDERER_MODEL_INS_OFFSET (renderer's model pointer); post-Continue it is null/invalid -> PROFILE_SPARED_MODEL_OK never increments, and profile_lookat_rt_sample then early-returns (no live model) so rt[samples/nonblack/changed] FREEZE at the pre-spare values (25/2/3). The renderer is drawn but EMPTY -- no character model behind it.

ROOT CAUSE: nulling the renderer's menu-table entry (profile_renderer_teardown_spare_hook on FUN_1409b2f00) spares only the RENDERER OBJECT. The MODEL behind it (renderer+model_ins) has a SEPARATE lifetime owned by the menu profile-model unload, which still frees it at Continue. Sparing the renderer alone is INSUFFICIENT -- must also retain/own the model (refcount bump or spare the model resource) so renderer+model_ins stays valid post-Continue. Also nowload=0 in every sample -- now_loading_active(base) never read 1 even in the post-Continue loading window (separate question; the now-loading flag/base offset may be wrong or the window not classified as now-loading). NEXT: log raw model_ins at spare-time + in dense sweep to confirm zeroed-vs-dangling, then find the model-resource owner/refcount to retain it. Supersedes the open question in postcontinue-portrait-HANDOFF-2026-06-29.

LOADING-SCREEN PORTRAIT -- THE TIMING WALL + DISPLAYED-TEXTURE WALL (2026-06-30). Two independent walls block the on-screen portrait; both characterized.

WALL 1 (timing -- can't bake the first forge): the now-loading background forge (loading_bg_replace_bind_hook, MENU_Load_NNNNN) PRE-BINDS at ~15.68s -- BEFORE menu-open (title-accept-byte ~15.8s), BEFORE the network-check Continue (~15.8s), and BEFORE the menu portrait IBL-renders (~16.0s). So the FIRST (displayed) forge ALWAYS bakes the checker; the real portrait is captured ~0.3s too late. Tried: bounded portrait-hold in network_check_job_run_hook (hold MenuJobState>1 until PROFILE_BAKE_RGBA_CAPTURED, capped PORTRAIT_HOLD_MAX_TICKS) -- but the forge fires BEFORE the network-check, so holding it doesn't delay the forge. The autoload stayed intact (player=1) -- the hold is bounded-safe + product-inert (gated on the real-pixels diagnostic flag).

WALL 2 (displayed-texture identity -- can't update post-hoc): re-forging the first rti with a portrait container FAILS (Scaleform pump registers the texture by NAME on first bind only). D3D12 pixel-upload (upload_rgba_to_texture, CPU->GPU CopyTextureRegion) into BOTH the forge container GX AND GetResCap(GLOBAL_TexRepository,name).gxTexture (GetResCap deobf 0x140b809a0=TEX_REPOSITORY_GET_RES_CAP_RVA, TexResCap+0x78) returns ok=true (no crash) but the screen stays checker. Implies Scaleform holds its OWN decoded GFx texture copy, distinct from the TexRepository CSGxTexture -- the actual sampled texture is in the GFx renderer's cache, not reached by uploading into the CS-side GX. (find_d3d12_resource 'largest TEXTURE2D' is also non-deterministic among same-size textures, compounding it.)

PROVEN GOOD (unchanged): surface binding works (force-checker fills the whole tip screen); the real IBL-lit 1024x1024 menu portrait IS captured into LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_RGBA (build_portrait_tpf bakes it); checker via TPF-bytes displays perfectly.

NEXT (two viable routes): (A) reach the Scaleform GFx-renderer texture that the now-loading sprite actually samples (RE the GFx texture cache / the CSScaleform pump's name->GFx-texture registration) and upload into THAT, OR overwrite the TPF cap's decoded bytes + force a re-decode; (B) render+capture the portrait DURING THE TITLE (before the ~15.68s pre-bind) so the first forge bakes real TPF bytes -- needs driving the profile-model IBL render at the title stage, ~0.5s earlier than menu-open. Route B reuses the PROVEN TPF-bytes display path and avoids all GPU-texture-identity issues -- likely the cleaner path. All infra committed, compiles, autoload intact. Supersedes the 'delay native Continue' idea in postcontinue-portrait-ONSCREEN-progress (the forge is BEFORE the continue, so that idea is moot).

CORRECTION to load2-root-return-title-movemap conclusion: I OVER-CONCLUDED that load2 uses a different mechanism than load1. The data shows BOTH loads reach mms=18 (MoveMapStep MOVE MAP) WITH the character loaded (present=True) -- so both go through the SAME MoveMapStep. The difference is PURELY the finalize 7->8 gate: load1 completes 7->8->9->readiness, load2 parks at substate 7. Substate 7 = 'REMO/SAVE-DRAIN WAIT'; gate = FUN_14067a170 && !ShouldSave() && !FUN_140679460() && FUN_140a9ceb0(CSRemo). STRONG HYPOTHESIS: ShouldSave() is stuck TRUE for load2 because we SUPPRESSED the in-world quit-save (to dodge the gaitem crash) -- so a save-REQUESTED flag (GameMan+0xb72 save_requested / +0xb73 companion) is left set with no save completing -> !ShouldSave=false -> gate never passes -> SAVE-DRAIN WAIT forever. Load1 (boot, never quit-saved) has no pending save -> !ShouldSave=true -> gate passes. FIX (native, not force): clear the SPURIOUS pending-save flags the suppressed quit-save left (so ShouldSave()=false), letting the game's OWN finalize gate pass naturally 7->8->9 and complete retaining the player. This is clearing a wrongly-set INPUT (native cleanup), not forcing the OUTPUT substate. NEXT: confirm save_requested/save_state differs load1 vs load2 at the finalize, then clear the pending-save flags. Supersedes [[load2-root-return-title-movemap-is-a-teardown-transition-need-fresh-world-load-mechanism-2026-07-19]] (mechanism-difference part was wrong).

ROOT PINNED + VALIDATED (run gate2 2026-07-20): load2 substate-7 stall is a GENUINE SAVE-DRAIN WAIT, and FORCING the 7->8 gate (clearing saveState/b72/b73) makes the finalize advance past 7 BUT then case 8 tears the world DOWN (observed: mms=18 fin=5 -> mms=-1 present=FALSE). So clearing the flags does NOT satisfy the gate legitimately -- it lets case 8 run BEFORE the save/remo drain is genuinely complete, so case 8 (WorldChrMan/fieldArea reset) tears down instead of re-establishing the world. The 300cap run settled AND stayed present because the drain COMPLETED NATURALLY there. THE REAL ROOT: we SUPPRESS the in-world quit-save (to dodge the CSGaitemImp::Deserialize crash), which leaves saveState=3 (save pending) that NEVER DRAINS -> the finalize SAVE-DRAIN WAIT (substate 7) waits forever -> load2 stuck; and forcing the flag desyncs case 8 -> teardown. THE FIX is NOT to force the gate flags: it is to make the suppressed quit-save DRAIN CLEANLY -- complete the save-completion side effect (the drain the finalize waits on) WITHOUT the gaitem crash -- so the gate passes legitimately and case 8 re-establishes the world in sync (like load1/300cap). This is the same suppressed-quit-save issue root: satisfy the SAVE side effect the game waits on, do not skip it. Every force (fin, warp, flags) has now been proven to teardown/softlock; only the natural drain completes cleanly. REVERT the broadened flag-clear (causes teardown). NEXT: RE the suppressed quit-save drain -- what completes saveState 3->0 that we skipped, and complete it offline-safely.

CRITICAL CORRECTION + FPS-ROOT INSIGHT (user 2026-07-20): the load2 did_move=116 / movement was the USER moving the character with THEIR input, NOT the harness injection -> CONTAMINATED. I can NOT claim the harness proved load2 movement, and must NOT change the move-proof to latch on did_move (would false-positive on user input). A clean harness-only proof needs NO live user input during the sample. BUT the correction reveals the real root: load2s character IS MOVABLE (user moved it) -> NOT frozen (my earlier frozen-at-mms18 read was WRONG). Load2 reaches a movable-but-UNSETTLED state: mms_state stays 18 (never reaches -1), finalize12a=0. The user insight: the inactive "settled/load-done" semaphore (mms never -1) means the GAME STILL THINKS IT IS LOADING, so it keeps the loading-mode overhead -- the boot-view per-frame GPU readback (composite_boot_progress_inner) + the now-loading render path -- running the whole time load2 sits at mms=18 -> THAT is the low FPS. FPS and non-settle are the SAME root. render_ready=False for BOTH loads too (oracle may not track visual-render). FIX: make load2 SETTLE (mms 18->19->20->-1) so the game EXITS loading mode -> the readback/loading overhead stops -> FPS recovers AND the harness can prove clean movement in the settled world. The cVar10=0 clears keep load2 movable but PREVENT settling (catch-22): need the finalize to COMPLETE after the movable window (release the clear / supply the real end-of-load trigger) so mms reaches -1. This is the single lever for both FPS and load3.

How to apply -- once our RE findings here are runtime-VALIDATED (e.g. the FD4StepTemplate/InGameStep load-step structures at crates/eldenring/src/fd4/task.rs, the title-flow SimpleTitleStep/TitleStep state machines, the load-job +0xd8 phase semantics, FD4TaskData usage), they are good candidates to upstream as typed bindings/structs to fromsoftware-rs via a fork PR -- but ONLY after they are tested (not just statically reverse-engineered) and the Ghidra documentation is updated. The front-end/menu bootstrap (construction of CSFeMan 0x143d6b880, session 0x1447ef360, the outer SimpleTitleStep, and the MoveMapListStep load menu) is SCHEDULER-OWNED, not directly callable: these objects are constructed only when the native task scheduler advances the title past press-any-button via the input-driven title-accept. At the pre-bootstrap PRESS-ANY-BUTTON title they are NULL, so any in-process attempt to call/force the load machinery hits a null-singleton cascade (see forcing-hits-session-singleton-cascade, outer-simpletitlestep-absent-at-title-wall). The zero-input autoload therefore cannot shortcut the dispatcher at the title; it must trigger the native title-accept that runs the proper bootstrap cascade, then drive the load. DEFINITIVE static RE (2026-06-15, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs) -- HOW to obtain slot N's REAL saved map for owner+0xbc / GameMan+0xc30, the packed-map format, and the MoveMap IsResident gate. Resolves the one remaining piece of csfeman-built-via-setstate5-breakthrough.

=== ANSWER (1): THERE IS NO LIGHTER HEADER-ONLY PEEK ===
Exhaustive writer scan of [GameMan(0x143d69918)+0xc30] across BOTH .text sections (mov [reg+0xc30] disp32=30 0c 00 00) -> 5 writers, only ONE writes a SAVED slot's map:
 (a) 0x140675ea0 GameMan ctor: [rsi+0xc30]=0xffffffff (clear-to-invalid). not a peek.
 (b) 0x14067aac0: [GameMan+0xc30]=ret of 0x140720260 = a small scene/menu id (0x12/0x9), NOT a map. caller 0x140b0e89e (new-game play-count cycle). irrelevant.
 (c) 0x14067afa0(rcx,rdx,r8=&dword): [GameMan+0xc30]=*r8 gated by 0x14067fbc0 which captures the CURRENT player world coords into +0xc00(xmm)/+0xc08/+0xc0c/+0xc10 and +0xc20=1. This is the save-and-quit/in-game CAPTURE of the LIVE map, not a slot record. callers 0x140af795a,0x140b01951,0x140625fc0.
 (d) 0x14067bd70 = THE save-header writer (see below). reached only from full sync-deserialize 0x14067b290 (call @0x14067b30e), save-WRITE 0x14067dc00 (@0x14067e215), and thunk 0x140677f2d (jmp 0x14067bd70).
The slot container 0x143d5df38 holds NO per-slot packed map id: its +0x120 is a 0..0x270f wrap counter, +0x8 a sub-obj ptr, +0x9c/+0x9d bools; the save/load LIST UI records live in the menu-step's OWN +0x188 map (insert/erase node ops 0x140724290/0x1407245a0/0x1407247c0), carrying display metadata not the packed map. NO header-only save read primitive exists: the 'lighter' reader 0x140e6eb80 (from 0x14067b200, used by STEP_LoadSaveData) still reads size 0x280000 (loop @0x140e6ee83). All 27 callers of save-IO getter 0x140e6e060 feed the full read or the save-write; none does a ~0x10/header-sized slot peek.
CONCLUSION: GameMan+0xc30 (slot's saved map) is populated ONLY by the full sync-deserialize. There is no separate highlight-time peek and no readable slot-record map field. The real menu's Continue handler 0x140b0e180 works because the deserialize already ran (it READS +0xc30 via getter 0x140679560; its only pre-call 0x140678a50 just sets GameMan+0xe70=1).

=== ANSWER (2): THE HEADER PARSE 0x14067bd70 + PACKED FORMAT ===
0x14067bd70(rcx=GameMan, rdx=save-buffer-ptr, r8d=buf-size): gate [0x143d68078]!=0; build stream over buffer (0x141edf9f0/0x141edfa50); read 0x10-byte header chunk into [rbp-0x48] via 0x141edfb60 (require ret==0x10); validate 0x1402624c0; then @0x14067be0f: mov [GameMan+0xc30], [rbp-0x44] = the DWORD at header+4 IS the packed map id, copied RAW. Public thunk/alias: 0x140677f2d (jmp 0x14067bd70, same args). The packed map is the mAA_BB_CC_DD int (byte3=AA,byte2=BB,byte1=CC,byte0=DD; e.g. m60_42_34_00=0x3c2a2200) -- play_game_submit/owner+0xbc gate it on area byte (cmd>>24) in 0x32..0x58. The save stores it in exactly that packed form (the native Continue copies +0xc30 -> owner+0xbc unchanged), so format MATCHES; no decode needed beyond the raw header read.

=== ANSWER (3): SYNC DESERIALIZE 0x14067b290(ecx=slot) -- THE convergent path that lands +0xc30 ===
0x14067b290(ecx=slot): (1) alloc 0x280000 buf via scheduler [0x143d872e0]->[+0x50]; (2) 0x14067b100(rcx=buf,rdx=0x280000) reads the WHOLE save section for the slot via 0x140e6e060->0x140e6e380 (bool ret); (3) mov [GameMan+0xac0]=slot; (4) 0x14067bd70(GameMan,buf,0x280000) -> writes [GameMan+0xc30]=saved map; (5) builds GameFlow cmd from it (0x140679560/0x14067abd0), sets [GameMan+0x3c]=0,[+0x10]=1; (6) APPLIES stats/inventory 0x140e365c0 vs PlayerGameData [0x144588268]; (7) frees buf. So step (4) is the ONLY way to get slot N's saved map into +0xc30 -- and it inseparably also (6) applies the character. It is the SOLE caller path; the driver 0x140afbac4 calls it (slot from [mgr+0x12c]). Preconditions: session/save subsys [0x143d68078]!=0, scheduler 0x143d872e0 non-null.

=== ANSWER (4): MoveMap IsResident GATE -- a CORRECT map DOES drain child+0xd8 ===
STEP_MoveMap_Update 0x140aec720, gate @0x140aec8fc-0x140aec915: lea rcx,[r14(child)+0xe0]; call 0x140eb5530 (IsResident: reads [handle+0x8], if non-null calls vtable+0x20, returns resident bool); test al,al; JE 0x140aecbea (NOT resident -> skip, +0xd8 stays 1); if resident -> call 0x14067af60 then mov [r14+0xd8]=2 (advance). So child+0xd8 1->2 is GATED on the map stream at child+0xe0 going RESIDENT. STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 builds the child (0x4c0 bytes @0x140aec1ca, ctor 0x140af28d0) from the parent's resolved map [parent+0x100] and sets the streaming descriptor [child_sub(+0xe8)+0xe0]/+0xe4 from map getters (0x140679340/0x140679320/0x140679450). With the m60 PLACEHOLDER the requested map's assets don't correspond to slot 9 -> stream never goes resident -> +0xd8 stuck at 1 (the observed stall). With slot 9's REAL packed map in owner+0xbc (-> child+0x100 via play_game_submit), the correct area streams, IsResident returns true, +0xd8 drains 1->2 and the InGameStep advances -> loads slot 9's actual area/character. So YES, a correct map satisfies the gate.

=== RECIPE (refines csfeman-built-via-setstate5-breakthrough) ===
At the live FE-host SimpleTitleStep (vtable 0x142b63bb0, committed state +0x48==10): to load slot 9 for real, you MUST run the deserialize to get its saved map (no shortcut):
 (1) set GameMan+0xac0=9 via 0x14067a810(ecx=9).
 (2) drive the full slot-9 deserialize so [GameMan+0xc30] = slot 9 saved map: cleanest is the native autoload/Continue path -- driver 0x140afbac4 / dispatcher 0x140afb880 with the +0xb72 force-flag + slot 0xac0 (see native-continue-and-slotn-recipe-2026) which calls 0x14067b290(9); requires CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 NON-NULL (so this is POST-CSFeMan, not at the raw title). Note step (2) ALSO applies the character via 0x140e365c0 -- i.e. obtaining the map and loading the save are the SAME operation; you cannot peek the map cheaply first.
 (3) read [GameMan+0xc30], set owner+0xbc = it, clear owner+0x284, SetState 0x140b0d960(owner,5) -> existing pump runs PlayGame -> play_game_submit -> child STEP_MoveMap_Init/Update -> IsResident true (correct map) -> +0xd8 drains -> world resident.
IMPLICATION for the breakthrough: because +0xc30 only comes from the full deserialize (which also applies the char), the cleanest single-shot is to let the NATIVE Continue/load dispatcher do the slot-9 deserialize+map+apply AFTER CSFeMan is built (the SetState(5) breakthrough builds CSFeMan), rather than trying to pre-peek the map. RUNTIME-READ-ONLY: [GameMan+0xc30] value, owner/child pointers. Cite: disasm above. Related: csfeman-built-via-setstate5-breakthrough, play-game-submit-and-continue-load-recipe-2026, native-continue-and-slotn-recipe-2026, real-continue-load-driver-recipe.

Chain of refuted roots: entry-missing(run1) -> wrong-loadlist(run1) -> stale-entry(run2) -> +0x35-stuck(run3) -> NOW: file-cap status=4/data=null resident short-circuit (run4).

INTRO/ANY CUTSCENE RAM SEMAPHORE (RE 2026-06-30, to detect the autoload regression where a save-load wrongly enters the new-game intro cutscene). GLOBAL_CSRemo (CS::CSRemoImp singleton) = qword at dump VA 0x143d6ea58 => deobf RVA 0x3d6ea58 (0-shift data convention; from CheckForMultiplayType 140a04700 which does MOV RCX,[0x143d6ea58] then CALL IsInCutscene). IS-IN-CUTSCENE = (*(remoMan + 0x14c) & 1) where remoMan = *(GLOBAL_CSRemo + 0x8). i.e. in_cutscene = ( *( *(base+0x3d6ea58) + 0x8 ) + 0x14c ) & 1. From IsInCutscene 140a9ce10: MOV RCX,[RCX+0x8] (remoMan) ; test ; flag at remoMan+0x14c bit0. GetCutsceneId reads CSRemoIns->cutsceneId (current cutscene instance, offset TBD if we need the specific intro ID). EMEVD opcode 2002 (EMEVD::ControlFlow::Cutscene2002 @140572720 / Cutscene2002Handler @140572a20) is the play-cutscene enqueue. WHY THIS MATTERS: a normal save-load should NOT enter a cutscene; if in_cutscene goes true during/after the autoload it proves the regression. ROOT-CAUSE HYPOTHESIS (verify): the autoload deserializes a DEFAULT level-9 Vagabond (stats 15/10/11/14/13/9/9/7 = Vagabond starting class at starting level) instead of the gold save level-150 Banon (ER_EFFECTS_GOLD_SAVE save-files/150-Banon/ER0000.sl2 slot0) -> a brand-new character -> intro cutscene plays + no character model to render -> the loading-screen portrait is empty/flat-gray. So the portrait emptiness is DOWNSTREAM of a broken save-load (loading default new-game, not the real save). NEXT: confirm loaded char != gold char (telemetry char name/level vs the gold save), then fix the save-load to actually load the gold slot-0 character.

PATH B tick ABI decoded (2026-06-16, objdump on-disk eldenring.exe). The MoveMapListStep selector-owner step tick 0x140826d50 signature = (rcx=step_this, rdx=context/owner [kept in rsi], r8=result_out [kept in rbx]). Body: cmp BYTE [rcx+0x68],0 -> if 0 (first tick) call delegate-installer 0x140828270; if it returns true, fall to PUMP; if false, call 0x1407a91e0(rdx, edx=3) and write result [r8]=vtable 0x1429c8e58 then ret. PUMP path (step+0x68!=0): rcx=[step+0x70] (the installed delegate/job); build a stack ctx [rsp+0x28]=vtable 0x1429c8e48, [rsp+0x30]=float[r8+0x8] (delta time); then call [[step+0x70]]+0x10 with rdx=context, r8=&stackctx -> this is the job invoke that ultimately runs the menu deserialize 0x14082c240. On return writes result [r8]=vtable (0x1429c8e58 step-incomplete / 0x1429c8e48 step-done markers). So result_out r8 is an FD4 step-result struct {[+0]=state-vtable, [+8]=float}. The slot reaches 0x14082c240 via the context rdx ([rdx] first dword = selected slot, slot-record [rdx+0x38]) per autoload-native-menu-mount-drive-design-2026.

DRIVE RECIPE (B1, synthetic, in our in-context idx10 state 10): build the step object via factory 0x140821bf0 (vtable 0x142ac71e0; needed fields +0x68 install-flag=0, +0x70 delegate); build a context with slot N at [ctx+0]; each frame call 0x140826d50(step, ctx, &result) -> first tick installs the async job (registers the FD4 stream worker natively), later ticks pump it until 0x14082c240 mounts GameMan+0xc30=real + char (NO save write -- it is the LOAD selector). Then read c30; if real, SetState(5) to stream the real world. RISK: synthetic FD4 step/context construction historically crashes (movemapstep-direct-pump-crashes-needs-scheduler); prefer factory 0x140821bf0 over zeroing, and verify the ctx/result layouts before calling. Alternative B2: drive the NATIVE title menu to ENTER the Load-Game item (factory instantiates the step) + inject slot, letting the native pump tick it. STILL-UNKNOWN: factory 0x140821bf0 args/output layout; the context (rdx) full layout beyond [+0]/[+0x38]; whether the installer 0x140828270 needs a live menu-task parent. Related: b80-hand-drive-exhausted-pathB-menu-job-is-the-way-2026, seamless-co2-menu-deserialize-is-vanilla-0x82c240-2026, autoload-native-menu-mount-drive-design-2026.

GROUNDED MODEL for the 2nd (runtime-accumulation) re-load soft-lock (2026-07-16, DLL 8a1ef64a). EARLIER VTABLE DOUBT RESOLVED: the finished-check IS correct. EzChildStepBase::RegisterStepTask (0x140eb55c0) does param_1->stepper = param_3 where param_3 is the MoveMapStep primary. So FUN_140eb5550 vcalls the MoveMapStep primary vtable (0x142b5ff38) slot +0x20 = FUN_140af5190 = returns (MoveMapStep+0x48 equals -1). So the InGameStep parent (step7 STEP_MoveMap_Update) completes the load ONLY when the child step index reaches the -1 TERMINAL (set by STEP_Finish). MECHANISM: the child must walk 18(STEP_MoveMap)->19(Cleanup)->20(Finish)->-1 to finish. STEP_MoveMap walks toward -1 ONLY while the ending-request flag cVar10 is set; cVar10 is computed by advancer FUN_140afa7c0 (called from MoveMapStep update FUN_140aff730) and WRITTEN to menuData+0x5e each frame, from inputs: menuData+0x5d (return-title byte), warp-requested, CSSessionManager protocolState WaitReload, CSEventMan deadReset value 2, force-flag 0x3d856a0, FUN_140679520/530, FUN_140e62b50 value 8, pad/bot. If cVar10 stays 0 on a re-load, FUN_140afa7c0 returns early -> STEP_MoveMap never advances -> child parks at 18, never -1 -> parent waits forever = the lock. Our switch ending-request is the return-title (menuData+0x5d); if cleared/absent at the wrong time on a re-load the child cannot finish. Access paths (.data, runtime RVA equals dump RVA): GLOBAL_CSMenuMan module_base+0x3d6b7b0 then menuData +0x8; GLOBAL_CSSessionManager module_base+0x3d7a4d0; GLOBAL_CSEventMan module_base+0x3d686f8. INSTRUMENTATION LANDED (SWITCH-ORACLE): pstep=InGameStep +0x48/+0x4c, end5e=menuData+0x5e (cVar10 OUTPUT, linchpin), rt5d=menuData+0x5d, force=0x3d856a0. READ NEXT REPRO: at the lock is end5e 0 (ending request never fires; which input missing) or 1 (fires but downstream blocks); pstep should be 7. FIX: satisfy the ending request / terminal walk on every switch, likely without prematurely clearing menuData+0x5d. Same reset-the-stale-runtime-flag play as bc4. Links: reload-lock-is-runtime-session-state-not-save-borne-2026-07-16, multi-char-load-FIXED-bc4-clear-2026-07-16.

MAJOR CORRECTION (2026-06-22, PROVEN). ER PC .sl2 saves are NOT encrypted. The whole-session + prior 'decrypt the save / AES key' premise is FALSE and was never tested. Ground truth: chozandrias76/ER-Save-Editor (Rust) PC save reader (src/save/pc/) does ZERO decryption -- Save::from_path = fs::read + check 'BND4' magic + PCSave::read; PCSaveSlot::read = read [0x10 checksum] + SaveSlot (plaintext); on write checksum = md5(slot_bytes).

PROVEN on save-files/45-Slots/ER0000.sl2 slot 0 (entry @0x300): the 16 bytes @0x300 = 49414531758e2530e4bf1aa539ea662a == md5(body[0x310 .. 0x310+0x280000]) EXACTLY. So the 16-byte prefix is an MD5 INTEGRITY CHECKSUM, not an AES IV. The body (0x280000) is PLAINTEXT (entropy ~3.6, ~50% zeros, structured: body first bytes fa 00 00 00 00 00 00 1c 97 21 1d 17...). c30 candidate body+4 = 0x1c000000. The earlier openssl AES-CBC decrypt (key 18f6326605bd178a5524523ac0a0c609) gave uniform garbage (entropy 7.54) = the tell that there's nothing to decrypt + that 'key' is bogus.

FORMAT (BND4, fully in docs/bnd4-save-format.md): header 0x40 @0x00 ('BND4', fileCount=0xC, headerSize 0x40, fileHeaderSize 0x20, fileHeadersEnd 0x300); 12 entry headers (0x20 each @0x40): +0x00 rawFlags 0x50, +0x04 -1, +0x08 entrySize i64, +0x10 dataOffset i32, +0x14 nameOffset i32, +0x18 0; UTF-16 name table @0x1c0..0x300; entries USER_DATA_000..009 = 10 char slots (entrySize 0x280010 = 0x10 md5 + 0x280000 body), USER_DATA_010 (0x60010 common/settings), USER_DATA_011 (0x240020 general/profile). Slot N entry = file[dataOffset(N) .. +0x280010]; body = file[dataOffset+0x10 .. +0x280000].

IMPACT on OWN-LOAD goal: the decrypt rabbit hole is DELETED. A slot load = read .sl2, slice the plaintext body (dataOffset+0x10, 0x280000 bytes), hand it to the engine parser. NO aes/cbc crate, NO key, NO BND4-entry decrypt. The OWN-LOAD-buffer-fed-replication-recipe stands but step 3 is now 'slice plaintext body' not 'AES decrypt'. DECISIVE ARCHITECTURAL ROOT of the load2 world-completion softlock (2026-07-19, from existing RE title_tick_cover.rs:1650-1697 + all this session's runs): load2 is routed System->Quit->[return-title]->own_load reload, which drives the destination via the MoveMapStep = a MAP TRANSITION. Two coupled problems: (1) We SUPPRESS the in-world quit-save (to avoid the CSGaitemImp::Deserialize 0x14067141a crash), so the ending-request INPUTS that quit-save sets -- menuData+0x5d (rt5d) and bc4->3 -- never get set. Those inputs feed the finalize advancer FUN_140afa7c0's cVar10 (menuData+0x5e). Without them cVar10 stays 0 and the finalize parks at substate 7 (REMO/SAVE-DRAIN WAIT), the 7->8 gate never passing. (2) The completion path IS a MoveMap/return-title = TEARDOWN: forcing the finalize (7->8, warp clear, or setting md_5d) completes the step (req_code 1->2) but TEARS DOWN the player (present->False, empty world), because a MoveMap moves to a NEW map and drops the old world. Load1 (boot autoload) loads the world FRESH with NO MoveMap transition, so it never hits this. CONCLUSION: reaching genuine load2 readiness needs EITHER (a) the finalize to complete WITH the player retained -- but the MoveMap-transition path inherently tears down -- OR (b) a DIFFERENT load2 mechanism that does a FRESH WORLD LOAD like load1's boot autoload (no return-title/MoveMap transition), i.e. route the repeat load through the same clean own-load/boot path load1 uses, not the menu return-title path. This is the 'No Compromises / deeper layer' rethink. Prior md_5d-supply attempts + all forces failed for the same reason (they trigger the teardown-y MoveMap). Do NOT force; re-architect the load2 load path toward load1's clean fresh-world-load mechanism.

RUNTIME (2026-06-21, gated offline ER telemetry-only, save-safe). CONFIRMED: the cold/menu-free save full-read is DISCARDED (not enqueued) at the worker post 0x14240e420. Instrumented cold_char_mount FULL-INIT with worker-queue field + DEREF before/after submit. Result: 'q8 0x7fffa0e87200->0x7fffa0e87200 [q8] 0x1431fbbf8->0x1431fbbf8 q10 0x7fff9eaf9940->0x7fff9eaf9940 [q10] 0x144844020->0x144844020 (any change=ENQUEUED; none=DISCARDED)'. NEITHER the queue fields [worker_mgr+0x8]/[+0x10] NOR their derefs change across submit => NOT enqueued => DISCARDED. ([q10] deref = 0x144844020 = our built FD4 stream worker; [q8] deref = 0x1431fbbf8 = a .rdata vtable.) Worker_mgr=0x144852fa0, noaccept[+0x19]=0 (gate1 CLEAR). So discard is at GATE 2 (0x141ee1240: caller key not found in registry list [registry 0x144843050 +0x28]) -- the read is rejected because the calling context/key is NOT registered in the IO worker registry. Corroborated by io18/io20 SET after submit then CLEARED within ONE native frame (b80 2->0), and dev40=1/dev30=0xffffffff (device 'ready' but no per-read enqueue). FIX DIRECTION: register the calling context/key into [registry+0x28] the way the live session does (native_fullread at the live menu DRAINS). Static-RE subagent ab5ac3c129cf2515e is hunting the register/insert routine + the key gate 2 matches + whether callable cold from the game task thread. CAVEAT: queue offsets 0x8/0x10 from agent-A's enqueue decode; gate-2 result not yet captured DIRECTLY (use linux-x86-debug tracebreakpoint at 0x14240e4bf once fixed to confirm al=0). Relates: b80-DEVICE-MOUNT-REFUTED-dev40-already-set-read-actively-fails-2026-06-21, linux-x86-debug-tracebreakpoint-tool-for-wine-runtime-RE-2026-06-21.

CAPTURE EXTRACT PIPELINE WORKS (2026) + key findings. scripts/extract-capture.py now extracts real cbuffers from a .rdc via qrenderdoc --python. TWO fixes were needed:
1. DEADLOCK: replay/readback calls (SetFrameEvent, GetBufferData) from qrenderdoc's --python MAIN thread deadlock (they need the Qt event loop pumped, but block it). FIX: run the extraction on a threading.Thread and let main() RETURN, so qrenderdoc's main thread pumps the event loop. threading.excepthook -> os._exit so errors don't hang. (--list worked on main thread because it never replays to an event.)
2. API names (RenderDoc 1.44): Descriptor.resource (NOT .resourceId), .byteOffset/.byteSize; UsedDescriptor.access.{index,arrayElement,descriptorStore,stage}; st.GetConstantBlocks(stage,True)/GetReadOnlyResources/GetShaderReflection. Run: write /tmp/er-extract.json {rdc,out,event_id,skip_textures}, then  (QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen via .envs/qrenderdoc.env). Logs to /tmp/er-extract.log. First SetFrameEvent on a 2.9GB cap loads ALL resources (minutes, cold); warm cache = seconds. Killing a wedged qrenderdoc + retrying warm works.

CRITICAL FINDING — vkd3d-proton uses VK_EXT_descriptor_buffer, so RenderDoc reports EVERY cbuffer at binding 'b0' with GENERIC names (CBV/registers) — the D3D register is ERASED. Cannot match captured->our cbuffers by register. Byte SIZES are intact (e.g. cbSceneParam=2048B, unique). Forward-lit draw found: eid 4596 in frame3995.rdc = 8 vertex + 7 pixel cbuffers + 3 textures (sizes pixel: 144,3328,768,2048,512,256,256). Depth/shadow draws (eg eid 2746) are vertex-only (no pixel cbuffers).

DEEPER ISSUE: the captured draws are DIFFERENT shaders/objects than our AEG301_012 (different cbuffer counts: capture 7 pixel cb vs our 8). So resources don't map 1:1. The SCENE cbuffers (cbSceneParam shadow/camera, cbLight) + IBL are SCENE-WIDE (object-independent, location-dependent) and reusable, BUT the captured cbSceneParam contains the CAPTURED object's camera/world — to render OUR object you must MERGE: keep the captured LIGHTING fields (FC_ShadowDir@560/FC_ShadowColor@544 in cbSceneParam, cbLight, IBL) but OVERWRITE the camera (VC_MatrixViewProj@384) + world (cbInstanceData) with OURS so our object is framed. OR (cleaner) capture OUR object's own draw (AEG301_012 on screen) for a 1:1 mapping. 6 captures exist at target/runtime-probe/capture-20260629-145930/ (frames 2168-10619, last indoors). Replay harness (replay_capture.rs) currently matches by register (broken under descriptor buffers) -> switch to size + camera-merge.

RUNTIME OPS (2026-06-15). The ER boot session-degradation (boot crashes/exits progressively earlier, e.g. ~15s before reaching the title, owner never found / state=-1, AV in a Wine-side 0x6ffe module during boot) can occur with NO ER process orphans present: verified there were zero eldenring.exe/start_protected/winedevice/wineserver and zero processes with the 1245620 compatdata marker in environ (only my own shell/python that sourced the runtime env), and Steam client was healthy. So [[runtime-degradation-wine-orphans]]'s 'kill orphans' fix does NOT always apply -- the degraded state can live in the wine PREFIX files / Steam launch-tracking, not live processes. CAUTION: pressure-vessel/libexec/steam + pv-adverb + bwrap processes are ALSO the STEAM CLIENT's steamwebhelper (PPID = steam), NOT ER orphans -- do NOT kill them to 'clear orphans' (I mistakenly did; Steam self-healed by respawning). Identify ER orphans only by eldenring.exe/start_protected_game.exe/winedevice or the 1245620 prefix marker. FIX when no orphans: restart the Steam/ER stack for a clean prefix. After ~19 runtime launches in one session, expect degradation. Related: runtime-degradation-wine-orphans, er-runtime-probe-fast-teardown.

RENDER-HANDOFF FREEZE ROOT CAUSE + FIX RECIPE, statically RE-confirmed 2026-07-18 (dump+deobf ground-truthed). NOT a draw-group-setter problem, NOT SetState5-specific; UPSTREAM world-streaming gap. CHAIN: load parks at MoveMapStep::STEP_WorldResWait (dump 0x140af9de0/DEOBF 0x140af9cf0; mms_state=[MoveMapStep+0x48]==3). WorldResWait advances ONLY when FieldArea residency gate FUN_140624cb0(fieldArea,time) (DEOBF 0x140624bd0) !=0; then places player (GameMan::GetLastLoadPosition)+FUN_140af9e80 advance (DEOBF 0x140af9d90)+field8_0x50=done. Never resolves: dest block-res never created. WHY: InGameStep::STEP_MoveMap_LoadlistInit (dump 0x140aec660/DEOBF 0x140aec570) builds loadlist ONLY if InGameStep.worldloadlistlistVirtualPath.size!=0 -> CSFileImp::CreateLoadlistlistFileCap (DEOBF 0x1401f2b20) -> InGameStep.loadlistlistFileCap(+0x238). Empty path => no fileCap => WorldInfoOwner::ProcessMsbLoadLists (DEOBF 0x14066b1d0) no-op => no WorldBlockRes => WorldResWait stalls. WHO WRITES PATH: InGameStep::RequestMoveMap FUN_140aebeb0 (DEOBF 0x140aebdc0): if BlockId arg *param_2!=-1 AND IsNonDebugArea(area>>0x18) (DEOBF 0x140720210) it does GameMan::SetMoveMapStepBlockId(*param_2), field32_0x100=result, and DlFixedString<wchar_t,128>::FormatV(&worldloadlistlistVirtualPath,'map:/WorldMsbList/worldloadlistl'@143b38958). If *param_2==-1/debug-area the WHOLE build incl FormatV is SKIPPED -> empty path -> stall. STRUCT InGameStep(0xb3870): +0xd8 busy-latch,+0xe8 moveMapStep,+0x100 target BlockId,+0x108 worldloadlistlistVirtualPath DlFixedString<wchar_t,128>(.size=DLL +0x220),+0x238 loadlistlistFileCap,+0x240 _dlc02. DRAW-GROUP ChrIns+0x1b4 bit0 has NO one-shot setter ('描画グループ' only in GetChrLoadedStateViewDebugString dump 0x1403fd3e0 debug view); maintained by per-frame ChrIns omission update, held off while load unresolved, re-enabled downstream once WorldResWait resolves + MoveMapStep::STEP_Finish (DEOBF 0x140af5a20). SetState5=TitleStep::SetRequestedState (DEOBF 0x140b0d960) only writes requestedState. Cover (CSFakeLoadingScreenImp::visible+0x8; CSMenuMan loadingScreenData.field_0x11 raised in InGameStep::STEP_RequestWait dump 0x140aecd00, latch cleared only when CSMenuMan+0x798==0) gated behind same unresolved load. ===== IMPLEMENTATION RECIPE (2026-07-18, answers AV-safe) ===== BlockId struct(4B): +0=indexId,+1=regionId,+2=blockId,+3=areaId(MSB). c30=0x1c000000 => areaId=0x1c=28, IS a valid BlockId (SAME encoding as c30; pass c30 DIRECTLY, no conversion). Q1a: GameMan::SetMoveMapStepBlockId(out BlockId*, in BlockId*) DEOBF 0x14067abd0 writes GameMan+0x14 moveMapStepBlockId (+ disaster-replace via CalcGetReplaceMapIdByDisaster DEOBF 0x1406783d0 ONLY when areaId in [0x32..0x58]; 0x1c passes through). GetMoveMapStepBlockId DEOBF 0x140679340 reads GameMan+0x14. Q1c CRITICAL: GameMan+0x14 is NOT what feeds the INITIAL load. Initial RequestMoveMap param_2 comes from GameMan+0xc30 stayInMultipleAreaBlockId via FUN_140679560 (getter, DEOBF 0x140679560) -> continue-confirm copies to TitleStep.field10_0xbc -> STEP_PlayGame passes it as param_2. GameMan+0xc30 == the coordinator's 'saved-map c30' field (same offset!). So SetMoveMapStepBlockId(+0x14) does NOT fix the initial loadlist; setting +0xc30 early DOES feed it BUT only if valid BEFORE the Continue confirm reads it (ordering hazard). Q2: IsNonDebugArea(area)= 'return area < 0x59' (< 89). area 0x1c=28 < 0x59 => TRUE; m28 is fine, FormatV runs. RECOMMENDED FIX (timing-independent, single choke point): HOOK InGameStep::RequestMoveMap DEOBF 0x140aebdc0, sig (InGameStep* rcx, BlockId* rdx=param_2, int r8d=areaEntityId, coordList* r9). In detour read i32 *param_2; if *param_2==0xffffffff OR ((*param_2>>24)&0xff)>=0x59, overwrite *param_2 = c30 (deserialized saved-map BlockId, e.g.0x1c000000) THEN call original. param_2 points to a WRITABLE caller stack int (PlayGame aiStackX_8 / MoveMap_Update &puStackX_8) so *param_2 write is safe. This fixes field32_0x100 + internal SetMoveMapStepBlockId + FormatV path consistently. ALT (native-field, no hook): ensure GameMan+0xc30 = c30 BEFORE continue-confirm runs (coordinator already sets c30 -> if still stalls, it is set too late/overwritten; verify by observing +0xc30 at continue-confirm time; candidate alt field GameMan+0xac8 loadTargetMapId). Do NOT use SetMoveMapStepBlockId(0x14067abd0) for the initial load. New reusable scripts: scripts/ghidra/{DisasmCallsDecomp,DecompCallees,ListByNameSubstr,DumpDwordRvaTable,DumpPtrTableFor,BytePatternScan,StructFields}.java.

DECISIVE CORRECTION (user direct observation 2026-07-19): load1 is NOT stalling. The user moved their MOUSE and the in-game CAMERA moved with the character -> the character IS in-world and CONTROLLABLE. Position stayed static ONLY because the input driver NEVER drove it (no forward movement, no menu opened). Therefore the oracle_stepfinish_mms_state=18 / oracle_can_move=False / oracle_player_render_ready=False / oracle_chr_draw_group_enabled=False readings are FALSE-NEGATIVES (the game reached readiness/control; the oracles say otherwise -- likely reading a stale/wrong MoveMapStep pointer or the wrong readiness field). My 'load1 stalled at mms=18 / testNetStep hung / deprecated-staged-save-softlock' analysis was WRONG. LIKELY MECHANISM for the failed forward-move: the can_move probe injects a PAD STICK (pad+0x8a0=1.0 at the FD4PadDevice::poll boundary), but on NATIVE WINDOWS with KEYBOARD+MOUSE the game reads locomotion from WASD KEYBOARD (+ mouse for camera via DInput), NOT the pad stick -- so the injected pad stick is IGNORED and the character never walks, while the user's real mouse->camera works (that path is live). FIX DIRECTION: (1) the can_move oracle false-negative must be fixed OR sq-repro must not gate on it (it currently BLOCKS sq-repro's WAIT_WORLD->drive). (2) inject forward movement at the correct layer for kb+mouse -- the merged FIELD-INPUT/locomotion vector or the W key via the game's keyboard input memory (RawInput/WM), NOT the pad stick. (3) the harness's own direct-memory driver still can't open the in-world menu (no reversed eventId) and is PRESENCE_TOGGLE_ONLY. The user has NEVER seen the harness drive the char or open a menu -- that is THE deliverable gap. Supersedes [[load2-runs-on-native-windows-focus-gate-drops-stick-2026-07-19]] re: the stall misdiagnosis.

UNIFIED ROOT of the switch-load lock (2026-07-16, runtime-proven, DLL reverted to cc33164b). The "2nd bug" (specific re-load stall at MOVE MAP 18/20, rt5d=0) is NOT separate from the original bc4-frozen-at-1 bug -- it is the SAME root. CHAIN (all runtime-confirmed): our final functor (title_tick_cover.rs ~1217, submits the return-title JOB that sets menuData+0x5d=1 and completes teardown) fires ONLY when return_title_job_predicate_bc4 == GAME_MAN_RETURN_TITLE_JOB_PREDICATE_READY (bc4==3) AND phase>=TITLE_OWNER_SEEN AND DIRECT_RETURN_TITLE_CHAIN_SUBMIT_COUNT>0 AND a one-shot. On the STUCK load bc4 goes 0->1 and NEVER reaches 3 (proven: bc4 run-length over the stuck-child window was 0x12,1x7, no 3; final-functor submit fired at bc4=0x3 only for the SUCCESSFUL sibling load, never the stuck one). So: bc4 never READY -> final functor never fires -> return-title job never runs -> rt5d never set -> the incoming MoveMapStep child (needs rt5d=1 from the start of STEP_MoveMap to walk to its -1 terminal) parks at 18 -> InGameStep parent (finished == child+0x48==-1) waits forever = the stall. Successful loads: bc4 pumps 1->2->3 -> functor fires -> rt5d=1 -> child advances. WHY bc4 does not pump on the stuck load: the quit-save orchestrator FUN_140afb970 (pumps bc4 1->2, then FUN_14067aa70 2->3) is called from the OLD world's MoveMapStep step-update FUN_140aff730, which ticks only if the old world's MoveMapStep CSEzUpdateTask is registered/ticking in CSTaskGroup 20. On the accumulating stuck sequence the OLD world does not tear down fully (its MoveMapStep teardown does not run/pump) = the user's "a world that didn't get torn down fully". MY bc4-CLEAR FIX (LOAD-3 FINALIZE FIX) MAKES THIS WORSE on the stuck case: it forces bc4=0 to unblock STEP_MoveMap's per-frame gate (FUN_140679010 clears field_0x4b8 when bc4!=0), but bc4=0 PERMANENTLY SKIPS the bc4==3 the final functor needs. So the clear treats a symptom (the per-frame gate) not the root (functor firing). CORRECT FIX DIRECTION: get bc4 to READY(3) on the stuck load so the final functor fires (build+submit the return-title job / set rt5d) -- either (a) fix why the old world's MoveMapStep teardown does not pump bc4 (task-registration accumulation over N loads), or (b) when bc4 is stuck at 1 with the load stalling, PUMP bc4 1->2->3 via the game's quit-save fns (FUN_14067b840/ba30 then FUN_14067aa70) OR fire the final functor directly, THEN let bc4->0 so STEP_MoveMap's gate clears and the child advances with rt5d set. Do NOT just clear bc4=0 (that is the current inadequate fix). All prior rt5d/menuData+0x5d fix attempts (force-set, defer-clear) FAILED because rt5d was never SET (functor never fired), not cleared. FAILED-fix DLLs: f826a4ad/dd67bbd3 (defer, reverted). Current cc33164b = reverted to known-good clear + all instrumentation. Links: multi-char-load-FIXED-bc4-clear-2026-07-16 (the bc4-clear fix, now understood as symptom-treatment), ending-request-fix-was-wrong-model-correction-2026-07-16, switch2-orchestrator-not-called-2026-07-16 (this was RIGHT).

TEARDOWN ROOT = CPU CORE CONTENTION, not a real stall (user 2026-07-20): the user runs cargo builds with OTHER parallel agents, and Elden Ring's asset loading is bound to a SINGLE CORE. Parallel cargo starves that core -> the loading bar progresses SLOWLY (the bar increases but labels/numerator crawl) -> the LOADING_PROGRESS_STALLED_10S teardown fires PREMATURELY on what is core-starvation slowness, not an actual hang. This is the determining variable behind the 'fluke' runs (per not-determined-not-nondeterministic). FIX (user directive): LOOSEN the loading-progress-stall teardown constraint (currently ~10s) so core-contention-slow loads are not torn down early. This overrides the 2026-07-19 standing order that made 10s a hard stall semaphore -- that order assumed a stalled bar; under parallel-cargo core contention a slow-but-progressing bar must be tolerated. Update the stall threshold (raise it substantially, e.g. 30-60s) and note the override in AGENTS.md. The CSSystemStep loading-substep oracle (oracle_system_step_label) now exists to distinguish real hangs (label frozen) from slow progress (label advancing).

RELIABLE dump<->deobf address mapping SOLVED (2026-06-23). TOOL: scripts/dump-deobf-shift.py '0x<dump_va>' (--reverse for deobf->dump, --json, --no-region to disable region assist). It is a RELOCATION-AWARE content matcher: decodes insns at the src VA with capstone, WILDCARDS the bytes that legitimately differ between the two images (RIP-relative disp32 + relative-branch rel8/rel32 -- they re-encode because the code moved), and matches the stable opcode/modrm skeleton in the other image. Unique match = exact ground truth (method content-unique). Validated: dump 0x14266def0->deobf 0x14266df00 (+0x10), 0x140e6da37->0x140e6d987 (-0xb0), etc. On named functions: ~78% content-verified, ~21% flagged region-estimate, ~99%+ resolved; the rest are exception funclets/import thunks with too few stable bytes (not real targets).

DRIVER (corrects ghidra-dump-vs-deobf-address-shift-2026-06-21): the shift is PIECEWISE-CONSTANT PER CODE REGION, NOT one constant. Measured: 0 near base; irregular -0x80..-0x120 staircase in low .text (0x1401-0x140d); rock-solid -0x20 across 0x140e-0x141e; rock-solid +0x10 across 0x141f-0x1426; messy tail. The documented '0x10-0x11' was just ONE region's value.

NOT ARXAN (disproven, don't chase dearxan for this): shift-step boundaries do NOT coincide with Arxan stubs (0/457 within 0x40; dump & deobf have ~equal stub counts 1604 vs 1619). dearxan decrypts encrypted regions IN PLACE at the same RVA -- regenerating eldenring-deobf.bin via dearxan from the on-disk exe produced a BYTE-IDENTICAL file, so 'improve deobf coverage' is a no-op and dearxan cannot compute the shift. The ~few-% genuinely-absent funcs are dearxan's hard un-decryptable limit + the packed 2nd .text (0x144c0e000+).

REGION ASSIST: committed table scripts/dump-deobf-shift.regions.tsv (107 entries, VA->shift, no copyrighted bytes) disambiguates multi-skeleton matches (real one sits on the regional shift -> content+region) and ESTIMATES when no src bytes (zeroed/non-resident dump page) -- estimates are verified=False, can be off by one region step near a boundary, VERIFY with disasm.

ER shader READ PATH validated end-to-end 2026-06-25. Chain: Data0.bhd/bdt -> managed RSA BHD5 decrypt -> BDT read -> DCX-KRAK Oodle decompress (under wine, oo2core_6_win64.dll) -> BND4 -> member DXContainer -> dxc -dumpbin -> readable DXIL IR. Toolchain: prebuilt Linux dxc from microsoft/DirectXShaderCompiler releases (linux_dxc_*.tar.gz) installed at ~/tools/dxc; wrapper scripts/shaderlab/dxc.sh. IMPORTANT: modern llvm-dis (LLVM22) CANNOT read DXIL bitcode (error: i8 must be 8-bit aligned) -- DXIL is a custom LLVM-3.7 fork; must use dxc's disassembler. dxc -dumpbin on a member shows: I/O sigs, original FromSoft debug pdb path (..\Dist\GR\WIN_D3D12_Pdb\flver\...), shader hash, PSV info, NAMED cbuffers (cbMtdParam/cbMatDynParam/cbInstanceData with fields FC_GlowScale, FC_EmissiveColor, FC_PhantomEdgeColor, FC_BloodAmount, ...) and full DXIL IR (define void @ps_main, @dx.op.sample/cbufferLoadLegacy/createHandle/discard). Reflection is intact. Edit path: recompile HLSL->DXIL with dxc (auto-signs HASH chunk); raw IR edits need re-signing via dxil.dll or driver rejects. dxil-extract.py carves raw bitcode from DXIL chunk. Docs: docs/shaderlab/er-shader-pipeline.md.

STATIC RE (2026-06-18, objdump on eldenring-deobf.bin base 0x140000000). DECISIVE correction to the cold-mount model. load_activate 0x1409a4670 has TWO selector-construction paths gated by boolean al at 0x1409a478e (je 0x1409a48d6 when al==0). EXACT GATE (rsi=slot record 0x140261b80(ProfileSummary,slot)=profile+0x18+slot*0x2a0; bl=1): record null -> NON-build 0x1409a48d6; [rec+0x295]<1 -> NON-build; [rec+0x295]>=1 AND [rec+0x44]==2 (accessor 0x140e362c0='mov eax,[rcx+0x44]'; cmpl 2; sete; INVERTED at 0x1409a4776 jne 0x1409a477e xor al) -> NON-build 0x1409a48d6; [rec+0x295]>=1 AND [rec+0x44]!=2 -> BUILD path 0x1409a4794 (register via 0x140762d50 rdx=0x61edb into MENU group 0x143d87350). KEY: NON-build path 0x1409a48d6 is where builder 0x140826510 is called (at 0x1409a4980, ret addr 0x9a4985 == the GOLD capture's 'builder called from inside load_activate 0x9a4985'). So the LIVE mount took NON-build with [rec+0x295]>=1 && [rec+0x44]==2 (Case C). The session's faked rec+0x44=2 was CORRECT to reach the builder; the gate was NOT the cold wall. THE WALL IS PUMPING: builder 0x140826510 -> factory 0x140821bf0 (vtable 0x142ac71e0 = CS::MenuJobWithContext<LoadJobContext,lambda>) builds the selector step; post-builder calls 0x1407a7bb0/0x140744c40 wire step+menu_deser into the DIALOG task slot (dialog+0x50/+0x10); the dialog is a child of MENU FD4 task-group 0x143d87350 (wide-str 'MENU' at 0x142bddfe8, boot-built 0x140e19891, 401 readers) -> ticked each frame ONLY when MENU group steps. A cold direct-built dialog is NOT a stepping child of MENU group, so the selector is never ticked -> installer 0x140828270 never runs -> io18/io20 never submitted -> menu_deser 0x14082c240 never runs. menu_deser reads FULL save intrinsically: 0x14082c2c8 alloc 0x280000 via 0x143d872e0 -> 0x14067b100; poll 0x140679180 state 3=resident from installer's io18/io20 request (NOT b80). Related: convergence-ACTIVATE-populates-dialog-rows-but-selector-mount-still-walled-2026, stage2-menu-drive-load-recipe-verified-2026, menu-load-capture-mount-is-io18-not-b80-2026, pathB-selector-step-0x140826d50-tick-abi-2026.

freeze-is-ALTERNATING-parity-not-onetime-2026-07-18: USER DATA (2026-07-18, manual 4th load 'for kicks'): load4 MIRRORS load2 -- no visible character, no movement (frozen). So the pattern is NOT 'load2 freezes then load3 recovers permanently'; it ALTERNATES by parity: load1(autoload)=GOOD, load2=FROZEN, load3=GOOD(recover), load4=FROZEN, ... A reload FROM a good/rendered state freezes; a reload FROM a frozen state recovers. This means the stale element is a TOGGLE set on a successful render-handoff and NOT cleared, which breaks the NEXT reload's finalize; the frozen load's teardown clears it so the following load recovers, which re-sets it, breaking the next. Supersedes the 'one-time stale-state cleared by load2 teardown' framing (that was only the first two transitions). RE TARGET: find the state that is SET when render-ready/draw_group is enabled (successful load) and is READ as a blocker of STEP_MoveMap 18->19 / requestCode 1->2 finalize on the next reload, but is CLEARED by a frozen load's teardown. Instrument candidate toggles across loads 1-4 (bc4, mms18 external-finalize gate, requestCode, CSRemo/testNetStep sub-gates, return-title/warp/save flags) and find the one that flips good/frozen/good/frozen in lockstep. Links [[freeze-deterministic-load2-recovers-load3-stale-state-2026-07-18]] [[grounded-gate2-mms18-external-finalize-never-fires-not-fade-2026-07-18]] [[re-correction-second-gate-requestcode-stepfinish-2026-07-18]].

CORRECTION (2026-07-15 design pass): the '~13s model build' I cited as the fundamental wall for the native animated portrait is STALE/WRONG. lookat_bone_hooks.rs:513-517's 13s comment is superseded by portrait_lookat.rs:194-199 (run #10) which MEASURED ~94-133ms and explicitly refutes the world-streaming-contention theory. The character model builds FAST (~100ms ResMan work), NOT 13s. The real delays/blockers are: (1) the Continue TEARDOWN at ~+17s freeing the kicked renderer, and the +0x755 6-tick teardown landmine DESTROYING a just-built model (must rebuild the table AFTER teardown on the PROFILE_LOADSCREEN_TABLE_OWNED path + re-kick when the renderer identity changes); (2) the FUN_141e90290 drive crash (settle gate = 8 identical inner-pointer frames, no AV in runs 30-31); (3) GX cmd-queue overflow if >1 renderer builds (keep it single-target, oracle_portrait_foreign_models==0); (4) the READBACK is the LINCHPIN unknown -- it MUST run on the game device (color_res.GetDevice; no cross-device handle to move the copy to our isolated overlay), untested standalone on native, may survive where the model-drive doesn't (it's a copy not a render-submit). model-built truth = model_ins at renderer+0x778 (NOT +0x754/+0x755 which are request latches). COLD SAVE-DIR FIX (implemented in cold_char_mount_drive PHASE_INIT, src/experiments.rs, before submit). ROOT CAUSE: the menu-free cold full read completes EMPTY (b80 2->0 one frame) because the worker builds a MALFORMED path -- the request's directory std::u16string is unset, so the worker's '%s\%s%s%s' (fmt 0x1431fc3c8 +.bak) yields a bare '.sl2' and the open fails fast. Gate 2 (0x141ee1240) PASSES; the read DOES enqueue; it fails in the worker on the bad path. The LIVE title->Continue boot populates that directory via the iodev state machine opcode 0x17/0x18 handler 0x140e6ded0; the menu-free path skips that opcode. FIX = replay the two native steps directly before submit: (1) SAVE_DIR_BUILDER 0x140e0e680(rcx=&out_u16string)->out (RVO): self-fetches userdata folder (SHGetFolderPathW CSIDL 0x1a) + Steam id (0x140e8d550), formats '%s/EldenRing/%s/' (fmt 0x142bda858). Guard: Steam interface *0x143b48ff0 must be non-null. (2) SAVE_DIR_SETTER 0x14240a2a0(rcx=[iodev+0x20] path-DB, edx=slot=0, r8=&out) stores dir into path DB (via 0x14240dce0 -> +0xb0). iodev from getter 0x140e6e060(). MSVC std::u16string is 0x20 bytes: SSO/heap union @+0, _Mysize @+0x10, _Myres @+0x18; empty = size 0 cap 7. Validation: post-submit readback of request io18 ([iodev+0x18]) directory at +0xe8 data/+0xf8 size -- size>0 = fix propagated through copy-ctor. Ground truth = b80 reaching 3 (RESIDENT). Consts added to src/lib.rs: SAVE_DIR_BUILDER_RVA=0xe0e680, SAVE_DIR_SETTER_RVA=0x240a2a0, STEAM_INTERFACE_GUARD_RVA=0x3b48ff0, U16STRING_SIZE/CAP_OFFSET. UNVERIFIED at runtime as of this write -- gated probe pending. Caveat: aliasing between path-DB [iodev+0x20]+0xb0 (setter target) and request copy-source [iodev+0x18]+0xe8 not fully decoded; readback confirms whether it reached the request.

GOTCHA: the headless MCP daemon (scripts/ghidra/mcp-ghidra-daemon.sh) takes an EXCLUSIVE Ghidra PROJECT-level lock, so it monopolizes the whole project while running. Any other analyzeHeadless on the same project (scripts/ghidra-query.sh, find-functions-rf.sh, custom Java) fails: 'LockException: Unable to lock project'. This is INDEPENDENT of --readonly (project-level, not program-level) -> read-only buys no concurrency, hence writable is the default. To run one-shot Java analysis on the daemon's project, stop the daemon first (mcp-ghidra-daemon.sh stop) then restart; for warm queries use the MCP tools. RF finder uses the separate erdeobf project so it doesn't conflict with the ermaporch daemon. find-functions-rf.sh detects this lock and points at the stop command. See [[ghidra-mcp-headless-prewarmed-daemon-2026-06-22]] and docs/ghidra-mcp-and-rf-finder.md.

FLOOR NAILED (2026-06-24, build-floor per-frame probe, onscreen runs 171834+172006). The pab_dismiss->menu_open residual after pab-outro-skip is NOT the menu build and NOT dialog settle -- it is BOOT-INIT TASK STARVATION. Per-frame TitleTopDialog SM logging shows the dialog is built + settled in Loop with a40==0 (accept-eligible) by ~12.9s (build ~43ms), but STEP_MenuJobWait (the title task) then STALLS: a DETERMINISTIC ~1271ms tick-gap (run1 12912->14183, run2 13092->14363, identical 1271ms) + a ~650-850ms gap, ~1.9-2.1s total, during which the title/menu task does NOT tick (synchronous boot-init blocks the thread; CPU-bound boot per the boot-parallelization profiler). So the accept-byte (menu_open) cannot be set until init releases the task. menu_open floor = ~15.37s wall-clock, ROCK SOLID across runs (15371ms / 15365ms, 4ms apart). CORRECTS the earlier "1431ms build" reading: that was starvation (the fadein-skip detour could not run during the stall, not slow build). The menu is READY ~2.5s before it can open. THE FLOOR IS BOOT-INIT, not the title UI: lowering it requires eliminating/parallelizing the ~1271ms (+~700ms) synchronous init stalls that block the title task -- the boot-parallelization spike target, NOT a title-flow lever. pab-outro-skip already extracts all the title-flow slack. Probe was reverted (read-only build-floor logging removed); parser kept at scripts/parse-build-floor.py. issue er-effects-rs-glz.

CORRECTION (2026-06-30, user caught it + pixel-diff confirmed): the earlier 'cursor tracking PROVEN' claim in this key was WRONG -- an over-claim. The displayed/captured head does NOT visibly track the cursor.

PIXEL-DIFF GROUND TRUTH (the 3 bucket dumps): slot200(cursor LEFT,probe4) vs slot202(cursor RIGHT,probe3) = 95% IDENTICAL pixels (meanAbsDiff 0.4/px) -- the two OPPOSITE extremes are the same image. The only sizable diff (slot200 vs slot201 same-run, 39% identical) does NOT correspond to a cursor-driven turn (it's render-settling/timing noise, not a head rotation). So the head is NOT following the cursor in the rendered pixels.

ROOT CAUSE (our own RE comments, startup_hooks.rs ~2950): the per-frame 'draw_step' (FUN_1409aa3e0 -> FUN_140bb73a0) is a CLEAR-render-target (FUN_141e8af80 = ClearRTV), NOT a rasterize. And post-table-build (PROFILE_LOADSCREEN_TABLE_BUILDS>0) even the clear is SKIPPED. So nothing re-rasterizes the posed model per-frame. The look-at writes the Head/Neck/Spine2 bones (hook_hits fire) and the push hook propagates to GPU skinning, but WITHOUT a real render pass the skinned result is never drawn into the offscreen RT. The captured/displayed head is just the engine's LAST genuine model render (its own slow cadence / at rebuild), so it's static and does not reflect look-at.

WHAT IS STILL TRUE: (1) display -- the head IS composited onto the real loading screen (one on-screen screenshot). (2) the readback per-frame republish fix (find_d3d12_resource(off) fallback) is real and made gx_nonblack True. (3) bones are driven. But NONE of that produces a VISIBLE per-frame head that tracks the cursor, because the model is not re-rasterized per-frame.

THE REAL BLOCKER (== the keystone): force a genuine per-frame RE-RASTERIZATION of the posed profile model (re-skin current bones + draw into the offscreen RT) on the render thread, post-Continue. draw_step is only a clear; need the actual model DRAW (the per-slot render that engine runs at build). This is the same core as the Tier-2 KEEPALIVE epic. Until the model re-renders per-frame with the current pose, neither 'autoupdate each frame' (visible) nor 'track mouse' (visible) is achieved. The cursor-sweep harness + bucket dumps + erpx-rs crate + readback fix are valid infra, but the CONCLUSION 'tracking works' was false. Goal NOT met. Relates: interactive-face-levers-roadmap-2026-06-29 (Tier-2 KEEPALIVE), autoload-load-FIXED-display-crash-remains-2026-06-30.

DEFINITIVE Q2/Q3/Q4 verdict + recipe (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY disasm). Answers the brief's 'does the dispatcher stop after the placeholder load completes' hypothesis and gives the cleanest in-context real-slot recipe. Cited fresh disasm.

=== Q2 VERDICT: the 'dispatcher stops after the placeholder load completes' hypothesis is FALSE ===
- MoveMapStep update 0x140aff640 calls all 3 b80 dispatchers (0x140afbad0 @0x140aff720, 0x140afb880 @0x140aff728, 0x140afa6d0 @0x140aff730) with a plain 'mov rcx,rbx; call' sequence and NO conditional branch guarding them (disasm of 0x140aff640 top confirms straight-line). recon_callscan: each dispatcher has EXACTLY 1 caller = those sites. So whenever the update runs, the dispatchers run.
- 0x140aff640 has ZERO E8/E9 callers (recon_callscan) -> it is invoked via the step vtable by the FD4 scheduler every frame while the MoveMapStep is the active step. The brief confirms the world renders + step ticks, so the dispatchers ARE pumped each frame. The load machine does NOT stop just because the placeholder world finished; the dispatcher keeps offering b72 to 0x14067b750 every frame.
- THEREFORE b80 staying 0 is NOT because the dispatcher stopped. It is an upstream SUBMIT-GATE failure (see b80-submit-kick-exact-false-gate-decoded-2026): most likely a STALE in-flight request on IO subsys 0x144589390 ([+0x10]!=0 or [+0x20]!=0) left by the placeholder m60 load, making async submit 0x140e6f940 bail FALSE @0x140e6f976/981 every frame; or the buffer alloc rsi==0 @0x14067b7c2; secondarily, even on submit-success the read never drains (stream worker task 0x144842d40 unregistered at our forced FE-host -- per stream-worker-registration-is-the-b80-wall-verified-2026).

=== Q4 VERDICT: load-the-REAL-slot-FIRST is the cleanest design (avoids the placeholder-then-swap dirty-handle trap) ===
The placeholder-first design is itself the problem: loading m60 placeholder leaves the IO subsys handle 0x144589390 and GameMan load-phase fields (c30, ac0, bc4) in a half-used state, then the real-slot submit collides with that residue (stale [iodev+0x10]/[+0x20]) or with c30 reverting to the placeholder. CLEANEST: make the FIRST load be the real slot so there is no swap.
RECIPE (in-context, zero-input), arm in idx10 PHASE1 BEFORE SetState(5):
 1. Set GameMan(0x143d69918)+0xac0 = real slot (0..9). [native autoload slot field]
 2. Set GameMan+0xb72 = 1 (autoload arm). [b72 force]
 3. Ensure b80==0, latch [0x143d856a0]==0, [GameMan+0xbc4]!=3, [inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 +0x13c]==0 (so getter 0x1406793d0 passes and dispatcher-2 G3 passes). These hold at idle pre-load.
 4. Then SetState(5) so MoveMapStep builds and its update 0x140aff640 starts ticking -> dispatcher-2 0x140afb880 sees b72 on a CLEAN IO handle and calls 0x14067b750 -> submit enqueues the REAL slot's 0x280000 read with NO stale residue -> b80 1.
 CAVEAT (the remaining wall): even first-load-real-slot still needs the STREAM WORKER 0x144842d40 REGISTERED+TICKED for the read to drain (b80 1->2->3) and the world to MSB-stream. SetState(5) skips IngameInit's tail 0x140b0a980 (+0x48>=7 arm) AND the menu MoveMapListStep helper 0x140af1b40 -- the only two registrars (per stream-worker-registration-is-the-b80-wall-verified-2026). So real-slot-first removes the dirty-handle submit failure but NOT the worker-drain wall.

=== Q3: the worker-registration lever (the genuine remaining blocker) ===
Two options, ranked:
 (A) [LEAST RISK, native] Do NOT use SetState(5) as the world host. Instead let the NATIVE title-accept -> PlayGame -> InGameStep -> IngameInit tail 0x140b0a980 (+0x48>=7 arm) run, which registers 0x144842d40 with the FD4 scheduler the native way. Our SetState(5) shortcut is what skips it. Closing that gap (drive the native FE bootstrap / IngameInit tail rather than SetState(5)) makes b80 complete natively. This is the autoload-dll-product-requirements 'drive native Continue' direction.
 (B) [direct, needs ABI reverse] Replicate the MoveMapListStep helper 0x140af1b40 tail from our handler once a session exists: lazily build task 0x144842d40 (via 0x141eceb10 if null), build the SSO name descriptor (string 0x1429d2b28 via name-builder 0x140116c20), then ecx=0x59682f01 (key); CALL FD4-scheduler-register 0x142656b00 @ the 0x140af1c05 pattern (task ptr bound at [rsp+0x30], name at [rsp+0x38]). OPEN/UNREVERSED: exact 0x142656b00 descriptor ABI (rdx/r8/r9 packing) -- decode 0x140af1b40 0x140af1bde..0x140af1c05 + 0x142656b00 fully before calling to avoid an FD4 assert (0x141eb97a0)/AV. NOTE: MoveMapListStep idx0 0x140af1720 hard-asserts session 0x144588e98!=0 + resmgr 0x143d5b0f8!=0, so option B requires a live session (our SetState(5) world IS in-session, so this precondition is met in-context).

=== CRASH RISK / VERIFY ===
- Do NOT poke b80=2/3 cold: deserialize 0x14067b290 needs IO-request state 0x14240a1f0==0x14 (resident) else no-op/free; dispatcher-2 apply arm @0x140afb9d7 derefs CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 (FD4 assert if null -> crash).
- VERIFY real-slot-first cleared the dirty handle: [0x144589390+0x10]==0 && [+0x20]==0 at the moment 0x14067b750 runs. VERIFY engagement: [GameMan+0xb80] 0->1; then with worker registered 1->2->3; IO-request state 0x14240a1f0 -> 0x14; [GameMan+0xc30] != 0xa010000 default; child(InGameStep+0x2e8)+0xd8 1->2->0.
Related: b80-submit-kick-exact-false-gate-decoded-2026, b80-blocker-final-synthesis-and-recipe-2026, stream-worker-registration-is-the-b80-wall-verified-2026, async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026, autoload-dll-product-requirements.

BREAKTHROUGH (2026-06-22, golden deep-stack sw-bp at MountEblArchive 0x1efc00, 40-qword RAW stack). The PLAYER-MAP mount (user's load, +16s) and the BOOT common-archive mounts (+0.8-2.8s) climb DISJOINT call chains -- proving the player map has its own in-game orchestrator the boot path never touches (and our menu-free SetState5 path skips). ER .shaderbdle = the render-ready per-material shader bundle (ground-truthed 2026-06-25). /shader/shaderbdle.shaderbdlebnd.dcx (750 members; +dlc01/dlc02/speedtree/_[rt] variants) -> each member 'CS[<material>][quals].shaderbdle' is ITSELF a BND4 containing the COMPLETE compiled DX shader set for that material: e.g. CS[DetailBlend][Rich][VA_Frame] holds 142 DXBC/DXIL containers = submesh-slot (_0_,_1_) x render pass (_Fwd,_Fwd_[A],_FwdDpt_[A],_FwdSSdw,_FwdSdw,_Gbuf,_Gbuf_[A],_GBufDpt,_DptA,_Velo,_PhantomLight...), .vpo(vertex)+.ppo(pixel) each with embedded ISG1 input signature (POSITION/NORMAL/TANGENT/COLOR/TEXCOORD/SV_InstanceID), OSG1 output sig, PSV0 resource info. So for the integrated render we do NOT need the generic /shader/gxflvershader.shaderbnd GXFlver_* members -- the per-material .shaderbdle gives exact vertex+pixel pair + vertex layout, organized by submesh x pass. Render path: FLVER material -> matbin (C[...].spx + samplers/textures + FC_* params) -> CS[...].shaderbdle -> pick submesh _N_ + pass -> passthrough render. OPEN RE QUESTION (the M1 crux): how matbin shader_path 'C[Detail3Blend].spx' resolves to a 'CS[...].shaderbdle' name -- naming differs (C[] vs CS[], 'Detail3Blend' vs 'DetailBlend', + [Rich]/[VA_Frame]/[S2] quals likely from FLVER vertex layout + quality). Resolve offline by cross-referencing all 15103 matbin shader_paths vs all bundle names. All data extracted under target/er-objectkit/{matbin,shaderbdle,flvershader}. See bd matbin-shaderpath-spx-indirection-2026-06-25, integrated-object-view-feasibility-2026-06-25.

APPROACH B -- SOLVED, ready to implement (RE complete 2026-07-01). THE NATIVE CLOSE PRIMITIVE: FUN_1407ac980 (dump 0x1407ac980, live/deobf 0x7ac890, content-unique) takes RCX=MenuWindow*, does MenuJobResult::SetResult(&r, Failed, 0) then calls window->vtable+0x60(window, r) -- the window's native close vmethod. This is the clean cancel/back close. MECHANISM PROVEN: CS::MenuWindowJob::Run (live 0x7ad1c0) ends with: r = owningMenuWindow->field254_0x1e8; if(MenuJobResult::ShouldContinue(&r)) loadParams->jobResult=r (TERMINAL -> ExecuteMenuJob pops the job, clears queue[0]); else SetResult(Continue) (stays at head). ShouldContinue = (Continue < state); terminal states Success/Failed > Continue. So closing ProfileSelect = make its MenuWindow (SYSTEM_QUIT_PROFILE_SELECT_WINDOW, = job+0x130 owner) result at +0x1e8 terminal; FUN_1407ac980 does that natively + runs the window close vmethod. NATIVE RETURN-TITLE SUBMIT (authoritative): FUN_1409616f0 (Quit-Game return-title DO) -> FUN_14094e9f0 (live 0x94ec70): if(!IsNotAlone(party) && FUN_1407a9320(owner+0x10)/*queue-ready: head empty*/){ build FUN_14079d7f0(owner+0x50) ; submit FUN_1407a9340(owner+0x10) }. So the native code ALSO gates on queue-ready(owner+0x10 head==0) -- confirming ProfileSelect MUST be popped first; no shortcut. IMPLEMENTATION: (1) always BLOCK the in-world load-job Run (system_quit_profile_load_job_run_hook) for this flow -- never deserialize into live world; (2) arm autoload target = picked slot (system_quit_arm_quickload_autoload, already done); (3) from a per-tick handler (system_quit_profile_select_top_menu_tick -- OUTSIDE the job Run, avoids re-entrancy), call FUN_1407ac980(SYSTEM_QUIT_PROFILE_SELECT_WINDOW) ONCE to natively close ProfileSelect -> pump pops it -> queue[0] clears; (4) existing system_quit_submit_direct_return_title_chain retry then finds queue ready and submits the return-title chain -> title teardown; (5) autoload loads picked slot after title; (6) REMOVE the gaitem guards (skip-deserialize 0x671130/empty-lookup 0x671810/skip-finalize 0x671670) -- they only corrupt CSGaitemImp and are unneeded once the in-world load never runs. Related: [[menu-job-queue-pump-dequeue-mechanism]] [[GOAL-system-quit-load-profile-switch-character]].

ME3 profile pitfall for er-effects-rs: do not include an empty savefile override such as savefile equals empty string. It makes ME3 pass an empty alternative savefile and can produce corrupted-save / no active profile slots. Omit savefile entirely to use the default active Steam-user save.

CRITICAL corrected semantics (Ghidra decompile of CSNowLoadingHelperImp::Update at 0x1402a2c40): the byte our now_loading_active() reads (CSNowLoadingHelperImp+0xed = load_done) is a load-COMPLETE latch, NOT 'loading in progress'. Update does loadDone_ = requestLoadDone_; requestLoadDone_ = false; where requestLoadDone_ is raised by the map-load system when the map finishes. So load_done is FALSE while loading and TRUE once the load completes, and it LINGERS true into gameplay (map-load system keeps asserting it in a loaded map). Runtime-confirmed: load_done went 0->1 at load completion (~+29.8s) and stayed 1 through gameplay. CONSEQUENCE: the loading-screen-portrait overlay must show the head while !load_done (loading) and pop at the load_done false->true edge (load complete). The earlier 'now_loading seen-then-gone' stop logic had inverted polarity -> head persisted into gameplay + tanked FPS. Fixed in src/experiments/gpu_readback.rs composite_portrait_inner (OVERLAY_STOP_REASON=1 at load complete). IN_WORLD_REACHED is NOT a valid pop signal: PlayerIns/controllable latches ~1.7s BEFORE the loading screen ends (player exists through the loading screen) -> premature pop.
