PRODUCT REQUIREMENT CLARIFICATION (user, 2026-06-17). When the autoload feature is DONE, the user is NEVER expected to see the startup menu -- thus NEVER sees ANY startup popups either (incl. the 'A connection error occurred / Unable to start in online mode' OK-modal that appears under offline-launcher.exe with EAC bypassed). The DLL must drive the ENTIRE boot->loaded-character flow HEADLESS: dismiss/disable the connection-error online-attempt popup, advance past the press-any-button title, reach the main menu, drive the Load-Game mount + confirm, and stream the character's world -- all WITHOUT the user seeing the title/menu/popups. Implication: the connection-error modal must be handled BY THE DLL (root-cause disable the online attempt, or auto-dismiss the dialog), NOT by a user OK press (there is no user at boot). The user CONFIRMED the modal is the NORMAL offline fallback ('just needs OK') and asked to find what triggers it and disable that mechanism. The 'faster than native' proof: the NATIVE baseline is the full manual path (dismiss popup -> press -> main menu -> Load Game -> scroll to slot -> confirm -> in-world); the DLL does all of it headless during boot, so it is dramatically faster. Related: autoload-dll-product-requirements, stage2-menu-drive-load-recipe-verified-2026, no-compromises-standard.

LOADING-SCREEN PORTRAIT -- D3D12 PRESENT OVERLAY infra BUILT; blocker = finding the game's live swapchain under vkd3d-proton (2026-06-30). The in-pipeline routes are dead (Scaleform separate GFx texture, see postcontinue-portrait-EXHAUSTIVE-2026-06-30); the overlay is the deterministic path and its hook infra now exists.

DONE (src/experiments/present_overlay.rs, committed, gated on portrait_lookat, compiles, installs, no crash, autoload intact player=1):
- install_present_overlay_hook(): builds a THROWAWAY hidden-window swapchain (vkd3d-proton has NO DirectComposition -> CreateSwapChainForComposition returns 0x80004001 'Not implemented'; must use CreateSwapChainForHwnd with a real HWND -> needs Cargo feature Win32_Graphics_Gdi for WNDCLASSW). Reads IDXGISwapChain vtable[8]=Present, vtable[22]=Present1; MinHooks both; also hooks the CreateDXGIFactory2 dxgi export.
- present_hook/present1_hook log first-hit + backbuffer desc, tail-call original.

BLOCKER: under vkd3d-proton NEITHER Present hook fires on the game's frames, and the CreateDXGIFactory2 export hook NEVER fires either. Conclusions: (1) the game creates its DXGI factory/swapchain BEFORE our +280ms install (export chain is too late), and (2) the dummy hidden-window swapchain's vtable funcs (Present=0x6ffffcb8fcf0) are NOT the same as the GAME's swapchain's (so hooking the dummy's vtable funcs doesn't catch the game). MinHook DOES work on Wine modules (our dinput8 GetDeviceState hooks fire), so it's purely swapchain-identity. The present/swapchain is NOT in CSFlipperImp/UpdateFlipTiming (pure frame-pacing, QPC/sleep, no swapchain) nor CSWindowImp (window/screen-mode mgmt). It IS in the GX graphics device CSGraphicsImp (dump 0x140b17b80) which allocates ~19 render sub-objects (param_1->field1_0x8 .. field19_0x98) and ends with FUN_140b1b040(param_1) -- the swapchain is created/held several layers in there.

NEXT (find the game's live swapchain, then hook ITS Present): decompile CSGraphicsImp FUN_140b1b040 + sub-object ctors to locate the IDXGISwapChain pointer + its global/offset (the call to IDXGIFactory2::CreateSwapChainForHwnd, factory vtable[15]); at runtime read GLOBAL graphics device -> swapchain -> *(swapchain) vtable -> [8]/[22], MinHook THOSE (the game's actual Present, MinHook-patchable). Then DRAW: the portrait is already a GPU texture via upload_rgba_to_texture; in the Present hook get the current backbuffer (swapchain GetBuffer/GetCurrentBackBufferIndex), transition PRESENT->COPY_DEST, CopyTextureRegion(backbuffer<-portrait) matched to backbuffer format (likely R8G8B8A8 or B8G8R8A8 -- handle RB swap), transition back, then call original Present. Gate on now_loading_active + PROFILE_BAKE_RGBA_CAPTURED. Alternative if GX-RE stalls: hook vkQueuePresentKHR (Vulkan export) -- but drawing then needs a Vulkan path (heavy). All overlay infra committed; ~60 capped runs this session.

KEY CORRECTION (2026-06-21) to the Arxan-wall worry. The ToS-build INVOKE is READABLE (not Arxan). Runtime wrapper-hook captured oracle_policy_ctor_wrapper_caller_rva=0x1407ad2bc (lever-3 run). Disasm at 0x1407ad2a0: 'mov rcx,[rdi+0xa8]; (null-check); mov rax,[rcx]; lea rdx,[rdi+0x10]; call [rax+0x10]  -> returns 0x1407ad2bc; mov r15,rax; store r15 into [rdi+0x130] (replacing/releasing old [rdi+0x130])'. So this is a GENERIC menu-component builder: it _Do_call's the std::function build-lambda at this+0xa8 (vtable+0x10 = _Func_impl _Do_call) with arg &this+0x10, and stores the built dialog at this+0x130. There is NO 'if save skip' gate at the invoke -- it unconditionally invokes whatever lambda sits at this+0xa8. So the ROUTING decision (build ToS vs go straight to main menu) is UPSTREAM: WHERE this+0xa8 is set to the ToS-build lambda vs not. That upstream path is statically tractable (NOT the Arxan-VM'd 0x140e4fda0 gate; only the ACCEPTED-value gate itself is Arxan).
NOTE: this+0x130 is the same offset as TITLE_OWNER_MENU_LIST_130 (the title owner's committed menu list) -> 'this' (rdi) is plausibly the title owner / a CSMenu step, and this+0xa8 is one of its component build-lambdas. With a save (vanilla) the component set at this+0xa8 likely excludes the ToS lambda; our lever-advance leaves it set.
NEXT (clean, static): (1) find the function entry containing 0x1407ad2bc + identify 'this'/rdi class (vtable); (2) find where [this+0xa8] is WRITTEN (the build-lambda installer) and the save/first-boot condition there; (3) make the zero-input advance take the branch that does NOT install the ToS build-lambda at this+0xa8 (or clears it) -- i.e. replicate the vanilla-with-save routing. This avoids the ToS BUILD entirely (no suppress-crash, no dismiss-input). Supersedes the 'must satisfy Arxan accepted gate' framing in tos-accepted-gate-0x140e4fda0-is-arxan-vm-protected-2026 for the ROUTING (the invoke + installer are readable). Related: tos-builds-on-any-title-advance-need-upstream-accepted-gate-2026, tos-multilang-modal-suppression-target.

DELIVERABLE (2026-07-01): scripts/deobf-emulate.py -- a small Unicorn-based dispatch resolver for Arxan-obfuscated functions in eldenring-deobf.bin. Complements the STATIC scripts/deobf-read.py + scripts/arxan-thunks.tsv (see arxan-cfg-trampoline-resolver-and-reader-2026-07-01).

WHAT IT DOES: maps the deobf image at 0x140000000 under Unicorn (unicorn 2.1.4 via uv --with unicorn --with capstone), gives the function a scratch stack with a SENTINEL return addr + dummy args (rcx/rdx/r8/r9 -> scratch), emulates from the TRUE thunk entry (so the Arxan check-stub establishes correct stack discipline -- starting at the resolved continuation instead DERAILS via stack drift), and records the executed stream. Concrete execution AUTO-RESOLVES the opaque dispatch that static following cannot: gadget rets (jmp qword [rsp-8]) and movabs-computed targets just become concrete rip. Direct calls into game .text are recorded+SKIPPED (rax:=0, rip past call) to resolve THIS fn not callees; arxan scaffolding is executed (and folded from output unless --arxan). Stops on: sentinel (clean ret), int3 (noreturn/padding), derail outside game/arxan .text, or per-addr >40x runaway (loop/VM). Flags ==> DISPATCH resolutions + real call targets.

PROVEN: 0x140110820 (flattened) resolves to 'call 0x1416de0c0; call 0x1424fb244; <normalize>; ret' clean-return; a CHAIN-DEEP thunk (static gave up) resolved to its real call 0x1424d2834. HONEST LIMITS (batch of 360, stratified): dummy-input emulation rarely runs a whole fn to natural return (~64% hit budget, ~26% derail because the return path depends on skipped-callee/real-arg state); ~55% surface >=1 real game call along the executed path. So it is a per-function DISPATCH RESOLVER (turns the maze into a linear trace + real calls + return point), NOT a full-function decompiler. The loop-guard is a NOISY VM signal (real loops also spin) -- clean VM-subset quantification needs a better detector. NEXT: seed real args (from a runtime dump) or go symbolic/multi-path (Triton/Miasm) for full CFG recovery; that larger 'engine' is what the true Arxan-VM stubs would need. Related: arxan-cfg-trampoline-resolver-and-reader-2026-07-01.

CORRECTION to menufree-coldmount-RUNS-tos-free-but-b80-fullread-never-resident-2026-06-21 (that memory's 'io-handles all 0 / no in-flight request / rules out stale-request' claim was WRONG -- it read the POLL-phase tail and MISSED the FULL-INIT line). CORRECTED runtime fact (debug log GAME_DIR/er-effects-autoload-debug.log, menu-free cold_char_mount run 2026-06-21): the full save-read SUBMITS SUCCESSFULLY: 'FULL-INIT slot=0 b78=0 worker=0x144844020 submit_ret=1 b80=2 io10=0x0 io18=0x7ffe...54b0 io20=0x7ffe...56f0 -> POLL'. submit_ret=1, b80=2 (full-read lane ACTIVE), io18/io20 request handles SET (in-flight request EXISTS), worker 0x144844020 registered. THEN within ONE frame -> 'POLL waits=0 b80=0 io10=0 io18=0 io20=0': b80 drops 2->0 and the handles CLEAR. So the in-flight read COMPLETES/CANCELS TO b80=0, NEVER reaching b80=3 (RESIDENT). cold_char_mount's POLL waits for b80==3 -> times out at 1200. NET: the wall is NOT submit-fails (submit works) and NOT a stale-request gate -- it is that the dispatched b80=2 full read COMPLETES TO 0 (empty/cancelled) instead of draining to RESIDENT(3). Data partially populated anyway: c30=0xa010000 (real m10_01 map) + partial fingerprint level=9 name_len=1, but fp_real=false (incomplete char). HYPOTHESIS for next RE (actionable): cold_char_mount's POLL phase ticks B80_LANE1_DRIVER 0x679510 (the b80==1 PREVIEW lane driver) + B80_POLL 0x679180 every frame -- but this is a b80==2 FULL read; ticking the b80==1 lane driver on a b80==2 request likely COMPLETES/resets it prematurely to 0 (the memory notes the lane driver 'resets b80=0 once the read completes'). So the b80==2 read may need a DIFFERENT lane/poll driver (or no lane tick) than the b80==1 preview. NEXT: RE the b80==2 completion path -- which driver advances a b80==2 read to RESIDENT(3) vs the b80==1 lane driver that zeroes it; compare to the live-menu native Load that reaches b80==3. The preview-works(c30 set)/full-read-completes-to-0 split is the precise clue. Relates: b80-submit-kick-exact-false-gate-decoded-2026, menu-free-load-entry-VERDICT-stage2-2026 (DRAIN lane 0x679510/poll 0x679180 until b80==3), stream-worker-registration-is-the-b80-wall-verified-2026.

SCOPE NOTE (added 2026-06-23): this is a FORGE-FAMILY result and it is VALID -- KEEP it. It proves our FORGED job (selector 0x1409a8eb0 -> dispatcher 0x1409b3070, installed outside the native lifecycle) is mis-contextualized. It does NOT prove zero-input load is impossible; it bears ONLY on the forge path, not on the three UNTESTED native zero-input paths -- (1) member-node run 0x1409aaba0(rcx=Continue_node), (2) native builder FUN_1409a7070 + set tfc+0x14c, (3) direct primitive 0x14067b750(-1,0,0). See autoload-confirm-fork-verdict-20260623. ORIGINAL FINDING FOLLOWS: --- DECISIVE / CORRECTS the overflow theory (2026-06-23). The cap-8 'overflow' is NOT 8-items-full -- the OVERFLOW-GUARD log proved [dialog+0x50+0x48] = [dialog+0x98] = 140736551539120 (~0x7fffc81f4130, a POINTER ~= r8), NOT a small count. So dialog+0x50 is NOT a valid CS::DLFixedVector (its +0x48 is a pointer, not a count). The DLFixedVector.inl:662 'out of memory' assert fired because the insert (0x140733ef0) read that GARBAGE pointer as the count -> >8 -> panic. My VEH overflow-guard reset [dialog+0x98] (the pointer) to 0 -> CORRUPTED the dialog -> NEW access-violation 0x1411bf3ec ~200ms later (player=false c30=default, no load). So the guard is HARMFUL -- REVERTED. ROOT (deeper than overflow): our load's CS::MenuWindowJob's +0x50 (push target) = dialog+0x50, which is NOT a job vector in our context. The dispatcher 0x9b3070 was called with r8=dialog+0x50 (ctx_parent, from the selector), and that propagated to the MenuWindowJob+0x50, but dialog+0x50 is some dialog field (pointer at +0x48), not a DLFixedVector. So our BUILT JOB IS MIS-CONTEXTUALIZED: building the LoadGame job via the native selector/dispatcher OUTSIDE the full native menu-transition context yields a job whose push target is wrong/garbage. The NATIVE Continue builds it inside the full lifecycle (menu transition) so its MenuWindowJob targets a REAL job vector. PATTERN (whole session): every replicate-the-build+install+pump variant produces a mis-wired job. The native build assumes the full menu-transition context we keep skipping. RECONSIDER APPROACH: (A) let the NATIVE confirm flow run via the exact input bit, or (B) RE the full native menu-transition lifecycle (TextFadeOut clears + re-inits dialog+0x50 as a fresh vector) and replicate it, or (C) revisit own_load_drive (proven deserialize) + a different world-stream trigger. Relates: overflow-fix-reset-too-early-items-fill-during-pump, autoload-confirm-fork-verdict-20260623, OVERFLOW-VECTOR-PINNED-dialog-plus-0x50, owner130-IS-drained.

DECISIVE (dialog-identity subagent, 2026-06-23; persisted er-continue-load-FINAL-verdict + er-continue-pushtarget-rootcause). CORRECTS the mis-context theory: our OFFSETS ARE ALL RIGHT. dialog+0x50 IS the correct push target (= MenuWindow DLFixedVector<ptr,8>, data +0x58..+0x90, COUNT at +0x98 == base+0x48; MenuWindow ctor 0x140741a60 inits count=0). A LIVE TitleTopDialog's +0x98 is PROVABLY always 0..8 (append-only; the erase routine 0x140733d79 has ZERO callers/dead; dtor never clears it; vtable slot10 0x140734300 reads +0x98 as a count; NO code writes a pointer there). So the ~0x7fff... garbage at +0x98 we see at push time => the object at *(owner+0xe0) is NOT a fully-constructed/live vtable-0x142b26468 TitleTopDialog AT THAT MOMENT -- we are racing the dialog construction/teardown because our owner+0x130 FORGE builds the MenuWindowJob OUTSIDE the dialog's job lifecycle.

NATIVE push-target = dialog+0x50 of the LIVE TitleTopDialog, supplied by FUN_1409b2630 (the TitleTopDialog title-flow builder) at 0x1409b27de:  (builds the 11-job LoadGame FixOrderJobSequence). No fresh sequence, no separate ProfileLoadDialog object -- it IS the title dialog's own +0x50 vector. MenuWindowJob factory FUN_1407acbf0 sets field2_0x50=param_2 (threaded from the menu-open caller); MenuWindowJob::Run entry = 0x1407ad1c0 (NOT 0x1407ad2b0, that was mid-fn); push at 0x1407ad53b. ProfileSelect builder FUN_14081f7e0 (scaleform L'05_010_ProfileSelect'). LoadGame seq builder FUN_140833880 (dump FUN_140833970). Both our paths wire dialog+0x50 correctly (selector 0x1409a8eb0 computes (*rcx)+0x50; own_load_pump build matches 0x140826510 (out,rdx=ctx_parent,r8d=slot,r9=owner_ctx)).

IMPLICATION / SHARPENED DECISION: the FORGE (Assign our built job into owner+0x130) is the root problem -- it builds+pumps the load MenuWindowJob outside the dialog's lifecycle, so the dialog isn't live/valid at the push. The selector-invoke is the RIGHT architecture but its built job is only pumped correctly by the NATIVE flow (the confirm-driven menu pump via FUN_1409b2630), which we lack without the confirm. So pure-zero-input is stuck: (a) forge -> mis-context crash; (b) selector-invoke-only -> not pumped (menu-jumps, no load). The native-confirm path handles the lifecycle correctly. SUBAGENT RECs: (1) prefer selector-invoke, AVOID the owner+0x130 forge; (2) strengthen fire-gate fail-closed: require *(owner+0xe0) non-null + vtable==0x142b26468 + dialog in Loop state (a40 latch) + *(dialog+0x98) in 0..7, else WAIT; (3) DELETE the count-reset hack (masks reading a non-live object, corrupts a real dialog). NEXT DIRECTION DECISION (user's): pursue a zero-input way to make the NATIVE pump process the selector's job at the right lifecycle (e.g. invoke FUN_1409b2630 / the menu-item confirm processor directly), OR the exact confirm input bit (goal sanctions 'input bits triggered'). Relates: CONFIRMED-miscontextualized-dialog0x50-not-stable-vector, autoload-OVERNIGHT-HANDOFF.

CORRECTION + DECISIVE RE (2026-06-22, static, deobf-grounded). The req_coord=0xffffffff1c000000 hypothesis was WRONG -- the coord is FINE. wrm=[MoveMapStep+0xf0]; block_coord at wrm+0x2c is a 4-byte BlockId {indexId,regionId,blockId,areaId}; low dword 0x1c000000 = the real saved map (GameMan+0xc30, m28_00_00_00, areaId 0x1c). 0x140660fe0 IsOverworldBlockId = (0x32<=areaId<=0x58); areaId 0x1c<0x32 => NON-overworld single-block map => the 0xffffffff high dword is the UNUSED upper half, never read by the gate (request/readiness only consume the low dword + areaId byte). So NOT a bad coordinate.

REAL STALL: readiness 0x14066d3e0(resmgr,&coord): count=[resmgr+0xb3140](=33, ok); scans [resmgr+0xb3030] stride8 for a block where block[+8][+0xc]==areaId(0x1c); READY iff that block's loadstate (vtable[+0x10]()) has [+0x2d]!=0 AND [+0x35]==0xa. STUCK = no block with areaId 0x1c at phase 0xa among the 33. So either the m28 block was never REGISTERED into [resmgr+0xb3030], or registered but never STREAMED to phase 0xa.

NATIVE HANDOFF (what primes the m28 block request): play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0(InGameStep,&mapid) [deobf; dump shifted] sets InGameStep+0xd8=1, +0x100=BlockId, appends req-vec +0xb35f0, calls SetMoveMapStepBlockId 0x14067acc0(GameMan+0x14). Then STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec210 reads InGameStep+0x100, calls FUN_14066da30 (seeds worldInfoOwner +0xb798/+0xb79c => becomes wrm), allocs MoveMapStep(0x4c0). GameMan named fields: +0x14 moveMapStepBlockId, +0xaa0 lastLoadPosition, +0xac8 loadTargetMapId, +0xc30 stayInMultipleAreaBlockId(saved). Our path: MoveMapStep IS built + reached WorldResWait + wrm+0x2c=0x1c000000 correct + 33 blocks -- but the m28 block isn't at 0xa. Open Q (next probe, LOW RISK reads): log InGameStep+0xd8 phase + InGameStep+0x100 (requested BlockId) + scan [resmgr+0xb3030] for an areaId==0x1c block and its loadstate[+0x35] phase -> distinguishes REGISTRATION gap (block absent => call play_game_submit 0x140aebdc0 / set req-vec) vs STREAMING gap (present but <0xa => the resource-decompress/FD4 stream worker isn't running). Candidate fix once known: replicate play_game_submit before/with continue, OR drive the missing stream worker. block registration likely in STEP_MsbLoad 0x140af8f00 or the FD4 stream task 0x144842d40. PROFILESELECT NATIVE STATS TEXT -- MECHANISM PROVEN (2026-07-04, branch fix/loading-portrait-swap-early-popout). The character's 8 attributes render as text on the Load-Game rows in the GAME'S OWN MenuFont_01 -- confirmed on-screen by user. HOW: hook the SetText wrapper FUN_14074a0f0 (deobf 0x74a000, PROFILE_SETTEXT_RVA) = profile_settext_hook in startup_hooks.rs; substitute our attribute string (build_stats_text_utf16 reads GameDataMan->PlayerGameData vigor..arcane via PGD_STAT_BASE_3C_OFFSET). Field targeting was via the assignComponentWithName hook (0x74a2f0, already installed) capturing out_proxy+SCENE_OBJ_PROXY_SCALEFORM_VALUE_OFFSET(0x8) for name=='Location'. Install: START_PROFILE_STATS_TEXT in lib.rs gated on stats_panel_enabled(). Oracles: oracle_stats_text_installed/_field_captures/_settext_subs (ran: installed=1, captures=2, subs=28). KNOWN BUG (why user said 'almost'): the stats appeared in PlayerName AND Location AND PlayTime, not just Location. ROOT: the native row-populate (FUN_1408758d0) reuses ONE stack SceneObjProxy 'tmp' for EVERY field -- assign(&tmp,'PlayerName');SetText(&tmp+0x8,name); assign(&tmp,'Location');SetText(&tmp+0x8,map); ... -- so out_proxy+0x8 is the SAME pointer for all fields on a row; matching by that pointer hits every field. FIX (not yet done): don't match by value pointer. Instead ARM a global flag in the assignComponentWithName hook when name==<target field>, and CONSUME it on the very NEXT SetText (clear the flag on ANY other assign so it stays tightly scoped to the assign->SetText adjacency). That routes each field's content correctly. CONFIRMED: SceneObjProxy.scaleformValue offset +0x8 is CORRECT (substitution landed). Pass PLAIN UTF-16 (engine wraps HTML+font). Related: [[profileselect-native-settext-RE-2026-07-04]] [[profileselect-stats-panel-redesign-remove-box-2026-07-04]].

AUTORESEARCH FINDING 2026-06-25: `title_native_ready_last_flags/masked/ret` are not three independent flags. They are one native SceneObjProxy readiness predicate at live/deobf 0x140733150, reported as raw byte `flags=[state+0x20]&0xff`, `masked=flags&0x8f`, and `ret=(masked!=0)`. Static disasm of live 0x140733150 is `state=this->vtable[0](this); return (byte[state+0x20] & 0x8f) != 0`. Correct dump address is 0x140733250. The caller at live 0x140745bf9 / dump 0x140745cf9 still gates downstream row/window work on this predicate (`if FUN_140733250(param_1+0x6a) { ... vtable calls ... }`). Therefore the zero values are still relevant as a real native gate, not obsolete telemetry; but they indicate the SceneObjProxy state object never becomes any nonzero masked state, not three separate missing product conditions. A visual run cannot directly verify these RAM flags; use in-process telemetry for the flags and visible capture only to correlate human-visible title/window state.

DETERMINISTIC-CONTROL LEVER for the parallel-cargo contention (user offered to help on Windows 2026-07-20): ER runs as a NATIVE WINDOWS process (eldenring.exe), so Linux taskset cannot touch it -- use Windows-side priority/affinity via powershell.exe from WSL (confirmed reachable, no admin needed for own-user process: read explorer PID/PriorityClass/Affinity OK; wmic.exe is ABSENT, use powershell Get-Process/.PriorityClass/.ProcessorAffinity). PLAN: a background helper watches for eldenring.exe and sets .PriorityClass='High' (+ optional .ProcessorAffinity to dedicated cores e.g. 0xF000 = cores 12-15 on the 16-core box) so the Windows scheduler favors ER's asset-loading/physics over the WSL2 VM (vmmem, Normal priority) running cargo -- deterministically un-starving ER without the user pausing cargo. If powershell cannot set priority (permission), the user can do it via Task Manager (set eldenring.exe priority High + affinity). This is the determining-variable control (not-determined-not-nondeterministic) to make the 3-load + stable-FPS proof deterministic. bd runs-coinflip-under-parallel-cargo-contention.

OBJECTIVE = IN-MEMORY / IN-GAME texture replacement, NOT disk modding (user directive 2026-06-28: 'we aren't concerned at all with reading them from disk, just in memory in game'). Do NOT build a disk TPF/BHD/BDT reader or repack Oodle from disk -- that is explicitly out of scope. IN-MEMORY PATH: at runtime the game has ALREADY decompressed Kraken->TPF->DDS into a live system texture; the goal is to replace/redirect THAT in-process. Hook seam already exists: TITLE_SCALEFORM_BIND_OBSERVER (deobf RVA 0x7451c0) observes native Scaleform image-symbol -> system-texture bindings (fields LAST_SYMBOL_PTR / LAST_TARGET_PTR / SYSTEX_HITS in constants.rs); extend it from observer to REPLACER to substitute our in-memory texture for a given image symbol (the symbol named by GFX tag 1009 GFX_DefineExternalImage2, e.g. MENU_*.tga). So 'er-tpf' = construct/own a valid IN-MEMORY texture (DDS/GPU resource) the game's texture system accepts + the runtime rebind, NOT a disk archive crate. Mirrors the magenta title cover (which swapped the Scaleform GFX MemoryFile in memory); a raster cover swaps the system-texture a bitmap-fill resolves to -- both pure in-process, zero disk. BACKGROUND ONLY (true facts, NOT the integration path): on disk menu textures are menu/hi(low)/00_solo.tpfbhd+tpfbdt = BXF4 (BHF4 header / BDF4 data) -> per-entry DCX (Oodle 'KRAK') -> TPF -> DDS; 100% KRAK (523/523 .dcx). Reading from disk would need Oodle Kraken (oo2core), which we are NOT doing. CONTAMINATION TRAP if anyone ever does touch disk: cleartext 'TPF\0'/'DDS ' magic scans in a .tpfbdt are false positives inside the Kraken stream -- decode the DCX format field instead.

FIX (verified 2026-06-28) for the ER agent-launched window landing off-screen at Hyprland logical (-3069,23): set DP-1 as the XWayland RandR PRIMARY -> 'xrandr --output DP-1 --primary' (DISPLAY=:0). Verified: a detached probe launch then kept the window at (2,23) on DP-1 through the whole render-init window that previously shifted it (user confirmed). ROOT CAUSE: XWayland had NO RandR primary set and presented the outputs reversed vs Hyprland (xrandr: DP-3 @ +0+0 at X11 origin, DP-1 @ +3840+0; Hyprland logical DP-1@(0,0)/DP-3@(3072,0), scale 1.25, xwayland.force_zero_scaling=true). With no primary, the game's own DXGI/swapchain output pick (deobf FUN_141e9d480; CSWindowImp window-mode apply FUN_140e89a80) targeted the wrong output for the unfocused/detached agent launch and Hyprland mapped it off-screen. NOT the er_effects DLL (proven: vanilla DLL-disabled detached launch shifted identically; move caller stacks were pure game code). The user's offline launcher works only because it is launched interactively with DP-1 focused (focused window uses its own monitor; unfocused falls back to primary). DURABLE FIX APPLIED: the user's real Hyprland config is ~/.config/hypr/hyprland.lua (Lua config, NOT hyprland.conf; there is no hyprland.conf). Added 'hl.exec_cmd("xrandr --output DP-1 --primary")' to the hl.on("hyprland.start", ...) autostart block. Validated luac -p OK + hyprctl reload ok + log clean. Backup at ~/.config/hypr/hyprland.lua.bak-er-fix. Additive: the config intentionally keeps DP-3 as the game monitor via workspace_rule ws1->DP-3 (RandR primary was previously unset, so this does not disturb interactive games launched focused on DP-3). CAVEATS: re-applies on every Hyprland start (X/XWayland is up at start -- existing apply-xresources autostart proves it), but NOT on monitor hotplug; full 'sticks across reboot' to be confirmed by user relogin/reboot.

Mounting at state 10 (FE-host MenuJobWait, BEFORE SetState(5), so no MoveMapStep dispatcher interferes) with the worker registered (0x140b0a980 stub built worker 0x144844020): (TEST 1) initiator 0x14067b4e0(slot) -> b80=1 AND the async read goes IN-FLIGHT (iodev 0x1445893b0 +0x10/+0x20 handles became NON-NULL) -- PROVES the save-IO subsystem works AND the worker drains at the bare title. BUT b80=1 is the PREVIEW lane: my poll 0x140679180(0,0) reset b80 1->0 without deserializing (c30 stayed -1). (TEST 2) initiator 0x14067b1a0(slot) -> b80=2 (the deserialize-arm state) BUT started NO read (inflight/reqhandle stayed NULL); poll then reset b80 2->0, c30 unchanged. So NEITHER individual initiator replicates the dispatcher: 0x14067b4e0 starts the read but wrong lane (b80=1); 0x14067b1a0 sets b80=2 but no read. The real menu (MoveMapListStep STEP_LoadSaveData) SEQUENCES them across frames (start read via item-cb 0x14082ba30 -> 0x14067b4e0; then 0x14067b200 sets b80=2; then dispatcher poll -> b80=3 -> deserialize 0x14067b290), driven by IO-completion state (request state 0x14240a1f0 -> 0x14 resident). 0x14067b1a0 is gated b80==0 so it cannot fire after 0x14067b4e0 set b80=1. CONCLUSION: hand-calling individual b80 initiators at the title does NOT converge; must REPLICATE the dispatcher-1 0x140afbad0 + dispatcher-2 0x140afb880 orchestration (or drive the MoveMapStep so its native dispatcher does it). Open: decode the EXACT menu mount sequence (read-start -> b80 1->2 transition -> poll->3 -> deserialize) incl. who transitions b80 1->2 and the request-state gating. SAFE: timeout path (b80 never 3) stays at the title, NO SetState(5), NO save write (hash verified untouched). NOTE the new-game default map 0xa010000 == the user m10 test char's real map, so c30 value alone never disambiguates -- verify a real load by NO intro cutscene + char-applied, or use a non-m10 char. MINIMAL SAVE-MOUNT PRIMITIVE RECIPE (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY static RE). Decodes the FD4 stream-worker registration ABI 0x142656b00 + helper 0x140af1b40/0x140b0a980 fully and reconciles all prior b80/mount memories. RESULT: the stream-worker registration is a SELF-CONTAINED, directly-callable primitive (no live-session arg, registry+task self-construct). The save-DATA mount (b80==3 -> deserialize 0x14067b290 -> char applied + c30=real map) does NOT need CSFeMan/MoveMapStep; only the worker registration + the b80 poll loop. Final blocker is NOT a missing call but whether the registered worker pool actually drains the queued read at a forced FE-host (the IngameInit/MoveMapList contexts only ever CALL the same register fn, nothing more).

=== Q1 STREAM-WORKER REGISTRATION ABI (decoded) ===
Helper 0x140af1b40 (MoveMapList tail) and 0x140b0a980 (IngameInit tail) are BYTE-IDENTICAL except the state threshold (cmp 0xb vs cmp 0x7). Both do, when [this+0x48]+1 > threshold:
 1. rcx = [0x144842d40] (the FD4 world-stream task ptr). If null: call 0x141eceb10 (thunk -> 0x141f1d190 -> if [0x144844008] null, 0x141f1d620 builds the task-runner singleton; returns task), store @0x144842d40. Self-constructing, lazy.
 2. [rsp+0x30] = task ptr; mov rax,[task]; lea rdx,[rsp+0x20]; xor r8d,r8d; call [task_vtable+0x18]  -- returns a runtime-type descriptor; then FD4 RTTI assert (shr ecx,5; test cl,1; 0x141eb97a0).
 3. Build a std::string name descriptor at [rsp+0x30..0x58] via 0x140116c20(rcx=&desc, rdx=0x1429d2b28). String at 0x1429d2b28 = UTF-16LE Japanese "移動先ステップ未定義" (MoveDestStep undefined) -- a debug/lookup NAME.
 4. rdx = SSO-resolved name ptr (cmp [rsp+0x50],8; cmovae rdx,[rsp+0x38]); ecx = key 0x59682f01; CALL 0x142656b00 @0x140af1c05.
ABI of 0x142656b00(ecx=key 0x59682f01, rdx=name_ptr, r8=?, r9=?): it saves rdx/r8/r9 to shadow, then call 0x141eba960 (= 0x141ebb680+0x28) which returns the FD4 named-task REGISTRY singleton (lazy self-constructing once-guard @0x1448020f8; ctor 0x141eba9b0; returns [rbx+0x10]; ASSERTS internally, never null after init). It then does a type-descriptor vcall [singleton_vtable+0x18], an RTTI assert, name string-copy (0x141ebba30 strlen, 0x14011fd30, 0x141ebba30), and a final vcall [name+0x68] (name dtor). Net: 0x142656b00 = FD4::registerNamedTask(key, name) that inserts the worker descriptor into the registry singleton. NO session/scheduler pointer is dereferenced; the only deref'd singleton (0x141ebb680 registry) self-constructs. CALLABLE AT THE TITLE WITHOUT A LIVE SESSION. The task vtable+0x18 call (descriptor getter) and the registry are both boot-available.
DIRECT REPLICATION: 
  rcx0 = [0x144842d40]; if 0 { rcx0 = 0x141eceb10(); [0x144842d40]=rcx0 }
  build name desc D via 0x140116c20(&D, 0x1429d2b28)
  0x142656b00(ecx=0x59682f01, rdx=name_ptr(D), r8=0, r9=0)   // r8/r9 unused by the callee path observed
  (free D via [D.vtable+0x68] if heap-allocated, per the tail)
Simplest: just CALL 0x140af1b40 or 0x140b0a980 on a tiny stub object whose [this+0x48] >= threshold and [this+0x4c] writable -- they do exactly this and nothing else. (0x140b0a980 needs [this+0x48]+1 > 7; 0x140af1b40 needs > 0xb.) A 0x50-byte zeroed stub with +0x48 preset to 7/0xb works (they only inc +0x4c and read +0x48).

=== Q2 WHAT DRIVES b80 1->2->3 ===
b80 poll 0x140679180(cl,dl): if [GameMan+0xdf0]!=0 -> b80=3 (dead fast-path, 0xdf0 has 0 writers). Else IO subsys 0x140e6e060() then poll 0x140e6e080(rcx=iodev,dl=arg0,r8d=arg1): reads request handle [iodev+0x18]/[+0x20] (null => not-in-flight => jump 0x140e6e2ef), then reads IO-request STATE via 0x14240a1f0 (0..0x19) and jump-tables. On the resident/complete state 0x140679180 sets b80=3 and returns 0; on in-progress returns 1 (b80 stays); on error b80=0. So b80 reaches 3 EXACTLY when the IO request reaches the resident state, which happens ONLY as the worker drains the queued read. b80 advance is NOT automatic from the MoveMapStep alone -- the dispatcher 0x140afbad0 calls 0x140679180 each frame, but 0x140679180 only flips b80 to 3 once the worker has serviced the read. We CAN call 0x140679180(0,0) ourselves each frame after a 0x14067b4e0(slot) initiate (which sets b80=1 + begins async read via 0x140e6ec80) -- it is the same poll the native dispatcher uses, no MoveMapStep context needed. So minimal b80 driver = 0x14067b4e0(slot) once; then loop 0x140679180(0,0) per frame until b80==3. The ONLY thing that makes that loop terminate is the worker draining -- i.e. Q1 registration must have made the worker pool actually run.

=== Q3 CHARACTER APPLY ===
0x14067b290(ecx=slot) is the ONLY char-apply path. Decode: alloc 0x280000 from scheduler 0x143d872e0[0]+0x50; gated read 0x14067b100(buf,0x280000) -- which itself asserts [GameMan+0xb80]==3 (else jne 0x14067b174 FAIL) and then either df0!=0 fast-complete (b80=0,ret1) or df0==0 sync read path 0x14067b138 (needs IO-resident state 0x14240a1f0==0x14). On read OK: 0x1402567b0/0x140261b80, [GameMan+0xac0]=slot, header-parse 0x14067bd70 -> [GameMan+0xc30]=saved map, writes GameMan+0x3c=0/+0x10=1, then 0x140e365c0(PlayerGameData 0x144588268, ...) APPLIES stats/inventory, frees buf. NO CSFeMan/MoveMapStep ref. So once b80==3 (+ resident) we can call 0x14067b290(slot) DIRECTLY to apply the char + set c30, no dispatcher context. Preconditions: b80==3, IO request resident (state==0x14), scheduler 0x143d872e0 non-null (boot), PlayerGameData 0x144588268 non-null (boot). 0x14067afa0 is the LIGHTER c30-only setter (sets c30 from a slot record, gated on the record being populated) but it does NOT apply the character -- so it is insufficient alone; the char still needs 0x14067b290. Use 0x14067b290 which does BOTH c30 and char in one call.

=== Q4 MOST-RECENT SLOT ===
Slot container [0x143d5df38] is a ptr to a 0x158 manager; [+0x8] = the store. 0x140678a50 (sync slot peek) reads [[0x143d5df38]+0x8], null-checks, calls 0x14025f8e0 (cmp [rax],0 word), then a SYNCHRONOUS read 0x140760970(token 0x47054) -- this is a sync peek of slot metadata that does NOT use the b80 machine (the proof the slot bytes can be read sync). The per-slot records are 0x2A0-stride; the most-recent pick is a timestamp scan over the records (fields +0x3c/+0x40/+0x44 hold the save timestamp/flags per prior memory). The native chooser is 0x140678a50/0x140679560 (the PlayGame item's slot resolver feeding owner+0xbc). For a deterministic pick, call the native resolver chain that the PlayGame confirm uses (0x140678a50 -> 0x140679560) rather than reimplementing the timestamp math; it returns the active/most-recent slot index.

=== Q5 ZERO-INPUT RECIPE / BLOCKER ===
PRECONDS at the title (committed FE-host +0x48==10): scheduler 0x143d872e0, slot container [0x143d5df38], PlayerGameData 0x144588268, inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 all non-null (all boot-built per ground-truth). GameMan 0x143d69918 b80==0.
 STEP A (register worker -- the wall): replicate 0x140b0a980 tail: rcx0=[0x144842d40]; if 0 rcx0=0x141eceb10(); [0x144842d40]=rcx0; build name via 0x140116c20(&D,0x1429d2b28); 0x142656b00(ecx=0x59682f01,rdx=name_ptr,r8=0,r9=0). (Or call 0x140b0a980 on a zeroed 0x50 stub with +0x48=7.) This is callable cold; registry+task self-construct.
 STEP B (pick slot): slot = native resolver via 0x140678a50/0x140679560 (most-recent), or a fixed slot.
 STEP C (initiate IO): 0x14067b4e0(cl=slot) -> b80=1, async read begins (iodev 0x1445893b0 +0x18/+0x20 go non-null).
 STEP D (drive b80 to 3): each frame call 0x140679180(0,0) until [GameMan+0xb80]==3 (poll flips it when the worker drains the read to resident state 0x14240a1f0==0x14). REQUIRES the worker pool to actually run (Step A).
 STEP E (apply char + c30): once b80==3, call 0x14067b290(ecx=slot). RESULT: [GameMan+0xc30]=slot real map; [GameMan+0xac0]=slot; char applied to PlayerGameData 0x144588268.
 STEP F (stream): set owner+0xbc=[GameMan+0xc30]; SetState(5) (0x140b0d960(obj,5)) -> native PlayGame->MoveMap streams the REAL save.
CRASH RISKS: do NOT poke b80 directly (deserialize/apply arms AV if state not truly resident / CSFeMan null in dispatcher-2 0x140afb880@0x140afb9e4); calling 0x14067b290 before b80==3 just no-ops (al=0, frees buf) -- safe but useless. The vcall [task+0x18] in Step A asserts RTTI; safe with the real task.
THE SINGLE HARDEST BLOCKER (honest): the registration primitive itself is fully callable cold, BUT it is UNVERIFIED at runtime that registering the named worker (key 0x59682f01) is SUFFICIENT to make the FD4 worker pool 0x144853048 actually TICK/DRAIN the queued slot read at a forced FE-host. The scheduler runs all task GROUPS every frame from boot (fd4-scheduler-no-group-active-gate-runs-all-2026), and the IngameInit/MoveMapList contexts do NOTHING beyond this same register call (0x140b0a980 is byte-identical to 0x140af1b40 and contains only the register sequence) -- which is STRONG evidence that registration is the complete activating step and Steps A-E should drain b80 to 3 and apply the char with zero input and zero session. The remaining risk is solely whether some OTHER boot subsystem (the runner-manager 0x14485d170 / pool 0x144853048) must already be primed; both self-construct lazily, so the expectation is they are live at the title. This is now a RUNTIME-VERIFY step, not a missing RE primitive: call Step A then Steps C-D and watch b80 reach 3. If it does, the entire char/save mount is achievable with standalone primitives and NO menu/session machinery; if it stalls, the blocker is pool activation (run-manager start), to be RE'd next. NO INPUT is needed at any layer (all SetState/calls are direct).
Related: stream-worker-registration-is-the-b80-wall-verified-2026, async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026, slot9-load-phase-machine-b80-csfeman-less-2026, fd4-scheduler-no-group-active-gate-runs-all-2026, ground-truth-real-load-and-c30-is-newgame-default-2026.

DECISIVE A/B CONFIRMATION (2026-06-22, sw-bp at MountEblArchive 0x1efc00, deep raw stack, golden vs menu-free). Boot common-archive mounts fire on BOTH paths (chain 0x1eef1c<-0x54c266a<-0xa7807<-0x1f8479). The PLAYER-MAP mount (chain ...<-0x79171f<-0x82dc1c) fires ONLY on the golden native-menu load, NEVER on our menu-free SetState5 path (menu-free run armed BP + fired 4 boot mounts hits0-3, ZERO 0x82dc1c-chain hits). => the in-game MAP-LOAD ORCHESTRATOR (fn entry ~0x14082dbf0: push rbx;sub rsp 0x30;...;call 0x14082faf0; a guarded step in the 0x14082xxxx in-game session region next to post-continue 0x14082cd60) is the EXACT step our path skips. That is why m28's FD4FileCaps load empty (cap+0x90=0) and the world never streams -- the whole stall reduces to this ONE missing call site. LEVER (the path to a loaded world): trigger the in-game map-load orchestrator menu-free. NEXT: (1) identify fn 0x14082dbf0 + 0x14082faf0 semantics (Ghidra dump / disasm) and its /args; (2) determine why SetState5 doesn't reach it (likely the InGameStep/MoveMapStep step machine our path doesn't tick -- mms_state stuck 3); (3) call/drive it from state we hold (cached owner / InGameStep / MoveMapStep). For load2's substate-7 softlock: the reload leaves the return-title/ending INTENT set (warpRequested=1 etc.), so the MoveMapStep correctly waits. The fix is to make the reload NOT leave that intent set (or use a load2 mechanism that does not go through return-title), NOT to force the substate. Stop forcing; fix the mechanism.

NATIVE-SHADER DRAW breakthrough (2026, session continued). ROOT CAUSE of the hardware GPUVM fault FOUND + patched. The native ER object .vpo (e.g. CS[AMSN]_AEG301_012_Fwd) computes localInstance = gl_InstanceIndex - gl_BaseInstance (SPIR-V: %71 = OpISub %68 %70, DrawParameters cap). wgpu's PASSTHROUGH path does NOT enable VK_KHR_shader_draw_parameters, so gl_BaseInstance reads GARBAGE -> %71 ~= 4 billion -> indexes g_InstanceIndexBuffer (SSBO) at ~1.94GB offset -> the deterministic GPUVM PERMISSION_FAULT (0x8000742df000) seen on radv. 

FIXES (crates/er-shaderkit/src/spirv_patch.rs, post-translation SPIR-V patches, applied in native_draw example): (1) neutralize_draw_parameters() rewrites gl_BaseInstance/gl_BaseVertex OpLoad -> OpCopyObject of const 0 (correct for firstInstance=0 draws). (2) force_readonly_ssbo_loads_zero() rewrites loads from NonWritable StorageBuffer (g_InstanceIndexBuffer) -> 0 (single-instance slot is 0). Both keep SPIR-V valid (spirv-val passes).

DEFINITIVE static RE (2026-06-15, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs) -- the slot-load FLOW decoded end-to-end, the cycle-breaker found, and a clean 3-phase zero-input slot-9 recipe. CITES disasm; supersedes the 'deserialize itself needs CSFeMan' claim in slot9-load-map-circular-dependency.

=== WHERE/WHEN CSFeMan BUILDS (Q1) ===
CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 is built ONLY by 0x140766010 (sole caller 0x140af2e47, inside spawner STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120). The whole load/menu lives in ONE object: the MoveMapStep (ctor 0x140af28d0, vtable 0x142b5ff38; inits +0x12c=-1 deserialize-slot, +0x100=3, +0xd8=-1, +0xe0=-1, +0xe8=inputmgr/map param). Its per-frame UPDATE = 0x140aff640 (rcx=owner, rdx=framectx; bound by ctor 0x140af28d0 @0x140af2be4). So the Load-Game list menu and CSFeMan-build are the SAME MoveMapStep object -- you cannot run the load list before the MoveMapStep exists, and the MoveMapStep build is what builds CSFeMan. CSFeMan is NULL at the press-any-button title only because the MoveMapStep has not been spawned (InGameStep not advanced to idx6). CONFIRMS csfeman-construction-chain-ingamestep-step6-2026.

=== THE b80 LOAD-PHASE MACHINE = THE ACTUAL SAVE LOAD (all CSFeMan-LESS, disasm-verified 0 refs) ===
GameMan(0x143d69918): +0xb80 = load phase (0/1/2/3/4/7), +0xb78 = slot to deserialize (0x1406793c0 reads it -> written to owner+0x12c), +0xac0 = active slot (0..9). Per-frame update 0x140aff640 calls, IN THIS ORDER: (a) 0x140afbad0 [DESERIALIZE dispatcher], then (b) 0x140afb880 [main CSFeMan/apply dispatcher], then (c) 0x140afa6d0.
PHASE WRITERS (scan of mov [GameMan+0xb80]):
 0x14067b4e0(cl=slot): if b80==0, begins async slot-IO read (0x140e6ec80) for slot cl, on success sets b80=1. THE LOAD INITIATOR. caller thunk 0x14082ba30 (a menu button cb; calls with ecx=0... per-item slot).
 0x14067b1a0(cl=slot): if b80==0 begins read (0x140e6eb80,size token 0xa), sets b80=2. (alt initiator)
 0x14067b200: sets b80=2 (used by STEP_LoadSaveData path).
 0x140679180(cl,dl): manages async-IO handle GameMan+0xdf0; when IO ready sets b80=3, returns 0.
 0x14067b290(ecx=slot): THE FULL DESERIALIZE. allocs 0x280000 via scheduler 0x143d872e0[+0x50], reads save (0x14067b100), sets GameMan+0xac0=slot, calls header-parse 0x14067bd70 -> writes [GameMan+0xc30]=saved packed map (DWORD@header+4, e.g. m60=0x3c2a2200), builds GameFlow cmd, APPLIES char via 0x140e365c0 vs PlayerGameData 0x144588268. NO CSFeMan ref.
 0x14067b030 sets b80=7; 0x14067b0b0 sets b80=4; 0x140679510 / 0x1406794b0 reset b80=0.
DISPATCHER-1 0x140afbad0(rcx=owner->rbx): gate 0x14067a010 (b80==1?). If b80!=1 -> jumps to the DESERIALIZE ARM @0x140afbc1e: call 0x140679ff0 (b80==2?); if true call 0x140679180(0,0); when it returns 0 (IO ready, b80->3) -> mov ecx,[rbx+0x12c]; CALL 0x14067b290(slot) -> lands [GameMan+0xc30]; set [rbx+0x12c]=-1. THIS ARM HAS ZERO CSFeMan REFS (verified). If b80==1 -> jump table on 0x140679510 result (other IO steps).
DISPATCHER-2 0x140afb880(rcx=owner->rdi): reads 5 predicate flags via getters 0x1406793d0/0x140679370/0x1406793c0(=b78 slot)/0x140679100/0x140679360; based on which is set, calls 0x14067b940 / 0x14067b750 / 0x14067b570(current_slot_load) / 0x14067b030; THEN @0x140afb9d7: if no-error AND [rdi+0x12a]==0 -> DEREF CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 + FD4-assert non-null @0x140afb9e4, write [CSFeMan+0x82a8]=1 (the 'apply loaded world to CSFeMan' step). This is the ONLY CSFeMan-dependent arm; it runs AFTER the deserialize dispatcher in the same frame and asserts/crashes if CSFeMan is null. So CSFeMan must be built BEFORE this arm fires.

=== HIGHLIGHT vs CONFIRM (Q2) -- the cycle-breaker truth ===
Slot HIGHLIGHT info reader = 0x14067b570 (current_slot_load): reads GameMan+0xac0 (slot 0..9), gate b80==0, allocs a SMALL 0x60000 buffer, reads slot preview via 0x140e6e060/0x140e6ec70 for the menu DISPLAY. It does NOT call header-parse 0x14067bd70 and does NOT write GameMan+0xc30 and has NO CSFeMan ref. => HIGHLIGHT does NOT populate +0xc30. The real saved map (+0xc30) is produced ONLY by the FULL deserialize 0x14067b290 in the b80 phase machine. There is NO header-only +0xc30 peek (confirms slot-saved-map-c30-via-full-deserialize-2026). BUT the cycle-breaker is: the full deserialize 0x14067b290 (and the entire b80 machine) is CSFeMan-LESS -- it only needs scheduler 0x143d872e0 + the save-IO subsystem + PlayerGameData 0x144588268. So you CAN deserialize slot 9 (landing +0xc30 and applying the char) the instant CSFeMan exists, WITHOUT the map-occupy problem, by calling 0x14067b290(9) directly instead of feeding a placeholder map through SetState(5).

=== REAL ORDERED LOAD SEQUENCE (Q3) ===
Real Load Game -> pick slot N -> confirm: menu button cb (e.g. 0x14082ba30) calls load-initiator 0x14067b4e0(slot)/0x14067b1a0(slot) -> b80=1/2 + async slot-IO begins (slot also lands in GameMan+0xb78 via the menu, read by 0x1406793c0). Each frame the MoveMapStep update 0x140aff640 runs DISPATCHER-1 0x140afbad0 (advances b80 via 0x140679180: IO-ready -> b80=3 -> 0x14067b290(slot) full-deserialize -> [GameMan+0xc30]=saved map + char applied), THEN DISPATCHER-2 0x140afb880 (once flags set + CSFeMan exists -> [CSFeMan+0x82a8]=1, the apply-to-world). Net order: build MoveMapStep(=CSFeMan) -> initiate slot IO (b80 1) -> phase machine 1->2->3 deserialize (+0xc30, char) -> CSFeMan apply arm -> STEP_MoveMap_Update IsResident gate (child+0xe0 stream) drains child+0xd8 1->2->0 -> world resident. The occupy-then-blocked failure happened because we fed a PLACEHOLDER map (m60) through SetState(5) which occupied the stream before deserializing slot 9; the real flow deserializes the slot FIRST (CSFeMan-less) so +0xbc/+0x100 get slot 9's REAL map and the stream goes resident.

=== CLEAN ZERO-INPUT 3-PHASE SLOT-9 RECIPE ===
On the live FE-host SimpleTitleStep (vtable 0x142b63bb0, committed +0x48==10), with scheduler 0x143d872e0 + slot container 0x143d5df38 + inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 non-null, latch 0x143d856a0==0:
 PHASE 1 (build CSFeMan): set GameMan+0xac0=9 (0x14067a810(ecx=9)), clear owner+0x284, write a VALID packed map to owner+0xbc (placeholder m60 0x3c2a2200 is fine here -- we will NOT let it occupy), SetState 0x140b0d960(owner,5). Existing pump -> PlayGame -> MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 -> CSFeMan 0x143d6b880 non-null. VERIFY 0x143d6b880 != 0.
 PHASE 2 (deserialize slot 9 CSFeMan-less): call 0x14067b290(ecx=9) directly (CSFeMan now exists but is NOT required by this fn). RESULT: [GameMan+0xc30]=slot 9 saved map; GameMan+0xac0=9; slot-9 stats/inventory applied to PlayerGameData 0x144588268. VERIFY [GameMan+0xc30] is slot 9's real map (not 0x3c2a2200 unless slot 9 is in m60).
 PHASE 3 (stream slot 9's real map): set owner+0xbc=[GameMan+0xc30], clear owner+0x284, SetState 0x140b0d960(owner,5) again -> play_game_submit feeds child+0x100=real map -> STEP_MoveMap_Update IsResident (0x140eb5530 on child+0xe0) true -> child+0xd8 drains 1->2->0 -> slot-9 area + character resident.

Static RE on whether existing TitleBackViewParts/MenuResource can host FUN_1407452c0: CS::MenuResource::MenuResource (dump 0x140748b30) initializes the embedded Scaleform load result/value, copies load_info name fields at +0x48/+0x50, and zeroes +0x58/+0x60. During TitleBackViewParts ctor (dump 0x1409a62d0) it then calls FUN_140749290(MenuResource=this+0x8, SceneObjProxy=this+0x70). FUN_140749290 calls FUN_140749840 if *(MenuResource+0x60)==0. FUN_140749840 loads the Scaleform file through FUN_140d6a990, stores the resulting inline owner/object at MenuResource+0x60, builds a CSScaleformLoadResult, constructs a SceneObjProxy, marks it visible, and finalizes through CSScaleform helpers. FUN_1407452c0 itself calls FUN_140748700(owner+0x60), then FUN_140d6ac00(GLOBAL_CSScaleform, owner, target, symbol). FUN_140748700 appends one pair to a fixed vector whose count is at owner+0x60+0x608 and capacity is 0x10 entries of 0x60 bytes. Therefore TitleBackViewParts+0x8 is not categorically invalid as a bind owner after construction, but a late bind must prove owner+0x60 is initialized, its count < 0x10, and the target/symbol pair storage is native-shaped and alive; otherwise it can panic/overflow/corrupt. Prior raw/direct bind crashes are consistent with calling at the wrong timing or with wrong pair storage/owner ABI, not proof the route is impossible. Next safe step: add observe-only telemetry for existing dialog+0xaa8 MenuResource+0x60 pointer/count/capacity after the aliased GFX load, and only if count is safe consider using the existing title resource owner for a single MENU_DummyProfileFace_01 -> SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00 bind.

=== TWO MENU LAYERS (distinct subsystems) ===
LAYER 1 TITLE MENU = FD4 MenuWindowJobs at owner+0x130, built by BeginLogo main-menu builder 0x14081f180 (5 items via per-item 0x140820500), behind an input-gated IfElseJob predicate. FIXED ORDER: Continue=idx0 (textid 0x142ac3c60), LoadGame=d180=idx1 (textid 0x142ac3be8), NewGame=idx2, Settings=idx3, Quit=idx4. d180 unique-IDed by [item+0xa8] action functor _Do_call 0x140820c60 (add rcx,8; jmp 0x14081ead0=dialog_factory). RUNTIME-PROVEN (d180-hover-vs-confirm-runtime-verdict-2026): d180 does NOT tick on hover; only the 3 title-composition widgets (c000/c140/c280) tick leaf 0x1407ad1c0 on hover. d180 is CREATED+invoked only when its functor fires (at confirm, or by us firing it directly).
LAYER 2 SLOT LIST = router_this CSMenu (ctor REAL ENTRY 0x1409060d0 NOT 0x1409060d8 [doc addr skipped 2 pushes]; vtable 0x142afa070; cursor [+0xb0c]=[+0xa38+0xd4]; bound [+0xb08]; ROW VECTOR [+0x1290]/[+0x1298] stride 0x210; list-model sub-obj [+0x12c8/+0x12d0] vtable 0x142af07c8). SUBAGENT-B KEY FINDING: router_this is the ProfileLoadDialog's SLOT-list controller, NOT the title menu. It is built ON DEMAND by the menu-window-id registry (registry slot .data 0x142abad08 -> builder thunk 0x1407fd450 -> factory 0x140907150 alloc 0x13d0 -> ctor 0x1409060d0) WHEN ProfileLoadDialog is constructed (dialog ctor 0x1409a3d90 -> sub-build 0x1409a51c0 -> slot-list CSMenu builder 0x14078c470). Its rows ([+0x1290]) = SAVE SLOTS, populated by set_items thunk 0x14078ec90 -> rebuild_rows 0x14078d2c0 -> spec_to_row 0x14078cf70 (zero-input, pure data). NOT populated at ctor time, NOT by the update pump 0x140745570.

=== WHY SELF-FIRE FAILED (structural) ===
own_stepper STAGE1d self-fire registrar 0x1409b24e0 operates ONLY on the TitleTopDialog (owner+0xe0): sets latch [+0xa40]=1, registers title-COMPOSITION specs (vtable 0x142b264f0). It NEVER fires d180, so ProfileLoadDialog is never built, so router_this is never constructed -> [router_this+0x1290] empty, cursor/bound 0 (exactly the runtime observation: 'router_this NOT found in dialog fields; rows unreachable'). NEXT: RE the kick target 0x1458f4ac8 (what rcx/global it derefs) so we can call post+KICK correctly (likely kick(rcx=dialog+0x10) or kick(rcx=dialog)), replicating the open-menu registrar's post+kick exactly but with the LoadGame job. STATE after 6 user-driven runs (2026-07-01). Each fix RELOCATED the crash rather than converging -> the inject-ProfileSelect-in-world + drive-confirm/close/return-title-via-hooks approach fights the native menu lifecycle, producing NON-DETERMINISTIC corruption (Scaleform type-confusion / wine-CRT faults / gaitem deserialize). CONFIRMED WINS: (1) robust in-world load-job block; (2) gaitem guards neutered (no gaitemInsTable corruption); (3) menu-pump-OWNED return-title submit works (moved submit out of the racy game-task tick into the MenuWindowJob::Run hook -- see 'menu-pump-run-hook' source; return_title_submit=1 observed); (4) removing the confirm hook's load_job_ctx+0x14c=2 write ELIMINATED the deterministic CSGaitemImp::Deserialize crash (0x67141a) -- that mode-arm is what set GameMan.saveState/b80=2 so DoSaveStuff deserialized the picked slot into the LIVE world. KEY COUPLING PROBLEM: the ProfileSelect CLOSE and the in-world LOAD are COUPLED through load_job_ctx+0x14c=2. Writing dialog+0x1e8=Success closes ProfileSelect ONLY when the load-mode is also armed; remove the load-mode and ProfileSelect stays open (profile_closed=0) -> stalls -> new corrupted-dispatch crash at the product-core handoff (RIP in wine/CRT, rcx=0x142c7bfa8 whose first field is a FUNCTION 0x141097f00 not a vtable). TWO CLEAN FORKS: (A) restore load_job_ctx+0x14c=2 (fixes close) AND hook the load-ONLY routine FUN_14067b380 (live 0x67b380, called by DoSaveStuff when saveState==2) to SKIP the in-world deserialize during the transition (gate: phase active && local player present) while allowing it at clean title for the autoload -- surgical 'block load-direction only'; needs a new MinHook. (B) decouple: don't confirm-load at all -- capture the picked slot, close ProfileSelect via the NATIVE CANCEL (FUN_1407ac980 live 0x7ac890, sets Failed) from menu-pump ownership (the confirm hook runs in the pump), then let the menu-pump return-title submit + autoload run. Deeper option: let the native Quit-Game return-to-title run UNMODIFIED (it is stable) and only redirect the final autoload target slot, capturing the pick from ProfileSelect without driving its confirm-load. Current tree: load-mode arm REMOVED (breaks close) -- revert or pick a fork. DURABLE BEHAVIORAL RULE (user, 2026-06-17, emphatic + repeated, ready-to-uninstall). LOOK BEFORE ACTING -- no blind spray. The analogy, kept deliberately crude so it STICKS (the user insisted it be recorded verbatim or the lesson is moot): LOOK WHERE YOU'RE TAKING A SHIT BEFORE YOU SHIT. Do not commit the act (build machinery, pick a parameter, launch a run, fire an action) before looking at exactly where it lands. Acting first and checking the mess afterward is a blind spray that hits the wrong target -- your toilet or Anthropic HQ, you won't know until you've already gone. CONCRETE: verify ground truth (read the binary/disasm, read live state, take a screenshot, check the actual value) BEFORE building or running. Do not encode assumptions as code/params then discover at runtime they were wrong. When a problem is DETERMINISTIC and you have the facts (cursor starts at index 0, target index 1 -> exactly ONE step), use the exact known value as a LITERAL; never add a tunable/env-var/multi-iteration loop to paper over uncertainty you do not actually have (this session: INJECT_NAV_MAX_CYCLES=14 for a one-Down problem -> cursor sailed past Load Game to System). If you catch yourself adding range/retry/cycle params to a fully-determined problem, STOP -- you skipped looking. Repeated wrong assumptions in one session, each 'fixed' by another run (rows-empty, XInput-polled, 14-cycles -- all wrong), is the SIGNATURE of acting-before-looking; switch to static/read-only verification FIRST. Recorded in /home/banon/AGENTS.md. Related: course-correction-persist-dont-reply-substanceless, menu-populated-zeroinput-offsets-wrong-xinput-not-polled-2026, privacy-never-enumerate-user-windows.

CORRECTS direct-build-rows-gated-by-menu-screen-registry-0x143d6b800-2026 (the gate-blame was WRONG) and closes the sync-peek lever from profilesummary-populate-is-the-dead-cold-b80-path-menu-session-wall-2026.

FINDINGS (all disasm-verified):
1. GATE 0x140765030(key 0x3c) is NOT the blocker. Jump table 0x143b34f50[key*8]; entry for 0x3c (@0x143b35130) = 0x140764df0 = 'mov al,1; ret' -> ALWAYS RETURNS TRUE. (The 0x140765040 'registry 0x143d6b800 linked-list walk' I and the prior subagent read is a DIFFERENT key's handler, not 0x3c.) So the dialog list-builder 0x140875590's gate passes; the empty rows are NOT a registry/screen-registration gate.
2. The REAL + ONLY blocker = per-slot active byte [ProfileSummary+8+slot]==0 (ProfileSummary=[0x143d5df38]+0x78). The list-builder slot-valid 0x140261cd0 = byte[profile+slot+8]; 0 for all slots cold -> no rows appended -> bound=0.
3. SYNC-PEEK 0x140678a50 = DEAD for populate: it reads [GameDataMan 0x143d5df38 +0x8] (the slot-record CONTAINER, NOT ProfileSummary +0x78), sync-reads a save PATH/NAME (token 0x47054 via 0x140760970), calls 0x1402610c0 which just copies the save file-name string into container+0x94, and sets GameMan[0x143d69918]+0xe70=1 (the 'save container ready' flag). It does NOT touch ProfileSummary or any active byte.
4. The activate fn 0x1407890c9 (one of the 4 ACTIVATE 0x140262250 callers): gate(always true) -> slot = 0x140261b60(ProfileSummary) = FIND-FIRST-ACTIVE-SLOT (loops 0..9 for byte[profile+8+slot]!=0, returns -1 if none) -> cold returns -1 -> ACTIVATE SKIPPED. So it only RE-activates an already-active slot (no-op cold). It has ZERO callers (reflection/vtable dispatched). Not a populate.
5. ACTIVATE 0x140262250 callers (the ONLY writers of the active byte): 0x14067b7fe + 0x14067ba13 (both inside register 0x14067b750, called from menu_deser 0x82bad1 + MoveMapList 0x140afb967), 0x14078911f (the no-op refresh #4), 0x140af19ec (MoveMapListStep LoadSaveData). ALL are the per-slot DESERIALIZE machine -- the active byte is set as a SIDE EFFECT of actually deserializing/mounting that slot (the b80/UserMan/menu-deser machine), which is RUNTIME-DEAD cold (primitive-b80-path-conclusively-dead-submit-fails / cold-b80-char-mount-dead-even-with-worker).

CONCLUSION: there is NO zero-input sync/standalone way to populate ProfileSummary (active bytes + records). Population is inseparable from actually running the slot DESERIALIZE, which needs the live menu SESSION/worker lane that does not exist cold. The direct ProfileLoadDialog BUILD is solved (direct-build-dialog-WORKS), but its rows + char-mount both wait on this same cold-dead deserialize machine. The 'easy populate' search is EXHAUSTED (sync-peek dead, gate not-a-gate, no standalone activate).

REMAINING REAL OPTIONS (both hard, known walls): (A) ONE-TIME user Load-Game capture with hooks to learn what the live menu-screen ENTRY does to make the UserMan/b80 IO actually DRAIN (the worker/session-lane setup) that the cold drive lacks -> replicate it; this is the 'why live-not-cold' question, the genuine crux. (B) STRATEGIC PIVOT to the long-standing fork: drive the native title-accept -> front-end/session BOOTSTRAP (frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned-not-callable) so the load runs in-context, abandoning the cold-menu-drive entirely. The direct-build proved the dialog layer is bypassable, but the SAVE-DATA layer is not bypassable cold. NAV-STALL DIAGNOSIS + user concurrency insight (2026-07-19, run ...194439): sq_repro_state reached 2 = SQ_REPRO_STATE_TO_SYSTEM (state enum: WAIT_WORLD 0, OPEN_MENU 1, TO_SYSTEM 2, TO_PROFILE 3, TO_SLOT 4, CONFIRM 5, DONE 6). So sq-repro DID advance WAIT_WORLD->OPEN_MENU->TO_SYSTEM (moved 60 frames, opened menu, started toward OptionSetting) but nav counters all 0 (never reached OptionSetting/ProfileSelect) then teardown. Verdict was 'PASS (a load PROVED movement)' -> the HARNESS tears down on the MOVEMENT-PROOF semaphore (can_move/60-frame), which is PREMATURE: it cuts off the menu navigation that still needs to run to reach load2. USER CONCURRENCY INSIGHT: open-menu + menu-nav can overlap the move-proof since NOTHING in the menu needs the analog stick, and the full menu path (open -> Load Save) may complete in UNDER 60 movement frames -- so don't serialize [move 60] then [nav] with the run dying on move-proof. FIX: (1) the run/teardown success condition must be LOAD2 completion (sq_repro DONE / switch_index>0 / a load2 world-ready semaphore), NOT the load1 movement-proof; (2) sq-repro should not gate the whole menu drive behind the full 60-frame CAN_MOVE_CONFIRMED -- start the menu drive as soon as in-world, run move-proof concurrently/first-but-not-blocking. Also RE why TO_SYSTEM->TO_PROFILE didn't complete (UP,A menu-nav input reaching the open menu vs OptionSetting-detection). See [[driver-moves-and-opens-menu-but-no-nav-then-teardown-2026-07-19]].

BUG B ROOT + FIX (DONE, user-confirmed 'didn't see the texture'): the portrait source is chosen by a 'largest TEXTURE2D' scan (find_d3d12_resource over the offscreen nest); our 256x256 neutral CreateTpfResCap textures gave it wrong candidates, so right after Continue (before the head RT is ready) it grabbed ours. Gate wired at all 3 store sites (site 5736 resets PROFILE_BAKE_RGBA_CAPTURED on reject to retry the one-shot). So Bug A = the live capture reads the WRONG renderer/nest (256) while the 1024 head is on a different renderer. DETERMINISTIC-POINTER attempt: resolve_content_resource_deterministic(srv_gx) returns None for the build-own renderer -- srv_gx+0x10 IS the CSOffscreenGxTexture (vtable RVA 0x2b80278 = PROFILE_GX_GPU_WRAPPER_VTABLE_RVA, hop1 OK) BUT h1+0x18 (GX_RES_CHAIN_HOLDER_A_OFFSET) is NULL on build-own (menu-renderer layout differs), and NONE of off/trc/srv_gx/h1's fields (0x00..0xf8) are in_d3d=true -- the ID3D12Resource is DEEPER (h1+0x10 -> 0x...vt_rva=0x2b761b0 ER obj, unexplored). NEXT for Bug A: either (1) select the RIGHT renderer (the one whose RT is 1024) for the live capture, or (2) finish RE'ing the build-own head-RT pointer chain past h1+0x10, or (3) make the target renderer reliably build its RT at 1024. resolve_content_resource_deterministic is now pub(crate). GATE-PINPOINT (2026-06-22, automated cold sw-bp probe, 5 bps on the worker IO path). Refines b80-cold-worker-runs-bails-before-OS-open. FIRING ORDER on the worker thread for the SAVE job (rcx=job 0x7fffb09fe0e0, job vtable rax=0x1431fc470):
- 0x2410cd0 (worker execute) FIRED.
- 0x240d5c0 FIRED from inside execute (caller 0x142410d52), returned rax=0 (worker then bailed).
- 0x2415200 (open fn) NEVER fired. 0xe45660 (OS open) fired only from boot device-init (0xdefec7) + our DLL, NEVER from the worker. So the worker bails BEFORE the open.
- 0x141edc930 (device readiness) fired 24x but ALL from an UNRELATED subsystem (callers 0x26exxxx, different thread) -- NOT the save worker. Red herring; device-readiness is NOT the save gate.

CORRECTION to the prior 'rdx=0xffffffff = invalid handle' read: DISASM of 0x14240d5c0 shows it FORCES edx=-1 itself (or /usr/sbin/zshxffffffff,edx @0x14240d5fb) as a sentinel -- the 0xffffffff is not a bad input. 0x14240d5c0 is NOT a one-line predicate: it does lea 0xb0(job),rdi; call 0x141ed6210; call 0x14240afe0 (FD4 FS-SERVICE singleton getter *0x144853048); mov rcx,[rax]; mov rax,[rcx]; call *0x18(rax) (FS-service vtable+0x18 method, rdx=&out); RTTI assert; builds an EMPTY cap-7 SSO string @rsp+0x40. => 0x14240d5c0 is the worker's SAVE-PATH / FS-SERVICE RESOLUTION step (resolve save dir via FS service *0x144853048).

ACCURATE FINDING: the cold worker reaches the FS-service path-resolution (0x14240d5c0) but NEVER the open (0x142415200) -- the read bails in/right after that resolution. This pins the long-standing 'empty/malformed save-dir' root cause (b80-ROOTCAUSE-worker-empty-iodev-dir, b80-worker-dir-component-malformed) to a SPECIFIC function: the FS-service resolution 0x14240d5c0 via singleton *0x144853048 (getter 0x14240afe0). Cold, that resolution yields empty/invalid -> open skipped -> read completes empty -> b80 2->0/stuck-0.

NEXT (static decode, no probe): fully decode 0x14240d5c0 -- what FS-service vtable+0x18 (*0x144853048) returns cold vs live, and where the save-dir it should resolve comes from (the per-request dir the LIVE menu/Continue flow sets that cold lacks). The live Continue worker (which opens+reads) broken at 0x14240d5c0 would show the valid resolution to diff against. NOTE bd b80-RUNTIME-savedir-installed-but-read-still-discarded set the dir on io20 path-DB and it did NOT fix it -- so the dir must be set where 0x14240d5c0's FS-service resolution reads it (likely the FS service *0x144853048 state / the job+0xb0 region passed to 0x141ed6210), not io20. Tooling: er-effects-breakpoints.txt (hex RVA/line) sw-bp engine, works under Wine. Save-safe (45-Slots fixture restored pristine ca22fcb5 after). Relates: b80-cold-worker-runs-bails-before-OS-open-swbp-2026-06-22, savedir-CONFIG-LEVER-setter-0x14240a2a0-io20-entry-bb0-2026-06-21, b80-fullread-ROOTCAUSE-iodev-unbound-no-file-handle-cold-2026-06-21, LIVE-CONTINUE-LOAD-CHAIN-captured-swbp-2026-06-21.

DEFINITIVE gate decode (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY disasm) of current_slot_load 0x14067b570 (the fn that sets GameMan+0xb80=1), answering Q1 'why b80 never engages at our in-context loaded state'. Decoded the COMPLETE gate chain (func 0x14067b570..0x14067b746, 470 bytes):

GATE ORDER (all must pass to reach the b80=1 write):
 (g0) 0x140e62ac0() -> test al; jne 0x14067b71d. DECODED 0x140e62ac0 = 'xor al,al; ret' (always returns 0/false). So this gate ALWAYS PASSES (jne never taken). NOT a blocker. [The bytes after 0x140e62ac2 are a different fn / pdata over-run, ignore.]
 (g1) slot = [GameMan 0x143d69918 +0xac0]; inc eax; cmp eax,0xa; ja 0x14067b713 (-> clears b73, fail). So SLOT must be in 0..9 (slot+1 <= 10). [GameMan+0xac0]==-1 (no slot) FAILS here.
 (g2) cmp [GameMan+0xb80],0; jne 0x14067b72b (fail, al=0). b80 MUST be 0.
 (g3) call 0x140256590; test rax; je 0x14067b72b (fail). SESSION/singleton 0x140256590() MUST be non-null.
 (g4) allocator [0x143d872e0]; rax=[rcx]; edx=0x60000; r8d=1; call [rax+0x50]; test rax; je fail. Scheduler/allocator 0x143d872e0 vcall+0x50 (alloc 0x60000 buf) MUST succeed.
 Then: builds request via 0x141ede5e0/.../ writes GameMan+0xb98/+0xba0 (rsp request handle copy), gets IO device 0x140e6e060, calls ASYNC SUBMIT 0x140e6ec70(rcx=iodev, rdx=buf, r8d=0xa size-token, r9d=ebx async-flag) -> al -> bl.
 FINAL b80=1 WRITE @0x14067b6f6: test bl,bl; je 0x14067b70e (SKIP). Only if 0x140e6ec70 returned TRUE: mov [GameMan+0xb80],1; mov byte [GameMan+0xb73],0. Returns al=bl.

KEY: b80=1 is set ONLY when the async submit 0x140e6ec70 succeeds (bl!=0). g1 (slot in 0..9) and g2 (b80==0) and g3 (session 0x140256590()!=0) and g4 (allocator) are the entry gates. At our in-context m60-loaded state: b80 stayed 0 the whole time per the brief. So EITHER current_slot_load is never CALLED (the dispatcher 0x140afb880 gate upstream fails), OR it is called but a gate fails (g1 slot==-1? g3 session null? g4 alloc?), OR 0x140e6ec70 returns false. Next: confirm whether the dispatcher even calls it (decode getter 0x1406793d0 + dispatcher-2 0x140afb880 gates), and whether session 0x140256590() is non-null at our placeholder-world state. Related: native-autoload-slot-ac0-plus-force-flag, real-continue-load-driver-recipe, save-mount-is-b80-inside-movemapstep-not-separate-2026.

EXACT FIELD for the cold-save-read fix (completes b80-cold-COMPLETE-CHAIN-SLLoadSession-emptydir-code8). Decoded slot-3 dir accessor 0x142410c60 (SLLoadSession vtable+0x18):
  mov rcx,[rcx+0xe0]   ; iodev/request object = [SLLoadSession+0xe0]
  call 0x14240aa60     ; = lea rax,[rcx+8]  -> iodev+8
  call 0x1424090c0     ; reads the std::u16string at (iodev+8)+0xe0 (SSO: cap check, data ptr)
  cmpw 0,(rax)         ; EMPTY-STRING CHECK (first wchar==0). cold -> empty -> caller FUN_14240d5b0 sees str.size==0 -> returns 0 -> _Func02 code 8 -> no open.

So the DIR string slot-3 returns lives at: [SLLoadSession+0xe0] (=the request/iodev object, == io18=[static iodev 0x1445893b0 +0x18] after submit) + 8 + 0xe0. i.e. on the request object: data/SSO at ~+0xe8, size at +0xf8 (matches the live winedbg capture req+0xe8='.sl2' req+0xf8=size in b80-iodev-identity-CORRECTED / b80-worker-dir-component-malformed). cmpw checks (req+8+0xe0) first wchar.

FIX: before the worker runs slot-3, set the u16string at [SLLoadSession+0xe0]+8+0xe0 to the REAL save folder (e.g. C:\users\steamuser\AppData\Roaming/EldenRing/76561197986456766/ -- the savedir builder 0x140e0e680 produces this, per savedir-CONFIG-LEVER). The request object = io18 = [0x1445893b0+0x18] (exists AFTER the 0x67b1a0 submit). NOTE prior savedir attempts set io20/path-DB ([0x1445893b0+0x20]) -- WRONG object; slot-3 reads [SLLoadSession+0xe0] which is io18/the request (+0x18), NOT io20. THAT mismatch likely why b80-RUNTIME-savedir-installed-but-read-still-discarded failed. Set the dir on the io18 request object (+8+0xe0) instead.

USER PREFERENCE (2026-07-04): when working with any file/data FORMAT (new or existing) -- GFX/SWF, TPF/DDS, FLVER, params/regulation, fonts, BND, save .sl2, etc. -- set up or BUILD UPON the dedicated format CRATE rather than handling the format ad-hoc inline in the DLL. Existing crates: er-gfx (Scaleform GFX/SWF codec incl. DefineFont3/DefineEditText/edit.rs + raster.rs glyph rasterizer), er-tpf (DDS/TPF builder incl. draw.rs compositing), er-flver, er-soulsformats/soulsformats, er-save-loader, er-shaderkit, er-objectkit, erpx-rs, er-safe-input, effects-data. WHY: keeps format logic host-testable (round-trip/unit tests), reviewable, and reusable across the DLL + tools; avoids scattering byte-twiddling in runtime glue. HOW TO APPLY: put format parse/build/transform/rasterize logic in the crate with tests; the DLL should only do runtime glue (hooks, game calls, memory reads) on top of the crate API. For the ProfileSelect stats panel this means: 05_010 GFX widen/EditText edits go through er-gfx (mirror title_05_000.rs with a title_05_010 module + edit tables); text glyph rasterization uses er-gfx::raster; texture/panel building uses er-tpf::DdsImage/draw + Tpf. SceneProxy-back-ref recipe REFUTED at runtime: [TitleTopDialog+0xa38] = 0x142ac7f20 = RTTI .?AVTitleFlowContext@CS@@ (NOT SceneObjProxy 0x142a94a70). Its +0x20 is null; diag scan [proxy..+0x40] shows +0x10 = a HeapAllocator<Win32RuntimeHeapImpl> (vt 0x1430c7d30), rest null/data -- NO MenuWindow vtable anywhere in it. So agent-1 +0x20 recipe was wrong: the SceneObjProxy-with-MenuWindow@+0x20 is the dispatch-time rdx arg (built by 0x140745280 FROM the MenuWindow), NOT the dialog capture.

CORRECTED UNDERSTANDING: [td+0xa38] holds the TITLE FLOW CONTEXT (the capture the Load-Game functor uses). rcx = td+0xa38 IS still the correct factory CAPTURE arg (factory r8 = *(td+0xa38) = TitleFlowContext ptr, matching native -- the ProfileLoadDialog ctor 0x1409a3d90 takes r8 = TitleFlowContext, not a SceneProxy). So the capture half is solved. The MenuWindow (factory RDX) is a SEPARATE dispatch-time arg that is NOT derivable from the capture and NOT present in TitleFlowContext[0..0x40]. The SceneObjProxy(+0x20=MenuWindow) is DOWNSTREAM of the MenuWindow, not a source.

=> THE MENUWINDOW MUST BE CAPTURED, not derived. The robust path is the HOOK (option 2, agent a84db266e818497bb): hook a per-frame menu/SceneProxy functor dispatch at the title that carries (MenuWindow* in rdx, SceneProxy& in r8) and capture the live MenuWindow* the engine itself uses. The title-host MenuWindow is the rdx that was passed when the TitleTopDialog was built (factory 0x14081e5e0) during boot -- so a per-frame menu dispatch hook is the way to recover it post-boot. If NO functor fires headless per-frame with both args, alternative: hook the TitleTopDialog factory 0x14081e5e0 / the dialog-factory dispatch at boot to latch the MenuWindow rdx the FIRST time any title dialog is built.

CORRECTION: finalize-drive SOFTLOCKS load2 (user killed the run, 2026-07-20) -- NOT a world-teardown. My prior memory [[finalize-drive-reveals-load2-has-no-valid-destination-world-teardown-fix-is-upstream-movemap-target-2026-07-20]] was WRONG (said present->False world-teardown). What actually happened: driving load2 finalize (write fin=5 + clear saveState/warp so it walks) SOFTLOCKED the game -- it hung/froze unresponsive, and the USER tore down the run manually. So force-driving the finalize is CONFIRMED HARMFUL (softlock), even worse than leaving load2 movable-but-unsettled. This matches the AGENTS.md warning: manually pumping/forcing native step machines is the wrong integration boundary; the finalize must complete via native ownership, not a forced write. The finalize-drive branch must be REVERTED (diagnostic only, softlocks). The underlying problem stands: load2 finalize does not naturally complete (diverges at MoveMapStep finalize per the divergence semaphore), and forcing it softlocks -> the fix is the native completion path, not a forced write. Do NOT ship the finalize-drive.

DECISIVE REFRAME (2026-06-23, runs 203349/203957, gamescope full 41-45s runs, game_task_ticks 745-759). The gold does NOT load because THE GAME NEVER READS THE SAVE AT ALL in our autoload title flow. PROOF: with dedicated diag budgets that early-boot churn can't exhaust -- NtCreateFile sl2-filtered diag (cap 120, catches ALL opens incl. RootDirectory-relative) = ZERO .sl2 opens; new SL2-QUERY logger (cap 40, GetFileAttributesW/ExW/FindFirstFileW via save_path_api_redirect, any path containing 'ER0000') = ZERO hits. So no open AND no existence-check of ER0000.sl2 ever. The save-redirect layer (SHGetFolderPathW->Z:, CreateFileW/GetFileAttributes redirect, free-space override) is CORRECT but NEVER EXERCISED. NOT a steamid/path/checksum issue (gold valid: md5 ok, steamid 766 matches, lvl150 Banon).

ROOT CAUSE is UPSTREAM in the title flow: the profile-summary/save read (SLLoadSession -> SAVE_DIR_BUILDER 0x140e0e680 -> open ER0000.sl2, per savedir-DISCOVERY-direct-path-CreateFileW-no-enum) is NEVER TRIGGERED. Our autoload flow = pab-advance (2) -> forced menu-open open_menu 0x1409b24e0 (1) + force-offline (1) + network-check/show-progress suppression -> 850x continue-scan (privacy policy up, no Continue node) -> save-load watchdog abort. The forced menu-open likely PREEMPTS the natural post-press-any-button title step that triggers the profile load, so the menu opens with an empty profile -> privacy policy (gated solely on char presence). Vanilla boot reads the save fine, so OUR hooks prevent it.

NEXT: (1) RE the vanilla title flow -- what step/function triggers the boot profile-summary read after press-any-button, and which of our hooks (forced menu-open / PAB advance / job suppression) skips it. (2) Let that read fire (reading our staged gold) BEFORE the privacy-vs-menu decision -- candidate: drop/defer forced menu-open and let the natural post-PAB flow read the profile + open the menu itself. Static RE: ProfileSelectDialog ctor FUN_1409a5310 has two direct constructor callers: FUN_1409a2f30 builds ProfileDeleteDialog and FUN_1409a3ee0 builds ProfileLoadDialog. Both first assign SceneObjProxy component "ProfileList/ItemList/ItemList/ItemList", then call FUN_1409a5310 with the root SceneObjProxy and list SceneObjProxy. FUN_1409a3ee0 then sets the vtable to CS::ProfileLoadDialog, labels text id 0x631f0, calls GetMenuSystemSaveLoad() and FUN_1409a6070(dialog, saveSlot) to select the row whose item+8 equals saveSlot, then installs callbacks/jobs through FUN_1407574d0 and FUN_140744590. FUN_1409a52b0 wires dialog subobjects at +0x1118 and +0xa38; FUN_1409a2e40 selects valid grid/list rows and refreshes. This means the native ProfileLoad/ProfileSelect constructor path is dialog/list-selection/job active and likely blocking if replayed wholesale; a title-safe portrait surface should reuse the resource/owner +0x3b8 binding and GFx symbols, but avoid installing ProfileLoadDialog selection callbacks/jobs or the full modal/list state.
