DEFINITIVE (2026-06-16, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY disasm) -- confirms WHERE the b72 consumer runs and the getter gates, narrowing why b80 stays 0 at the brief's state.

=== DISPATCHERS RUN UNCONDITIONALLY INSIDE THE MoveMapStep UPDATE (no extra gate) ===
recon_callscan: dispatcher-1 0x140afbad0 has EXACTLY 1 caller (0x140aff720); dispatcher-2 0x140afb880 EXACTLY 1 caller (0x140aff728); 0x140afa6d0 1 caller (0x140aff730). All three are called BACK-TO-BACK and UNCONDITIONALLY from the MoveMapStep per-frame update 0x140aff640 @0x140aff720/728/730 (decoded: plain 'mov rcx,rbx; call' sequence, NO branch guarding them). So IF the MoveMapStep is ticking (brief confirms world loaded+rendered => it ticks), dispatcher-2 IS calling the b72 consumer every frame. The MoveMapStep update also itself calls 0x14067a080 @0x140aff73a (b80==0 check) for a timeout accumulator at +0x130.

=== b72 CONSUMER GETTER 0x1406793d0 GATES (DECODED, 84 bytes) ===
Returns the EFFECTIVE b72 = b72_byte filtered by 3 gates:
 ebx = [GameMan 0x143d69918 +0xb72]
 call 0x14080d060; if al!=0 -> cmovne ebx,0 (ZEROES it). So 0x14080d060() MUST be FALSE.
 call 0x14080d570; if al==0 -> cmove ecx,0 (ZEROES). So 0x14080d570() MUST be TRUE.
 cmp [GameMan+0xbc4],3; if !=3 -> cmovne edi,edx(=filtered b72) else edi stays 0. So [GameMan+0xbc4] MUST be != 3.
 returns dil.
0x14080d060 DECODED: inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0 (assert non-null); returns setne([inputmgr+0x13c]!=0). So [inputmgr+0x13c] MUST be 0 (no pending input action) for b72 to survive.
0x14080d570 DECODED: inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0; if null OR [inputmgr+0x80] null -> returns 1; else returns ([im80+0x298]==0 && [im80+0x290]==0). Returns TRUE when input idle. So at our forced state (no input held) it returns 1 -- PASSES.

=== NATIVE b72 ARMING (save-mgr update 0x14067f5d0 @0x14067fa17..0x14067fa83), for reference ===
The native arm sets [GameMan+0xb72]=1 ONLY if: (1) a timestamp 'setb' condition @0x14067fa17 true (autoload-due), (2) inputmgr non-null, (3) 0x140765780(inputmgr)==true (input idle), (4) 0x14080d570()==true, (5) [GameMan+0xac0]!=-1 (slot set), (6) [0x143d856a0](latch)==0. The save-mgr update itself bails early @0x14067f6cc if session 0x140256590()==null and derefs session 0x1447ef360 -- it is SESSION-scoped, runs only once session exists. The brief FORCED b72=1 directly, bypassing this arm, so these arming gates are moot for the brief's test; only the CONSUMER getter gates above matter.

=== NARROWED ROOT-CAUSE for 'b80 stays 0 with b72=1 forced' ===
With the MoveMapStep ticking + b72=1 forced + ac0=slot, the only ways b80 stays 0: (A) getter 0x1406793d0 returns 0 because [inputmgr+0x13c]!=0 (0x14080d060 true) or [GameMan+0xbc4]==3; OR (B) it routes to 0x14067b750 but the async submit 0x140e6ec70 returns FALSE (so the b80=1 write @0x14067b8d4 is skipped) -- which per async-io-b80-lane-mismatch is the WORKER-LANE issue (submit enqueues onto pool 0x144853048 backed by stream task 0x144842d40 that is NOT registered/ticked at the forced FE-host). Note: even if submit SUCCEEDS and b80=1, the read never completes (b80 stuck 1) for the SAME worker-registration reason. SUBMIT decode: 0x140e6ec70 is a thunk (cmp [iodev+0x40],0; jne 0x140e6f760; else 0x140e6f940). 0x140e6f940 bails false if [iodev+0x10]!=0 (request in-flight) or [iodev+0x20]!=0 or buf==0; on success calls KICK 0x140e6fb50 -> pool submit 0x14240ae10, stores to [iodev+0x20], returns its success. So submit returning false at our state == the pool/worker lane not accepting/draining. Related: dispatcher2-routing-correction-b72-targets-0x14067b750-2026, b80-engage-gate-decode-current-slot-load-2026, async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026, menu-load-path-is-movemaplist-self-driven-not-movemapstep-2026.

MAJOR CORRECTION (2026-06-15). The ~15s ER boot crashes long blamed on environmental 'session degradation' / wine-orphans / a 'tick-120 ceiling' were actually OUR DLL: (1) find_title_owner_by_vtable's full-memory VirtualQuery+raw-deref scan raced the booting game (region freed between query and deref -> access violation), and (2) a session-RVA TYPO I introduced (SESSION_SINGLETON_RVA 0x447ef360 should be 0x47ef360 = abs 0x1447ef360 - base 0x140000000) made an unconditional read deref 0x1847ef360 -> AV (~9s). PROOF the game/env are fine: vanilla no-DLL boot reaches a window+render (RUNTIME_NO_DLL harness flag), inert-mode DLL boots 30s, and the user's manual Steam launch is fine. FIX: title_accept_tick is now SCAN-FREE (the autoload never needed the owner scan -- splash-skip is a static patch, movie-dismiss reads the movie singleton global, load uses GameMan); session RVA corrected. Boot now stable 30s. SUPERSEDES the primary-cause claims in runtime-degradation-wine-orphans and runtime-session-instability-tick120-ceiling (orphan cleanup is still good hygiene but was NOT the boot-crash cause). Lesson: isolate with a vanilla/inert baseline BEFORE blaming the environment. Related: degradation-can-occur-without-process-orphans.

WHAT WORKS NOW (my fix): the per-frame look-at FINALLY reaches the rendered pixels. ROOT CAUSE of the prior 'static head' was: our OWN-BUILT renderer (OWN_STEPPER_SLOT) was never re-rasterized post-build -- draw_step only loops the title table (base+0x3d6d8d0), not our renderer, and post-table-build it is skipped. FIX (startup_hooks.rs realtime tick, in the readback block, gated portrait_render_drive_enabled): call the offscreen-draw thunk PROFILE_OFFSCREEN_DRIVE_RVA (0xbb8ca0, FUN_140bb8ca0 -> FUN_140bb73a0(*(r+0xa8))) on OUR built renderer r each render-thread frame BEFORE the RT->SRV copy+readback, so the posed model re-rasterizes. PROVEN: oracle_portrait_render_drive_hits 0->227/229, NO crash, world_stable. Pixel proof the look-at now moves the head: rasterize-5 cursor LEFT vs RIGHT = 23/px diff (was 0.4 = identical before); calib-6 LEFT vs CENTER = 37/px. So drive->bones->RE-RASTERIZE->pixels works.

NEW BLOCKER (the next real problem): the model RENDERS FACING AWAY from the camera -- the back of the head is shown at BOTH cursor extremes AND at center (look-at~0). So it is NOT the look-at and NOT the look-at gains (I cut gains ~6x in calib-6: NO change to the back-view -> gains were a red herring; the over-rotation diagnosis was WRONG). The model root/skeleton faces away when our drive(r) rasterizes it; the engine's NATURAL profile render faced front (the pre-fix stale captures + the loading-screen screenshot showed Banon's FACE). So the camera/view our drive(r) uses points at the model's back.

MULTI-LAYER ROOT CAUSE + FIXES for System->Quit->Load-Profile (2026-07-01, runs 1-5, all NO-CRASH since the menu-pump-ownership architecture). (2) WE armed it ourselves: title.rs product_core_autoload_tick was writing gm+0xb78 = picked slot every tick -> FUN_140afb970 saw slot requested -> RequestLoadSlot -> saveState=2. FIX: title.rs now writes b78 = OWN_STEPPER_SLOT_NONE(-1) while old world up (player present), b78 = slot only once player absent (clean title). Plus a gated defense-in-depth hook system_quit_request_load_slot_hook on 0x67b200 (returns 0 'not armed' while switch active + player present). Result: NowLoading + saveState=2 GONE, no spin. (3) REMAINING (run 5): world still never tore down -- menu closes, still in-world, SAME char, stray cursor. The return-to-title TEARDOWN never executes. Our system_quit_submit_direct_return_title_chain only BUILDS+SUBMITS a menu-job (builder 0x79d700, submit 0x7a9250) to the System dialog queue, which stops being pumped once the menus close -> orphaned job. Our final functor (title.rs ~1671) is gated on GameMan+0xbc4 == GAME_MAN_RETURN_TITLE_JOB_PREDICATE_READY(1), and NOTHING sets bc4: the native return-title REQUEST FUN_14067a490 (live 0x67a3a0; sets GameMan.saveRequested=true, field_0xb73=1, and +0xbc4=1) was DEFINED as SYSTEM_QUIT_RETURN_TITLE_REQUEST_RVA but NEVER CALLED (grep: only the const def; request_count=0). FIX (uncommitted, next build): call the native return-title request 0x67a3a0 once inside system_quit_submit_direct_return_title_chain (menu-pump owned, plain GameMan field write) BEFORE the build+submit -> sets bc4=1 + saveRequested -> game saves + performs native return-to-title teardown AND our final functor can fire. HYPOTHESIS run6: OK -> ProfileSelect cancel-close -> return-title request sets bc4/saveRequested -> world saves + tears down -> player absent -> b78=slot -> clean-title autoload of the DIFFERENT char. KEY GAMEMAN OFFSETS (GLOBAL_GameMan @0x143d69918): +0xb78 requestedSaveSlotLoad, +0xb80 saveState (2=load), +0xbc4 return-title predicate (1=ready), +0xb73/+0xb75 quit flags, saveRequested. USER REFRAME (2026-06-22, correct, reconciles the autoresearch finding): the ToS/legal popup is NOT an independent wall to suppress -- it is a SYMPTOM of advancing the title BEFORE the save/common-profile is loaded. The autoresearch lane runs 248/249 concluded ToS-builds-on-any-advance, but that was the accept-byte advance walking press-any-button to menu WITHOUT loading any save -> the ToS-accepted flag (which lives in the loaded save/common-profile data) was not set -> ToS built. The NATIVE Continue never shows the ToS precisely because it LOADS the save (setting the accepted flag). So loading the save at the right time makes the ToS resolve as a side effect -- do NOT separately RE/suppress the ToS gate (0x140e4fda0 -> 0x145ba1c41 is heavily CONTROL-FLOW-OBFUSCATED: all push/pop/jmp *[rsp] trampolines, impractical to RE directly). REFOCUS: the real lever is loading the save correctly (reach the settled load-menu state, then the native Continue/build recipe that loads the save), which sets the profile/ToS-accepted state -> no ToS -> load completes. This means the prior tos-suppress angle (tos-builds-on-any-title-advance-need-upstream-accepted-gate-2026, own-stepper-setstate-replays-splash-use-accept-byte-advance step 2 ToS-suppress) is DE-PRIORITIZED -- focus on the save-load, not the ToS. Relates: ownload-must-reach-load-menu-state-no-bare-title-shortcut-2026-06-22, golden-native-continue-loadgame-build-args-2026-06-22.

BUG (2026-07-15, native Windows, game 1.16.2.0): System->Quit->'Load Profile' / 'Load Save Profiles' (our own-menu character-switch path) CRASHES the game. Debug-log sequence from the live user-inspection run: click -> 'system-quit-dup: profile-load route FIRE 05_010_ProfileSelect wrapper' -> SUBMIT job -> ProfileSelect appears (10x MENU_DummyProfileFace_01..10 binds, rewritten_visible_profile_surface=true to SYSTEX_ErTpf_Prf00..09) -> 'own_stepper: builder ret(owner)=... slot=5 effective_slot=5 for native selector self-pump' -> 'AcquireMenuResource 01_010_MessageBox' -> 'msgbox-builder #1: dialog=0x..80 vt_rva=0x2b03550 captured=true in_world=true' -> GAME DIES (log stops, no AV marker in DLL log; me3 saw process exit rc=0). The msgbox-builder caller stack interleaves OUR dll (self+0x12c8ec, self+0x31f9, self+0xfa664, self+0xeda03) with game frames (game+0x7b04c7, +0x7ad2bc, +0x7aa2bb, +0x7a968e), so our profile-load hook path is what routes into the CS::MessageBoxDialog build. Per AGENTS.md a MessageBoxDialog around character load is a HARD crash trigger; product proof requires ZERO msgbox builds -- the fix is to identify the native side-effect the msgbox gates (likely the 'load this save data?' confirm) and satisfy/skip it without UI, not to auto-accept. NOTE game is now 1.16.2.0 (our RVAs derived from 1.16.1) -- vt_rva=0x2b03550 for MessageBoxDialog must be re-verified for 1.16.2. Boot+portrait+autoload work fine on 1.16.2, so core RVAs are OK; the profile-switch/msgbox path is the suspect. Also a SECOND bug: visual ghosting/overlap where a different profile's text ('...Shunning-Grounds', different playtime) bleeds over the System menu after clicking (may be non-deterministic). See [[wslenv-env-not-propagating-to-windows-game]].

ROOT CAUSE (refined, RAM-pinned) of product-route reload freeze + HARNESS BLIND SPOT (2026-07-18, frozen snapshot target/product-runtime-manual/frozen-reload-20260718-143500/telemetry.json). The reload COMPLETES logically: return-title tears down old world (mms 18->19->20->-1), own_load_switch_reload_fire feeds slot -> continue_confirm -> SetState5, WORLDRES 0x1c runs phase 0->10 status4, new MMS steps 1->16, 'menu-free reload COMPLETION: stable in-world 60 frames -> phase IDLE'. BUT the render handoff never happens: oracle_player_render_ready=FALSE and oracle_chr_draw_group_enabled=FALSE (while chr_render_group_enabled=True, chr_enable_render=True), oracle_now_loading(load_done)=1, oracle_loading_screen_close_sent=1 yet oracle_fake_loading_any_visible=True. player.chr_ins.load_state.draw_group_enabled() is a GAME field: the game's end-of-load world-resume step that re-enables the character draw group + marks player render-ready NEVER FIRES on this synthetic reload path (own_load_switch_reload_fire SetState5), so the char is present+controllable-by-model but INVISIBLE and the world is frozen. User saw exactly this: in-world, nothing moving, no char. HARNESS BLIND SPOT: scripts/multi-load-proof-monitor.py controllable() (lines 121-123) gates on oracle_player_present OR player_available AND (oracle_chr_ctrl_present OR oracle_chr_model_ins_present) -- all TRUE in the frozen state -- and NEVER checks oracle_player_render_ready or oracle_chr_draw_group_enabled. So the 'acceptance vanilla core PROVEN 5/5' (acceptance-vanilla-core-proven-2026-07-18) verified logical load (identity+stats+gear+present+model+stable) but NOT render-readiness; it would FALSE-PASS a render-frozen load. Different-map switches MAY have really rendered (full world takeover) while same-map reload doesn't, OR all harness passes were render-frozen and unnoticed (automated). FIX (two parts): (1) DURABLE SEMAPHORE: add oracle_player_render_ready (and chr_draw_group_enabled) as a HARD gate to multi-load-proof-monitor.py controllable/stable so a frozen load FAILS; (2) PRODUCT FIX: make the reload path drive the game's end-of-load world-resume so the char draw group re-enables + player_render_ready flips true (RE where the game normally enables chr_ins.load_state.draw_group at load-complete and why SetState5 reload skips it). msgbox 0->1 was a suppressed '01_010_MessageBox' at return-title (msgbox-skip #0), postload_builds=1 -- investigate if related. Links [[product-route-same-slot-reload-nowload-stuck-2026-07-18]] [[same-slot-reload-must-equal-any-load-user-decision-2026-07-18]] [[acceptance-vanilla-core-proven-2026-07-18]].

SAVE-DIRECTORY CONFIGURATION LEVER (zero-input, in-process; env-prefixed-launch friendly). This is the injection point to make the game read save files from an arbitrary directory -- completes part of the major autoload feature.

WHERE THE GAME STORES THE SAVE DIR IT READS: the path-db object io20 = *(*(0x144589390)+0x20) (dispatch obj 0x1445893b0, +0x20). Inside io20's slot collection, the slot-0 entry holds a std::u16string at entry+0xb0 -- THIS is the directory the worker formats into the .sl2 open path. io20 only exists AFTER submit (NULL before).

INJECT VIA SETTER 0x14240a2a0(rcx=io20, edx=slot(0), r8=char16_t* RAW DATA PTR). Internally: db=[io20]; if db: edx2=[io20+8]; entry=0x14240c270(db,edx2); if entry: 0x14240dce0(entry, slot, char16_t*); then republish 0x14240c630(db,[io20+8]). The 0x14240dce0 path ASSIGNS/COPIES our string into entry+0xb0 (via assign helpers 0x141ed6210/0x141ed6280, lea 0xb0(rcx),r14 @0x14240dd0b) -- so our char16_t* can be a TRANSIENT caller-owned UTF-16 buffer; no game allocator needed for the setter call. This is OPTION (c), the clean lever.

COMPUTING THE DEFAULT DIR (fallback when no override): BUILDER 0x140e0e680(rcx=&wrapper) formats '%s/EldenRing/%s/' (UTF-16 fmt @0x142bda858) = <userdata>/EldenRing/<steamid>/; steamid via Steam singleton chain 0x140e8d550->0x140e8d510, vtable call thru [0x143b48ff0] (guard non-null). Variant 0x140e0e7e0 = '%s/EldenRing' (fmt @0x142bda880, no steamid/trailing slash). The wrapper is a MSVC std::u16string: data union @+0x08 (SSO 8 char16 if cap<8, else heap char16_t*), size @+0x18, cap @+0x20 (init 7), bool @+0x28; S+0 = allocator from getter 0x141eba960 (trivial singleton: call 0x141ebb680; add rax,0x28; ret). Raw ptr = (cap>=8 ? *(S+0x08) : (S+0x08)). For the lever we DON'T need the builder -- we can supply our own char16_t* directly to the setter.

ENV-LEVER DESIGN: at submit time, read an override env (e.g. ER_SAVE_DIR), UTF-16-encode + NUL-terminate, call SETTER on io20 slot 0. Makes the cold full-read open the real .sl2. Zero simulated input, single in-process DLL. NO global directory TEMPLATE exists (the request copy-ctor template [0x1445893b0+0] is zero-init and never carries the dir -- see b80-COLD-FIX-REFUTED...). The directory is per-request, written by the pump handler 0x140e6ded0 on the LIVE boot; the cold path leaves io20 slot-0 EMPTY (cause still TBD: handler opcode 0x17/0x18 not dispatched cold, OR builder yields empty -- needs probe). The setter lever writes io20 directly regardless of cause. Links: [[b80-COLD-FIX-REFUTED-pathdb-transient-setter-wants-char16ptr-2026-06-21]] [[b80-ROOTCAUSE-worker-empty-iodev-dir-string-e0-2026-06-21]]

ROUTING candidate (2026-06-21) for the no-ToS zero-input title advance (follow-up to accept-advance-plus-tossuppress-crashes). The broad global accept byte 0x144589bdc advances the title but routes through the language/ToS (builds TosMultiLangDialog). A NARROWER title-specific advance-to-main-menu path exists at 0x1409b1260: 'mov rax,[0x143d5dea8]; mov rcx,[rcx+0x8]; test rax,rax; je +; movb [rax],1; jmp 0x1409b24e0' -- it sets a flag at *[0x143d5dea8]=1 then TAIL-JUMPS straight to the open-menu registrar 0x1409b24e0 (TitleTopDialog::open_menu = main menu Continue/Load/NewGame), with NO ToS build. This is near the title accept-getter callers (0x1409b1244 region, Arxan-obfuscated around it). HYPOTHESIS: replicating this narrower path (set [0x143d5dea8]=1 if non-null, then call registrar 0x1409b24e0(rcx=dialog)) advances to the main menu WITHOUT the global-accept over-trigger that builds the ToS -- avoiding both the ToS modal AND the null-suppress crash. CAVEAT: registrar self-fire alone previously opened a COMPETING dialog and reverted, so the [0x143d5dea8] flag set may be the missing piece that makes the registrar open the menu IN PLACE (not a competing dialog). NEXT: RE 0x143d5dea8 (what object/flag), confirm 0x1409b1260 is the title press-accept handler, then wire a lever that sets the flag + calls the registrar, verify-only probe -> expect menu_opened_latch=1 AND total_builds=0 (no ToS) AND no process exit. Related: title-global-accept-byte-144589bdc-zeroinput-advance-2026, tos-multilang-modal-suppression-target.

MILESTONE (2026-06-16, runtime-confirmed). We have a WORKING scriptable in-process software-breakpoint engine for ER 1.16.1 under Proton/wine -- the CE-VEH-equivalent the user demanded. Built in er_effects_rs.dll (committed). Two pieces:

1) ANTI-ANTI-DEBUG (ported from Dasaav-dsv/ProDebug, source at /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/ProDebug-main, but CORRECTED). ProDebug.dll itself deterministically crashes 1.16.1 (AV 0x6ffffc4b2eb0 = caller + 0x140000000) because getExeImage()/GetModuleHandle(NULL) resolves the WRONG module base under the LazyLoader. FIX: port its 4 timed-anti-debug (find,patch) patterns into our DLL and scan THIS module via the correctly-resolved game_module_base() (.text only, from PE section headers). crashlog.rs apply_anti_antidebug_once(base): parse hex/wildcard patterns (ANTI_ANTIDEBUG_CHECKS in lib.rs, expressed as strings to satisfy the no-magic-number lint), match over .text, write tiny branch-offset patches via write_code_byte (VirtualProtect RWX), FlushInstructionCache. RUNTIME: patched 181 (0x7a)/1 (0x0f)/138 (0x73)/10 (0x72) sites over .text 0x140001000..0x1429a2c00, NO crash. Auto-enabled when sw-breakpoints are on; runs ONCE from the game task BEFORE arming breakpoints (our controlled timing, not the LazyLoader's). NOTE: do this under the OFFLINE launcher (offline-launcher.exe, no EAC) -- patching .text under Seamless/EAC is risky.

2) INT3 SOFTWARE BREAKPOINTS (crashlog.rs, gated by er-effects-breakpoints.txt = one hex RVA/line). install_sw_breakpoints_once patches 0xCC; the VEH crash_vectored_handler handles EXCEPTION_BREAKPOINT (0x80000003): WINE-ROBUST -- accept the saved Rip AT the int3 (wine) OR PAST it (Windows, Rip-1); restore orig byte, back RIP to the matched bp, set EFlags trap flag, single-step, then re-arm 0xCC on the single-step (persistent). Logs full ctx: rcx/rdx/r8/r9/rax/rsp + 6 stack qwords + RtlCaptureStackBackTrace callers. Without the anti-anti-debug the #BP terminates the process (NtTerminateProcess 0x80000003) -- the anti-debug swallows debug exceptions; WITH it, #BP reaches our VEH.

RUNTIME PROOF: sw-bp #0 at rva 0x7ad1c0 (MenuWindowJob::Update 0x1407ad1c0) FIRED 23+ times, each logging rcx=0x7fff..(menu item this), rax=0x142aa97e8 (MenuWindowJob vtable), caller chain [0x7a968e (menu pump),0x766eec,...]; the game KEPT RUNNING (restore/step/re-arm correct). 

USAGE: drop er-effects-breakpoints.txt (hex RVAs) in GAME_DIR; launch via /tmp/er-offline-walk.sh (offline-launcher, ProDebug NOT chainloaded -- our DLL does it); read hits from GAME_DIR/er-effects-crash.log 'sw-bp #N rva=.. hit=N rcx=..'. WINEESYNC=1 WINEFSYNC=1 set. Wait for eldenring.exe to actually spawn (offline-launcher spawns an intermediate terminal) -- use /tmp/er-wait-and-watch.sh.

NEXT: use this to breakpoint the title->main-menu pump-switch (what makes owner+0x130's menu sequence tick / d180 surface zero-input) -- breakpoint the menu pump 0x140791620 + the registrar, navigate/observe, capture what flips the active-menu holder. Related: prodebug-github-build-crashes-er-1161-2026 (rejected key, info here), wine-esync-fsync-enables-veh-cheat-engine-2026, mainmenu-built-at-owner130-not-pumped-zeroinput-2026, er-teardown-must-sweep-full-wine-helper-set-2026.

bd prime output ballooned to ~2.5MB (~650k tokens) because it inlines all ~1400 memory bodies; this overflowed compaction (PreCompact hook ran 'bd prime') causing 'Compaction failed - conversation could not be reduced below the context limit' and 'Prompt is too long' across Claude/Pi/Codex sessions from 2026-07-17 onward. Evidence: bd prime ~100KB -> 7/7 compactions OK (session b16ef7d2); bd prime ~2.5MB -> 0 OK + 3 genuine /compact failures. FIX: scripts/beads-prime.sh regenerates a titles-only .beads/PRIME.md (bd prime honors PRIME.md as a full override for EVERY caller), cutting output to ~80KB/~20k tokens (32x). PreCompact+SessionStart hooks now call the wrapper. PRIME.md is gitignored (regenerated per-machine). Tune per-memory detail with BEADS_PRIME_MEM_CHARS (0=titles only).

PROCESS LESSON (2026-07-03, cost one wasted 10-switch ER soak + a pushed broken commit c8c0225): (1) NEVER judge a cargo build by a piped exit code -- 'cargo xwin build 2>&1 | tail -5' reports TAIL's exit (0) even when compilation fails; a soak was launched on a stale DLL because the build had actually failed with E0603. Use pipefail, or redirect to a file and check $? directly. (2) LOOK BEFORE LAUNCHING: before any runtime probe on new instrumentation, verify the staged artifact contains the change -- cheapest check is grepping the built DLL bytes for a distinctive new literal (python: b'newstring' in open(dll,'rb').read()). The stale-DLL run was detected only because the first window-reset line lacked the new fields. (3) The E0603 itself: src/experiments/mod.rs declares 'mod gpu_readback;' PRIVATE with 'pub(crate) use gpu_readback::*' -- from OUTSIDE experiments (e.g. telemetry.rs), reference re-exported items as crate::experiments::ITEM, never crate::experiments::gpu_readback::ITEM (sibling modules inside experiments CAN use the full private path, which is why startup_hooks compiled).

WORKFLOW PREFERENCE (user, 2026-06-17). When the context is heavily RESUMED/already-COMPACTED (compacted once or more) and a clean fresh start with a written handoff would serve better than another /compact, suggest /new (new context + fresh handoff) INSTEAD of /compact. Rule of thumb: /compact for ordinary length; /new when the session is so layered that a clean slate + crisp handoff beats squeezing the existing context. ALWAYS put the /compact-or-/new suggestion at the VERY END of the message as the LAST line -- never intermixed mid-message. Durably bank everything (bd memories) BEFORE suggesting /new so the fresh session resumes immediately. Recorded in /home/banon/AGENTS.md. Related: dont-pause-when-authorized.

CSCloth EXIT-CRASH root cause + fix (2026-07-07). SYMPTOM: FromSoft DLPanic 'CSCloth: accessed an uninitialized singleton' (FD4Singleton.h:0xb4) -> AV write-null at deobf 0x141eb9999 -> NtTerminateProcess 0xc0000005 (CTD) at game shutdown/return-to-title. Occurred in 2 of the user's manual quit sessions + 1 autonomous teardown (3 of 12 crash-log sessions); intermittent (timing race). NOT caused by the blank-pane semaphore/fix (identical crash on old DLL). ROOT CAUSE: our loading-portrait feature SPARES a CSMenuProfModelRend (overlay_composite.rs: 'do NOT drop the spared renderer') and our DRAW-phase CSTaskImp task (profile_lookat_realtime_draw_tick, lookat_bone_hooks.rs pump ~line 436-500) drives its UPDATE (FUN_140bba820 / PROFILE_MODEL_UPDATE_TASK_RVA 0xbba730) + DRAW (per_frame_push_hook -> FUN_140bba7d0) every render frame. At shutdown the world FD4Singleton<CSCloth> is freed FIRST; our drive then triggers the profile model's CLOTH RELEASE (FUN_1409f0250: if model->field_0x130 != 0 && CSCloth==null -> DLPanic). Crash stack: CSTaskRunner FUN_1426d5580 -> our task (self+0x388a) -> FUN_140bba7d0 -> FUN_1409f0250. GLOBAL_CSCloth ptr = live/dump 0x143d75f28 (data, shift 0; disasm-verified: mov rcx,[0x143d75f28]; test rcx,rcx; DLPanic). RVA 0x3d75f28. The existing PROFILE_RENDERER_TEARDOWN_FENCE guards renderer TOCTOU, NOT the CSCloth singleton; catch_unwind can't catch DLPanic (native abort). FIX: in the pump, skip update+draw when safe_read_usize(base+CS_CLOTH_GLOBAL_RVA)==0 (CS_CLOTH_GLOBAL_RVA=0x3d75f28), counter PROFILE_DRIVE_CLOTH_SKIPS / oracle_profile_drive_cloth_skips. Safe: FUN_1409f0250 only reaches the panic when the model HAS cloth AND manager gone, so guarding never skips a legit in-menu render (CSCloth up throughout title/ProfileSelect/in-world). VERIFIED no-regression (in-world run: render_drives=178, cloth_skips=0, clean exit, no CSCloth AV). Shutdown-catch is disasm-grounded but the intermittent race was not empirically triggered in the verify run; confirm over a few real quit-to-menu sessions.

er-flver crate (created 2026-06-29) = the FLVER half of native-shader vertex binding. crates/er-flver, host-only, pure Rust, edition 2024. WRAPS fstools_formats::flver::reader for structural parse (panic-safe: crate::parse_structural wraps FLVER::from_reader in catch_unwind -> FlverError, because fstools panics on unknown semantic/asserts on padding). TWO views over one parse: (a) parse()->ObjectModel = normalized positions/normals/uvs/tangents (ported verbatim from old er-objectkit/flver.rs, proven on c4800) for Bevy PBR; (b) parse_raw()->RawFlver = RAW interleaved buffer slices (bytes[data_offset+buffer_offset..+buffer_length]) + VertexMember{semantic,semantic_raw,semantic_index,format_code,struct_offset,unk0} + map_format() authoritative SEMANTIC-KEYED format table + match_isg1(sig) reconciliation (pairs FLVER members to shader ISG1 by D3D SemanticName+SemanticIndex; semantic_to_d3d_name: Position->POSITION, Normal->NORMAL, Tangent->TANGENT, Bitangent->BINORMAL, Color->COLOR, UV->TEXCOORD, BoneWeights->BLENDWEIGHT, BoneIndices->BLENDINDICES). Optional 'wgpu' feature adds VertexFormat::to_wgpu + RawVertexBuffer::wgpu_layout. KEY FORMAT-TABLE DECISIONS (ER FLVER2 0x2001A): bind packed members RAW with bit-faithful wgpu format, let the .vpo do its own un-pack. (1) biased-byte normals/tangents 0x10/0x12/0x13/0x2F -> Uint8x4 + shader (b-127)/127, NOT Snorm8x4 (off by 127 vs 127.5 bias). (2) uv-scaled shorts 0x15/0x16 + UV arm of 0x12/0x13 -> Sint16x2/x4 + shader /uvFactor (2048 for ER); wgpu has no scaled-int vertex format. (3) 0x1A & 0x2E -> Snorm16x4 (i16/32767). (4) 0xF0 EdgeCompressed = unbindable (skip). (5) 0x04 Float4 Normal exists in ER (not in C# enums). er-objectkit keeps 'pub mod flver { pub use er_flver::* }' alias = zero consumer edits. OPEN RISKS to validate vs real c4800 .vpo (see issue er-effects-rs-bq0): 0x2E snorm-vs-half disagreement, biased-byte vs hw-snorm in actual shader decode math, uvFactor 2048 vs 1024, parallel multi-buffer per-D3D-slot binding.

GOLDEN ORACLE BASELINE (2026-06-18, captured LIVE from a user-driven native load -- continuous snapshot /tmp/banon-capture.log + in-game Status screenshot target/smoke/banon-in-world.png showing "Banon" Level 150). This is the definitive oracle signature the zero-input autoload must reproduce.

CONFIRMED REAL LOAD (Banon = save-files/150-Banon, slot 0, account 76561197986456766):

- oracle_saved_map_c30 = 0xe000000  (Banon real map; NOT 0xa010000 = m10 new-game default)

Load progression captured: 15:22:03 level=150 present=False c30=0xe000000 slot=0 (loading) -> 15:22:06 present=True (in world) -> 15:22:08 grounded=True blk=0xe000000 (walking).

FAILURE / NEW-GAME-DEFAULT signature (what ALL ~14 prior probes showed): oracle_char_level=9 (or transient -1/371 at menu), oracle_saved_map_c30=0xa010000 (m10) or 0xffffffff, player_present=False. So c30==0xa010000 == new-game default == FAILURE.

Zero-input LoadJob assembly/enqueue recipe (extends native-loadjob-menujobresult-loadjobcontext). At the factory site 0x14082aa4c-0x14082aaaf the orchestrator (this=rbx, arg rdi=context/slot source, reads dword [rdi]) builds the job and enqueues it:
- Job ASSEMBLER = 0x1408288e0 (3 callers: 0x140828db3, 0x140828f13, 0x14082aaa3). Called as 0x1408288e0(rcx=&out@[rsp+0x40], rdx=rdi(ctx src), r8=rax(aux from 0x1407600a0), r9=rsi, stack[rsp+0x20]=&completion_functor@[rbp-0x70], stack[rsp+0x28]=&loadjob_functor@[rsp+0x50]). The loadjob_functor@[rsp+0x50] = {vtable 0x142ac7728, fn 0x14082c240}; the completion_functor@[rbp-0x70] uses _Func_base<bool> vtable 0x142a93c38 then 0x142ac76f0 (+ descriptor 0x142ac7108 via helper 0x1407600a0).
- ENQUEUE = 0x1407418d0(rcx=this=rbx, rdx=job) -- GENERIC CSTask-family enqueue (52 callers across the binary; same family as our existing CSTaskImp enqueue, NOT load-specific). So enqueuing is generic; load identity is in the assembler + callback.
STEPPER PLAN: build the MenuJobResult(LoadJobContext&) LoadJob (callback 0x14082c240) with a slot-0 LoadJobContext + completion functor via 0x1408288e0, then enqueue via 0x1407418d0 -- zero input, no menu nav. STILL TODO (next iteration): (1) the orchestrator function entry + its this(rbx) class; (2) the LoadJobContext field layout that 0x14082c240 reads (slot index, dest, flags); (3) what 0x1407600a0 + descriptor 0x142ac7108 set up. Anchors for callback/factory pinned in scripts/check-menu-constructor-static.py (run 242, commit d3b417c).

POST-CONTINUE PORTRAIT -- OWN-RENDERER BUILD PATH WORKS (2026-06-29, branch spike/boot-parallelization). REVERSES the 'must spare the menu renderer' framing. The user's instinct was right: build our OWN renderers that outlive the menu's, rather than sparing the menu's (whose model is torn down externally). PROVEN end-to-end in a clean capped native-continue run (0 MessageBoxDialog builds, 0 crashes):

MECHANISM (all in maybe_build_profile_table_for_loading + force_profile_render_tick, src/experiments/startup_hooks.rs):
1. TRIGGER: empty-table streak. After Continue the menu's profile-renderer title table (base+0x3d6d8d0) goes empty and STAYS empty (menu teardown+rebuild is synchronous, so menu cycling never shows a multi-tick-empty table; only a real Continue does). Fire when streak>=PROFILE_TABLE_EMPTY_STREAK_BUILD_THRESHOLD(3) -- ~17.5s, well before the now_loading flag (~21s, too late on the fast gold-save load). HARD SAFETY: gate on PROFILE_TABLE_WAS_POPULATED (set once we've seen a populated table) -- at the title screen the table is ALSO empty and calling the builder before the engine/ResMan is up access-violates inside the builder (observed: crash at builder+0x9f, game_man unresolved).
2. BUILD: call the engine builder PROFILE_TABLE_BUILDER_RVA=0x9af3a0 (FUN_1409af3a0). It HeapAllocs+ctors 10 fresh CSMenuProfModelRend, each self-registering its own ResMan model build/draw tasks. Safe to call post-Continue (no crash). Result: built[r=10].
3. FEED (the critical missing piece): the mark+refresh feed (PROFILE_MARK_SLOT_USED_RVA + PROFILE_RENDERER_REFRESH_RVA, reads ProfileSummary = GameDataMan+SLOT_MANAGER_CONTAINER_OFFSET) that KICKS the async model build only ran every 240 ticks. The now-loading window is <240 ticks, so a single feed kicks the build then it DECAYS (built[m] 10->0 in ~0.7s). FIX: open a bounded post-Continue feed window (PROFILE_LOADSCREEN_FEED_TICKS=PROFILE_LOADSCREEN_FEED_WINDOW_TICKS=1800) and run the idempotent mark+refresh every 8 ticks while open + reset PROFILE_FORCE_TICK_COUNTER=0 at build so the first feed is immediate. Then built[m] PERSISTS 7-10 live and the offscreen RT renders continuously: rt nonblack 4->24, changed 6->36 (real animated character pixels, look-at sinusoid driving head motion) across +19.0s->+20.7s. Models collapse to 0 only at +21.4s AFTER player=1/world-load (loading screen ends) -- expected; a longer load = longer window.

REMAINING (recipe step 4, the last piece for VISIBILITY): the portrait renders into its offscreen RT (oracle-proven nonblack+changed) but is NOT yet bound to the now-loading surface texture, so not visible on the actual loading screen. Bind the built renderer's offscreen RT (renderer+0xa8 CSEzOffscreenRend -> +0x10 CSRuntimeTexResCap) to the now-loading sprite's texture NAME via TexRepositoryImp::InsertCSRuntimeTexResCapIfNotExists deobf 0x1401e96e0 (replace the static-TPF forge loading_bg_replace_bind_hook 0x140d697d0). Open: exact wide texture name the now-loading sprite samples (probe saw '02_903_NowLoading2' / 'MENU_Load_NNNNN'). POLISH: built[m] oscillates 7-10 (some slots' models flicker/rebuild); for a single clean portrait focus slot 0 stability. Supersedes postcontinue-portrait-spare-DISPROVEN-2026-06-29 (spare is still dead; THIS is the live path). Instrumentation: lookat-spared-sweep now reports built[r m] + loadbuilds.

HARNESS BUG (fixed 2026-06-21): reusing an ARTIFACT_DIR across runtime probes stranded every rerun. The DLL writes telemetry to $ARTIFACT_DIR/er-effects-telemetry.json (ER_EFFECTS_TELEMETRY_PATH); the readiness watcher (.auto/runtime_probe.sh / er-readiness-watch.py) reads it each poll. On a REUSED dir the file held a PRIOR run's oracle_cold_char_mount_phase=5 / game_task_ticks=927, so the watcher read stale completion and tore the new eldenring.exe down within ~1s (user saw a brief white game-sized window), BEFORE the new process booted to the title/own-stepper/cold-mount. SYMPTOM: fresh debug log had ONLY the DLL attach hooks (online-disable/input-block/xinput-block/auto-accept), no own_stepper pass-through, no cold-char-mount; readiness-result.json reason=cold_char_mount_complete with identical ticks=927 every run; er-effects-telemetry.json mtime frozen at an old time. ROOT: scripts/run-product-continue-direct-probe.sh never reset per-run evidence before launch. FIX: added  right before write_autoload_request (after the auth gates) so the watcher waits for FRESH telemetry (reproduces first-run-in-fresh-dir). LESSON: always verify a runtime probe actually executed (fresh debug-log lines + telemetry mtime within the run window) before trusting reason=*_complete; a reused artifact dir silently false-positives. Combine with runtime-probe-requires-steam-running-2026-06-21.

Pre-warmed HEADLESS Ghidra MCP (no GUI/Xvfb), verified. 13bm built from source at /home/banon/tools/GhidraMCP-13bm (Go bridge + extension in ghidra_12.1_PUBLIC/Ghidra/Extensions/GhidraMCP-13bm). new MCPServer(null) runs headless (only get_current_address/function need the tool). scripts/ghidra/MCPServeHeadless.java runs the TCP server from a long-lived analyzeHeadless -process script; scripts/ghidra/mcp-ghidra-daemon.sh {start|stop|status|restart} manages it. DURABILITY FIX (2026-06-22): GhidraScript wraps run() in an auto-transaction (named after the script) that blocks commit+save; the daemon blocks forever so it never closes -> edits never persisted. Fix: call end(true) right after server start to commit+close it; then MCP mutations commit via their own transactions and a periodic Program.save() (guarded by getCurrentTransactionInfo()==null, default 60s, --save-interval to tune) + a flush on clean stop persist them. end() is idempotent (FlatProgramAPI: no-op when transactionID==-1) so the framework's post-run end(true) is safe. The earlier 'headless cannot persist' note was WRONG/superseded. --readonly for query-only. .mcp.json registers the bridge; restart MCP client to load ghidra__* tools. === CORRECTED VTABLES (prior memories wrong) ===
- ProfileLoadDialog vtable = 0x142b21bf8 (RVA 0x2b21bf8), NOT 0x142b229f8 (that is the RTTI col-locator region). PROOF: load_activate 0x1409a4670 appears in .data exactly ONCE, at VA 0x142b21c98 = vtable+0xa0 (slot 20). Slots: [0]=dtor 0x1407342b0, [0x10]=update 0x1409a3860, [0x90]=0x1409a3480, [0x98]=0x1409a3490, [0xa0]=load_activate 0x1409a4670 (LAST real slot; +0xa8=RTTI 0x1433270c8).
- TitleTopDialog vtable = 0x142b25668 (RVA 0x2b25668) CONFIRMED (NOT 0x142b26468). Slots: [0]=dtor 0x1407342b0, [0x10]=update 0x1409aac10, [0x18]=0x1409aa420, [0x20]=0x140744300. Vtable is SHORT (~7 slots; +0x90 spills into ASCII PressStartInfo string) -- TitleTopDialog has NO load_activate slot; active-list select uses the realized-list path, not a dialog vtable slot.

=== ACTIVE LIST = REALIZED ENTRY VECTOR (distinct from +0x1f8 widgets and +0xa48 specs) ===
Selectable Continue/Load entries live in a 3rd vector: [menu+0x1290]..[menu+0x1298], STRIDE 0x210, menu=dialog+0xa38. Resolver index->entry = 0x14078fbd0(rcx=menu, edx=index): 0<=idx<count, count=([+0x1298]-[+0x1290])/0x210, ret [+0x1290]+idx*0x210. CURSOR = [menu+0xd4] = [dialog+0xa38+0xd4] = [dialog+0xb0c]; getter 0x140739e20 = mov eax,[rcx+0xd4];ret. Count/bound mirrored at [dialog+0xb08].

=== SELECT/CONFIRM ROUTER (0x14078e1c0 region, CSMenu input router) ===
At 0x14078e20e: lea rcx,[rsi+0xa38]; call 0x140739e20 (read cursor); call 0x14078fbd0 (cursor->entry); if [entry+0xf8]!=0: rax=[entry]; call [rax+0x10] (entry action). Confirm variant 0x14078e255 -> 0x14078ef20(menu,entry,0). CONFIRM = resolve cursor->entry, invoke entry->vt[+0x10]; Load-Game entry action ends at dialog_factory 0x14081ead0.

=== load_activate 0x1409a4670 ABI (ProfileLoadDialog slot 20=+0xa0) ===
rcx=dialog. Reads cursor [dialog+0xb0c]; clamps if <0 or >=[dialog+0xb08]. FD4-ASSERTS PlayerGameData [0x144588268]!=0. Registers selector step 0x140826d50 child. PRECOND: WRITE [dialog+0xb0c]=row (< [dialog+0xb08]) BEFORE call; PGD non-null.

=== dialog_factory 0x14081ead0 ABI ===
d180 functor wraps thunk 0x140820c60 = add rcx,8; jmp 0x14081ead0. 0x14081ead0(rcx=capture+8, rdx=ctx): 0x1409aa680 (TitleTopDialog accessor), op-new 0x1cd0, ProfileLoadDialog ctor 0x1409a3d90(r8=[r14]=owner-from-capture). Returns ProfileLoadDialog* (vt 0x142b21bf8). DO NOT cold-call -- needs valid capture; invoke d180 functor instead.

=== continue_confirm 0x140b0e180 (SAVE-WRITE gate) ===
rcx=shim, owner=[rcx+8]. cmp [owner+0x284],1 -> ==1 NewGame(edx4) else LOAD(edx5). reads c30 -> [owner+0xbc]; SetState 0x140b0d960(owner,5=PlayGame) at 0x140b0e1bd. ONLY save-write call; gate (ac0==slot && c30 real).

=== d180 leaf functor-invoke (0x1407ad1c0) ===
Precond: [item+0x130]==0 AND [item+0xa8]!=0 AND [item+0x10]==0. Invoke 0x1407ad2a0: rcx=[item+0xa8]; rax=[rcx]; rdx=item+0x10; call [rax+0x10] -> 0x140820c60 -> 0x14081ead0. Result->[item+0x130].

DURABLE PROTOCOL (user, 2026-06-23; CLARIFIED: 'semaphore' here = an IN-PROCESS MEMORY-READ TELEMETRY ORACLE -- a value the DLL derives by reading the game's PE/RAM, i.e. the oracle_* fields in er-effects-telemetry.json (oracle_msgbox_total_builds, oracle_player_present, oracle_saved_map_c30, oracle_server_status_any_seen, oracle_char_name, ...). NOT a bd memory, NOT the screenshot. The RAM-read oracle is always the PRIMARY detector; the screenshot is only a FALLBACK for discovering a phenomenon the oracles missed). Every ER runtime probe produces a low-quality teardown screenshot of the validated ER window (class steam_app_1245620, focus-then-grim, privacy fail-closed) via scripts/capture-er-window.py, run FIRST in the probe cleanup() trap (EXIT/INT/TERM/HUP), BEFORE the game is killed. Path: target/runtime-probe/<probe>-<timestamp>/teardown-screenshot.jpg (or .txt note when fail-closed). Keep it wired into every probe harness. THE LOOP: when a run does something UNEXPECTED that NO in-process memory-read semaphore detected -> (1) READ that run's teardown-screenshot.jpg (ground truth for the on-screen state the telemetry missed); (2) resolve it into RAM-read telemetry so the image is never needed again: (a) a memory-read oracle SHOULD have caught it but is broken/incomplete (wrong address, false-negative) -> fix/extend that telemetry oracle (the in-process reader + its oracle_* field); or (b) genuinely new phenomenon, no oracle -> ADD a NEW in-process memory-read semaphore (find the native struct/flag/dialog in PE memory, expose an oracle_* field) and classify it GOOD (desired milestone evidence) or BAD (blocker/regression the watcher fails on). Never let 'the image showed X' stay a one-off visual observation: every on-screen phenomenon must end up detectable from PE/RAM telemetry (consistent with 'prefer native/in-process evidence over OCR'). Record the RE finding in bd for the next agent, but the SEMAPHORE itself lives in the DLL memory-read telemetry, not bd. Wired into AGENTS.md 'Teardown screenshot -> semaphore loop'. Relates: save-override-no-default-fallback-mandatory-env-2026-06-23.

STATIC RE VERDICT (2026-06-23, deobf eldenring-deobf.bin base 0x140000000 + Ghidra ermaporch dump): Zero-input activation of the registrar's EXISTING idle Continue job = NO. The load is fundamentally job-BUILD-driven; confirm BUILDS A FRESH job in-context. There is no pre-built idle Continue load job to merely flip on.

Q1 (two builds differ?): YES, different. (a) Menu-open registrar build = dump FUN_1408337a0 (deobf region near 0x140833880) chains ONLY the title-flow NAVIGATION jobs (FUN_140839290 + FUN_140838a00, the ~9-job sequence) and installs into dialog+0x10 -- the menu nav/selection graph, NOT a load job. (b) Big 11-job builder = dump FUN_140833970. (c) CONFIRM does NOT run a pre-built job: per-frame pump FUN_1409aad60 calls FUN_1409b2630 (deobf 0x1409b2630, prologue 48 8d 45 d8 ... verified) which BUILDS A BRAND-NEW FixOrderJobSequence from the LIVE dialog (titleFlowContext = dialog->titleFlowContext, target = dialog->MenuWindow+0x50) via FUN_140833970, chains TextFadeOut + the load seq, and INSTALLS into dialog+0x10 via FUN_1407a9340. Our failed build-new-and-forge-install attempts were architecturally the SAME thing confirm does; the crash was mis-contextualization, not a wrong path.

Q2 (native confirm-activation): FUN_1409aad60(TitleTopDialog*, evt, InputData*) is the title MenuWindow per-frame update. It builds an InputData predicate via FUN_14075a5c0/450/4d0 (stores std::function bool(CS::InputData const&) at predicate+0x38) and evaluates cVar3 = (vmethod+0x10)(pred, InputData). On match (FadeIn-state branches) it calls FUN_1409b2630(dialog) which builds+installs the load seq. The dispatcher that distinguishes Continue vs NewGame is FUN_1409a9000 (invoked as a menu-job std::function callback, NOT a direct call; impl-table refs at 0x144902624 / 0x14369a620): sel = *(int*)(dialog->titleFlowContext + 0x14c); if sel==0 or sel==3/5 -> idle/clear; else -> FUN_1409b31c0(dialog, ..., dialog+0x50) which checks TitleFlowContext::IsNotReleaseFlag55 then builds the real LoadGame job (FUN_140834340 wrapped by FUN_1407a8120). Slot-confirm handler FUN_1409a9500 (from FUN_1409b1180) gates on the flag then writes chosen slot via FUN_140738e40(titleFlowContext+0x147, slotIdx, 0) and advances.

THE GATE FLAG: DAT_144589bdc (byte global). Reader RE-GROUNDED to deobf 0x140e85f50 (0f b6 05 85 3c 70 03 c3 = movzbl [0x144589bdc],al; ret; dump FUN_140e85f60 is shifted -0x10 in this region). Writer = deobf ~0x140e85f64 (mov cl,[0x144589bdc]; ret; dump FUN_140e85f80). In FUN_1409aad60 final gate LAB_1409aaf43: if (FUN_140e85f60() != 0 AND dialog->field_0xa40 == 0) { GLOBAL_CSPcKeyConfig=0; FUN_1409b2630(dialog); } -- when the flag is SET, the pump fires the load build with NO input predicate read.

Q3/Q4 (zero-input trigger?): The flag is a non-input state, BUT setting it does NOT bypass input nor avoid building a new job; it merely makes the SAME per-frame pump take the same FUN_1409b2630 build path. The flag is normally SET by the confirm-input handlers themselves. There is NO state you can write that runs the EXISTING idle job; the only load mechanism is (re)building via FUN_1409b2630 / FUN_140833970 / FUN_1409b31c0 with the live dialog. Closest non-input shims = the two direct builder callers: FUN_1409b13b0 and FUN_1409b0950 (both: dialog = *(ctx+8); GLOBAL_CSPcKeyConfig = 0 or 1; FUN_1409b2630(dialog)). These are menu-job result callbacks (rdata refs 0x142b26638 / 0x142b266a8).

PRODUCT IMPLICATION: activate-existing-job is NOT a distinct path -- it does not exist. Correct zero-input recipe: call FUN_1409b2630(live_dialog) (or FUN_1409b13b0 / FUN_1409b0950 with a ctx whose +8 is the live dialog) FROM THE MENU/GAME THREAD WHILE THE TITLE MENU IS OPEN AND IDLE (dialog->field_0xa40 == 0, dialog anim in FadeIn/idle) so dialog->titleFlowContext and dialog->MenuWindow+0x50 are the live ones the builder reads. Prior crashes came from firing the build during an out-of-band FD4 pump when +0x50/titleFlowContext were not the live menu's. So: not input-gated in the sense of REQUIRING a device read (the build path can be entered via the flag or the direct callbacks with zero input), but it IS fundamentally a BUILD that MUST run in the dialog's own live menu-thread context.

Re-grounded deobf entries: load build+install FUN_1409b2630=0x1409b2630; pump FUN_1409aad60=0x1409aad60 (body confirmed, region unshifted there); flag reader=0x140e85f50; per-step load builder FUN_1409b31c0=0x1409b31c0; LoadGame job ctor FUN_140834340; nav build dump FUN_1408337a0; 11-job build dump FUN_140833970; confirm factory wrapper deobf 0x140826510 = dump FUN_140826600.

For an OFFLINE single-player load the ONLY live input is GameMan::warpRequested, which the native full deserialize 0x67b290 sets =true; the advancer consumes+auto-clears it at case 8. BUG: OUR code cleared it -- set_warp_requested(false) at system_quit_repro_guards.rs:1681 (during switch handoff, BEFORE finalize = PRIMARY starve) and title_tick_cover.rs:1370 (stable-proof latch). FIX APPLIED: removed BOTH set_warp_requested(false) calls (kept save_requested clears), so warpRequested stands and the advancer finalizes 18->19(0x140af5a...)->20(FINISH 0x140af5a20)->-1 -> requestCode 1->2->0 -> render. The title-bounce those clears fought = downstream invalid warp TARGET, now handled by the RequestMoveMap BlockId fix. RUNTIME FOLLOW-UP (RE flagged): verify field25_0x12a walks 0..8 and the case-7->8 gate (FUN_14067a170 && !ShouldSave && !FUN_140679460 && CSRemo-idle 0x140a9ceb0) clears; if it stalls before case 8 that gate is the residual holdout. VALIDATED (user hypothesis + profiler evidence): the post-init MENU phase is FRAME-PACED ANIMATION, not compute -- a TRACTABLE optimization target (unlike the irreducible ~12.6s engine-init). Evidence (dense run onscreen-capture-20260624-143025, main-thread RIP in 13-24s window): dominated by UpdateFlipTiming (0x140e82xxx, render present/vsync frame-pacing; entry 0x140e829d0) + a tight ntdll wait cluster (0x6ffffff3fxxx, 0x7fd5cd8e6180 ~48% of samples = per-frame present/vsync wait). Completely different code than the init phase (which was memcpy/CRC/crypto load fns). Main thread is ~90% 'busy' but it is the 60fps present/vsync wait loop, NOT loading -> menu transitions advance per-frame => wall-clock = frames x frame-time = ANIMATION PLAYBACK SPEED. Milestone mapping: pab_dismiss 13.2s -> title_owner 15.9s -> menu_open 19.9s ~= 6.7s of title+menu transition animation (the 'two menus' the user navigates); menu_open 19.9 -> in_world 23.9 ~4s is real world-load. DEFINITIVE FD4 scheduler group-activation decode (2026-06-15, ER 1.16.1, base 0x140000000, ASLR off, full VAs, READ-ONLY static RE). Answers the brief Q1-Q4. RESULT: there is NO per-group GameFlow 'active' gate for CSTaskGroup 16-20 -- the scheduler runs the WHOLE group range [0..168] every frame unconditionally. The group-20 stall in our forced-title state is NOT a scheduler gate; it is the MoveMapStep step-machine self-gating on unprimed world/stream state. The brief's option (a) ('set the group-activation flag so the scheduler ticks group 20') DOES NOT EXIST as a separate lever. CITES disasm.

=== TWO SINGLETONS (corrected) ===
0x14458be88 = CSTaskGroup singleton: array of 168 CSTimeLineTaskGroupIns at [singleton + index*8 + 0x8] (bound idx<=0xa8=168). BOTH register variants insert here: register_task 0x140eb1fe0 (rcx ARG=CSTaskImp but the fn actually loads 0x14458be88) and 0x140eb2120 both -> indexed insert 0x140eb27c0/0x140eb2790 reading [0x14458be88 + idx*8 + 0x8]. (CORRECTION to fd4-scheduler-tasktick-movemap-group20-gate-2026: register_task reads 0x14458be88, not 0x14458be08.) Group-count/order getter 0x140eb26f0(rcx=CSTaskGroup,edx=idx): returns [task_groups[idx] + 0x54]. The 168 group objects are all created at CSTask system init (ctor 0x140eb1970 -> group-array alloc 0x140eb28a0), so task_groups[16..20] EXIST from boot, before any InGame flow.
0x14458be08 = CSTaskImp singleton (vftable + inner CSTask at +8). 0x14485d170 = CSTaskRunnerManager singleton (asserted with resource 0x14485d0d8); sole writer is the CSTask system ctor 0x140eb1970 @0x140eb1b10. The runner manager is the multithreaded worker pool that actually executes queued group tasks (enqueue 0x1426a0c60 / 0x142655000; submit-range 0x14268a240).

=== THE PER-FRAME GROUP RUN = FULL RANGE, NO GATE (Q1) ===
The ONLY full-group-range submit in the whole binary is 0x140e35880 (callscan: 1 caller). 0x140e35880(rcx=subject 0x144588168): reads task_groups[0xa8].+0x54 (ebx) and task_groups[0].+0x54 (eax) via 0x140eb26f0, then tail-jmps the runner range-submit 0x14268a240(rcx=subject, edx=eax, r8d=ebx) -> enqueues the ENTIRE group priority range to the runner-manager 0x14485d170 every call. NO per-group active/enable bool is tested anywhere in this path. The fromsoftware-rs TaskGroupEntry{index,name[64],active} 'active' bool is a registration-validity flag, NOT a GameFlow phase gate. 0x140e35880 has ONE caller: 0x140dec2f7, inside 0x140deb0b0.

=== 0x140deb0b0 = CSTask SYSTEM INIT + frame-task install (Q2) ===
0x140deb0b0 (5085 bytes, gate 0x143d85738 via once-check 0x140e8a7e0, config singleton 0x144588de8). @0x140dec2b8 it register_task 0x140eb1fe0(CSTaskImp 0x14458be08, edx=7=SystemStep, r8=runner buffer) -- installs the master per-frame frame-driver task into CSTaskGroup 7. @0x140dec2f7 it then runs 0x140e35880 (full-range submit). callscan: 0x140deb0b0 has exactly ONE caller = 0x140c8fe9b, inside the BOOT/INTRO worker thread 0x140c8fe90 (decode-0x140c8fe90-is-intro-thread-not-handoff). So 0x140deb0b0 is the ONE-TIME CSTask bring-up: it registers the recurring frame-driver into group 7 and the runner threads then re-submit all groups [0..168] every frame from that group-7 task. There is NO second 'enable InGame groups' submit -- all groups run every frame from the moment CSTask is initialized at boot.

=== WHY GROUP 20 DOES NOT ADVANCE IN OUR FORCED STATE (Q3/Q4) -- the real floor ===
Because the scheduler runs ALL groups every frame, the MoveMapStep (registered into CSTaskGroup 20 by STEP_MoveMap_Init 0x140aec120 @0x140aec1ca via 0x140eb55a0 edx=0x14) IS being ticked once it is registered. The stall is NOT 'group 20 never ticks'; it is that the MoveMapStep's execute + its 9 node executors SELF-GATE / early-exit on unprimed singletons & world-stream state:
 - MoveMapStep vtable 0x142b5ff38 slot2 Execute 0x140af49b0: asserts 0x144589578 / 0x144588268 / inputmgr 0x143d6b7b0; @0x140af4a55 cmp [0x143d6b7b0],0 -> if null EARLY-EXIT 0x140af4b1e; it only does get_step + HUD/camera, it does NOT drive BeginInit->MsbLoad (that lives in the 9 node tasks).
 - The 9 node executors (node1 0x140aff640 etc.) read world/stream state and AV (0x14061f71d) when called outside a primed context (movemapstep-direct-pump-crashes-needs-scheduler).
This reconciles the runtime: in our forced state our manual InGameStep pump 0x140b0bd60 advances the InGameStep step machine but the MoveMapStep's own machine (BeginInit 0x140af5140 -> MsbLoad 0x140af8f00 -> world singletons 0x143d691d8/0x143d69ba8) only completes once the world-STREAM subsystem is primed -- which is what is missing, NOT a group-activation flag.

=== ZERO-INPUT LEVER VERDICT ===
Brief option (a) is a NON-LEVER: there is no group-activation flag/policy to set -- the scheduler already runs group 20 every frame. The genuine blocker is the world-stream priming that the MoveMapStep node machine needs (the same scheduler-owned/stream layer as slot9-deserialize-works-world-stream-needs-priming). Option (c) (re-register MoveMapStep execute into an already-ticking group) is moot -- group 20 IS ticking; re-homing won't change the node self-gate. The remaining real work is priming the world-stream so the MoveMapStep's MsbLoad node completes natively (drive the b80 load-phase machine to completion so child+0x100 has slot 9's real map and the stream goes resident, per slot9-load-phase-machine-b80-csfeman-less-2026), NOT activating a task group.

=== STRUCTURAL FLOOR (Q4) ===
The architecture has no GameFlow 'turn on InGame task lines' bitmask. CSTask runs every registered group every frame from boot. GameFlow phases are expressed by (1) WHICH step objects are constructed+registered into the InGame groups (the FE-host/InGameStep/MoveMapStep chain, scheduler-owned construction per frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned-not-callable), and (2) each step machine's internal state gating its own work. We already force (1) via SetState(5). The wall is (2)+world-stream priming, not a missing scheduler group gate. Related: fd4-scheduler-tasktick-movemap-group20-gate-2026, movemapstep-direct-pump-crashes-needs-scheduler, slot9-load-phase-machine-b80-csfeman-less-2026, slot9-deserialize-works-world-stream-needs-priming, csfeman-construction-chain-ingamestep-step6-2026, decode-0x140c8fe90-is-intro-thread-not-handoff.

=== CROSS-LINK / RECONCILED with async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026 (disasm-verified 2026-06-15) ===
NO CONTRADICTION between 'scheduler ticks ALL groups every frame' and 'the world-stream worker task is not scheduler-registered'. A task must be REGISTERED INTO a group to be ticked; all groups ticking does not help a task that was never registered. The world-stream worker task 0x144842d40 is constructed+registered ONLY by IngameInit's tail SetState-helper 0x140b0a980, and ONLY in its '+0x48>=7' arm: VERIFIED @0x140b0a99e inc [this+0x4c]; @0x140b0a9a1 eax=[this+0x48]+1; @0x140b0a9a6 cmp eax,7; jbe SKIP(0x140b0aa76); the taken arm lazy-constructs 0x144842d40 (alloc 0x141eceb10, store @0x140b0a9c3) and configures/registers it. The forced FE-host path (SetState(5), or synthetic-IngameInit-this with +0x48==0) NEVER has +0x48>=7, so this arm is SKIPPED and 0x144842d40 is never set up -> the global engine worker pool 0x144853048 (backed by 0x144842d40) never services the queued slot reads -> the async b80 IO-request state never reaches 0x14 (resident) -> world singletons stay null. So 'group 20 ticks the MoveMapStep' (this memory) AND 'the world-stream worker is unregistered' (the b80 memory) are the SAME wall from two angles: the MoveMapStep machine IS ticked but its MsbLoad node has no serviced stream to go resident on. The real zero-input lever = replicate IngameInit's +0x48>=7 tail: construct+register 0x144842d40 with the FD4 scheduler (0x142656b00, key 0x59682f01) so the pool drains the reads. Related: async-io-b80-lane-mismatch-worker-not-the-blocker-2026, fd4-scheduler-tasktick-movemap-group20-gate-2026, ingameinit-decoded-world-built-by-movemapstep-msbload-2026, frontend-bootstrap-is-scheduler-owned-not-callable.

USER CORRECTION 2026-07-18 (supersedes the retracted 'intermittent/timing-race' claim): the freeze is DETERMINISTIC and SEQUENCE-SPECIFIC, not intermittent. Pattern: LOAD 1 (boot) renders fine -> LOAD 2 (first reload of a character) FREEZES (character invisible, world frozen) -> LOAD 3 (reload again) renders FINE. So it is a LOAD-2-SPECIFIC freeze that RECOVERS on load 3. This is a STALE-STATE / PARITY bug: load 1 leaves some state stale that breaks load 2's render handoff; load 2's teardown clears that state so load 3 is fine (same family as the stale-bc4 finding -- bc4 was ONE such stale element; there is at least one more stale element that specifically breaks the FIRST reload). My automated probe (boot=load1 -> auto-switch=load2) reproduces the load-2 stall (grounded: reload settles at mms18 external-finalize, blocks resident, render_ready=False) -- so the probe's load-2 mms18 stall is LIKELY the user's 2nd-load freeze. GAP: I never ran a 3rd load to observe the recovery. GROUNDED NEXT EXPERIMENT: run a probe that does load1 -> load2 (expect freeze/mms18) -> load3 (expect render OK), and DIFF the semaphores between the FROZEN load-2 state and the GOOD load-3 state -- the field(s) that differ = the stale element that breaks load 2. That diff is the bug. Instrument any candidate stale flags (bc4, the mms18 external-finalize gate the RE agent is finding, requestCode, return-title/save_requested flags) across loads 2 vs 3. Do NOT conclude the mechanism until the load2-vs-load3 semaphore diff is captured. Links [[grounded-gate2-mms18-external-finalize-never-fires-not-fade-2026-07-18]] [[third-load-softlock-root-is-stale-bc4-blocks-step-movemap-2026-07-16]].

Static RE (subagent, 2026-06-15) -- the orphaned load job at inner-TitleStep owner+0x2e8 is an InGameStep object (ctor 0x140aeaaa0, size 0xb3870, stored via child runner header owner+0x2e0). job+0xd8 is a PHASE gate not a boolean -- 0 idle/done, 1 request-submitted, 2 advanced/ready. Submit 0x140aebdc0(job,&req,-1,&handle) early-outs if [req]==-1, else sets +0xd8=1, copies request to +0x100/+0x104, appends id to vector +0xb35f0. It only ENQUEUES, runs nothing. Poll 0x140aebd30 is a pure status query (reads [job+0xe8]+0x244), never touches +0xd8 -- GameStepWait 0xb0cde0 only uses it to set cosmetic busy flag owner+0x3d8. The drain chain is the InGameStep step-handler table 0x143d70130 (built 0x1400a3710), dispatched by 0x140aea840 -- STEP_MoveMap_Update 0x140aec720 (gated on +0xe0 menu-job readiness via 0x140eb5530, sets +0xd8=2 at 0xaec915 and re-submits 0xaecba7), STEP_MoveMap_Loadlist 0x140aec120 (consumes +0x100, triggers the streaming load), drain 0x1409db1c0 (sets +0xd8=0 at 0x9db2a0 when done, emits step-result 2 via 0x7a91e0), registrar 0x1409d8fa0 (task_enqueue 0x7a7b60). CRITICAL -- these handlers run ONLY when the InGameStep step list / +0xe0 child runner is TICKED by the FromSoft task scheduler (singleton 0x143d872e0), which the inner TitleStep advances (via child owner+0x2e0) each frame ONLY while the OUTER SimpleTitleStep pumps its Ingame/MenuLoop lane. With the outer parked in STEP_Ingame and MenuLoop not running, the step list never ticks so +0xd8 stays 1 forever -- THIS is the orphan (runs 297-298). force_play_game (PlayGame 0xb0d5b0 bare-submit at 0xb0d6df) writes state 5 + fills +0x100/+0xd8=1 but skips the scheduler-driven ticking -- the omission is not a single call, it is the per-frame tick of the step graph. DIRECT-CALL options -- (1 robust) keep the outer MenuLoop lane running so it ticks owner+0x2e0 each frame (= solve the outer spin-up [[outer-simpletitlestep-state4-ingame-gate]]). (2 direct) tick the InGameStep dispatcher ourselves EACH FRAME from the game thread via owner+0x2e0 child runner update (vtable family 0x140eb5470/0x140eb55a0) -- multi-frame, job must be registered in scheduler 0x143d872e0, requires +0xe0 menu-job to resolve slot owner+0xbc. (3 fragile) call phase steps 0xaec720 then 0xaec120 then 0x9db1c0 in sequence on the game thread -- reentrancy risk (the 0x82a0f0 crash class). NO single call completes the load -- it is a multi-frame scheduler-ticked step graph. Related: [[outer-simpletitlestep-state4-ingame-gate]] [[force-play-game-sets-load-value]] [[force-play-game-needs-job-precondition]].
